ARNOTT, JAMES, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.D., C.M., Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical service, 1st April, 1867; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1867, and was employed under the military department till Aug., 1868, including service in the Abyssinian expedition (medal); and subsequently as assistant-surgeon in the David Sassoon hospital, Poona, and in the European general hospital, Bombay, and as professor of pathology in the Grant medical college; also acted as civil surgeon, and registrar of marriages, Poona, and in medical charge of the Deccan and civil engineering colleges, and as superintendent of the lunatic asylum and jail; acting surgeon, Gokaldas Tejpal hospital, Bombay, Jan., 1878; also acted as presidency surgeon and marine surgeon, Bombay; surgeon, European general hospital, Bombay, March-April, 1882; professor of midwifery, Grant medical college, and obstetric physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital from April, 1882; physician, European general hospital, May, 1891.

ARNOTT, NAPOLEON, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Military Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 22nd Dec., 1865; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal and the Punjab on irrigation duty; executive engineer, Jan., 1879; officiated as secretary to agent to governor general, Baluchistan, and as secretary to defence committee in 1882; employed on special defence works, Aug., 1887; superintending engineer, Dec., 1889; deputy director general, military works, Sept., 1890; transferred to Madras command as chief engineer, March, 1894.

ARTHUR, OSWALD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Winchester and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived, 8th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

ARTHUR, SIGISMUND RAYNOR, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Charterhouse, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1886; arrived in India, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer.

ARUNDEL, ARUDNEL TAGG, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived, 18th Nov., 1865; served in various districts of Madras from Aug., 1866, as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector, and acting collector and magistrate; private secretary to governor of Madras, July, 1875; acting deputy director, revenue settlement, April, 1878; president of the municipal commission, Madras, June, 1881; fellow of the Madras university, March, 1882; principal assistant to collector and magistrate and agent, Vizagapatam, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Dec., 1884; collector and magistrate, Kistna, Aug., 1887; member, board of revenue, and member of legislative council, Jan., 1894.

ARUNDELL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., F.C.H., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on surveys and other railway work in the Punjab; executive engineer, May, 1883; officiating deputy consulting engineer to government of India for railways, central division, April, 1886; confirmed, May, 1887; officiating in similar capacity in Bombay, April, 1891; returned to his previous post, June, 1893; officiating consulting engineer in same division, April, 1894.

ASHBURNER, LIONEL R., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, 1848; served in the revenue and judicial departments in Broach, Surat, and Kaira; on the outbreak of the mutiny in 1857, was appointed superintendent of police. Kaira, and organised a body of horse and foot for the protection of the eastern frontier; special commissioner for trial of rebels, 1858; collector and magistrate, Kandeish, 1861; revenue and police commissioner, N. division, 1872; member of council, Bombay, Aug., 1877; acted as governor, 13th March to 28th April, 1880; retired, 1883.

ASHTON, GEORGE, Financial Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 7th April, 1884.

ASTON, HENRY FAURE, B.L., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at the South African College; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 9th Oct., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting political assistant in Kathiawar (April, 1871, to June, 1874), acting judge and sessions judge, railway magistrate and cantonment magistrate; on famine duty, Sholapur, Sept., 1877; acting under secretary to government of Bombay in the political, judicial, and educational departments, and secretary to the council for making laws and regulations, Dec., 1881, to March, 1883; 1st grade assistant judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; judicial assistant to the political agent, Kathiawar, March, 1885; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; 2nd grade, June, 1891; on special duty in connection with settlement cases, Baroda, in 1888; officiating judicial commissioner, Lower Burma, April, 1894.

ATA-ULLAH KHAN, MIRZA, Sardar Bahadur, Hon. Lieut.-Colonel.—British agent at Kabul, June, 1885; retired, June, 1891.

ATKINS, CHARLES HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at King Henry VIII's School, Coventry, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 30th Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

ATKINS, JOHN DE COURCY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at University College School, London University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived, 9th Nov., 1880, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer; acted as under-secretary to government, Bombay, revenue, financial, &c., departments, in 1886, and again in 1889 and 1890; acting postmaster-general, Bombay, April-Sept., 1891; officiating collector and magistrate, and political agent, June-Oct., 1893, and from May, 1894.

ATKINSON, FREDERIC JAMES, Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as probationer, 8th July, 1873, and served in Bengal as district grain accountant and on audit duty and as officiating accountant-general; served also as assistant accountant-general, Bombay, Bengal, and N.W. Provinces; assistant comptroller, Burma, December, 1888; officiating comptroller and commissioner of paper currency in 1890; deputy accountant general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, June, 1891.

ATKINSON, JOHN NATHANIEL, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Marlborough College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and assistant sessions judge; on special duty under the commissioner of salt revenue, April, 1885; acting deputy commissioner of salt revenue, Oct. to Dec., 1885; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Sept., 1886; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1891.

ATLAY, Venerable B. T., M.A., late Ecclesiastical Department, Bengal.—Arrived in India, 17th Oct., 1867; chaplain of St. Paul's Cathedral and of the general hospital, Dec., 1867; senior chaplain, Oct., 1877; chaplain of Naini Tal, Feb., 1879; senior chaplain, St. Paul's Cathedral, and chaplain of the general hospital and presidency jail, Jan., 1882; commissary to the Lord Bishop, and officiating chaplain in charge of the diocese, March, 1883; archdeacon of Calcutta, Sept., 1883; retired, 1889, and was appointed vicar of Willesden.

ATUL CHUNDER DUTT, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Free Church Institution, Calcutta, and at Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in Madras, 14th Jan., 1890, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

AUCOTT, ELLIOTT, Funds Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 27th Feb., 1886.

AULAD HUSSAIN, Khan Bahadur, C.I.E., Central Provinces Commission.—Joined the service, 29th July, 1844; served in various subordinate posts till Dec., 1864, when he was appointed extra-assistant commissioner; on special duty at the Delhi assemblage, Dec., 1876; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; acting small cause court judge,

Jubbulpore, July, 1883; assistant commissioner, 1st class, Sept., 1883; settlement officer, Jan., 1885; also assistant commissioner, June, 1890.

AUSTIN, WARE PLUMPTRE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Gloucester School; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived, 28th Dec., 1864; served in the revenue and judicial department from May, 1866, as assistant magistrate and collector, sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate, and political agent; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1884, and again in 1888; collector and magistrate, Nov., 1886; acting collector of sea customs, and of the Madras district, protector of emigrants, superintendent of stamps and stationery, and chairman of the harbour trust board, Jan., 1891; confirmed, Nov., 1891.

AYLING, WILLIAM BOCK, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Weymouth College and Magdalen College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in Madras, 30th Jan., 1889, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

BADCOCK, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Harrow School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1867; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1869; served in the revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant-magistrate, joint-magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating district and sessions judge, and was placed on special duty, as settlement officer, Bhagulpore, Monghyr, and Purneah, July, 1873, and also in the office of the board of revenue, Calcutta, Jan., 1879; officiating inspector-general of registration, March, 1879; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1888.

BADDELEY, WILLIAM LEWIS CLINTON, Major, R.E., Madras Public Works Department. —Joined the department, 23rd July, 1874; assistant engineer, Aug., 1875; executive engineer, May, 1883; deputy chief engineer and under-secretary to government, public works department, Nov., 1886, to March, 1891.

BADEN-POWELL, BADEN HENRY, C.I.E., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at St. Paul's School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; assistant commissioner, May, 1862; officiating postmaster-general, Punjab and Sind, Aug., 1865; from July, 1866, to April, 1869, served as small cause court judge, Lahore, and subsequently in the forest department as officiating conservator and officiating inspector-general, and was placed on special duty, Nov., 1872, and April, 1876; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Umballa, April, 1880; on special duty in Madras, Oct., 1880, to Oct., 1881; additional commissioner, Lahore and Rawulpindi, Oct., 1881; deputy commissioner, 1st class, Dec., 1882; officiating judge, chief court, Punjab, May, 1883, and again April, 1884, and Jan., 1887; divisional judge, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; divisional judge, 1st grade, Sept. 1886, but to continue to act as judge of chief court; confirmed in latter appointment, March, 1889; retired, May, 1889, but employed for a time on special duty under government of India, revenue and agricultural department; has published 'Punjab Products,' 'Pun-

jab Manufactures,' 'Manual of the Land Revenue System and Land Tenures of British India,' and 'Manual of Jurisprudence for Forest Officers.'

BADGLEY, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Colonel, I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commission, April, 1857; arrived in India, May, 1857; served during the mutiny in Central India and Oudh, and afterwards with the Lushai expedition in charge of a survey party (medals and clasp); appointed to the topographical survey, May, 1865, and was employed in Rewah and Bundelcund, and in the Khasi, Garo, and Naga Hills; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, Dec., 1880; in charge of Guzerat survey, March, 1884; officiating assistant surveyor-general in charge of photographic and lithographic offices, March, 1886; retired, June, 1889.

BADOCK, HENRY WALTER, Accounts Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, 11th Feb., 1876; senior clerk, June, 1893.

BAGLEY, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed to the department, 16th Sept., 1871, and was employed on road works in the Central Provinces, and on famine relief works in Bengal; transferred to state railways, and employed in Burma on railway survey and construction; executive engineer, May, 1883; engineer-in-chief, Mandalay-Kunlon railway survey, Dec., 1892.

BAGNELL, HENRY WILLIAM JAMES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Eastbourne College, Keble College, Oxford, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1883; forest settlement officer, Aug., 1887; 2nd assistant collector, May, 1891; also forest settlement officer, Oct., 1893.

BAGSHAWE, CECIL, Forest Department, Hyderabad Administration.—Joined the service as temporary sub-assistant conservator, 26th Oct., 1869; assistant conservator, 14th Aug., 1871; deputy conservator, Oct., 1876; employed in the military department in the Kuram Valley, Aug., 1879, and at the forest school, Dehra Dun, Dec., 1879; conservator, Jan., 1889; conservator of the school circle and director of the forest school, March-Nov., 1890.

BAILEY, FREDERICK, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., late Forest Department, North-Western Provinces.—Arrived in India as a lieutenant, R.E., 12th Nov., 1864; served under the military department till July, 1867, when he was appointed assistant engineer, 1st grade, P.W. department; executive engineer, 3rd grade, June, 1871; from Aug., 1871, served in the forest department as deputy conservator and superintendent of forest surveys; conservator of forests, and director of Indian forest school, in addition to his duties as superintendent of forest surveys, Sept., 1878; promoted to 2nd grade of conservators, April, 1883; retired, April, 1892; is lecturer on forestry at Edinburgh University, and secretary to Royal Scottish Geographical Society.

BAILEY, RICHARD JONES, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed to the department, 25th Oct., 1872, and posted to the military works branch in the Punjab; transferred to Burma, Jan., 1881; executive engineer, May, 1883; in charge Amherst division, Feb., 1886; Tharrawaddy division, Oct., 1892.

BAILLIE, DUNCAN COLVIN, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Inverness Academy, Glasgow Academy, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; assistant settlement officer, 1st grade, April, 1888; officiating collector and magistrate, Sept., 1889; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Sept., 1889; 1st grade, May, 1891; provincial superintendent of census operations, April, 1890, and prepared the report on the provincial census, 1891.

BAINBRIDGE, GEORGE, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.D., M.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., L.S.A., Bombay Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1867; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Khandesh, Feb., 1871; acted as deputy sanitary commissioner; also as ophthalmic surgeon and professor of ophthalmic medicine and surgery and of comparative anatomy, Grant medical college, May, 1878; surgeon-major, April, 1879; acting presidency surgeon, Aug., 1882; acting senior surgeon of Sir Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital, and professor of surgery, Grant medical college, Feb., 1885; civil surgeon, Karachi, Dec., 1891.

BAINES, JERVOISE ATHELSTANE, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Rugby, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived, 12th Nov., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant political agent, acting educational inspector, and cantonment and railway magistrate; on special duty at Bombay as compiler of general administration report, Sept., 1878, and again Sept., 1879; acting under secretary to government, evenue, financial, and general departments, March, 1880; deputy superintendent of census operations in Bombay presidency, and under secretary to government, general department (census), April, 1880; 2nd assistant collector and forest settlement officer, Dec., 1882; on special duty to supervise the work under the income tax act in the Bombay presidency, May, 1886; also acting as director of agriculture from Dec., 1886; 1st assistant collector and magistrate, Oct.-Nov., 1886; acted as junior collector and magistrate, 1887, 1888, and 1889; on special duty, June, 1888; served under the government of India as census commissioner for India from Aug., 1889; on deputation to England to complete census report, May, 1892; on deputation at India office, from July, 1893, and prepared decennial report on moral and material progress of India, 1891-92; C.S.I., Jan., 1894; secretary to royal commission on opium, May, 1894.

BAIRD, ANDREW WILSON, Lieut.-Col., R.E., F.R.S., A.I.C.E., Mint Department, Calcutta.—Lieutenant, R.E., Dec., 1861; arrived in India, March, 1863; served from May, 1864, in the Bombay public works department as assistant engineer, and was employed on the harbour defences and reclamations; executive engineer, Oct., 1867; served in the Abyssinian expedition as assistant field engineer, 1868 (medal); from Dec., 1868, served in the trigonometrical survey as assistant surveyor, and was employed in Kattywar and Guzerat; assistant superintendent,

1st grade, Oct., 1871; in July, 1877, was appointed to the general superintendence and control of tidal observations upon the Indian coasts; on deputation as commissioner, Venice geographical congress and exhibition, Aug., 1881; deputy superintendent, July, 1882; officiating master of the mint, Calcutta, in 1883 and 1886; and at Bombay in 1886, 1888, and 1891; mint master, Calcutta, Aug., 1889; is the author of various contributions to the Bombay Gazetteer, and of papers and reports on tidal observations, including a paper read before the British Association, 1876, and an account of the spirit-levelling operations of the great trigonometrical survey of India, read before the British Association in 1885; joint author of the annual tide tables for Indian ports.

BAKER, EDWARD NORMAN, Indian C.S.—Educated at Christ's College, Finchley; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; services placed at disposal of census commissioner, March, 1881; officiating under secretary, government of Bengal, financial department, in 1882-84, and was appointed to that post, May, 1885; acted as under secretary, government of India, finance and commerce department, Aug. to Nov., 1885; deputy commissioner, June, 1890; officiating deputy-secretary to government of India, finance and commerce department, July, 1892; confirmed, Sept., 1892.

BAKER, FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Berkhamstead Grammar School and Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; first assistant secretary to government, Nov., 1873, and again in 1879; private secretary to lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces, April, 1874; junior secretary, board of revenue, May, 1878, and officiated as secretary, Sept., 1878; officiating junior secretary to government, March, 1881; under secretary to government, May, 1881; superintendent of Dehra Dun, March, 1884; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Aug., 1886; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, March, 1888; magistrate and collector, March, 1889; acting opium agent, Benares, April, 1894.

BAKER, MONTAGU BERNARD, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Winchester, and Pembroke College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1864, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial department as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, acting assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan, and acting judge and sessions judge; acting judicial commissioner in Sind, and judge of the sadar court, April, 1879; 2nd grade judge and sessions judge, Kandeish, May, 1882; 1st grade judge, Nasik, Jan., 1884; retired, June, 1891.

BAKER, OSWALD, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Burma.—Joined the Bengal medical service, 1873; was employed under the military department up to May, 1874, and served subsequently in Burma as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail; medical officer to the mission to Siam under Major C. W. Street,

Feb., 1879; surgeon-major, April, 1885; officiating inspector-general of jails for Burma, May, 1887; joint medical officer, Simla, Sept., 1889; junior civil surgeon, Rangoon, Nov., 1890.

BALTHASAR, GUSTAVE, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1893, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner.

BAMBER, HENRY WILLIAM JOHN, late Police Department, Bengal.—From 1st Oct., 1857, to May, 1862, served as assistant superintendent, and superintendent of salt chowkies; from May, 1862, served in the Bengal police department, and was promoted to district superintendent, 1st grade, Aug., 1885; retired, July, 1891.

BANERJEE, DURGAGATI, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, Bengal Administration.—Joined the service, 21st Aug., 1865, and held various subordinate posts, till April, 1889, when he was appointed deputy collector of Calcutta, superintendent of excise revenue, &c.; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

BANERJEE, GOOROO DASS, M.A., D.L., Judicial Department, Bengal.—Educated at the Presidency College, Calcutta, and Calcutta University; assistant lecturer on mathematics at that college, 1865; law lecturer, Berhampur College, 1866-72; Tagore law professor, 1878; officiating puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 19th Nov., 1888; confirmed, 16th Jan., 1889; member of Bengal legislative council, 1888; vice-chancellor of Calcutta University, 1890-92; author of 'The Hindu Law of Marriage and Stridhan.'

BANKS, SAMUEL HENRY O'BRIEN, F.R.C.S.I., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bombay Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1866, and arrived in India, 17th April, 1867; served in the Abyssinian campaign, and afterwards on civil, military, and jail duty; surgeon-major, Oct., 1878; presidency surgeon, 3rd district, Nov., 1888; presidency surgeon, 2nd district, and marine surgeon, Nov., 1889; brigade-surgeon, Nov., 1889; also in medical charge, Elphinstone college, Jan., 1891; acted as principal medical officer, Karachi, Feb.-April, 1893.

BANNERMAN, PATRICK WILSON, Colonel, I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—Ensign, Bombay army, June, 1852; assistant to the superintendent, Neemuch, July, 1859; assistant to governor-general's agent, Central India, 1860; political assistant, Bhopawar, Aug., 1865; also Bheel agent and commandant, Malwa Bheel corps; political agent, Rewah, March, 1871; political agent, Bhopal, 1880; resident, eastern states, Rajputana, Oct., 1881; resident, Gwalior, March, 1882; acted as agent to governor-general for Central India in 1883-1884, and again in 1887; reverted to military department, 1888.

BANTON, WILLIAM HERBERT PHILIP, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 5th Aug., 1875; clerk specially attached, 26th March, 1893.

BARADA CHARAN MITRA, M.A., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Appointed under native civil service rules as assistant magistrate and collector, 30th March, 1886, on probation; confirmed, 23rd March, 1888; assistant settlement officer, Orissa, March, 1891; acting magistrate and collector, Tippera, Aug., 1893; again employed on settlement work in Orissa, Nov., 1893, and in Tippera, Feb., 1894.

BARBOUR, SIR DAVID MILLER, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 20th Dec., 1863; served in Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; under secretary, government of India, financial department, Aug., 1873; accountant general, Punjab, and deputy commissioner, paper currency, Lahore circle, June, 1877; officiating secretary, government of Bengal, financial department, April, 1878; accountant-general, Madras, and commissioner of paper currency, Jan., 1880; on special duty with the government of India, May, 1881; accountant-general, Bengal, Sept., 1881; officiating secretary, government of Bengal, revenue and general departments, Jan., 1882; Bengal legislative council, Jan. to Oct., 1882; secretary, government of India, department of finance and commerce, July, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; on special duty in India, Sept.-Oct., 1886, and on special duty in England to serve as member of the royal commission on currency in 1886; financial member of council of governor-general, 22nd Nov., 1888 to 24th Nov., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1889; member of royal commission on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, May, 1894; author of 'The Theory of Bimetallism.'

BARCLAY, ERNEST, Judicial Department, Madras.—Served from 18th Sept., 1861, as French interpreter to the supreme court, Madras, acting deputy clerk of the crown and acting assistant registrar, and was appointed government solicitor, Feb., 1876.

BARCLAY, PRINGLE DAVID, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the department, March, 1875, as assistant traffic superintendent, Rajputana state railway; traffic superintendent, Punjab Northern railway, March, 1881; Eastern Bengal railway, June, 1884; officiating manager, in addition, June, 1892.

BARKLEY, DAVID GRAHAM, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1857; arrived in India, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Punjab as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; officiating small cause court judge, Delhi, Sept., 1863; personal assistant to commissioner, Umballa, Nov., 1864; officiating registrar, chief court, Punjab, Aug., 1866; on special duty in the chief court, Nov., 1870, and again at Simla, June, 1871, and at Lahore, April, 1872; assistant sessions judge, March, 1873; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1874; additional commissioner, Delhi, May, 1876, and subsequently at Jallandur and Lahore; officiating judge, chief court, Punjab, Aug., 1878, and again in 1880; civil and sessions judge, Nov., 1880; commissioner and superintendent, Mooltan, Sept., 1881; additional judge, chief court, Jan., 1882; additional member of governor-general's council for making laws and regulations, May, 1883, and again in 1884; judge of the chief court, Punjab, Feb., 1886; retired, Jan., 1887; has published 'Directions to Revenue and Settlement Officers, Non-Regulation Law.'

BARLOW, SIR RICHARD WELLESLEY, Bart., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1855; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Madras, and was appointed acting judge, small cause court, Tellicherry, Nov., 1869; acting civil and sessions judge, Chingleput, Feb., 1870; from 1871 to 1878 served as collector and magistrate of Chingleput, and subsequently as commissioner, Nilgiris, 1878-80, and again in 1881; government member of the Lawrence asylum committee, April, 1878; major commandant Nilgiri volunteer rifles, Dec., 1878, to Nov., 1879; acted as member of the board of revenue in 1880 with charge of the Inam department, and again in 1881, in charge of settlement department, and in 1886; collector and magistrate, and additional sessions judge, Nilgiris, Feb., 1882; collector of sea customs and of the Madras district, and protector of emigrants, Madras, Jan., 1883; acting resident, Travancore and Cochin, Feb. to Aug., 1884; member of the legislative council, Madras, Sept., 1885; chairman of the harbour trust board, May, 1886; superintendent of stamps and stationery, April, 1887, in addition to his other duties; retired, Nov., 1889.

BARNES, FRANCIS CARNAC, Stamps and Stationery Department, Bengal.—Served in the financial department, government of India, from 9th Nov., 1872, and was appointed assistant to accountant general, N. W. Provinces, Jan., 1877; assistant comptroller-general, Calcutta, Nov., 1878; private secretary to lieutenant-governor of Bengal, April, 1882; services retained under the government of Bengal as superintendent of stamps and stationery, April, 1887.

BARNES, HUGH SHAKESPEAR, Indian C.S., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Educated at Malvern College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to financial department of government of India, Dec., 1876, and appointed assistant to accountant-general in the N.W. Provinces; private secretary to the financial member of the governor-general's council, Dec., 1876; acted as under secretary in the home department in 1878; on special duty under the foreign department, Nov., 1879; assistant to resident, Kandahar, Nov., 1880; served in the Afghan campaign, 1881, as political officer with General Wilkinson's brigade; assistant to the governor-general's agent in Baluchistan, 1881-83; political agent in Quetta and Pishin, June, 1883; in charge of arrangements, supplies, &c., for passage of Afghan boundary commission across Baluch Desert, Aug., 1884; officiating under-secretary to government of India, foreign department, April, 1889; and was confirmed in that appointment, May, 1890; officiating deputy secretary in the same department, May, 1890; revenue commissioner, Baluchistan, Dec., 1890; officiated as governor-general's agent and chief commissioner in 1891 and 1892; acting resident, Kashmir, Oct., 1894.

BARR, DAVID WILLIAM KEITH, Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, Sept., 1864; served in Abyssinian expedition (medal); boundary commissioner, Rewa Kantha, and

Malwa Bheel agency, 1869; political assistant and boundary officer, Bhopal, July, 1869; assistant to governor-general's agent, Central India, 1870; officiating political agent, 2nd class, Jodhpur, 1878-79; political agent, Bhagelkhand, July, 1881; also superintendent, Rewa state; resident at Gwalior, May, 1887; officiating resident in Kashmir, Nov., 1892; confirmed, Nov., 1893.

BARRETT, EDWIN CLIFFORD, Survey of India Department.—Appointed probationary assistant surveyor, Indus-Sind revenue survey, 18th Nov., 1862; assistant superintendent, 2nd grade, Dec., 1868; accompanied Lushai expeditionary force, 1871-2 (medal and clasp); assistant superintendent, 1st grade, July, 1882; deputy superintendent, Oct., 1884; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892.

BARRON, ALBERT HENRY, India Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 27th May, 1868, as assistant engineer, and was employed on irrigation work in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Sept., 1875.

BARRON, CLAUD ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Aberdeen Grammar School, Aberdeen University, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

BARROW, FRANK, Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Oudh Commission.—1st commission, lieutenant, 2nd Dragoons, 18th Sept., 1867; admitted to Bengal staff corps, 18th Sept., 1867; from Aug., 1869, served under the government of India, in the foreign department, as assistant commissioner in Oudh, and in 1870 and 1871 officiated as junior secretary to the chief commissioner, and as cantonment magistrate and deputy commissioner for various periods; appointed deputy commissioner, June, 1889.

BARROW, FRANK HUNTER, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Bruton and Felsted Grammar Schools, and Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 17th Jan., 1870; served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1881; magistrate and collector, March, 1887; retired, Aug., 1894.

BARROW, OSCAR THEODORE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Gloucester College School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1877; served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; transferred to financial department, government of India, Dec., 1882, as officiating assistant accountant-general; officiating assistant comptroller of Indian treasuries, Nov., 1886; assistant accountant-general, Bombay, March, 1887; deputy accountant-general, Bombay, April, 1887; officiating accountant-general and deputy commissioner, paper currency, N.W. Provinces, May, 1888; accountant-general, Bengal, April, 1891; accountant-general, Bombay, March, 1894.

BARROW, REGINALD POCOCK, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Oxford University; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India,

13th Nov., 1885, and served in Sind as assistant collector and magistrate, 2nd assistant, and forest settlement officer.

BARRY, THOMAS DAVID COLLIS, Surgeon-Captain, Bombay Educational Department.—Joined the department, 27th May, 1889, as acting chemical analyser, professor of chemistry and medical jurisprudence, Grant medical college, and professor of chemistry, Elphinstone college; confirmed in these posts, April, 1892.

BARRY, WILLIAM, M.R.C.S., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the High School and University of Glasgow, and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 14th Feb., 1863; from Dec., 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; employed on famine duty under the government of Bengal, Dec., 1873, to Jan., 1875; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Banda, Aug., 1877; from May, 1881, served in the judicial department, and was appointed district and sessions judge, 3rd grade, Nov., 1882; 2nd grade, April, 1887; retired, March, 1890.

BARSTOW, HENRY CLEMENTS, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from July, 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating small cause court judge; served under the government of India, foreign department, Jan., 1874, to Oct., 1875, as officiating political agent, 1st class, Bhopal; officiating superintendent, Dehra Dun, Feb., 1876; officiating civil and sessions judge, 2nd grade, Banda, Jan., 1880; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Cawnpore, April, 1880; retired, Nov., 1886.

BARTER, JOSEPH FRANCIS, Brigade-Surgeon, late Madras Medical Department.—Joined the Madras medical service, July, 1859; arrived in India, 27th Jan., 1860; from April, 1862, served in the Central Provinces as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, and acted for various periods as inspector-general of jails and as sanitary commissioner; rank of surgeon-major, July, 1873; sanitary commissioner, superintendent of vaccination, and inspector-general of dispensaries, Central Provinces, Dec., 1878; civil surgeon and superintendent of lunatic asylum, Nagpur, July, 1883; brigade-surgeon, March, 1886; retired, April, 1890.

BARTHOLOMEUSZ, MATHEW LORENZ, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.B., C.M., Bombay Medical Department.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1873, and served on military duty, and as medical officer at Zanzibar; appointed civil surgeon, Sukkur, April, 1884; transferred to Nasik, June, 1888; civil surgeon, superintendent of lunatic asylum, &c., Ahmedabad, Nov., 1889.

BARTHOLOMEW, ROBERT, Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Cavalry, late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 2nd April, 1862; from 1st Aug., 1866, served in the Punjab commission as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; judicial assistant, Sept., 1881; district judge, 1st grade, Nov., 1884, Delhi, officiating as divisional judge; deputy commissioner, July, 1886; retired, April, 1894.

BARTLETT, HUBERT FREDERICK, Indian

C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; transferred to the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Jan., 1880, and served as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, June, 1885; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Aug., 1885; magistrate and collector, July, 1889.

BARTLETT, THOMAS WALTER, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1871, as assistant engineer, and was employed on Rajputana state railway; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; deputy superintendent, way and works, July, 1884; superintendent of works, Bolan railway, April, 1885; officiating engineer-in-chief, Aug., 1886; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway company, Jan., 1888; transferred to Madras, Nov., 1889, and employed on Villupuram-Guntakal and Eastern Bengal state railways.

BARTON, EBENEZER JOHNSTONE, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 10th Nov., 1862; from Sept., 1863, served in the revenue and judicial department, Bengal, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, March, 1882; also served as officiating superintendent of stamps and stationery, Sept., 1871, and was on special famine duty, Bhagulpore division, Dec., 1873; retired, Aug., 1887.

BASANTA KUMAR MULLICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at University College School, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 13th Jan., 1890, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

BACHELOR, STANLEY LOCKHART, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Edmund's College, Ware, and University College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as assistant magistrate and collector.

BATE, THOMAS ELWOOD LINDSAY, Surgeon-Major, Jail Department, Punjab.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1875; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1875; served in military department to Nov., 1880, when he was appointed civil surgeon, Gujrat; officiated as inspector-general of prisons, Punjab, in 1889 and 1890, and was confirmed in that appointment, April, 1891.

BATESON, ROBERT SANDER, Deputy-Surgeon-General, late Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the Indian medical department in 1856; arrived in India, and was attached to military department as assistant surgeon, 7th Aug., 1856; placed in medical charge of the civil station and in executive charge of jail at Umballa, Nov., 1871; civil surgeon, 2nd class, Oct., 1877; brigade-surgeon, Oct., 1882; retired, Sept., 1886.

BATTEN, HALLET GEORGE, Burma Commissioner.—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1869, and on 13th Feb., 1871, was appointed extra assistant commissioner, Burma; assistant commissioner, Nov., 1877, and deputy commissioner, May, 1888; served also as assistant cantonment magistrate and judge of the small cause court in 1886.

BATTEN, JOHN KAYE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Haileybury College, and at Oriel College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in Madras, 29th November, 1887, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; special assistant, Malabar, Nov., 1893.

BATTY, HERBERT, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1870, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, professor of logic and moral philosophy in the Deccan college, Poona (1872-75), assistant judge and sessions judge (1st grade, Feb., 1885), and acting judge and sessions judge; under secretary to government of Bombay, political, judicial, and educational departments, and secretary to the council for making laws and regulations, Nov., 1885; acted as district and sessions judge between 1886 and 1888; acting chief presidency magistrate, Bombay, April, 1888; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, May, 1888, and again in 1890; confirmed, June, 1892; additional member of governor's council, June, 1893.

BAXTER, BERNARD, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 1st Nov., 1871, and employed on state railways in Sind and Baluchistan; transferred to Bengal, April, 1881; executive engineer, May, 1883; employed in Burma, April, 1888; and in Madras, on East Coast railway, from Nov., 1889.

BAYLAY, CHARLES ALLEN, Colonel, I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—Lieutenant, Bengal Artillery, Dec., 1857; assistant commissioner, Berar, Jan., 1866, and subsequently in Ludhiana; political assistant, Rajputana, 1868-70; assistant general superintendent for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, Dec., 1870; political agent, Haraoti and Tonk, 1876; additional political agent, 1st class, Kotah, Dec., 1879; acted as resident, western states of Rajputana, in 1884; political agent in Kotah, 1888; retired, May, 1890.

BAYLEY, CHARLES STUART, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Harrow and Heidelberg; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 31st Dec., 1877; assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal, January, 1878; officiating assistant secretary to chief commissioner of Assam, July, 1880; officiating under secretary, government of Bengal, general and revenue departments, March, 1881, and subsequently in the financial department, Oct., 1882; member of the executive committee, Calcutta international exhibition of 1883-84, Aug., 1883; officiating under secretary, government of India, revenue and agricultural department, Feb., 1884; officiating registrar, high court, March, 1884; under secretary, government of India, revenue and agricultural department, April, 1885; assistant commissioner, Ajmere, Sept., 1886; political agent, Bikanir, June, 1888; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Jan., 1889; officiating political agent, Bikanir, Dec., 1890; confirmed, Sept., 1891; officiating general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti department, April, 1894.

BAYLEY, LYTTLETON HOLYOAKE, B.L., Judicial Department, Bombay.—Called to the bar

(Middle Temple), May, 1850; appointed advocate-general, Bombay, 17th March, 1866; judge of the high court, Bombay, June, 1869; acting chief justice, May-Dec., 1883, Nov., 1887, to Feb., 1888, June-Sept., 1892, and from May, 1894.

BAYLEY, SIR STEUART COLVIN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury; arrived in India, 4th March, 1856; assistant magistrate and collector, 24 Pergunnahs, Nov., 1856; junior secretary to government of Bengal, Feb., 1863; officiated as secretary to the government of Bengal in 1865, 1867, and 1871; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Monghyr, Feb., 1867; officiating civil and sessions judge, Shahabad, May, 1867; commissioner, Dacca division, July, 1873, and subsequently of the Patna division, Sept., 1873; secretary, government of Bengal, May, 1877; additional secretary, government of India, financial department, Aug., 1877; personal assistant to H.E. the viceroy, for famine affairs, Sept., 1877; additional secretary, government of India, public works department, famine branch, in addition to his own duties, Dec., 1877; secretary, government of India, home department, June, 1878; officiating chief commissioner, Assam, June, 1878; officiating lieutenant-governor of Bengal, July-Nov., 1879; chief commissioner of Assam, June, 1880; resident, 1st class, Hyderabad, March, 1881; governor-general's council, 9th May, 1882; lieutenant-governor of Bengal, April, 1887; retired, Dec., 1890; appointed secretary, political and secret department, India office, Dec., 1890; received the thanks of Lord Rosebery and Lord Kimberley for services in connection with the Burma-China frontier and trade convention of 1st March, 1894; also thanked by Lord Kimberley and Mr. Fowler for services in the delimitation of British and Italian spheres of influence in Somaliland, May, 1894.

BAYLY, WILLIAM GOODENOUGH, B.A., late India Public Works Department.—Appointed to the accounts branch as assistant controller, 28th July, 1868; deputy controller, Nov., 1871; assistant examiner, April, 1872; deputy examiner, July, 1877; examiner, Jan., 1882; examiner, public works accounts, Burma, Feb., 1889; retired, Aug., 1893.

BAYNE, CHARLES GERWIEN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Uppingham, and University College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1880, and served in British Burma as assistant commissioner, and officiating assistant secretary to chief commissioner; junior secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1886; under secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1887, and officiating secretary to chief commissioner, Feb., June, and Aug., 1888; deputy commissioner, 4th grade, Nov., 1888; junior secretary to chief commissioner, Dec., 1890; secretary to chief commissioner, March, 1891; officiating chief secretary, March-July, 1892; revenue secretary, March, 1893.

BEACHCROFT, CHARLES PORTER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Rugby, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

BEACHCROFT, FRANCIS PORTEN, B.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated

at Rugby, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 12th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; judicial assistant, April, 1878; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Dec., 1883; divisional judge, 3rd grade, Nov., 1884; 2nd grade, April, 1889; 1st grade, April, 1891; officiating judge of chief court, Punjab, April to Nov., 1890, and from May, 1891; retired, Nov., 1891.

BEADON, CECIL, Colonel, Madras Cavalry, late Punjab Commission. — Arrived in India, 24th March, 1858; from May, 1862, served in the Punjab commission as assistant commissioner, cantonment magistrate, and judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, March, 1877; on special duty in connection with viceregal durbar, Nov., 1880; officiated as commissioner and superintendent at various periods; also officiated as divisional judge, Dera Ismail Khan, July to Oct., 1889; reverted to military department, Oct., 1893.

BEAMAN, FRANK CLEMENT OFFLEY, Indian C.S. (Bombay). — Educated at Bedford Grammar School, and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 22nd Dec., 1879, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting assistant superintendent of police; acting assistant to the political agent, Kathiawar, March, 1882, to Jan., 1883, and again 1885-86; acting junior collector and magistrate and political agent, March-April, 1883; from April, 1883, served in the judicial department, and was appointed acting judicial assistant to political agent, Kathiawar, May, 1884, and 3rd grade assistant judge, May, 1885; special settlement officer, Baroda, May, 1886, and Nov., 1887; acting district and sessions judge, Oct., 1887; 2nd grade assistant judge, April, 1890; judicial assistant to the political agent in Kathiawar, May, 1891.

BEAMES, JOHN, late Bengal C.S. — Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 16th March, 1858; from March, 1859, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and from Dec., 1861, in Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, July, 1867; officiated as commissioner, Orissa division, Aug., 1873, and subsequently as commissioner of the Chittagong, Rajshahye, and Cooch Behar and Dacca divisions; commissioner, Burdwan division, Nov., 1881; officiating member of board of revenue, March, 1887, and April, 1889; commissioner, Bhagulpore, Aug., 1887; president of the police revision committee, Aug., 1890; commissioner, Presidency division, Nov., 1891; resigned, March, 1893; author of 'Outlines of Indian Philology,' 1867, 'Comparative Grammar of the Modern Aryan Languages of India,' 1872-79, and 'Grammar of the Bengali Language,' 1891; edited Sir H. M. Elliot's 'Races of the N.W. Provinces of India,' 1869.

BEAN, MAURICE, Captain, R.I.M., Port Officer, Bombay. — Joined the service as assistant port officer, Bombay; officiating port officer, Madras, April, 1891; port officer, Bombay, October, 1891.

BEARD, HENRY GOODLAKE, Store Department, India Office. — Educated at the City of

London School; writer, India store depôt, 5th June, 1868; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

BEATSON, JOHN FULLARTON, Surgeon-General, B.A., M.D., C.I.E., late Bengal Medical Service. — Educated at the University of Glasgow; licentiate of the Royal College of Surgeons of Edinburgh; assistant surgeon, Bengal, 16th June, 1843; served with the army of the Sutlej, 1845-46, taking part in the defence of Ferozepore, and battle of Ferozeshah (medal and clasp); served in the Punjab Campaign, and was present at various actions (medal); surgeon, June, 1857; in charge of the Allahabad general field hospital during the period of the Mutiny; surgeon-major, June, 1863; garrison surgeon, Allahabad, 1865-68; medical storekeeper, Umballa, 1868-69; deputy inspector-general, Meerut, May, 1869; surgeon-general, Nov., 1875; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; retired, March, 1880.

BEATTY, JOSEPH, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras. — First commission as lieutenant, Dec., 1852; arrived in India, 6th Jan., 1855; served in the Madras public works department from March, 1855, as assistant engineer, and executive engineer; supervisor of the canals, Godavari, May, 1874; superintending engineer, 3rd grade, Aug., 1877; promoted to 1st grade, Oct., 1878; retired, May, 1889.

BEATTY, THOMAS BERKELEY, Surgeon-General, M.D., late Bombay Medical Service. — Educated at Trinity College, Dublin (A.B. and M.B.); joined the Bombay medical service, May, 1851; arrived in India, 27th June, 1851; civil surgeon, Karachi, July, 1853; superintendent of vaccination, April, 1856; acting civil surgeon, Poona, May, 1866, and again in 1872; superintendent, Matheran, Dec., 1867; deputy surgeon general, Poona, Oct., 1876; principal medical officer, Malta expeditionary force, May to Sept., 1878; surgeon-general with the government of Bombay, April, 1880; retired, March, 1885; is the author of various contributions on medical subjects in the Transactions of the Medical and Physical Society, Bombay.

BEAUMONT, THOMAS, Deputy Surgeon-General, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., late Madras Medical Department. — Arrived in India, 13th Sept., 1857; served under the military department till Feb., 1864, when he was appointed residency surgeon, Indore; residency surgeon, Hyderabad, June, 1881; also superintendent of vaccination and superintendent of medical school; retired, Oct., 1885.

BEAUMONT, WILLIAM, late Revenue Survey Department, Madras. — Served in the Madras revenue survey department from 27th May, 1858, as assistant superintendent, and deputy superintendent; reorganisation committee, Madras, March, 1871; promoted to 1st class deputy superintendent, June, 1877; board of examiners, Nov., 1882; retired, Nov., 1887.

BEAVAN, REGINALD, Colonel, I.S.C., late Survey of India Department. — Ensign, 4th May, 1860; arrived in India, 11th June, 1860; served in the military department, and in Sept., 1867, was appointed to the staff corps; in July, 1868, joined the topographical survey department as assistant surveyor; served with various survey parties, and in July, 1880, organised and took charge of the Baluchistan topographical survey; deputy superintendent, 4th grade, Nov., 1884, and 3rd grade, June, 1889; retired, June, 1892; in the military department served in Southern Afghanistan,

from Oct., 1878 (medal); accompanied the Marri field force and the Bozdar field force in 1880 and 1881.

BECHER, ALFRED READ, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined, 23rd July, 1868; arrived in India, 14th Oct., 1868; served in the Punjab as assistant engineer on the Sirhind canal; from April, 1873, served in the accounts branch, Punjab, as assistant controller, and deputy controller; transferred to Bombay, Nov., 1875; served subsequently in Hyderabad, Oudh, Rajputana, and Mysore; examiner of accounts, Calcutta, June, 1886; examiner, Sept., 1886; officiated as deputy accountant-general and under secretary to government of India in 1888 and 1889, and from Oct., 1890; confirmed, July, 1893; reverted to examiner, Dec., 1893.

BECKETT, HENRY BARRON, Punjab Commission.—Served from 10th May, 1860, in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating inspector of schools, officiating deputy commissioner, and judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, Peshawur, Aug., 1884; officiated as divisional judge, Dec., 1884, and again in 1886 and 1890; and was appointed district judge, 1st grade, Oct., 1885, and deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Aug., 1886; 1st grade, May, 1889; officiated as commissioner in 1891.

BEDFORD, CHARLES AUGUSTUS S., Bengal Non-Regulation Commission.—Joined the service, 25th July, 1864, as assistant commissioner; civil assistant political officer, Lushai expeditionary force, Feb., 1889; deputy commissioner, April, 1891.

BEDFORD, JAMES PERCH, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Clifton College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in Madras, 14th Dec., 1889, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

BEESTON, ROBERT CHAPMAN, India Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1873, and was employed on state railways in Central India and Burma; executive engineer, Feb., 1881; on special duty in Upper Burma in 1885-86; employed on Kashmir railway survey, Feb., 1891; and on North-Western line, from Jan., 1893.

BEGBIE, ALFRED GLYNN, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Department.—1st commission, July, 1861; arrived in India, Nov., 1870; joined the public works department, Feb., 1871, as assistant engineer in the railway department, Central India; executive engineer, Oct., 1871; Agra-Gwalior railway survey, Nov., 1872; officiating deputy controller of accounts, Bengal, Aug., 1874; officiated as examiner, guaranteed railway accounts, at Calcutta and Bombay, 1874-75; controller of accounts, Mysore, Aug., 1875; examiner guaranteed railway accounts, Calcutta, 1878; Punjab Northern railway accounts, 1880; deputy accountant-general, and ex officio under secretary to government of India, Jan., 1886; officiating accountant general, public works department, and deputy secretary, government of India, April, 1886, to March, 1887; examiner, 1st class, May, 1886; officiating accountant-general and deputy secretary to government of India, public works department, May to Oct., 1887, and Sept., 1888; confirmed in those appointments, Oct., 1889; retired, July, 1893.

BEHARI LAL GUPTA, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Colootola Branch School, Calcutta, Calcutta University, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 29th Sept., 1871; assistant magistrate and collector, Nov., 1871; on famine relief duty, Sept., 1874; presidency magistrate, Calcutta, and coroner of Calcutta, June, 1881; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1888.

BEIGHTON, THOMAS DURANT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King Edward's School, Birmingham; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived 4th Dec., 1867; served in Bengal in the revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating small cause court judge, officiating district and sessions judge, and officiating magistrate and collector; on special duty in connection with boundary disputes on the Bhutan frontier, Jan., 1878; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1878; district and sessions judge, May, 1885; author of 'The Ethnography of the Bhutan Frontier.'

BELCHAMBERS, ROBERT, Judicial Department, Bengal.—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1848; deputy registrar, Calcutta high court, Feb., 1851; registrar, Oct., 1862; is also keeper of records, taxing officer, accountant-general, and sealer; author of 'Practice of the Civil Courts,' and other legal publications.

BELL, CHARLES ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1891, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

BELL, HENRY JAMES, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Gordon's College, Aberdeen, Aberdeen University, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

BELL, HORACE, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed probationary assistant engineer, 7th July, 1862; served in the Central Provinces and Central India, and was promoted to executive engineer, 1st grade, March, 1871; subsequently served on the Punjab Northern state railway, and on the Rajputana state railway, as superintendent of way and works, and as acting manager, also as officiating engineer-in-chief on the Sindia and Neemuch state railways; superintending engineer, 3rd grade, Jan., 1880; transferred to Bengal, Nov., 1881; engineer-in-chief, Dacca and Mymensingh railway surveys, Dec., 1881; while on furlough superintended assistant engineers on practical course in England; superintending engineer, 2nd class, Jan., 1883; on special duty, Aug., 1883, on committee for considering reorganisation scheme for public works department; manager and engineer-in-chief, Tirhoot state railway, Feb., 1884; held charge Nalhati railway, July-Sept., 1884; superintending engineer, 1st class, March, 1886; acting director, North-Western railway, July to Oct., 1887; engineer-in-chief and manager, Tirhoot state railway, Oct., 1887, to March, 1888; engineer-in-chief, Great-Western of India railway survey, Sept., 1888, and of the Moghal Sarai

Howrah railway survey, Feb., 1880; consulting engineer to the government of India for railways, Aug., 1890; chief engineer, Oct., 1890; officiating director-general of railways, and deputy secretary, Jan. to April, 1893, when he reverted to his post of consulting engineer; retired, June, 1894; author of 'Laws of Wealth,' a work on political economy for native readers, and of 'Railway Policy in India.'

BELL, JAMES RICHARD, M.I.C.E., Railway Branch, Government of India.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, and served as assistant engineer, executive engineer, and superintendent of works in various projects; received the special thanks of the government of India in connection with the construction of the Ferozepore bridge, 1887; engineer-in-chief, Hyderabad-Umarkot railway survey, Jan., 1890; in charge of Mari extension of the Sind-Sagar branch of the North-Western railway, Feb., 1890; superintending engineer, 3rd class, Oct., 1890; 2nd class, Nov., 1890; 1st class, June, 1892; officiating consulting engineer to the government of India for state railways, July-Oct., 1891; on special duty, Oct.-Dec., 1891; engineer in chief of the Mari-Attock extension railway and frontier railway surveys, Dec., 1891; engineer-in-chief, East Coast railway, May, 1893; consulting engineer to the government of India for state railways, June, 1894.

BELL, JOHN MACKENZIE, M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1867, and was appointed assistant engineer; executive engineer, Sept., 1877.

BELL, MATTHEW DENNY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Bedford Grammar School, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1881, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; acting senior assistant collector, Ganjam, March, 1887; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Nov., 1889; acting district and sessions judge, May, 1893, and again, March, 1894.

BELL, NICHOLAS DODD BEATSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Clifton Bank, St. Andrews, Edinburgh Academy, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1889, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

BELL, WILLIAM, M.A., Educational Department, Punjab.—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1885, as principal, central training college, Lahore; professor in the government college there, March, 1888; acting principal, June to Nov., 1891; confirmed in that appointment, April, 1892.

BELLETT, GEORGE, M.A., late Education Department, Bengal.—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1861; served successively as 2nd master, head master, and professor, Dacca college; inspector of schools, Nov., 1868; professor, Presidency college, Dec., 1873; principal, Berhampore college, May, 1875; 2nd class, educational service, March, 1876; subsequently served as officiating principal, Patna college, officiating principal, Presidency college, and officiating director of public instruction (Jan., 1883); inspector of schools, Nov., 1881; 1st class, educational service, June, 1887; retired, April, 1893.

BELLINGHAM, HORACE COHEN, Financial Department, India Office.—Writer, India office,

18th May, 1875; clerk specially attached, Sept., 1892.

BENNETT, WILLIAM CHARLES, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough College, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner, 3rd class, and assistant settlement officer, and was appointed to take up land for railway purposes in Fyzabad, Feb., 1870; manager of encumbered estates, Gonda, March, 1872, to Feb., 1875; officiated as city magistrate, Lucknow, in 1878 and 1879; officiating secretary to government, Oudh revenue department, Sept.-Nov., 1879; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Jan., 1882; director of agriculture and commerce, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April, 1891; secretary, board of revenue, May, 1886; officiating secretary to government, revenue and judicial departments, June, 1886; confirmed, Nov., 1888; officiated as chief secretary in 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; secretary in judicial department, April, 1889; member of lieutenant-governor's legislative council, Feb., 1889, to Dec., 1891; author of 'History of the Chief Clans of the Rae Bareli District' and contributions to the Oudh Gazetteer.

BENGER, ALFRED, Auditor's Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, 20th Nov., 1866; junior assistant, June, 1867; senior examiner, July, 1877; designation changed to senior clerk, Oct., 1892.

BENNETT, JOHN, M.D., Brigade-Surgeon, late Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the Indian medical department, 1865; arrived in India, 26th Sept., 1865; services placed at disposal of civil department, Sept., 1870; civil surgeon, Murree, Dec., 1872; superintendent of vaccination, Punjab, July, 1877; superintendent-general of vaccination, Punjab, March, 1880; deputy sanitary commissioner, eastern circle, Dec., 1881, to April, 1884; civil surgeon, Mooltan, Jan., 1883; also superintendent of central jail, Dec., 1883; civil surgeon, Rawalpindi, April, 1884; civil surgeon, 1st class, Sept., 1884; medical adviser to the Maharaja of Patiala, Aug., 1885; retired, Sept., 1890.

BENSLEY, EDWIN CLEMENT, Surgeon-Major, late Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1859; served from 22nd July, 1863, as civil assistant surgeon at Midnapore and Rajshahye; civil surgeon, Rajshahye, Oct., 1875; rank of surgeon-major, Feb., 1871; retired, Nov., 1885.

BENSON, CHARLES, M.R.A.C., Madras Revenue Department.—Joined the service, 30th Jan., 1874, as assistant superintendent, Saidapet farms; assistant director of agriculture, April, 1884; assistant commissioner of land records and agriculture, April, 1887; assistant director of that department, Nov., 1887; deputy director of agriculture, Jan., 1892.

BENSON, RALPH SILLERY, M.A., LL.B., B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Rathmines School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1873, and served as assistant collector and magistrate (Salem), acting head assistant collector and magistrate of Cuddapah during the famine of 1876-78, acting assistant commissioner and acting commissioner (Nilgiris), 1878 to 1881, and acting collector and

magistrate; was specially appointed as officer for the settlement of lands in the Nilgiri districts; head assistant to the collector and magistrate, North Arcot, June, 1882; on special duty as secretary to the local self-government committee, Oct., 1882; forest settlement officer, May, 1883, and secretary to the committee for drafting new municipal and local board bills; acting under-secretary to government in the chief secretary's department, Jan., 1885, and again in Oct., 1887; acting registrar, high court, July, 1885; acting secretary to the commissioners of land revenue, Nov., 1887, to April, 1888; sub-collector and joint magistrate and acting district and sessions judge, April, 1888; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1889; author of the settlement registers and descriptive memoirs of the Nilgiri district, and of the 'Report on the Settlement of the Nilgiri District,' 1885.

BENSON, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Tavistock Grammar School, Marlborough College, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant settlement officer, manager of the Awa estate under the court of wards, officiating district and sessions judge, and officiating magistrate and collector; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Oudh, Oct., 1883; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Nov., 1883; district and sessions judge, April, 1887.

BENTINCK, BARON JOHN, Punjab Commission.—Educated at Leyden University (doctor of Roman and Dutch law); joined the service, 6th Dec., 1869, as assistant commissioner; officiated as magistrate at various periods; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1892.

BENTON, ALEXANDER HAY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the University and King's College, Aberdeen; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1861; arrived in India, 20th Feb., 1864, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Punjab as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; judicial assistant, Nov., 1876; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1879; divisional judge, Nov., 1884; judge of chief court, Punjab, Nov., 1889; retired, July, 1894.

BENTON, JOHN, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrigation work in the Punjab; executive engineer, Oct., 1881.

BERESFORD, JOHN STUART, M.E., M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as assistant engineer, N.W. Provinces, 26th July, 1867; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; had charge of various divisions of Ganges canal; personal assistant to chief engineer, irrigation, May, 1877, and from Nov., 1884, to March, 1885; employed on Betwa canal in 1884; superintending engineer, Dec., 1889; joint secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Aug., 1892; secretary to chief commissioner, Central Provinces, public works department, March, 1893.

BERESFORD, LORD W. LESLIE DE LA POER, Colonel, K.C.I.E., V.C., 9th Lancers.—First commission, as cornet, June, 1867; served in the Jowaki-Afreedee expedition, 1877-8 (medal

and clasp); served also in the Zulu war of 1879, and was present at battle of Ulundi (Victoria Cross, medal, and clasp); served in the Afghan war, 1878-80, and was present at the capture of Ali Musjid (medal and clasp); A.D.C. to viceroy of India, 1875 to 1881; military secretary on the personal staff of the viceroy and governor-general, Dec., 1881 to Dec., 1893; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1894; served with the Burmese expedition of 1886, as military secretary to governor-general (brevet of lieut.-colonel and clasp).

BERKELEY, JAMES CAVAN, C.I.E., Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, Madras infantry, Oct., 1857; served in civil employ from June, 1862, as assistant to the governor-general's agent in Central India; also served as boundary settlement officer, deputy opium agent in Malwa, assistant general superintendent for thagi and dakaiti, and sessions judge and registrar for Mhow; political agent, Haraoti and Tonk, 1873; political agent, Bhagalcund, Jan., 1879, to Nov., 1882; political agent, Jacobabad, 1882; officiating resident, Gwalior, March, 1883; on special duty, Kashmir, April-Aug., 1884; political agent, Bundelcund, 1884; officiating resident, Nipal, 1885; resident, 2nd class, and governor-general's agent at Baroda, April, 1886; reverted to military department, Nov., 1887.

BERNARD, SIR CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S., Revenue and Statistics Department, India Office.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 12th Feb., 1858, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and was appointed under-secretary to government of India, financial department, Aug., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the Central Provinces as judge of the small cause court, Nagpur, settlement officer, secretary to the chief commissioner (July, 1864), commissioner of Nagpur division (Feb., 1869), and acting judicial commissioner; from May, 1871, to Jan., 1875, served in Bengal as member of the lieutenant-governor's council, and acting secretary to the government, first in the judicial department, then in the revenue department, and lastly in the scarcity and relief department; from Nov., 1876, served as commissioner of Nagpur division; secretary to Sir Richard Temple, Bart., K.C.S.I., deputed on a special mission to the Bombay and Madras Presidencies, Jan., 1877; judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, Aug., 1877; additional secretary to the government of India in the department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, Aug., 1877, and in the financial department, Dec., 1877; served as secretary to the government of India in the home department, May, 1878, to July, 1880; secretary to government of India in revenue and agricultural department, July, 1881; from July, 1880, acted as chief commissioner of British Burma, and was confirmed in this appointment, April, 1882; retired, March, 1888, and was appointed secretary in the revenue and statistics department, India office; in 1893 edited Sir George Campbell's 'Memoirs.'

BERNARD, JAMES HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Clifton College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1882; arrived in India, 30th Dec., 1884; assistant magistrate and collector, Midnapore, Jan., 1885; personal

assistant to the chief commissioner, British Burma, June, 1886; services replaced at disposal of government of Bengal, Feb., 1887; officiated as under secretary to government of Bengal in 1887; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1891.

BERRILL, EDWARD, Police Department, N.W. Provinces.—Joined the service as adjutant, military police, Central Provinces, 14th Aug., 1858; inspector of police, N.W. Provinces, 27th Feb., 1861, and assistant inspector-general, Dec., 1861; acted for various periods as district superintendent, and was appointed to that post, May, 1867; assistant inspector-general, railway police, Oct., 1885, and of the special branch, June, 1888; in addition district superintendent of police, Jan., 1890; junior deputy inspector general of police, March, 1893; served in the Mutiny in Barrow's volunteer cavalry, July, 1857 to April, 1858.

BEST, JAMES WILLIAM, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Wimborne Grammar School, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived, 8th Dec., 1862; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and sub-collector and joint magistrate in various districts; from Jan., 1875, served in the judicial department, and was appointed district and sessions judge, South Canara, Sept., 1877; officiated as judge of high court, Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, Chingleput, Jan., 1891; officiating judge of the high court, Feb., 1892; confirmed, March, 1893.

BEVAN, JAMES FORBES, Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as assistant, 21st June, 1866; arrived in India, 14th Sept., 1866, and served on various lines in Bengal and the Punjab; superintendent, 19th June, 1880; held charge of the Indore, Rajputana, Arakan, Bengal, Burma and Bombay divisions; in charge, check office, June, and Oct., 1891; officiating director, construction branch, Sept., 1891; in charge, Bombay division, Jan., 1892.

BEVERIDGE, HENRY, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1857; arrived in India, 20th Jan., 1858; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating deputy commissioner, Cooch Behar, and magistrate and collector (2nd grade, April, 1874); on special duty in Manipore under the government of India, foreign department, Nov., 1863; from Oct., 1874, served in the judicial department as officiating district and sessions judge, and was appointed district and sessions judge, March, 1875; resigned, Jan., 1893; author of a 'History of Backergunge,' and 'The Trial of Nandakumar.'

BEVERLEY, HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Sedbergh School, Yorkshire, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived 31st Oct., 1861; from May, 1862, served in the revenue and judicial department as assistant magistrate and collector, deputy commissioner, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; officiating under secretary, government of Bengal, July, 1865, and Aug., 1867; registrar-general of

assurances, March, 1867; officiating junior secretary, board of revenue, Dec., 1870; inspector-general of registration, Dec., 1870; superintended the first census of Bengal in 1872; superintended the census of Calcutta, 1876; officiating judge of Nuddea, April, 1875; officiating inspector-general of jails, Lower Provinces, Aug., 1876; district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, and additional judge, 24-Pergunnahs and Hooghly, Dec., 1877; member of the prison conference, 1877; member of the census committee, 1878; officiating commissioner of police, Calcutta, and chairman of the corporation, March, 1880; special census officer of Calcutta and suburbs, Dec., 1880, and published a report on the census, 1881; to officiating superintendent and remembrancer of legal affairs, Nov., 1883; member, Bengal legislative council, Jan., 1884; member of sanitary commission, Calcutta, Sept., 1884; officiated as judge of the high court, Calcutta, 9th April, 1884, to 15th Sept., 1884, and again from 12th Jan., 1885; confirmed, 8th May, 1885; has published a 'Registration Manual' and 'The Land Registration Act.'

BHAGWAN CHUNDER CHAKARBUTTI, late Judicial Department, Bengal.—From 9th Oct., 1861, served in the subordinate judicial service, Bengal, and was appointed subordinate judge, 1st grade, May, 1884; retired, Nov., 1891.

BIHARAT SINGH, Kunwar, Statutory C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Appointed probationer under native civil service rules, 16th Feb., 1880; confirmed, March, 1882; served as assistant magistrate and collector and assistant commissioner; joint magistrate, March, 1892.

BIDDULPH, CUTHBERT EDWARD, M.A., Political Department, Government of India.—Educated at Westminster School and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service as probationary assistant settlement officer, Sind, 19th Jan., 1878; services placed at disposal of agent to governor-general for Baluchistan, Nov., 1878; assistant to the political superintendent, Sawant Wari, in charge of the Sir Dasai, Feb., 1880; from Aug., 1880, to June, 1881, served in the commissariat department; assistant survey and settlement commissioner, Baroda, Nov., 1883; revenue settlement officer, Meywar, Dec., 1886; assistant commissioner, Berar, Feb., 1891; acting cantonment magistrate, Secunderabad, March, 1892, to Oct., 1893; served during the Afghan war of 1878-80 in political charge at Dadur in Kutchi in 1878-9, of Sibi district in 1879, and in commissariat charge of Bolan Pass, 1880 to 1881 (medal); author of 'Our Western Frontier of India,' 'Afghan Politics,' 'Afghan Poetry of the 17th Century,' 'Army Reforms in Native States,' 'Four Months in Persia,' &c.

BIDDULPH, JOHN, Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—Cornet, Bengal cavalry, Jan., 1858; arrived in India, 4th March, 1858; served with various cavalry regiments, and received the mutiny medal; aide-de-camp to the Viceroy, May, 1872, to Feb., 1877; on special duty as member of mission to Yarkand, &c., 1873-74; employed on a secret mission to the countries beyond Gilgit, 1876 (brevet of major); on special duty in Gilgit, Sept., 1877; acting agent to governor-general, Baluchistan, May-Nov., 1882; political agent, Bhopawur, Dec., 1882; acted as resident and boundary settlement officer in Meywar, 1885;

political agent in Haraoti and Tonk, April, 1886; resident 2nd class and commissioner, Ajmere, April, 1890; officiating agent in Baluchistan, July, 1891; officiating resident, Gwalior, April, 1892; agent, Baroda, Jan., 1893; author of 'Tribes of the Hindukush.'
