COCKERELL, HORACE ABEL, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1853; served as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and magistrate and collector; also as superintendent of survey, Sept., 1859, and as officiating chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, and commissioner of police, March, 1869, and Dec., 1872; commissioner of the Chittagong division, June, 1872, and subsequently of the Dacca and Burdwan divisions; secretary to the government of Bengal, judicial and political departments, April, 1878; lieutenant-governor's council, April, 1878, and again, March, 1880, and 1882; board of revenue, March, 1883; officiating lieutenant-governor of Bengal, Aug., 1885; retired, Aug., 1887.

COCKS, WALTER CAREW, late Auditor, India Office.—Appointed, 1st Aug., 1874; retired, Dec. 1894.

CODRINGTON, GEORGE HENRY FORBES, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 4th May, 1857; served on military duty till Dec., 1865, and subsequently in the Bombay police department as assistant superintendent, and district

superintendent, and was promoted to 1st grade superintendent, April, 1883; reverted to military department, 1892.

COGHLAN, ROBERT NESBIT, Customs Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 23rd April, 1871; from March, 1872, served as general manager of the customs' bonded warehouses, Bombay, and as acting assistant commissioner of customs, salt, and opium, in charge of the tobacco department, Bombay; from March, 1874, served as assistant collector of salt revenue; collector of sea customs, Karachi, and assistant commissioner of salt revenue in Sind, Sept., 1882; collector of customs and salt revenue in Sind, March, 1891.

COLDSTREAM, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating small cause court judge, officiating personal assistant to financial commissioner, officiating registrar, chief court, Punjab, and officiating deputy commissioner; judicial assistant, July, 1876; deputy commissioner, March, 1877; officiating commissioner and sessions judge, Lahore, April, 1880; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Umballa, Sept., 1883; deputed to Calcutta to act as one of the representatives of the Punjab in the committee on museum and exhibition held there, Dec., 1883; officiating commissioner, Hissar, April to Nov., 1884; Lahore, Feb. to July, 1887; retired, June, 1894; author of a work on the grasses of the Southern Punjab.

COLE, WILLIAM HAMMOND, M.A., late Survey of India Department.—Served in the education department, N. W. Provinces, April, 1862, to March, 1864, as professor of mathematics at the government college, Agra; from Jan., 1867, served in the great trigonometrical survey of India as assistant surveyor attached to the computing office, Dehra; assistant superintendent of surveys, Sept., 1869; deputy superintendent, July, 1882; appointed to charge of head quarters office, Dehra Dun, and survey detachment in Kashmir, Jan., 1884; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, Nov., 1884; 2nd grade, Oct., 1890; retired, April, 1892; author of various contributions to the 'Account of the Operations of the Great Trigonometrical Survey of India,' and to the 'Synopsis of the Results of the Operations of the Great Trigonometrical Survey,' also of various translations relating to geodesy.

COLEBROOK, HENRY WILLIAM VAUGHAN, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, May, 1883; transferred to Burma, April, 1894, and placed in charge of central irrigation division.

COLES, GEORGE EDWARD, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, May, 1883.

COLLEN, SIR E. H. H., K.C.I.E., Maj.-Gen. I.S.C.—First commission, royal artillery, July, 1863; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, May, 1873; 3rd assistant secretary to government of India, military department, March, 1875; 2nd

assistant secretary, April, 1876; 1st assistant secretary, Dec., 1876; deputy secretary, military department, April, 1884; accountant general, military department, government of India, July, 1886; secretary to government of India, military department, Nov., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1893.

COLLIER, FRANK ROBERT STANLEY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King's College and London University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1872; served in the revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector and officiating cantonment magistrate; on famine duty, March, 1874; officiating collector of customs, Calcutta, May, 1884; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1886; magistrate and collector, April, 1889; member of Bengal legislative council, July, 1893; published an annotated edition of the Bengal Municipal Act, 1884, another of the Bengal Local Self-Government Act and Rules, 1886, and the Bengal Municipal Manual, 1887.

COLLIER, GEORGE HERMAN, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Uppingham School; junior clerk, India office, 17th Feb., 1876.

COLLIN, EDWARD WOODFIELD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Eton, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1878; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; private secretary to financial member of governor-general's council, Jan., 1880; served under the government of India, foreign department, Jan. to July, 1881, and in the financial department, March to Sept., 1885; settlement officer, Mozufferpore, Jan., 1886; officiated as director of land records, Bengal, April, 1888; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1889; on special duty in 1889 and 1890.

COLLINS, ARTHUR HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 1st Jan., 1879, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Aug., 1889; has also officiated as district and sessions judge.

COLLINS, SIR ARTHUR JOHN HAMMOND, Knt., Q.C., Madras High Court.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge, of which he was fellow; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1860; Q.C. and bencher of his inn, 1877; recorder of Poole, 1873-9, and of Exeter from 1879, until he was appointed chief justice of the high court of judicature, Madras, 28th Nov., 1885; vice-chancellor, Madras University, 1889.

COLLINS, SIDNEY ARTHUR, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, 4th Dec., 1885.

COLQUHOUN, ARCHIBALD ROSS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Department.—Entered the public works department in Oct., 1871, as apprentice engineer; served in the grades of assistant and executive engineer in various divisions of Burma; on special duty as personal assistant to Major Street on his mission to Siam, Jan.-May, 1879; executive engineer, 3rd grade, Nov., 1881; was posted to special duty in Upper Burma as civil officer, and appointed a deputy

commissioner, Feb., 1886; retransferred to public works department, and posted to Baluchistan, Aug., 1889; services lent to British South African Company, 1891-92; retired, Nov., 1893; author of 'Across Chryse,' 'Among the Shans,' 'The Truth about Tonquin,' 'The Opening of China,' 'Burma and the Burmans.'

COLVIN, SIR AUCKLAND, K.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Eton; appointed to Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1857; arrived in India, 17th Jan., 1858; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant settlement officer; officiating under secretary to the government of India in the home department, May, 1864, and in the foreign department, Jan. to Nov., 1865; settlement officer, 2nd grade, July, 1869; secretary, board of revenue, N.W. Provinces, March, 1870; on special duty to Naini Tal, in connection with the revision of settlement in the Allahabad district, June, 1870; officiating secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, April, 1873, and again in 1875; officiating commissioner of excise and stamps, and inspector-general of registration, June, 1873; member of commission for investigating disturbances in Poona and Ahmednagar, Aug., 1875; magistrate and collector, Basti, Feb., 1877, and afterwards at Bijnor; officiating commissioner of inland customs under the government of India, Nov., 1877; transferred for employment in Egypt, Jan., 1879, and served as controller of Egyptian finance, and subsequently as finance minister to the Egyptian government; acted as consul-general in Sir E. Malet's absence; financial member of council of the governor-general of India, Oct., 1883; lieutenant governor of the N.W. Provinces, and chief commissioner of Oudh, Nov., 1887, to Nov., 1892; resigned the service, Dec., 1892.

COLVIN, BAZETT WETENHALL, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Eton; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1850; arrived in India, 29th June, 1850; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector till Jan., 1854, and subsequently in the N.W. Provinces, and was appointed assistant commissioner, Kumaon, April, 1855, magistrate and collector, Mainpuri, July, 1865, and civil and sessions judge, Allahabad, July, 1873; officiated as commissioner of the Jhansi division, April, 1873, and of the Rohilkhand division, Nov., 1873; on special duty at Agra during visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Feb., 1876; officiating secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, May, 1876; commissioner, Meerut division, Jan., 1879; additional member, governor general's council, Jan., 1877, to May, 1881; member, board of revenue, N.W. Provinces, Jan., 1862; retired, April, 1885.

COLVIN, CLEMENT SNEYD, Public Works Department, India Office.—Educated at Brighton College, and by private tuition in England and on the Continent; temporary clerk, 1863; junior clerk, military department, 1864; funds department, 1867; private secretary to secretary of state (the Duke of Argyll), 1873-74; private secretary to permanent under secretary of state, 1874-77, and accompanied Sir Louis Mallet on his deputation to India in 1875-76; senior clerk, revenue, statistics, and commerce department, 1877; resident clerk, 1877-85; public works department, 1877; government director of Madras irrigation and canal company, 1880-82;

assistant secretary in the public works, railway and telegraph department, Sept., 1882; assistant government director of Indian railway companies, 1882; secretary, public works department, March, 1892.

COLVIN, ELLIOT GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Charterhouse, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived, 4th Jan., 1883; assistant magistrate and collector, Jan., 1883; transferred to Assam as personal assistant to chief commissioner, May, 1884; acting assistant secretary to chief commissioner, June, 1884; assistant political agent, Rajputana, Sept., 1886; political assistant, 3rd class, May, 1888; private secretary to lieutenant governor of Bengal, April, 1887; 1st assistant to governor general's agent in Baluchistan, and political assistant, 2nd class, April, 1889; served under financial department of government of India from June, 1890; officiating postmaster-general, Bengal, July, 1890; joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1891.

COLVIN, JOHN RUSSELL COLQUHOUN, Captain, I.S.C.—Joined the service, 13th Aug., 1879, as lieutenant, 76th Foot, and was employed under the military department to August, 1886, including service in the Eastern Soudan, 1885; appointed assistant to the agent in Rajputana, Aug., 1886; private secretary to lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Nov., 1887; governor to H.H. the Nawab of Rampur, April, 1889; resident political officer, Rampur state, April, 1894.

COMINS, DENIS WOOD DEANE, M.D., Surgeon-Major, Bengal Jail Department.—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1881, and served on general and jail duty, and as officiating first resident surgeon, medical college hospital, Calcutta; officiated as protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Sept., 1888, and was confirmed, Jan., 1889; from Jan., 1891, served under the government of India, finance and commerce department; appointed inspector-general of jails, Bengal, March, 1892.

CONDER, JAMES, late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Joined the public works department, Sept., 1870; executive engineer, Neemuch railway, Oct., 1873; Punjab Northern state railway, 1874; personal assistant to engineer-in-chief, Punjab Northern railway, June, 1875; provisional assistant director of state railways, western system, 1877; traffic superintendent, Indus Valley state railway, in 2nd class of the state railway revenue establishment, May, 1878; officiating manager, southern section, Western Rajputana railway, Aug., 1879; manager, Holkar, Sindia-Neemuch, and Neemuch-Nusseerabad railways, Feb., 1881; officiating manager, Nagpur-Chhattisgarh and Wardha coal railways, Nov., 1883; traffic superintendent, Rajputana-Malwa railways, Sept., 1884; class I., grade 3, revenue establishment, April, 1887; retired, 1890.

CONDON, JAMES HUNT, M.D., Brigade-Surgeon, late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, Feb., 1859; arrived in India, 25th July, 1859; served under the military department till Oct., 1861, when he was appointed civil assistant surgeon in Oudh; from April, 1868, served in the N.W. Provinces, and was promoted to surgeon, Feb., 1871, and surgeon-major, July, 1873; civil surgeon and superin-

tendent of jail, Cawnpore, March, 1871; brigade surgeon, Oct., 1886; retired, March, 1891.

CONINGHAM, HENRY EVELYN, Major-General, I.S.C., late Police Department, Madras.—Arrived in India, 18th March, 1855; served in the Madras police department from Dec., 1861, as assistant superintendent and superintendent, and acted as deputy inspector-general, Sept., 1883, to May, 1884; 1st grade superintendent, Nov., 1884; retired, Feb., 1886.

CONNAN, WILLIAM, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 22nd Dec., 1868, and posted to Bengal; executive engineer, Aug., 1879; officiating under-secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, Aug., 1888; and again, May, 1889; officiating superintending engineer, Orissa irrigation circle, April, 1891.

CONNAUGHT AND STRATHEARN (DUKE OF), H.R.H. ARTHUR WILLIAM PATRICK ALBERT, K.G., K.T., K.P., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., K.C.B., P.C., General.—First commission, 19th June, 1868; assistant adjutant-general, Gibraltar, Oct., 1875; brigadier-general, Aldershot, Sept., 1880; accompanied the expeditionary force to Egypt, Aug., 1882, and was present at the battles of Mahuta, and Tel-el-Kebir (medal with clasp, 2nd class Medjidie, and C.B.); commander-in-chief, and 2nd member of council, Bombay, 14th Dec., 1886, to March, 1890; on return to England was appointed to command of southern division, Aug., 1890; commanding at Aldershot, Oct., 1893.

CONNEMARA (BARON), RIGHT HON. ROBERT BOURKE, G.C.I.E., P.C.—Educated at Enniskillen Royal School and at Trinity College, Dublin; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, Nov., 1852; M.P. for Lynn Regis from Dec., 1868; parliamentary under secretary of state for foreign affairs, Feb., 1874, to April, 1880; a royal commissioner on the laws relating to copyright, Oct., 1875; privy councillor, April, 1880; again parliamentary under secretary for foreign affairs, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; appointed governor of Madras, Sept., 1886; took his seat as governor, 7th Dec., 1886; created a peer, 12th May, 1887; resigned, 1st Dec., 1890.

CONNOLLY, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (unjab).—Educated at Belvedere College, Dublin, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 6th Nov., 1890, and served as assistant commissioner in the Punjab; officiating under-secretary to government, April, 1893; confirmed, March, 1894.

CONOLLY, EDWARD ROBERT, Colonel, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 9th April, 1858; from Sept., 1866, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating cantonment magistrate; on special duty as assistant to Major Cavagnari, Feb., 1879, and again at Khyber, Feb., 1880; judicial assistant, 2nd grade, Sept., 1883; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, Bannu, Nov., 1884; also district judge; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Feb., 1887; retired, Aug., 1890.

CONSTABLE, EDWIN THEOBALD, M.A., late Educational Department, North-Western Provinces.—Joined the service as mathematical professor, Bareilly College, 25th Feb., 1862; inspector of public instruction, 1st circle, Aug., 1870; inspector of schools, Meerut division, Dec., 1877;

Benares division, Dec., 1882; officiating director of public instruction, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Feb., 1883; 1st grade of education officers, April, 1883; inspector of schools, Meerut, and inspector of European schools, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Sept., 1884; officiating director of public instruction, April, 1890; retired, Sept., 1891.

CONSTABLE, WILLOUGHBY VERNER, Major, R.E., India Public Works Department.—First commission, 12th Sept., 1872; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; assistant manager, Rajputana state railway, July, 1882; employed in military department, on Suakim-Berber railway, 1885; officiating deputy consulting engineer for railways, Bombay, Aug., 1885; confirmed, Nov., 1886; executive engineer, Feb., 1887; officiating manager, Bhavnagar, Gondal and Morvi railways, April-June, 1887; deputy agent, Bengal-Nagpur railway company, 1887-89; officiating deputy consulting engineer for railways, and under secretary to government of Madras, public works department, Feb., 1891; transferred temporarily to superior revenue establishment, as officiating deputy manager, North Western railway, April, 1893; confirmed, Nov., 1893.

CONWAY-GORDON, LEWIS, C.I.E., Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 1857; joined the public works department, May, 1862, and served in the Punjab; assistant principal, Roorkee College, 1865; assistant consulting engineer, railway department, and examiner of accounts, Punjab, Oct., 1866; deputy consulting engineer and assistant secretary, railway branch, Punjab, Oct., 1869; executive engineer, 1st grade, Punjab, Sept., 1870; deputy consulting engineer to government of India for guaranteed railways, 1871; examiner of guaranteed railway accounts, Calcutta, April, 1872; officiating superintending engineer, Indus Valley railway, March, 1874; controller of accounts, Madras, 1875-79; 1st class examiner, Jan., 1879; officiating manager, Indus Valley railway, April, 1881; deputy accountant-general, public works department, and ex officio under secretary to government of India, Sept., 1881; acted as accountant-general and deputy-secretary, May to Aug., 1883; deputed on special duty to England in 1884, to give evidence before the select committee of the House of Commons on railways in India; temporarily manager, Eastern Bengal state railway, 1884; manager, North-Western railway, Jan., 1886; director, North-Western railway, March, 1887; director-general of railways and deputy secretary to government of India, public works department, May, 1887; retired, May, 1892.

CONYBEARE, HENRY CRAWFORD ARTHUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Westminster School, King's College, London, and Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 3rd Nov., 1873, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector and officiating magistrate and collector; on special duty for preparation of provincial gazetteer, July, 1877; joint magistrate, Jan., 1885; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1890; magistrate and collector, Aug., 1891; author of 'An Account of Pargana Dudhi, in South Mirzapur,' and of contributions to the provincial Gazetteer.

COODE, MONTGOMERY PENROSE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Hyderabad; executive engineer, Jan., 1885; transferred to Burma, Oct., 1889; assistant to chief engineer and officiating under secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1894.

COOK, HENRY, M.D., Surgeon-Colonel, late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical service, 31st March, 1865; arrived in India, 31st Aug., 1865, and served as civil surgeon at various stations, superintendent of vaccination, and superintendent of jail; acted as second surgeon, Jamsetji Jijibho hospital, and professor of anatomy and curator of museum at the Grant medical college, 1879-80, and as senior surgeon and professor of surgery, 1881-82; civil surgeon, Dharwar, Dec., 1881; civil surgeon, Poona, with medical charge of the Deccan college and college of science, and superintendent of lunatic asylum, April, 1883; also superintendent and teacher of medicine and therapeutics at the medical school; acted as deputy sanitary commissioner, Oct., 1884; brigade-surgeon, April, 1887; deputy-surgeon-general, Sept., 1890; reverted to military department, 1891.

COOK, HENRY, M.D., F.R.C.P., F.G.S., F.M.S., Deputy Surgeon-General, late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical service, 24th Jan., 1855; arrived, 28th July, 1855, and served under the military department up to 1859, also holding the civil surgery of Karachi in 1856; served with the Persian expeditionary force in 1857 (medal); appointed medical officer to political agency, Khelat, April, 1859; and accompanied H.H. the Khan of Khelat and Sir H. Green on a military expedition into Mekran; from April, 1861, served as civil surgeon in Ahmednagar and Satara, and as superintendent of Mahabaleshwar, and assistant collector and magistrate at that station; scientific officer, Abyssinian field force, Dec., 1867 (medal); from June, 1871, served in Bombay as acting 2nd physician, Jamsetji Jijibho hospital, and acting professor of physiology and of hygiene at the Grant medical college, and was confirmed in these appointments, May, 1873; 1st physician, Jamsetji Jijibho hospital, principal and professor of medicine and hygiene, Grant medical college, Sept., 1876; brigade surgeon, Nov., 1879; deputy surgeon-general, June, 1884; retired, Nov., 1886; author of various papers published in the transactions of the Medical and Physical Society of Bombay, on the geology, topography, and meteorology of different portions of Baluchistan, and on other scientific and medical subjects; also of various reports on similar subjects published by order of government, and of a paper read before the British Association on the registration of atmospheric ozone in Bombay, with reference to epidemic cholera, 1869.

COOK, HENRY DAVID, M.B., C.M., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service as assistant surgeon, 1st April, 1870; arrived in India, 22nd Dec., 1870, and served on military, general and jail duty; surgeon-major, April, 1882; surgeon, 1st district, and medical inspector of emigrants, Madras, May, 1888.

COOK, JOHN HOME, Madras Revenue Survey Department.—Joined the service, 23rd Jan., 1858,

as probationary assistant-superintendent; confirmed, July, 1876; promoted to deputy-superintendent, May, 1887.

COOKE, CRAWFORD BOYD, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—First commission as ensign, March, 1864; served under the military department up to Dec., 1868, and subsequently as assistant commissioner in Burma; acted as assistant secretary to chief commissioner, Jan., 1871, to Feb., 1874; from Feb., 1874, to May, 1877, served as political agent at Bhamo, Upper Burma, and from May, 1879, to Jan., 1884, as cantonment magistrate in Nowgong, Bundelcund; from Jan., 1884, served as deputy commissioner and officiating commissioner in Burma, and was appointed to special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; commissioner, Oct., 1890; author of the 'British Burma Manual.'

COOKE, FREDERICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Bath College and Oriel College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; transferred temporarily to Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, Nov., 1891.

COOKE, GORDON HENRY, Survey of India.—Joined the survey department, 6th Dec., 1866, and was appointed assistant superintendent, May, 1871; in 1871-72, served with the Lushai expeditionary force (medal); on special duty, Chittagong, Nov., 1872; served in various districts, and at head-quarters, Calcutta, and was appointed deputy superintendent, Oct., 1885.

COOKE, HENRY GIRAUD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 12th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1881; magistrate and collector, June, 1886; officiating commissioner, Dacca, June, 1892; officiating commissioner, Orissa, and superintendent, tributary mahals, Nov., 1892.

COOKE, JOHN EDWARD, Financial Department, Government of India.—From Oct., 1863, to April, 1868, served as assistant secretary to the government of India in the financial department; officiated as deputy accountant-general, first in Bengal and then in Hyderabad, and as accountant-general in Bengal, 1868-69; deputy accountant-general, Central Provinces, Sept., 1870, and in Madras, Jan., 1874; officiating accountant-general, Madras, Aug.-Oct., 1874; assistant comptroller-general, May, 1875; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, Oct., 1878; deputy-accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, July, 1883; acted as accountant general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, in 1884; as deputy auditor-general, Dec., 1885; and as comptroller, Indian treasuries, April, 1886; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, Oct., 1888; deputy auditor-general, April, 1891; officiating accountant-general, Madras, Aug.-Sept., 1893.

COOKE, SAMUEL, M.A., F.I.C., F.G.S., A.M.I.C.E., Educational Department, Bombay.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1868, and served in the Bombay educational department from that date as professor of chemistry and geology at the engineering college, Poona, and also acted as principal of the college for various periods;

promoted to 3rd grade of the education department, June, 1875; 2nd grade, Dec., 1890.

COOKE, THEODORE, C.I.E., M.A., M.Eng., LL.D., F.G.S., F.L.S., late Educational Department, Bombay.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; arrived in India, Nov., 1860; from June, 1865, served in the Bombay educational department as principal of the civil engineering college, Poona, and also officiated as lecturer on chemistry and geology; 1st grade of the educational department, March, 1883; acting director of public instruction in 1884, 1889, and from April, 1892; also as director of land records and agriculture in 1891; retired, Oct., 1893, and was appointed technical sub-director, Imperial Institute.

COOPER, CHARLES PHILIP, Barrister-at-Law, Police Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 28th Jan., 1863; served from June, 1864, as examiner of the insolvent debtors' court, Bombay; also acted as master and registrar in equity, commissioner for taking accounts, and taxing officer in the high court; police magistrate, Bombay, from Oct., 1871; acting first judge of the small cause court, Sept., 1873; senior magistrate of police and revenue judge, Bombay, June, 1874; chief presidency magistrate and revenue judge, Bombay, June, 1878.

COOPER, HARRY ALBERT, B.A., Audit Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, accountant-general's department, 3rd June, 1880; junior examiner, audit department, Nov., 1881; graduated B.A., London University, 1887; designation changed to junior clerk, Oct., 1892.

COPELSTON, FREDERICK SELWYN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Marlborough College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Burma, as assistant commissioner, Sept., 1874; on special duty in Rangoon, Nov., 1879; on forest demarcation duty, Tounghee, Jan., 1880; deputy superintendent of census, Sept., 1880; judge under the Land Acquisition Act, March, 1881; officiating deputy commissioner, Dec., 1881; commissioner of excise and stamps, March, 1885; additional sessions judge, Tenasserim and Pegu, March, 1886; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1887; commissioner, Dec., 1890; officiating judicial commissioner, Upper Burma, June, 1894.

CORBETT, FRANK VINCENT, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1866; assistant engineer, Feb., 1877; served on canal duty, and was appointed executive engineer, March, 1871; employed chiefly on the Betwa canal; superintending engineer, Dec., 1889; chief engineer and secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, irrigation branch, June, 1894.

CORDERY, JOHN GRAHAM, M.A., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1855; arrived in India, 22nd Oct., 1856; served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner, and officiating small cause court judge; from Oct., 1864, to Sept., 1868, served in the Hyderabad Assigned Districts as deputy commissioner

and assistant resident; officiating director of public instruction, Punjab, Jan., 1872, to May, 1873; deputy commissioner, 1st class, March, 1877; officiating secretary to government of Punjab, April-Aug., 1878; commissioner and superintendent, Multan, April, 1879; also acted as political agent and superintendent, Bahawalpur state, May-Nov., 1879; commissioner, Peshawur, April, 1881; resident, Hyderabad, April, 1883; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; retired, July, 1888.

CORDNER, ROBERT ALLEN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 20th Feb., 1868, and employed on irrigation works in N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, March, 1871; employed temporarily on famine relief works in Bengal, 1873-74; member of accounts committee, Nov., 1888; since Feb., 1889, has officiated as superintending engineer in charge of various irrigation circles.

CORNISH, ROBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Lancing College, and Oriel College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1870; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; on famine relief duty, 1874; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1882; magistrate and collector, Dec., 1887.

CORNISH, WILLIAM HENRY, Bengal Police Department.—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1859; served in the military department to May, 1863, when he joined the police department as assistant superintendent; district superintendent, April, 1870; 1st grade, Oct., 1890; served in Assam, 1870-74, and 1879-83.

CORNISH, WILLIAM ROBERT, Surgeon-General, F.R.C.S., C.I.E., late Madras Medical Service.—Studied at St. George's Hospital (scholar and prizeman); appointed to the Madras medical service, April, 1854; arrived in India, 2nd July, 1854; served under the military department, and also as civil surgeon of Coimbatore, 1857-58; secretary to the director general of the medical department, Oct., 1858, to Feb., 1870; consulting physician to the Irrigation Canal Company, April, 1860; on special duty at Calcutta as Madras member and secretary to the commission for the reorganization of medical establishments, Nov., 1865, to April, 1866; secretary of Madras medical fund, 1864-70; on special duty for revision of medical code of regulations, 1869-70; sanitary commissioner, Madras, March, 1870, to April, 1880; member of committee for review of general hospital establishments, 1873-74; on special duty as member of the cinchona alkaloid commission in 1874 and 1879; during the famine of 1876-78, was specially engaged in organising a system of sanitary and medical inspection; surgeon-major, April, 1874; was superintendent of Madras census, and proceeded on special duty to Calcutta as member of census committee, Jan.-Feb., 1878; brigade-surgeon, Nov., 1879; appointed C.I.E., Jan., 1880; deputy surgeon-general, April, 1880; surgeon-general with the government of Madras, 1880 to 1885; was member of a commission to draw up a code for famine relief, and member and president of a committee on local government; additional member of council, Madras, for making laws and regulations, Dec., 1883; retired from the service, April, 1885; was appointed fellow of

Madras University, Feb., 1867, and president of the medical faculty, 1880-85; hon. physician to the Queen; author of various contributions to the medical press, of sanitary reports to government, and the Madras census report, 1873; was for some time editor of the 'Madras Quarterly Journal of Medical Science.'

CORNWALL, WILLIAM WOLFAN GARDNER, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1861; from Dec., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating superintendent of Dehra Dun, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and assistant settlement officer; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Fatehpur, March, 1881; and afterwards at Farukhabad; retired, Aug., 1887.

COSTELLO, CHARLES PETER, F.R.C.S., Surg.-Col., late Bengal Medical Department.—Joined the service as assistant surgeon, 10th Feb., 1859; served with the 6th Punjab infantry in the Umbeyla campaign, 1863-4 (medal and clasp); and in the Jowaki expedition, 1877-8 (clasp); served also in the Afghan war, 1878-80, with the Koorum field force, and the Khyber line force (medal); sanitary commissioner, Assam, Sept., 1888; principal medical officer and sanitary commissioner, Assam, June, 1892; retired, Jan., 1894.

COTGRAVE, THOMAS MANSON, Salt Department, Bombay.—Joined the service as acting assistant commissioner of customs, salt and opium, 30th Jan., 1866; opium inspector, Bombay, May, 1866; confirmed as assistant commissioner, July, 1867; assistant collector, salt revenue, Feb., 1874; deputy collector, salt revenue, April, 1890.

COTTON, SIR ARTHUR THOMAS, General, Kt., K.C.S.I., R.E.—Joined Addiscombe, 1818; arrived in Madras, Sept., 1821, as 2nd lieutenant of engineers; assistant to superintending engineer, tank department, May, 1822; served with the Burmese expedition, 1824-26; from June, 1826, served as superintending engineer in the tank department, and in 1828 was appointed to the charge of the Cauvery irrigation, being also employed in the survey and improvement of the Paumben pass; in 1844 was appointed to the northern division of the tank department, and was employed in carrying out the systematic irrigation of the Godaveri delta; promoted to chief engineer, 1852; in 1858 was ordered to Cuttack, and planned works for the irrigation of the Mahanadi delta; was afterwards employed under the government of Bengal, reporting upon the water communication between the Ganges and Calcutta; resigned government service in 1862; in 1863, was employed under the Madras irrigation company, reporting on their works, and also inspecting the Ganges canal works, and proposed additional works, which were afterwards carried out as the Lower Ganges canal; also projected works for the Sone irrigation, which have since been partially executed; general, 1876; retired from the army in 1877.

COTTON, HENRY JOHN STEDMAN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Magdalen College School, Brighton College, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1865;

arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1867; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial department, and was appointed officiating small cause court judge, Sealdah, March, 1872; officiating under secretary, government of Bengal, June, 1873; officiating registrar, high court, Dec., 1873; assistant secretary, government of Bengal, June, 1875; junior secretary, government of Bengal, April, 1876; magistrate and collector, 3rd grade, Nov., 1878; officiating commissioner, Chittagong, Sept., 1879, and again, April, 1884; secretary, board of revenue, Jan., 1882; officiating commissioner of police, Calcutta, and chairman of the corporation of the town of Calcutta, April, 1887; port commissioner, Calcutta, May, 1887; secretary to government of Bengal, financial and municipal departments, May, 1890; acting chief secretary, Dec., 1891; member of lieut.-governor's council, June, 1892; confirmed as chief secretary, July, 1892; author of a 'Memorandum on the Revenue History of Chittagong,' 1880, 'Memorandum on the Land Tenures of Bengal,' 1884, 'England and India,' 1883, and 'New India, or India in Transition,' 1885.

COTTON, JULIAN JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Sherborne School, and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 24th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

COUCH, RIGHT HON. SIR RICHARD, Knt.—Recorder of Bedford, 1858 to 1862; judge of the high court of judicature at Bombay, 1862-66; chief justice, Bombay, 1866-70; chief justice of Bengal, March, 1870, to April, 1875, and was president of the commission appointed to inquire into the charges made against the Gaekwar of Baroda, Feb., 1875; made a privy councillor, and sworn in, 27th Nov., 1875; member of the judicial committee, Jan., 1881.

COUCHE, THOMAS BENSON, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, 22nd Jan., 1875.

COUCHMAN, MALCOLM EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Leamington College, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

COUPER, SIR GEORGE E. W., Bart., K.C.S.I., C.B., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Sandhurst, and served in H.M.'s 15th regiment for two years before joining the East India College, Haileybury; appointed to the Bengal civil service, 1846; served in Bengal and the Punjab in the grades of assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; under secretary, government of India, home and finance department, 1853; under secretary, government of India, foreign department, 1854; secretary to chief commissioner of Oudh, 1855; served through the siege of Lucknow, and was present at several engagements during the Mutiny as aide-de-camp to Sir H. Lawrence and Sir John Inglis, and afterwards to Sir James Outram; commissioner in Oudh, 1858; secretary to government of N.W. Provinces, 1859; judicial commissioner of Oudh, 1863; chief commissioner of Oudh, 1874; lieutenant-governor of N.W. Provinces, 1876, as well as chief commissioner of Oudh; retired, 1882.

COURTENAY, ROBERT, B.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1874; served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate and political agent; compiler of the 'General Administration Report of the Bombay Presidency for 1883-84'; acted as assistant judge, first grade, at intervals from Dec., 1885; retired, Sept., 1890.

COURTNEY, DAVID CHARLES, Major, R.E., Store Department, India Office.—Entered the Royal Engineers, 17th April, 1866; served in the South African war, 1879, and was present at the battle of Ineyazane, defence of Etshowe and advance on Ulundi (medal with clasp); assistant-inspector of works, Royal Arsenal, Woolwich, 1880-83; served in the Soudan expedition of 1884-85, and was present at the action of Kirbekan (brevet of major, medal with two clasps, bronze star); retired from army, November, 1887; appointed assistant superintendent of the India store depôt, 1st April, 1887.

COWAN, SAMUEL HUNTER, Colonel, I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commission, lieutenant R.A., 9th Dec., 1859; arrived in India, 9th April, 1860; served in the military department, and on the 19th Jan., 1867, was admitted to the staff corps; assistant superintendent, survey department, Bengal, 19th Jan., 1867; held charge of the Cachar survey, Nov., 1878, to Nov., 1879; and of the Hooghly River survey, April, 1882, to July, 1883; deputy superintendent, April, 1883; 3rd grade, July, 1888; 2nd grade, March, 1891; retired, Oct., 1891.

COWIE, DONALD WILLIAM GARDEN, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Charterhouse and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1883; arrived, 30th Nov., 1886, and served as assistant to collector and magistrate; acting deputy registrar, high court (appellate side), May, 1891; head assistant collector, Jan., 1892; acting assistant secretary, judicial and legislative departments, May to Nov., 1892; acting registrar, high court (appellate side), Jan.-April, 1894.

COWIE, HENRY GEORGE, B.A., Financial Department, Government of India.—Educated at Pembroke College, Oxford; from 25th Nov., 1868, to April, 1876, served in the grades of assistant accountant-general and assistant comptroller-general, and as assistant commissioner of paper currency, in the N.W. Provinces, and at Calcutta; from April, 1876, to March, 1883, served in the Central Provinces as deputy accountant-general and deputy commissioner of paper currency; comptroller, Assam, March, 1883; deputy accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, 1887; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, June, 1889; and Madras, Jan., 1890; deputy comptroller-general, March, 1893.

COWIE, THOMAS RENNY, Colonel, I.S.C., Oudh Commission.—Arrived in India as ensign, 12th Aug., 1860; employed under the military department till Aug., 1869, from which date he served in Oudh as assistant commissioner, officiating cantonment magistrate, sub-judge, and deputy commissioner; cantonment magistrate, 1st class, Allahabad, Feb., 1881, and subsequently at Cawnpore, 1886; deputy commis-

sioner, 3rd grade, Oct., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1891.

COWLEY, FRANCIS WILLIAM RICE, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863; served in the revenue and judicial department, as assistant magistrate and collector and joint magistrate and deputy collector; services placed at the disposal of the public works department, March, 1873; deputy commissioner, wards' estate, Patna, June, 1875; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1878; from April, 1879, served in the judicial department as district and sessions judge; officiating judicial commissioner, Chota Nagpore, May, 1886, and again in Aug., 1887; confirmed, Sept., 1887.

COX, ARTHUR FREDERICK, Indian C.S.—Educated at King's College School and King's College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1871, and served in Madras as assistant magistrate and collector, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; on special duty in connection with visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Nov., 1875; from Sept., 1882, served in the financial department, and was appointed officiating assistant accountant-general, first in Madras and afterwards in Bombay; assistant comptroller-general, 3rd class, July, 1883; deputy accountant-general, Bombay, April, 1885; officiating accountant-general, Bombay, and commissioner of state paper currency, May, 1886; sub-collector and magistrate, Nov., 1887; and acting district and sessions judge, Oct., 1887; confirmed as district and sessions judge, April, 1889; employed under government of India, in financial department, May, 1889, as acting accountant-general, Bombay; confirmed, Dec., 1889; officiating comptroller and auditor general, March, 1894; compiled 'The North Arcot District Manual.'

COX, HARRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Olave's Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 28th Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1889; has also acted as district and sessions judge.

COX, HENRY WILLIAM HOLMES, Colonel, I.S.C., Police Department, Madras.—Ensign, Madras Infantry, Nov., 1857; arrived in India, 26th Dec., 1857; served in the Madras police from April, 1861, and was promoted to superintendent, April, 1873; assistant inspector-general, Dec., 1881; acting commissioner of police, Madras city, September, 1883; presidency magistrate, Oct., 1883; political officer in charge of ex-King Thebaw of Burma, Dec., 1885, to April, 1886; deputy inspector-general of police, Aug., 1886; acted as inspector-general in 1890; commissioner of police, Madras city, Dec., 1891.

COX, JOHN HUGH, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Clifton College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

COXE, HENRY REYNELL HOLLED, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1832; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1834, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Burma, Nov., 1837, as assistant commissioner; under secretary to chief commissioner, Nov., 1838; resumed appointment in Bengal, April, 1891; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1893.

CRADDOCK, REGINALD HENRY, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Reading Grammar School, Wellington College, and Keble College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1832; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1834; supernumerary assistant commissioner, Central Provinces, Dec., 1834; assistant to commissioner of settlements and agriculture, Dec., 1838; assistant commissioner, Nagpur, Sept., 1839; also settlement officer, Nov., 1890; commissioner of excise, April, 1893; deputy commissioner, June, 1893.

CRAIG, JAMES, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed assistant engineer, 19th July, 1866; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; officiating superintending engineer and secretary to the agents to the governor-general for Rajputana and Central India, April-Oct., 1891; officiating superintending engineer and secretary to the agent for Baluchistan, April, 1892; superintending engineer, June, 1892; employed in Burma from March, 1893.

CRANBROOK (EARL OF), Rt. HON. GATHORNE GATHORNE-HARDY, G.C.S.I., P.C.—Born, 1814; educated at Shrewsbury School, and at Oriel College, Oxford (B.A. in 1836, 2nd class in classics); called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1840, and practised as a barrister for several years; contested Bradford unsuccessfully in 1847; sat in the House of Commons for Leominster from 1856 to 1865, when he was returned for Oxford; under secretary of state for the home department, 1858; president of the poor law board, July, 1866; secretary of state for the home department, May, 1867, to Dec., 1868; secretary of state for war, Feb., 1874; raised to the house of peers as Viscount Cranbrook, May, 1878; secretary of state for India, March, 1878, to May, 1880; lord president of council, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886, and again, Aug., 1886, to Aug., 1892; created Earl of Cranbrook and Baron Medway, Sept., 1892.

CRASTER, GEORGE AYTON, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, North-Western Provinces.—First commission as lieutenant, 11th Dec., 1849; served under the military department till 1853; from Nov., 1853, served as assistant and executive engineer in Assam, Oudh, and the Punjab; promoted to executive engineer, 1st grade, April, 1865; transferred to N.W. Provinces as officiating superintending engineer, March, 1869; officiating superintending engineer and deputy secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, public works department, April, 1872; superintending engineer, 3rd class, April, 1872; promoted to 1st class, March, 1881; retired, 1886.

CRAWFORD, CLEMENT EDWARD, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after

open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 10th Nov., 1892, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

CRAWFORD, JAMES ADAIR, Indian C.S.—Educated at Rugby; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 17th Sept., 1878, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from Feb., 1881, served in the political department, and acted as assistant to the governor-general's agent for Rajputana, boundary settlement officer in Meywar, and assistant to the resident at Hyderabad (June, 1881); attaché, foreign department of government of India, May, 1882; in charge of office of press commissioner, June, 1882, to Nov., 1883; acting political agent, 3rd class, and assistant commissioner, Ajmere, April, 1884; assistant secretary, foreign department, Jan., 1885; political agent, Quetta and Pishin, Nov., 1885, to March, 1886; junior under secretary to government, foreign department, March, 1888; political agent, 2nd class, and acting first assistant and secretary for Berar to resident at Hyderabad, May, 1890; under-secretary to government of India, foreign department, April, 1892; acted as deputy secretary in 1892; on special duty in connection with the revision of Aitchison's 'Treaties,' Oct., 1892; and again with the Archduke of Austria during his tour in India, 1892-93; officiating political resident, Persian Gulf, July-Dec., 1893.

CRAWFORD, RICHMOND IRVINE, Colonel, I.S.C., Sind Commission.—1st commission, Oct., 1857; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1857; served in the pursuit of the Gwalior rebels in 1858 (medal and clasp for Central India); from Sept., 1864, served in Sind as deputy collector and magistrate, and (from April, 1867) as superintendent of police; from May, 1873, acted as political superintendent of Thar and Parkar, and was confirmed in this appointment, May, 1875; deputy commissioner, Thar and Parkar, Aug., 1881; collector and magistrate, Shikarpur, June, 1885; and subsequently at Karachi; acted as commissioner in Sind in 1891, 1892, and 1893.

CRAWFURD, JAMES, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at the Collegiate School and University of Glasgow, and Dublin University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1868; assistant magistrate and collector, Jan., 1869; officiating under secretary, government of India, home department, Feb., 1875; under secretary, government of Bengal, July, 1876; first inspector of registration offices, Dec., 1877; registrar, high court, Nov., 1878; district and sessions judge, March, 1885; retired, Nov., 1894.

CRAWLEY, CHARLES EDWARD, Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the department, and was attached to the accountant general's office, Bombay, 25th Feb., 1874; acted as assistant accountant general, Madras, May, 1877, to Jan., 1878; assistant commissioner, paper currency, Bombay, March, 1878; acted as deputy comptroller general, Calcutta, Aug., 1883, and comptroller, Central Provinces, April, 1884; assistant accountant general, Calcutta, Oct., 1883; Bombay, Aug., 1884; officiated as deputy accountant-general, Punjab, Jan., 1888; assistant comptroller, India treasuries, Jan., 1889; assistant accountant-general and examiner of local accounts, Bengal, March, 1889; officiating comp-

troller, Burma, March, 1892; on foreign service, as comptroller-general to H.H. the Nizam's government, Aug., 1893.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE BURRIDGE, Captain, I.S.C., Judicial Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—First commission, 30th Jan., 1878; entered civil employ as cantonment magistrate, 2nd class, July, 1885; 1st class, Dec., 1892.

CRAWLEY-BOËVEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1868, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, special assistant to political agent, and acting political agent in Rewa Kantha; from May, 1873, to Dec., 1874, served in Baroda as acting assistant resident, and performed the duties of the cantonment and railway magistrate, and small cause court judge, May–Nov., 1873; in special charge of Sachin state, Dec., 1874; on special duty at Baroda, Feb., 1875; on famine duty at Kaladgi, Aug., 1877; talukdari settlement officer, Gujarat, June, 1879; pro tem. special officer for the settlement of claims to giras and watans in Baroda territory, April, 1883, and again April, 1885; acting chief presidency magistrate and revenue judge, Bombay, May, 1887; junior collector and magistrate, May, 1889; senior collector and magistrate, Nov., 1891; retired, Aug., 1893.

CREAGH, BOYLE PURDON, Commander, R.I.M., Marine Department, Bengal.—Joined the service, 26th March, 1878; commander of 'Tenasserim,' Sept., 1880; transport officer, Suakim, March, 1885; captain in turn of 'Canning' and 'Clive,' 1885–91; acting port officer, Madras, Oct., 1891; and Bombay, April, 1892; appointed port officer, &c., Calcutta, April, 1893.

CREGEEN, ARCHIBALD CRELLIN, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the public works department, June, 1859, as assistant engineer, Central Provinces; transferred to Punjab, Feb., 1861; executive engineer, April, 1866; railway department, Central India, Holkar state railway, Oct., 1870; engineer-in-chief, provincial light railway survey extensions, Lucknow, June, 1878; Neemuch-Naseerabad survey, Nov., 1878; engineer-in-chief, Western Rajputana state railway, Aug., 1879; engineer-in-chief, Vizagapatam–Raipur railway survey, Oct., 1882; member of committee on reorganisation of public works department, Sept., 1883; engineer-in-chief, construction establishment, Nagpur–Bengal railway, Oct., 1884; superintending engineer, 1st class, Dec., 1885; agent and chief engineer, Indian Midland railway, Feb., 1886; retired, Feb., 1888.

CRESSWELL, CHARLES ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commission.—First commission, royal artillery, Jan., 1866; arrived in Madras, 12th Jan., 1867; joined the Burma commission, Feb., 1871, as officiating assistant commissioner and cantonment magistrate at Toungheoo; personal assistant to chief commissioner, Dec., 1875; boundary officer, Sandoway, March, 1881; president, Sandoway town committee, April, 1884; deputy commissioner, March, 1885.

CROFT, SIR ALFRED WOODLEY, K.C.I.E., M.A., Educational Department, Bengal.—Appointed by the secretary of state, 8th Dec., 1865; served as professor, Presidency college, principal, Dacca college, inspector of schools,

officiating principal, Presidency college, and officiating registrar, Calcutta university; director of public instruction, Aug., 1878; president of the commission to draw up a code for European schools, Oct., 1881; on special duty with the government of India, as member of the education commission, Jan., 1883; C.I.E., May, 1884; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; member of the legislative council of Bengal, Feb., 1887; on special duty with the government of India for the preparation of a report on education in British India, May–Sept., 1887; is also fellow, member of the syndicate and president of the faculty of arts of Calcutta university; vice-chancellor, Dec., 1893.

CROFTON, JAMES, Lieutenant-General, late R.E.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, 9th Dec., 1844; joined the public works department, Nov., 1847; assistant engineer, Punjab circle, Dec., 1849; superintendent, 1st division, Bari Doab canal, July, 1854; executive engineer, Punjab irrigation, 1858; superintending engineer, 2nd class, Jan., 1862; superintendent, Sutlej canal surveys; officiating superintendent, Eastern Jumna canals, 1863; chief engineer, irrigation, and under secretary to government, Punjab, Sept., 1864; chief engineer, 1st class, April, 1872; inspector-general of irrigation and deputy secretary to government of India, public works department, April, 1874, to Jan., 1882, when he resigned the service.

CROLE, CHARLES STEWART, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Edinburgh High School and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived, 9th Dec., 1863; served in the revenue and judicial departments in various districts as assistant collector and magistrate, acting small cause court judge, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; acted as sub-secretary, board of revenue, July–Sept., 1869, and as collector of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants in 1879–80; acting commissioner of salt revenue, Madras, Jan.–March, 1880; district and sessions judge, Kistna, June, 1881; collector and magistrate, Nov., 1882, in the district of Kistna, and afterwards in the districts of Madura and Anantapur; district and sessions judge, North Malabar, Aug., 1886, and North Arcot, Jan., 1887; member of board of revenue, Dec., 1890; and of legislative council, Dec., 1893; compiled the 'Chingleput District Manual.'

CROMBIE, ALEXANDER, M.D., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; officiating resident surgeon, Calcutta medical college hospital, Jan., 1873; professor of surgery, medical college, in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1873; professor of materia medica, Sept., 1874; transferred to Burma, Feb., 1875, and appointed junior civil surgeon, Rangoon, and health officer to the port; from April, 1877, served in Bengal as civil surgeon; also at Simla as joint medical officer, March, 1880, to Feb., 1882; civil surgeon, Dacca, with charge of the jail, March, 1882; also superintendent of the lunatic asylum, medical school and Mitford hospital; surgeon superintendent, presidency general hospital, Calcutta, June, 1889.

CROMER (BARON), RIGHT HON. EVELYN BARING, G.C.B., G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Lieutenant, R.A., June, 1858; captain in 1870,

and major in 1876; A.D.C. to Sir Henry Storks, in Ionian Islands, in 1861, and secretary to him in 1865, during the enquiry into the outbreak in Jamaica; private secretary to the governor-general of India (the Earl of Northbrook), 1872 to 1876; commissioner of the Egyptian public debt, 1877-79; comptroller-general in Egypt, Sept., 1879; financial member of council of governor-general of India, June, 1880; agent and consul-general in Egypt, and a minister plenipotentiary in the diplomatic service, Aug., 1883; financial assistant at the conference in London on Egyptian finance, June to Aug., 1884; G.C.B., Jan., 1895; has 1st class of Medjidie.

CRONIN, JOHN JOSEPH, Captain, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—First commission, Aug., 1880; employed in Burma as assistant commissioner, July, 1886.

CROOKE, WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Erasmus Smith's Grammar School, Tipperary, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Dec., 1886; deputy commissioner, July, 1889; magistrate and collector, Feb., 1890; author of a rural and agricultural glossary for the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, an 'Ethnographical Handbook for the N.W. Provinces and Oudh,' 'Popular Religion and Folklore of Northern India,' and 'Gazetteer of Jalesar.'

CROSS (VISCOUNT), RIGHT HON. RICHARD ASSHETON, P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I.—Born, 30th May, 1823; educated at Rugby School, and at Trinity College, Cambridge, where he graduated B.A. in 1846; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1849, and for several years went the northern circuit; represented Preston in the House of Commons, March, 1857, to March, 1862, and S.W. Lancashire, 1868 to 1886; secretary of state for the home department, Feb., 1874, to 1880; bencher of the Inner Temple, 1876; hon. LL.D. of Cambridge, Oct., 1878; G.C.B. in 1880; again secretary of state for the home department, June, 1885, and was appointed president of the royal commission on the Education Acts; in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886, was appointed secretary of state for India, and raised to the peerage under the title of Viscount Cross; G.C.S.I., Aug., 1892; was formerly an ecclesiastical commissioner for England; is a deputy lieutenant for Lancashire, and late chairman of the Lancashire quarter sessions; author of two legal works—'The Acts relating to the Settlement and Removal of the Poor, with Notices of Cases, Indices, and Forms,' 1853; and 'The General and Quarter Sessions of the Peace: their Jurisdiction and Practice in other than Criminal Matters' (written in conjunction with Mr. H. Leaming), 1858, 2nd edition, 1876.

CROSTHWAITE, SIR CHARLES HAUKESTON, K.C.S.I., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at St. John's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1857; arrived in India, 29th Dec., 1857; from Nov., 1858, to March, 1877, served in N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate

and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector and settlement officer, and acted as secretary to the board of revenue for two years from March, 1875; from April, 1877, served in the Central Provinces as commissioner of the Nagpur and Jubbulpur divisions, and was appointed judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, Aug., 1881, and additional member of the governor-general's council for the purpose of making laws and regulations, Dec., 1881; acting chief commissioner, British Burma, March, 1883, to April, 1884; chief commissioner, Central Provinces, Jan., 1885; member of the public service commission, Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; chief commissioner of Burma, March, 1887; K.C.S.I., May, 1888; member of council of governor-general, Oct., 1890, to Feb., 1891, and again from April, 1892; lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces, and chief commissioner, Oudh, 28th Nov., 1892; author of 'Notes on the N.W. Provinces' and 'Land Revenue Law.'

CROSTHWAITE, ROBERT JOSEPH, B.A., B.L., C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School, and Brasenose College, Oxford; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1863; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector and joint magistrate and deputy collector; on special duty on the revenue committee at Naini Tal, July, 1872; assistant superintendent, sub-judge, and registrar of Dehra Dun, Dec., 1873; small cause court judge, Allahabad, March, 1875; registrar of the high court, N.W. Provinces, Jan., 1878; acting judicial commissioner, British Burma, Dec., 1878, to Feb., 1881; acting secretary to the government of India, legislative department, March to May, 1881; district and sessions judge, Allahabad, May, 1881; additional secretary to government of India, legislative department, Jan., 1883; acted as judicial commissioner of the Central Provinces in 1882 and 1883, and was confirmed in the appointment, Jan., 1885; member of viceroy's legislative council, June, 1889; officiating chief commissioner, Central Provinces, July-Oct., 1889; resident and agent to the governor-general, Central India, Jan., 1891; C.S.I., May, 1890; author of 'The N.W. Provinces Revenue Law' (legal part).

CROW, WILLIAM, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk for railway stores, 23rd Oct., 1883.

CROWDY, JOHN HENRY, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., late Military Works Department, Government of India.—First commission as lieutenant, Dec., 1857; posted to Indian establishment, Sept., 1870; from Dec., 1870, served as executive engineer in the Punjab; transferred to military works, 1872, and served in the Punjab, the N.W. Provinces, and Bengal; superintending engineer, 3rd class, March, 1886; retired, March, 1890.

CROWE, WILLIAM HENRY, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Godolphin Grammar School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1867, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, professor of mental and moral philosophy and logic at the Deccan college, Poona, acting assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan, and assistant judge and sessions judge; editor of the 'Bombay Gazetteer,' Oct.,

1868; acting special judge under the Deccan agriculturists' relief act, March, 1882; on special duty at Kathiawar, Nov., 1882; judge and session judge, Aug., 1883; also agent for sardars in the Deccan, April, 1892.

CROZIER, ROBERT GEORGE, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Brighton College; temporary clerk, India office, 30th Nov., 1865; junior clerk, Jan., 1867; senior clerk, March, 1887.

CRUICKSHANK, ALEXANDER, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Aberdeen University and King's College, Aberdeen; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in various districts of Madras from Oct., 1863, as assistant collector and magistrate, acting small cause court judge, principal assistant collector, and acting district and sessions judge; collector and magistrate, South Arcot, special agent, French settlement, and political agent for Pondicherry, Sept., 1878; collector and magistrate, Anantapur, Nov., 1885; district and sessions judge, Cuddapah, Jan., 1886, and Kurnool, Aug., 1886; retired, Nov., 1887.

CRUICKSHANK, ALEXANDER WALMESLEY, B.L., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector; junior secretary, board of revenue, Jan., 1878; under secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Sept., 1886; settlement officer, Oct., 1886; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1890; magistrate and collector, Nov., 1891.

CRUICKSHANK, JOHN, Deputy Surgeon-General, M.D., late Medical Department, Bombay.—Appointed to the Indian medical service, 20th Feb., 1856; arrived in India, 9th April, 1856; civil surgeon, Dharwar, Jan., 1865; from March, 1866, acted as surgeon to the governor of Bombay, also as private secretary, Oct., 1866; on special duty to Egypt as surgeon to Lord Mayo, Nov., 1868; superintendent of Mahabaleshwar, Dec., 1870; from April, 1871, served as inspector-general of prisons, Bombay, and was a member of the prisons conference at Calcutta, Jan., 1877; retired, April, 1887.

CRUICKSHANK, JOHN DONALDSON, Colonel, R.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—First commission, 9th Dec., 1859; probationary assistant engineer, July, 1862; executive engineer, Dec., 1865; on special duty, Karachi, in 1875-76; superintending engineer, Nov., 1891; chief engineer, Feb., 1894; acting secretary to government of Bombay, public works department, May-Nov., 1893.

CRUMP, CHARLES GEORGE, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 21st Jan., 1884, and served as assistant magistrate and collector, N.W. Provinces; resigned, March, 1886.

CRUMP, HENRY ASHBROOKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1885; super-numerary assistant commissioner, Nov., 1885;

assistant commissioner, Dec., 1889; commissioner of excise, June, 1893; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1894.

CRUMP, LOUIS CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated under private tuition and at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 10th Dec., 1890; assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1891.

CULLEN, PETER, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.D., late Medical Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the Bengal medical service, July, 1859; arrived in India, 24th Jan., 1860; was employed under the military department, including service in the China war; from Feb., 1862, served in the Central Provinces as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail; also superintendent of cotton affairs, May, 1865; rank of surgeon-major, March, 1877, and of brigade-surgeon, April, 1888; retired, June, 1892.

CUMINE, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Trinity College, Glenalmond; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived, 28th Oct., 1874, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting superintendent of police, railway magistrate, and acting junior collector and forest settlement officer; acting special assistant collector and settlement officer, Ratnagiri, Nov., 1884; 2nd assistant collector, May, 1887; 1st assistant collector, Nov., 1890; junior collector and magistrate, Kaira, and political agent, Cambay, May, 1894.

CUMING, JOSEPH HENRY, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Westminster, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1891, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

CUMMING, ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Aberdeen Grammar School, Aberdeen University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 18th Dec., 1892, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

CUMMING, CHARLES LENNOX BRUCE, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1867, and served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting district and sessions judge, and acting collector and magistrate; principal assistant to collector, magistrate and agent, Ganjam, Nov., 1883; retired, March, 1888.

CUMMING, JOHN ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Aberdeen Grammar School, and Aberdeen University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived, 16th Dec., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

CUMMING, JOHN GHEST, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at High School, Glasgow, and Glasgow University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1890, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

CUMMING, WILLIAM GORDON, Colonel, R.E., C.I.E., India Public Works Department.—First

commission, Madras engineers, 1860; arrived in India, 7th Oct., 1862; joined the public works department, Feb., 1864, as assistant engineer, Port Blair; executive engineer, April, 1866; transferred to Madras, Aug., 1868; Hyderabad, May, 1870; from Feb., 1874, served in British Burma; officiating assistant secretary to government of India, civil and military works branch, May, 1881; superintending engineer, special, Jan., 1884; officiating secretary to governor-general's agent, Rajputana, Oct., 1884; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, public works department, April, 1887; confirmed, May, 1888; rank of chief engineer, May, 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1893.

CUNINGHAM, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Brigadier-General, Military Secretary, Bombay.—First commission, 20th June, 1858; served in the Afghan war as assistant adjutant general (medal); deputy assistant adjutant general, Bombay, 1877-80, and 1881-88; deputy adjutant general, Bombay, 1888-92; officiating assistant adjutant general, Bengal, May, 1892; confirmed, Feb., 1893; secretary to government of Bombay, military, &c., departments, Dec., 1892.

CUNINGHAM, JAMES MACNABB, Surgeon-General, M.D., C.S.I., late Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the Indian medical department, 1851; superintendent of central prison, Bareilly, 1861, and subsequently at Meerut; officiating superintendent, government press, N.W. Provinces, 1863; surgeon, April, 1864; secretary to sanitary commissioner, 1866; professor of hygiene, Calcutta medical college, 1866; sanitary commissioner, Bengal, 1869; sanitary commissioner with the government of India, 1875; surgeon-general, March, 1880; retired, March, 1885; member of army sanitary commission, Feb., 1891.

CUNINGHAM, WILLIAM JOHN, C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educated at Edinburgh Academy, and Clapham Grammar School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from Aug., 1873, served in Mysore as assistant commissioner on special duty at Bangalore and Chitaldrug, acting deputy commissioner, additional secretary in the famine department (Aug., 1878), and secretary to the chief commissioner, general department (Jan., 1879); political agent, assistant to the resident in Mysore, and secretary to the chief commissioner, Coorg, April, 1881; political agent, 3rd class, and assistant to the resident, Hyderabad, Dec., 1883; under secretary, government of India, foreign department, Sept., 1885; acting secretary, Nov., 1889; deputy secretary, May, 1890; secretary, April, 1894; also secretary of orders of Star of India and Indian Empire, May, 1894; C.S.I., Jan., 1894.

CUNNINGHAM, ALEXANDER FREDERICK DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Kensington Grammar School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating judicial assistant; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab (civil department), Sept., 1878; on special duty as political assistant to Major Cavagnari for employment in Afghanistan, Nov., 1878; political officer,

Khyber, Oct., 1879; 1st assistant to chief political officer, Afghanistan (Kabul), Feb., 1880; officiating secretary to government, April-May, 1881; officiating superintendent, Kapurthala state, Feb., 1884; under secretary to government, Punjab, Dec., 1884; officiated as deputy commissioner at Dera Ismail Khan, Feb., 1885, at Hazara, 1886, and at Bannu, April, 1887; on special duty at Rawul Pindi durbar, March, 1885; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Jan., 1887; officiated as divisional judge, Jhelum, Aug., 1887, and deputy commissioner and district judge, Sept., 1887; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1888; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Peshawar, Oct., 1892, and from April, 1894.

CUNNINGHAM, DAVID DOUGLAS, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.S., F.L.S., Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1868; from 19th Dec., 1874, served as special assistant to the sanitary commissioner with the government of India; professor of physiology, Calcutta medical college, from June, 1879; also acted as government quinolologist, and was in charge of the cinchona plantations, Sept., 1879; officiated as superintendent of botanical gardens in 1880; appointed to conduct special cholera inquiries in immediate connection with the sanitary commissioner with the government of India, Dec., 1884; on special duty as naturalist with the Tibet Mission, 1886; C.I.E., June, 1893; has also acted as special assistant to the sanitary commissioner with the government of India; honorary surgeon to H.E. the viceroy; author of many medical and scientific treatises, the result of microscopical and physiological research in relation to cholera and other subjects.

CUNNINGHAM, SIR HENRY S., K.C.I.E., late Calcutta High Court.—Advocate-general, high court, Madras, and additional member of the governor's council, 1872-77; puisne judge, high court of judicature, Calcutta, Nov., 1877; member of the famine commission, May, 1878 to Jan., 1881; member of the finance committee, March, 1886; resigned his seat in the high court, Calcutta, July, 1888; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889.

CURRIE, BERTRAM WODEHOUSE, Council of India.—Appointed to the council of the secretary of state for India, 11th Dec., 1880; and on the completion of his ten years of office in Dec., 1890, was re-appointed for a further term of five years; appointed a delegate to international monetary conference at Brussels, 1892; member of royal commission on financial relations between Great Britain and Ireland, May, 1894.

CURRIE, FENDALL, B.L., Colonel, Oudh Commission.—Arrived in India, 31st Dec., 1858, as cornet, 1st European light cavalry; assistant commissioner, Hazaribagh, Feb., 1863; from Sept., 1863, served in the Oudh commission, and was appointed small cause court judge, Sitapur, June, 1868, and junior secretary to chief commissioner, Nov., 1868; on special duty to collate laws and regulations in force in Oudh, May, 1869; on special duty for taking up lands for railway purposes, Oct., 1869; city magistrate, Lucknow, Feb., 1870; on special duty to review the Bara Banki forgery report, March, 1874; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1878; officiated as commissioner in the Rae Bareli division, Aug., 1882; and subsequently in the Fyzabad and Lucknow divisions; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Oct., 1883; commissioner, March,

1888; author of commentaries on The Penal Code, Act XLV. of 1860, The Indian Code of Criminal Procedure and The Oudh Rent Act, 'The Oudh Land Revenue Manual,' 'A Commentary on Act XXII. of 1886,' and the 'Indian Law Examination Manual.'

CURRIE, GEORGE MAINWARING, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862; served in the revenue and judicial department, Bengal, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, March, 1884, and 1st grade, Aug., 1887; retired, March, 1892.

CURSETJEE RUSTOMJEE, B.L., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at King's College School, London; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, April, 1886; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1891; magistrate and collector, Dec., 1893.

CURTIS, GEORGE SEYMOUR, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Marlborough College, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1888; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1889; in 1889-91 acted as assistant political resident, Aden.

CURZON, THE HON.
