ELIAS, NEY, C.I.E., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Extra attaché, foreign department, March, 1874; second in command, Yunan expedition, and assistant to resident in Mandalay, Sept., 1874; officiating political agent, Bhamo, April-Aug., 1875, in addition to other duties; extra attaché, foreign department, Oct., 1875; from Oct., 1877, served as joint commissioner of Ladakh; political assistant, 1st class (supernumerary), June, 1879; on special duty to Yarkand, June-Aug., 1879; on special duty to Kashgar, March-Aug., 1880; political agent with the Kashgar Mission, May, 1885; political agent, March, 1886; C.I.E., Jan., 1888; boundary settlement officer, Jeypore, 1887-88; assistant agent, Rajputana, March, 1888; on special duty in connection with Sikkim expedition, Nov., 1888; British commissioner, Anglo-Siamese boundary commission, Oct., 1889; agent to the governor-general in Khorassan and Seistan, Dec., 1891.

ELIOT, JOHN, M.A., Meteorological Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, June, 1869; from 27th Dec., 1869, to Feb., 1876, served in the N.W. Provinces as professor of mathematics, Thomason college, and also at Muir college; superintendent of the meteorological observatory, Allahabad, and meteorological reporter to government; from Feb., 1876, served in Bengal, as professor, Presidency college, and meteorological reporter, also as officiating meteorological reporter to government of India, April, 1878, and Dec., 1881; officiating principal, Presidency college, and registrar of Calcutta university, Jan., 1883; appointed meteorological reporter to government of India, May, 1889.

ELIOTT, LAWRENCE EDWARD, Captain, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—1st commission, Aug., 1880; employed in Burma as assistant commissioner, July, 1886; officiating superintendent, northern Shan states, July-Nov., 1890; and from May, 1894.

ELLENOR, THOMAS RALPH, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872.

ELLERCAMP, HERBERT McDONALD, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 10th Oct., 1879.

ELLIOT, ALGERNON, Berar Commission.—Educated at Repton School; from 28th Jan., 1869, served in the police department of the Central Provinces, and as assistant commissioner, Berar; deputy commissioner, April, 1889; in 1890 officiated as civil and sessions judge.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS HUGH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Harrow, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from June, 1873, to Nov., 1875, served in Berar

as educational inspector, and acting director of public instruction; principal of the Raja's school, and tutor and governor of the Gaekwar of Baroda, Dec., 1875; on special duty, Baroda, April, 1882; C.I.E., May, 1883; on duty with the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1884; survey and settlement officer, Baroda, March, 1890.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK EDEN, late Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1858; arrived in India, 16th March, 1858; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Hamirpur, April, 1877, and district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, March, 1881; 1st grade, Jan., 1887; retired, March, 1894.

ELLIOTT, ALGERNON, Berar Commission.—Educated at Repton School; joined the service, 16th March, 1869, as acting assistant district superintendent of police, Central Provinces; transferred to Berar as acting assistant commissioner, June, 1869; confirmed, April, 1874; deputy commissioner, July, 1839; acted as civil and sessions judge, Amraoti, in 1860-91.

ELLIOTT, SIR CHARLES ALFRED, LL.B., K.C.S.I., Lieutenant-Governor of Bengal.—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1856; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1856, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; from April, 1858, to May, 1861, served in Oudh as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner, and from Feb., 1863, to Oct., 1866, in the Central Provinces as settlement officer, Hoshangabad; served subsequently as settlement officer, Farakhabad, and as secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, 1870 to 1877; commissioner, Meerut, Oct., 1877; famine commissioner, Mysore, Sept., 1877; additional secretary to government of India, famine branch, May, 1878; secretary to famine commission, June, 1878; census commissioner, Oct., 1880; chief commissioner of Assam, March, 1881; president of the committee for the re-trenchment of public expenditure, Feb., 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; ordinary member of governor-general's council, Dec., 1887; lieutenant-governor of Bengal, 18th Dec., 1890; author of 'Chronicles of Unao' and of the Famine Commission Report, 1879, and the Finance Commission Report, 1887.

ELLIS, LOVEYS EDWARD, Audit Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1804.

ELLISON, THOMAS EDWARD, late Bengal C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at St. Andrew's College, Bradford; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 19th Oct., 1864; assistant commissioner, 3rd class, Delhi, Aug., 1865; transferred to Central Provinces, Oct., 1868; assistant commissioner, 1st class, Oct., 1881; judge, small cause court, Nagpur, Sept., 1883; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1884; retired, Dec., 1890.

ELPHINSTONE, GRAHAM WARBURTON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of

1885; arrived in Madras, 19th Nov., 1887, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

ELSMIE, GEORGE ROBERT, B.L., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 12th Feb., 1853, and was appointed assistant commissioner, Punjab; officiating judge, small cause court, Lahore, Nov., 1863; on special duty at Lahore, July, 1867; officiating under secretary to government of India, Oct., 1868; deputy commissioner, May, 1870; from 1871 to 1877 officiated as additional commissioner of Jullundur and Amritsar, and of Peshawur; civil and session judge, March, 1877; officiating judge, chief court, 1878-79; commissioner and superintendent, Lahore, Nov., 1880; judge, chief court, Punjab, Jan., 1882; commissioner and superintendent, Lahore, Feb., 1885; 2nd financial commissioner, May, 1887; appointed also additional member of governor-general's legislative council, May, 1888, which office he resigned, Dec., 1888; first financial commissioner, March, 1889; on special duty at Rawal Pindi durbar, March, 1885; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; retired, Feb., 1893; author of 'Notes on Peshawur Crime.'

ELWES, The Ven. WILLIAM WESTON, M.A., Ecclesiastical Department, Madras.—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed junior chaplain, 20th Oct., 1870; domestic chaplain to bishop, June, 1874; senior chaplain, Oct., 1880; acted as archdeacon of Madras in 1888, and from April, 1891; confirmed, April, 1893.

ELWIN, EDGAR ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Dover College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1883, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; manager, Jeypore estate, July-Oct., 1890; senior assistant collector, Vizagapatam, Feb., 1891; acting assistant secretary to government, judicial and legislative departments, April, 1891; officiating deputy commissioner of salt, &c., revenue, July-Oct., 1891.

ELWIN, ERNEST BARNES, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Dover College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 5th January, 1891, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

ENGLISH, ALEXANDER EMANUEL, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Rugby, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

ENTHOVEN, REGINALD EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Wellington College and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as assistant magistrate and collector, and inspector of factories.

ERSKINE, GEORGE ELPHINSTONE, Colonel, Kumaun Commission.—Arrived in India as cornet, Bombay light cavalry, 9th Dec., 1857; was employed under the military department, including service with the Rajputana field force, till Feb., 1863, when he joined the Oudh commission as assistant commissioner; from May, 1865, to July, 1871, served as assistant settlement officer, and acting settlement officer;

also officiated as personal assistant to financial commissioner in 1867-69, and as superintendent of excise and stamps in 1868-69; personal assistant to chief commissioner, Oudh, July, 1871, to April, 1878; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, May to Aug., 1876; secretary to government of N.W. Provinces, in forest branch, and Oudh revenue department, April, 1878; commissioner, Sitapur division, Jan., 1882; Lucknow division, March, 1888, and Kumaun division, Jan., 1890; on special duty to inquire into condition of tenantry in Oudh, Dec., 1882, to May, 1883.

ETESON, A., M.D., Deputy Surgeon-General, late Medical Department, Assam.—Joined the Indian medical department as assistant surgeon, May, 1854; arrived in India, 30th June, 1854; held various military medical charges, and was promoted to surgeon-major, May, 1874; medal for services during the Mutiny; secretary to surgeon-general, Indian medical department, March, 1879; deputy surgeon-general, eastern frontier district, and sanitary commissioner, Assam, Jan., 1884; retired, Jan., 1889.

EUAN-SMITH, SIR CHARLES BEAN, Colonel, K.C.B., C.S.I.—Ensign, Madras infantry, March, 1859; arrived in India, June, 1859; served in Abyssinian expedition, 1867-68, and was present at capture of Magdala (medal); secretary to Sir F. Goldsmid's mission to Persia, Aug., 1870, to Nov., 1872; C.S.I., Nov., 1872; private secretary to Sir Bartle Frere, on special mission to Zanzibar and Muscat, Nov., 1872, to July, 1873; on special duty in India office, 1873-74; political agent and consul-general, Zanzibar, May, 1875; on special duty, foreign department, on occasion of Prince of Wales's visit to Calcutta, 1875; first assistant resident, Hyderabad, March, 1876; political agent, and consul-general, Muscat, July, 1879; during the Afghan war, 1879-80, was on special political duty on the staff of Lieutenant-General Sir D. Stewart, and subsequently with General Hill's division, May, 1880, and with the Kabul-Kandahar force under General Roberts, Aug., 1880 (medal with two clasps, bronze star, brevet of lieutenant-colonel); acted as resident, Meywar, in 1882 and 1884; assistant political agent, Banswara, Dec., 1882; from May, 1883, served as additional political agent, Bhurtpur and Karauli; appointed consul-general at Zanzibar, 1888; K.C.B., Nov., 1890; envoy extraordinary and minister plenipotentiary at Tangiers, 1891-93; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1893.

EVANS, CHARLES THOMAS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; from Aug., 1860, served in subordinate offices; appointed to the department, Sept., 1869, as assistant engineer, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, June, 1876.

EVANS, HENRY FARRINGTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough College, and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; from May, 1870, to Sept., 1879, served in the settlement department, and was appointed settlement officer, 3rd grade, Sept., 1878; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, April, 1880; officiating district and

sessions judge, July, 1881; special judge, Jhansi, July, 1882; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Oct., 1882; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1887; on special duty in Madras and Bombay, Nov., 1890, to April, 1891, and as member of Deccan agriculturists relief act commission, Dec., 1891, to May, 1892; officiating judicial commissioner, Oudh, May-July, 1893; commissioner and agent, Benares, Dec., 1893.

EVANS, LOFTUS GEORGE, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner, district judge, &c.; joint magistrate, Sept., 1886; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, March, 1891; district and sessions judge, April, 1891.

EVANS-GORDON, WILLIAM EDEN, Captain, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, 15th July, 1876; admitted to Madras staff corps, 3rd July, 1877; from Feb., 1883, served under the foreign department of the government of India with the Central India horse; boundary settlement officer, Central India, Oct., 1883, to March, 1884; and from the latter date served as officiating political assistant; attaché, foreign department, government of India, Oct., 1885; translator of foreign European languages in addition to other duties, Aug., 1887; assistant secretary, foreign department, Nov., 1887; appointed political officer in charge of Sardar Ayub Khan, March, 1888; assistant resident, Kashmir, and joint commissioner, Ladakh, Nov., 1890; political officer in attendance on H. H. the Gaekwar of Baroda in Europe.

EVERETT, STEPHEN HENRY, Audit Department, India Office.—Educated at Dulwich College; writer, India office, May, 1872; junior examiner, audit department, Dec., 1873; designation changed to junior clerk, Oct., 1892.

EVERS, BENJAMIN, M.D., C.M., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the service, April, 1868; arrived in India, 27th Oct., 1868; served with the 18th and 11th Native Infantry; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail at various stations from April, 1876.

EWART, CHARLES HENRY, Colonel, I.S.C., Police Department, Punjab.—First commission, 20th Feb., 1857; arrived in India, 2nd April, 1857; from Feb., 1861, served in the police department, Punjab, as district superintendent, and was promoted to deputy inspector-general, 2nd grade, May, 1881, 1st grade, March, 1888; acting inspector general, and under secretary to government, police department, March, 1892; confirmed, Aug., 1892.

EWBANK, ALFRED LLOYD VANDYKE, M.A., Education Department, Bengal.—Appointed, 16th Jan., 1868; arrived, 28th Feb., 1868; served as professor, Patna college, and as officiating inspector of schools; principal, Dacca college, March, 1876; professor, Patna college, Oct., 1879; principal, Patna college, Oct., 1881.

EYRE, GERVAS SELWYN, B.L., Major, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—1st commission, 12th June, 1869; served in military department until July, 1879, when he was appointed assistant commissioner in Burma; with the Burma field force,

Nov., 1837; on special duty with the force operating against the Chins, May, 1838; deputy commissioner, May, 1838; officiating inspector-general of police, March-July, 1893, and again from March, 1894; Royal Humane Society's medal for saving lives of two brother officers.

EZÉCHIEL, JOSEPH ALOYSIUS, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner.

FAGAN, PATRICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating district judge, &c.; officiating junior secretary to financial commissioners, Oct.-Nov., 1890; settlement officer, Bikanir, Aug., 1892, to Dec., 1893.

FAICHNIE, ALEXANDER GEORGE, Post Office Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the post office department, 1st May, 1858; served for some time as superintendent of the Rawal Pindi military horse van and bullock train; deputy postmaster-general, Central Provinces and Berar, Nov., 1879.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES, M.D., Deputy Surgeon-General, late Medical Department, Punjab.—Arrived in India, 20th March, 1856; in civil charge of Rawal Pindi and its jail, April, 1869; civil surgeon at Gurdaspur, April, 1874, and subsequently at various other stations in the Punjab; brigade surgeon, Nov., 1879; officiated as sanitary commissioner in 1878, 1879, and 1883; officiating surgeon-general, Punjab, April, 1885; officiating inspector-general of prisons, July, 1886; acted as medical adviser to His Highness the Maharaja of Patiala in 1884, 1885, and 1886; retired, 1886; has contributed papers on professional subjects to the 'Indian Medical Journal' and the 'Lancet.'

FANSHAW, ARTHUR UPTON, Indian C.S.—Educated at Repton; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, Nov., 1871, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, acting registrar of the judicial commissioner's court, and acting commissioner of excise, superintendent of stamps, and inspector-general of registration; assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, July, 1880, and acted as secretary in 1880 and 1881; in charge of model farm, Nagpur, July, 1880, to Jan., 1881; from April, 1881, served in the postal department as officiating postmaster-general, Bombay, and was confirmed in this appointment, March, 1882; also officiated as director-general of the post office of India, Aug., 1884; officiating secretary to government of India, finance and commerce department, December, 1883; on special duty under government of India, home department, Jan., 1889; postmaster-general, 1st grade, Feb., 1888; director general of the post office of India, May, 1889; member of the royal commission on opium, Nov., 1893.

FANSHAW, HERBERT CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Bedford, Tonbridge, and Repton; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1873, and was appointed assistant commissioner in the Punjab;

served temporarily in Bengal on famine duty, Feb.-Nov., 1874; officiating settlement officer, Rohtak, Aug., 1875, to Feb., 1876, and again in 1877; acting under secretary to government, revenue department, Punjab, March, 1879; assistant to the officer on special duty in Kashmir, June to Sept., 1879; officiating junior secretary to government, Oct. to Dec., 1882, and again in 1883 and 1884; officiating secretary to government, Jan. to April, 1884, and again in 1888 and 1889; officiating junior secretary to government, Punjab, Dec., 1887; confirmed, Nov., 1888; revenue secretary, March, 1890; officiated in 1890 as chief secretary; deputy commissioner, April, 1892; acting chief secretary, Sept., 1893; author of 'Rohtak Settlement Report,' and 'Punjab Famine Code.'

FARMER, HENRY RICHARD, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Loughborough, Bromsgrove, and Uppingham Grammar Schools; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived, 4th Dec., 1868, and served in various districts of Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; acted as Tamil translator to government, Feb., 1880, to Sept., 1881; on special duty completing treatise on Madras Act viii. of 1865, July, 1882; district and sessions judge, Kurnool, Dec., 1887; Vizagapatam, Nov., 1889.

FARQUHAR, WILLIAM, Deputy Surgeon-General, M.D., late Madras Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, 28th May, 1858; arrived in India, 31st Dec., 1858, and served under the military department in Madras, Burma, and the Straits Settlements; also held civil medical charge, Tounghoo, in 1859; medical officer, Ootacamund, from Nov., 1882; promoted to brigade surgeon, April, 1885; deputy surgeon-general, Belgaum and Western Districts, July, 1885; retired, July, 1890.

FARRAN, CHARLES FREDERICK, B.L., Judicial Department, Bombay.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1864; served as reporter, high court, Bombay, 1868-74; also as acting government professor of law, April, 1868, to June, 1870, and Perry professor of jurisprudence, June, 1870, to Jan., 1872; acted as clerk of the crown, Sept., 1874, to Sept., 1876, and was confirmed in this appointment, May, 1880; acting advocate general, Bombay, May-Dec., 1884; acted as additional judge of the high court, Bombay, Nov., 1886, to Nov., 1887; appointed judge of the high court, April, 1890.

FARRAN, G. H., M.A., Judicial Department, Bombay.—Served from 17th May, 1866, as head assistant in the prothonotary's office, high court, Bombay, acting prothonotary, ecclesiastical and admiralty registrar, acting official assignee of the insolvent debtors' court, and acting clerk and sealer of the insolvent debtors' court; deputy registrar, commissioner for taking affidavits, and judge's clerk, Nov., 1873; assistant commissioner for taking accounts and assistant taxing master, July, 1876; master and registrar in equity, commissioner for taking accounts, and taxing officer, Sept., 1893.

FARRELL, G., Deputy Surgeon-General, C.B., late Punjab Medical Department.—Joined the service as surgeon, 4th Aug., 1856; deputy surgeon-general, Sept., 1886; officiating in-

spector-general of civil hospitals, Punjab, Nov., 1888, and was confirmed in the appointment, March, 1889; from Dec., 1888, to Feb., 1889, was honorary surgeon to the viceroy; retired, Sept., 1891.

FARRER, HENRY, Indian C.S.—Educated at Heversham Grammar School, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1875; served in revenue and judicial department as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; postmaster-general, Madras, June, 1886.

FAULDER, CECIL JOSEPH SALKELD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Harrow; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1876, and served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1887; magistrate and collector, March, 1892.

FAUNTHORPE, JOHN CHAMPION, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Rossall School and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 26th Oct., 1892, and served in the North-Western Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

FAWCETT, CHARLES GORDON HILL, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Harrow and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 9th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

FAYRER, SIR JOSEPH, Surgeon-General, K.C.S.I., LL.D., M.D., F.R.S.S., F.R.C.P., F.R.C.S.—Acting assistant surgeon, R.N., Aug., 1847; assistant surgeon, royal artillery, 1849; joined the East India Company's service, April, 1850, and served under the military department, also holding civil surgery of Cheera Poonjee, Nov., 1850; served in the Burma campaign, and officiated as civil surgeon, Rangoon, Feb., 1853; appointed residency surgeon, Lucknow, by Lord Dalhousie, for services during war in Burma; honorary assistant resident at Lucknow, Sept., 1854; civil surgeon, Lucknow, March, 1856; brevet rank of surgeon for services in defence of Lucknow residency during the siege, Sept., 1858; officiating professor of surgery and ex-officio 1st surgeon of medical college hospital, Calcutta, May, 1859; confirmed in these appointments, June, 1860; medical charge of Mysore princes, June, 1860; fellow of university of Calcutta and member of the senate, March, 1861; served for two years as president of the medical faculty, and received the public thanks of the faculty; surgeon, July, 1863; consulting surgeon to Howrah general hospital Nov., 1865; president of the Asiatic Society of Bengal, 1867; J.P. for Calcutta, Feb., 1868; government trustee for India museum, 1860; officiating surgeon to viceroy, Jan.-Feb., 1869; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Edinburgh in his tour through India, Jan.-March, 1870; surgeon-major, June, 1870; honorary physician to the Queen, July, 1871; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to India, 1875-76, in medical charge of his party; retired, 1874; president of medical board, India office, Dec., 1874, to Jan.,

1895; member of army sanitary commission; member of senate of army medical school at Netley; consulting physician to Charing Cross hospital; author of 'Clinical Surgery in India,' 'Clinical and Pathological Observations in India,' 'Thanatophidia of India' (presented to the Indian government, by whom it was published in 1872), and other works; has also published numerous papers, monographs, and addresses, on medical and scientific subjects.

FENNER, HENRY ALGERNON SHRAPNEL, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed 2nd assistant engineer, 20th June, 1861; 1st assistant engineer, April, 1865; executive engineer, Sept., 1868; 3rd superintending engineer, March, 1887; 2nd Dec., 1889; 1st, July, 1891; officiating chief engineer and joint secretary to the government of the Punjab, Jan.-Feb., 1892; retired, May, 1893.

FENTON, LAYARD LIVINGSTONE, Major, I.S.C., Bombay Political Department.—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1870, and after serving under the military department, was transferred to the revenue survey department as assistant superintendent, Nov., 1873; assistant to political agent, Kathiawar, March, 1880; acting superintendent of surveys under the Rajasthanik court in 1885, 1887 and 1889; confirmed, Dec., 1890; acted as president of the court in 1891 and 1892.

FENTON, MICHAEL WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Royal School, Armagh, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 18th Nov., 1883, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; officiating junior secretary to financial commissioner, Nov.-Dec., 1885; officiating under secretary to government, April, 1887; confirmed, Oct., 1887; assistant commissioner, Nov., 1891; senior secretary to financial commissioners, July, 1892; author of 'Punjab Municipal Manual.'

FENTON, THOMAS CHARLES, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd May, 1873; attached to the store department till Aug., 1879, and subsequently to the registry department.

FERARD, HENRY CECIL, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Eton, and University College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; joint magistrate, April, 1893.

FERARD, JOHN EDWARD, India Office.—Educated at Eton, and Magdalen College, Oxford; upper division clerk, local government board, 2nd Feb., 1892; junior clerk, India office, 7th April, 1893; private secretary to parliamentary under secretary of state, Feb., 1894.

FERET, CHARLES JAMES, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Jan., 1875.

FERGUSON, HENRY TANNER, late Railway Department, Government of India.—Joined the public works department as locomotive superintendent, Rangoon and Irrawaddy Valley railway, Oct., 1879; Punjab Northern state railway, Aug., 1881; class I, state railway, revenue establishment, Jan., 1886; deputy locomotive

superintendent, North-Western railway, Jan., 1886; deputy locomotive superintendent, Eastern Bengal railway, Oct., 1887; officiating locomotive superintendent, Feb., 1888; retired, 1888.

FERGUSON, RT. HON. SIR JAMES, BART., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., M.P.—Born in 1832; educated at Rugby, and University College, Oxford; entered the Grenadier Guards, and served in the Crimean campaign, 1854-55 (medal and three clasps); retired from the army in 1855; represented the county of Ayr in parliament in the Conservative interest from Dec., 1854, to April, 1857, and from Oct., 1859, to 1868; under secretary of state for India, June, 1866, to July, 1867; under secretary for the home department, July, 1867, to Aug., 1868; governor of South Australia, Aug., 1868; governor of New Zealand, March, 1873, to 1874; governor of Bombay, April, 1880, to March, 1885; parliamentary under secretary of state, foreign office, Aug., 1886; royal commissioner for the Adelaide exhibition of 1887; postmaster-general, Sept., 1891, to Aug., 1892.

FERRAR, MICHAEL LLOYD, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Belfast Academical Institution, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 21st Jan., 1863; from Jan., 1864, to Sept., 1865, served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Oudh, Sept., 1865, and served as assistant commissioner; from July, 1868, to April, 1875, served in the settlement department, and was appointed to take up lands for railway purposes in Lucknow, Aug., 1870; officiating commissioner, Fyzabad, in 1862, 1883, and 1889; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, April, 1883; magistrate and collector, March, 1884; officiating commissioner, Fyzabad, April, 1889; commissioner in Oudh, Aug., 1889; officiating commissioner, Gorakhpur, April, 1891; confirmed, July, 1891.

FFINCH, BENJAMIN TRAILL, Director-in-Chief, Indo-European Telegraph Department, India Office.—Appointed to the Indian telegraph department, 5th Nov., 1857; transferred to Indo-European telegraph department as deputy director of the Persian Gulf section, April, 1875; officially designated director, March, 1884; in political charge, Mekran coast, Aug., 1870; justice of the peace for Mekran, Aug., 1880; director-in-chief, Indo-European telegraph department, 1st April, 1893.

FFORDE, ARTHUR BROWNLOW, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at the Bedford Grammar School, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 31st Oct., 1892, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner.

FIDDIAN, JAMES PAULI, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Birmingham Grammar School, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived, 13th Nov., 1875, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Bellary, Dec., 1884; also acted as collector and magistrate, and district and sessions judge; sub-collector and joint magistrate,

Jan., 1889; collector and magistrate, Jan., 1892; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1894.

FIDDIAN, WILLIAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Huddersfield College, Shrewsbury Grammar School, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in revenue and judicial department, Bengal; on famine duty, May, 1874; officiating commissioner, Soonderbunds, Jan., 1879; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1880; magistrate and collector, April, 1886; retired, June, 1894.

FIELD, CHARLES DICKENSON, M.A., LL.D., Barrister-at-Law, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 11th Oct., 1860; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating judge of the principal court of small causes, Kishnaghur and Jessore, officiating magistrate and collector, and registrar, high court (July, 1867); from Dec., 1870, served in the judicial department, and was placed on special duty in connection with a commission to consider the question of amending the rent law of Bengal, May, 1879; district and sessions judge, 1st grade, Oct., 1879; lieutenant-governor's council, Dec., 1879; judge, high court, Oct., 1880; retired, Sept., 1886; author of 'Rent Law Procedure in Bengal,' 'Essays on Indian Topics,' 'Chronological Table of, and Index to, the Indian Statute Book,' 'The Law of Evidence in British India,' 'The Law relating to Protestant Curates,' 'The Regulations of the Bengal Code,' 'A Digest of the Law of Landlord and Tenant in Bengal,' and 'Landholding and the Relation of Landlord and Tenant in various Countries.'

FIELD, GEORGE ALFRED, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Educated at Christ's Hospital; supernumerary writer, 30th May, 1859; clerk in registry department, Sept., 1860; employed in military department, 1867; assistant to registrar, March, 1882.

FIELD, GEORGE MOYLE ROBERT, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed to the department, 16th Oct., 1872, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, Oct., 1881; services lent to Patiala state, 1887-93; officiating under secretary to government of Punjab, irrigation branch, May, 1894; on special duty in secretariat, June, 1894.

FILGATE, ALEXANDER JEROME, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, India.—First commission, 1857; served in the China war, 1860 (medal and clasps); joined the Madras public works department, Oct., 1863, as assistant engineer, South Canara; deputy controller, public works accounts, July, 1865; deputy consulting engineer for railways, and examiner of accounts, Feb., 1866; from March, 1867, served in the public works department of the government of India, as assistant accountant-general; deputy accountant-general, and ex-officio under secretary to government, March, 1872; officiating accountant-general and deputy secretary to government of India, March, 1879, to Nov., 1880; confirmed as accountant-general, and deputy secretary, Sept., 1881; member of

finance committee, April, 1886, to March, 1887; retired, Nov., 1890.

FILGATE, TOWNLEY MACARTNEY, Registrar-Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 1st March, 1867, and served as military secretary to governor of Bombay, and registrar-general of assurances, Bombay presidency (from Aug., 1870); from July, 1871, served as inspector-general of registration; on special duty, Nov.—Dec., 1876, engaged in selecting prisoners for release from the jails of the Bombay presidency on the occasion of the proclamation of the Empress of India, 1st Jan., 1877; on special duty, Dec., 1879, as member of the committee on the registration law, at Calcutta; acting inspector-general of prisons, Dec., 1886; confirmed as such in addition to his other duties, April, 1887; also registrar-general of births, deaths, and marriages, Bombay, Sept., 1888.

FINCH, CHARLES THOMAS, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872.

FINK, WILLIAM ROBERT, High Court, Calcutta.—Joined the service, 12th April, 1869, as clerk to the chief justice; assistant registrar, and assistant to the clerk of the crown, June, 1875; chief clerk, Feb., 1885; 1st assistant or deputy registrar, June, 1890.

FINLAY, HERBERT BLAKEY, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the High School and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, Dec., 1865; from May, 1866, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; from Oct., 1873, to April, 1880, served in the settlement department, and was appointed settlement officer, 3rd grade, Banda, July, 1878; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Nov., 1880; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, July, 1885; magistrate and collector, Aug., 1885; officiated as commissioner of division in 1887, 1891, and 1893; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1893.

FINLAY, JAMES FAIRBAIRN, M.A., Indian C.S.—Educated at Edinburgh Academy and University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1875, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; on special duty in the public works department, Oct., 1880; transferred to finance department of government of India, May, 1881, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, as officiating assistant accountant-general and officiating deputy accountant-general; assistant comptroller-general in 4th class of financial department, July, 1882; officiated as under secretary to government of India, financial department, and as secretary, at various periods; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, June, 1886; deputy auditor-general, Nov., 1887; under secretary to government of India, financial department, Nov., 1887; officiating accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Dec., 1887; deputy secretary to government of India, financial department, April, 1889; officiated as secretary in 1889, and again Jan., 1891; confirmed, May, 1892.

FINNEY, STEPHEN, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as assistant engineer, Oct., 1874; served in various divisions, and as personal assistant, engineer-in-chief's office; assistant manager,

Northern Bengal state railway, May, 1881; and deputy manager, Eastern Bengal state railway, July, 1889; officiated as manager, 1890, and again from March, 1891; confirmed, Oct., 1892.

FINUCANE, MICHAEL, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Munchin's College, Limerick, Limerick Collegiate Academy, and Queen's College, Cork; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872; assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal, Dec., 1872; manager, Raj Durbhunga, Sept., 1879; settlement officer, Jan., 1880; officiating under secretary, government of Bengal, judicial, political, and appointment departments, Jan., 1882; on special duty, Midnapore, May, 1882; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1884; director of the agricultural department in Bengal, Dec., 1884; also member, central examination committee, April, 1886; officiating secretary, board of revenue, from April, 1893; in March, 1894, acted as under secretary to government of India, department of revenue and agriculture; edited the Bengal Tenancy Act of 1885.

FIREBRACE, FREDERICK, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, Dec., 1861; joined the public works department as assistant engineer, N.W. Provinces, May, 1865; assistant principal, Roorkee College, 1865-68; officiating assistant consulting engineer for railways, Bengal, May, 1868; manager, Calcutta and South Eastern railway, Feb., 1870; executive engineer, Sept., 1870; Neemuch railway survey, Feb., 1871; assistant director of state railways, March, 1875; manager, Rajputana state railway, Nov., 1876; 1st class, state railway revenue establishment, April, 1877; on duty at the Royal Engineering College, Cooper's Hill, Sept., 1878, to Nov., 1879; under secretary to government of India, railway branch, Jan., 1880; superintending engineer, Feb., 1882; consulting engineer and joint secretary to government (railway branch), Bombay, Feb., 1887; chief engineer, July, 1891; author of a 'Manual of Surveying.'

FISCHER, FRANCIS NORTON, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Fettes College, Edinburgh, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 20th Dec., 1889, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

FISHER, CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Charterhouse and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived, 15th Dec., 1890, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

FISHER, FREDERICK HENRY, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Boston Grammar School, and University of London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1870, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; assistant superintendent and small cause judge of Dehra Dun, March, 1879; officiating civil and sessions judge, Saharanpur, Oct., 1881; on special duty in connection with the preparation of the 'N.W. Provinces Gazetteer,

Dec., 1881; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, April, 1885; retired, Aug., 1885.

FISHER, JOHN HADDEN, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Harrow School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1861; from June, 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating small cause court judge; from Jan., 1872, to April, 1879, served in the Central Provinces as deputy commissioner; from April, 1879, served in the N.W. Provinces as joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade; magistrate and collector, April, 1880; retired, July, 1890.

FISHER, WILLIAM ROGERS, B.A., late Indian Forest Service.—Appointed assistant conservator, 28th Sept., 1872; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1872; deputy director of forest school, Dehra Dun, April, 1881, and Feb., 1884; conservator and director of the school, March, 1885; conservator of the 3rd grade, Dec., 1888; 2nd grade, May, 1891; retired, Jan., 1893; assistant professor of forestry, Cooper's Hill College, from Nov., 1890; editor of 'The Indian Forester,' and author of a translation of the 'Aménagement des Forêts.'

FITZGERALD, EDWARD AMBROSE, Brigade Surgeon, late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 27th July, 1859; arrived in India, 27th Jan., 1860, and was employed under the military department, 1860-63; from Feb., 1868, served in the N.W. Provinces as civil assistant surgeon and superintendent of jail, Jaunpur; rank of surgeon, July, 1871; surgeon-major, July, 1873; served subsequently as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail at various stations in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; brigade surgeon, Dec., 1887; retired, March, 1890.

FITZGERALD, SIR GERALD, K.C.M.G..—Educated at St. Mary's College, Galway, and in France; appointed to the late ordnance office, Aug., 1855; junior clerk, war office, Jan., 1856; was estimate clerk, war office, 1861-63; assistant commissioner for the investigation of the system of accounts in the public works department, India, Sept., 1863; officiating deputy comptroller-general of military expenditure, March, 1864; transferred to the financial department, India, Feb., 1868; personal assistant to financial member of governor-general's council, July, 1869; assistant comptroller-general, Aug., 1869; officiating deputy comptroller-general, Sept., 1870; deputy accountant-general, Madras, April, 1871; in charge of the office of accountant-general, British Burma, Dec., 1871; officiating deputy comptroller-general, March, 1872; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, Aug., 1872; and deputy comptroller-general, Nov., 1876; allowed to accept temporary service under the Egyptian government, Jan., 1877, and appointed director-general of accounts in Egypt, Dec., 1878, and member of the committee of finance, March, 1884; accountant-general of the navy, 1st June, 1885, and deputy and assistant to the parliamentary and financial secretary, by order in council of 18th Nov., 1885; was attached to the intelligence department for the military operations at Alexandria in 1882; commissioner of the royal patriotic

fund; member of the pensions commutation board; C.M.G. in 1880, and K.C.M.G. in 1885; 3rd class of the order of Osmanieh, 1880; 2nd class of the order of the Medjidie, 1884; grand cordon of the order of the Medjidie, 1885; war medal and Egyptian bronze star for service in the Egyptian campaign, 1882.

FITZGERALD, JAMES, Colonel, I.S.C., late Berar Commission.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, June, 1856; arrived in India, Sept., 1856; was employed under the military department up to 1862, including service in the Mutiny, 1857-59, and was present at the siege and capture of Delhi (medal and clasp); served in the China war, 1860, and was present at the capture of Pekin (medal and two clasps); from Aug., 1862, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as assistant commissioner, railway magistrate, and deputy commissioner; retired, April, 1889.

FITZGERALD, JAMES RICHARD, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Uppingham Grammar School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 5th Nov., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from Aug., 1875, served under the foreign department, government of India, as assistant general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti department, and assistant to resident, Hyderabad; on special duty in connection with control of Moghias, Jan., 1878; political assistant, 1st class, March, 1878; officiating 1st assistant and secretary for Berar to resident, Hyderabad, March, 1882; held charge of the office of resident, May, 1882; from July, 1882, served in Ajmere, as assistant commissioner, and acted as commissioner, Aug.-Oct., 1882; magistrate of the district of Ajmere, Oct., 1882; first assistant, governor-general's agent, Baluchistan, May, 1883; political agent, 3rd class, Oct., 1883; held charge of the agent's office in 1883-84, and of the Kalat political agency, Nov.-Dec., 1884; from April, 1885, acted as 1st assistant and secretary for Berar, Hyderabad; retired, 1888.

FITZGERALD, PERCY SEYMOUR VESEY, Bombay Political Department.—Joined the service, 17th June, 1871, and was employed in the Bombay revenue survey; appointed 4th assistant to the political agent, Kathiawar, June, 1878; assistant to the political agent, Kolhapur, Aug., 1884; resumed his appointment at Kathiawar, Oct., 1886; 3rd assistant, March, 1889; 1st assistant, Sept., 1893; acting political agent, Mahi Kantha, June, 1894.

FITZGERALD, SIR WILLIAM GERALD SEYMOUR VESEY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., India Office.—Educated at Harrow, and Oriel College, Oxford; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1865; private secretary to governor of Bombay, 1867-72; political aide-de-camp to the secretary of state for India, 14th Aug., 1874; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; K.C.I.E., Aug., 1887.

FITZMAURICE, JOHN DAY STOKES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at the Royal School, Armagh, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, Jan., 1884; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1884; forest settlement officer, May, 1886; acting assistant judge and sessions judge, July, 1887; 2nd assistant collector and magistrate, Nov., 1891; 1st assistant, May, 1894; under-secretary,

political, &c., departments, and secretary to council, July-Nov., 1892.

FITZPATRICK, Sir DENNIS, B.A., K.C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1858; served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner, and small cause court judge, and was placed on special duty in England in connection with Begum Sumroe's case, March, 1869, and at Calcutta in the legislative department of government of India, May, 1873; deputy secretary to the government of India in the legislative department, 1874; secretary in the legislative department from 1877 to 1885; was also secretary to the governor-general's council; acted as judge of the chief court, Punjab, in 1876-77, and as secretary to government of India, home department, Jan.-April, 1885; acting chief commissioner, Central Provinces, Dec., 1885; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; member of the public service commission, 1887, and acting resident in Mysore; chief commissioner of Assam, Sept., 1887; resident at Hyderabad, Aug., 1889; lieutenant-governor of the Punjab, 6th March, 1892; K.C.S.I., May, 1890.

FLEET, John FAITHFULL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1867, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, educational inspector, acting assistant political agent, and acting collector and magistrate; first assistant collector, Dec., 1878; on special duty in England, Jan.-May, 1879, and again in 1884 and 1885; epigraphist to the government of India, Jan., 1883; junior collector and magistrate, and political agent, Sholapur, June, 1886; senior collector, Nov., 1889; acting commissioner, Dec., 1891; commissioner of customs, &c., Bombay, March, 1893.

FLEMING, ALLAN STOPFORD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Harrow, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1882, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner; officiating under secretary to chief commissioner, April-Oct., 1888; deputy commissioner, April, 1890.

FLETCHER, WILLIAM MORRIS, late Revenue Survey Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 2nd June, 1865, and served in the Bombay presidency as assistant superintendent of revenue survey, acting assistant settlement officer, and acting deputy superintendent of survey; on special duty in connection with the famine, Nov., 1876; superintendent of survey, Sept., 1879; acted as survey and settlement commissioner in 1891 and 1892; retired, Nov., 1893.

FOLEY, BLANCHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Highgate School, and Merton College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 14th December, 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

FOORD, EDWARD ARCHIBALD, Major-General, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—Second lieutenant, Madras Engineers, 9th Dec., 1842; arrived in India, July, 1844; assistant civil engineer, Oct., 1846; adjutant

of engineers, 1850-52; assistant to secretary, board of revenue, public works department, Madras, Aug., 1852; served as 1st class district engineer in South Arcot and Tanjore, Dec., 1856, to May, 1863; superintending engineer, Tanjore and Trichinopoly, May, 1863; inspecting engineer for civil and military buildings, Madras, Dec., 1872; superintending engineer, Kistna and Nellore, June, 1874; deputy chief engineer and under secretary to government of Madras, Dec., 1875; chief engineer, 2nd class, and joint secretary to government, buildings and roads branch, Feb., 1877; chief engineer, 1st class, and secretary to government, public works department, April, 1877; resigned the public works department and retired from the army, Dec., 1878.

FOOTE, ROBERT BRUCE, F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India.—Appointed from England as assistant in the geological survey, and joined 28th Sept., 1858; served in various districts of the Madras presidency, the Nizam's territory, and Bombay; deputed to Vienna exhibition in joint charge of the survey collections, 1873; 1st grade officer, April, 1876; deputy superintendent, June, 1881; fellow of the Madras university and member of the faculty of engineering, 1884; designation changed to superintendent of survey, Sept., 1885; officiated as director in 1887; retired from the department, Oct., 1891; state geologist, Baroda, Oct., 1891; director, geological department, Mysore, Oct., 1894; author of various contributions to the survey publications.

FORBES, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Sedbergh School, Yorkshire, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; assistant magistrate and collector, Burdwan, Dec., 1867; officiating deputy collector of customs, Calcutta, Sept., 1873; also secretary to the central relief committee, Jan., 1874; assistant commissioner in Assam, Nov., 1874, to May, 1876; on special duty at Sibsaur, Jan., 1876; joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1878; junior secretary, board of revenue, April, 1882; magistrate and collector, May, 1887; officiating commissioner of excise, March, 1890; commissioner, Patna, June, 1892.

FORBES, ARTHUR TREVOR, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Thame Grammar School, Oxford, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in Madras, 18th Oct., 1887, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; deputy registrar, high court, Feb., 1893.

FORBES, GEORGE FORREST GREENLAW, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1883, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector and assistant commissioner; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Nov., 1889; 1st grade, Feb., 1893.

FORBES, GEORGE STUART, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Aberdeen Grammar School and University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived, 16th Oct., 1872, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting principal assistant, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting

deputy commissioner of salt revenue; from Sept., 1880, to Jan., 1883, was employed in the Madras secretariat as acting under secretary in the revenue department, assistant secretary, judicial and legislative department, and under secretary in the departments under the chief secretary, and also served on the board of examiners from Jan., 1882; from Jan., 1883, served under the government of India in the home department as officiating under secretary, also in the foreign department, on special duty, Dec., 1884, and as junior under secretary, Jan., 1885; also in charge of duties of press commissioner, March, 1885; officiating under secretary to government of India, foreign department, March, 1887; acting 1st assistant and secretary for Berar to the resident at Hyderabad, Dec., 1887; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Sept., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1890; collector and magistrate, Jan., 1891.

FORBES, JOHN GREENLAW, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—First commission as lieutenant, 9th Dec., 1854; arrived in India, 1st Feb., 1857; during the Mutiny, 1857-58, served at the siege of Delhi, the relief of Lucknow, the siege and capture of Lucknow, and various engagements (medal and three clasps); served in the public works department, Punjab, from 21st June, 1858, as assistant engineer and executive engineer, and from July, 1863, in the N.W. Provinces; on special duty in the Madras Presidency, Feb., 1867, reporting on the Kaveri, Krishna, and Godaveri canals; superintendent of irrigation works, Oudh, Dec., 1867; officiating chief engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, Oudh, July to Nov., 1872; from Nov., 1872, served as superintending engineer in the irrigation branch in the N.W. Provinces, in the Punjab (Bari Doab canal), and in Bengal (Sone circle), and officiated as chief engineer and joint secretary, irrigation branch, Bengal, May to July, 1877; on special duty under the government of India to prepare a revised scheme for the Sardah canal, June, 1878; on special duty on the Scinde, Punjab, and Delhi, and Indus Valley railways, Nov., 1878; superintending engineer and secretary to resident, Hyderabad, Dec., 1879; chief engineer and joint secretary, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, buildings and roads branch, April, 1881, and in the irrigation branch from Nov., 1881; chief engineer, 2nd class, March, 1886; 1st class, March, 1887; secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Nov., 1887; officiated as inspector-general of irrigation and deputy-secretary to government of India, public works department, Feb.-May, 1889; confirmed, Dec., 1889; secretary in the same department, Nov., 1890; reverted to military department, Aug., 1892.

FORBES, L. R., late Bengal Non-Regulation Commission.—Served in the judicial department, Bengal, from March, 1864, and on the 1st May, 1875, was appointed assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1887; deputy commissioner, Chittagong hill tracts, Jan., 1888; retired, March, 1891.

FORBES, WILLIAM A., C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Rugby and Haileybury College, from which he was appointed to the Bengal civil service in 1847; served in the revenue and judicial departments in the N.W. Provinces, Punjab, and Oudh; served as political officer

throughout the Mutiny (medal with clasp for Lucknow); magistrate and collector, Jaunpore, 1862; commissioner, Allahabad division, 1872; officiated as member, board of revenue, N.W. Provinces; retired, 1875.

FORBES, WALTER ERNEST, Colonel, I.S.C., late Oudh Commission.—Arrived in India as lieutenant, Bengal artillery, 13th April, 1859; served from Oct., 1860, in the Oudh commission as assistant commissioner, assistant settlement officer, and officiating settlement officer; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1876; commissioner, Lucknow division, July, 1883; Fyzabad division, Oct., 1883; retired, June, 1889; author of report of the Partabgarh settlement.

FORD, ERNEST, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived, 6th Dec., 1890, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

FORD, WILLIAM, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1842; arrived in India, 7th July, 1842; attached to the N.W. Provinces, and served in the grades of assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner and joint magistrate and deputy collector; on special duty in the Sikh states, April, 1846; assistant to the governor-general's agent, N.W. Frontier, May, 1846; served subsequently in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and superintendent, cis-Sutlej territory, and officiating deputy commissioner; magistrate and collector, Gurgaon, 1856; deputy commissioner, 1st class, 1858; deputed on special duty to Cashmere, March-Nov., 1860; commissioner and superintendent, Hissar division, Oct., 1864; Mooltan division, March, 1865; placed in charge of the state of Bhawalpur, 1866; retired, 1869; author of a report on native government as contrasted with the English system.

FORDHAM, JOHN WILLIAM, late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the public works department as assistant accountant, July, 1859; assistant controller, July, 1869; deputy controller, Holkar and Neemuch state railways, Dec., 1871; examiner, 4th class, Jan., 1880; ditto, 2nd grade, Sept., 1881, and 1st grade, Feb., 1884; officiating examiner of accounts, Rajputana-Malwa railway, Sept., 1884; examiner, 3rd class, May, 1886; retired, 1889.

FORREST, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A., Director of Records, Calcutta.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1872, as head master, high school, Surat; acted at various periods as professor of history and political economy, Elphinstone college, and professor of English literature, Deccan college; professor of mathematics, Deccan college, July, 1879; acting census commissioner for Bombay, 1882; on special duty in connection with the Bombay records, 1884-86; professor of English history, Elphinstone college, Aug., 1887; in addition, director of records, Bombay, April, 1888; professor of English literature, Elphinstone college, March, 1889; officer in charge of records of the government of India, and of the imperial library, March, 1891; in addition, officer in charge of patent office, Jan., 1894; author of 'Articles on Indian Finance,' 'Selections from

Bombay State Papers (Mahratta Series), 'Selections from Bombay State Papers (Home Series),' 'Selections from the State Papers preserved in the Foreign Office of the Government of India, 1772-1785,' 'The Administration of Warren Hastings,' 'Selections from the State Papers relating to the Indian Mutiny,' and 'The Administration of the Marquess of Lansdowne'; has also edited, with a memoir, 'Selections from the Writings of Mountstuart Elphinstone.'

FORREST, HENRY TELFORD STONOR, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Plymouth College, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1802; arrived in India, 26th Dec., 1803, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

FORSYTH, WILLIAM, F.R.C.S., Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the service, 9th May, 1876, and served at various stations as civil medical officer; officiating health officer, Calcutta, April, 1891; confirmed, March, 1892.

FOSTER, SIR CHARLES J., General, K.C.B.—Served with the 16th Lancers during the campaign in Afghanistan under Lord Keane, including the siege and capture of Ghuznee (medal); served also at the battle of Maharajpore, 9th Dec., 1843 (bronze star); and in the campaign on the Sutlej in 1846, including the battles of Buddiwal, Aliwal (as aide-de-camp to Brigadier Cureton), and Sobraon (medal and clasp); appointed to the council of the secretary of state for India, July, 1878; retired, 1888; K.C.B., June, 1893.

FOSTER, HERBERT WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Liverpool Institute, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived, 26th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting principal assistant collector and magistrate, and acting registrar, high court; head assistant, Jan., 1890; registrar, high court (appellate side), Dec., 1890.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, B.A., Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Sept., 1882; B.A., London University, 1888; editor, India Office List, from Aug., 1891; in 1892 assisted in editing 'The Register of Letters, etc., of the East India Company, 1600-19,' and in 1893 prepared a descriptive catalogue of the paintings, statues, &c., in the India office.

FOSTER, WILLIAM SHRUBSOLE, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; served in Madras from Nov., 1861, as assistant magistrate and collector, and sub-collector; fellow of university of Madras, April, 1873; collector and magistrate and political agent, Godaveri, April, 1875; retired, 1885.

FOWLER, RIGHT HON. HENRY HARTLEY, M.P.—Born, 16th May, 1830; educated at Woodhouse Grove School, and St. Saviour's Grammar School; mayor of Wolverhampton, 1863, and first chairman of the Wolverhampton school board; deputy lieutenant for Staffordshire; has represented Wolverhampton in parliament since 1880; under-secretary of state, home department, 1884-85; financial secretary to the treasury, 1886; president, local government board, Aug., 1892; secretary of state for India, March, 1894.

FOWLER, JAMES TOWNSHEND, F.S.A., late Education Department, Madras.—Principal, government normal school, Madras, 2nd Dec., 1855; fellow of Madras university, Sept., 1857; on special duty superintending formation of normal schools, May, 1858; from Jan., 1859 served as inspector of schools, and was promoted to 1st class of educational department, April, 1880; on special duty to Calcutta as member of the educational committee, Feb., 1882, and again, Aug., 1882, to March, 1883; acting director of public instruction, Madras, April-June, 1883; retired, Dec., 1888.

FOX, CHARLES EDMUND, Barrister-at-Law, Judicial Department, Burma.—Officiated as government advocate, British Burma, 1879-80, and 1884-85, and as additional recorder, Rangoon, 1885-86, and 1888; confirmed as government advocate, March, 1886.

FOX, CHARLES EDWARD, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, late Judicial Department, Bombay.—Served from 9th Jan., 1866, as master and registrar in equity, commissioner for taking accounts and local investigations, and taxing officer, high court, Bombay; retired, Aug., 1893.

FOX, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Independent College, Taunton, and Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in the Central Provinces, and from March, 1877, in Burma, as assistant commissioner; superintendent of central jail, Moulmein, in 1877 and 1878; transferred to N.W. Provinces, Dec., 1883, and served as assistant magistrate and collector, and acting district and sessions judge; assistant commissioner, 2nd class, Jhansi, May, 1885, 1st class, Nov., 1887; judge of the small cause court, Allahabad, Jan., 1889; district and sessions judge, April, 1890.

FOX, GEORGE JAMES BUSCALL, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th July, 1877.

FOX, HARRY CROKER, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 12th July, 1872, and posted to Hyderabad; executive engineer, Oct., 1875; employed temporarily in military works department, 1890-91, and in Burma, 1892-93; acted in 1891 as public works secretary to resident, Hyderabad, and was appointed to that post, May, 1893.

FOX-STRANGWAYS, MAURICE WALTER, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Charterhouse, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1882; supernumerary assistant commissioner, Nagpur, Nov., 1882; assistant commissioner, Oct., 1887; settlement officer, Nov., 1887; judge, small cause court, Jan., 1892; commissioner of excise, March, 1892; deputy commissioner, April, 1892.

FOXTON, WILLIAM, B.A. (Oxon.), late Judicial Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 23rd Aug., 1857; from Oct., 1857, served in the educational department as acting professor at the Poona and Elphinstone College, and head master of the English school, Ahmednagar, and was appointed tutor to ex-Ameers of Hyderabad, Sind, July, 1860; from Oct., 1860, served in

Sind as deputy collector and magistrate, deputy collector of income tax, acting judge and sessions judge, and acting collector and magistrate; appointed judge of the court of small causes, cantonment magistrate, and superintendent of jail, Karachi, also Sindi translator to government, May, 1876; acting collector of customs and shipping master, Karachi, and assistant collector of salt revenue in Sind, April, 1886; retired, April, 1887.

FRANCIS, EDWARD BELCHAM, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Repton Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; transferred temporarily to Bengal on famine duty, Feb. to Nov., 1874; officiating superintendent of Chamba, April to Aug., 1879; officiating secretary to financial commissioner, Aug., 1879, and again in 1883-84; officiating judicial assistant in various districts, 1880-83; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, Feb., 1885; 1st grade, May, 1887; deputy commissioner, May, 1889; officiating director of land records and agriculture, Nov., 1892, to Dec., 1893.

FRANCIS, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Hyderabad Administration.—Educated at Cheltenham College; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1873, as assistant superintendent, Bombay revenue survey; divisional inspector, land records and agriculture, Dhwarwar, May, 1891; director of land records and agriculture, Hyderabad, Aug., 1891.

FRANCIS, WALTER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Repton and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 11th Nov., 1890, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

FRANKLIN, BENJAMIN, M.D., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Medical Service.—First commission, April, 1869; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1869; civil surgeon in Oudh, Dec., 1869, to June, 1876; superintendent, central prison, Lucknow, July, 1876; on special duty with the viceroy, Dec., 1880; civil surgeon of Simla, Dec., 1881, to Nov., 1886; honorary surgeon on the personal staff of the viceroy, Jan., 1886; on special duty with the lieutenant-governor of the Punjab, Nov., 1886, to March, 1887; civil surgeon and superintendent of lunatic asylum, Jubbulpore, Oct., 1887; surgeon to the viceroy, Jan., 1894.

FRANKS, CHARLES NASH BAYLEY, Public Works Department, India Office.—East India house writer, old establishment, 19th Nov., 1856; junior clerk, railway department, India office, 1860; senior clerk, Feb., 1875.

FRANKS, EDWARD, Military Department, India Office.—East India house writer, old establishment, 1st Sept., 1858; junior clerk, financial department, India office, Dec., 1866; funds department, 1867; senior clerk, military department, Aug., 1881.

FRASER, ALEXANDER, General, C.B., R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, 8th Dec., 1843; joined the public works department, Sept., 1850, as executive engineer, Lower Provinces; executive engineer, Arracan, 1853, and subsequently at Rangoon; garrison engineer, presidency, Feb., 1856; under secretary to government of India, public works depart-

ment, 1859; superintendent, Alguada Reef and other lighthouses, 1860; chief engineer, 2nd class, Feb., 1862; officiating secretary to government of Bengal, and chief engineer, 1862; chief engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, British Burma, Aug., 1867; chief engineer, 1st class, Jan., 1873; chief engineer and secretary to government of N.W. Provinces, May, 1871, to Jan., 1879; secretary to government of India in the public works department, Jan., 1879, to Feb., 1882.

FRASER, ALEXANDER THOMAS, Colonel, R.E., late Madras Public Works Department.—Arrived in India, 2nd March, 1860, and served from that date in the public works department, Madras; executive engineer, Oct., 1866; transferred to Central Provinces, Dec., 1866; and to Rajputana, June, 1867; posted to N.W. Provinces, military works branch, April, 1872; served also in Jhansi and Mysore; returned to Madras, as executive engineer, March, 1881; acted also as superintending engineer, and deputy chief engineer and under secretary to government, public works department; reverted to military department, Aug., 1894.

FRASER, ANDREW HENDERSON LEITH, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at the Academy, Institution, and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 23rd Oct., 1871; served in Central Provinces as assistant commissioner; acting registrar of the judicial commissioner's court, Jan., 1877; assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, April, 1877; commissioner of excise, superintendent of stamps, and inspector-general of registration, Central Provinces, Aug., 1881; junior secretary to the chief commissioner, and director of agriculture, Feb., 1882; acting secretary to the chief commissioner, April, 1882, and again at intervals in 1883-86; acting deputy commissioner, Wardha, March, 1884; acting commissioner, Chhattisgarh, April, 1888; commissioner, Jan., 1891; employed under government of India as member of hemp drugs commission, from July, 1893.

FRASER, EDWARD ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 13th Sept., 1864; served with the 7th dragoon guards and in India with the 18th hussars; joined Madras staff corps, May, 1867; served under the foreign department of the government of India from July, 1874, as assistant resident in the Persian Gulf, assistant to the agent to the governor-general in Rajputana, assistant general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti, Jolna, and assistant to resident, Hyderabad; 2nd assistant agent in Central India, May, 1878, to April, 1879; officiated for various periods as political assistant, 1st class, and was confirmed, Oct., 1883; British joint commissioner, Ladakh, April, 1884; assistant secretary to government of India, foreign department, Nov., 1884; assistant resident in Mysore, and secretary to the chief commissioner of Coorg, April, 1886; additional political agent, 1st class, and guardian to the heir apparent of Jodhpur, Dec., 1886; officiating political agent, 2nd class, and 1st assistant to the agent to the governor-general in Rajputana, and to the chief commissioner, Ajmere-Merwara, June, 1887; poli-

tical agent, Ulwar, Oct., 1891; has translated the 'Tanzimat-I-Hasanah.'

FRASER, HENRY, Colonel, late Police Department, Central Provinces.—First commission, Madras cavalry, 1854; arrived in India, 14th March, 1854; assistant to general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti department, Nagpur, July, 1860; joint magistrate, Nagpur and Berars, Aug., 1861; from Nov., 1861, served in the police department, Central Provinces; acted as inspector general, July-Aug., 1868; 1st class, district superintendent, Nov., 1885; reverted to military duty, 1890.

FRASER, HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Wellington College, and Oriel College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, June, 1885; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Feb., 1888; acting registrar of high court, N.W. Provinces, in 1888; confirmed, April, 1889; on special duty in secretariat, Nov., 1891, to April, 1892; deputy commissioner, March, 1892; magistrate and collector, April, 1892.

FRASER, JAMES DENHOLM, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Christ's Hospital, and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 21st Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

FRASER, STUART MITFORD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1884; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1885; forest settlement officer, Aug., 1887; tutor and guardian to the Raja of Kholapur, the Chief of Kagal, and Kunwar Saheb of Bhavnagar, May, 1889, to April, 1894.

FRAZER, ROBERT WATSON, B.A., LL.B., M.R.I.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Rathmines School, Kingstown School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1877, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, engineer and manager of the Parla Kimedi estate (Jan.-Feb., 1882), acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting deputy director of revenue settlement; retired, 1886.

FREMANTLE, SELWYN HOWE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Eton and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant settlement officer, and assistant commissioner.

FRENCH, FRANCIS COOPE, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Marlborough and Eton, and at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 20th Dec., 1889, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner, and personal assistant to chief commissioner.

FRIZELLE, JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Royal School, Dungannon, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 20th Dec., 1863,

and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Punjab, May, 1864, and served as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, officiating small cause court judge, and officiating cantonment magistrate; judicial assistant, July, 1876; deputy commissioner, April, 1877; divisional judge, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; officiating judge, chief court, Punjab, June, 1888; divisional judge, 1st grade, and additional judge of chief court, March, 1889; confirmed as judge of chief court, May, 1889.

FROST, CHARLES EVITT, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Kensington Grammar School, and foreign schools; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting collector and magistrate and political agent, acting railway magistrate, and acting forest settlement officer; second assistant collector, Dec., 1884; first assistant collector, Aug., 1887; junior collector and magistrate, Nov., 1891; senior, June, 1894.

FRY, HENRY JAMES WAKELY, B.A., Stone Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, 18th Aug., 1873; B.A. (honours in logic and moral philosophy), London university, 1875.

FRY, THOMAS DUNCAN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Paul's School and University College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1889, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

FRYER, SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM RICHARDS, K.C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at foreign schools, and at Bromsgrove Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and settlement officer; officiating settlement secretary to financial commissioner, Oct., 1875; judicial assistant, April, 1877; on special duty with the Quetta field force, Oct., 1878, and again, Feb., 1879; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1882; officiating superintendent, Kapurthala state, May, 1883; commissioner in Burma, Aug., 1886; financial commissioner, Burma, June, 1888; C.S.I., May, 1890; retransferred to Punjab, and appointed commissioner and superintendent, March, 1891; officiating chief commissioner, Burma, May, 1892, to May, 1894, when he returned to the Punjab as officiating financial commissioner; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895; author of 'A Report on the Settlement of the Dera Ghazi Khan District.'

FRYER, WILLIAM HERBERT, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892.

FULLER, JOSEPH BAMPFYLDE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1875; from Nov., 1875, to July, 1882, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and acting joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was placed on special duty as assistant director of agriculture and commerce, Feb., 1878; transferred to Central Provinces, July, 1882, and appointed acting director of agriculture and commerce, and acting junior secretary to the chief commissioner; assistant secretary to chief com-

missioner, Aug., 1882; commissioner of settlements and agriculture, Oct., 1885; also junior secretary to the chief commissioner, April, 1890, to Dec., 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; author of 'A Primer of Agriculture,' 'Field and Garden Crops of the N.W. Provinces and Oudh,' 'The Central Provinces Settlement Code,' and a 'Review of the Progress of the Central Provinces, 1862-92.'
