FULLERTON, JOHN CAMPBELL, M.B., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Foreign Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1874; medical officer, general hospital, Calcutta, 1874-75, and 1875-76; acting civil surgeon, Nuddea, May, 1877; and at Bhagulpur, May, 1878; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80; acting medical officer, Baluchistan agency, March, 1880; confirmed, Aug., 1885; agency surgeon, Quetta, and administrative medical officer for Baluchistan, April, 1891.

FULTON, EDMUND McGILDOWNY HOPE, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Rugby; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial department as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant commissioner, branch inspector-general of registration and educational inspector in Sind, acting judge and sessions judge, and agent for sardars in the Deccan; appointed registrar of the high court, Bombay, Feb., 1881; 2nd grade judge and sessions judge, Feb., 1886; transferred to Lower Burma as judicial commissioner, May, 1891; 1st grade judge and sessions judge, and acting judge, high court, Bombay, Nov., 1892, to Dec., 1893, and from March, 1894.

FURLONG, MALCOLM, Colonel, I.S.C., late Burma Commission.—Joined the Madras army as ensign, and arrived in India, 10th Feb., 1857; served at the capture of Copul, 1858, and with the Bellary movable column, 1859 (medal); personal assistant to inspector-general of police, Burma, June, 1865; served in the Burma commission from Aug., 1867, as assistant commissioner, assistant secretary to the chief commissioner (July, 1869, to Feb., 1871), and town magistrate of Prome; from Jan., 1880, acted as deputy commissioner of Moulmein, also officiating as judge in 1880; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, May, 1884; president, Moulmein municipal committee, Jan., 1885; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Dec., 1888; retired, 1891.

FURNIVALL, W. C., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the public works department, May, 1869, as superintending engineer on the Delhi and Rewari railway; 1st grade superintending engineer, Rajputana railway, March, 1874; under secretary to government of India, railway branch, Oct., 1873, in charge of Rajputana railway as engineer-in-chief; officiating director of railways, central system, and joint secretary to government of N.W. Provinces, May, 1877; joint secretary and officiating chief engineer, N.W. Provinces, Jan., 1879; director of construction for state railways, March, 1880; chief engineer, and secretary to chief commissioner, British Burma, Oct., 1880; services lent to the Bengal Central, and Bengal and N.W. railway companies, May, 1883; Nizam's guaranteed railway company, March, 1884; chief engineer, 2nd

class (supernumerary), March, 1882; retired from public works department, 1886.

GABBETT, POOLE ROBERT, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service in Oct., 1871, and served in the Madras presidency; served in England (Woolwich), Jan., 1877; at Gibraltar, March, 1881; in Egypt with expeditionary force, Feb., 1885; in England, June, 1885; in the Madras presidency, March, 1889; surgeon to the governor of Madras, Jan., 1891, to May, 1892.

GAFFNEY, JOHN BURKE, Brig.-Surg.-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Central Provinces.—Appointed to the Indian medical service, 1st Oct., 1866, having previously served as assistant surgeon, Her Majesty's home service, 1864-65; arrived in India, 27th April, 1867; was employed under the military department, including service in the Nicobar expedition, 1867; house surgeon, medical college hospital, Calcutta, March, 1868; from June, 1868, served in the Central Provinces as civil assistant surgeon, and assistant to the deputy commissioner, Upper Godavery district; from June, 1878, served as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail at Raipur, and afterwards at Seoni and Saugur; civil surgeon and superintendent of lunatic asylum, Jubbulpur, with medical charge of central jail, Jan., 1894.

GAHAN, HENRY HERBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed to the department, 18th Sept., 1873, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, May, 1883; services lent to Dholepur state, 1883-86; officiating deputy consulting engineer to government of India for railways, Lucknow, May, 1893.

GAISFORD, GILBERT, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1868; admitted to staff corps, March, 1870; from May, 1870, served with the Punjab frontier force, and took part in the Jowaki expedition, 1877-78 (medal); served throughout the Afghan war, 1873-80 (medal); assistant superintendent of levies in Baluchistan, April, 1883; assistant political officer on expedition to Zhob, Sept., 1884; officiating political agent and deputy commissioner, Quetta and Pishin, April, 1889; confirmed, Feb., 1891.

GAIT, EDWARD ALBERT, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 11th Dec., 1884, and served as assistant commissioner in Assam; provincial superintendent of census operations, 1890, and wrote the provincial report; acted as secretary to the chief commissioner, Oct., 1891, to Jan., 1892; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1892; director of land records and agriculture, Nov., 1893.

GAITSKELL, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Cheltenham, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served as assistant commissioner and settlement officer.

GALBRAITH, EDGAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Portora Royal School, Enniskillen, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant superintendent and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, March,

1885; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Dec., 1886; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, June, 1889; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Feb., 1890; deputy commissioner, Sept., 1892; magistrate and collector, April, 1894.

GALTON, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at St. Peter's College, Radley, Magdalen Grammar School, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1862; arrived, 10th Nov., 1863; served in Madras from Aug., 1865, as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant commissioner (Nilgiri Hills), acting under secretary to government in the revenue department (March, 1872, to March, 1873), secretary to the board of revenue (Oct., 1879, to March, 1881, and in 1881-82), and acting collector of sea customs at Madras, and protector of emigrants; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1883; collector and magistrate, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1885; acting commissioner of salt and abkari revenue, April, 1886; acting additional member, board of revenue, April, 1887; acting secretary to government, revenue department, July, 1888; confirmed, Nov., 1888; acting chief secretary, and member of legislative council, Jan. to April, 1893; retired, Sept., 1894.

GAMBLE, JAMES SYKES, M.A., F.L.S., Forest Department, North-Western Provinces—Educated at Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed assistant conservator of forests, Oct., 1871; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1871, and served in British Burma; transferred to Bengal, Aug., 1872; served under the government of India as assistant to the inspector-general of forests, April, 1877, to Jan., 1879; deputy conservator, Bengal, July, 1880; conservator, 3rd grade, Sept., 1882; transferred to Madras, Dec., 1882; conservator, 2nd grade, Dec., 1887; transferred to N.W. Provinces as conservator of school circle, and director of the forest school, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1890; promoted to 1st grade, Feb., 1892.

GAMBLE, REGINALD ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S.—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 30th Dec., 1884; assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1885; transferred to financial department, government of India, as acting assistant controller of India treasuries, Nov., 1889; acting deputy accountant general, Allahabad, June, 1890; officiating under secretary in the finance and commerce department, Jan., 1891.

GANPATRAO DAMODHAR PANSE, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Appointed probationer under the native civil service rules, and assistant collector and magistrate, 13th Dec., 1881; confirmed as member of the Bombay civil service, 5th June, 1884; 2nd assistant collector and magistrate, Dec., 1890; 1st assistant, June, 1894.

GARBETT, COLIN HUBERT, Colonel, I.S.C., Bengal Non-Regulation Commission. — First commission in army, 27th June, 1859; from 4th Dec., 1866, served in the non-regulation commission, Bengal, as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1879.

GARBETT, HUBERT, late Public Works Department, Punjab.—Joined the public works department, Nov., 1854; executive engineer, Bari Doab canal, Feb., 1860; Western Jumna

canal, Aug., 1863; executive engineer, 1st grade, on the inundation canals, Punjab, Feb., 1870; superintendent of works, Derajat circle, Punjab, 1870; superintending engineer, July, 1878; superintending engineer, Bari Doab circle, Jan., 1880; and in the Derajat circle, April, 1882; superintending engineer, 2nd class, March, 1881; officiated as superintending engineer, 1st class, at intervals, from May, 1881; retired, 1890.

GARDINER, JOHN WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Lycée Impérial Bonaparte, Paris; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; on special duty under financial department of government of India, Sept.—Nov., 1873; again on special duty, Nov., 1880; promoted to judicial assistant, 1st grade, Dec., 1880, and to deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Nov., 1885; settlement officer in addition to his own duties, March, 1887; officiating divisional judge, Rawul Pindi, June, 1888; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, April, 1889; retired, Nov., 1889.

GARDINER, RICHARD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Lieutenant, 8th Jan., 1868; 2nd assistant engineer, Rajputana state railway, 10th Nov., 1870; 1st assistant engineer, Sept., 1872; executive engineer, Western Rajputana section, Feb., 1875; deputy manager, class ii., state railway revenue establishment, July, 1882; in the office of director-general of railways, Jan., 1885; officiating under secretary to government of India, public works department, railway branch, Nov., 1886; manager and engineer-in-chief, Bhavnagar-Gondal railway, June, 1887; also charge of Morvi railway, June, 1887; retired, April, 1893.

GARRETT, ALFRED HOWARD, Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, May, 1883.

GARRETT, ANNESLEY JOHN, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Hyderabad Administration.—Educated at Marlborough; first commission, 29th May, 1866; military secretary to the resident, Hyderabad, March, 1890.

GARRETT, CHARLES BAZETT, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 2nd March, 1858; from Jan., 1859, served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating registrar of assurances, district and sessions judge, and magistrate and collector; judge of the principal court of small causes in Nuddea and Jessore, Dec., 1882; district and sessions judge, 1st grade, Feb., 1883; officiating superintendent and remembrancer of legal affairs in 1884, 1888, and 1891; retired, March, 1893.

GARRETT, HENRY WILLIAM, Political Department, India Office.—Educated at the King's School, Ely; clerk, 2nd class, accountant general's department, India office, 12th April, 1882; transferred for service in the political department, June, 1890.

GARRETT, JAMES HUGH ELIOT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Reading Grammar School, Clifton College and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India,

25th Nov., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

GARSTIN, JOHN HENRY, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1857; arrived, 1st Dec., 1857; served in revenue and judicial departments, Madras, and was appointed private secretary to H.E. Lord Napier, March, 1866; acting collector of sea customs and protector of emigrants, Jan., 1871; collector and magistrate, South Arcot, and political agent, Pondicherry, May, 1871; served as acting secretary, and additional secretary to government, revenue department, 1876-80; C.S.I., May, 1878; collector and magistrate and agent, Vizagapatam, March, 1880, to April, 1881; board of revenue, July, 1882; appointed to revise the establishments of collectors and divisional officers in Madras presidency, Feb., 1883; member of the committee in connection with the draft tenancy bill, July, 1885; fellow of Madras university, March, 1886; second member of board of revenue and commissioner of land revenue, April, 1887; acting first member, July to Sept., 1887; member of council of governor of Madras, Jan., 1889; acting governor of Madras, 1st Dec., 1890, to 23rd Jan., 1891; retired, Jan., 1894; author of a 'Review of the Madras Famine of 1876-78'; and compiler of the 'District Manual of South Arcot,' 1878.

GARSTIN, NORMAN ANTHONY, late Police Department, North-Western Provinces.—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1857, as deputy collector and magistrate, Bengal; transferred to Oudh, Sept., 1859, as extra assistant commissioner; assistant district superintendent of police, Dec., 1863; district superintendent, Jan., 1867; retired, Dec., 1893.

GARTH, RIGHT HON. SIR RICHARD, Kt., M.A., Q.C.—Educated at Eton, and Christ Church, Oxford; called to the bar in Lincoln's Inn in 1847, and went the home circuit; represented Guildford in Parliament, 1866-68; nominated chief justice of the high court of judicature, Calcutta, March, 1875; retired, March, 1886; sworn of Her Majesty's privy council, Feb., 1888.

GARTHWAITE, LISTON, B.A. (London), late Education Department, Madras.—Served from 8th April, 1857, as head master of the zillah school, Cuddalore, of the provincial school, Calicut, and of the normal school, Cannanore, and as deputy inspector of schools; also acted as Malayalam translator, and Canarese translator to government; from Sept., 1869, served as inspector of schools; compiled Canarese and Malayalam Arzis for the secretary of state, and also various text-books; fellow of Madras university, March, 1884; 2nd class, education department, May, 1884; on special duty at Madras in connection with preparation of a scheme for development of technical and scientific education, in 1884-85, and again in 1885-86; retired, Dec., 1888.

GARWOOD, JOHN FREDERICK, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Indian Military Works Department.—First commission, 17th April, 1866; arrived in India, 17th Feb., 1872; after serving in various divisions, and acting (in 1886) as public works secretary to the agent in Baluchistan, was promoted to superintending engineer, Sept., 1890; on field service in the Zohb valley, 1890; acted as superintending engineer, Baluchistan civil works, and public works secretary to agent, in 1890 and 1892.

GASKIN, LIONEL EDWARD PALMER, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Kingswood School, Bath, Bedford Grammar School, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

GATES, FRANK CAMPBELL, B.L., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1882; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1882; from Aug., 1884, served in Burma as assistant commissioner; on special duty in Upper Burma, March, 1887; junior secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1887; under secretary, Oct., 1887; deputy commissioner, 4th grade, Aug., 1889; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, Jan., 1890; junior secretary to chief commissioner, March, 1891; secretary, March, 1893.

GATHERER, ARTHUR BRANDER, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Arrived in India, 8th Nov., 1864, and served in the public works department, Madras and Mysore; executive engineer, Sept., 1873; assistant secretary to resident, public works department, Hyderabad, Dec., 1885; superintending engineer, March, 1889; transferred to Burma, April, 1889.

GAUNTLETT, MAGER FREDERIC, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Dulwich College, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

GAY, EDWARD, M.A., late Financial Department, Government of India.—Appointed by the secretary of state as assistant in the finance department, Sept., 1865; assistant comptroller-general of accounts and acting assistant commissioner of paper currency, Calcutta, June, 1867; acting deputy accountant-general, Bombay, April, 1868; deputy comptroller-general, Calcutta, Sept., 1869; 1st class of the financial department, Jan., 1871; on special duty in England, March, 1872, to Dec., 1873; acting accountant-general, Bengal, May, 1874, to Jan., 1875; accountant-general, Bombay, and commissioner of paper currency, April, 1879; officiating comptroller and auditor-general, April, 1885; comptroller and auditor-general and head commissioner of paper currency (provisionally), Nov., 1887; confirmed, Nov., 1888; retired, Nov., 1891.

GEAKE, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Plymouth College and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1884; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1886, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, April, 1893; officiating district and sessions judge, April-July, 1893.

GEE, HERBERT WALTER, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Sherborne School and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating district judge, &c.

GEIDT, BERNARD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Shrewsbury; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 30th

Nov., 1876; assistant magistrate and collector, Burdwan, Dec., 1876; transferred to Assam, Oct., 1877, and served as assistant commissioner and assistant inspector of immigrants; personal assistant to the chief commissioner, Jan., 1882; officiated as deputy commissioner in 1884 and 1887; retransferred to Bengal as joint magistrate and deputy collector, Jan., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1894.

GELL, Right Rev. FREDERICK, D.D., Ecclesiastical Department, Madras.—Fellow of Christ College, Cambridge; nominated lord bishop of Madras, 16th May, 1861; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1861; acting metropolitan bishop, Calcutta, March, 1876, to Feb., 1877.

GEOGHEGAN, HENRY THOMAS, B.A., M.I.C.E., late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; joined the public works department, Nov., 1868, as executive engineer, Punjab Northern state railway; superintending engineer, Indus valley railway, May, 1877; officiating consulting engineer to government of India for state railways, Feb., 1879; engineer-in-chief, Bhopal railway, June, 1879; engineer-in-chief, Madras state railway surveys, Feb., 1885; superintending engineer, 1st class, March, 1886; retired, 1890.

GEORGE, EDWARD CLAUDIUS SCOTNEY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Dulwich and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 7th March, 1887, and served as assistant commissioner; officiating deputy commissioner, Aug., 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1894.

GEORGE, JAMES MORGAN TOMKINS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Malvern College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner and settlement officer; officiating under secretary to chief commissioner, May-Aug., 1891, and again in 1892.

GERRARD, ARTHUR STAPLES, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 2nd Aug., 1873, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, July, 1881; officiating consulting engineer to government of India for railways, Lucknow, Feb., 1892, to April, 1893.

GHOSE, MAHIMOHUN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at St. Xavier's College, Bombay. St. Peter's College, Exmouth, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 11th Feb., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

GIBB, MALCOLM COUPER, LL.B., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Harrow, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived, 11th Dec., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1883; on special duty, Surat, June, 1887; 2nd assistant collector and magistrate, Nov., 1890; 1st assistant, Feb., 1893.

GIBBONS, JAMES BARRY, Surgeon-Captain, Bengal Medical Department.—First commission, 1st Oct., 1881; from Aug., 1884, acted as civil surgeon; and as resident physician, medical college hospital, Calcutta; appointed to the latter post, Feb., 1885; officiated for various periods as professor at the medical college;

superintendent, Campbell medical school and hospital, May, 1893.

GIBSON, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at St. Alban's Grammar School; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864; served in Madras revenue and judicial department from Feb., 1866, as assistant collector and magistrate and acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, and was on special duty, Jan., 1868, inquiring into condition of poorer classes, North Arcot, and on famine duty in Kurnool, Nov., 1876, to March, 1878; acting under secretary to government, chief secretary's department, Feb., 1880; private secretary to governor of Madras, Dec., 1880; sub-collector and joint magistrate, June, 1882; secretary to the board of revenue, Jan., 1884; collector and magistrate, Sept., 1888; member of board of revenue, Nov., 1893; member of legislative council, Jan., 1894; officiating secretary to government, revenue department, Jan.-April and Sept.-Dec., 1893, and from March, 1894; confirmed, Nov., 1894.

GIBSON, FREDERICK EDWARD, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Bath Grammar School, London University, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; served in various districts of Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector, acting district and sessions judge, and acting collector and magistrate; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Nellore, Oct., 1883; retired, Aug., 1887.

GIBSON, HENRY WILLIAM, B.L., late Oudh Commission.—Volunteer in the Meerut light horse, 1857-58; served subsequently in the military police, 1858-61; assistant district superintendent of police, Oudh, June, 1861; district superintendent, 4th grade, April, 1863; from July, 1864, served in the Oudh commission as assistant commissioner, and officiated as settlement officer, Feb.-Dec., 1865; acted as superintendent of encumbered estates, Sitapur, July to Oct., 1874; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1877; retired, April, 1894.

GIBSON, J. R., late Revenue Survey Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1862; arrived, Sept., 1860, and served as assistant superintendent, assistant settlement officer, and deputy superintendent; from 1881 to 1885 served in Egypt, being attached to the intelligence department during the campaign of 1882 (medal and Khedive's star); superintendent, revenue survey, Ratnagiri, May, 1885; from 1885 to 1890, was again employed under the Egyptian government, and received the order of the Osmanieh; resumed his post in May, 1890, but returned immediately to Egypt; retired from the department, June, 1892.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM GILCHRIST, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, Aug., 1870, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; services lent to South Indian railway company, 1887-88, and to Nilgiri railway company, 1890-91; in charge of Kalka-Simla railway survey, March, 1892.

GILES, ARTHUR HENRY, Police Department, Bengal.—Joined the Bengal police department as assistant superintendent, 14th Feb., 1863; district superintendent, Jan., 1866; officiating personal assistant to inspector-general, March,

1869; officiating deputy commissioner of police and justice of the peace for the town of Calcutta, May, 1870; 1st grade district superintendent, April, 1885; deputy inspector-general, Oct., 1890; served temporarily under the government of Ceylon in 1888.

GILES, EDWARD, M.A., Bombay Educational Department.—Educated at Christ Church, Oxford; arrived in India, 14th May, 1873, and served from that date as professor of history and political economy, Elphinstone college, Bombay; from May, 1875, served as educational inspector, and was promoted to the 1st grade of the department, Oct., 1893.

GILES, FRANK, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector; under secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, Oct., 1886; officiating secretary to board of revenue, Sept., 1887; senior assistant commissioner, May, 1888; officiating commissioner, Kumaun, 1889, 1891, and 1893; deputy commissioner, Almora, Oct., 1891; acted as legal rememberance in 1893; member and secretary of provincial legislative council, Nov., 1893, to Jan., 1894.

GILES, ROBERT, M.A., Sind Commission.—Arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1868; assistant settlement officer, Indus survey, March, 1869; served from Jan., 1870, as deputy collector, superintendent of police, and educational inspector in Sind; 2nd assistant collector in Sind, July, 1884; deputy commissioner, Upper Sind frontier, Dec., 1885; acting collector and magistrate, Shikarpur, April, 1892.

GILL, JOHN EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Harrow, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 23rd Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Aug., 1887; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Nov., 1889; settlement officer, 2nd grade, March, 1890; joint magistrate, 1st grade, July, 1890; district and sessions judge, March, 1893; author of 'Gazetteer of the Ghazipur District.'

GILLAN, Rev. G. G., M.A., late Ecclesiastical Department, Bengal.—Assistant chaplain of the Church of Scotland, 4th Jan., 1866; served under the military department with the 91st Highlanders at Dum Dum, 1866, and with the 72nd Highlanders at Umballa, 1871; chaplain, Jan., 1876; senior chaplain of the church of Scotland in Bengal, and of St. Andrew's Church, March, 1880; retired, 1889.

GILLAN, ROBERT WOODBURN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Ayr Academy and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 11th November, 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Aug., 1893; officiating city magistrate and wasika officer, Lucknow, March, 1894.

GILLESPIE, JAMES THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Newry Intermediate

School, the Royal University of Ireland, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 21st Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

GILLILAND, JOHN HOWARD, B.A., Bengal Education Department.—Appointed to the service, 15th June, 1877, and served in the Presidency and the Howrah colleges as professor of civil engineering; professor in Presidency college, Nov., 1888; has acted as second assistant meteorological reporter to the government of India.

GILLMAN, HERBERT FRANCIS WEBB, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Dulwich College and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed member of the Madras civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived, 1st Dec., 1888, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

GIRINDRA NARAIN DEB, KUMAR, Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Officiating deputy magistrate and deputy collector, Burdwan, Aug., 1878; appointed to the Bengal civil service under the native civil service rules, and served as probationary assistant magistrate and collector from Dec., 1880; confirmed in his appointment in the civil service, 19th Feb., 1886; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1892.

GLADSTONE, CECIL ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in the Punjab as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating judicial assistant; on special duty at Delhi at the Imperial assemblage, Dec., 1876, and again, in the foreign department of the government of India, Feb., 1877; from Sept., 1885, acted as district judge, Rawul Pindi; transferred to Burma as deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Sept., 1886; 1st grade, May, 1889; officiated as commissioner, Southern Division, April, 1889; re-transferred to the Punjab, Dec., 1889; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1889; retired, Sept., 1894.

GLASS, JAMES GEORGE HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—2nd assistant engineer, Central Provinces, 27th June, 1867; executive engineer, April, 1871; secretary to chief commissioner, public works department, Central Provinces, July, 1885; superintending engineer and secretary, Aug., 1888; C.I.E., May, 1890; chief engineer and secretary, Jan., 1893; officiating chief engineer, buildings and roads branch, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, March, 1893; confirmed as chief engineer and secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, buildings and roads branch, March, 1894.

GLAZIER, EDWARD GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at North London Collegiate School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861; from April, 1862, served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Oct., 1879; retired, 1891; author of 'Report on the district of Rungpore,' 'Further Notes on the Rungpore Records,' 'Report on the Famine in Rungpore.'

GLENNY, WILLIAM HENRY, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Newry School, Royal School, Dungannon, and Trinity College, Dub-

lin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in various districts of Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting small cause court judge, and acting district and sessions judge, and was placed on special duty, Jan., 1868, enquiring into the condition of the poorer classes, South Arcot; collector and magistrate, Kurnool, April, 1880; acting director of revenue settlement and agriculture, Oct., 1885; in charge of the inam department, Oct., 1885; collector and magistrate, North Arcot, May, 1886; retired, 1888.

GLOSTER, JOHN CLERKE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Midleton College, Cork, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

GODFREY, CHARLES WILLIS, Colonel, I.S.C., Revenue Survey Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 9th Sept., 1858, and served in the Bombay artillery; from Aug., 1864, served in the Bombay revenue survey department as assistant superintendent, and acting deputy superintendent, and was promoted to superintendent of survey, Jan., 1885; colonel, Dec., 1888; acted as survey and settlement commissioner, June, 1889.

GODFREY, FRANCIS EDWARD, India Public Works Department.—Appointed to the superior accounts branch as assistant controller, and posted to Bombay, 1st April, 1871; deputy examiner, Jan., 1880; examiner, Jan., 1882; officiating deputy accountant-general for inspection duty, Dec., 1892; examiner of telegraph accounts, April, 1894.

GODFREY, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Marlborough College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 20th Oct., 1873; assistant magistrate and collector, Bengal, Oct., 1873; on famine duty, May, 1874; transferred to Assam, Dec., 1874, as assistant commissioner, and acted as assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, July to Oct., 1877; from Jan., 1880, served in Bengal as officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; again transferred to Assam, Oct., 1883, and served as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1885; acting inspector-general of police, jails, registration, commissioner of excise and superintendent of stamps, April, 1892; judge and commissioner, Assam Valley districts, Oct., 1892.

GODLEY, SIR ARTHUR, K.C.B., Under Secretary of State, India Office.—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; Hertford scholar, 1868; Ireland scholar, 1870; Eldon law scholar, 1874; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1876; private secretary to the first lord of the treasury, 1872-74, and again, 1880-82; commissioner of inland revenue, 1882-83; C.B., 1882; permanent under secretary of state for India, 30th Sept., 1883; K.C.B., Jan., 1893.

GODSELL, WILLIAM, Auditor, India Office.—Junior assistant, 30th Sept., 1860; junior inspector, Dec., 1868; assistant to the auditor, Oct., 1892; auditor, Dec., 1894.

GODWIN-AUSTEN, HAROLD, Berar Administration.—Educated at the Royal Academy, Gosport; joined the service, 27th Feb., 1877,

and held officiating appointments at Port Blair and in the Nicobars; on special duty with the joint commissioner of Ladakh in 1880; appointed extra assistant superintendent, Port Blair, Feb., 1883; transferred to Berar as assistant commissioner, Nov., 1889; special assistant commissioner, June, 1893.

GOLDINGHAM, JOHN DALRYMPLE, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1853; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Madras, as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-judge, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; from July, 1862, to July, 1865, served as judge of the court of small causes, Madura, and from Oct., 1868, to Oct., 1872, as civil and sessions judge at the same place; district and sessions judge, Nellore, Nov., 1872, to Feb., 1881, and afterwards at Bellary; retired, Sept., 1888.

GOLDSMITH, SEPTIMUS JESSE, Surgeon-Major.—Joined the Bombay medical service, April, 1873; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1873; served as civil surgeon in North Canara and Guzerat; agency surgeon, Bhaghelkhand, Sept., 1875; also tutor to Maharaja of Rewah from Oct., 1883; afterwards in medical charge of Bhaghelkhand agency and dispensaries; retired, March, 1891.

GOLDWYER-LEWIS, The Ven. ALEXANDER, B.A., B.D., Ecclesiastical Department, Bombay.—Joined the service as chaplain, 19th May, 1875; senior chaplain, May, 1885; archdeacon of Bombay and commissary, Oct., 1890.

GOMPERTZ, HENRY JAMES COLLEY, late Madras Revenue Survey Department.—Appointed, 10th Aug., 1859; served as assistant superintendent on special duty on boundary questions, Bellary, 1871-72; deputy superintendent, 1st class, July, 1887; employed in Oct., 1888, on special duty on boundary questions, Pudukottah, and in Jan., 1889, Karikal and Tanjore; retired, Jan., 1893.

GONNE, CHARLES, C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College in 1853; arrived in India, 10th March, 1854; served in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, and assistant judge and sessions judge, and was appointed judge of the small cause court, Ahmednagar, May, 1861; joint judge and sessions judge, Thana, March, 1863, and acted as judge and sessions judge, June, 1863, to March, 1864; acting judge and sessions judge, Poona, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, April-June, 1864; acting registrar, high court, Bombay, June-Oct., 1864; from Oct., 1864, served as secretary to government of Bombay in the judicial and political departments; chief secretary to government in the political, secret, judicial, and separate departments, April, 1879; temporary member of council, Bombay, March-April, 1884; retired in 1885.

GOOD, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Jan., 1879.

GOODFELLOW, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, Lieut.-General, R.E., V.C., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, July, 1853; 2nd lieutenant, June, 1855; arrived in India, March, 1857; served with the Central India field force, 1857-58 (medal and clasp, V.C.), and with the Okhamandal and Kathiawar field force,

1859; joined the public works department as special assistant engineer, harbour defences, Bombay, April, 1860; executive engineer, Feb., 1862; joined the Abyssinian expedition, Dec., 1867, as brigade major of royal engineers (medal); executive engineer, 1st grade, Jan., 1877; superintending engineer, 3rd class, May, 1884; 2nd and 1st class, Nov., 1886; chief engineer for irrigation and officiating superintending engineer, September, 1887; chief engineer, 1st class, Aug., 1888; reverted to military duty, Nov., 1891.

GOODFELLOW, WILLIAM WEST, General, C.B., R.E., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—2nd lieutenant, Bombay Engineers, Dec., 1851; arrived in India, Oct., 1853; joined the public works department, July, 1854; served with the Persian expeditionary force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp), and with the Abyssinian expedition, 1867 (C.B. and medal); executive engineer, Bombay defences, Jan., 1869, to May, 1870; acting controller of public works accounts, June - July, 1870; served subsequently as executive engineer at Aden and in the Poona districts; from June, 1876, served as superintending engineer, and was promoted to 1st class, March, 1882; chief engineer, 1st class, and secretary to government of Bombay, public works department, April, 1885; returned to Europe, 1887.

GOODRICH, HARRY ST. AUBYN, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Eton, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in Madras from July, 1865, as assistant collector and magistrate, deputy director of revenue settlement, acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; acting district and sessions judge, Nellore, Feb.-Sept., 1881; appointed collector and magistrate, March, 1882, in South Canara, and subsequently in the districts of Ganjam and Vizagapatam; on duty to Calcutta as additional member of governor-general's legislative council, Dec., 1884, to March, 1885, and again, Nov., 1885, to March, 1886; on special duty at Madras conferring upon proposals for legislating on relations of landlord and tenant in Malabar, March, 1885; member of the draft tenancy bill committee, July, 1885; collector and magistrate, Bellary, Nov., 1885; retired, Feb., 1890.

GOODRICK, G. M., late Bengal Customs Department.—Served from 30th Nov., 1859, as preventive officer and appraiser, Calcutta custom house, and was appointed assistant collector of customs, July, 1871, and deputy collector of Calcutta, March, 1880; also superintendent of excise revenue, collector of stamp revenue, and a collector under the licence tax act; retired, 1889.

GOODRIDGE, JOHN PERCH, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 3rd Nov., 1869; served as assistant commissioner in the Central Provinces, and held acting appointments at various dates as district superintendent of police, inspector of schools, cantonment magistrate, and small cause court judge, and was deputed on famine duty to Madras, Sept., 1877; assistant commissioner, 1st class, Bilaspur, Nov., 1884; deputy commissioner, Sept., 1889; in addition political agent, Chhattisgarh feuda-

tories, Dec., 1889; officiated as commissioner of settlements and agriculture in 1889; and as commissioner, Chhattisgarh division, in 1890; additional sessions judge, Jubbulpore and Nerbudda divisions, Jan., 1892.

GOPAL DAS, Rai Bahadur, late Punjab Commission.—Served in the settlement department and subordinate judicial department of the Punjab from 6th July, 1851; appointed extra judicial assistant, Oct., 1888; retired, 1889; author of a 'Manual for preparation of Records of Rights' and of histories of the Gujranwala and Peshawur districts.

GOPENDRA KRISHNA DEB, KUMAR, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Served in the subordinate executive service, Bengal, from 27th Jan., 1876, and officiated as registrar of Calcutta and second inspector of registration offices; appointed to the Bengal civil service under the native civil service rules, Nov., 1882, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Sept., 1891.

GORDON, ALEXANDER EVANS, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Bengal Non-Regulation Commission.—First commission, 18th April, 1865; from May, 1872, served in the Bengal non-regulation commission as assistant commissioner, and officiating deputy commissioner; on special duty with H.H. the Maharajah of Johore, Dec., 1875; cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, Dum-Dum, March, 1876; tutor to the Maharajah of Durbhunga, June, 1877; on special duty in Calcutta, Jan., 1880, and in the foreign department, Nov., 1880; on deputation to Cooch Behar, Dec., 1883; deputy commissioner, March, 1887; acted as judicial commissioner, Chota Nagpur, in 1893.

GORDON, DONALD CLUNES, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the public works department in 1868, as assistant controller of accounts, N.W. Provinces; served subsequently as deputy controller in the Punjab; controller, 3rd class, on the Indus Valley state railway, July, 1874, and afterwards on the Punjab Northern railway; examiner of guaranteed railway accounts, Bombay, 1877-79, and afterwards in Madras; examiner, public works accounts, Madras, May, 1881; examiner, 1st class, Sept., 1886; examiner, public works accounts, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, March, 1887; examiner, public works accounts, Punjab, March, 1891.

GORDON, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Radley College, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, April, 1893; returned to Bengal as joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1894.

GORDON, HAMILTON WINKUP, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1864; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating inspector of schools, July, 1872; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1879; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1881; has officiated at various periods as judge of high court, Calcutta.

GORDON, HUNTLY PRYSE, late Madras C.S.—Educated at New College, London, and Trinity

College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, small cause court judge, assistant commissioner (Nilgiri Hills), and acting district and sessions judge; appointed collector and magistrate, Kurnool, Dec., 1878, and afterwards at Bellary, April, 1880; and in South Arcot, Nov., 1885; retired, Oct., 1887.

GORE, ST. GEORGE CORBET, Major, R.E., Survey of India Department.—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1870; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1872; from March, 1873, served on the great trigonometrical survey of India; on service in Afghanistan, Feb., 1879, to May, 1880, with Sir D. Stewart's column on march from Kandahar to Kabul, as officer in charge of survey; attached to Afghan boundary commission, Aug., 1884; deputy superintendent, March, 1886; on military duty in Europe, Feb., 1888, to Jan., 1889; charge of trigonometrical branch office, drawing section, Jan., 1889; officiating assistant surveyor-general, Oct., 1889, to Oct., 1890; superintendent, trigonometrical surveys, Dehra Dun, May, 1894.

GORST, RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN ELDON, Q.C., M.P.—Graduated at St. John's College, Cambridge; 3rd wrangler, 1857; M.A., 1860; fellow of his college; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1865; Q.C., 1875; civil commissioner of Waikats, New Zealand, 1861 to 1863; represented Cambridge (borough) in the House of Commons, 1866 to 1868, and Chatham, 1875 to 1892, when he became member for Cambridge University; solicitor-general in Lord Salisbury's first administration, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; under secretary of state for India, in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886, to Nov., 1891, when he was appointed financial secretary to the treasury; was British plenipotentiary at the labour conference at Berlin, 1890; author of 'The Maori King.'

GOSS, LOUIS ALLAN, Education Department, Burma.—Held non-gazetted appointments from 5th Aug., 1874, to 7th April, 1878, when he was appointed junior inspector of schools; senior inspector, March, 1893; author of 'The Story of Wethan-da-ya.'

GOUDGE, JOSEPH ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Queen's College, Taunton, London University, and Pembroke College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

GOUGH, ARCHIBALD EDWARD, M.A., Educational Service, North-Western Provinces.—Educated at Lincoln College, Oxford; joined the department, 2nd Nov., 1868, and served as Anglo-Sanskrit professor, Benares college, and as professor of English literature and professor of history and philosophy at the Muir college, Allahabad, and also officiated for various periods between 1872 and 1878 as principal, Benares college, and held charge of the meteorological observatory; served in Bengal, 1878-86, as professor in the Hooghly and Presidency colleges, and principal of the Calcutta Madrasa; principal, Muir central college, Allahabad, July, 1886; author of 'Papers relating to the Collection and Preservation of the Records of Ancient Sanskrit Literature in India,' 1878, &c.

GOULDSBURY, DUDLEY ELPHINSTONE, Lieut.-Col., Punjab Administration.—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1866; arrived, 1st March, 1867; served in military department to June, 1882, when he was transferred to the Punjab as officiating cantonment magistrate, Ferozepur; re-transferred to military department, Oct., 1882; resumed his appointment, May, 1883; transferred to Jullundur, Aug., 1889; 1st class cantonment magistrate, Feb., 1891.

GOVAN, GEORGE MONCRIEFF, Surgeon-Major, M.D., L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.E., late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, Dec., 1851; arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1852, and served under the military department up to 1883, in Bengal, Burma, and Assam, and throughout the Bhootan campaign, 1864-66 (medal and clasp for Burma); rank of surgeon, June, 1864; surgeon-major, Dec., 1871; from Jan., 1883, served under the government of the N.W. Provinces, and was appointed civil surgeon, 2nd class, Fategarh, and posted to Azamgarh; officiating deputy sanitary commissioner, Aug., 1883; superintendent of pilgrim hospitals in Kumaun and Garhwal in addition to other duties, Aug., 1883; served subsequently as civil surgeon at Budaon and at Almora; retired, March, 1887.

GRACE, CHARLES HALLYBURTON, Col., I.S.C., late Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, as ensign, Madras infantry, March, 1854; served in the Central Provinces from Sept., 1859, as assistant commissioner, deputy commissioner, and settlement officer, and was appointed deputy commissioner, 1st class, Sept., 1883, at Jubbulpore; acted as commissioner of the Jubbulpore division, May-June, 1882; reverted to military department, 1892.

GRACEY, HUGH KIRKWOOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at City of London School, London University, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 17th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector and assistant commissioner.

GRACEY, THOMAS, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Educated at Enniskillen Royal School; 1st commission, 17th Dec., 1862; 1st assistant engineer, N.W. Provinces, Sept., 1867; executive engineer, Sept., 1870; engineer-in-chief, Pindi to Kohat section of Punjab Northern railway, 1879; engineer-in-chief, provincial railways under construction, N.W. Provinces and Oudh railway branch, Oct., 1882; special superintending engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, public works department, Burma, April, 1887; superintending engineer, Feb., 1888; officiating consulting engineer for railways, and joint secretary to government, railway branch, Madras, July, 1890; officiating consulting engineer to the government of India for railways, central division, March, 1891; confirmed, June, 1891; in similar capacity, Lucknow, June, 1892; chief engineer, June, 1892; officiating director-general of railways, and deputy secretary, public works department, railway branch, July, 1894.

GRAHAM, ROBERT ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Winchester, and Brasenose College, Oxford; appointed after open

competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

GRAHAME, WILLIAM FITZWILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at the Charterhouse, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

GRAHAME, WILLIAM FRANCIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1861; arrived, 4th Jan., 1864; served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments from Oct., 1866, and was placed on special duty in July, 1868, with Dr. Janssen in connection with the solar eclipse; collector and magistrate, Anantapur, June, 1882; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1882.

GRANT, ALEXANDER, India Public Works Department. —Appointed assistant engineer, July, 1867, and employed in N.W. Provinces and Oudh on irrigation works; executive engineer, March, 1873; under-secretary to government, irrigation branch, in 1888; superintending engineer, Jan., 1893.

GRANT, ARTHUR JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Berkhamstead Grammar School, Dulwich College, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1889, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; officiating under secretary to government, Nov., 1891; junior secretary to financial commissioners, March-Nov., 1892.

GRANT, SIR CHARLES, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Harrow, Trinity College, Cambridge, and Haileybury College, from which he was appointed to the Bengal civil service in 1858; arrived in India, 8th April, 1858; attached to the N.W. Provinces, and served in the revenue and settlement departments; transferred to Central Provinces, Nov., 1863, and served as settlement officer and acting deputy commissioner; in charge of settlement commissioner's office, Jubbulpore, Jan., 1868; secretary to chief commissioner, Central Provinces, Nov., 1861; compiled the 'Central Provinces Gazetteer'; commissioner of Chhattisgarh division, Nov., 1870, and subsequently of the Jubbulpore and Narbada divisions; acting judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, Aug., 1877; acting chief commissioner, April to Nov., 1879; additional member of governor-general's council, Dec., 1879; acting secretary, government of India, home, revenue, and agricultural departments, May, 1880; foreign secretary, government of India, Sept., 1881; retired, Feb., 1885.

GRANT, CHARLES, Major-General, late Political Department, Government of India.—Ensign, 4th Jan., 1857; arrived in India, 6th Feb., 1857; served with the Guzerat Horse, and subsequently with the Mahratta Horse; special officer in charge, Kathiawar local battalion, and extra assistant political agent, Dec., 1867; in charge of state prison, Asirgarh, March, 1870; assistant resident, Persian Gulf, June, 1871, and acted as resident in 1877; political agent, Eastern States, Rajputana, April, 1878; political agent, Muscat, Feb., 1880; superintendent, Rajputana railway police, Dec., 1880; from Feb., 1881, served as cantonment magistrate, Nimach; retired, Jan., 1887.

GRANT, GEORGE, Brigade-Surgeon, M.B., late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 10th Feb., 1859; arrived in India, 11th July, 1859, and served under the military department till 1860; from June, 1860, served under the government of the N.W. Provinces as civil assistant surgeon, Bijnor, and was in medical charge of the lieutenant-governor's camp, Nov., 1861; civil assistant surgeon and superintendent of jail, Farakhabad, March, 1862; on special deputation to Calcutta to give evidence in a criminal case at the high court, Jan., 1864; officiating superintendent, government press, and curator of government books, N.W. Provinces, and chemical examiner, N.W. Provinces, Oudh, and Central Provinces, Aug., 1866; superintendent of new central prison, Farakhabad, Dec., 1866; rank of surgeon, Feb., 1871; representative of N.W. Provinces at the prison committee, Calcutta, Jan., 1877; officiating inspector-general of prisons and civil hospitals, N.W. Provinces, in 1877, 1879, and 1882; superintendent, central and district jails, Bareilly, Dec., 1877; deputy sanitary commissioner, 1st circle, Dec., 1883; retired, Oct., 1885.

GRANT, GEORGE FRANCIS MYTTON, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Eton, and Brighton; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1864, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant commissioner, branch registrar-general, education inspector in Sind, and acting talukdari settlement officer; in charge of the Sachim state, May, 1879; acted as collector, magistrate, and agent at Surat, 1879-80, and subsequently in the Panch Mahals, and at Broach, Kaira, and Kaladgi; junior collector of salt revenue, Bombay, Dec., 1882; acting collector of Bombay and superintendent of stamps and stationery, Nov., 1883; acting collector of customs, Bombay, May, 1884; junior collector and magistrate and political agent, Thana, March, 1885; senior collector, Sept., 1886; acting commissioner, northern division, May, 1889, southern division, June, 1889; appointed commissioner, northern division, April, 1890, and 1st grade commissioner, June, 1890; retired, Nov., 1890.

GRANT, GREGORY COLQUHOUN, Colonel, I.S.C., late Judicial Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 4th Nov., 1853; on military duty till Nov., 1860, including service with the Okhamandal field force, in Oct., 1859; served subsequently as deputy collector and magistrate in Sind; judge and sessions judge, March, 1865; also acted as judicial commissioner in Sind, and judge of the sadar court; reverted to military department, 1890.

GRANT, J. G. GARROW, Dr., late Emigration Department, Bengal.—Served in the emigration department from 22nd May, 1863, as medical inspector and protector of labourers, Kooshtea; on special duty at the Presidency, Oct., 1870; protector of emigrants and superintendent of labour transports, Calcutta, Feb., 1871; superintendent of emigration, Calcutta, Jan., 1874; retired, 1889.

GRANT, JOHN ANDREW, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 3rd Jan., 1878, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating

deputy commissioner, and officiating judicial assistant; served under the foreign department, government of India, at Kurram, May-Aug., 1880; transferred to Umballa-Karnal settlement as settlement officer, Dec., 1883; officiating junior secretary to financial commissioner, Sept., 1884; officiating registrar, chief court, June, 1885; on special duty at Sialkot, Nov., 1885; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, Sept., 1887, and 1st grade, April, 1890.

GRANT, JOHN HAY, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, 1856; arrived, 6th Feb., 1857, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, railway magistrate, assistant revenue and police commissioner, acting collector and magistrate, and acting commissioner of customs, salt, and opium; junior collector, Kaladgi, Sept., 1877; municipal commissioner, Bombay, Nov., 1877, to Feb., 1881; collector of Bombay, and superintendent of stamps and stationery, Aug., 1883; special commissioner for Bombay for purpose of international exhibition at Calcutta, Nov.-Dec., 1883; president of a commission to make certain enquiries in connection with abkari act, July, 1885; commissioner of income tax for town and island of Bombay, in addition to his other duties, April, 1886; retired, Jan., 1888.

GRANT, JOHN PYNE, Colonel, I.S.C., late Revenue Survey of Mysore.—First commission as ensign, Bombay infantry, Jan., 1856; joined the revenue survey, 1860; superintendent of revenue survey, Mysore, Jan., 1868; retired, Jan., 1890.

GRANT, THOMAS, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 28th May, 1875.

GRANT, TREVOR JOHN CHICHELE, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 27th Jan., 1858; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, in the grades of assistant and joint magistrate, and deputy collector, and was promoted to magistrate and collector, May, 1874; officiating secretary, board of revenue, April, 1874; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, March, 1878; retired, Jan., 1893.

GRANT-BROWN, GEORGE EUSTACE RIOU, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served as assistant commissioner.

GRANT-DUFF, Rt. HON. SIR MOUNTSTUART ELPHINSTONE, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S.—Born in 1829; educated at Edinburgh, and at Balliol College, Oxford (M.A., 1853); called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1854; represented the Elgin burghs in parliament from Dec., 1857, to July, 1881; under secretary of state for India, Dec., 1868, to Feb., 1874; under secretary of state for the colonies, April, 1880, to Aug., 1881; governor of Madras, Nov., 1881, to Dec., 1886; was lord rector of the university of Aberdeen, 1866-72; author of 'Studies in European Politics,' 'Elgin Speeches,' 'A Political Survey,' &c.

GRAVES, SOMERSET HENRY PAUL, Major, I.S.C., late Burma Police Department.—First commission, 2nd Sept., 1868; served in the 26th native infantry, and in June, 1885, was appointed superintendent of police, Port Blair; deputy inspector-general of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; reverted to military duty, 1888.

GRAY, EVELYN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Central

Provinces).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived, 27th Dec., 1878, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; forest settlement officer, March, 1884, and again in 1886 and 1889; acted as junior collector and magistrate in 1886 and 1887; 2nd assistant collector and magistrate, May, 1889; transferred to Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, Dec., 1891; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1892.

GRAY, MALCOLM ALEXANDER, B.A., Major, I.S.C., Assam Commission.—Ensign, 3rd (Buffs) Regt., 7th Nov., 1868; arrived in India, 1st Feb., 1870; served in the Assam commission, from Aug., 1873, as officiating assistant commissioner and assistant inspector of immigrants; confirmed as assistant commissioner, July, 1877; forest settlement officer, Oct., 1885; deputy commissioner, Sept., 1888.

GRAY, PERCY, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Wellington College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 30th December, 1878, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating cantonment magistrate, Roorkee, Oct., 1883; junior secretary, board of revenue, April-July, 1885; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Sept., 1887, and assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Feb., 1890; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Dec., 1890; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, May, 1891; has also officiated as magistrate and collector, and registrar, high court; confirmed in the latter post, March, 1892; deputy commissioner, March, 1894.

GRAY, R., Brigade-Surgeon, M.B., late Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the Indian medical department in 1859; arrived in India, 11th March, 1860; civil assistant surgeon, Gujranwala, Feb., 1863; superintendent, central jail, Jan., 1864; civil surgeon, Mooltan, May, 1868; officiated as inspector-general of prisons, March, 1869, to March, 1871, and again in 1877; surgeon-major, July, 1873; in medical charge of lieutenant-governor's camp, Nov., 1877; served afterwards as civil surgeon of Murree, Mooltan, and Lahore; inspector-general of prisons, Punjab, Sept., 1884; retired, April, 1891.

GRAY, WELLINGTON, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, L.M., L. Ch., late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical service, 31st March, 1866; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1866, and was appointed professor of anatomy and curator of museum at the Grant medical college, and 2nd surgeon at the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital, Bombay; curator of Victoria and Albert museum, and professor of botany, Grant medical college, June, 1871; also acted as professor of chemistry and medical jurisprudence, and chemical analyst to government; secretary to the surgeon-general, Indian medical department, June, 1878; senior surgeon, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital, and professor of surgery and clinical surgery, Grant medical hospital, May, 1883; brigade-surgeon, June, 1888; principal of the Grant medical college, Dec., 1888; retired, April, 1893.

GREAVES, Lieut.-Gen. SIR GEORGE RICHARDS, K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—1st commission, 30th Nov., 1849; served in the Indian mutiny of 1857-58, and in the Eusofzye expedition of 1858 (medal with clasp); served in the war in New Zealand

as deputy-assistant-quartermaster-general, 1862-66 (brevets of major and lieut.-col., and medal); chief of the staff of Ashantee expedition of 1873-74, and was present at battles of Omoafu and Ordahsu, and capture of Coomassie (medal with clasp and C.B.); served in Soudan campaign in 1885 as chief of the staff to Sir Gerald Graham (K.C.B., medal with clasp); appointed commander-in-chief of Bombay army, and second member of council of governor of Bombay, and took his seat, 13th March, 1890; resigned, April, 1893.

GREAVES, RICHARD HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 19th Jan., 1870; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating magistrate and collector, and officiating district and sessions judge; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, March, 1884; transferred to Assam for employment as district and sessions judge, Sylhet, Oct., 1887.

GREENE, WALTER JAMES, Financial Department, India Office.—Writer, 1st July, 1867; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878; clerk for examination of army non-effective accounts, March, 1885.

GREENSHIELDS, ROBERT SCOTT, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Birkenhead School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1879; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1879; transferred to Assam, March, 1882, and served as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, Assam, March, 1886; officiated as director of the department of land revenue and agriculture, in 1889; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1889.

GREENSTREET, WILLIAM LEES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Military Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission as lieutenant, Oct., 1864; arrived in India, Jan., 1870; joined the public works department, Feb., 1871, as assistant engineer, N.W. Provinces; transferred to military works branch, 1872; executive engineer, Nov., 1874; superintending engineer, July, 1885; took part in the Hazara expedition, 1891; chief engineer, Sept., 1893.

GREER, RICHARD TOWNSEND, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Erasmus College, Drog-heda, and Kingstown School, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived, 10th Dec., 1877, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner, assistant secretary to chief commissioner, and officiating deputy commissioner; returned to Bengal, Nov., 1888, as joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, March, 1893.

GREEVEN, RICHARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Dulwich and at Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1888, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Dec., 1891; assistant superintending and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, March, 1892, to Feb., 1893; author of 'Heroes Five.'

GREGG, WILLIAM HENRY, M.B., D.P.H., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1869; served in the

4th Bengal native infantry, May, 1870, to Dec., 1873; in the Looshai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp); civil surgeon, Dinagepore, 1873, Jessore, 1875, Hooghly, 1876-86; lecturer on botany, Hooghly government college, 1877-86; officiating protector of emigrants, Calcutta, April, 1888; sanitary commissioner, Bengal, Jan., 1889.

GREIG, G., late Forest Department, North-Western Provinces.—Arrived in India, 20th Sept., 1857, as a lieutenant, 93rd Sutherland Highlanders; retired from the army, June, 1865; joined the forest department, Dec., 1868, and was appointed assistant conservator, Aug., 1869, deputy conservator, March, 1871, and conservator, Nov., 1876; promoted to 1st grade of conservators, April, 1883; retired, 1889.

GREY, CHARLES, late Political Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, military department, India office, 18th May, 1864; private secretary to parliamentary under secretary of state, April-July, 1866; financial department, 1866; assistant précis writer to the secretary of state, 1872; accompanied Sir Bartle Frere on his mission to Zanzibar, 1872-73; political department, 1874; reading clerk to council from 1875 to 1890; senior clerk, April, 1877; retired, Feb., 1894.

GREY, LEOPOLD JOHN HERBERT, C.S.I., Colonel, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 3rd Jan., 1857; assistant commissioner in Assam, Feb., 1865; officiating civil officer with Bhootan Dooar field force, Dec., 1865; from April, 1866, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, but was employed under the government of Bengal, Dec., 1868, to June, 1869; on special duty, Oct., 1869, to March, 1870; political agent, Bahawalpur state, March, 1871, to Jan., 1873, and again in 1877; judicial assistant, 1st grade, July, 1876; on special duty in connection with imperial assemblage, Sept., 1876, to Jan., 1877; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1876; officiating civil and session judge, Peshawur, Aug., 1881; secretary to board of examiners (under government of India), March, 1882; commissioner and superintendent, Delhi division, March, 1886; officiated as first financial commissioner in 1891; and as second in 1893; reverted to military department, Dec., 1894.

GREY, WILLIAM FRANCIS HUNGERFORD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commission.—Ensign, 2-19th regiment, Aug., 1868; served in the Burma commission from April, 1874, as assistant commissioner, and acted as assistant secretary to chief commissioner, June-Oct., 1876; boundary officer, Paungde, Aug., 1881; cantonment magistrate, and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, Sept., 1882; was elected president of the Kyaukpyu town committee, March, 1884; and of the Akyab municipal committee, Dec., 1885; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1886.

GRIERSON, GEORGE ABRAHAM, C.I.E., B.A., Ph.D., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Bees, Shrewsbury, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived, 25th Oct., 1873; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating magistrate and collector, and officiating cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge; on famine duty, March, 1874; officiating inspector of schools, Oct., 1880; on special duty in the revenue department, Dec., 1882; joint magistrate

and deputy collector, Oct., 1884; magistrate and collector, March, 1890; C.I.E., May, 1894; hon. Ph.D. (Halle), Aug., 1894; has published various works in connection with the Behari language and its dialects, translations, collections of folk songs, &c.; also 'Behar Peasant Life,' 1885; 'Report on Colonial Emigration from the Lower Provinces of Bengal,' 1883; 'Curiosities of Indian Literature,' 1885; 'Vidyapati and his Contemporaries,' 1885; 'Modern Vernacular Literature of Hindustan,' 1890; 'Inscriptions of Pigadasi,' 1891.

GRIERSON, JOHN, Colonel, I.S.C., late India Public Works Department.—2nd lieutenant, Bombay artillery, Dec., 1857; arrived in India, 24th Jan., 1858; joined the P. W. department, Feb., 1864; executive engineer, Jan. 1871; from May, 1872, served as deputy controller of accounts, Bombay; deputy examiner, N.W. Provinces, Aug., 1875; examiner of accounts, Dec., 1876; transferred to Burma, July, 1880, and subsequently to Bombay, and to N.W. Provinces; examiner of telegraph accounts, Aug., 1885; examiner, public works accounts, Rajputana and Central India, March, 1889; N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Sept., 1890; reverted to military department, Aug., 1894.

GRIESBACH, CARL LUDOLF, C.I.E., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India.—Appointed assistant superintendent, 26th Sept., 1878; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1878; on special duty with troops in southern Afghanistan, 1880; employed on Afghan boundary commission, 1884-86; deputy superintendent, Nov., 1886; on deputation as geologist to H.H. the Amir of Kabul, 1888-89; superintendent, June, 1889; director, July, 1894; author of numerous contributions to the survey publications.

GRIFFIN, HENRY DALY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at French College, Blackrock, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant superintendent, Dehra Dun, April, 1889; joint magistrate, July, 1890.

GRIFFIN, SIR LEPEL HENRY, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1860, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; under secretary to government, Punjab, April, 1870; on special duty to frame track rules between Punjab and Rajputana states, Feb., 1873; superintendent, Kapurthala state, April, 1875; deputy commissioner, 1st class, April, 1877; acted as secretary to government, Punjab, from Feb., 1876, and was confirmed in this appointment, Nov., 1878; on special duty at the Paris exhibition, July, 1878; during the occupation of Afghanistan, held the special appointment of chief political officer in north and west districts of that country (Afghanistan medal); resident, 1st class, and agent to the governor-general, Central India, Feb., 1881; K.C.S.I., May, 1881; royal commissioner, Indian and colonial exhibition, 1886; retired, Jan., 1889; author of 'Punjab Chiefs,' 1865, 'The Law of Inheritance to Chiefs,' 1869, 'The Great Republic,' 1884, and 'Famous Monuments of Central India,' 1886.

GRIFFITH, HUGH, Brigade-Surgeon, late Medical Department, Burma.—Joined the Indian medical department (Madras establishment), 1859; served under the military department from 1859 to 1871, and also held civil surgeoncies of Thayety-myo and Hoshungabad during that period; civil surgeon and health officer, Rangoon, April, 1871; also superintendent of lunatic asylum, and in charge of state prisons and lock hospital, June, 1871; in medical charge of Rangoon jail, Nov., 1871, to March, 1885; also superintendent of vaccination, May, 1875; in medical charge, Burma state railway, Nov., 1885; brigade-surgeon, March, 1886; retired, May, 1890.

GRIFFITH, RALPH THOMAS HOTCHKIN, M.A., C.I.E., late Educational Department, North-Western Provinces.—Arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1853, and served as professor and head master, Benares college, also as officiating inspector of public instruction, 3rd circle; principal, Benares college, Jan., 1861; superintendent of observatory, Benares, May, 1866; promoted to 1st grade, April, 1873; on special committee under government of India for selection of text-books, May, 1877; inspector-general of schools, N.W. Provinces, March, 1878; director of public instruction, Feb., 1879; retired, 1885.

GRIFFITHS, WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., Education Department, Bengal.—Appointed, 1st July, 1869; arrived in India, 20th Jan., 1870, and served as assistant professor, civil engineering department, Presidency college; professor, Presidency college, Feb., 1876; fellow of Calcutta university, 1879; principal, Hooghly college, April, 1880; principal, Presidency college, Dec., 1892.

GRIGG, EDWARD EVANS, Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Infantry, Oudh Commission.—Arrived in India as ensign, Dec., 1861; served under the military department till Jan., 1864, and subsequently as assistant commissioner in Assam up to March, 1867, when he was transferred to the Oudh commission, and served as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner in various districts; officiating cantonment magistrate, Lucknow, May-June, 1881; deputy commissioner, May, 1885; officiating commissioner, May to Aug., 1892, and from April, 1894.

GRIGG, HENRY BIDEWELL, M.A., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Elizabeth College, Guernsey, and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived, 8th Jan., 1865; assistant collector and magistrate, May, 1866; acting inspector of schools, Feb., 1867; from March, 1872, to Feb., 1876, served as assistant commissioner and acting commissioner, Nilgiris; acting under secretary to government of Madras, revenue department, Feb., 1876, and acting secretary, March, 1877; on special duty, preparing annual administration report, Oct., 1877; under secretary, public department, Oct., 1879; acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, Feb., 1880; fellow of university of Madras, March, 1880; director of public instruction and commissioner for the uncov. civil service examinations, July, 1880; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; acting resident in Travancore and Cochin in 1890, and again in 1891; appointed to that post, Nov., 1892; compiler of the 'Nilgiri District Manual.'

GRIMES, HENRY ROBERT, late Jail Department, Madras.—Assistant deputy commissioner of paper currency, Vizagapatam, 29th Nov.,

1864; from April, 1866, served as superintendent of the central jail, Coimbatore; inspector-general of jails, Madras, July, 1884; retired, April, 1889.

GRIMLEY, REGINALD FEARNS, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at King Edward's School, Birmingham, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

GRIMLEY, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King Edward's School, Birmingham, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived 12th Dec., 1863; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiated as superintendent of surveys, Feb., 1869, and as inspector of schools, Aug., 1870; on special duty in connection with the examination of candidates for the native civil service, Jan., 1874; officiated as secretary to the board of revenue, 1874 to 1877, and again in 1879 and 1883-84; junior secretary to the board of revenue, 1877-79; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, March, 1879; officiating collector of customs and ex-officio shipping master, Calcutta, April, 1881; served under the financial department from April, 1883; commissioner of income tax in Bengal, March, 1886; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Dec., 1887; officiating commissioner, Chota Nagpur, April, 1889; confirmed, Oct., 1889; author of 'The Sea Customs Law,' 1878, 'Tariff Act, with notes,' an 'Income Tax Manual' (being Act II of 1886 with notes), 'Manual of Revenue Sale Law, and Certificate Procedure.'

GRINDLAY, WALTER, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Fettes College, Edinburgh, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1881; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; retired, 1888.

GRISH CHANDER CHAUDHURI, B.L., Bengal Judicial Department.—Served in the judicial department from 9th March, 1866, as subordinate judge, and judge of small cause courts; appointed subordinate judge, 1st grade, April, 1883; small cause court judge, Hugli, Nov., 1891.

GROSE, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S.
