(Madras).—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1859; arrived, 7th Oct., 1860; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, under secretary to government in the revenue department, secretary to commissioner for uncov. civil service examinations, secretary to the central committee for the examination of assistants, sub-secretary board of revenue (Aug., 1867), and receiver of the Carnatic property; fellow of the Madras university, May, 1868; acting collector of sea customs and protector of emigrants, Madras, March, 1870; secretary to board of revenue, 1870 to 1877; collector and magistrate, Nellore, March, 1877; acting member, board of revenue, April, 1886; acting director of revenue settlement, Nov., 1886; on special duty to Delhi, March, 1888; acting inam

commissioner, May, 1883; additional member of legislative council in 1888, and 1891-92; member of board of revenue, and commissioner of land revenue, Jan., 1889; member of executive council, Jan., 1894.

GROVES, HENRY SAMUEL, B.A., Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as probationer, financial department, July, 1873; served in the offices of the accountant-generals of Bombay, Punjab, Madras, and Bengal; assistant accountant-general, Bombay, Nov., 1887; deputy accountant-general, Punjab, Nov., 1890; deputy accountant-general, Madras, April, 1893; officiating comptroller, Central Provinces, June, 1893.

GROVES, HERBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed to the department, 23rd July, 1868, and posted to Mysore; executive engineer, Feb., 1876; transferred to Central India, Oct., 1882, and employed on state railways; transferred to Burma, May, 1887; services lent to Mysore state, July, 1890; under secretary to government of Mysore, railway branch, July, 1891; joint secretary, July, 1892.

GROWSE, EDWARD FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Charterhouse, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived, 18th Nov., 1881; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, July, 1890.

GRUNING, JOHN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Eastbourne College, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

GUBBINS, FREDERICK BEBB, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1835; served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, Delhi division; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Moradabad, 1842, and subsequently in Rohtak and Banda; magistrate and collector of Benares, 1852; civil and sessions judge, Benares, 1857; commissioner of revenue and circuit, Benares, agent to governor-general, and superintendent of ex-rajah of Coorg, 1858; retired, 1863.

GUN, WILLIAM HENRY MAFFETT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Greenock Academy, and Glasgow University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived, 17th Nov., 1871; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1883; district and sessions judge, December, 1888.

GUNGARAM, Rai Bahadur, M.I.M.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed to the department, 2nd Oct., 1873, and posted to Punjab provincial establishment; executive engineer, May, 1883.

GUNTER, AUSTIN HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Dover College, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

GUPTA, INANENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Calcutta University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in

India, 12th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

GUPTA, KALI PADA, M.A., M.B., F.R.C.S., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Department.—Joined the service, 13th June, 1872, on inspection duty, medical relief measures, Burdwan district; served as superintendent of vaccination and on general and jail duty; on special duty to Nuddea medical fever relief operations in 1881; resumed his post as superintendent of vaccination, metropolitan circle, Nov., 1886; in 1888 acted in addition as sanitary commissioner for Bengal; civil surgeon, July, 1891.

GURDON, EVELYN PULTENEY, Major-General, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 2nd April, 1852; from Aug., 1860, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, deputy commissioner, and small cause court judge; promoted to deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Jan., 1881; additional commissioner, Jullundur, Umballa, and Amritsar divisions, Nov.-Dec., 1881, and subsequently in the Lahore and Rawal Pindi divisions; promoted to commissioner and superintendent, Aug., 1884; divisional judge, 1st grade, Oct., 1885; retired from civil employment, Jan., 1890.

GURDYL SINGH, Sirdar, Punjab Commission.—Probationer under native civil service rules, 26th Jan., 1880; assistant commissioner, Jan., 1882; district judge, Nov., 1892.

GÜTERSLOH, FREDERIC NICHOLAS, late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Joined the public works department, June, 1874, as assistant engineer on the Indus Valley state railway; in charge of locomotive and carriage department, Holkar state railway, Oct., 1875; officiating manager and locomotive superintendent, Holkar state railway, 1877; manager, Wardha Valley railway, Dec., 1878; locomotive superintendent, Rajputana railways, March, 1880; 1st class, 2nd grade, locomotive department, state railways, Jan., 1886, and 1st grade, March, 1890; retired, June, 1891.

HABIB-ULLAH, SAIYID, Maulvi, Barrister-at-Law, Statutory C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed by the government of India to be an assistant magistrate and collector, N.W. Provinces, under the statutory rules, 14th Feb., 1887; joint magistrate, Sept., 1892.

HACKET, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, late Geological Survey Department.—Joined the department as assistant, 15th Nov., 1861; served in the Sone Valley, on the Vindhyan, in Central India, and in Jabalpur and South Rewah; promoted to 2nd grade, June, 1881; designation changed to deputy superintendent of the geological survey of India; deputy superintendent, 1st grade, Dec., 1887; officiated also as superintendent; retired, July, 1888.

HACKETT, ARTHUR LUKE, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, 1st April, 1867; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1867; served under the military department, also acting as civil surgeon at various stations, and as superintendent of jail; surgeon, April, 1873; from June, 1874, acted as presidency surgeon, also as ophthalmic surgeon at the general hospital, Sept, 1874; garrison assistant surgeon, Bangalore, and superintendent of jail, 1877-78; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Bellary, 1878-81; surgeon-major, April, 1879; surgeon, 3rd district,

Madras, Feb., 1881, and acted as superintendent of prisons, in 1881 and 1883; was subsequently appointed civil surgeon at Cannanore, April, 1883, at Salem, July, 1883, and at Negapatam; principal medical storekeeper, Madras, 1885; surgeon, 2nd district, Madras, health officer, and superintendent of lock hospital, Oct., 1885.

HAIG, CHARLES THOMAS, Major-General, R.E., late Survey of India.—Obtained his first commission from Addiscombe, June, 1854; lieutenant, Bombay engineers, May, 1856; arrived at Bombay, July, 1856; served with the Persian expeditionary force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp), and with the Rajputana field force, 1857 (medal and clasp); assistant executive engineer at Nee-much, May, 1858; appointed as assistant in the great trigonometrical survey of India, Sept., 1859; served with the Okhamandal field force in 1859, and was subsequently variously employed in the survey department in Cutch, Kattywar, Guzerat, Meywar, Malwa, the Deccan, and Kanara; deputy superintendent, Oct., 1867; on deputation as commissioner, Venice geographical exhibition, April-Nov., 1881; deputy superintendent, 1st grade, Nov., 1883; officiating deputy surveyor-general in charge of trigonometrical survey branch, Oct., 1884; confirmed, Feb., 1887; retired from civil employment, 1890; and from the army, April, 1891.

HAILEY, HAMMET REGINALD CLODE, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School, and St. John's College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner.

HAINES, SIR FREDERICK PAUL, Field-Marshal, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Entered the army as ensign in 1839; captain in 1846; lieut.-colonel in 1850; colonel in 1854; major-general in 1864; military secretary to Sir Hugh Gough in the war on the Sutlej, 1845; was present at the battles of Moodkee and Ferozeshah (medal and clasp); served through the Punjab campaign of 1848 and 1849, and was present at Ramnuggar, the passage of the Chenab, and the battles of Chilianwallah and Goojerat; served with the 21st fusiliers through the campaign of the Crimea, 1854-55 (medal with four clasps, 5th class of the Medjidie and Turkish medal); K.C.B., 1871; lieutenant-general, 1873; brevet-general, and G.C.B., 1877; commander-in-chief of the Madras army, and member of the governor's council, May, 1871 to 1875; appointed colonel of the 104th regiment, 1874; commander-in-chief of the forces in India, with local rank of general, and extraordinary member of the governor-general's council, April, 1876 to 1881, and directed the military operations of the Afghan campaigns of 1878-79 and 1879-80; thanked by both houses of parliament, 4th Aug., 1879, and 5th of May, 1881.

HAINES, HERMANN ANDERSON, Political Department, India Office.—Educated at Wellington College, and Keble College, Oxford; junior clerk, financial department, India office, 15th Nov., 1881; political department, 1886; précis writer, Dec., 1889, to Dec., 1894; senior clerk, Feb., 1894.

HALL, CHARLES FREDERICK, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived

in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from May, 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating superintendent of jail, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; officiating district and sessions judge, 3rd grade, Bareilly, Dec., 1882; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Budaun, Feb., 1883; retired, Dec., 1886.

HALL, GEOFFREY CRAYTHORNE, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Jail Department, North-Western Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment; served under the military department until March, 1875, when he entered civil employ as officiating civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Jhansi; officiating superintendent, central prison, Allahabad, Dec., 1876; confirmed, Nov., 1877; author of three medical works.

HALL, MONTAGU STANLEY, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 21st Jan., 1875; clerk, 2nd class, Oct., 1878.

HALL, WILLIAM THOMAS, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Rossall, Kings-town School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1878, and served in British Burma as assistant commissioner and settlement officer; director of land records and agriculture, Oct., 1887; on special duty to Perak tin mines in 1888-89; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1892; officiating director of land records and agriculture, April-Nov., 1893; acting president, Rangoon municipality, March, 1894.

HALLEN, JAMES HERBERT BROKENCOTE, C.I.E., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., late Civil Veterinary Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 23rd May, 1850, as veterinary surgeon; acted as principal veterinary surgeon, Abyssinian field force, Dec., 1867; president of the Indian cattle plague commission, Nov., 1869; inspecting veterinary surgeon, Bengal army, July, 1872; ditto, horse-breeding operations, May, 1875; inspecting veterinary surgeon and general superintendent, horse-breeding operations, Nov., 1884; inspector-general, civil veterinary department in India, April, 1892; C.I.E., June, 1893; retired, May, 1894; author of 'Manual of the more deadly forms of Cattle Disease in India,' and the report of the Cattle Plague Commission.

HALLETT, HENRY HONYWOOD HUGHES, Colonel, I.S.C., Jail Department, Central Provinces.—First commission as ensign, July, 1858; arrived in India, 11th Aug., 1858; was employed under the military department up to 1868, including service with the Bundelcund field force, 1859; from 26th Dec., 1868, served in the Central Provinces as personal assistant to chief commissioner, assistant commissioner, acting district superintendent of police, acting cantonment magistrate, and was appointed superintendent of the school of industry and central jail, Jubbulpore, Aug., 1878; in addition, superintendent of reformatory school, June, 1891; acted also as small cause court judge, Jubbulpore, 1883, and as cantonment magistrate at various periods; deputy commissioner, and officiating inspector general of police, July, 1893.

HALLETT, JOHN REVANS, M.A., late Bengal

C.S.—Educated at the University of Melbourne; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1863; arrived, 17th Oct., 1864; assistant magistrate and collector, Burdwan, May, 1865; inspector of schools, Aug., 1870; officiating judge of the small cause courts, Bhagulpore and Monghyr, March, 1876; second inspector of registration offices, March, 1876; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, April, 1878; district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, Sept., 1880, and 1st grade, Dec., 1887; retired, Feb., 1890.

HALLIDAY, SIR FREDERICK JAMES, K.C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service in 1824; arrived in India, 8th June, 1825; served in Bengal as junior assistant to the agent in the Saugor division and assistant registrar of the sudder court; joint magistrate and deputy collector in Bundelcund, and afterwards in Noacolly and Bullooh, 1831-35; magistrate and collector of the zillah court at Dacca, Feb., 1835; magistrate, collector, and salt agent, Cuttack, Feb., 1836; secretary to the sudder board of revenue, April, 1836; secretary to the government of Bengal, judicial and revenue departments, May, 1838; member of the general committee of public instruction, June, 1838; junior secretary to the government of India, revenue, judicial, and legislative departments, in addition to his other duties, March, 1840, to 1843; secretary to the government of India, home department, 1849; member of governor-general's council, Dec., 1853; lieutenant-governor of Bengal, 1st May, 1854; retired in 1859; member of council of secretary of state for India, 30th Sept., 1868, to 31st Dec., 1886.

HALLIDAY, FREDERICK MYTTON, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury; arrived, 30th Oct., 1856; served in the revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating civil and sessions judge, and magistrate and collector (1st grade, May, 1871); officiated as opium agent, Benares, in 1876 and 1877; commissioner, Presidency division, April, 1878, and Patna division, June, 1878; officiating member, board of revenue, Sept., 1884, to April, 1885, and from July, 1886, to Feb., 1887, and again in Nov., 1887, and March, 1888; chairman, port commissioners, Calcutta, April, 1887; officiated at various periods as additional member of governor-general's council; confirmed as member of board of revenue, April, 1889; resigned, Nov., 1891.

HALLIFAX, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived, 28th Dec., 1890, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, Nov., 1892.

HALLIFAX, CHARLES JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and All Souls' College, Oxford; appointed member of the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 31st Dec., 1888, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; officiating councillor to H.H. the Raja of Suket, March, 1891; officiating district judge, Sept.-Oct., 1893.

HALLIFAX, HENRY FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Blundell's

School, Tiverton, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 20th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

HAM, WILLIAM JAMES, Postal Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, and was attached to the sea post office, Bombay, 6th May, 1868; supernumerary inspector, 1st grade, director-general's office, Oct., 1877; deputy postmaster-general, Central India, May, 1880, and at Calcutta, attached to the railway mail service, April, 1882; assistant director-general, June, 1887; officiating deputy postmaster-general, Burma, Dec., 1889; inspector-general, railway mail service, Sept., 1890.

HAMBLIN, RICHARD EBB, M.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at King's College School, and Christ College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 4th Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant commissioner, June, 1887; joint magistrate, March, 1890; officiating magistrate and collector, 1887, and again in 1890; officiating deputy commissioner, March, 1891; confirmed, April, 1893.

HAMILTON, FRANK SUMNER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Manchester Grammar School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1885, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1892.

HAMILTON, RIGHT HON. LORD GEORGE FRANCIS, M.P.—Born Dec., 1845; educated at Harrow; ensign, rifle brigade, 1864; transferred to the Coldstream guards, 1868; was elected member for Middlesex, Dec., 1868, and represented that county in parliament until the election which followed the reform bill of 1885; when he was returned for the Ealing division; under secretary of state for India, Feb., 1874; vice-president of the committee of council on education, April, 1878; first lord of the admiralty in the Marquis of Salisbury's first administration, June, 1885, and again, Aug., 1886, to 1892; chairman, London school board, 1894.

HAMILTON, RICHARD ERRIS, Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the financial department, 1st Aug., 1868; served as assistant accountant-general, N.W. Provinces, March, 1869; also in British Burma, May, 1870; assistant comptroller-general, June, 1872; from July, 1873, to March, 1876, served in Bombay as assistant accountant-general and as assistant commissioner of paper currency, and subsequently in Calcutta, March, 1876, to Dec., 1877; reposted to Bombay, March, 1878; officiated as deputy accountant-general in Bombay, the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, the Punjab, and Madras, 1879-81; officiating assistant secretary, government of India, financial department, March, 1882; comptroller and deputy commissioner of paper currency, Central Provinces, Jan., 1883; officiating comptroller, India treasuries, April, 1891; confirmed, Nov., 1891; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, March-Dec., 1892; accountant-general, Punjab, April, 1894.

HAMILTON, ROBERT CLIFTON, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Christ's Hospital and

Keble College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner.

HAMILTON, THOMAS SHARP, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Old Trafford School, Manchester, the College, Chester, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 6th Nov., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting assistant judge and sessions judge; promoted to 2nd assistant collector, Feb., 1883; 3rd grade assistant judge, Nov., 1887; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, April, 1890; 2nd grade, May, 1891; 1st grade, June, 1894.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM HENRY, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 1st Nov., 1893.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM ROBARTS, B.L., Police Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1869, as superintendent in the political, &c., departments, of the secretariat; assistant to the revenue and police commissioner, Oct., 1869; 1st grade deputy collector, May, 1874; city magistrate, Sept., 1874; Huzur deputy commissioner, July, 1876; 3rd presidency magistrate, Jan., 1888, and 2nd ditto, Aug., 1890; author of a police manual, and works on 'Local Self-Government' and 'Villages.'

HAMILTON, WILLIAM STIRLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Winchester, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

HAMMICK, MURRAY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Charterhouse; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived, 18th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; from Jan. to June, 1882, served in Coorg as acting 1st assistant commissioner and district magistrate; from Jan., 1883, served in the Madras secretariat as assistant secretary, judicial and legislative departments, and acting under secretary in the revenue department; head assistant to the collector and magistrate, South Canara, April, 1885; acting postmaster-general, Madras, Jan.-May, 1886; secretary to the commissioners of land revenue, Oct., 1887; also acted as secretary to the board of examiners; collector and magistrate, Malabar, Nov., 1893.

HAMMICK, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Marlborough, and Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1868, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant educational inspector, acting assistant judge and sessions judge, and acting assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan; assistant political agent, and tutor to the Maharaja of Kolhapur, Feb., 1873; on famine duty, Kaladgi, Sept., 1877; judicial assistant to the political agent, Kathiawar, Aug., 1883; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, Shikarpur, Feb., 1885; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, June, 1885; 2nd grade judge, Sept., 1885; member of Deccan agriculturists' relief act commission, 1891-92; 1st grade judge, April, 1892; retired, June, 1894.

HAMNETT, FREDERICK HARPER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Clifton College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and was on special duty, Sept., 1882, preparing the Madras administration report; from March, 1885, to March, 1886, served in the financial department as assistant accountant-general, Madras, and acting deputy-accountant-general; acting sub-collector, Godavari, April, 1886; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Nov., 1887; sub-collector and joint magistrate, June, 1891.

HAMNETT, GEORGE C.I.E., late Registration Department, Madras.—Served from 11th March, 1843, in the public works and revenue departments as writer, head clerk, and manager and surveyor in office of consulting engineer for railways; uncovenanted assistant to the consulting engineer, Jan., 1859; marriage registrar for town of Madras, Jan., 1866; first uncovenanted assistant, chief secretary's office, Feb., 1867; receiver of the Carnatic property, Feb., 1875; inspector-general of registration, Nov., 1880; C.I.E., June, 1885; was also registrar-general for Mysore, including Bangalore; retired, 1889; appointed additional member of the legislative council of the governor of Madras, Aug., 1890.

HANCOCK, GEORGE EDWARD, Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1859, and served under the military department till Nov., 1863; from Nov., 1863, to March, 1868, served as assistant superintendent of revenue survey and assessment, Gujarat; from March, 1868, acted as political assistant and personal assistant to the political agent, Kathiawar; acting superintendent of the Rajkot and Limbdi states, Aug., 1869; acting assistant resident at Aden, May, 1870, and at Baroda, May, 1871; assistant general superintendent for thuggee and dacoity, Oct., 1871; acting cantonment magistrate, Baroda, Oct., 1871; assistant political agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1872; from Oct., 1873, served as superintendent of surveys under Rajasthanik court, Kathiawar, and acted on various occasions as president of the court; on special duty at Delhi, Nov., 1876, to Jan., 1877; acting joint administrator of Gondal state, Nov., 1880, to Feb., 1881; on special duty with the Thakor Saheb of Gondal on tour in Europe, April, 1883; on special duty to inquire into administration of Rajpipla state, Nov., 1883; third assistant political agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1885, continuing to be superintendent of surveys; president of the Rajasthanik court, Kathiawar, Jan., 1886; political superintendent, Savantvadi, May, 1891; resumed his post in Kathiawar, Oct., 1891; acting political agent, July, 1893; political agent, &c., Kolhapur, still officiating in Kathiawar, Jan., 1894.

HANDLEY, ALFRED PERCIVAL, B.L., Judicial Department, Bengal.—Joined the service, 14th March, 1890, as officiating chief presidency magistrate and judge of the court for trial of pilots, Calcutta, and served until Oct., 1891; from Feb., 1892, acted as presidency magistrate, and was appointed judge of Calcutta small cause court, May, 1892; chief judge, Oct., 1893.

HANDLEY, FRANCIS FREDERICK, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Sydenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open

competitive examination of 1870; arrived, 7th Oct., 1872; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; officiating inspector-general of registration, and senior marriage registrar, Calcutta, Dec., 1880; first inspector of registration offices, Dec., 1881; officiating district and sessions judge, 1884, and 1888; acting inspector-general of registration, April, 1885; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1885; district and sessions judge, April, 1889.

HANDLEY, JAMES WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., late Judicial Department, Madras.—Served as government pleader from 29th Oct., 1868, to July, 1881; fellow of Madras university, March, 1881; first judge of the court of small causes, Madras, July, 1881; officiating judge of the high court, July to Nov., 1885, and from July, 1889; retired, April, 1893.

HANKEY, HERBERT, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Harrow, and Haileybury; appointed to the Bengal civil service in 1856; served in the Punjab till 1862, and subsequently in the Lower Provinces, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Maldah, 1867, and subsequently at Chittagong; superintendent of transport during famine of 1874; inspector-general of police, Lower Provinces, 1876; retired, 1878.

HANKIN, EDWARD LEWIS, Lieut.-General, I.S.C., late Madras Police.—Ensign, Indian army, Dec., 1847; arrived in India, 16th June, 1848; served in the public works department, Madras, from Aug., 1853, to July, 1859; served subsequently in the police department as superintendent of police and acting deputy inspector-general, and temporarily in the department of the deputy inspector-general of hospitals, Jan.—June, 1861; acting magistrate of police for town of Madras, March, 1870; joint magistrate, Ootacamund, Nov., 1870, to Sept., 1876; superintendent of family payments and pensions, Oct., 1876; secretary to government, military department, Madras, Dec., 1882; returned to Europe, 1888.

HANKIN, ERNEST HANBURY, M.A., Chemical Examiner, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the service, 26th Oct., 1892, as chemical examiner, analyst, and bacteriologist.

HANNAGAN, JOHN SALISBURY, Judicial Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1859, and held various non-gazetted appointments till May, 1880, when he was appointed sub-judge at Lucknow; acted at various times as district and sessions judge, and was appointed to that grade, Nov., 1892; served through the Mutiny (medal).

HANNAN, JOHN, Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 31st July, 1866, and served in the Madras presidency as assistant engineer and executive engineer; superintending engineer, Dec., 1891; officiating joint secretary to government, public works department, April-Nov., 1893.

HANNAY, ALEXANDER LIDDERDALE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Uppingham, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 5th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

HANNYNGTON, JOHN CHILD, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from

Haileybury College, 1857; arrived, 8th Nov., 1857; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector, small cause court judge, and acting collector and magistrate; district and sessions judge, Salem, Nov., 1873; on special duty, Dec., 1880, as arbitrator of boundary disputes between states of Travancore and Cochin; on tour with H.H. the Maharajah of Travancore on various occasions; resident of Travancore and Cochin, April, 1882; retired, Nov., 1892.

HAPPELL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Crosshill School, Maybole, Glasgow University, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1864; assistant collector and magistrate, Nov., 1865; acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, Sept.-Nov., 1879; sub-collector and joint magistrate, April, 1880; acting commissioner of salt revenue, Madras, April to Nov., 1881; district and sessions judge, Nellore, Jan., 1883; collector and magistrate, Kistna, Nov., 1883; collector and magistrate, and government agent, Godaveri, Sept., 1886; retired, Jan., 1892.

HARCOURT, ALFRED FREDERICK POLLOCK, Colonel, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 15th Oct., 1855; from Sept., 1862, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating small cause court judge; judicial assistant, 1st grade, March, 1877; deputy commissioner, April, 1877; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Nov., 1885; retired, March, 1889; author of 'Down by the Drawl,' 'The Royal Umbrella,' and 'Kulu, Lahoul, and Spitti.'

HARDIE, ROBERT, Council of India.—Appointed to the council of the secretary of state for India on 17th March, 1837; vice-president, 1892-93; was previously engaged in the profession of banking, and held the office of secretary and treasurer of the Bank of Bengal for 15 years; in 1886 was a member of the finance committee appointed by the government of India for the reduction of expenditure.

HARDING, FRANCIS HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Tonbridge School, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1885; district and sessions judge, May, 1890.

HARDING, HERBERT OLIVE DENMAN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1886, and served as assistant to magistrate and collector; head assistant collector, Coimbatore, Feb., 1893.

HARDING, HERBERT WILLIAM, Audit Department, India Office.—Associate of King's College, London; writer, India office, 27th April, 1868; junior examiner, audit department, Nov., 1873; designation changed to junior clerk, Oct., 1892; senior clerk, Jan., 1895.

HARDY, RICHARD GILLIES, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Whitney Grammar School, and Victoria College, Jersey; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in

the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating magistrate and collector, and officiating cantonment magistrate; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Aug., 1885; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, May, 1885; magistrate and collector, July, 1889; officiated as commissioner of Jhansi, 1889.

HARE, LANCELOT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. John's College, Hurstpierpoint, and City of London School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived, 5th Nov., 1873; served in revenue and judicial departments in Bengal and Assam as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; on famine duty, July, 1874; assistant secretary to chief commissioner of Assam, March, 1879, and officiating secretary, March, 1880; officiating inspector-general of police, jails, and registration, commissioner of excise, and superintendent of stamps, Assam, July, 1880; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1884; magistrate and collector, June, 1890; officiated as commissioner, Dacca division, in May, 1891.

HARE, ROBERT DOUGLAS, Berar Commission.—Joined the service as extra assistant commissioner, Chota Nagpur (Bengal), 14th June, 1869; served on famine duty, May, 1874, and in Berar from Oct., 1875; assistant commissioner, May, 1878; judge of small cause court, June, 1883; deputy commissioner, July, 1889; director of land records and agriculture, July, 1890, to Aug., 1891.

HARI KISHEN KAUL, Pandit, M.A., Punjab Commission.—Probationer under native civil service rules, 17th April, 1890; extra assistant commissioner, April, 1892; officiating junior secretary to financial commissioners, June, 1893.

HARINGTON, ARTHUR HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Wadham College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1862; served in Oudh from Aug., 1863, as assistant commissioner and junior secretary to chief commissioner; from Dec., 1866, to Jan., 1868, served under the government of India as officiating assistant secretary, foreign department, and officiating under secretary, home department; officiating superintendent of excise and stamps, Oudh, July, 1868; appointed to take up lands for railway purposes, Nov., 1868; officiating personal assistant to financial commissioner, March, 1869; officiating settlement officer, Hardoi, March, 1870; manager of encumbered estates, Fyzabad and Gonda, 1872-75; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1879; magistrate and collector, Saharanpur, Oct., 1883; acted as commissioner in the Lucknow, Sitapur, Rae Bareli, Fyzabad, and Allahabad divisions; commissioner in Oudh, June, 1889; commissioner, Allahabad division, Nov., 1889; Meerut division, May, 1891; author of 'Economic Reform in Rural India.'

HARINGTON, HERBERT BARING, M.A., late Oudh Commission.—Arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1857, as assistant in telegraph department; for services during the Mutiny at the relief of Lucknow received medal and clasp; from Feb., 1860, served in the Oudh commission as assistant commissioner, assistant settlement officer, and officiating settlement officer; officiating secretary to chief com-

missioner, 1869-73; also officiating director of public instruction, April-July, 1870; deputy commissioner, July, 1875; district judge, 2nd grade, Lucknow, Aug., 1879; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Sultanpur, Dec., 1881; officiated as commissioner of the Sitapur division in 1876, and of the Rae Bareli division in 1881, 1884, and 1885; retired, Nov., 1887.

HARMAN, CHARLES, Audit Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 19th Aug., 1878.

HARMAN, JOHN MACDONALD, India Public Works Department.—Trained at the Calcutta Civil Engineering College; appointed, 29th May, 1872, and employed on military works in the N.W. Provinces; transferred to state railways, Jan., 1880; executive engineer, May, 1883.

HARRIDANCE, HENRY, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th March, 1878.

HARRIS, EDWARD BRANSON, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Bedford Commercial School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived, 14th Dec., 1877; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1888; magistrate and collector, June, 1892.

HARRIS (BARON), RIGHT HON. GEORGE ROBERT CANNING, G.C.I.E.—Educated at Eton, and at Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1874; is J. P. and D. L. for Kent, and deputy chairman of the East Kent quarter sessions; under secretary for India in Lord Salisbury's first administration, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; under secretary of state for war, Aug., 1886; took his seat as governor of Bombay in succession to Lord Reay, 12th April, 1890.

HARRIS, GEORGE STEEL TRAVERS, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed, 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Burma; executive engineer, Nov., 1881; lent to Rewah state, Dec., 1883; and to Gwalior, Nov., 1886.

HARRIS, LEONARD TATHAM, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Falmouth Grammar School, Bath College, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

HARRIS, ROBERT LISTON, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at King's College School, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1877, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating judicial assistant, and officiating district judge; deputed to settlement department, Dec., 1883, to Feb., 1884; district judge, Nov., 1889.

HARRISON, CHARLES HOLMES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Rugby, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1893, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

HARRISON, CHARLES WILLIAM INGLEBY, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—First commission as lieutenant, 11th June, 1858; arrived in India, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public works department, irrigation branch, North-Western Provinces, from 10th April, 1863, as assistant

engineer, executive engineer, and assistant to chief engineer, and was employed on the Eastern Jumna, Rohilkund, and Ganges canals; superintending engineer, 3rd class, Dec., 1881; deputed to Bengal as member of commission to enquire into system of canal administration in Orissa, Dec., 1884, to April, 1885; superintending engineer, 2nd class, May, 1886; officiated as chief engineer and joint secretary to government, Bengal, July, 1886, and as secretary, April-June, 1888; superintending engineer, 1st class, Feb., 1888; 3rd chief engineer, Dec., 1889; 2nd, July, 1891; 1st, March, 1892; chief engineer and joint secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Jan., 1890; officiating secretary, May, 1890; confirmed, July, 1891; reverted to military department, June, 1894.

HARRISON, FRANCIS CAPEL, Indian C.S.—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1882; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1884; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1884; served in Assam from Jan. to May, 1889, as assistant commissioner; officiating assistant accountant-general, Madras, May, 1889, and deputy accountant-general, Bengal, March, 1890; confirmed, Sept., 1890; officiating under-secretary to government of India, finance department, May, 1892; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, Oct., 1892.

HARRISON, HENRY ANTHONY, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1855; arrived in India, 3rd April, 1855; served from May, 1856, to Sept., 1858, in Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, Cuttack, and subsequently in the N.W. Provinces; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, April, 1862; assistant, settlement department, Feb., 1864; magistrate and collector, Hamirpur, March, 1872; magistrate and collector, Farukhabad, 1873 to 1877; officiating commissioner, Jhansi division, Sept., 1875; civil and sessions judge, Allahabad, March, 1878, and subsequently at Mirzapore, Farukhabad, and Meerut; 1st grade judge, March, 1884; retired, June, 1887.

HARRISON, JOHN HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Erasmus Smith's School, and Queen's College, Galway; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant superintendent, Dehra Dun, Nov., 1876; officiating assistant director of department of agriculture and commerce, Nov., 1880; acting junior secretary, board of revenue, March, 1881; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, Aug., 1885; 2nd grade settlement officer, Oct., 1887; has also officiated as magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 1st grade, April, 1889; deputy commissioner, April, 1891; magistrate and collector, Dec., 1892; officiating district and sessions judge, April-July, 1893.

HARRISON, PERCY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at St. Paul's School and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner, and assistant magistrate and collector; officiating under secretary, Nov., 1893; confirmed, March, 1894.

HARRISON, RICHARD PRYCE, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1834; arrived in India, 19th Sept., 1835; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and officiating collector, Moorshedabad, 1849; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Boghra, 1850; officiating abkari commissioner, Dacca division, 1851; magistrate, collector, and salt agent, Cuttack, 1853-56; civil and sessions judge, 1856; accountant-general, government of Madras, and a government director of the Madras bank, 1859; accountant-general and auditor-general to government of India, and a government director of the bank of Bengal, 1860; also first member and president of the board of audit; comptroller-general, 1863-67; resigned, April, 1867.

HARRISON, WILLIAM PRINGLE, Lieut.-Col., Oudh Commission.—Arrived in India as ensign, 10th Feb., 1861; employed under the military department till Oct., 1867, from which date he served in the Oudh commission as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner, and acted as district superintendent of police in 1873-74, and as superintendent of encumbered estates in Sultanpur in 1874; sub-judge, Paratagurh, Aug., 1879; cantonment magistrate of Fyzabad, July, 1881, and subsequently of Lucknow, April, 1883; also acted as district judge in 1884-85; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1888.

HART, GEORGE HENRY REEVES, Financial Department, Government of India.—Probationer in financial department, 20th July, 1869; from Dec., 1870, served as assistant to comptroller-general; on special duty in Behar, Jan.-March, 1874; assistant to private secretary to viceroy, April, 1874, to April, 1876; assistant to accountant-general, Bombay, April, 1877; from Feb., 1878, acted as private secretary to governor of Bombay, and was confirmed in this appointment, March, 1880; appointed assistant accountant-general in charge of paper currency office, Bombay, Aug., 1887; deputy accountant-general, Lahore, Jan., 1889; Bombay, Feb., 1891; officiating deputy auditor-general, Oct., 1892; officiating comptroller, India treasuries, Aug., 1893; in 1886-87, 1890-91, and 1893-94, acted as assistant secretary to the government of India, finance and commerce department.

HART, JOHN HENRY EUSTACE, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1854; joined the Bombay public works department, Jan., 1855, and served as civil engineer at Satara, Nasik, and Dharwar; acting executive engineer for reclamations, Bombay, May, 1869; architectural executive engineer and surveyor, Bombay, May, 1871; acting superintendent, harbour works, Karachi, Nov., 1874; acting superintending engineer in Sind, April, 1876; acting chief engineer for irrigation, Sept., 1877; superintending engineer, 1st class, March, 1880; joint secretary to government of Bombay for irrigation, March, 1880; chief engineer, 2nd class, Dec., 1885; chief engineer, 1st class, and secretary to government of Bombay, public works and irrigation department, Sept., 1887; retired, 1888.

HART, WILLIAM, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service in 1834; served in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate and assistant judge and sessions

judge; commissioner for enquiring into claims, &c., South Mahratta country, 1847; secretary to government of Bombay, in the general and ecclesiastical departments, 1855, also in the public works department, and railway branch, 1856; puisne judge of the court of Sudder Dewani, 1860; revenue and police commissioner, southern division, 1862; additional member of Bombay council for making laws and regulations, 1864; retired in 1865.

HART-DAVIES, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Marlborough; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from May, 1876, served in Sind as educational inspector, acting judge and sessions judge, and acting assistant commissioner and branch inspector-general of registration; manager of encumbered estates in Sind, Oct., 1879, to Sept., 1885; 1st assistant collector, April, 1887; joint judge and sessions judge, Ahmedabad, June, 1891; 2nd grade judge and sessions judge, April, 1892; 1st grade, March, 1894.

HARTNOLL, HENRY SULIVAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Exeter Grammar School, and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1883; assistant commissioner, Burma, Dec., 1883; deputy commissioner, July, 1890.

HARTT, WILLIAM EDWARD, Railway Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 19th April, 1876; assistant traffic superintendent, superior revenue establishment of state railways, 1878; district traffic superintendent, 1882.

HARTWELL, SYDNEY CHARLES ELPHINSTONE, Railway Department, Government of India.—Appointed traffic superintendent, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, 1st Jan., 1889; officiating manager, Sept., 1891, to Nov., 1892.

HARVEY, EDWARD, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 22nd Dec., 1859; served in the public works department from April, 1864; executive engineer, military works branch, June, 1871; served in the Lushai expedition, 1871-72 (medal and clasp), in the Jowaki Afridi campaign, 1878-79 (clasp), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal, brevet of major); superintending engineer, May, 1889; member of accounts committee, 1888-89; retired, Oct., 1892.

HARVEY, JOHN, Educational Department, Punjab.—Served as assistant master and officiating head master in the zillah schools at Amritsar, Lahore, and Gujrat, from 1st Nov., 1869; head master district school, Delhi, March, 1876; assistant inspector of schools, April, 1877; tutor to H.H. the Raja of Chamba, Nov., 1879; inspector of schools, Nov., 1883; 3rd grade, Delhi circle, Dec., 1889.

HARVEY, ROBERT, Surgeon-Colonel, M.D., D.S.O., Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 31st March, 1865; civil surgeon, Bhurtpore, 1867-69 and 1870-71; Simla, 1876-77; officiating sanitary commissioner, Bengal, Jan., 1878; member of committee to report on management of the medical institutions, and on medical expenditure in Bengal, March, 1878; civil surgeon, Bankoora, May, 1878; officiating professor of midwifery, medical college, Calcutta, and superintendent-general of vaccination,

Aug., 1878; civil surgeon, 24 Pergunnahs, and medical inspector of emigrants, Nov., 1878; also in medical charge of the Alipore and Russa jails, May, 1879; also superintendent of emigration, July, 1879; officiating professor of midwifery, medical college, and obstetric physician to the college hospital, Sept., 1880; confirmed in this appointment, Sept., 1882; also officiating principal of the college, May to July, 1882; honorary surgeon on personal staff of H.E. the viceroy, May, 1883; reverted to military department, March, 1890; appointed inspector-general of civil hospitals, Bengal, May, 1894.

HARVEY, WILLIAM LEATHEM, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at the Royal Academic Institution, Belfast, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived, 25th Dec., 1883; assistant collector and magistrate, Oct., 1884; forest settlement officer, April, 1887; officiating under secretary to government, revenue, financial and general departments, March, 1891; confirmed, Aug., 1891.

HARWARD, ALFRED EDGAR, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Rossall School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1885, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1892.

HASHMAT-ULLA, Maulvi, M.A., Statutory C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed a probationer under the native civil service rules, March, 1886; confirmed as member of the civil service, 13th June, 1889, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, April, 1894.

HASTED, JOHN ORD, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—2nd lieutenant, Madras engineers, Dec., 1853; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1855; served in the Madras public works department from 30th Jan., 1856, as assistant engineer, and was promoted to executive engineer, April, 1865, and superintending engineer, Feb., 1877; officiated as chief engineer for irrigation, and joint secretary to government for various periods between 1875 and 1884; and was confirmed in this appointment and promoted to chief engineer, 2nd class, April, 1884; fellow of Madras university, March, 1883; chief engineer, 1st class, Sept., 1886; secretary to the government of Madras, public works department, general and buildings and road branches, 1886; retired, July, 1890.

HASTINGS, FRANCIS THEOPHILUS CLINTON, India Office.—Educated at Haileybury College; admiralty writer, 8th Oct., 1868, to Jan., 1874; civil service copyist from Jan., 1874, to Feb., 1877; clerk specially attached to the political and secret department, India office, 7th March, 1877; resident clerk, July, 1888; assistant private secretary to the secretary of state, June, 1892.

HASTINGS, WARREN, Major, I.S.C., Berar Commission.—1st commission, 28th Feb., 1874; served in Bengal as cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge from Jan., 1881; special assistant commissioner, Berar, Sept., 1887; deputy commissioner, July, 1891; in charge of provincial census operations, April, 1890.

HATCH, GEORGE WASHINGTON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in

India, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as assistant collector.

HATCH, WILLIAM KEITH, Surgeon-Major, M.B., C.M. (Aber.), F.R.C.S. (London), Bombay Medical Department.—Arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1877; second surgeon of the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital, and professor of anatomy, &c., Grant medical college, May, 1884; senior medical officer and senior surgeon of the hospital, and professor of surgery, &c., Grant medical college, April, 1893; acted as medical officer on the governor's personal staff, Jan. to March, 1885.

HATCHELL, CHARLES, Surgeon-Major, late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 23rd July, 1858; arrived in India, 30th Sept., 1858; served in N.W. Provinces as civil assistant surgeon from Dec., 1859, to Oct., 1864, and was subsequently employed under the military department, and served in the Bhutan expedition; served again under government of N.W. Provinces from Nov., 1869, and was appointed civil assistant surgeon, and superintendent of jail, Bulandshahr; rank of surgeon, July, 1870; served subsequently as civil surgeon at Bulandshahr, Jaunpur, Muzaffarnagar, Aligarh, and Farukhabad; senior surgeon-major, July, 1878; retired, Oct., 1888.

HATHERLY, JOHN RODNEY, Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Feb., 1876.

HATHORNTHWAITE, JAMES THOMAS, M.A., Educational Department, Bombay.—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; arrived in India, 3rd Oct., 1872, and served in the Bombay educational department from that date as professor of mathematics at the Elphinstone college, Bombay; also acted as principal of the college for various periods, and was appointed to that post, in addition to his professorship, April, 1894.

HAWES, ARTHUR BRISCOE, Captain, India Office.—Educated at Westminster; ensign, Bengal army, 12th Dec., 1849; chief executive officer of telegraph from Bagdad to Constantinople, 1857-58; captain, May, 1858; retired, Oct., 1858; assistant auditor, India office, March, 1859; member of committee for valuation of military equipments, April, 1863; author of a work on small arms ammunition.

HAWKINS, ROBERT WILLIAM LYONS, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 3rd Oct., 1873, and employed on irrigation works in N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Jan., 1884.

HAY, GEORGE WILLIAM ROBERTSON, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.D., Bombay Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1868; arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1868; served in the military, civil, and jail branches of the service, and was appointed port surgeon at Aden, June, 1876; secretary to the principal medical officer, Poona, Oct., 1890.

HAYDON, WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1862; arrived in India, 1st March, 1865; executive engineer, Jan., 1870; and was employed on Bombay reclamation works and defences, Tapti bridge, and with the Kandahar field force;

employed under military works department, 1890-91; reverted to military duty, Dec., 1894.

HAYES, ALFRED, India Public Works Department.—Appointed, 5th Feb., 1873, and employed on irrigation works in Bengal; on duty in Madras on famine relief works, 1877-78; executive engineer, May, 1883.

HAYWARD, MAURICE HENRY WESTON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Harrow and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 19th Nov., 1889, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

HAZLETT, HENRY JAMES, M.D., Brigade-Surgeon - Lieut. - Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived in Madras, 8th Nov., 1867, and was employed on general military and jail duty; on special duty as analyst to salt commission, Madras, Feb. - June, 1876; surgeon-major, April, 1879; surgeon, 4th district, Jan., 1887, to May, 1888; also professor of medical jurisprudence in medical college, Aug., 1887; employed in military accounts department of government of India, June, 1888, and again in 1889; surgeon, 3rd district, Feb., 1893.

HEATON, JOSEPH JOHN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Bradford Grammar School, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 24th Dec., 1881, and served as assistant collector and magistrate and assistant judge; acting under secretary to government of Bombay, political, judicial, and educational departments, Oct., 1887; assistant judge and sessions judge, July, 1889; acting private secretary to governor of Bombay, May, 1894.

HEAVISIDE, WILLIAM JAMES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1857; lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1859; arrived in India, Nov., 1861; served in the public works department as assistant engineer from Sept., 1862, to Feb., 1865; assistant field engineer with the Bhutan force, 1865-66 (medal and clasp); joined the great trigonometrical survey, as 2nd grade assistant, Jan., 1866; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, April, 1872; served with the Southern Afghanistan field force in 1878-79, on special survey duty (medal, brevet of major); served subsequently in Khandeish and Bombay native states, and was promoted to deputy superintendent, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; retired, July, 1888.

HEBBERT, FREDERICK BENBOW, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed to the department as assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874; executive engineer, Sept., 1883; officiating deputy consulting engineer for railways, Lucknow, April, 1887; under-secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, March, 1888; on special duty under director-general of railways, Aug., 1888; officiating assistant secretary to the government of India, public works department, Jan., 1889; officiating under-secretary, Feb.-June, 1889; officiating under-secretary and deputy director-general of railways, July, 1890; confirmed, May, 1892; officiating deputy-secretary, railway branch, July, 1894.

HEINIG, JOSEPH, India Public Works Department.—Appointed, 26th July, 1867, and posted to N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive

engineer, Oct., 1877; in charge of 3rd circle, March, 1892.

HEMING, DEMPSTER, Lieut. - Colonel, late Police Department, Madras.—First commission, 16th Nov., 1861; probationary assistant superintendent of police, Dec., 1865; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1874; assistant with the government of Madras, July, 1888, and again, April-Nov., 1890; superintendent of police, Jan., 1892; retired, Feb., 1894.

HENDERSON, EDWARD PIERCY, Barrister-at-Law, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy, and St. Peter's College, Radley; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating judicial assistant; from Feb., 1878, was served as government advocate, Punjab.

HENDERSON, GEORGE, M.D., Surgeon-Major, late Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the Indian medical department in 1859; arrived in India, 20th November, 1859; civil assistant surgeon, Shahpur, May, 1862; officiating professor of medicine and surgery, April, 1865, to Jan., 1867; superintendent of Lahore central jail, April, 1868; officiating superintendent, botanical gardens in Bengal, Dec., 1872; civil surgeon, Gujranwala, Dec., 1875, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; civil surgeon, 1st class, Oct., 1886; retired, Nov., 1889.

HENDERSON, PHILIP DURHAM, Colonel, C.S.I., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, Madras cavalry, Feb., 1857; attaché, foreign department, May, 1871; under secretary to the government of India, foreign department, Nov., 1872, to July, 1874; resident, 3rd class, on special duty in Cashmere, 1874; political officer on staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Sept., 1875; C.S.I., March, 1876; general superintendent of operations for suppression of thuggee and dacoity from March, 1878; resident in Mysore, and chief commissioner, Coorg, April, 1892.

HENNESSY, SEPTIMUS HOPE, late Judicial Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the service as acting extra assistant commissioner, 13th July, 1864, and was confirmed, 8th July, 1867; acted as deputy commissioner, and as judge small cause court for various periods, and was appointed to the latter office at Jubbulpore in Sept., 1889; acted also as cantonment magistrate from Nov., 1888; retired, Jan., 1892.

HENNIKER, FREDERICK CHANDOS, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at St. Paul's School and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1883; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner.

HENRY, EDWARD RICHARD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Edmund's College, Ware, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1873; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, June, 1882; on famine duty, Tirhoot, Dec., 1873; private secretary to Lieutenant-governor of Bengal in 1879, and 1880 to 1882; magistrate and collector, May, 1890; officiating secretary to board of revenue, Nov., 1890; officiating inspector general of police, April, 1891; confirmed, May, 1892.

HENSLowe, Cecil William Edward, late Railway Department.—Joined the public works department, June, 1866, as assistant engineer, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, and in the Central Provinces (1870-71); transferred to railway establishment, Jan., 1880; engineer-in-chief and manager, Cawnpur-Farukhabad and Muttra-Hathras railways, Dec., 1880; class 2, revenue establishment of state railways, May, 1881; manager, Cawnpur-Achneyra state railway, 1884; transferred to Burma state railway, Dec., 1886; retired, 1890.

HENVEY, Frederick, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Rugby School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from Dec., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, under secretary to government, officiating secretary to board of revenue, and officiating junior secretary to government, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Sept., 1869; placed on special duty for purpose of compiling a narrative of the famine of 1869-70, April, 1871; under secretary to government of India, foreign department, July, 1874; officiating resident, Nepal, Jan., 1877, to March, 1878; on special duty, Kashmir, April, 1878; resident, 2nd class, and commissioner, Hyderabad assigned districts, Jan., 1883; resident, Eastern Rajputana states, Dec., 1885; officiating agent to the governor-general in Central India, May, 1888; confirmed, Jan., 1889; retired, Jan., 1891.

HERALD, John Lumgair, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Arbroath High School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 24th Dec., 1883; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, Oct., 1888; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1892; returned to Bengal as joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1894.

HERBERT, Charles, Major, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India—1st commission, June, 1874; served under the military department to 1882, including Afghan war of 1879-80; extra aide-de-camp to viceroy, June, 1878, and again from March, 1882; officiated as assistant to the governor-general, Rajputana, June, 1882; magistrate of Abu, Nov., 1882; on deputation to Meywar states to settle boundary disputes, Nov., 1883; officiating cantonment magistrate, Nasirabad, May, 1884; returned to his post in Rajputana, July, 1884; again officiated as cantonment magistrate at Nasirabad, Nov., 1885; confirmed in political department, March, 1886, and held various officiating appointments; confirmed as assistant commissioner, Ajmere, April, 1889; assistant to the agent, Rajputana, and chief commissioner, Ajmere-Merwara, March, 1891; officiating political agent in eastern states of Rajputana, May, 1891, to Feb., 1892; 1st assistant to agent, Central India, Oct., 1892; political agent, Kotah, March, 1894.

HERBERT, Patrick Herbert Coghlán, Judicial and Public Department, India Office.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; junior clerk, accounts branch, India office, 11th March, 1872; judicial and public department, 1877; resident clerk, 1885-88; senior clerk, May, 1891; private secretary to parliamentary under

secretary of state (Mr. G. W. E. Russell, M.P.), Oct., 1892, to Feb., 1894; editor of India Office List from July, 1887, to Aug., 1891; compiled jointly with Mr. Newmarch the India Office cypher code.

HERRON, Herbert George Whitley, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Rugby and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived, 27th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Burma as assistant commissioner, Nov., 1888, and officiating under secretary to chief commissioner; resumed his post in Bengal, Nov., 1890.

HERSCHEL, John, F.R.S., Colonel, R.E., late Survey of India.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1855; first commission, Dec., 1856; arrived at Calcutta, Jan., 1859; served in the great trigonometrical survey from Nov., 1859, in the grades of assistant surveyor and deputy superintendent; 1st grade deputy superintendent, Jan., 1884; retired, March, 1886.

HERVEY, Hamilton Law, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Giggleswick School and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 27th March, 1887; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1888; 2nd assistant, May, 1894.

HEWAT, Richard Alexander, Judicial and Public Department, India Office.—East India house writer, old establishment, 18th Aug., 1858; junior clerk, military department, India office, Aug., 1860; senior clerk, Jan., 1875; assistant secretary, judicial and public department, Feb., 1894.

HEWETSON, John, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Repton, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; served in Burma, from March, 1886, as assistant commissioner, judge, civil court, Mandalay, and district officer; returned to Madras, Oct., 1888; senior assistant to collector and district magistrate and agent, Ganjam, July, 1889; head assistant, Godavari, Aug., 1893.

HEWETT, John Prescott, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Winchester, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; in charge of provincial gazetteer, Aug., 1883; acted as assistant accountant-general, Jan., 1884; junior secretary, board of revenue, April, 1884; officiating under secretary to government of India, home department, Oct., 1886, and confirmed in the appointment, Aug., 1887; officiated as private secretary to the viceroy, in 1888, and as secretary to government of India, home department, March, 1890; officiating census commissioner for India, July, 1890; deputy secretary to government of India, home department, Dec., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1891; officiating secretary, Jan., 1892; officiating private secretary to viceroy, April, 1892; deputy commissioner, March, 1893; secretary of royal commission on opium, Oct., 1893; magistrate and collector, April, 1894; officiating secretary to government of India, home

department, June, 1894; author of contributions to provincial gazetteer.

HEWISH, JOHN, Accounts Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, India office, 1st Feb., 1869; senior clerk, Sept., 1883.

HEWITT, JAMES FRANCIS KATHERINUS, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1853; arrived, 12th Feb., 1859; assistant magistrate and collector, Feb., 1860; officiating deputy commissioner, Lohardugga, July, 1863; settlement officer, Chuttergurh, Dec., 1864; deputy commissioner, wards' estate in Behar, Feb., 1873; officiating junior secretary, government of Bengal, May, 1874; officiating secretary, board of revenue, Sept., 1874; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Oct., 1878; commissioner, Chota Nagpur division, March, 1879; retired, July, 1885; author of 'The Ruling Races of Pre-Historic Times in India, &c.'

HEYWOOD, JOHN MAGEE, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1854; 1st commission, Bengal engineers, June, 1856; arrived in India, April, 1858; served in the Bengal public works department from April, 1858, as assistant engineer and executive engineer; on special duty, Oct., 1866; deputed to examine reservoirs in France and Spain while on leave in 1867; ordered to report on arrangements connected with towing in inland rivers in Belgium; special survey duty, Sept., 1870; deputed on special duty to the N.W. Provinces and Punjab, Aug., 1876; superintending engineer, 2nd class, May, 1881; officiating chief engineer, and joint secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, April, 1883, to Feb., 1884; superintending engineer, 1st class, Dec., 1884; chief engineer and joint secretary, irrigation branch, Punjab, 1886; chief engineer, 3rd class, Nov., 1886; retired, Dec., 1889.

HIGGINS, ALFRED WILLIAM BUCKLE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Haileybury College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1876, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector; on famine duty, July, 1877, to Nov., 1878; on special duty as secretary to the draft forest bill committee, April–Nov., 1882; deputy conservator of forests, 1st grade, Cuddapah, Dec., 1882; also forest settlement officer, July, 1886; sub-collector and joint magistrate, June, 1890; principal assistant to collector, Jan., 1891; collector and magistrate, Godavari, Jan., 1892; collector, magistrate, and agent, Ganjam, March, 1893.

HIGGINS, ANDREW FRANK, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomasson College; appointed assistant engineer, 23rd Oct., 1873, and posted to Hyderabad; executive engineer, May, 1881.

HIGGINS, JOHN JAMES, late Police Department, Central Provinces.—Appointed acting adjutant, military police, Narsinghpur, for services rendered during the Mutiny under Sir Henry Havelock, Dec., 1858; from March, 1861, served in the police department of the Central Provinces, and was promoted to district superintendent, 1st class, June, 1879; on special duty at Kalahandi, June to Aug., 1882; retired, March, 1894.

HIGHAM, THOMAS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed probationary assistant engineer, July, 1867; arrived in India, Oct., 1867; served in the irrigation department, Punjab, and was promoted to executive engineer, Sept., 1873; assistant secretary to government, irrigation branch, Nov., 1882; superintendent of works, Derajat, Sept., 1883; superintending engineer, Aug., 1886; officiated as chief engineer and joint secretary to government, Punjab, July–Oct., 1891; officiating chief engineer, and secretary, irrigation branch, April, 1893, and again April, 1894; confirmed, Oct., 1894; author of 'Hydraulic Tables.'

HILDEBRAND, ARTHUR HEDDING, C.I.E., Burma Commission.—From Feb., 1868, to March, 1872, served in the Burma police department as assistant superintendent and acting superintendent; from March, 1872, served in the Burma commission as assistant commissioner and acting deputy commissioner, and was sent on special mission to Chiengmai, March, 1875, and in charge of expedition to Western Karenni, Jan., 1876; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1885; superintendent, southern Shan states, May, 1888; C.I.E., May, 1888.

HILDEBRAND, GODFREY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Military Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, Oct., 1864; arrived in India, Jan., 1868; joined the public works department, March, 1869, and served in Bengal and the N.W. Provinces; transferred to military works, May, 1872; secretary to defence committee, 1881; superintending engineer, March, 1886; deputy director-general, military works, Sept., 1890, to 1891.

HILL, CHARLES H., B.L., Calcutta High Court.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1889, as public prosecutor to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh; acted as judge, Calcutta high court, in 1889, 1891, and 1892, and was confirmed in that post, 21st April, 1892.

HILL, CLAUDE HAMILTON ARCHER, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Mark's School, Windsor, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1888; acting under-secretary, political, &c., departments, and secretary to legislative council, Jan., 1892; confirmed, Nov., 1892.

HILL, FREDERICK GEORGE, Funds Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th July, 1882.

HILL, HARRY CHARLES, India Forest Department.—Arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1872, and appointed assistant conservator of forests; deputy conservator of forests, April, 1879; transferred to the Punjab as officiating conservator, Jan., 1883; conservator of forests, 2nd grade, and ex officio secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, in the forest department for Upper Burma, March, 1887; on deputation to Calcutta as officiating inspector-general of forests in 1889 and 1893, and to Bombay in the same capacity, March, 1891; 1st grade conservator, Naini Tal, March, 1893.

HILL, HENRY, Public Works Department, India Office.—Educated at the Edinburgh Academy and Edinburgh University; India office writer, old establishment, 1859; junior clerk, financial department, 1860; assistant private secretary and précis writer to the

secretary of state, 1867-74; senior clerk, Jan., 1872; revenue, statistics and commerce department, 1874; assistant secretary in the revenue, statistics, and commerce department, Sept., 1877; assistant in the public works department, and assistant government director of guaranteed Indian railway companies, June, 1892; compiled the annual official Reports on Sanitary Measures in India from 1873-74 to 1877-78, and the Moral and Material Progress Reports from 1873-74 to 1875-76.

HILL, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Survey of India.—Lieutenant, royal engineers, June, 1862; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1865; appointed to the great trigonometrical survey of India as assistant surveyor, Dec., 1866, and was employed in Kumaon and Garhwal, and afterwards in Bombay, and on the eastern frontier; assistant superintendent, 1st grade, Oct., 1870; deputy superintendent, July, 1882; attached to Afghan boundary commission, Sept., 1884; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, Oct., 1884; 2nd grade, Oct., 1890; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892; in charge of tidal and levelling operations, Poona, Nov., 1890.

HILL, THOMAS COUTTS, M.I.E.E., late Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Appointed to the department, 1st Aug., 1857; assistant superintendent, 1st grade, Jan., 1867; superintendent, British Burma, Feb., 1879; 1st grade superintendent, March, 1885; employed in connection with occupation of Upper Burma, 1886 (special thanks of government); chief superintendent, Sept., 1892; retired, March, 1894.

HILL, WILLIAM, Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, Madras infantry, Aug., 1853; served in the Mysore commission from July, 1859, as junior assistant; deputy superintendent, 3rd class, Mysore commission, Nov., 1862; deputy commissioner in Mysore, April, 1873; acted for various periods as commissioner in the Nandipur and Nagar divisions; acting commissioner of Coorg, 1881; from March, 1885, served as civil and sessions judge of the civil and military station of Bangalore; returned to Europe, 1891.

HILTON, JOHN EDWARD, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 8th Oct., 1868, and posted to the Punjab; transferred to military works department, May, 1872; executive engineer, Aug., 1877; transferred to Punjab provincial establishment, Nov., 1880; officiated as under-secretary to government in 1888-89; appointed under-secretary, Sept., 1892; superintending engineer, May, 1893.

HIRTZEL, FREDERIC ARTHUR, B.A., Judicial and Public Department, India Office.—Educated at Dulwich College, and Trinity College, Oxford (Craven scholar); appointed junior clerk, India office, 11th June, 1894.

HOARE, HERBERT JAMES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at private schools and at St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

HOBART, ROBERT THOMPSON, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examina-

tion of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; deputy inspector-general of police, N.W. Provinces, Dec., 1873; inspector-general of prisons, Oct., 1882; inspector-general of police, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, May, 1886; retired, Dec., 1889.

HOBDAY, JAMES RAMSAY, Major, I.S.C., Survey of India Department.—1st commission, 13th Jan., 1869; probationary assistant superintendent, survey department, Sept., 1874; confirmed, July, 1875; served in the Afghan war, 1879, and with Upper Burma field force, 1886; deputy superintendent, Jan., 1887; accompanied special expedition in Upper Burma along the Chinese frontier, 1890-91.

HOBHOUSE (BARON), RIGHT HON. ARTHUR HOBHOUSE, Q.C., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Born 10th Nov., 1819; educated at Eton and Balliol College, Oxford, where he gained a first-class in classics in 1840; called to the bar, 1845, and practised in the Rolls court; Q.C. in 1865; appointed a charity commissioner in 1866; endowed schools commissioner, 1869; legal member of council of the governor-general of India, 1872-77; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1877; arbitrator under the Epping Forest Act, 1878; privy councillor, and member of the judicial committee, 1881; created a baron in 1885; one of the quorum of the House of Lords for judicial purposes; author of 'The Dead Hand,' a collection of addresses connected with endowments and the settlements of land, 1880; contested Westminster unsuccessfully in the general election of 1880.
