HOBHOUSE, SIR CHARLES PARRY, BART., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Rugby; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury; arrived in India, 22nd July, 1844, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and in the financial department, government of India, as assistant to the sub-treasurer, and assistant to accountant-general; collector of Burdwan, 1857-59; magistrate and collector, 1859; officiating civil and sessions judge, 1859-60; deputed on special duty in 1860 to adjudicate on suits arising out of the indigo disturbances, Nuddea and Jessore; superintendent and remembrancer of legal affairs, and government advocate, 1862, and also member of council of lieutenant-governor of Bengal for making laws and regulations, 1863-64; civil and sessions judge, 1864-66; on special duty in Calcutta to revise law relating to stamp duties; additional member of governor-general's council, 1866; on special duty as chairman of the Orissa famine and orphan relief fund, 1867; judge of the high court of judicature, 1868 to 1871; retired, 1872.

HODGES, ROBERT NATHANIEL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 15th Oct., 1873, and posted to the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; transferred to state railways, Nov., 1879; executive engineer, Sept., 1880; employed as engineer-in-chief on several railway surveys, including Saugur-Katni scheme (Oct., 1893).

HODGSON, GODFREY BECKETT, Captain, I.S.C., Survey of India Department.—First commission, 1st May, 1878; served in Afghan war, 1880; appointed probationary assistant superintendent, 13th Sept., 1883; confirmed, July, 1884; deputy superintendent, May, 1889.

HODGSON, JOSEPH, Audit Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 19th Aug., 1878.

HODSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, April, 1883; superintendent, way and works, North Western railway, Nov., 1891.

HODSON, RICHARD GEORGE, late Education Department, Burma.—Joined the education department as deputy inspector of schools, Mysore, April, 1861; inspector of schools, April, 1867; was for two years in special charge of all Canarese normal schools in the province; acting director of public instruction, Mysore and Coorg, April, 1879, to April, 1880; transferred to Burma as acting principal, government high school, Rangoon, April, 1880; from Oct., 1881, served as inspector of schools in Burma, and officiated as director of public instruction in 1884, and again in 1887 and 1888; retired, Feb., 1893; has translated Munshi Srinivasaya's 'Dialogues in Canarese.'

HOEY, WILLIAM, M.A., D.LIT., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Wesleyan Connexional School, and Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 18th Oct., 1872, and served in N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner, and assistant magistrate; officiating city magistrate, Lucknow, Sept., 1879; joint magistrate, 1st grade (sub. pro tem.), March, 1885; has acted as magistrate and collector of Banda, Fatehpur, and Etawah, and was employed on archaeological explorations in Gondia in 1884; officiating deputy commissioner, Partabgurh, March, 1887; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Aug., 1887; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1890; magistrate and collector, Aug., 1890; author of contributions to the 'Gazetteer of Oudh,' and to the 'Imperial Gazetteer of India,' 'A Monograph on Trades and Manufactures in Northern India,' 'A History of Asaf-ud-Daulah, Nawab Wazir of Oudh,' 'Buddha; His Life, His Doctrine, and His Order,' and 'Memoirs of Delhi and Fyzabad.'

HOGG, ADAM GEORGE FORBES, Lieutenant-General, C.B., I.S.C., late Political Department, Bombay.—First commission, Bombay infantry, Jan., 1854; served with the Persian expedition, 1857 (medal and clasp), in the Indian mutiny, the China war, 1860, the Abyssinian expedition, 1867-68, as deputy assistant quarter-master-general (medal and brevet of major), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80, as director of transport with Bombay reserve and brigade on Kandahar line, and, subsequently, with the Kabul field force (medal and clasp, brevet of colonel); appointed political resident and collector of stamp revenue with charge of the vice-admiralty court, Aden, May, 1885; reverted to military duty, 1890.

HOGG, SIR FREDERIC RUSSELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1856; arrived in India, 12th Feb., 1857; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; postmaster-general, Punjab and Sind, July, 1863; officiating postmaster-general, Bombay, April, 1867; postmaster-general,

Bengal, Nov., 1868; officiating deputy director-general of post office of India, March, 1870; officiating director-general, 1871-72, and again, 1873-76, and 1880-82; representative of the government of India at the postal congress, Paris, 1878; confirmed as director-general of post office of India, March, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; K.C.I.E., May, 1888; retired, April, 1889.

HOGG, STAPLETON COTTON, Financial Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, 25th July, 1861; junior clerk, accountant-general's department, 1862; financial department, 1863; senior clerk, Dec., 1875.

HOGG, SIR STUART S., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1853; arrived in India, Jan., 1853; served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner till 1859, and afterwards in Oudh as deputy commissioner; magistrate and collector, Burdwan, Bengal, 1861; chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, 1866; commissioner of police, Calcutta, and member of council of lieutenant-governor of Bengal, 1874; retired, 1878.

HOGG, THEODORE WILLIAM, Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, 4th Dec., 1857; arrived in India, 28th Jan., 1858; and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner from Aug., 1867; acted as cantonment magistrate, Jubbulpore, 1874-80; also acted at various times as small cause court judge at the same place; confirmed as small cause court judge, March, 1883; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1889.

HOJEL, ABRAHAM NICKSON, Brig.-Surgeon, M.K.Q.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Ir.), late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical service, 27th July, 1859; arrived in India, 16th Feb., 1860, and served under the military department till Feb., 1865; professor of anatomy and physiology, and curator of the museum, Grant medical college, and assistant surgeon, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital, Feb., 1865; also acted as surgeon to coroner of Bombay, and professor of medicine at the Grant medical college; from July, 1866, to Nov., 1871, served in the Persian Gulf as civil surgeon, Bushire, and assistant to the resident; served subsequently under the military department, and as acting ophthalmic surgeon, presidency surgeon and marine surgeon, Bombay; appointed professor of physiology, Grant medical college, and second physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital, Sept., 1876; also acted as 1st physician of the hospital and principal of the college, March-April, 1878, as surgeon of the Gokuldas Tejpal native general hospital, Oct., 1883, to May, 1884; brigade surgeon, and confirmed as physician of the European general hospital, April, 1887; retired, April, 1891.

HOLBROW, STANLEY CHARLES, Police Department, Punjab.—Joined the service, 16th May, 1864, as assistant district superintendent of police; district superintendent, April, 1873; promoted to 1st grade, Oct., 1892.

HOLDERNESS, THOMAS WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at University College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 17th Feb., 1873, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant secretary to government, March, 1877; junior secretary, board of revenue, Nov., 1878, and officiated as secretary in 1879 and 1881; under secretary

to government of India, revenue and agricultural department, Sept., 1881, to 1885; joint magistrate, N.W. Provinces, April, 1885; under secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, Nov., 1886; officiated as secretary to government in the financial department in 1887, 1888, and 1890; director of land records and agriculture, Nov., 1888; appointed secretary to government, financial department, March, 1892, at the same time acting as secretary in the judicial department; member of legislative council, Aug., 1892; magistrate and collector, continuing to act as judicial secretary, Aug., 1893.

HOLDICH, THOMAS HUNGERFORD, C.B., C.I.E., Colonel, R.E., Survey of India.—Lieutenant, royal engineers, Dec., 1862; arrived in India, 3rd March, 1865; served on special survey duty in the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (frontier medal and clasp); joined the topographical survey as assistant superintendent, July, 1866; on special survey duty with the Abyssinian expedition, 1867-68 (medal); served subsequently in the Gwalior and Central India topographical survey, and was appointed to the charge of the Vizagapatam agency and Central Provinces survey, Dec., 1873; joined the Afghanistan field force in 1879, and was in charge of survey operations in Northern Afghanistan during second period of the campaign, and was assistant field engineer during defence of Sherpur (medal and clasp); in charge of Kohat topographical survey, Feb., 1881; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, April, 1883; in charge of Baluchistan topographical survey, Oct., 1883; attached to the Afghan boundary commission, Sept., 1884; deputy superintendent, 2nd grade, March, 1889; 1st grade, July, 1892; on special duty as survey officer with the Zohb field force in 1890; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892; C.B. and C.I.E., May, 1894; author of contributions to the records of the Royal Geographical Society, on the subject of the geography of Afghanistan and the Indian frontier; also to the journal of the Royal United Service Institute, on military instruction in topography.

HOLIDAY, FREDERICK CHARLES, Auditor's Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, 17th June, 1861; junior assistant, June, 1863; senior examiner, Dec., 1868; designation changed to senior clerk, Oct., 1892; assistant auditor, Jan., 1895.

HOLME, ALAN THOMAS, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Bedford Grammar School, Clifton College, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner.

HOLME, HENRY EDWARD, B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 27th Sept., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; officiating under-secretary to government, March, 1894; confirmed, May, 1894.

HOLMES, ROBERT ANDREW KING, M.A., M.D., M.R.C.S., L.M., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Jail Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment; acted

as superintendent, central and district jails, Meerut, in 1875, and was confirmed in that post, April, 1876; transferred in similar capacity to Lucknow, Feb., 1885; in 1892 acted as inspector-general of prisons.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Windermere College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating cantonment magistrate, and officiating superintendent of Dehra Dun; appointed to take charge of H.H. Amir Yakub Khan, March, 1880; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, April, 1881; private secretary to lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces, March, 1883; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Oudh, Dec., 1883; joint magistrate, 1st grade, June, 1884; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Aug., 1887; magistrate and collector, Dec., 1888; acted as inspector-general of police in 1893; retired, Sept., 1894.

HOLMES, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served as assistant magistrate and collector, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; from June, 1881, to Aug., 1882, served in Bombay as officiating assistant accountant-general; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Coimbatore, Dec., 1884; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, S. Canara, Feb., 1893.

HOLMS, JOHN MITCHELL, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1883, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, April, 1889; junior secretary to board of revenue, May, 1889, and from Feb., 1891; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Oudh, March, 1892; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Sept., 1892; acted as registrar, high court, Feb. to May, 1894.

HOLMWOOD, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Uppingham; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1879; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1890; officiating inspector-general of registration, March, 1890; confirmed, Nov., 1890; officiating district and sessions judge, May-Dec., 1893, and from April, 1894.

HOLROYD, WILLIAM RICE MORLAND, Colonel, I.S.C., late Educational Department, Punjab.—First commission as ensign, Dec., 1853; served from 11th Aug., 1853, in the Punjab educational department as inspector of schools, and was appointed director of public instruction, Punjab, Feb., 1868; 1st grade, education department, April, 1870; under secretary to government of Punjab, educational department, Oct., 1884; reverted to military duty, Oct., 1890.

HOLT, JAMES RICHARD, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Bective College, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1880, and served in N.W. Pro-

vinces as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating deputy superintendent, family domains of Maharaja of Benares, Oct., 1884; officiating under secretary to government, Nov., 1884, to Feb., 1885; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Nov., 1888; officiated in educational department, as professor in Muir central college, March, 1889; retired, Aug., 1892.

HOME, ALEXANDER LEFREVRE, Indian Forest Department.—From 1st March, 1864, served in the forest department in the Punjab, Central Provinces, and Bengal, and was appointed deputy conservator, Oct., 1871; assistant comptroller-general of forests, April, 1877; conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1883; in charge of western circle, Upper Burma, Oct., 1892.

HOME, FREDERICK JERVIS, C.S.I., Colonel, R.E., late India Public Works Department.—First commission as lieutenant, 11th June, 1858; arrived in India, 6th Sept., 1860; served in the public works department, N.W. Provinces, irrigation branch, from Feb., 1861, as assistant engineer and executive engineer, and was employed on the Ganges, Eastern Jumna, and Agra canals, and other works; superintending engineer, Jan., 1883; officiating chief engineer, irrigation branch, and joint secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, 1885; superintending engineer, 2nd class, March, 1886; on special duty in the Bhurtpur state, Oct., 1886; officiating chief engineer and joint secretary, Punjab, Nov., 1886; superintending engineer, class I., Nov., 1887; 3rd chief engineer and joint secretary to government of Punjab, Dec., 1889; officiating secretary, Sept., 1890, and again in 1893; inspector-general of irrigation and deputy-secretary to the government of India, public works department, Nov., 1890; C.S.I., May, 1892; reverted to military department, Oct., 1894.

HOME, ROBERT, C.I.E., Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Punjab.—First commission, Bengal engineers, 1853; assistant engineer, irrigation department, N.W. Provinces, Sept., 1856; transferred to Punjab, Nov., 1858; executive engineer, July, 1861; superintendent of works, Sutlej canal, Nov., 1867, and afterwards on the Sirhind canal, Jan., 1869; superintending engineer, Sirhind canal circle, Dec., 1869; officiating chief engineer for irrigation, and joint secretary to government of Punjab, Dec., 1876, to Jan., 1879; chief engineer and joint secretary for irrigation, Punjab, 1882; officiating inspector-general of irrigation, and deputy secretary to government of India, public works department, in 1884 and 1885; confirmed in this appointment, Nov., 1886; chief engineer, 2nd class, March, 1886; retired, Dec., 1889.

HOOKER, WILLIAM HENSLOW, Store Department, India Office.—Supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depot, Aug., 1877; 1st grade, July, 1888.

HOOPER, JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at University College, and University of London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1871, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; settlement officer, Basti, Nov., 1884; 1st grade, April, 1889; magistrate and collector, April, 1890; secretary, board of revenue, Aug., 1890.

HOOPER, WILLIAM ROE, Brigade Surgeon-Lieutenant-Colonel, Medical Board, India

Office.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, Feb., 1859; arrived in India, Aug., 1859, and served in Bengal, holding various military medical charges; from July, 1861, served in the N.W. Provinces as civil assistant surgeon, and was appointed superintendent of central prison, Benares, Dec., 1864; rank of surgeon, Feb., 1871; civil surgeon of Benares, Sept., 1874; Lucknow, May, 1890; civil surgeon and jail superintendent, Benares, Nov., 1890; Lucknow, Aug., 1891; president of the India office medical board, Jan., 1895.

HOPE, CHARLES WILLIAM WEBLEY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Mannamead School, Plymouth, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Aug., 1890; assistant commissioner, Oudh, June, 1891; settlement officer, April, 1893; has acted as city magistrate, Lucknow, and as under secretary to government.

HOPE, HUGH RICHARD, Brigadier-General, Madras Secretariat.—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1860; arrived, 10th Dec., 1860, and was attached to 1st dragoon guards; aide-de-camp to Sir W. Denison, governor of Madras, Oct., 1863, to Nov., 1864; adjutant, 1st Madras light cavalry, Nov., 1865; brigade-major, Aug., 1876; deputy assistant adjutant general, June, 1877; assistant adjutant general, Jan., 1882; commandant, 1st Madras lancers, Sept., 1886; deputy adjutant general, March, 1888; secretary to government, military department, Oct., 1891.

HOPE, JAMES, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; assistant collector and magistrate, May, 1863; served in Bombay from Oct., 1864, to July, 1867, and subsequently in Madras, and was appointed principal assistant collector, Ganjam, Sept., 1870; acted as collector and magistrate and political agent in the districts of Ganjam, Kurnool, and Godaveri, 1870-77; from Jan., 1877, served in the judicial department as district and sessions judge of Chingleput, and from Dec., 1882, in South Arcot; retired, 1888.

HOPE, SIR THEODORE CRACRAFT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Barrister-at-Law, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 5th June, 1853, and served as assistant collector and magistrate and educational inspector; private secretary to governor of Bombay, May, 1860; president of committee on finances of educational department, March, 1861; joint commissioner with the secretary in the public works department to receive North Kanara from the Madras government, Feb., 1862; deputy commissioner of customs, salt, and opium, Oct., 1862; collector and magistrate, Kaira, Jan., 1867; collector and magistrate and agent to governor, Surat, March, 1867; acting municipal commissioner, Bombay, Nov., 1871; acting commissioner of customs and opium, Nov., 1874; member of tariff committee at Calcutta, Dec., 1874; from July, 1875, served as additional member of the governor-general's council for making laws and regulations, also acting as secretary to the government of India in department of revenue, agriculture,

and commerce, and was appointed additional secretary for famine, Dec., 1876; reappointed as additional member of governor-general's council in 1877 and 1878; commissioner, Northern division, and agent to governor, Surat, Jan., 1878; on special political duty in England, Oct.-Nov., 1878; provisional member of council of governor of Bombay, Oct., 1880; commissioner, 1st grade, July, 1881; from April, 1881, acted as secretary to government of India, finance and commerce department, and was confirmed in this appointment, Aug., 1881; ordinary member of governor-general's council in charge of public works department, July, 1882; retired, Jan., 1888.

HOPKINS, EGBERT SILAS, Financial Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, registry department, 14th Nov., 1883; transferred to financial department, Sept., 1891.

HOPKINS, HENRY MAYNE REID, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Dulwich College, and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Aug., 1892; assistant superintendent and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, Feb.-Dec., 1893.

HOPKINS, JOHN ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1860; arrived, 14th Oct., 1861, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; also officiating district superintendent of police, March, 1872, inspector of schools, 1872-76, and officiating inspector-general of registration, July, 1875, to Feb., 1876; magistrate and collector, March, 1878; officiating commissioner, Chota Nagpore division, June, 1888; Orissa division, Feb., 1889; Patna, Oct., 1890, and June, 1892; acting opium agent, Bihar, June, 1892; appointed to that post, April, 1893.

HOPKINSON, WILLIAM, Colonel, Bengal Infantry.—First commission, 1859; from 28th Nov., 1867, served in the non-regulation commission, Bengal, as assistant commissioner, and sub-registrar of assurances; cantonment magistrate, Dinapur, March, 1874, and subsequently of Barrackpur, and small cause court judge, Nov., 1875; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, April, 1886; cantonment magistrate, Dinapur, April, 1887; Barrackpur, May, 1891; retired, Jan., 1892.

HORDERN, PETER, B.A., late Educational Department, Burma.—Appointed to the department, 19th Nov., 1861; arrived in India, March, 1862; assistant professor of English literature, Presidency college, Calcutta, March, 1862; director of public instruction, British Burma, Nov., 1867; fellow of Calcutta university, April, 1876; retired, 1889; author of a 'Handbook of Rules and Practice of the Education Department, Burma.'

HORMASJI DADABHAI, Khan Bahadur, Bombay Judicial Department.—Joined the service, 26th April, 1889, and acted for various periods as 4th and 3rd judge, Bombay small cause court; appointed 5th judge, July, 1891; 4th judge, Oct., 1892.

HORN, DAVID BAYNE, A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant

engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in Bengal; assistant secretary to government, irrigation branch, Feb., 1878; executive engineer, March, 1882; under-secretary, public works department, Aug., 1885; member of public works accounts committee, 1889; in charge of south-western circle in 1893 and 1894.

HORNE, WILLIAM OGLIVIE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; acting deputy registrar, high court, Sept., 1886, to July, 1887; head assistant collector and magistrate, March, 1890; officiating secretary to commissioner of salt, &c. revenue, in 1891, and 1892-94; special assistant, Ganjam, Feb., 1893; deputy commissioner of salt, &c., revenue (S. division), Nov., 1893; head assistant, S. Canara, March, 1894.

HORSFALL, JEREMIAH GARNETT, C.I.E., B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Giggleswick Grammar School, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Madras, and was appointed district and sessions judge, South Malabar, April, 1878, and collector and magistrate, Kistna, Jan., 1879, and afterwards in Vizagapatam and Ganjam; retired, April, 1890; C.I.E., May, 1890.

HORSFALL, THOMAS MENDELSSOHN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Wilhelms-Gymnasium, Berlin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; on special duty preparing Madras administration report, Sept., 1880, and again, Sept., 1881; head assistant to the collector and magistrate, Madura, Dec., 1884, also forest settlement officer, Nov., 1885; acted as collector and magistrate; member of the board of examiners, May, 1888; registrar of high court (appellate side), Sept., 1888; principal assistant to the collector, &c., Ganjam, and acting district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; collector and magistrate, Dec., 1891; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1892.

HORSFORD, NORMAN MACLEOD THOMAS, Colonel, I.S.C., Port Blair Administration.—Arrived in India as ensign, Bengal infantry, 29th Dec., 1857; served at the siege and capture of Lucknow, March, 1858, at the action of Nawabganj, June, 1858, and at the capture of Fort Oomerin, in Oudh, Dec., 1858 (medal and clasp); from Aug., 1861, to Dec., 1867, served in the police department, N.W. Provinces, as assistant inspector-general, and district superintendent; from Dec., 1867, served in Oudh as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; junior secretary to chief commissioner, Aug., 1869, to Jan., 1874, and acted for various periods as personal assistant to chief commissioner; officiating director of public instruction, Oct., 1873, to Jan., 1874; superintendent of encumbered estates, Rae Bareli, 1874-76; officiating superintendent of excise and stamps, and inspector-general of registration, April, 1876; cantonment magistrate, Lucknow,

March, 1878; city magistrate and wasika officer, Lucknow, April, 1883; officiating assistant director of agriculture and commerce, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Aug.-Nov., 1884; district judge, 3rd grade, April, 1886; deputy commissioner, March, 1887; chief commissioner, Andaman and Nicobars, and superintendent of Port Blair, April, 1892.

HORSLEY, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at King's School, Canterbury, and Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1870; served as assistant collector and magistrate, and was in charge of the forests in Khandeish, Oct., 1872, to Feb., 1874; employed from March to Oct., 1874, on famine relief operations in Dinajapore, in Bengal, and subsequently on forest demarcation work in Khandeish; on famine duty, Kaladgi, Aug., 1877; forest settlement officer, Nov., 1878; second assistant collector and acting junior collector and magistrate, Sept., 1883; from June, 1884, acted as assistant judge and sessions judge, and from May, 1886, as 2nd grade judge; joint judge and sessions judge, May, 1887; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, June, 1891; 2nd grade, March, 1894.

HORSPool, WILLIAM PADDISON, Auditor's Department, India Office.—Educated at the Newark Collegiate School; clerk, 2nd class, India office, 19th Aug., 1878.

HÖRST, HENRY, late Survey Department, Government of India.—Librarian and accountant, Thomason college, Roorkee, Jan., 1853; sub-assistant, great trigonometrical survey, Sept., 1854; in charge of picket which repulsed night attack on Murree sanitarium, Sept., 1857 (mutiny medal); transferred to topographical survey, senior department, April, 1868; assistant superintendent, 1st grade, Oct., 1869; served in various parts of India, and was appointed deputy superintendent, Burma topographical survey, Sept., 1882; 3rd grade, deputy superintendent, Nov., 1884; retired, 1889.

HOSE, JOHN WALTER, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Dulwich and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, May, 1891; acting under-secretary to government, Dec., 1891; confirmed, April, 1893; officiated as financial secretary, Nov., 1893; registrar, high court, March, 1894.

HÖSKING, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at King's College School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 1st Feb., 1869, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, acting assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan, and registrar of the high court; judge and sessions judge, Khandeish, Jan., 1884; 2nd grade judge, Sept., 1885; district judge, Thana, March, 1888; 1st grade judge and sessions judge, Karachi, June, 1891; acted as judicial commissioner and judge of the sadar court in Sind, in 1890-91, and from May, 1892; judicial commissioner, Lower Burma, Nov., 1892.

HOSKINS, WILLIAM HENRY, Colonel, I.S.C., Police Department, Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, July, 1858; arrived in India, 7th Sept., 1858; served in Madras police department from May, 1863, as assistant superintendent and superintendent.

HOUGH, ALFRED LACEY, Burma Commission.—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1870, as sub-assistant conservator of forests; appointed to the commission as extra assistant commissioner, Oct., 1874; assistant commissioner, Feb., 1885; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1891.

HOUGHTON, BERNARD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at All Saints, Bloxham, Wellingborough Grammar School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 23rd Jan., 1885; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1885; transferred for employment in Burma, Aug., 1886; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, Dec., 1888; deputy commissioner, March, 1891; author of an 'Essay on the Language of the Southern Chins.'

HOUSE, HENRY FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Bristol Grammar School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1884; attached to the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Feb., 1890; assistant superintendent, Dehra Dun, April, 1891, to April, 1892; assistant commissioner, Feb., 1893; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Aug., 1893; settlement officer, Nov., 1893; has edited the N.W. Provinces Rent Act.

HOUSLEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India office, 19th Feb., 1872; junior clerk, June, 1882.

HOUSTON, JOHN, Deputy Surgeon-General, M.D., late Madras Medical Department.—Appointed, 4th Aug., 1855; arrived in India, 3rd March, 1856, and served in Mysore in various military charges, and also as civil surgeon, chemical examiner, and superintendent of jail; surgeon-major, Aug., 1867; from July, 1871, served as surgeon to the Mysore commission; brigade surgeon, June, 1881; officiating residency surgeon, May, 1883; surgeon to the Mysore government; retired, March, 1886.

HOWARD, EDWARD STAFFORD.—Educated at Harrow, and Trinity College, Cambridge; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1875; represented East Cumberland in the House of Commons from April, 1876, till the general election of 1885, when he was returned for the Thornbury division of Gloucestershire, but lost his seat in the succeeding election of 1886; under secretary of state for India, April-Aug., 1886; appointed a commissioner of woods and forests, Oct., 1893.

HOWARD, WILLIAM STUART, M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service as probationer, 10th June, 1859; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1859; assistant engineer, 1st class, April, 1864; executive engineer, Dec., 1865; acted as principal, engineering college, Poona, 1867; acting secretary to the government of Bombay, public works department, Sept., 1887; chief engineer, Aug., 1888; 1st class, Nov., 1891; secretary to government of Bombay, public works department, June, 1892; retired, Feb., 1894.

HOWELL, ARTHUR PEARSE, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at St. John's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1857; arrived in India, 27th Jan., 1858; from May, 1859, to April, 1864, served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, assistant secretary, and acting under secretary to the government; from April, 1864, to March, 1878, served in the secretariat of the government of India, first as under secretary, financial department, and then as under secretary and acting secretary, home department; also as acting deputy secretary, department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, and was placed on special duty March, 1869; commissioner, Jubbulpore division, Central Provinces, April, 1877; secretary, famine commission, March, 1878; on special duty, June, 1878; commissioner, Nerbudda division, Sept., 1878; acting judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, Dec., 1879, to Feb., 1880, and again in 1885; commissioner, Hyderabad assigned districts, June to Dec., 1882; member of education commission under the government of India, Dec., 1882, to Sept., 1883; commissioner, Nerbudda division, Sept., 1883; officiating resident, 1st class, Hyderabad, March, 1888; retired, Aug., 1889; author of notes (2) on education in India in 1866-67 and 1870-71, the former of which was presented to parliament; of a note on jails and jail discipline in India, and of a paper on educational legislation.

HOWELL, MORTIMER SLOPER, LL.D., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Christ's Hospital, and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1862; from Jan., 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating inspector of public instruction, April, 1867; assistant superintendent and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, March, 1868; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1871; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Nov., 1879; from March, 1880, served in the judicial department as district and sessions judge in various districts; acted as additional judicial commissioner in Oudh in 1891, and was confirmed in that post, Dec., 1891; judicial commissioner (sub. pro tem.), Nov., 1892; author of a grammar of the classical Arabic language.

HUDSON, CHARLES WILLIAM MAITLAND, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Doveton Protestant College, Madras, Madras University, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1887; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1888.

HUDSON, WILLIAM HEBARD, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Pembroke College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; from Dec., 1873, to Jan., 1875, served under the government of Bengal on famine duty in Behar; from May, 1880, served in the judicial depart-

ment, N.W. Provinces, as civil and sessions judge; retired, Jan., 1890.

HUGHES, ARTHUR JOHN, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India. —Joined the service, 11th July, 1862, and arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1862; executive engineer, Dec., 1869; assistant secretary to government of Bengal, irrigation branch, May, 1872; on famine works, Salem district, Sept., 1877; served under the foreign department in Kashmir, Dec., 1884, to Feb., 1885; superintending engineer, Nov., 1888; superintending engineer for waterworks, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, July, 1888, to July, 1893.

HUGHES, DAVID ERSKINE, Surgeon-Colonel, M.D., F.R.C.S., late Bombay Medical Service. —Joined the service, March, 1866; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1866; served under the military department, and also acted as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Satara, 1880-81, as surgeon of the European general hospital, Bombay, 1881, and as civil surgeon at Surat, 1881-82; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Belgaum, from May, 1882; brigade surgeon, Oct., 1883; civil surgeon, Poona, Sept., 1890; has acted as deputy sanitary commissioner, and as first physician of the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital; reverted to military department, Sept., 1892.

HUGHES, EDWARD WILLIAM MACKENZIE, late Public Works Department, Government of India. —Joined the department, April, 1871, as assistant engineer, Punjab irrigation, and was employed on the Sirhind canal; locomotive superintendent, Dhond-Manmad state railway, May, 1879; locomotive superintendent, Indus valley railway, Feb., 1881; deputy locomotive superintendent, North-Western railway, Jan., 1886; on special duty in Mexico in 1887-88; locomotive superintendent, East Coast railway, Jan., 1892; retired, Aug., 1893.

HUGHES, GEORGE, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab). —Educated at the Liverpool Institute, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 23rd Oct., 1875, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating judicial assistant, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating district judge; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab, Nov., 1887; district judge, Aug., 1890; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Dec., 1890; retired, Dec., 1891.

HUGHES, JOHN WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the City of London School, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

HUGHES, RALPH BULLER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Marlborough, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 4th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

HUGHES, THEODORE WILLIAM HUGHES, A.R.S.M., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India. —Appointed from England as assistant in the geological survey, and joined, 11th Nov., 1862; was employed chiefly in the Central Provinces, Central India, and Bengal, in connection with coal and iron deposits; 1st grade officer, April, 1883; superintended Rewah coal explorations

from March, 1883; designation changed to deputy superintendent of the geological survey of India; superintendent, April, 1887; acted as director in 1892; author of contributions to the survey publications.

HUGHES, WALTER CHARLETON, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1868; served in various grades of the public works department, Bombay; assistant secretary to government in that department, April, 1882; under secretary, March, 1884; secretary, Nov., 1888; chairman, Bombay port trust, June, 1892.

HUGHES, WILLIAM, B.A., M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as probationary civil engineer; assistant engineer, Sept., 1870; executive engineer, Sept., 1877.

HUGHES, WILLIAM GWYNNE, Colonel, I.S.C., late Burma Commission.—First commission as ensign, June, 1858; arrived in India, 11th Sept., 1858; was employed on military duty and in the police department, Burma, till June, 1864; transferred to the Burma commission, June, 1864, and served as assistant commissioner in the Amherst district, cantonment magistrate of Rangoon, town magistrate of Akyab, superintendent of the hill tracts, Arakan (Nov., 1871), and deputy commissioner (1st grade, July, 1886); retired, June, 1889; author of 'The Hill Tracts of Arakan.'

HUGHESDON, JOSEPH CHARLES B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Sutton Valence Grammar School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; served in various districts of Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, small cause court judge, sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting district and sessions judge, and acting collector and magistrate; on famine duty, June, 1877; appointed district and sessions judge, Tinnevelly, March, 1880; retired, Aug., 1888.

HULLAH, ARTHUR, Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Appointed assistant superintendent, 20th Feb., 1869, and employed on various lines; superintendent, Aug., 1885; in charge, Rajputana division, April, 1892; personal assistant to director general, Feb., 1893.

HUME, ALLAN OCTAVIAN, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1849; arrived in India, 7th March, 1849; served in N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, Etawah, March, 1858; officiating civil and sessions judge, Bareilly, May, 1867; commissioner of customs, N.W. Provinces, Punjab, and Central Provinces, July, 1867; officiating secretary to government of India, home department, 1870-71; secretary to government of India in department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, June, 1871; member of board of revenue, N.W. Provinces, Oct., 1879; retired, 1882; author of several works on ornithology.

HUME, WILLIAM WHEELER, Colonel, I.S.C., late Bengal Police.—1st commission, 5th Aug., 1854; served in the Indian mutiny with the Meerut volunteer cavalry; afterwards as a volunteer with the artillery during siege and capture of

Delhi (medal and clasp); district superintendent of police, Aug., 1863; 1st grade, April, 1888; reverted to military duty, 1889.

HUMFRESS, GEORGE, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, May, 1883; employed on North-Western railway from Jan., 1886, and in 1893 officiated as engineer-in-chief, main line section.

HUMFREY, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., Bombay Police Department.—Joined the service, 2nd June, 1865, and arrived in India, 22nd Dec., 1865; lieutenant in staff corps, Nov., 1868; served in the Abyssinian expedition in charge of commissariat camel train (medal), Nov., 1868; assistant superintendent of police, Aug., 1873; on special duty in political department for short periods in 1873, 1874, 1876, 1877, 1883; superintendent of police, Aug., 1875; inspector-general of police, Bombay, February, 1890; on special duty to England with Thaker Sahib of Morvi's eldest son, March, 1890; resumed his post, Nov., 1890.

HUMPHRYS, ISAAC HUTCHINSON, Accounts Department, India Office.—Educated at the Royal School, Raphoe, and Trinity College, Dublin (exhibitor and prizeman); clerk, 2nd class, 5th July, 1872; junior clerk, Feb., 1876; senior clerk, Sept., 1894.

HUNT, SAMUEL BRADSHAW, Surgeon-Colonel, Indian Medical Service.—Assistant surgeon, 31st March, 1865; arrived, 10th Dec., 1865, and served under the military department, and also as civil surgeon at Coimbatore and at Vizagapatam with charge of the jail and lunatic asylum; from May, 1875, served as surgeon in the 4th, and subsequently in the 3rd district, Madras; surgeon, 4th district, and professor of medical jurisprudence, medical college, Feb. to Aug., 1893; principal medical officer, Rangoon.

HUNTER, FREDERICK MERCER, C.B., C.S.I., Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India as ensign, Bombay infantry, June, 1861; was employed on military duty up to Aug., 1871, from which date he served as assistant political resident at Aden; also acted on various occasions as political resident, as cantonment magistrate, and as Arabic interpreter; first assistant at Aden, Sept., 1880; also political agent for the Somali coast, and collector of abkari and deputy collector of salt revenue; acting political agent at Aden, 1887, and at Cutch, July, 1888; confirmed in latter appointment, Feb., 1889; political agent, Southern Maratha country, Sept., 1893; political agent, Cutch, Jan., 1894; acted as political agent, Kolhapur, Jan.-June, 1894.

HUNTER, GEORGE YEATES, Brigade-Surgeon, M.R.C.S. (Lon.), L.S.A., late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical department, 23rd July, 1858; arrived in India, 9th Sept., 1858, and served in the military department, and as civil surgeon in various stations; acting second surgeon, Jamsetji Jijibhoi hospital, acting professor of anatomy, Grant medical college, and curator of the museum, Bombay, April-Oct., 1872; acting superintendent of lunatic asylum, Colaba, Nov.-Dec., 1872; civil surgeon, Karachi, Feb., 1882; presidency surgeon, Bombay, in medical charge of jail, house of correction, Byculla schools and

Elphinstone college, May, 1884; brigade surgeon, Feb., 1885; retired, Oct., 1888.

HUNTER, JOHN MUIR, Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Bombay.—First commission, royal artillery, April, 1864; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1865; boundary settlement officer, Baroda and Mahi Kantha states, Oct., 1869; from June, 1872, served as assistant political agent in Mahi Kantha, Kolhapur, and Kathiawar; assistant to the political agent and magistrate of Kathiawar state railway, Jan., 1880; from Feb., 1883, served as superintendent of surveys under the Rajasthanik court, Kathiawar, and was employed on special duties in settling claims in dispute between native states, from Dec., 1884; acting president, Rajasthanik court, May, 1887, and again in 1889; confirmed, May, 1891; assistant agent, Baroda, Oct., 1891; first assistant political agent, Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; political agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1893; president, Rajasthanik court, Jan., 1894.

HUNTER, SIR WILLIAM GUYER, Surgeon-General, K.C.M.G., M.D., F.R.C.P., F.R.C.S.—Educated at King's College, London, Aberdeen University, and at various hospitals; joined the Bombay medical service, 1850; served in the Burmese war and Indian Mutiny; principal of the Grant medical college, Bombay, 1868; surgeon-major, Feb., 1870; surgeon-general, Bombay, Feb., 1877; vice-chancellor of the university of Bombay, 1879; retired, March, 1880; consulting physician to Charing Cross hospital, 1883; served on the cholera commission in Egypt, 1883; British delegate to the international sanitary conference at Rome, June, 1885; hon. commandant of the volunteer medical staff corps, Sept., 1885; was returned to the House of Commons for Hackney (central division) in 1885, and again in 1886.

HUNTER, SIR WILLIAM WILSON, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A. (Oxford), and LL.D. (Cambridge and Glasgow), late Bengal C.S.—Educated at the Universities of Glasgow, Paris, and Bonn; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived, 10th Nov., 1862; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1863; superintendent of labour transport, Kooshtea, July, 1865; on deputation for compiling a history of Beerbhoom, March, 1866; officiating inspector of schools, May, 1866; officiating superintendent of stamps and stationery, Dec., 1868; director and compiler of the statistical account of Bengal, and on deputation in home department, government of India, July, 1869; compiler of a plan for a statistical survey of India, Jan., 1871; officiating under-secretary, government of India, home department, Feb., 1871; director general of statistics, government of India, Sept., 1871; on special duty to England for purpose of compiling statistical account of Bengal, and 'Imperial Gazetteer,' March, 1875, and March, 1876; on special duty in England, March, 1877, to Nov., 1881; director-general of gazetteers, Oct., 1877; C.I.E., 1878; director general of gazetteers and statistics, June, 1878; director-general of statistics, Dec., 1881; additional member, governor-general's council, Dec., 1881, and again Dec., 1883, and Dec., 1885; president of the education commission, Feb., 1882, to Nov., 1883; deputed to England to give evidence before the committee of the House of Commons

on Indian railways, May, 1884; C.S.I., 1884; member of the finance committee, Feb., 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; retired, Sept., 1887; author of 'The Annals of Rural Bengal,' 1868; 'A Dictionary of the Non-Aryan Languages of India and High Asia,' 1868; pamphlets on Indian legislation and finance, 1869; memorandum on the older records of the board of revenue, N.W. Provinces, 1870; 'The Indian Mussulmans,' 1871; 'The Province of Orissa,' 1872; 'Famine Aspects of Bengal Districts,' 1874; a collected edition of 'Essays on the External Policy of India,' by the late J. W. S. Wyllie, 1875; 'A Life of Lord Mayo,' 2 vols., 1875; 'A Statistical Account of Bengal,' 20 vols., 1875-77; a descriptive catalogue of 382 MS. volumes of historical records in the India office library, 1878; a 'Statistical Account of Assam,' 1879; 'The Imperial Gazetteer of India,' 9 vols., 1881 (2nd edition, 14 vols., 1887); 'The Indian Empire,' 1881 (3rd edition, 1893); 'England's Work in India,' 1881; 'A Brief History of the Indian People,' &c.

HURRY, ARNOLD EARDLEY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at City of London School and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1882, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; from March, 1886, served in Burma as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner; re-transferred, Dec., 1890.

HUTCHESON, GEORGE, M.D., C.M., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Medical Service.—Joined the service as assistant surgeon, 1st Oct., 1866; surgeon, July, 1873; surgeon-major, Oct., 1880; served on general and military duty, and from Feb., 1870, to Sept., 1871, on special duty as chemical analyst; on special duty, cholera enquiry, Sept., 1872, to Dec., 1873; officiated as statistical officer to the government of India, in the sanitary and medical department; sanitary commissioner and superintendent-general of vaccination, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, May, 1889; author of 'Cholera, its Causes, and Mode of Dissemination,' and 'Cholera as a Preventible Disease.'

HUTCHINGS, FRANCIS SMITH, Auditor's Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 23rd June, 1879.

HUTCHINS, SIR PHILIP PERCEVAL, K.C.S.I., B.L., late Madras C.S., Judicial and Public Department, India Office.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1857; arrived, 27th Feb., 1858; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acted as deputy secretary to government in the revenue department in 1862 and 1863, and in the departments under the chief secretary in 1862, as secretary to board of examiners in 1863, and as judge of the court of small causes, Chittoor, Sept., 1863; private secretary to governor of Madras, Nov., 1863; registrar, high court, March, 1865; from Feb., 1869, acted as civil and sessions judge in various places, and was appointed district and sessions judge, Madura, April, 1873; called to the bar (Inner Temple), June, 1875; member of rent law committee, Sept., 1882; acting secretary, revenue department, Dec., 1882; on special duty in connection with reorganization of judicial establishments, March, 1883; board of examiners, May, 1883; judge of the high court,

Madras, Nov., 1883; fellow of the Madras university, March, 1884; member of governor's council, Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; member of the council of the governor-general, Nov., 1888; K.C.S.I., May, 1891; retired, Nov., 1893; secretary, judicial and public department, India office, Dec., 1893.

HUTCHINSON, FREDERICK THOMAS, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 25th March, 1871, as assistant controller; deputy controller, May, 1874; examiner, Sept., 1881; examiner, public works accounts, Bombay, May, 1890.

HUTCHINSON, JAMES BIRD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 26th May, 1864; from March, 1868, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating judicial assistant, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating settlement officer; district judge, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1884; services placed at the disposal of the government of India, Aug. to Oct., 1887; commissioner and superintendent, Lahore, March, 1884; has published a 'Guide to Dalhousie,' and a general index of regulations and acts in force in the Punjab.

HUTTON, EDWARD, late Postal Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as superintendent of mails, Jubbulpore, 10th Feb., 1858; superintendent travelling post office, Nov., 1877; inspector, field force, Peshawur column, Feb., 1879; presidency postmaster at Calcutta, April, 1881; acted as postmaster-general, Lahore, in 1890, at Bombay in 1891, and at Calcutta from April, 1892; retired, April, 1893.

HYDE, HENRY, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Arrived, 27th June, 1867; surgeon, Oct., 1871; zillah surgeon and jail superintendent, April, 1872; civil surgeon, Nov., 1884.

HYNES, GEORGE JOHN, Post Office Department, Government of India.—Entered the post office department, 4th May, 1868; assistant director-general of the post office of India, Oct., 1880; deputy postmaster-general, Burma, June, 1887; postmaster-general, Punjab, Jan., 1891; deputy director-general of post office of India, July, 1891; officiating postmaster, Bengal, March, 1894.

IBBETSON, DENZIL CHARLES JELF, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Collegiate School, Adelaide (South Australia), and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1870, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and settlement officer; deputy superintendent, census, Punjab, Aug., 1881; compiler, 'Punjab Gazetteer,' March, 1883; officiating director of public instruction, March, 1884; deputy commissioner, April, 1885; officiating superintendent, Kapurthala state, Nov., 1885; on special duty in connection with census conference, Dec., 1889, and jail conference, Jan., 1890; officiated as 2nd financial commissioner in the Punjab in 1890; in charge of Gujrunwala settlement, May, 1891; member of Deccan agriculturalists commission, 1891-92; on special duty connected with the cantonment act, 1893; officiating commissioner and superintendent, 1891, 1892, and 1893, and from March, 1894; has published 'Census Report of the Punjab (1881),'

'Report on the Settlement of the Karnal District,' 'Outlines of Punjab Ethnography,' 'Gazetteer of the Districts of the Punjab.'

ILBERT, SIR COURTENAY PEREGRINE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Educated at Marlborough, and Oxford; scholar, and afterwards fellow of Balliol College; first class (classics), moderations, 1862, and the final schools, 1864; Hertford scholar, 1861; Ireland scholar, 1864; Craven scholar, 1864; Eldon law scholar, 1867; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 7th June, 1869; counsel to education department, 1878-82; took his seat as law member of the council of the governor-general of India, 1st May, 1882, and was president of the council, 3rd Feb. to 8th March, 1886; resigned, 13th Nov., 1886; vice-chancellor of Calcutta university, Feb., 1885 to Nov., 1886; assistant parliamentary counsel to the treasury, 1886; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895.

IMPEY, WILLIAM HENRY LOCKINGTON, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Wellington College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant commissioner, Naini Tal, Nov., 1886; acting under-secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Dec., 1887; confirmed, May, 1888; settlement officer, 2nd grade, Oct., 1889; joint magistrate, April, 1892; acting secretary to government, financial department, April, 1892; confirmed, Aug., 1893; also member of legislative council, Dec., 1893.

INAM ALI, Maulvi, B.A., Punjab Commission.—Appointed assistant professor, government college, Lahore, 31st Oct., 1887; appointed probationer under native civil service rules, 4th Feb., 1888; assistant commissioner, April, 1891; has published in Urdu a translation and an epitome of Hamilton's lectures on psychology.

INCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

INGLIS, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Wellington College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived, 27th Nov., 1880; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1890; acting junior secretary to board of revenue, July, 1891; confirmed, July, 1892; also officiated as secretary, Aug., 1893, to March, 1894.

INGLIS, WILLIAM ARBUTHNOT, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in Bengal; executive engineer, May, 1883; officiating under-secretary, public works department, Bengal, Oct., 1891, to Feb., 1892.

INGRAM, GERALD CONSTANTINE WINNINGTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Broms Grove School, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

INNES, JAMES JOHN McLEOD, Lieutenant-General, V.C., R.E., late Public Works Department, India.—First commission in Bengal

engineers, 1848; served under the civil engineer, Punjab, from 1852; assistant engineer, Bari Doab canal, 1854; assistant to chief engineer, Oudh, Jan., 1857; during the Mutiny served at the defence of Lucknow, the battle of Cawnpore, the action of Sultanpur, and the siege of Lucknow (medal and two clasps, V.C.); executive engineer, Cawnpore, Dec., 1857; garrison engineer, Presidency, April, 1858; controller and examiner of accounts, Lahore, Feb., 1861; officiating accountant-general, public works department, 1865-66, and again 1867-68; on special duty on bank of Bombay committee, 1868-69; superintending engineer, Punjab, Oct., 1869; accountant-general and deputy secretary, public works department, June, 1870; chief engineer, 1st class, Sept., 1881, and deputy inspector-general, military works; inspector-general of military works, 1882; retired, 1886.

INNES, LEWIS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Charterhouse and Haileybury, and appointed to the Madras civil service in 1844; served as assistant collector and magistrate in various districts till Jan., 1855, and afterwards in the judicial department, and was appointed sub-judge of Combaconum, Feb., 1856, and civil and sessions judge of Nundial, July, 1858; judge of the high court of judicature, Madras, Feb., 1865, to Sept., 1883, and officiated on several occasions as chief justice; retired, 1883; fellow of the university of Madras, 1865 to 1883, and was on the syndicate, 1873 to 1883; vice-chancellor of the university, 1874 to 1880.

INNES, WILLIAM, Railway Department, Government of India.—Appointed to superior revenue establishment, state railways, July, 1884; served on the Eastern Bengal and the Punjab Northern state railways, and was appointed traffic superintendent, Burma state railway, Nov., 1885.

IRELAND, WILLIAM DE COURCY, B.A., LL.D., late Burma Commission.—Joined the service as accountant in office of commissioner of Pegu, April, 1859; assistant superintendent of forests, Tougoo, April, 1860; collector of customs, Nov., 1860; from April, 1861, served in the Burma commission as assistant commissioner, town magistrate, and deputy commissioner (1st grade, June, 1879); on special duty, Aug., 1878; acted as inspector-general of police in 1875, 1881-83, and 1885, and as commissioner of Arakan, June-Sept., 1880; boundary officer, Akyab district, Sept., 1880; additional sessions judge, Tenasserim, Jan., 1886; commissioner, Irrawaddy division, July, 1886, and Arakan division, Jan., 1890; retired, Dec., 1890.

IRVINE, GEORGE DUNCAN, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School, and St. John's College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments from May, 1865, as assistant collector and magistrate, acting small cause court judge, sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting collector of sea customs at Madras and protector of emigrants, and acting collector and magistrate; district and sessions judge, Kurnool, July, 1882, and afterwards in various other districts; retired, June, 1891.

IRVINE, ROBERT HAMILTON GRANT, late Bengal Police.—Joined the service as deputy magistrate and deputy collector, Aug., 1861; appointed assistant superintendent of police,

April, 1863; district superintendent, July, 1869; 1st grade, July, 1889; retired, Sept., 1891.

IRVINE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at St. Anne's School, Brixton, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1863; from May, 1864, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, acting cantonment magistrate, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was placed on special duty under the board of revenue, Jan. to April, 1879; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Ghazipur, Dec., 1881; 1st grade, July, 1885; retired, 1888; author of 'Canal Rates versus Land Revenue,' 'The Rent Digest: or the Law Procedure relating to Landlord and Tenant, Bengal,' 'Bangash Nawabs of Farukhabad,' and settlement report of Ghazipur district.

IRVING, L. A., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Army Medical Department.—Medical officer on the personal staff of the governor of Bombay (Rt. Hon. Lord Reay), 1885 to 1890.

IRWIN, ALFRED MACDONALD BULTEEL, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab till June, 1877, and subsequently in Burma as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1886; officiating commissioner, March, 1894.

IRWIN, GEORGE ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S.—Educated at Uppingham, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1878, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner, and officiating city magistrate, Lucknow; assistant secretary to government of India, foreign department, Dec., 1886; officiating junior secretary, Nov., 1887, and May, 1888, and under secretary, foreign department, April, 1889, and again, May, 1890; first assistant secretary for Berar to resident, Hyderabad, March, 1892; additional political agent, Jhalawar, April, 1893.

IRWIN, HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service as assistant engineer, Central Provinces, 1st Feb., 1868; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; superintendent of works, Simla imperial circle, Oct., 1881; C.I.E., June, 1888; acting public works secretary to agents to governor-general for Rajputana and Central India, Nov., 1888; transferred to Madras public works department as superintending engineer, and appointed consulting architect to government, Sept., 1889.

IRWIN, HENRY CROSSLY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Blackheath Proprietary School, St. Edmund's Hall, and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1871, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Oct., 1886; deputy commissioner, July, 1889; magistrate and collector, Nov., 1889; acted as secretary to the board of revenue in 1893; author of 'The Garden of India, or Chapters on Oudh History and Affairs.'

ISAACSON, STEPHEN HENRY HOLDEN, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 15th April, 1869; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878.

ISMAY, STANLEY, B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Bromsgrove Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1871; served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, acting inspector of schools, acting district superintendent of police, and acting deputy commissioner, and was placed on special duty in the Kalahandi state, Jan., 1882; registrar, judicial commissioner's court, May, 1884; officiating inspector-general of police and jails, May to Nov., 1885, and again in April, 1890; judge, small cause court, Jubbulpore, Dec., 1889; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1890; additional sessions judge, Jan., 1892; author of 'Rules for the Superintendence and Management of Jails in the Central Provinces' (1885).

IVENS, FLETCHER JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant locomotive superintendent, Indus Valley railway, 3rd Aug., 1878; acted as locomotive superintendent, Punjab Northern state railway, 1880, 1884, and 1885; district locomotive superintendent, North-Western railway, Jan., 1886; deputy locomotive superintendent, same line, Sept., 1893.

IVENS, THOMAS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 26th July, 1867, and posted to the Punjab; executive engineer, Sept., 1874; on special duty in public works secretariat, Punjab, March, 1886; officiating under-secretary, Oct., 1886, and again, May, 1889; superintending engineer, Oct., 1892.

IZAT, ALEXANDER, M.I.C.E., late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Joined the public works department, June, 1863, as assistant engineer, Hyderabad; executive engineer, March, 1868; transferred to railway branch, 1870, as executive engineer, Nizam's state railway; Holkar state railway, Oct., 1874; transferred to Dhond and Manmad railway, Bombay, 1877; engineer-in-chief, Kathiawar state railway, 1879; engineer-in-chief, Bombay Eastern Deccan state railway, Aug., 1879; superintending engineer, 3rd class, April, 1880; officiating under secretary to government of India, railway branch, Jan., 1883; services lent to Bengal and N.W. railway company as agent and chief engineer, Feb., 1883; 1st superintending engineer, Feb., 1887; retired, 1889.

JACKSON, ARCHIBALD, Audit Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 24th Dec., 1892.

JACKSON, ARTHUR MASON TIPPETTS, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Westminster School, and Brasenose College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 17th December, 1888, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; assistant collector and inspector of factories, Bombay, Sept., 1892.

JACKSON, CHARLES J. J., Deputy-Surgeon-General, M.D., late Bengal Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1855; served in Bengal from Sept., 1859, as civil assistant surgeon and civil surgeon at various stations; also officiated as sanitary commissioner, Bengal, Jan., 1872; civil surgeon, 21 Pergunnahs, with medical charge of

Alipore and Russa jails, and medical inspector of emigrants, Feb., 1883; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail and lunatic asylum, Moorsheedabad, 1885; surgeon-major, Aug., 1867; brigade surgeon, Jan., 1881; retired, April, 1887.

JACKSON, EDWIN JAMES, Survey of India Department.—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1864; assistant superintendent, Nov., 1868; deputy superintendent, Oct., 1885.

JACKSON, FRANK ERNEST, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at the City of London School, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

JACKSON, FREEMAN HENRY, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Bombay.—First commission, Bombay infantry, 1861; arrived in India, 10th Jan., 1862; from Jan., 1867, acted in charge of office of assistant resident, Amreli, and was appointed to settle boundaries in Eastern Kathiawar, Jan., 1868; acting assistant-resident, Dwarka, and commandant of H.H. the Gaekwar's battalion, March, 1869; assistant resident, Baroda, in charge Okhamandal, July, 1874; on special duty, Baroda, Jan., 1875; treasury officer, Baroda residency, Feb., 1875; acting cantonment magistrate, Baroda, April, 1875; extra assistant general superintendent, thuggee and dacoity department, June, 1875; from May, 1876, served as assistant agent to the governor-general, Baroda, and from Feb., 1879, as superintendent of the Gaekwar's contingent, Kathiawar, assistant in charge of Amreli Mahals, and superintendent for the improvement of the breed of horses in Kathiawar; officiated for various periods as resident and governor-general's agent, Baroda.

JACKSON, HUGH MILBOURNE, Captain R.E., Survey of India Department.—First commission, 10th June, 1877; entered the department as assistant superintendent, June, 1883; deputy superintendent, April, 1889; served in the Burma expedition, 1886, the first southern Shan expedition, 1886-87, and with the northern Karen column, 1888-89.

JACKSON, WILLIAM GRIERSON, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Manchester Grammar School, and University of London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 12th Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; from Feb., 1877, to Jan., 1880, served in British Burma as assistant commissioner, 4th grade; deputy superintendent of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, March, 1882; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Nov., 1887, and 1st grade, April, 1889; also officiated as magistrate and collector, district and sessions judge, and deputy commissioner; confirmed in the latter grade, Aug., 1891; magistrate and collector, Sept., 1892; author of Mirzapur volume of N.W. Provinces Gazetteer.

JACOB, EDWARD FOUNTAINE, A.M.I.C.E., Railway Department, Government of India.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as assistant engineer; district traffic superintendent, Feb., 1880; attached to public works secretariat, government of India, April, 1883; deputy traffic superintendent, North-Western railway, May, 1890; traffic superintendent, same railway, May, 1892.

JACOB, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Bedford Grammar School, and Highgate Grammar School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1879, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; in charge of forest settlement and demarcation officer's office, Khandeish, Oct., 1881, to Feb., 1882; from May, 1883, served as assistant judge and sessions judge, and also from Dec., 1884, to Oct., 1885, as assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan; from March, 1886, served as senior assistant judge, and from Nov., 1886, as joint judge and sessions judge; acting registrar, high court, appellate side, Feb., 1888; 1st assistant judge, Nov., 1891; joint judge and sessions judge, March, 1894.

JACOB, HERBERT BRUCE, Colonel, I.S.C., late Judicial Department, Bombay.—First commission, Dec., 1857; served from 26th June, 1869, in the educational department, Central Provinces, and was appointed inspector of schools, 3rd grade, April, 1871; acting inspector-general of education, 1882-84; cantonment magistrate, Karachi, 1889 to 1892.

JACOB, LIONEL MONTAGUE, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, May, 1883.

JACOB, SAMUEL SWINTON, C.I.E., A.I.C.E., Colonel, I.S.C., India Public Works Department.—Appointed, 17th Nov., 1862, and posted to Bombay; services lent to Jeypore state, July, 1867; executive engineer, Sept., 1870; public works secretary to agent in Rajputana, 1886, 1887, and 1889; C.I.E., May, 1890; superintending engineer, Jan., 1893.

JACOB, STEPHEN, C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educated at Highgate School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1871, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, officiating registrar in the judicial commissioner's court, and officiating deputy commissioner, till Dec., 1878, when he was transferred to Bengal; on special duty, legislative department, government of India, April, 1879, and again in March, 1880; officiated as under secretary and as assistant secretary in department of finance and commerce, government of India, in 1880 and 1881; assistant comptroller-general, in class III. of the financial department, Nov., 1880; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, Nov., 1881, and in 1882; on special duty with the government of India in department of finance and commerce, Jan. and Dec., 1882; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, June, 1882; under secretary, government of India, finance and commerce department, July, 1882; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, July, 1883; deputy auditor-general, Dec., 1884; officiating accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Feb., 1885; deputy accountant-general, Bengal, Nov., 1885; secretary to the finance committee, March, 1886; on special duty, financial department, Feb., 1888; accountant-general, class I., and commissioner of the department of state paper currency, Bombay, May, 1888; officiating comptroller and auditor-general and head-commissioner of paper currency, April, 1890; confirmed, Nov.,

1891; officiating secretary to government of India, finance and commerce department, Nov., 1892, to Jan., 1893, and again from March, 1894; C.S.I., Jan., 1895.

JACOB, SYDNEY LONG, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 13th January, 1869, and served on irrigation works in Bombay and the Punjab as assistant engineer and executive engineer; served through the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); superintending engineer, Oct., 1890.

JADU NATH ROY, Bengal Judicial Service.—Joined the service, 13th July, 1861, as officiating munsif; sub-judge, July, 1873; small cause court judge, June, 1883; additional judge, Calcutta small cause court, Aug., 1890; 4th judge, Oct., 1893.

JAMES, ARTHUR HUTTON, Police Department, Bengal.—Joined the service, 16th Feb., 1863, as assistant superintendent of police, Assam; also assistant commissioner, Nov., 1866; promoted to district superintendent, Oct., 1873; transferred to Bengal, Dec., 1875; assistant inspector-general, railway police, Nov., 1884.

JAMES, HENRY EVAN MURCHISON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at the Cathedral School, Carlisle, and Durham Grammar School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived, 14th Dec., 1865, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and as acting assistant commissioner, branch inspector-general of registration and educational inspector in Sind; from May, 1873, acted as under secretary to government in the revenue, financial, and general departments, and was confirmed in this appointment, Nov., 1875; also acted as postmaster-general, Bombay, from Nov., 1875; 2nd grade, post-master-general, Bombay, Nov., 1877; post-master-general, Bengal, March, 1882; acted as director-general of the post office in India, 1883-85; on special duty in England, Aug., 1884, to April, 1885; deputy director-general, Nov., 1885; senior collector and collector and magistrate, Ahmedabad, April, 1888; 2nd grade commissioner, June, 1890; 1st grade, Nov., 1890; acting commissioner in Sind, June, 1891; confirmed, Nov., 1892.

JAMES, STEPHEN HARVEY, M.R.A.S., Indian C.S.—Educated at Probus Grammar School, and Shrewsbury Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1870, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; junior secretary, board of revenue, May, 1875, and officiated as secretary, 1877; under secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, and officiating junior secretary, Aug., 1877; on special duty under the orders of the high court, Feb., 1881, and under the orders of the local government in 1882-83; registrar, high court, May, 1881; legal remembrancer, N.W. Provinces, May, 1884; officiating deputy secretary to the government of India, legislative department, March, 1885; officiating secretary to the government of India, legislative department, Dec., 1885; confirmed, Sept., 1887.

JAMESON, ARTHUR SAMUEL, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1886, and served in the locomotive department, North-Western railway;

transferred to Eastern Bengal state railway, Aug., 1888; district locomotive superintendent, July, 1889; locomotive superintendent, Oct., 1890.

JAMESON, ROBERT, M.D., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Sanitary Department, N.W. Provinces.—Joined the Bengal medical service as assistant surgeon, Oct., 1865; arrived in India, 11th April, 1866, and was employed on civil and military duty; in 1868 was in medical charge at Calcutta of troops and coolies, en route to Abyssinia; served in Abyssinia with 12th Bengal cavalry; served in Bombay and Punjab, 1868 and 1869, and in the N.W. Provinces from 1870 on civil and jail duty; deputy sanitary commissioner, Nov., 1886.

JAQUET, ROBERT GLOVER, Accounts Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, India office, 11th May, 1877.

JARDINE, JOHN, Indian C.S.
