(Bombay).—Appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived, 30th Oct., 1864, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, and acting assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan; acting under secretary to government, political, judicial, and educational departments, and secretary to the council for making laws and regulations, June, 1869, to May, 1871; from May, 1871, to Oct., 1873, served in Kathiawar as political assistant, and was in charge of the agency in Sept., Oct., and Dec., 1871; also appointed judicial assistant in Kathiawar, June, 1873; acting registrar of the high court, Bombay, Oct., 1873; secretary to the Baroda commission for the trial of the Gaekwar, Feb.—May, 1875; acting judge and sessions judge at Ratnagiri, Tanna, and Khandeish, Oct., 1875, to Feb., 1877; secretary to the delegates of the government of India in the negotiations for a commercial treaty with Portugal, March to Sept., 1877, also acting as deputy secretary to government of India, legislative department, April, 1877; acting secretary to government of Bombay, political, judicial, educational, secret, and Persian departments, Nov., 1877, to Jan., 1878; from Feb., 1878, served in British Burma as judicial commissioner, and also presided in court of recorder of Rangoon, Oct.—Nov., 1881; president of the educational syndicate, Sept., 1881; president of the Poundeh reformatory committee, Nov., 1881; acting chief secretary to government of Bombay, Oct., 1885; judge of the high court, Bombay, Nov., 1885; author of 'Notes on Buddhist Law'; editor of Moung Tet Pyo's Customary Law of the Chin Tribe, also of a reprint of Dr. Tandy's translation of Father Sangermano's description of the Burman Empire, and a compilation of printed judgments of the judicial commissioner of British Burma; wrote a preface to Dr. Forchhammer's 'King Wagaru's Dhamma-Sattham.'

JARDINE, WILLIAM ELLIS, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Fettes College, Edinburgh, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in Madras, 20th Feb., 1889, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; acting second assistant to resident, Hyderabad, Nov., 1893.

JARRETT, HENRY SULLIVAN, C.I.E., Colonel, I.S.C., late Legislative Department, Government

of India.—First commission, Dec., 1856; during the Mutiny was present at the outbreak at Mooltan, 1858, and was engaged in the pursuit of the mutineers under Brigadier-General Farquharson (medal); also served in the Mahsud Waziri expedition, 1860 (received medal and clasp), and the 2nd Eusufzai expedition, 1863 (received clasp); secretary to board of examiners, Fort William, March, 1871; in addition, assistant secretary, legislative department, government of India, from Aug., 1875; reverted to military department, June, 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

JEFFERY, THADDEUS JAMES PASSMORE, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrigation works in Bombay and the Punjab; executive engineer, Feb., 1879.

JEFFREYS, WILLIAM, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, North-Western Provinces.—First commission as lieutenant, 8th June, 1854; arrived in India, 24th Jan., 1856; served in the irrigation branch, Punjab, from May, 1857, as assistant engineer, executive engineer, and officiating superintendent, Western Jumna canal; also officiated as assistant principal, Roorkee college; transferred to N.W. Provinces, Jan., 1862; temporarily transferred to P.W. secretariat, government of India, Nov., 1864, to April, 1865; transferred to Bengal, and specially deputed to investigate a project of irrigation and navigation for the river Gandak, Oct., 1867, to April, 1869; executive engineer, 1st grade, March, 1869; officiating assistant to chief engineer, and assistant secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, May, 1869; superintending engineer, 3rd class, April, 1872; 1st class, Jan., 1880; acting chief engineer and joint secretary, irrigation branch, March, 1887; retired, Nov., 1887.

JEHANGIR KAIKHASRO NAVROJI KABRAJI, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Appointed in India as probationer under native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1888; assistant collector and magistrate, March, 1888; confirmed as member of the civil service, 24th Feb., 1890.

JEHANGIR, PESTANJI, C.I.E., Khan Bahadur, late Bombay Uncovenanted Service.—Joined the service as assistant master, Elphinstone institution, Bombay, 10th Oct., 1853; served in the settlement department from April, 1857; 1st grade, deputy collector, Aug., 1869; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; talukdari settlement officer, May, 1889; retired, 1889.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Bristol Grammar School, and Wadham College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived, 15th Jan., 1880, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from June, 1881, served in Sind as acting deputy collector, and acting city magistrate, Karachi; 3rd assistant collector in Sind, June, 1885; manager of encumbered estates in Sind, and educational inspector, September, 1885; 2nd assistant collector, May, 1889; 1st assistant, Feb., 1892; assistant commissioner in Sind, March, 1892; acting collector of salt revenue, Jan., 1893.

JENKINS, ROBERT GEDOIN, Colonel, I.S.C., late Madras Police.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1856; arrived in India, 8th Feb., 1857; served in the Madras police from July, 1863, and was promoted to superintendent, 1872; on

special duty at Madras, July-Oct., 1874, and in the Godaveri district, June-Sept., 1879; acted as assistant inspector-general in 1874 and 1879, and as deputy inspector-general in 1885; 1st grade superintendent, Feb., 1886; reverted to military duty, 1890.

JENKINS, THOMAS MORRIS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commission.—Ensign, 2nd March, 1866; arrived at Madras, 16th Sept., 1866, and served with the 2-19th regiment; joined the Madras staff corps, June, 1869; served in the Burma commission as assistant commissioner from 28th April, 1874; acted as port officer and collector of customs, Bassein, in July, 1881; settlement officer, 1st grade, Dec., 1884, to June, 1885; appointed to special duty in Upper Burma, Nov., 1885; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1886.

JENKINSON, SIR EDWARD GEORGE, K.C.B., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Harrow and Haileybury College; appointed to the East India company's service in 1856, and served in the N. W. Provinces as joint magistrate and deputy collector of Benares, and afterwards of Farukhabad; served through the Indian mutiny, for which he received medal and the thanks of the Queen and the governor-general; served also as settlement officer, magistrate, and collector, commissioner of revenue in Jhansi, and commissioner in Oudh; retired, 1880; was private secretary to Earl Spencer when lord-lieutenant of Ireland, and assistant under secretary for police and crime, 1882-85; C.B. in 1883, and K.C.B. in 1888.

JENNINGS, ROBERT HENRY, Major, R.E., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 12th Sept., 1872; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1875, and was employed as assistant engineer in the Punjab and Baluchistan; served through Afghan war, 1879-80, and the operations against the Maris, 1880; from Oct., 1879, acted at various times as personal assistant to governor-general's agent in Baluchistan, assistant to ditto, political officer, Pishin, and in the same capacity in Thal-Chotiali; on boundary settlement duty in Central India, 1883-84; deputy assistant quartermaster-general, Simla, July, 1885; district staff officer, Meerut, Sept., 1888; deputy assistant adjutant-general, Meerut, April, 1890; executive engineer, military works department, June, 1890; at school of military engineering, Chatham, March-Nov., 1891; acting assistant political agent and consul, Bassora, Feb.-Aug., 1892; political officer, with Sardar Ayub Khan, Feb., 1893; author of an account of a journey through Western Baluchistan, Persia, &c., and of contributions to Persian and Baluchistan gazetteers.

JERVOISE, ALAN ARTHUR CLARKE, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, 1856; arrived, 23rd March, 1857, and served in various districts of Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, political agent and collector of stamp revenue, and acting deputy commissioner of customs and opium; junior collector and magistrate, Belgaum, Sept., 1877; senior collector and magistrate, March, 1879; retired, April, 1887.

JOBBINS, WILLIAM HENRY, Bengal Education Department.—Art master, South Kensington (gold medalist, national competition, 1876); appointed superintendent of government school of art and art gallery, Calcutta, 15th June, 1887.

JOHNSON, SIR ALLEN BAYARD, General, K.C.B., I.S.C., late Military Department, India Office.—Educated at Winchester; joined the service (infantry), July, 1846; appointed to 4th Sikhs as volunteer for service in Burma (medal and clasp for Pegu); in civil employ in Punjab, 1854; served in the Mutiny in 1858 (medal and clasp and brevet for Lucknow); assistant adjutant-general, 1861; military department, Calcutta, 1862; assistant secretary to government of India in the military department, 1875; deputy military secretary, 1876; secretary in the military department, India office, from 18th Jan., 1877; temporarily employed as military secretary to the government of India, 1879-81; C.B., 1881; retired from India office and was made K.C.B., May, 1889.

JOHNSON, The Most Rev. EDWARD RALPH, D.D. (Oxford and Durham), LL.B. (Cambridge).—Educated at Rugby, and Wadham College, Oxford; ordained, 1851; vicar of Northenden, Cheshire, 1866-76; archdeacon of Chester, 1871-76; appointed bishop of Calcutta and metropolitan of India, 14th Dec., 1876.

JOHNSON, EVANS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1867; served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments from Jan., 1868, in various districts as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant commissioner and acting commissioner (Nilgiris), acting sub-collector, and acting district and sessions judge; on special duty, Rumpu disturbances, 1879-80; sub-collector and joint magistrate, South Arcot, Jan., 1883; from Feb., 1883, acted as principal assistant in Vizagapatam, and as collector, magistrate, and agent in the same district, and afterwards in Kurnool; acting district and sessions judge, Vizagapatam, May, 1884, to April, 1885; collector and magistrate, May, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1886; collector and magistrate and agent to the governor, Ganjam, May, 1890; retired, March, 1893.

JOHNSON, JOHN, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; junior clerk, June, 1882.

JOHNSON, JOHN WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 23rd Dec., 1867, and posted to Bengal; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; transferred to Assam, April, 1892.

JOHNSON, THOMAS GEORGE, Captain, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—1st commission, Aug., 1877; employed in Burma as assistant commissioner, March, 1884; deputy commissioner, March, 1891.

JOHNSON, WARWICK HUSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 1st Sept., 1865, and posted to the Punjab; transferred temporarily to military works, April, 1872; and to Bengal on famine relief duty, Nov., 1873; executive engineer, March, 1874.

JOHNSTON, CHARLES, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at High School, Dublin, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 5th Jan., 1889, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; retired, April, 1892.

JOHNSTON, The Ven. CHARLES FRANCIS HARDING, M.A., late Ecclesiastical Department, Bombay.—Educated at Christ College, Cambridge; appointed junior chaplain, 13th Feb., 1869; senior chaplain, Feb., 1879; acted as archdeacon of Bombay in 1886, and held charge of the diocese in 1888; appointed archdeacon, Oct., 1888; retired, Oct., 1890; editor of 'St. Basil on the Holy Spirit.'

JOHNSTON, DAVID LANDALE, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Boston Grammar School, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served as assistant commissioner and assistant magistrate and collector.

JOHNSTON, JOHN LEECH, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at the Royal School, Rangoon, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1867, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting political superintendent of Akalkot, acting collector and magistrate, and assistant judge and sessions judge; 2nd grade judge and sessions judge, Kanara, Jan., 1884; 1st grade judge and sessions judge, Feb., 1886.

JOHNSTONE, DONALD CAMPBELL, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; served under the government of Bombay from May, 1882, as assistant collector and magistrate, Belgaum, to Feb., 1884, when he was re-transferred to Punjab; officiating junior secretary to financial commissioner, Punjab, Oct., 1887; confirmed, Nov., 1888; officiated as senior secretary at various periods; district judge, Dec., 1891.

JOHNSTONE, FREDERICK JOHN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Served in the public works department, Central Provinces, from July, 1861, and was appointed executive engineer, 1st grade, March, 1871; assistant to the chief engineer, Aug., 1873; officiating controller of public works accounts, April to Nov., 1875; transferred temporarily to Punjab, Oct., 1879; promoted to superintending engineer, Nov., 1880; posted to British Burma, Jan., 1881; transferred to Assam as superintending engineer, and secretary to chief commissioner in the public works department, Jan., 1884; 1st class superintending engineer, March, 1886; chief engineer, June, 1888; officiating chief engineer and joint secretary to the government of Bengal, Feb., 1891; confirmed, June, 1891; secretary to the government of Bengal, public works department, March, 1892; fellow of Calcutta university, Jan., 1893.

JOHNSTONE, HUGH, M.A., M.D., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Burma.—Joined the Indian medical service, 30th Sept., 1867; arrived in India, 2nd May, 1868, and served in Calcutta at the general hospital and as house surgeon at the medical college hospital; in medical charge of the British residency, Mandalay, Upper Burma, July, 1869; civil surgeon, Akyab, March, 1877; from April, 1878, officiated as civil surgeon, Rangoon, and was appointed junior civil surgeon, Rangoon, April, 1880, and civil surgeon, May, 1890;

also port health officer and in charge of the lock hospital and meteorological observatory.

JOHNSTONE, SIR JAMES, Colonel, K.C.S.I., late Assam Commission.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, 1858; assistant superintendent of police, 1864; in charge of Keddahs in the Gurjat Mehals of Cuttack, 1868-74; assistant commissioner in Assam, 1874; political agent, Manipur, 1877; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, March, 1884; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; retired from Assam commission, 1888.

JOHNSTONE, JAMES WILLIAM HOPE, Colonel, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 4th Feb., 1853; from March, 1858, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, deputy commissioner, and officiating settlement officer; officiated as commissioner and superintendent, Peshawur, in 1880 and 1883, also in the Jullundur division, Sept., 1880; promoted to deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Rawal Pindi, Feb., 1881; civil and sessions judge, Peshawur, Sept., 1881; 1st grade divisional judge, Nov., 1884; retired, Feb., 1886.

JOLL, HENRY, late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served as assistant engineer and executive engineer; superintendent engineer, Nov., 1890; retired, March, 1892.

JOLLIFFE, EDWIN WILLIAM, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 2nd April, 1885.

JONES, HOWELL HEWSON, Store Department, India Office.—2nd class clerk, India store depot, 14th July, 1883.

JONES, JOHN ALFRED, M.I.C.E., Madras Sanitary Department.—Joined the service as executive engineer, public works department, Madras municipal commission, Jan., 1877; acted as president of the municipal commission in 1886 and 1888; appointed sanitary engineer to government of Madras, April, 1890; author of 'Switches and Crossings,' a manual for railway and tramway engineers, and of a manual for district and municipal boards.

JONES, OSWALD ROUTH, Judicial Department, Madras.—Joined the service, 3rd Sept., 1870, under the military department; served subsequently in Natal, Mauritius, and in England; in April, 1879, was appointed to the Madras presidency as assistant superintendent of police; assistant inspector-general of police, Aug., 1886; superintendent, Aug., 1889; chief presidency magistrate, March, 1891.

JONES, THOMAS, B.L., late Bengal Judicial Department.—Assistant secretary, government of Bengal, 7th April, 1866; clerk and officiating 5th judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1873; registrar and chief ministerial officer of the court, June, 1882; 4th judge, Oct., 1884; and 3rd judge, Oct., 1889; retired, 1892.

JONES, WILLIAM BRITTAIN, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1855; arrived in India, 15th Sept., 1856; from March, 1857, to April, 1865, served in the N.W. Provinces and Punjab as assistant magistrate and assistant commissioner; from April, 1865, served in the Central Provinces as deputy commissioner, acting superintendent of cotton affairs, secretary to the chief commissioner, and acting commissioner in the Jubbulpore, Nerbudda, and Nagpore divisions; on special

duty as commissioner for drawing up land revenue and tenancy bills for Central Provinces, Sept., 1874; on special duty in the home department, Calcutta, Nov., 1876, also in April, 1877, and at Simla, May, 1877; commissioner, Hyderabad assigned districts, April, 1877; resident, Hyderabad, June, 1882; chief commissioner, Central Provinces, 1st May, 1883; retired, Jan., 1885.

JONES, WILLIAM RICHARD SUMPTION, late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Deputy superintendent, Roorkee workshops, Nov., 1867; joined the public works department, Feb., 1873, as executive engineer, North-Western Provinces; transferred to the locomotive department, Rajputana state railways, April, 1879, as deputy locomotive superintendent; carriage and wagon superintendent, Ajmere, Jan., 1880; retired, Dec., 1893.

JONES - BATEMAN, HERBERT BURLETON, Indian C.S. (North - Western Provinces).—Educated at Marlborough; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 16th Oct., 1876, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; officiating city magistrate, Lucknow, Aug.-Nov., 1883; joint magistrate, Aug., 1886; small cause court judge, March, 1892; district and sessions judge, April, 1892.

JOPP, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Winchester, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived, 10th Oct., 1873; second assistant collector and magistrate, March, 1886; and first assistant, Nov., 1889; acting registrar, high court, Bombay, May, 1890; joint judge and sessions judge, Ahmedabad, April, 1892; confirmed as registrar, high court, Bombay, May, 1893; acting special judge under Deccan agriculturists relief act, Nov., 1893.

JOPP, JOHN, C.B., Brigadier-General, Political Department, Bombay.—1st commission, 20th Oct., 1856; served at capture of fort of Sheikh Othman, near Aden, in 1858, in the Abyssinian war of 1868 (medal), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80, as assistant quartermaster-general, and took part in march to Candahar (medal); deputy quartermaster-general, Bombay, May, 1888, to May, 1890; brigadier-general, May, 1890; political resident and collector of stamp revenue, in charge of vice-admiralty court, and commandant Aden brigade, May, 1890; C.B., May, 1894.

JOSCELYNE, DOUGLAS, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 11th Aug., 1870, and posted to Rajputana; employed in Madras on famine relief, 1878-79; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; transferred to Burma as assistant secretary in the public works secretariat, July, 1888; under secretary, Nov., 1889; superintendent of works, 4th circle, March, 1890; on special duty to prepare irrigation projects, Sept., 1890; and in 1891 was deputed to Upper India in connection with the same work; in charge, 1st circle, June, 1892, and from April, 1893.

JOSEPH, GEORGE JOHN, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engineering College; appointed, 23rd May, 1872, and posted to the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, May, 1883.

JOSEPH, HUGH GORE, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Charterhouse, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting cantonment magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; transferred for employment as deputy-commissioner in Burma, Aug., 1886; re-transferred to Madras, Oct., 1887, as head assistant to collector and magistrate; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1894.

JOUBERT, CHARLES HENRY, M.B., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was in military employ until 1874; served as civil surgeon, resident surgeon, Presidency hospital, officiating superintendent of emigration, officiating health officer for Calcutta, officiating sanitary commissioner for Bengal, and officiating professor, medical college; confirmed in the latter appointment, June, 1890; fellow of Calcutta university, Jan., 1893.

JOWERS, HAROLD, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Shrewsbury, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

JOYCE, ALFRED, M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as assistant engineer, and was employed in the N.W. Provinces; transferred to Madras, April, 1883; executive engineer, Jan., 1886.

JOYNER, ROBERT BATSON, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Jan., 1879; acted as sanitary engineer in 1892; superintending engineer, May, 1894.

JWALA PRASAD, Kunwar, B.A., Statutory C.S. (N. W. Provinces and Oudh).—Appointed a probationer under the native civil service rules, Dec., 1884; confirmed, as assistant magistrate and collector, Feb., 1887; assistant commissioner, Nov., 1891; has translated Sir Alfred Lyall's 'Asiatic Studies' into Hindustani.

JWALA PRASAD, Pandit, M.A., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Appointed a probationer under native civil service rules, July, 1886; confirmed in the civil service, 12th March, 1889, as assistant commissioner; joint magistrate, Nov., 1893.

KALI PRASANNA MUKERJI, B.A., B.L., Bengal Judicial Service.—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1866, as munsiiff; subordinate judge, Feb., 1879; 1st grade, Nov., 1893.

KAVASJEE JAMASJEE BADSHAH, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Elphinstone High School, Bombay University, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 12th Nov., 1879; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; officiating embarkation agent and superintendent of emigration, Oct., 1882; postmaster-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Feb., 1887.

KAYE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N. W. Provinces).—Appointed to Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1858; arrived in India, 8th April, 1858; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating small cause court judge, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Bareilly, April, 1877, officiating civil and sessions judge, Gorakhpur, March, 1880, and commissioner of the Jhansi division, Dec., 1881; commissioner of the Agra division, July, 1885, and of the Rohilkhand division, Nov., 1889; acting member of board of revenue, Jan.-Nov., 1890; on special duty in connection with the police re-organisation commission, Nov., 1890, to April, 1891; commissioner of the Rohilkhand division, April, 1891; junior member of board of revenue, Nov., 1891; retired, April, 1893.

KAY-SHUTTLEWORTH, RIGHT HON. SIR UGHTRED JAMES, Bart., M.P.—Educated at Harrow, and at the London University; sat in the House of Commons as member for Hastings from Oct., 1869, till 1880; served for two years on the London school board, and was also a member of the royal commission on reformatory and industrial schools; in 1885 was returned for the Clitheroe division of N.E. Lancashire, and was appointed under secretary of state for India in Feb., 1886, and shortly afterwards chancellor of the duchy of Lancaster; in the election of 1886, and again in 1892, was returned for N.E. Lancashire; parliamentary secretary, admiralty, Aug., 1892; was for some time vice-president of University College, London.

KEATINGE, RICHARD HARTE, Lieut.-General, V.C., C.S.I., R.A., Indian Staff Corps.—First commission, Bombay artillery, June, 1842; from 1847 was employed in civil duties on the N.W. frontier, and was appointed assistant superintendent, Nimar, Dec., 1851; in the Mutiny of 1857 served in Central India, and was present at the sieges of Dhar and Chandaree, and in the fighting near Mundesore (dangerously wounded, brevet of major, medal and clasp and V.C.); also took part in pursuit of Tantia Topee, 1858-59; political agent, Nimar, 1860; political agent, Gwalior, and subsequently in Kattywar, 1862; served in Kattywar against the rebel Wagheers in 1865; agent to the governor-general in Rajputana, 1868-74; also ex-officio chief commissioner of Ajmere, 1871-74; chief commissioner of Assam, Feb., 1874, to June, 1878.

KEDAR NATH ROY, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—From 4th April, 1878, served as munisif, and on 27th July, 1888, was appointed member of the statutory civil service as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1893; officiating district and sessions judge, March, 1894; author of three legal works.

KEDAR NATH MOZUMDAR, B.A., B.L., Bengal Judicial Department.—Served in the subordinate judicial department from 15th December, 1865, and was appointed subordinate judge, 1st grade, July, 1890.

KEEFER, WILLIAM NAPIER, Surgeon-Major, late Indian Medical Department.—Assistant-surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 1st Oct., 1869; served under the military department up to 1884, when he was appointed senior medical officer, Port Blair and the Nicobars; served in the Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp),

the Duffla expedition, 1874-75, the Jowaki campaign, 1877-78 (clasp), the Afghan campaign, 1878-79-80 (medal and clasp), the Zaimusht expedition, 1879, and the Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal and clasp, Khedive's bronze star, and 4th class Order of the Osmanieh); retired, Dec., 1889.

KEEGAN, DENNIS FRANCIS, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., M.D., late Indian Medical Service.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; joined the Bengal medical service in 1866; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1866; from Nov., 1868, served with the Central India Horse, and acted as residency surgeon, Indore, 1877-78; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and clasp, bronze star); residency surgeon, Indore, and civil administrative medical officer, Central India, June, 1881; retired, April, 1894; author of several articles and papers on medical subjects.

KEELAN, BERNARD CALLASE, Surgeon-Major, L.K. & Q.C.P., M.R.C.S., L.M.K. & Q.C.P. (Ir.), late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Bombay medical service, 1st April, 1867; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1867, and served under the military department, and also as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail at various stations; served in the Abyssinian campaign, and was present at the storming of Magdala (medal); civil surgeon, Sukkur, Jan., 1881; civil surgeon, superintendent of jail, lunatic asylum, and medical school, Hyderabad, April, 1884; retired, March, 1891.

KEELAN, EUGENE JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 3rd Oct., 1868, and employed on irrigation works in Oudh; transferred to state railways, Nov., 1879; executive engineer, Oct., 1880.

KEENE, CHARLES JAMES, Railway Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 29th Dec., 1874, as assistant traffic superintendent, Punjab northern state railway; served also on the Northern Bengal state railway, the North-Western and other railways; district traffic superintendent, 1890.

KEENE, HENRY G., C.I.E., M.A. (Hon.), late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Rugby, and Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1847; arrived in India, 3rd Oct., 1847; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant superintendent of Dehra Dun, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; superintendent, Dehra Dun, May, 1856; magistrate and collector, April, 1860; civil and sessions judge April, 1867; officiated as commissioner of the Agra division in 1872 and 1875, and of the Allahabad division in 1874; retired, 1883; fellow of the Calcutta University; member of committee of the Muhamadan College of Aligurh; medal from the zoological society; medal and diploma from the Austrian Exhibition of 1863; author of 'Indian Administration,' 1867; 'Fall of the Mughal Empire,' 1876; 'History of Hindustan,' 1885; 'Life of Madhava Rao Sindhia,' 1892; 'History of India for the use of Students,' 1893; and numerous contributions to the periodical press.

KEENE, HENRY GEORGE HARRIS, Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the department, and was attached to the accountant-general's office, Rangoon, 14th March, 1874;

assistant accountant-general, Allahabad, Feb., 1878; assistant comptroller-general, March, 1878; assistant accountant-general, Bombay, Nov., 1880; assistant comptroller, Burma, Dec., 1882; comptroller, Hyderabad, March, 1886; assistant accountant-general, Calcutta, July, 1886; assistant comptroller-general (in charge of paper currency), March, 1888; officiating comptroller, Hyderabad, Oct., 1893.

KEESS, JAMES, Deputy Surgeon-General, M.D., M.R.C.P., and L.M. (London), and M.R.C.S., late Madras Medical Department.—Served in the subordinate medical department as warrant officer from 11th Sept., 1848, to May, 1855; assistant surgeon, 4th Aug., 1856; arrived 11th Sept., 1856, and served under the military department up to May, 1862, and subsequently as civil surgeon and superintendent of district jail, Kurnool, and was placed on special duty experimenting on alkaloids in Gudalur and Wynnaad, June, 1866; fort surgeon, Madras, and professor of anatomy and physiology in the medical college, April, 1867; medical inspector of seamen, Aug., 1868; acting principal medical storekeeper and inspector of emigrants, May, 1869; acting health officer and superintendent, lock hospital, Madras, April, 1880; fellow of Madras university, May, 1882; principal and professor of medicine in the medical college, and senior physician, general hospital, Aug., 1883; retired, Jan., 1887; author of 'Syllabus of Medicine for use of Medical Students,' 'Notes on Zoology and Comparative Anatomy,' 'History of the Madras Medical College,' a pamphlet on the 'Prevention of Cholera,' and a 'Calendar of the Medical College.'

KEITH, STANLEY, Financial Department, India Office.—Educated at Lancing College; writer, India office, 6th June, 1873; clerk, 2nd class, April, 1879.

KEITH, THOMAS W., late Accountant-General, India Office.—Educated at Highgate School; East India house writer, old establishment, 20th Aug., 1845; junior clerk, treasury, 1848; senior clerk, Sept., 1858; accountant-general's department, 1860; accountant, Jan., 1872; assessor of income tax, 1872; accountant-general, Feb., 1879; retired, June, 1893.

KELLAKKA COVILAGOM MANAVEDAN RAJA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Madras University; appointed by government of India probationer for the civil service, 4th June, 1880; confirmed, Aug., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1882; head assistant, Aug., 1888; acting district and sessions judge, May-Sept., 1893, and from June, 1894.

KELLEHER, JAMES, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at the Catholic University of Ireland; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 29th Sept., 1869; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; transferred to Assam as district and sessions judge of Sylhet, March, 1884; services replaced at disposal of government of Bengal, Sept., 1887; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1887; retired, Aug., 1894; author of 'Possession in the Civil Law,' and 'Principles of Specific Performance and Mistake.'

KELLEY, WILLIAM, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

KELLNER, EDWIN WELSH, C.I.E., late Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as assistant comptroller-general, 29th April, 1870; served as deputy accountant-general, and, for a short period, as accountant-general, Bengal, 1874-76; deputy comptroller-general, Jan., 1877; assistant commissioner of paper currency, Calcutta, Dec., 1877; deputy comptroller-general in 1st class of financial department, Aug., 1878; acted as accountant-general, Bengal, in 1883, 1886, and 1887; officiating comptroller and auditor-general, Aug., 1887; officiated as accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, in 1887; and Madras, 1888; class i., finance department, enrolled list, July, 1889; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, in 1889; accountant-general, Punjab, March, 1890; retired, March, 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894.

KELLY, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Oriel College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, June, 1868; also served as assistant-superintendent of survey, 1863-64, and officiating inspector of schools, 1869; appointed small cause court judge, Bhagulpore, Jan., 1876, and served subsequently in the judicial department; district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, Jan., 1879; retired, Jan., 1888.

KELSALL, JOHN, late Madras C.S.—Educated at University College School, and University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; from March, 1865, to Jan., 1867, served as probationary assistant-superintendent of police, and subsequently in the revenue and judicial departments in various districts of Madras, as assistant collector and magistrate, principal assistant, and acting collector and magistrate; compiled the 'Bellary District Manual'; appointed district and sessions judge, Aug., 1880, in the Godaveri district, and afterwards in South Malabar and Vizagapatam; retired, Nov., 1889.

KEMBALL, SIR ARNOLD BURROWES, General, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., late Political Department, Government of India.—Educated at Addiscombe; 2nd lieutenant, Bombay artillery, Dec., 1837; served in the Afghan war, 1838-39, and was present at the siege and capture of Ghuznee (medal); appointed assistant to the resident at Bushire, Feb., 1842; political resident, Persian Gulf, May, 1852; consul general, Bagdad, and political agent, Turkish Arabia, April, 1855, to 1873; served in the Persian expedition of 1857, and was present at the capture of Mohumrah (medal and clasp, C.B., and brevet of major); served with the Turco-Persian boundary commission, 1873; during the Russo-Turkish war, 1876-77, was British military attaché at the headquarters of the Turkish army.

KEMBALL, CHARLES GURDON, Barrister-at-Law, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College in 1850; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1850; served in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, judicial assistant commissioner (in Sind), and deputy magistrate; collector and magistrate, Karachi, Nov., 1861; served as judge and sessions judge,

Surat, from Jan., 1866, to June, 1870, and from the latter date acted as judge of the high court, Bombay, in which appointment he was confirmed in Aug., 1871; acted as member of council, Bombay, in 1880, and again in 1881; retired in 1885.

KEMBLE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Oct., 1873; inspector-general of jails, July, 1876; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Jan., 1878; officiating opium agent, Benares, May, 1882; opium agent, Behar, May, 1884; officiating commissioner, Patna, May–Nov., 1890; acting member of board of revenue, June, 1892; retired, April, 1893.

KENDALL, EDWARD AUSTIN, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at the Bedford Grammar School, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1892, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

KENNEDY, CHESSBOROUGH GORDON MACKENZIE, Assam Commission.—Educated at Southampton College; sub-lieutenant, H.M.'s 59th regiment, 12th Nov., 1873; resigned his commission, Jan., 1877; in 1879–80 served as transport officer, Naga Hills field force; appointed to the Assam commission as extra assistant commissioner, 25th June, 1880; assistant commissioner, May, 1886; deputy commissioner, April, 1894.

KENNEDY, JAMES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at the High School and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1863; from July, 1864, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, settlement officer, officiating cantonment magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, June, 1879; magistrate and collector, Gorakhpur, March, 1884; retired, April, 1890.

KENNEDY, JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at the High School and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 23rd Oct., 1874; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; on special duty, Madras, in 1878; transferred to Assam, May, 1884, as assistant commissioner, 1st class; deputy commissioner, July, 1886; retransferred to Bengal as magistrate and collector, Nov., 1892.

KENNEDY, SIR MICHAEL KAVANAGH, General, K.C.S.I., R.E., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—2nd lieutenant, Bombay engineers, 11th June, 1841; arrived in India, Nov., 1842; assistant engineer, roads and tanks, June, 1843; executive engineer at Surat, Aden, and Satara, 1848–60; on special duty, Ceylon, Madras, and Calcutta, Oct., 1860, to April, 1861; superintending engineer, Southern division, April, 1862; secretary to government of Bombay, public works and railway departments, July, 1863; resigned the public works department in 1880; controller-general of supply, Kabul expedition; additional secretary to government of India for supply and transport, 1880.

KENNEDY, ROBERT GREIG, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, Oct., 1881; transferred to Baluchistan, Oct., 1884; returned to the Punjab, Aug., 1890.

KENNEDY, ROBERT MUIRHEAD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Rugar; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1877; and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting junior collector and magistrate, and railway magistrate, Bandra; 2nd assistant collector, April, 1888; 1st assistant, May, 1891; in 1891 acted as political agent, Mahi Kantha, and as administrator, Rajpipla state; talukdari settlement officer, Nov., 1891; on deputation to adjudicate on Cutch–Morvi, &c., disputes, June, 1894.

KENNEDY, THOMAS JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Liverpool Institute, Balliol College, Oxford, and London University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1880, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and settlement officer; transferred to Central Provinces, Oct., 1885; settlement officer, Raipur, Oct., 1885; retransferred to the Punjab, and appointed assistant commissioner, Lahore, Nov., 1887; district judge, April, 1892; officiating deputy commissioner, Nov., 1892.

KENNEDY, WILLOUGHBY PITCAIRN, Major, I.S.C., Political Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, 8th Feb., 1870, and served under the military department; assistant to political agent, Mahi Kantha, March, 1875; served subsequently in the same capacity at Aden, Zanzibar, Baluchistan, and Kathiawar; assistant to the agent to the governor-general, Central India, Aug.–Oct., 1880; acted as administrator of the Porbandar State in 1888 and 1889; and as political agent, Kolhapur, in 1890; special political agent, Cambay, Oct., 1890, to April, 1894; appointed second assistant to political agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1893.

KENNEY, HERBERT, ARTHUR ROBERT, Colonel, Madras Cavalry.—Cornet, 20th Sept., 1859; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1859; served on regimental duty with various cavalry regiments; adjutant, governor's body guard, Sept., 1865; deputy assistant quarter-master general, 1873–76; major, Sept., 1879; military secretary to the governor of Madras, May, 1881, to April, 1884; officiating assistant secretary to government of India, military department, Dec., 1881; from April, 1884, acted as secretary to the government of Madras, military department, and was confirmed in that appointment, Dec., 1885; retired, April, 1892.

KENSINGTON, ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S.—Educated at Marlborough, and University College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 12th Feb., 1878, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and assistant settlement officer; on special duty in civil secretariat, Oct., 1882; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab, Nov., 1882; settlement officer, April, 1884; on special duty in Umballa settlement, Dec.,

1889; appointed under-secretary to government of India, finance and commerce department, April, 1890; has officiated as deputy secretary.

KER, THOMAS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 16th May, 1874, and employed on state railways in Rajputana; executive engineer, May, 1883.

KERNAN, JAMES, M.A., Q.C., late Judicial Department, Madras.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; judge of the high court, Madras, 3rd May, 1870; fellow of the Madras university, Feb., 1871; vice-chancellor of the Madras university, June, 1885, and 1887; officiating chief justice, July to Nov., 1885, and in July, 1888; retired, July, 1889.

KERR, JOHN HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Glasgow Academy, Glasgow University, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

KERR, THOMAS COULTER, Captain, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Liverpool College; served during China war, 1860-61, and Abyssinian war, 1868-69, in command of hired troopships; appointed surveyor of shipping, India office, 1st April, 1881.

KERSHAW, LOUIS JAMES, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Belfast Methodist College and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 31st Oct., 1890, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, April, 1892.

KETTLEWELL, ARTHUR BRADLEY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 31st October, 1892, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

KEYS, ARTHUR INNES, Indo-European Telegraph Department, India Office.—Appointed clerk, London office, Indo-European telegraph department, 7th Feb., 1880; transferred to registry and record department, India office, 1st Jan., 1889; clerk specially attached, Aug., 1891; rejoined Indo-European telegraph department, June, 1894.

KEYSER, ALFRED, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Guildford Grammar School, and Royal Academy, Gosport; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1865, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; junior collector and magistrate and political agent, Surat, Dec., 1884; senior collector, &c., April, 1887; acting commissioner, Southern division, March, 1890; retired, March, 1892.

KHANDRAO CHIMANRAO BEDARKAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., LL.B., Judicial Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, 25th April, 1863, as Gujarati and Marathi translator and interpreter, high court; deputy registrar and sealer, Feb., 1867; services lent to H.H. the Maharaja Holkar, Oct., 1890; 4th judge, Bombay small cause court, Dec., 1890, continuing to be in the foreign service.

KHANDUBHAI GULABHAI DESAI, L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 11th April, 1870, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, April, 1884; is a fellow of the Bombay university.

KHARSEDJI MANEKJI KHARSEDJI, Khan Bahadur, B.A., Barrister - at - Law, Judicial Department, Bombay.—Served from 29th May, 1874, as judge of the court of small causes, Ahmednagar, and subsequently at Ahmedabad; appointed 4th judge, Bombay small cause court, June, 1888, and 3rd judge, May, 1889.

KHARSEDJI RATANJI BAMANJI, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Probationer under the native civil service rules, and assistant-collector and magistrate, Nov., 1882; confirmed as member of the Bombay civil service, 8th Jan., 1885; 2nd assistant, April, 1892.

KHUSHWAKT RAE, RAE, B.L., N.W. Provinces Administration.—Appointed by the Government of India probationary assistant magistrate and collector, under the native civil service rules, 8th April, 1890.

KIERNANDER, CHARLES ROBERT CAMPBELL, late Financial Department, Government of India.—Assistant accountant-general, British Burma, 5th Aug., 1864; officiating deputy accountant-general, Central Provinces, April, 1870; assistant accountant-general, N.W. Provinces, Sept., 1870; from Feb., 1874, to Jan., 1876, served as deputy accountant-general in the Punjab, and subsequently in Bombay, N.W. Provinces, British Burma, and Bengal; officiated as accountant-general, Bengal, in 1878 and 1879; inspector of local offices of account, Oct., 1878; deputy comptroller-general in 1st class of financial department, Feb., 1883; assistant secretary, government of India, financial department, Nov., 1884, to Jan., 1885; comptroller of Indian treasuries, April, 1885; deputy auditor-general, Oct., 1886; officiating deputy comptroller-general, Aug., 1887; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, Sept., 1890, and again from May, 1891; deputy comptroller-general, April, 1891; retired, March, 1892.

KILBY, GEORGE CHARLES, B.L., Bengal Judicial Service.—Deputy superintendent and remembrancer of legal affairs, 28th Feb., 1876.

KILBY, SANFORD JAMES, late Bengal Preventive Service.—Assistant collector of customs, Chittagong, 30th June, 1865; deputy magistrate and deputy collector, July, 1866; from Aug., 1868, to Dec., 1876, served in the police department, and was appointed assistant superintendent, 1st grade, May, 1874; on famine relief duty, Dec., 1873, and March, 1876; on special duty in Chumparum, April, 1874; from Dec., 1876, served in the customs preventive service, and was appointed superintendent, Jan., 1877; also in charge of the Sulka salt golahs, April, 1880; served in Madras as deputy commissioner, abkari and salt department, Sept.-Nov., 1885, when he returned to his Calcutta appointment; retired, Nov., 1892.

KILGOUR, FREDERICK, Colonel, I.S.C., Police Department, Madras.—First commission, 11th July, 1857; probationary assistant superintendent of police, Nov., 1865; assistant superintendent, Aug., 1873; superintendent, June, 1874; deputy inspector-general, May, 1892.

KIMBERLEY (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. JOHN WODEHOUSE, K.G., P.C., D.C.L.—Born, 7th Jan., 1826; educated at Eton, and Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1847, with 1st class in classics; succeeded to title of Baron Wodehouse, May, 1846; under secretary of state for foreign affairs, Dec., 1852; to 1856; ambassador at St. Petersburg, 1856-58;

under secretary for foreign affairs, June, 1859, to Aug., 1861; on a special mission to the North of Europe, in connection with the Schleswig-Holstein question, 1863; under secretary of state for India, April-Nov., 1864; lord lieutenant of Ireland, Nov., 1864, to July, 1866; raised to earldom of Kimberley, May, 1866; elected governor of the Hudson's Bay Company, April, 1868; lord privy seal, Dec., 1868, to July, 1870; secretary of state for the colonies from July, 1870, to Feb., 1874; member of the royal commission to enquire into the working of the Penal Servitude Acts, Feb., 1878; secretary of state for the colonies, May, 1880; held provisionally the office of chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, on Mr. Bright's resignation, June, 1882; secretary of state for India, Dec., 1882, to June, 1883, and Feb. to Aug., 1886; again secretary of state for India, and also lord president of the council, Aug., 1892; secretary of state for foreign affairs, March, 1894; K.G., 1885; hon. D.C.L. (Oxford), June, 1894; was a royal commissioner for the exhibition of 1886; has been a member of the senate of the University of London since 1859, and was for some time president of University College, London (resigned, 1887).

KINCAID, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Sherborne School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1839; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1891, and served in Sind as assistant collector and magistrate.

KINCAID, WILLIAM, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—Ensign, Madras Army, March, 1849; appointed to adjust boundaries disputes, Bhopal agency, March, 1864; assistant political agent, Bundelcund, small cause court judge and cantonment magistrate, Nowgong, 1866; second in command, Malwa Bheel corps, Nov., 1870; political agent, Bhopal, Aug., 1876; political agent, Bhopawar, and commandant, Malwa Bheel corps, Oct., 1879; political agent, Bhopal, June, 1881; returned to Europe, 1887.

KINDERSLEY, JOHN ROBERT, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury in 1847; served in the grades of assistant magistrate and collector till 1857, when he was appointed assistant judge of Mangalore; subordinate judge, Bellary, 1859; civil and sessions judge, Rajahmundry, March, 1865, and subsequently at Bellary; officiating judicial commissioner, Mysore, Sept., 1866, and again 1868; judge of the high court of judicature, Madras, June, 1877; retired in 1884.

KING, CHARLES MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 10th Dec., 1892, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

KING, GEORGE, C.I.E., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1865; house surgeon, medical college hospital, from 9th May to 25th Aug., 1866, when his services were placed at disposal of government of India, military department; superintendent of royal botanical gardens, professor of botany in Calcutta medical college, and superintendent of cinchona plantation in British Sikhlim, from July, 1871; also quinologist, Dec., 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; in addition director of the botanical

survey of India, May, 1891; author of 'Manual of Cinchona Cultivation in India,' and other botanical works; and editor of 'Annals of the Royal Botanical Gardens, Calcutta.'

KING, JOSHUA, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived, 26th Oct., 1860, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting assistant judge and session judge; in charge of the small cause court, Ahmedabad, April-May, 1864; acting under secretary to government, revenue, financial, and general departments, May, 1866; acting collector and superintendent of stamps and stationery, Bombay, July, 1869, to Jan., 1870; talukdari settlement officer, Dec., 1874, to Feb., 1876, and again, Feb., 1876, to Nov., 1877, for carrying Bombay Act VI of 1862 into effect, and manager under Act XV of 1871; on special duty, Baroda, Nov., 1877, to June, 1879; junior collector and magistrate, Ahmednagar, May, 1879; senior collector and magistrate and political agent, Satara, May, 1884; retired, 1886.

KING, LUCAS BLACKER BURNET, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived, 11th Oct., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served in the Bengal revenue, survey, and judicial departments, and was appointed superintendent of survey, Aug., 1866, joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Jan., 1877, and district and sessions judge, 1st grade, July, 1880; retired, Aug., 1885.

KING, LUCAS WHITE, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Ennis College, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; officiating junior secretary to financial commissioner, Punjab, Sept., 1882; revenue officer, Swat river canal, Feb.-May, 1885; transferred to foreign department as officiating political agent, and assistant commissioner, Ajmere, June, 1885; political agent, 3rd class, sub. pro tem., Oct., 1885; boundary settlement officer, Meywar, Tonk border, Feb., 1887; officiating political agent, and assistant commissioner, Ajmere, March, 1887; assistant to resident, Mysore, and secretary to chief commissioner, Coorg, July, 1887; on special duty with Maharaja of Mysore, Dec., 1887; first assistant to agent to the governor-general for Central India, Feb.-July, 1888; re-transferred to Punjab as assistant commissioner, Sept., 1890; also political officer, Shirani field force, Oct., 1890; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1893.

KING, MAURICE, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engineering College; appointed assistant engineer, 20th Jan., 1868, and employed on irrigation works in N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, April, 1876; transferred to Burma as superintending engineer, May, 1892.

KING, WALTER GAWEN, M.B., C.M., D.P.H., Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., Madras Medical Department.—Arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1874, and served under the military department in Madras and Burma; acted as zillah surgeon,

superintendent of district jail, surgeon to H.E. the governor, and professor at the presidency college; civil surgeon, June, 1877; also superintendent of jail, March, 1878; district surgeon, April, 1890; twice officiated as inspector of vaccination, and deputy sanitary commissioner; reverted to military department, Jan., 1892; acting superintendent, Madras lunatic asylum, April, 1893; officiating sanitary commissioner, August, 1893.

KING, WILLIAM, B.A., D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India.—Educated at Queen's College, Galway; appointed assistant in the geological survey, and joined the service, 4th March, 1857; served in various districts of the Madras presidency, and was appointed deputy superintendent for Madras, Nov., 1868; served subsequently in the Godaveri district and the Nizam's territories, and discovered the Singareni coal-field, 1871-72; deputed to Vienna in joint charge of the survey collections at the exhibition, 1873; 1st grade officer, April, 1875; fellow of the Madras university and member of the faculty of engineering, 1884; officiated as superintendent of the geological survey, May-Nov., 1884; designation changed to superintendent of survey; appointed director, April, 1887; retired, July, 1894; author of contributions to the survey publications.

KING, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 21st Dec., 1858, and posted to Bengal; transferred to military works, April, 1872; executive engineer, Sept., 1879; re-transferred to Bengal, 1879; employed in Baluchistan, Sept., 1885, and acted as joint secretary to governor-general's agent; returned to Bengal, May, 1888.

KING - HARMAN, MONTAGUE JOCELYN, Colonel, I.S.C., Military Secretariat, Government of India.—First commission, 2nd Oct., 1860; served in Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal), and Lushai expedition, 1871-72; officiating deputy assistant quartermaster-general, Bengal, Jan., 1875; confirmed, 1877; second deputy secretary, military department, government of India, Aug., 1892.

KINGSMILL, JAMES, late Central Press, Bombay.—General superintendent of the government central press, and editor of the 'Bombay Government Gazette,' 16th March, 1872, to 1891.

KINSMAN, FREDERICK, B.Sc., M.S.A., India Telegraph Department.—Appointed assistant superintendent, 24th Feb., 1870; promoted to superintendent, Sept., 1886; personal assistant to director general, Feb.-May, 1883; in charge, Upper Burma division, May, 1893.

KIPLING, JOHN LOCKWOOD, C.I.E., late Education Department, Punjab.—Joined the government service as architectural sculptor, Bombay, May, 1865; from April, 1875, served in the Punjab as principal of Mayo school of industrial art, and curator, Lahore museum; retired, May, 1893.

KIRK, HENRY ALEXANDER, Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as assistant superintendent, 24th Feb., 1870; superintendent, March, 1876; served during the Afghan war, 1879-80; in charge, telegraph workshops, Calcutta, Oct., 1885.

KIRKHAM, THOMAS BROWN, B.L., Educational Department, Bombay.—Joined the edu-

cational department, 1st Jan., 1862; arrived in India, 21st June, 1862, and served as head-master of the Poona high school, principal of the Elphinstone high school, Bombay, and professor of English literature at the Elphinstone college; was also a member of the first municipal corporation of Bombay, Aug., 1873; from April, 1878, served as educational inspector, and was promoted to 1st grade of the department, April, 1894; on special duty in England in connection with technical education, Nov., 1886.

KIRKWOOD, TOWNSEND MOLLOY, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Bromsgrove Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863; served in the revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, April, 1876, and district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, March, 1880; served temporarily under the public works department, Oct., 1869, to Oct., 1871, and was employed on famine duty, Bhagulpore, March, 1874; district and sessions judge, 1st grade, April, 1886; retired, 1888.

KISCH, HERMANN MICHAEL, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S.—Educated at University College, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1873; assistant magistrate and collector, Bengal, Nov., 1873; on famine duty, Patna, March, 1874, and at Bellary, Feb., 1877; assistant to director-general of statistics, Feb., 1875; officiating under secretary, government of Bengal, revenue and general departments, May, 1878; under secretary, judicial and political departments, Jan., 1880; officiating secretary in the financial department, June, 1881; officiating under secretary, government of India, revenue and agricultural department, Dec., 1883; postmaster-general, Bengal, Nov., 1885; officiating deputy secretary to government of India, finance and commerce department, Jan., 1889; officiating director-general of post office of India, May, 1890; on deputation to Vienna postal congress, April-July, 1891; resumed his post in Bengal, Oct., 1891; officiating director-general, Nov., 1893; author of statistical accounts of Chittagong and other districts.

KISSORY MOHUN CHATTERJEE, B.A., B.L., Judicial Department, Bengal.—Officiated as judge of the small cause court in 1888 and 1889, and was confirmed as 4th judge, 3rd Oct., 1889; 3rd judge, Oct., 1893; author of a legal treatise on the transfer of immovable property.

KITCHIN, ARTHUR JAMES WARBURTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Clifton College, and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 4th Jan., 1894, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

KITTS, EUSTACE JOHN, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Portland Grammar School, Mill Hill Grammar School, and University of London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Berar, Aug., 1875, and served as

assistant commissioner and also as small cause court judge of Akola; on special (famine) duty in Madras, 1877-78; assistant commissioner, Ajmere, May to Nov., 1883; from April, 1884, served in the N.W. Provinces, and was appointed joint magistrate, 2nd grade, May, 1885; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Nov., 1887; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Nov., 1888; small cause court judge, Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; author of 'Berar Census Report' (1882), and 'Castes and Tribes found in India.'

KNAPP, ARTHUR ROWLAND, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1839; arrived, 20th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

KNIGHT, RAYMONT, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Paul's College, Stony Stratford, and University College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 12th Jan., 1885; assistant collector, April, 1885; 2nd assistant and forest settlement officer, March, 1892.

KNOWLYS, HENRY BOLD, late Berar Commission.—Served as a writer in the Ceylon civil service, 15th Feb., 1859, to Aug., 1866; from Sept., 1866, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as assistant commissioner; small cause court judge, Sept., 1873; deputy commissioner, May, 1886; retired, July, 1891.

KNOX, GEORGE, late Punjab Administration.—Assistant engineer, May, 1854; from June, 1856, served in the Punjab commission, and was appointed personal assistant to commissioner of Jullundur, April, 1864; assistant commissioner, 1st class, Oct., 1864; deputy commissioner, 1st class, Dec., 1877; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Rawul Pindi, Nov., 1878, to April, 1879; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; retired, 1890.

KNOX, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1865; from June, 1867, served in the N.W. Provinces, as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; from March, 1877, served in the judicial department, and was appointed small cause court judge, Allahabad, Aug., 1877; judge, 3rd grade, March, 1884; officiating legal remembrancer, Feb., 1885; member of the lieutenant-governor's council, Dec., 1886, and again, Feb., 1889; district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, continuing to act as legal remembrancer, Sept., 1887; confirmed in the latter post, Sept., 1887; judge of the high court, N.W. Provinces, Dec., 1890; author of a 'Digest of the Criminal Law of the Bengal Presidency,' a 'Digest of Civil Procedure,' 'The Legal Remembrancer, 1879-82,' and 'Treatise upon the Indian Stamp Law.'

KNOX, HUBERT THOMAS, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as assistant magistrate and collector, acting deputy commissioner of salt revenue, acting principal assistant, joint sessions judge for trying Rump cases, and assistant commissioner (Nilgiris); acted as district and

sessions judge, Ganjam, April-Sept., 1884, and afterwards in North Arcot; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Malabar, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Trichinopoly, Nov., 1887; collector and magistrate, Nov., 1888; district and sessions judge, Bellary, Feb., 1890; resigned, Jan., 1892.

KNYVETT, WILLIAM LEYCESTER NATHANIEL, Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Bengal.—First commission, 10th June, 1854; served in the Bengal police department from June, 1863, as district superintendent (1st grade, April, 1875); officiating personal assistant to inspector-general, July, 1875; deputy inspector-general, April, 1885; officiating inspector-general of police, Bengal, July, 1888; reverted to military duty, 1891.

KONSTAM, EDWIN MAX, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Marlborough, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 29th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

KOUGH, CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Kingstown, Dungannon, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1867, and served in various districts of Madras in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, head assistant, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; forest settlement officer, March, 1884; acted for various periods as collector and magistrate in the districts of Madura, Cuddapah, and North Arcot; collector and magistrate, Kurnool, Nov., 1887; retired, July, 1894.

KRISHNA CHUNDER CHATTERJEE, B.A., B.L., Judicial Department, Bengal.—From 19th Dec., 1865, served in the subordinate judicial service, Bengal, and was appointed subordinate judge, 4th grade, May, 1877; small cause court judge, Backergunge, Dec., 1877; subordinate judge, 1st grade, May, 1884.

KRISHNA GOVINDA GUPTA, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Mymensing Zilla School, and Pogose School, Dacca, and matriculated at Calcutta University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1873; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; on famine duty, March, 1874; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Sept., 1884; has also officiated as magistrate and collector, and as junior secretary to the board of revenue, and was confirmed in the latter appointment, May, 1890; in 1891 and again in 1893 acted as commissioner of excise, Bengal; confirmed, March, 1893.

KRISHNA MOHUN MOOKERJI, B.L., Judicial Department, Bengal.—Served in the subordinate judicial service, Bengal, from 28th March, 1865, and was appointed subordinate judge, 1st grade, Dec., 1884; small cause court judge, Nov., 1890.

LAING, JAMES ANDERSON, M.D., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Assistant-surgeon, 1st April, 1870; arrived in India, 26th Dec., 1870, and served in the Madras medical department as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, and acting resident surgeon at the general hospital, Madras; deputy sanitary commissioner and inspector of vaccination, Feb.,

1878; surgeon-major, April, 1882; officiating sanitary commissioner, April, 1886; confirmed in appointment, June, 1888.

LAKSHMAN GOPAL DESHMUKH, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Appointed probationer under the native civil service rules, 16th April, 1881, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; confirmed as member of the Bombay civil service, 19th June, 1885; 2nd assistant collector and magistrate, Nov., 1889.

LAMB, RICHARD AMPHLETT, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Highgate Grammar School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 21st Oct., 1879, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; forest settlement officer, Khandeish, March, 1885; served in Burma as deputy commissioner, March, 1887, to July, 1890 (India medal with Burma clasp); 1st assistant, and acting collector and magistrate, Aug., 1892.

LAMBE, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1868, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1881; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Nov., 1886; magistrate and collector, Mainpuri, April, 1889; deputy commissioner, Jalaun, Nov., 1890; magistrate and collector, Ghazipur, April, 1892.

LAMBERT, SIR JOHN, K.C.I.E., Police Department, Bengal.—Served from 23rd Nov., 1863, as assistant superintendent, district superintendent, and officiating personal assistant to the inspector-general; on special duty, March, 1872; visitor of the presidency jail, and of the lunatic asylums, July, 1872; deputy commissioner of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1874; served under the government of India, foreign department, from March, 1882, to March, 1884, as officiating political resident, 2nd class, and general superintendent of the operations for the suppression of thuggee and dacoity; acted as commissioner, Calcutta, and president of the boiler commission, 1885-86; commissioner of police, Calcutta, April, 1889; member of lieut.-governor's council, June, 1892; K.C.I.E., June, 1893.

LANE, CLAYTON TURNER, Colonel, I.S.C., Police Department, Hyderabad Administration.—Ensign, Bengal army, 11th Dec., 1858; from March, 1863, served in the Punjab as assistant superintendent and district superintendent of police; transferred to Berar police, Sept., 1865; inspector-general of police and jails, Hyderabad assigned districts, April, 1877; is also inspector-general of jails and registration and stamps, and registrar of joint stock companies.

LANE, HERBERT HARDINGHAM, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd July, 1892.

LANE, JAMES HORSBURGH, M.I.E.E., late Telegraph Department, Government of India. —Appointed to the Indian telegraph department, 1st Feb., 1857; superintendent, 1st grade, Bombay, Aug., 1870; officiating deputy director-general, March, 1872; director of traffic, Feb., 1879; officiating deputy director-general, Feb., 1886, and Jan., 1890; confirmed, April, 1890; officiating director-general, April to July, 1892;

retired, June, 1894; compiler of 'Word Code for Foreign Telegrams.'

LANE, JOHN MACDONALD, M.I.E.E., late Telegraph Department, Government of India. —Appointed to the Indian telegraph department, 22nd Sept., 1862; assistant superintendent, 1st grade, Jan., 1866; superintendent, Bengal, Oct., 1869; served subsequently in the Malabar Coast and Nagpur divisions; 1st grade superintendent, British Burma, Sept., 1883, and afterwards in Bombay; charge of Bengal division, Dec., 1889; retired, April, 1891.

LANE, WILMOT, Barrister-at-Law, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1854; arrived in India, 13th January, 1855; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; from Aug., 1863, to March, 1866, served in Oudh as officiating deputy commissioner, Lucknow; magistrate and collector, Mainpuri, Jan., 1872; civil and sessions judge, Moradabad, Aug., 1877; commissioner, Rae Bareli division, Sept., 1880, and subsequently of the Agra and Meerut divisions; officiated as member, board of revenue, N.W. Provinces, Oct.-Dec., 1883; retired, April, 1889.

LANG, ARTHUR MOFFATT, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, N.W. Provinces. —1st commission, June, 1852; arrived in India, July, 1854; assistant engineer, Punjab, Nov., 1855; superintendent, civil buildings, Lahore, Aug., 1856; executive engineer, March, 1857; during the Mutiny served at the siege and capture of Delhi, the relief and capture of Lucknow, and various engagements (medal and clasps); assistant to chief engineer, Oudh, April, 1858; assistant secretary, public works department, to chief commissioner, Oudh, July, 1865; principal, Thomason engineering college, March, 1871, to May, 1877; superintending engineer, 1st grade, deputy inspector-general military works, and secretary, defence committee, Feb., 1879; secretary to agent to governor-general, Baluchistan, public works department, Feb., 1881; chief engineer, and secretary to chief commissioner, British Burma, public works department, Jan., 1883; chief engineer and joint secretary, buildings and roads and railway branches, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, July, 1885; chief engineer, 1st class, March, 1886; retired, Nov., 1887.

LANG, BASIL, Judicial Department, Bombay.—Officiated as advocate general, May-Nov., 1891, and from April, 1892; confirmed, 23rd Oct., 1893.

LANG, GEORGE LUKIS, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1858; arrived in India, March, 1858; from Nov., 1858, to Nov., 1866, served in Oudh as assistant commissioner and assistant settlement officer; from Nov., 1866, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, deputy commissioner, Jhansi, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; appointed magistrate and collector, Aligarh, April, 1877, and was subsequently posted to Bulandshar; officiating district and sessions judge, July-Sept., 1880; acted for various periods as commissioner in the Agra, Rohilkhand, and Fyzabad divisions; commissioner of Jhansi, July, 1889; retired, May, 1891.

LANG, JOHN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

LANG, RICHARD TICKELL MONTGOMERY, Colonel, Bengal Cavalry, Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1859; from May, 1862, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, April, 1877; officiating additional civil and sessions judge, Peshawur, April–Nov., 1880; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Multan, March, 1881, and subsequently in the Rawal Pindi division; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Jan., 1882.

LANGLEY, EDWARD HARTLEY RAYNSFORD, Brigade-Surgeon, B.A. (Dub.), M.R.C.S. (Lon.), late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Bombay medical service, 23rd July, 1858; arrived in India, 28th Sept., 1858; was employed under the military department, and as acting civil surgeon in various stations; served with 5th regiment, N.L.I., in China in 1860–62; appointed presidency surgeon and marine surgeon, Bombay, Oct., 1876; also in medical charge of the European general hospital, May–June, 1882; brigade-surgeon, June, 1884; retired, Nov., 1889.

LANSDOWNE (MARQUESS OF), RIGHT HON. HENRY CHARLES KEITH FITZMAURICE, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., D.C.L.—Born, 1845; educated at Eton, and Balliol College, Oxford; succeeded to the marquise in 1866; a lord of the treasury, 1868 to 1872; under secretary of state for war, 1872 to 1874; under secretary of state for India, April to July, 1880; governor-general of Canada, May, 1883; appointed governor-general of India, and assumed office, 10th Dec., 1888; returned to England, 27th Jan., 1894; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1888; K.G., 1894.

LARGE, PHILIP TOWNSEND SOMERVILLE, B.C.E., M.I.C.E.—Appointed assistant engineer, 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Bombay; transferred to state railways, Punjab, Jan., 1870; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; employed on Bengal-Nagpur railway from Oct., 1884; services lent to Gwalior state, Oct., 1892; superintending engineer, Feb., 1893.

LARKINS, ARTHUR BROOKS, Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Appointed assistant superintendent, 30th Oct., 1869, and employed on various lines, including Tavoy-Siam line; superintendent, March, 1886, and held charge of various divisions; in charge, check office, Calcutta, April, 1892; in charge, telegraph stores, Calcutta, July, 1892; in charge, Dacca division, Feb., 1894.

LARMINIE, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as probationary assistant engineer; executive engineer, Sept., 1877.

LARMINIE, WILLIAM REA, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived, 28th Dec., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served in the revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, March, 1879; officiating commissioner, Orissa division, and superintendent of tributary mehals, Cuttack, June, 1883; officiating commis-

sioner, Dacca, April, 1885; confirmed, Oct., 1886; retired, Aug., 1889.

LARYMORE, ALEXANDER DOUGLAS, Jail Department, Bengal.—Served in the Behar cavalry as trooper, 1857–58; joined the Bengal police as assistant superintendent, 31st July, 1862; district superintendent, June, 1872; on famine relief duty, Nov., 1874; superintendent, Midnapore jail, Jan., 1877; superintendent of Alipore jail, March, 1884; has also officiated as inspector-general of jails.

LAST, WALTER ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Westminster, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served as assistant magistrate and collector and assistant commissioner.

LATHAM, FRANCIS LAW, M.A., B.L., late Judicial Service, Bombay.—Served as acting remembrancer of legal affairs, 23rd May, 1872, to 1st Jan., 1873; also acted as advocate-general, and as judge of the high court, Bombay, for various periods between 1st Jan., 1880, and 5th March, 1884; advocate-general, Bombay, 6th March, 1884, to 23rd Oct., 1893.

LATOUCHE, CECIL D'URBAN, Major-General, I.S.C., late Cantonment Magistrate, Bombay.—Ensign, 29th July, 1850; arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1850, and served on military duty up to Nov., 1883, and subsequently as cantonment magistrate, Poona; returned to Europe, 1888.
