LA TOUCHE, HENRY CHRISTOPHER DIGGES, late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Joined the public works department, Nov., 1868, as executive engineer, Punjab Northern state railway; Agra-Gwalior railway survey, Nov., 1872; engineer-in-chief, Sindia state railway, construction division, Nov., 1879; Bundelcund railway surveys, 1881; engineer-in-chief, Jhansi-Manikpur state railway, Sept., 1883; superintending engineer, 3rd class, Dec., 1885; engineer-in-chief, Bellary-Kistna and Cuddapah-Nellore railways, March, 1886; lent to Southern Maharratta railway company, Jan., 1888; superintending engineer, 2nd class, March, 1889, and 1st class, Feb., 1890; retired, May, 1892.

LATOUCHE, JAMES JOHN DIGGES, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1867; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant settlement officer, till Dec., 1871, when he was transferred as settlement officer to Ajmere; also served as officiating assistant commissioner of Bewar and officiating deputy commissioner, Ajmere; returned to N.W. Provinces, 1875, and was appointed district superintendent of police, 1st grade, Bulandshahr, Sept., 1875; served subsequently in various districts in officiating appointments as settlement officer, magistrate and collector, and district and sessions judge, and was promoted to joint magistrate, 1st grade, July, 1882; settlement officer, 1st grade, Gorakhpur, Nov., 1883; from Aug., 1886, served in Upper Burma as commissioner of the southern division; rejoined the N.W. Provinces as magistrate and collector, April, 1890; officiating commissioner, Allahabad, May, 1891; acting chief secretary, Nov., 1891; member of lieut.-governor's council, Dec., 1891; commissioner

and agent, Benares, April, 1893; acting chief secretary to government, May, 1893; confirmed, Dec., 1893; author of 'Gazetteer of Ajmere-Mairwara.'

LATOUCHE, WILLIAM PAGET, Colonel, I.S.C., late Salt Department, Bombay.—First commission, Bombay army, Sept., 1854; arrived in India, 17th Jan., 1855; was employed on military duty, and served in the Persian war, 1856, and the Mutiny campaign, and was also appointed assistant magistrate, Dec., 1859, and assistant district superintendent of police, Jan., 1865; acting political superintendent, Palanpur, June-Nov., 1867; from Dec., 1867, served in the Bombay police department as assistant superintendent and district superintendent (1st grade, May, 1882), and also acted as superintendent of the Bombay, Baroda, and Central India railway company's police, Nov., 1868, to Nov., 1869; on special duty, June, 1879, to Feb., 1880; from Feb., 1883, served as deputy collector of salt revenue; returned to Europe, 1887.

LAUDERDALE (EARL OF), RT. HON. FREDERICK HENRY MATTLAND, Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C.—First commission, 4th Hussars, Feb., 1861; served in Central India in civil employ from 1869 as boundary officer, and political assistant; 2nd assistant agent, Indore, 1877; 1st assistant agent, Indore, 1878; additional political agent and superintendent, Charkhari, 1880; retired, Jan., 1886.

LAUGHLIN, ROBERT CAMPBELL, M.I.E.E., late Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as assistant superintendent, Oct., 1866; held charge of various divisions, chiefly in Madras and Bengal, and charge of the telegraph stores, Calcutta; class iv., 2nd grade, Oct., 1887; retired, May, 1891.

LAUGHTON, DANIEL WILSON, Major-General, I.S.C., late Berar Commission.—First commission in the army, Jan., 1854; arrived in Madras, April, 1854; from Jan., 1865, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as assistant commissioner, small cause court judge, and cantonment magistrate; acted as inspector-general of registration, Jan., 1873; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Feb., 1885; retired, May, 1886.

LAURIE, HENRY MONTAGUE, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1887; assistant commissioner, Central Provinces, Dec., 1887; officiating under secretary to the chief commissioner in 1890-91; also acted as commissioner of excise in 1892.

LAURIE, LEWIS KOSUTH, B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 27th Oct., 1873; from Nov., 1873, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, acting deputy commissioner and acting district superintendent of police; acted as registrar of the judicial commissioner's court, July, 1880, to March, 1883, and as small cause court judge, Nagpur, in 1881-1882, and 1887; acting assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, 1883-85; acting commissioner of excise, superintendent of stamps, and inspector-general of registration, July-Oct., 1883, and again, March, 1885; acting director of agriculture and junior secretary to chief

commissioner, Sept.-Oct., 1884; under secretary to chief commissioner, Oct., 1885; assistant commissioner, Oct., 1887; acted as secretary to chief commissioner in 1889 and 1890; judge, small cause court, Jubbulpore, Jan., 1890; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1890; commissioner of excise, Jan., 1891; officiating inspector-general of police and jails, May, 1891; confirmed in that appointment, April, 1892; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1892, and from Feb., 1893; confirmed, April, 1894; author of a digest of rulings of judicial commissioners, Central Provinces, in civil cases (1881), and some manuals relating to the Central Provinces.

LAURIE, MAXWELL, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Watson's College, Edinburgh, and Edinburgh University, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1889, and served as assistant commissioner and settlement officer.

LAW, VICTOR EDWARD, Colonel, Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, Madras cavalry, 20th April, 1859; acted as government agent at Chepak, pay-master of Carnatic stipendiaries, and Persian and Hindustani translator to government, July, 1869; attaché, foreign department, July, 1872; served in Rajputana as superintendent of railway police, 1872 to 1877; political agent, Jypoor, 1877, and afterwards in Ulwar, 1877 to 1883; officiating political agent, Charkhari, 1883; assistant at Sujangarh to governor-general's agent in Rajputana, Feb., 1884; agent to governor-general with king of Oudh, and superintendent of political pensions, March, 1884; appointed to the political charge of ex-Amir Muhammad Yakub Khan of Kabul, Aug., 1884; officiated as political agent, Mussoorie, 1890-91.

LAWRENCE, ALEXANDER JOHN, C.I.E., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1856; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1856; served from March, 1853, in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; from March, 1864, served in the Central Provinces as settlement officer of Bhandara; re-attached to N.W. Provinces, Feb., 1869, and served as settlement officer and officiating magistrate and collector; member of the commission under the military department on the Lawrence asylums, June to Nov., 1872; on special duty with the government of India, home department, Dec., 1872; officiating civil and sessions judge, Mainpuri, June, 1874; magistrate and collector, Etawah, Oct., 1874, and subsequently of Agra; officiated for various periods as commissioner, Agra division; commissioner of the Allahabad division, April, 1885, and Meerut division, April, 1889; C.I.E., May, 1888; retired, Nov., 1891.

LAWRENCE, EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S.—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived, 12th Nov., 1878, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer; acted as assistant to the political resident at Aden, March, 1882, and as under secretary to government, political, judicial, and educational departments, and secretary to the governor's council for making laws and regulations, April, 1885; compiler of general adminis-

tration report for 1884-85; acted as assistant judge and sessions judge, and as under secretary to government, in revenue, financial, and general departments; under secretary to the government of India, finance and commerce department, April, 1889; deputy accountant-general, Bombay, April, 1890; officiating accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, March, 1891.

LAWRENCE, HENRY JOHN, Lieutenant-Colonel, Bengal Infantry, Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 14th February, 1862; from Aug., 1866, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, judicial assistant, and officiating deputy commissioner; district judge, Nov., 1884; additional divisional judge, Amritsar, Jan., 1885; divisional judge, April, 1889.

LAWRENCE, HENRY STAVELEY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Haileybury College, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1891.

LAWRENCE, RICHARD CHARLES, C.B., General, I.S.C., late Punjab Commissin.—First commission, Bengal infantry, June, 1834; served in the Sutlej campaign in 1846, and was present at the battle of Sobraon (medal); second in command, Loodianah regiment, Dec., 1847; assistant commissioner, Punjab, April, 1849; captain of police, Lahore division, Jan., 1853; served in the Indian mutiny as political officer in charge of the Cashmere contingent, under General Wilson, in 1857, and was present at the assault and capture of Delhi (brevet of major, C.B., medal with clasp); military secretary to lieutenant governor of the Punjab, 1860; deputy commissioner, Simla, April, 1862; resident, Nepal, 1867 to 1872.

LAWRENCE, WALTER ROPER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; from Sept., 1881, to April, 1884, served in Ajmere and Merwara as officiating political assistant and assistant commissioner, and officiating cantonment magistrate; in charge of Ajmere treasury and office of registrar of assurances, May, 1882; under-secretary to government, Punjab, Feb., 1885, and again in April, 1886; under secretary to government of India, revenue and agricultural department, Sept., 1886; officiated as secretary in 1888; settlement officer, Kashmir, April, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891.

LAWRIE, EDWARD, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.B., Indian Medical Department.—First commission, Bengal medical service, 15th Nov., 1872; resident surgeon, Calcutta, medical college hospital, April, 1874; professor, medical college, June, 1875; civil surgeon, Monghyr, March, 1879; professor of surgery, Lahore medical school and meteorological reporter, Oct., 1879; acting residency surgeon, Hyderabad, April, 1885; confirmed, Oct., 1885.

LEA, JAMES HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1888, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

LEAPINGWELL, ARTHUR HENRY, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1873, and was employed under the military department until Nov., 1879, when he was appointed superintendent, lunatic asylum, Madras; zillah surgeon and superintendent of jail, Berhampur, Jan., 1882; civil surgeon, Negapatam, Feb., 1885; zillah surgeon and superintendent of jail, Cuddalore, Oct., 1885; district surgeon and superintendent, lunatic asylum, Calicut, March, 1892; district surgeon and superintendent of jail, Vizagapatam, March, 1893.

LEARY, ALFRED SIDNEY, Registry Department, India Office.—Educated at the Moravian colleges of Fulneck in Yorkshire, and Neuwied on the Rhine; temporary clerk, India office, 15th Nov., 1860; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

LEATHER, JOHN WALTER, Ph. D., F.I.C., F.C.S., Agricultural Chemistry Department, Government of India.—Appointed agricultural chemist to the government of India, 17th Nov., 1892.

LE BRETON, WILLIAM INGLIS, Major, I.S.C., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—First commission, royal artillery, Dec., 1864; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1865; joined the public works department, Nov., 1866, and served as assistant engineer in the Bombay presidency till Nov., 1869, and subsequently as assistant controller and deputy controller of accounts in the Punjab, N.W. Provinces, and Bombay; controller, 3rd class, and examiner of railway accounts, Bombay, Sept., 1874; examiner of guaranteed railway accounts, Calcutta, Nov., 1877; from Dec., 1879, served as examiner of guaranteed railway accounts, Bombay, and was promoted to 2nd class, May, 1886; also officiated as examiner of public works accounts, Sept.-Dec., 1883, and port storekeeper, state railways, March-June, 1884; examiner, public works accounts, Bombay, Feb., 1887; retired, June, 1888.

LEE, HARRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Yarmouth Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1874; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge; officiating assistant secretary, government of Bengal, March, 1876; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1885; chairman of the commissioners, Calcutta, April, 1890.

LEE, WILLIAM HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School and St. John's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1884; arrived in India, 19th Dec., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, June, 1890.

LEEDS, ROBERT JAMES, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 25th Sept., 1861; served from Aug., 1862, in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating deputy superintendent of the family domains of the Maharaja of Benares, joint magistrate and

deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; from Nov., 1879, served in the judicial department as civil and sessions judge; retired, April, 1887.

LEEDS, WILLIAM HENRY ARTHUR ST. JOHN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1885; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1885; transferred to Burma, as assistant commissioner, Nov., 1888; officiated as director of land records and agriculture, Nov., 1890, to Feb., 1891; deputy commissioner, June, 1893.

LEES, DONALD HECTOR, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at North London Collegiate School, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 1st Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, Dec., 1892.

LEE-WARNER, JOHN, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Marlborough College, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; served in various districts of Madras from April, 1866, as assistant collector and magistrate and acting cantonment magistrate; on special duty, inquiring into condition of poorer classes, North Arcot and Salem, Jan., 1868; boundary arbitrator between the Kalastrizemindari and Madras district, Sept., 1868; special assistant collector, Madura, for the management of the Ramnad estate, Dec., 1871, to March, 1878; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1882; acting collector of sea customs and protector of emigrants, Madras, May, 1884; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Oct., 1884; collector and magistrate, Dec., 1884; retired, Jan., 1890.

LEE-WARNER, WILLIAM, M.A., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Rugby School, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 8th November, 1869; assistant collector and magistrate, March, 1870; officiating director of public instruction, Berar, Feb., 1872; private secretary to governor of Bombay, May, 1872, to Nov., 1874, and Jan.-Dec., 1876; on special duty in the secretariat, Nov., 1874; acting under secretary to government, political, judicial, and educational departments, and acting secretary to the legislative council, Bombay, Nov., 1874; from Dec., 1876, to Aug., 1878, served in Sind as assistant commissioner and branch inspector-general of registration, acting educational inspector, and acting commissioner; acting junior collector and magistrate, Satara, March, 1881; on special duty as member of the education commission, Calcutta, 1882; on special duty at the Nira canal, April, 1882; from Jan.-Oct., 1884, acted as junior under secretary to government of India, foreign department, and as private secretary to the president in council of the governor-general (Jan.-Feb.), and was on special duty in the foreign department, March-June, 1884; acting press commissioner, Sept., 1884; acting under secretary to government of India, foreign department, Oct., 1884; first assistant collector and acting director of public instruction, Bombay, March, 1885; on special duty in the Hyderabad state, Nov., 1885; acting political agent, Kolhapur, and Southern Mahratta country, Jan.,

1886; also president of the regency council, Kolhapur, May, 1886; secretary to government of Bombay, political, &c., departments, Aug., 1887; C.S.I., Jan., 1892; additional member of governor-general's council, Dec., 1893, to March, 1894; author of 'The Protected Princes of India.'

LE FANU, WILLIAM JOSEPH HENRY, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Kings-town School, Tipperary Grammar School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived, 28th Oct., 1865; served in Madras revenue and judicial departments from Oct., 1866, in various districts as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector, and joint magistrate, acting district and sessions judge, and acting collector and magistrate; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Dec., 1885; collector and magistrate, Jan., 1886; acting member, board of revenue, Jan.-July, 1893; compiler of 'Salem District Manual.'

LEGEYT, PHILIP HARRISON, Major-General, I.S.C., late Political Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 27th March, 1851; was employed under the military department, including service with the Persian expeditionary force (medal and clasp); served subsequently with the Gujerat irregular horse, and also acted as assistant political agent, Mahi Kantha (April, 1858); from May, 1863, served in the political department as assistant political agent, Kathiawar and Cutch, and magistrate of the district of Bhavnagar; political agent, Mahi Kantha, 1872-80; political superintendent, Palanpur, Sept., 1880; 1st grade, political agent, Feb., 1885; returned to Europe, 1889.

LEGGATT, EDWARD OWEN EVERY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Lincoln College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1885; served in Oudh as assistant commissioner, and was appointed assistant superintendent and judge of the small cause court, Dehra Dun, April, 1890; joint magistrate, May, 1890; officiating district and sessions judge from June, 1893.

LEGGATT, ERNEST HUGH EVERY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Keble College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 15th Dec., 1891, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

LEIGH, HENRY PERCY POINGDESTRE, C.I.E., Major, I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—In military employ from 11th Nov., 1872; joined the Punjab commission, 23rd April, 1879, and served as assistant commissioner, officiating district judge, &c.; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; deputy commissioner, April, 1894.

LEITNER, GOTTLIEB WILLIAM, M.A., Ph.D., LL.D., D.C.L., Barrister-at-Law, late Education Department, Punjab.—Served in education department as principal of the Lahore College from 12th Nov., 1864, and was promoted to 2nd grade of the department, Nov., 1880; in July, 1867, was deputed to Kashmir for a philological examination of the Kashmiri and Chilasii dialects; on special duty in connection with the school text-book committee at Simla, May, 1877; attended the congress of Orientalists at Florence, Oct., 1878; officiating director of public instruc-

tion, Punjab, July, 1879; on special duty with education commission, May, 1882; retired in 1886.

LELY, FREDERIC STYLES PHILPIN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Pembroke College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 8th December, 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting city survey and inquiry officer at Ahmedabad, acting talukdari settlement officer, and acting collector and magistrate and political agent; in charge of the Sachin state, Oct., 1881; first assistant collector, Surat, Dec., 1884; administrator of the Porbandar state in Kathiawar; junior collector, June, 1890; member of Deccan agriculturists relief act commission, 1891-92; senior collector and magistrate, March, 1893.

LEMAN, GEORGE DOWNTON, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1857; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Madras, and was appointed principal assistant to the collector and magistrate and agent, Ganjam, Dec., 1866; acting civil and sessions judge, Guntoor, July, 1867, and afterwards at Tellicherry; acted as collector of sea customs and protector of emigrants, Madras, in 1868, and again in 1875 and 1881-82, and as inam commissioner in 1869; from Feb., 1872, to Nov., 1877, served as collector and magistrate of Kistna, and subsequently in Ganjam and Coimbatore; retired, March, 1888.

LE MESSURIER, AUGUSTUS, C.I.E., Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—First commission, Bombay engineers, Dec., 1856; served in the Indian Mutiny at the sieges of Forts Beyt and Dwarika; joined the Bombay public works department, as assistant engineer, May, 1860, and was employed on the Karachi harbour works, on irrigation in Sind, on the Bombay harbour defences, &c.; executive engineer, March, 1863; deputy consulting engineer for railways, Bombay, Aug., 1864; served in the Abyssinian expedition, 1868 (medal); deputy consulting engineer for railways in Sind, Aug., 1871; acted as consulting engineer, Lahore, April-July, 1875; acted as secretary to the railway transport committee at Delhi, in 1875-76, and to a similar committee at Agra, 1876-77; deputed to Brussels exhibition to report on continental system of transport by railways, Aug., 1876; superintending engineer, May, 1877; officiating under secretary to government of India, railway branch, May, 1877, to Nov., 1878; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80, (medal and brevet of lieutenant-colonel); engineer-in-chief, Mysore state railway, Nov., 1879; joint secretary to chief commissioner, Mysore, 1880; on special duty reporting on timber-cutting operations in east of France, April, 1880; manager, Mysore state railway, Feb., 1881; consulting engineer to Mysore government, and secretary to chief commissioner, Coorg, March, 1882; superintending engineer, 1st class, Jan., 1883; engineer-in-chief, Bangalore-Harihar railway; 3rd chief engineer, March, 1887; on special duty, military department, Dec., 1887; officiating consulting engineer, railways, Calcutta, Feb., 1888; 2nd chief engineer, April, 1888; on special railway duty, Dec., 1888; chief engineer and secretary to government of Punjab, May, 1889; reverted to military duty, June, 1892; retired from the army, May, 1893.

LE MESURIER, HAVILLAND, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1884; arrived, 9th Nov., 1886; supernumerary assistant commissioner, Assam, Nov., 1886; assistant secretary and personal assistant to chief commissioner, March, 1891; transferred to Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, April, 1892; joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1893.

LE MESURIER, JOHN, Lieutenant-General, R.E., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1852; second lieutenant, Dec., 1853; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1855; served in the public works department in Sind from June, 1856, as assistant engineer and executive engineer, and was employed on the Nara works and the Rohri canal, Hyderabad; served with the Persian expedition, 1856-57 (medal and clasp); on special duty, Karachi, Jan., 1867; acting superintending engineer for irrigation in Sind, April, 1871, to Dec., 1873, and for various periods between 1875 and 1880; superintendent of works, Western Indus, Karachi, Dec., 1873; superintending engineer, Jan., 1879; officiating chief engineer for irrigation and under secretary to government of Bombay, June-Aug., 1883; (temporary) chief engineer, 3rd class, and chief engineer for irrigation, and joint secretary to government, public works department (irrigation), March, 1884; superintending engineer in Sind, Jan., 1886; retired, March, 1887.

LE ROSSIGNOL, WALTER AUBIN, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Victoria College, Jersey, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

LESLIE, JOHN TASMAN WADDELL, M.B., Surgeon-Captain.—First commission, 1st Oct., 1884; in military employ to 19th April, 1889, when he was appointed secretary to inspector-general of jails, Burma; junior civil surgeon, Rangoon, May, 1890; resumed his former post, Nov., 1890; acting chemical examiner and professor of chemistry, Calcutta medical college, April, 1892; acting secretary to surgeon-general and sanitary commissioner to government of India, Nov., 1892; confirmed, Nov., 1893.

LESLIE, MORICE, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Hyderabad; executive engineer, May, 1884; transferred to Central Provinces, Feb., 1886; assistant secretary to chief commissioner, June-Sept., 1887; transferred to Coorg, Jan., 1893.

LESMOND, WILLIAM ALOYSIUS, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 4th Nov., 1871, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, Oct., 1880; employed in the Punjab, 1888-90; officiating superintendent, way and works, Eastern Bengal line, April, 1892.

LETHBRIDGE, ALFRED SWAINE, M.D., C.S.I., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Medical Service.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1867; served from March, 1872, as superintendent of central jail and officiating civil surgeon, Bhagulpore; officiating inspector-general of prisons, sanitary commissioner, and superintendent of vaccination,

British Burma, July, 1876; officiating sanitary commissioner, Bengal, May, 1877; inspector-general of jails, Bengal, Jan., 1878; C.S.I., May, 1890; president of the factory commission, Sept., 1890; president, native passengers ship committee, Nov., 1890; resumed his post as inspector-general of jails, March, 1891; general superintendent of operations for the suppression of thagi and dakaiti, April, 1892; is a member of the viceroy's legislative council.

LETHÉREN, SAMUEL KEMP, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at King's College, London; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 6th July, 1877.

LEUPOLT, JOHN CUNNINGHAM, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at King's College School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1862; from Aug., 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; from Aug., 1882, served in the judicial department as civil and sessions judge; retired, April, 1889.

LEVESON, HENRY GEORGE ASHWORTH, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Marlborough College and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1887; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1889, and served as assistant commissioner; acting assistant superintendent, southern Shan states, May, 1891; confirmed, July, 1892.

LEVINGE, EDWARD VERE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 23th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating under-secretary, judicial, &c., departments, Feb., 1893; confirmed, Nov., 1893.

LEWIS, EDWIN MORRIS.—Superintendent, Bengal secretariat press, April, 1869; superintendent of government printing, Bengal, Sept., 1877.

LEWIS, THOMAS CROMPTON, M.A., Punjab Educational Service.—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service as professor, government college, Lahore, 13th Oct., 1881; principal of government college, Lahore, May, 1886; inspector of schools, Nov., 1891; director of public instruction, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Oct., 1894; author of 'Arithmetic for Indian Schools,' and various articles on mathematical subjects.

LIARDET, CHARLES AYLMER, Colonel, I.S.C., late Revenue Survey Department, Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Oct., 1855; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1855; Madras staff corps, Sept., 1866; served in the Madras revenue survey department from Oct., 1868; on famine relief works, Jan., 1877, to March, 1878; deputy superintendent, 2nd grade, Sept., 1884; retired, April, 1888.

LIDDERDALE, ROBERT, M.D., Brigade-Surgeon, late Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1858; superintendent of vaccination, 8th November, 1869; in medical charge, Jalpaiguri, in addition to his own duties, June, 1872; sanitary commissioner, Bengal, April, 1880; professor of hygiene, medical college; rank of surgeon-major, Jan., 1870; deputy surgeon-

general (local rank), Bengal, Jan., 1884; retired, Jan., 1889.

LIGHTFOOT, JABEZ, Railway Department, Government of India.—Appointed from the Scinde, Punjab, and Delhi railway as deputy manager, North-Western state railway, Jan., 1886; class 1, revenue establishment, state railways, July, 1886; manager, Punjab section, March, 1887; Sind section, May, 1887; auditor, Oudh and Rohilkund railway, Jan., 1889; transferred to superior accounts establishment, Sept., 1889; acted as manager, Oudh and Rohilkund railway, in 1890, and again in 1891; examiner of guaranteed railway accounts, Madras, Oct., 1891; officiating in same capacity at Bombay, April, 1894.

LILLINGSTON, EDWARD GORDON, Colonel, I.S.C.—First commission in army, 30th Aug., 1859; from 5th Feb., 1864, served in the non-regulation commission, Bengal, and was appointed assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Aug., 1867, officiating political agent, Hill Tipperah, Feb., 1874, superintendent of the central jail and the European penitentiary at Hazaribagh, May, 1875, and deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, March, 1886; 1st grade, Aug., 1887; reverted to military department, March, 1892.

LIMOND, DAVID, Major-General, C.B., R.E., late Public Works Department, Punjab.—First commission, Bengal engineers, 1850; assistant engineer, public works department, Bengal, Feb., 1853; during the Mutiny, served at relief and defence of Lucknow (medal and clasp); executive engineer, Cawnpore, 1858; officiating superintendent engineer, Allahabad, April, 1867, and in Bengal, western circle, from March, 1868; transferred to Rawal Pindi, Jan., 1869; superintendent engineer, military works, Rawal Pindi, April, 1872; in 1879-80 served as commanding engineer, Peshawur Valley field force (medal, C.B.); chief engineer, 3rd class, military works, April, 1881; chief engineer and secretary to government, Punjab, Jan., 1883; chief engineer, 1st class, July, 1884; retired, 1886.

LINDSAY, BENJAMIN, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at the Methodist College, Belfast, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and in Oudh as assistant commissioner; assistant superintendent, and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, April, 1894.

LINDSAY, CHARLES ROBERT, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Woolwich, and Haileybury; appointed to the Bengal civil service, 1844; arrived in India, 1845; served in the revenue and judicial departments in the N.W. Provinces; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Farakhabad, 1858; magistrate and collector, Farakhabad, 1862; extra judge of the sadar court, Agra, 1863; civil and sessions judge, Mainpuri, 1865, and subsequently of Goruckpore and Moradabad; judge of the chief court, Punjab, 1869; retired, 1880.

LINDSAY, JAMES GAVIN, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department.—Lieutenant, Madras engineers, Aug., 1855; joined the public works department as assistant engineer, Dec., 1857; during the Mutiny served at the battle of Banda (medal and clasp); served subsequently as executive engineer in Banda and Orissa; deputy consulting engineer for railways, 1863; executive

engineer, 1st grade, railway survey, Mysore, April, 1870; officiating superintending engineer, and engineer-in-chief, Northern Bengal railway, 1872; acted as director of state railways, N.E. system, and joint secretary to Bengal government, Nov., 1878, to March, 1879; engineer-in-chief, Harnai and Gulistan-Karez sections of Kandahar railway, Feb., 1880; served in the Afghan campaign, 1879-80 (medal); superintending engineer and secretary to resident, Hyderabad, March, 1881; engineer-in-chief, Southern Mahratta railway, Nov., 1881; superintending engineer, 1st class (supernumerary), April, 1881; services lent to Southern Mahratta railway company, May, 1884; engineer-in-chief, Bolan railway, April-June, 1885; 3rd chief engineer, March, 1887; 2nd chief engineer, April, 1888; retired, April, 1891.

LINDSAY, WILLIAM, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Winchester; junior clerk, India office, 31st Jan., 1867; private secretary to parliamentary under secretary of state, 1877-81; senior clerk, Jan., 1882.

LIST, GEORGE HENRY, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as personal assistant to superintending engineer, 12th April, 1873; executive engineer, July, 1875; assistant secretary, railway branch of public works secretariat, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Oct., 1879; served subsequently on various railways; acted as manager and engineer-in-chief, Muttra-Hathras and Cawnpore-Farukhabad railways, in 1881; superintending, way and works, North-Western railway, Nov., 1891; superintending engineer, March, 1892.

LISTER, ALLEN LINDSAY, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Rossall School; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments from March, 1865, in the grades of assistant and sub-collector, and was placed on special duty in connection with the relief of the poorer classes, South Arcot, Jan., 1868; on famine duty, Sept. to Dec., 1877; on special duty, Irrigation and Canal Company, Jan., 1878; collector and magistrate, South Canara, July, 1882; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1882, in Vizagapatam, and afterwards in the Godaveri district; retired, March, 1890.

LISTER, WASTEL JAMESON, Major, R.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Educated at Wellington College; entered the service, 6th Jan., 1872; joined the department, 26th April, 1875, as assistant engineer; served with the Kandahar field force in 1881; executive engineer, Sept., 1884.

LISTON, JOHN, Colonel, I.S.C.—Arrived in India as ensign, 29th May, 1856; served with the Bundelcund field force in the campaign of 1859; from Aug., 1863, served in Jhansi, Kumaon, and Ajmere, as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, Lalitpur, Dec., 1879; officiating commissioner, Jhansi, Aug. to Nov., 1882; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1887; retired, Nov., 1889.

LITTLE, CHARLES, Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., M.D., M.C., L.R.C.S., L.M., Madras Medical Service.—Arrived in Madras, 1st Nov., 1872, and served under the military department; civil surgeon, Akola, Jan., 1877; sanitary commissioner, inspector-general of dispensaries, and superinten-

dent of vaccination of the Hyderabad assigned districts, July, 1881.

LITTLE, FREDERICK ARTHUR, Judicial Department, Bombay.—Educated at Uppingham School; admitted a solicitor, 1874; acted as government solicitor and public prosecutor, Bombay, Oct.-Nov., 1879, and May to Nov., 1883, and was confirmed in this appointment, May, 1884.

LITTLE, JOHN ALBERT, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., India Public Works Department.—Joined the department, 9th March, 1867, as assistant engineer, and served in various divisions in Central India; transferred to state railways, Feb., 1871; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; officiating deputy consulting engineer to the government of India for railways, Calcutta, Oct., 1888; confirmed, April, 1889; acted as consulting engineer, Calcutta, in 1891 and 1892, and was confirmed in that post, Aug., 1892, with rank of superintending engineer.

LITTLE, THOMAS DAVID, C.I.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, June, 1859, and arrived in India, Oct., 1859; appointed assistant engineer, Aug., 1862, and executive engineer, Jan., 1869; superintending engineer, Sept., 1889; additional member of the governor's council for making laws and regulations, Dec., 1889, to May, 1890, and from Jan., 1894; chief engineer, Nov., 1891; officiating secretary to government, public works department, Feb., 1893; confirmed, Feb., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

LIVESAY, JOHN JAMES, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and deputy commissioner, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Oct., 1878; employed on famine relief duty, March to Oct., 1874; retired, March, 1888.

LLOYD, ERNEST THOMAS, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Charterhouse, Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1882; assistant magistrate and collector, Feb., 1882; transferred to N.W. Provinces, Nov., 1885, and served as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; officiating city magistrate, Lucknow, Dec., 1886; joint magistrate, March, 1889; retired, Sept., 1894.

LLOYD, FRANCIS, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at the College School, Gloucester; appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College in 1844; served in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, and assistant judge and session judge; judicial assistant to magistrate, Satara, 1860; judge and session judge, Dharwar, 1861; judge and session judge, Poona, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, 1866; judge of the high court, Bombay, 1869; retired in 1873.

LOBB, THEOPHILUS, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1866, and posted to the Punjab; transferred to military works, Jan., 1875; and to Hyderabad, Oct., 1876; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; employed in Madras, 1884-86.

LOCH, JAMES HENRY, M.D., Deputy Surgeon-General, late Central Provinces.—Joined the Bengal medical service, Dec., 1854; arrived in India, 31st Jan., 1855; served under the military department till Aug., 1859, including service with the Oudh field force in 1855, and at the relief of Azamgarh, 1858 (medal), and subsequently in the N.W. Provinces as civil assistant surgeon and civil surgeon in various stations and superintendent of jail, and held medical charge of the lieutenant-governor's camp in 1869-70, and again, 1872 and 1873; brigade-surgeon, Nov., 1879; deputy surgeon-general, Central Provinces, Jan., 1884; retired, Jan., 1889.

LOCH, WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, March, 1866; served in the 104th Foot; joined the Bombay staff corps, May, 1868, and served subsequently in the Sind horse; assistant political superintendent, Sawantwari, May, 1877; assistant to general superintendent of operations for suppression of thuggee and dacoitee, 1878; 2nd assistant to governor-general's agent, Indore, 1879; assistant at Banswarra and Pertabgurh, 1880; during the Afghan war was on political duty with Major-General Sir R. Phayre's force, marching to the relief of Kandahar (medal); 2nd assistant agent, Baluchistan, in charge of Pishin valley, Nov., 1880; acting assistant resident, Western Rajputana, and boundary settlement officer in Marwar, Dec., 1881; confirmed, April, 1889; acting political agent, Bikanir, April, 1894.

LOCH, WILLIE WALKER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Trinity College, Glenalmond, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1868, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; acting principal of the Raja's school, and tutor and governor of the Gaekwar, Baroda, April-Nov., 1880; first assistant collector and railway magistrate, Oct., 1882; junior collector and magistrate, Sept., 1888; senior collector and magistrate, Nov., 1890; acting commissioner of customs, &c., Bombay, May, 1891; also British delegate for the Portuguese-Indian treaty; acting commissioner, northern division, April-July, 1892; collector, &c., Nasik, and political agent, Surgana, May, 1894.

LOGAN, ALEXANDER COCHRANE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1881, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer; officiating under secretary to government in the judicial, political and educational departments, and secretary to legislative council, April, 1890, to Jan., 1892.

LOGAN, ROBERT, late Indian C.S.—Educated at the Royal Academy, Inverness, and University College, London; matriculated at the London University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 28th Oct., 1869; served in the Central Provinces from 1869 to 1881, in the grade of assistant commissioner, and officiated as district superintendent of police, March, 1872, inspector of schools, Dec., 1873, to March, 1874, and registrar of the judicial commissioner's court,

April, 1874, to Dec., 1876; from April to Nov., 1881, served in the financial department in Bengal as deputy accountant-general and officiating accountant-general; appointed assistant comptroller-general, Sept., 1881; acted as under-secretary, government of India, finance and commerce department, from Nov., 1881, and was confirmed in this appointment, July, 1883; officiated as accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, and deputy commissioner of paper currency, Aug.-Oct., 1883, and as deputy accountant-general, Feb., 1884, and again in 1885; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, March, 1884; officiating deputy accountant-general, Bengal, Sept., 1884; officiating accountant-general, Punjab, March, 1885; deputy accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Dec., 1885; officiating accountant-general, Punjab, April, 1887; confirmed, Oct., 1889; accountant-general, Bombay, and commissioner of the issue of state paper currency, March, 1890; on special duty in Kashmir, 1891; officiating comptroller and auditor-general, April, 1892; retired, Aug., 1894.

LOGAN, WILLIAM, late Madras C.S.—Educated at Musselburgh Grammar School, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Madras, as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector and acting district and sessions judge; collector and magistrate of Malabar, March, 1876; on special duty, Feb., 1881, as commissioner for inquiring into certain important matters in the Moplah districts; fellow of University of Madras, March, 1881; member of forest draft bill committee, March, 1882; acting member, board of revenue, Nov., 1882; acting resident of Travancore and Cochin, May, 1883 to Feb., 1884; on special duty at Madras in connection with report on land tenures of Malabar, Feb., 1884, and again in connection with the Attipadi valley suit, July, 1884; retired, 1888; has published a collection of treaties, engagements, &c., relating to British affairs in Malabar, 1879; and edited Mr. Græme's glossary of Malayalam words and phrases, 1882.

LOKENDRA NATH PALIT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at University College, London, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1886, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1893; officiating district and sessions judge, March, 1894.

LONGE, FRANCIS BACON, Captain, R.E., Survey of India Department.—First commission, 2nd Feb., 1876; assistant superintendent, survey department, March, 1880; deputy superintendent, April, 1888; served in Afghan war, 1879-81, and with the Suakim expeditionary force, 1885.

LORD, ROBERT JOHN CORNWALLIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Peter's School, Weston-super-Mare, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

LORD, WALTER FREWEN, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at King's College School, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive

examination of 1879; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1881, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; retired, Feb., 1889.

LORIMER, JOHN GORDON, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Dundee Institution, High School, Dundee, Edinburgh University, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

LOVE, HENRY DAVISON, Major, R.E., Madras Education Department.—Educated at University College, Bath, and Royal Military Academy, Woolwich; lieutenant, R.E., 12th September, 1872; acted as inspector of schools for science and art department, 1875-76; admitted to Madras establishment, Oct., 1876; arrived in India, 5th December, 1876; first assistant master, civil engineering college, July, 1877; on special duty in connection with Madras harbour works, Dec., 1877; served subsequently in the public works department, and acted as deputy consulting engineer for railways in 1878 and 1879, and as deputy chief engineer and under secretary to government, irrigation branch, June-Nov., 1879; assistant examiner of accounts, Nov., 1879; principal, civil engineering college, Jan., 1880; fellow of Madras University, March, 1881; member of committee in connection with scheme of technical education, Sept., 1885; compiled a 'Manual of Hydraulics,' 1886.

LOVE, ROBERT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Royal Academy, Belfast, Queen's College, Galway, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 16th Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

LOVETT, BERESFORD, Major-Gen., C.B., C.S.I., R.E., late Military Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, June, 1858; arrived in India, Sept., 1860; joined the public works department, April, 1861, and served in the Punjab till Sept., 1866, when he was transferred to the Persian telegraph department; assistant commissioner, Seistan boundary commission, Sept., 1870; rejoined public works department as executive engineer, Sept., 1872; C.S.I., Feb., 1873; attached to Bori Valley field force, Nov., 1877 to Jan., 1878; served in the Afghan war, 1878-79; transferred to Bengal, Feb., 1881; British consul at Astrabad, Sept., 1880 to Jan., 1883; superintending engineer, Bengal, 1885; transferred to military works department, April, 1886; commanding engineer, Hazara field force, Sept., 1888; C.B., 1889.

LOVETT, HARRINGTON VERNEY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Sherborne, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1884, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner; joint magistrate, Dec., 1893.

LOW, CHARLES ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Rugby, and Wadham College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 19th Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

LOWIS, CECIL CHAMPAIN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Newton College, Devonshire, Government Gymnasium, Göttingen, and Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open com-

petition of 1885; arrived in Burma, 8th Jan., 1888, and served as assistant commissioner and registrar to judicial commissioner, Burma; under secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1891; in 1894 acted as revenue secretary.

LOWIS, EDMOND ELLIOT, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 1st Feb., 1857; assistant magistrate and collector, Aug., 1858; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Sept., 1861; deputy commissioner, Western Doorsa, Oct., 1867; deputy collector of customs, Calcutta, Oct., 1867; magistrate and collector, Maldah, Dec., 1867; officiating civil and sessions judge, Aug., 1868; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Aug., 1872; commissioner, Chittagong division, March, 1875; officiating secretary, board of revenue, Jan., 1879; officiating opium agent, Behar, Jan. to Nov., 1879; commissioner, Dacca division, Dec., 1885, and in the Rajshahye division, Oct., 1886; retired, Dec., 1891.

LOWNDES, THOMAS, Major-General, I.S.C., late Police Department, Burma.—Joined the Madras army, and arrived in India, Dec., 1854; served in the Burma police department from March, 1861, as superintendent; on special mission to Chiengme, March-May, 1871; political agent, Bhamo, May, 1872, to Oct., 1873; 1st grade, superintendent, May, 1875; inspector-general of police, British Burma, Jan., 1876; appointed to special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; retired, Aug., 1887.

LUARD, CHARLES HENRY, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—First commission as lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1855; joined the public works department as assistant engineer, Lower Provinces, Nov., 1857; deputy superintendent, Western Jumna canal, N.W. Provinces, Jan., 1859; assistant principal, Thomason College, 1863; assistant secretary, government of India, public works department, officiating as deputy consulting engineer for railways, Bengal, March, 1865; deputy superintendent, Ganges canal, 1865; confirmed as deputy consulting engineer, Bengal, Oct., 1867; officiating under secretary, government of India, railway branch, Oct., 1873 to 1875; superintending engineer, 3rd grade, June, 1874; officiating mint master, Calcutta, July to Oct., 1876; officiating consulting engineer, Lucknow, 1877; consulting engineer for guaranteed railways, Lahore, 1884, and subsequently at Calcutta, Dec., 1885; chief engineer, 2nd class, May, 1886; 1st class, March, 1887; while on leave, in 1887, deputed to attend railway conference at Milan; consulting engineer for railways, Dec., 1889; retired, Jan., 1890.

LUCAS, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1883, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, forest settlement officer, and W. Bhil agent.

LUCAS, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Clifton College, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1889; employed in Sind from Nov., 1891.

LUCKSTEDT, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason

College; appointed, 10th Oct., 1871, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, May, 1884.

LUDLOW, EDMOND SAMUEL, C.I.E., Colonel, I.S.C., Hyderabad Administration.—Served with the Madras native infantry from 1858 to 1864, and with the Saurashtra field division in 1859; from Oct., 1864, served in Mysore and Coorg as assistant superintendent; judge of the small cause court, Bangalore, 1874 and 1875; assistant commissioner, June, 1874; cantonment magistrate, Secunderabad, Sept., 1881; services lent to H.H. the Nizam's government, and appointed judge of the suburban court, Hyderabad, Feb., 1884; inspector general of police to the Nizam's government, April, 1884; deputy commissioner, May, 1887.

LUGARD, HENRY IBBETSON, Colonel, I.S.C., late Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, Dec., 1853; was employed under the military department till 1864, also serving in the Madras police in 1857 and 1860-61; from Jan., 1864, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner; acted as superintendent of the school of industry, Jubbulpore, May to Sept., 1869; cantonment magistrate, Jubbulpore, March, 1873; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1877; reverted to military duty, Jan., 1893.

LUKE, STEPHEN PAGET WALTER VYVYAN, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as assistant superintendent, 16th Jan., 1868; arrived in India, 29th Feb., 1868, and served in Bengal, Madras, and N.W. Provinces; had charge of field telegraph with Peshawar Valley force, Nov., 1878, and with Afghanisthan force, Sept., 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1880; superintendent, April, 1880; on special duty in director-general's office in connexion with field telegraphs, Jan., 1887; officiated as director, construction branch, in 1887 and 1888; has held charge of various divisions, including Madras, July, 1889; on special duty, Calcutta, Jan., 1890; director, construction branch, April, 1890; officiating deputy director-general of telegraphs, April to July, 1892.

LUMSDEN, JOHN JAMES FOOTE, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1856; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1856; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant settlement officer, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, Gorakhpur, July, 1874; on special duty to Naini Tal, July-Sept., 1875; commissioner, Fyzabad division, Dec., 1879, and subsequently of the Benares division, and agent to the governor-general; senior member of board of revenue, Nov., 1889; member of the lieutenant-governor's legislative council, Jan., 1891; retired, Nov., 1891.

LUMSDEN, SIR PETER STARK, General, G.C.B., C.S.I., late Council of India.—Ensign, Bengal army, Dec., 1847; assistant executive engineer, 1849; served with the force employed in various expeditions against the frontier tribes in 1852-56; deputy assistant quartermaster-general at the action of Punghao, 15th April, 1852; served in the expeditions against the Boree Afreedees in 1853; against the Mohmund tribe in 1854; and the Meranzaie tribe in 1855; and in the Meranzaie and Kooran expeditions of Nov., 1856; on a special military mission to Afghanistan, 1857-1858 (medal and clasp); assistant quartermaster-

general, Sept., 1858; accompanied China expedition in 1860, and was present at actions of Sinho and Tangchow, assault and capture of Taku forts and advance on Pekin (medal, 2 clasps, and brevet of major); deputy quartermaster-general, April, 1863; with Bhootan field force in 1865 (clasp); quartermaster-general, May, 1868; officiating resident, Hyderabad, 1872; adjutant-general, Sept., 1874; chief of the staff, 1879; member of council of India, 1st Dec., 1883, to 1st Dec., 1893; Afghan boundary commissioner, Sept., 1884, to June, 1885.

LUSHINGTON, EDWARD HARBORD, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1841; arrived in India, 22nd June, 1841; served in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was appointed magistrate of Chittagong in 1847, and afterwards of Patna; deputy collector of government sea customs, Calcutta, 1855; collector, Nuddea, 1856; junior secretary, board of revenue, Lower Provinces, 1857; acting commissioner of the Burdwan, Manbhum, and Singbhum district, 1857-58 (Mutiny medal); commissioner of revenue and circuit, Nuddea, 1861; secretary to government of Bengal, 1861; secretary to government of India in the financial department, government director of Bank of Bengal, and member of mint committee, 1863; retired, 1871.

LUSON, HEWLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Olave's, Southwark, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1882; arrived in India, 30th Dec., 1884, and served as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; officiating under secretary to government, judicial &c. departments, Dec., 1890; confirmed, Oct., 1891; acting under-secretary to government of India, home department, April-Dec., 1892, and was appointed to that post, March, 1893.

LUTTMAN-JOHNSON, HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1867; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; officiating private secretary to lieutenant-governor of Bengal, April, 1872; acting under secretary, government of Bengal, department of scarcity and relief, Oct., 1873, to Feb., 1874; transferred to Assam, Feb., 1874, as deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, and secretary to chief commissioner; 1st grade, deputy commissioner, July, 1879; judge and commissioner, Assam Valley districts, May, 1886; retransferred to Bengal as commissioner, Nov., 1892.

LYALL, SIR ALFRED COMYNS, K.C.B., K.C.I.E., D.C.L., late Bengal C.S., Council of India.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1855; arrived in India, 2nd Jan., 1856; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; transferred to Central Provinces, April, 1864, and served as deputy commissioner, officiating secretary to chief commissioner, and officiating commissioner of the Nagpur division; transferred to Hyderabad assigned districts, July, 1867, and appointed commissioner, West Berar, Aug., 1867; secretary to the govern-

ment of India, home department, March, 1873; from Nov., 1874, served in the foreign department of the government of India, and was appointed resident, 1st class, and agent to the governor-general in Rajputana, and chief commissioner of Ajmere; on special duty in England, Aug., 1876; secretary to government of India, foreign department, April, 1878; on special political duty in Kabul, March, 1880, and again in Sept., 1880; K.C.B., May, 1881; lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces, and chief commissioner of Oudh, 17th April, 1882; C.I.E., May, 1882; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retired, Dec., 1887; appointed member of the council of India, 17th Jan., 1888; D.C.L. (Oxon.), 1889; author of 'Rajputana Gazetteer,' 'Berar Gazetteer,' 'Asiatic Studies,' 'Manual of the Law of the Hyderabad Assigned Districts,' 'Life of Warren Hastings,' 'Verses written in India,' 'Natural Religion in India' (Rede Lecture for 1891), and 'The Rise of the British Dominion in India.'

LYALL, CHARLES JAMES, M.A., LL.D., C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S.—Educated at King's College School, King's College, London, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiated at various times as under secretary to government, and as junior secretary, Oct., 1872; assistant under secretary to government of India, foreign department, April-June, 1872; from April, 1873, served under the government of India in the department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, and was appointed under secretary to government, Sept., 1873, and was placed on special duty, Sept., 1878; C.I.E., Jan., 1880; transferred to Assam as officiating secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1880; officiating judge and commissioner, Assam Valley districts, 1883, and July, 1888; secretary to chief commissioner, Oct., 1883; officiating secretary to government of India, revenue and agricultural department, Feb.-Nov., 1886, and in the home department, Aug., 1889; on special duty to report on penal settlement, Port Blair, Dec., 1889; secretary to government of India, home department, Nov., 1890; C.S.I., June, 1893; officiating chief commissioner of Assam, July-Oct., 1894; author of article on 'Hindustani Literature' in Encyclopædia Britannica, 1880; 'Sketch of the Hindustani Language,' 1880; 'Translations of ancient Arabic Poetry,' 1885; and 'Guide to the transliteration of Hindu and Muhammadan names in the Bengal army,' 1885.

LYALL, DAVID ROBERT, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and magistrate and collector; officiating commissioner, Dacca division, March, 1878, and Presidency division, Feb., 1882; inspector-general of police, March, 1883; officiating commissioner of Chittagong, Oct., 1884; confirmed, Aug., 1887; officiated as political officer with the Lushai expeditionary force in 1889; C.S.I., Jan., 1891; member of revenue board, May, 1892; member of Bengal legislative council, June, 1893;

on special duty in military department, April, 1894.

LYALL, FRANK FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 11th Oct., 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

LYALL, SIR JAMES BROADWOOD, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury in 1858; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, settlement officer, and deputy commissioner; settlement commissioner, Mooltan and Derajat, March, 1874; financial commissioner, Punjab, Jan., 1879; resident in Mysore and chief commissioner of Coorg, Dec., 1883; also judicial commissioner of Coorg; graded in the political department as resident, 1st class; lieut.-governor of the Punjab, April, 1887, to Nov., 1892, when he retired from the service; K.C.S.I., June, 1888; G.C.I.E., May, 1892; member of the royal commission on opium, Sept., 1893.

LYLE, DAVID RANKEN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at the Academical Institution, Coleraine, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1893, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

LYLE, HENRY WARD, LL.B., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Winchester, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant commissioner; assistant magistrate and collector, July, 1891; joint magistrate, Oct., 1891; deputy superintendent, family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April-July, 1892.

LYNCH, SIDNEY S., late Medical Department, Bengal.—From 16th Nov., 1858, served in Assam as civil medical officer, and inspector of landing of native labourers, and from Jan., 1866, in Bengal, as superintendent, presidency jail; superintendent of the Alipore jail, and ex-officio deputy inspector-general of jails, Lower Provinces, Jan., 1871; officiating health officer, port of Calcutta, May, 1879; confirmed in this appointment, April, 1882; retired, 1889.

LYON, ISIDORE BERNADOTTE, C.I.E., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., M.R.C.S., L.S.A., F.I.C., late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical service, 31st March, 1865; arrived in India, 1st March, 1866; acting professor of anatomy and curator of museum, Grant medical college, March, 1866; professor of chemistry, Grant medical college, and chemical analyser to government, Jan., 1867; in addition to these appointments was also third physician at the Sir Jamsetji Jijibhoj hospital, July, 1867, to Oct., 1872, professor of medical jurisprudence, Grant medical college, from July, 1868, and professor of chemistry at the Elphinstone college from Dec., 1875; on special duty conducting petroleum experiments, Oct.-Nov., 1882; brigade surgeon, Sept., 1887; acting principal, Grant medical college, June to Dec., 1888; C.I.E., May, 1889; retired, April, 1892.

LYON, PERCY COMYN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King's School, Bruton, and Oriel

College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 1st Jan., 1884; served in Assam as assistant commissioner and personal assistant to the chief commissioner; officiated as private secretary to lieutenant-governor of Bengal, Dec., 1888, and again in 1890; under secretary to government of Bengal, judicial, political and appointment departments, June, 1890; officiating under secretary to the government of India, revenue and agricultural department, April, 1891; assistant settlement officer, Cuttack, Nov., 1891; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1891; employed on N. Behar survey, as assistant superintendent, Oct., 1892.

LYS, WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Bath College, and Brasenose College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

LYTTELTON, HON. NEVILLE GERALD, Col., Rifle Brigade.—1st commission, ensign, Rifle Brigade, 17th Jan., 1865; major, Feb., 1882; col. (army), Nov., 1886; A.D.C. to lord lieutenant of Ireland, Jan., 1869, to Oct., 1873; served in the Jowaki expedition of 1877 (medal with clasp); private secretary to secretary of state for war, April, 1880, to Aug., 1882; A.D.C. to chief of staff, expeditionary force, Egypt, Aug.-Oct., 1882, including battle of Tel-el-Kebir (medal with clasp, bronze star, brevet lieutenant-colonel, 4th class Osmanieh); assistant military secretary to governor of Gibraltar, Jan., 1883, to March, 1885; military secretary on personal staff of governor of Bombay, 31st March, 1885, to 11th April, 1890.

MACAULIFFE, MICHAEL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Newcastle School, Limerick, Springfield College, and Queen's College, Galway; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 9th Feb., 1864, and served as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Dec., 1882; divisional judge, Nov., 1884; retired, April, 1893; author of 'Life of Baba Nanak,' and of articles on the Sikh religion in the 'Calcutta Review,' 1880-81.

MACAUSLAND, REDMOND CONYNGHAM SAMUEL, Major, I.S.C., Judicial Department, Punjab.—Lieutenant, 13th Nov., 1872; arrived in India, 9th March, 1873, and served in the military department, and in the Punjab as cantonment magistrate; 1st class cantonment magistrate, Dec., 1887.

MACCARTHY, The Ven. WELBORE, Ecclesiastical Department, Bengal.—Appointed junior chaplain, 20th Dec., 1873; senior chaplain, Dec., 1883; officiated as archdeacon of Calcutta in 1881, and again from Dec., 1891; confirmed in that post, Aug., 1892.

MACCARTIE, CHARLES FALKINER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Manchester Grammar School, and Brasenose College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay till Nov., 1872, and subsequently in Madras as assistant magistrate and collector, acting principal assistant, additional sessions judge, and acting collector, magistrate, and agent, and was placed on

special settlement duty, Nov., 1882; transferred permanently to the Madras civil service, Jan., 1882; officiating postmaster-general, Madras, April-Dec., 1883; acting collector of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants, May, 1884; acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, Nov., 1884; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Chingleput, Feb., 1885; acting secretary, board of revenue, Dec., 1885, to May, 1886; collector and magistrate, Jan., 1889; private secretary to governor of Madras, Dec., 1891.

MACCARTIE, FREDERICK FITZGERALD, Surgeon-Major, M.B., B.Ch. (Dub.), Bombay Medical Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1878, and arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1878; served on general military and jail duty and as deputy sanitary commissioner; appointed health officer of port of Bombay, June, 1884; served under the government of India in the finance department as assay probationer in Bombay in 1886 and 1889; reverted to his appointment as health officer, Jan., 1890; surgeon-major, March, 1890; acting deputy assay master, Bombay mint, in 1891, 1892, and 1893.

MACDONALD, DENIS PETER, M.D., Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., Port Blair Administration.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1873, and officiated as medical officer with various regiments; on special duty in Egypt, Aug.-Nov., 1883; medical officer, 11th Bengal Lancers, Nov., 1883; 2nd Gurkhas, July, 1887; served with the field hospital, Lushai expedition, Nov., 1889; senior medical officer, Port Blair, July, 1890.

MACDONALD, DONALD, Colonel, I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commission, Bengal infantry, Oct., 1853; arrived in India, Dec., 1853; appointed to the survey department, revenue branch, Dec., 1860; revenue surveyor, Jan., 1865; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, April, 1866; promoted to 2nd grade, Jan., 1878, and 1st grade, Jan., 1887; in charge of the N.W. frontier survey, 1879-1884; officiating assistant surveyor-general, March, 1886; confirmed, Sept., 1887; reverted to military duty, 1890.

MACDONALD, ROBERT GRAHAM, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Served in the subordinate accounts establishment, Oct., 1863, to Dec., 1867; examiner of railway accounts, Oudh, Dec., 1867; auditor, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway accounts, Jan., 1870; examiner, railway accounts, Madras, 1875; W. Rajputana railway, 1876; assistant accountant-general, Calcutta, Jan., 1878; examiner of accounts, Punjab, 1881; N.W. Provinces and Oudh, 1882; examiner of military works accounts, 1883, inspector of public works accounts, 1884; officiated as deputy accountant-general, and under secretary to government, Nov., 1885, to Feb., 1886, and April, 1886, to March, 1887, and again May, 1887; examiner, 2nd class, in the accounts establishment, May, 1886; examiner, 1st class, Oct., 1889; officiating accountant-general, public works department, and deputy secretary to government of India, accounts branch, May, 1888, and April, 1890; deputy accountant-general and under-secretary, Sept., 1888; president of public works accounts committee, Oct., 1888; and of the railway accounts committee, Oct., 1890; officiating accountant-general, public works department, and deputy secretary to government of India, Sept., 1891; confirmed, July, 1893.

MACDONELL, ALEXANDER, M.A., Bengal Education Department.—Educated at Aberdeen University, and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; joined the department, 30th Aug., 1879, and served in succession as professor at Patna, Dacca, Presidency, and Hugli colleges; professor, Sibpur civil engineering college, Nov., 1890.

MACDONNELL, SIR ANTONY PATRICK, M.A., K.C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Queen's College, Galway; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1864; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1865; assistant magistrate and collector, Aug., 1866; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1874; on special duty in the Bengal secretariat, May, 1875; officiating magistrate and collector, 1875; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, May, 1881, and again, Jan.—April, 1882; officiating secretary, government of Bengal, revenue and general departments, Sept., 1881, and again, April, 1882; confirmed as secretary, government of Bengal, revenue and general departments, July, 1884; lieutenant-governor's council, Nov., 1884; officiating secretary, government of India, home department, March, 1886; confirmed in this appointment, March, 1887; C.S.I., June, 1888; officiating chief commissioner, Burma, Sept., 1889; chief commissioner, Central Provinces, Nov., 1890; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1893; acting lieutenant-governor of Bengal, May—Nov., 1893; member of governor-general's council, 30th Nov., 1893; author of 'Food Grain Supply and Famine Relief in Behar and Bengal,' 1876.

MACDOUGALL, JAMES WILLIAM, Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, June, 1858; arrived in India, 24th Sept., 1858; from Feb., 1864, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner; acted at various times as cantonment magistrate of Kamptee, and as small cause court judge of Nagpur, April, 1871, to March, 1873; held charge of the model farm, Nagpur, in addition to other duties, 1873–79; deputy commissioner, June, 1880.

MACEWEN, ROBERT SUTHERLAND TAYLOR, B.L., Bengal Judicial Department.—Joined the service as sub-assistant commissioner, Sonthal commission, Sept., 1862; officiating assistant commissioner, May, 1863; clerk of the Calcutta court of small causes, July, 1866; served from Dec., 1870, as judge, Calcutta court of small causes, and was promoted to 2nd judge, Oct., 1884; served temporarily as recorder of Rangoon on various occasions; published 'The Practice of the Presidency Courts of Small Causes under Act XV. of 1882,' 1883.

MACFARLAN, JOHN, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury, 1857; arrived in India, 21st Dec., 1857, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, Satara, till 1860; deputy collector and magistrate, in Sind, 1860–61; second assistant collector and magistrate at Poona and Dharmwar, 1862 to 1864; acting collector and magistrate, Kanara, April, 1866; first assistant collector and magistrate, Nasik, July, 1869; acted as postmaster-general, Bombay, 1870–72, and from Feb., 1873, and was appointed postmaster-general, Madras, in 1875; officiating postmaster-general, Bengal, April, 1877, to Dec., 1878; retired, Dec., 1878.

MACGEORGE, HENRY JOHN, late Central Provinces Commission.—Joined the service as deputy superintendent of Jhansi, 10th June, 1854;

deputy collector, Betul, Nov., 1856; assistant commissioner, Wardha, Dec., 1860; in charge of settlement duties, Wardha and Chanda, Dec., 1862, to April, 1863; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1877; retired, March, 1890.

MAGREGOR, JAMES LISTER LAIRD, Forest Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1871; arrived, 16th Jan., 1872, and served as assistant conservator and deputy conservator of forests; conservator, 3rd grade, Dec., 1886; 2nd grade, May, 1891; 3rd grade (new scale), April, 1892.

MACIVER, STEWART WILLIAM GRAHAM IVER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 20th Dec., 1892, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

MACIVOR, CHARLES VERNON, M.A., L.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 23rd Dec., 1867, and posted to Central Provinces; transferred to state railways, Jan., 1870; executive engineer, April, 1875; officiating engineer-in-chief, Rajputana-Malwa railway, May, 1894.

MACIVOR, IVAR, C.I.E., B.A., Major, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—Sub-lieutenant, 18th Hussars, Oct., 1872; arrived in India, March, 1873; 1st regiment, Central India Horse, Aug., 1877; assistant to governor-general's agent, Central India, Oct., 1878; assistant to political resident, Persian Gulf, Feb., 1880; 1st assistant to governor-general's agent, Baluchistan, Jan., 1885; political agent, Zohar, Jan., 1890; officiating revenue commissioner for Baluchistan, April, 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894.

MACKAY, DUNCAN LACHLAN MACPHERSON, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Madras College, and University, St. Andrew's; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 8th Nov., 1875, and was appointed assistant commissioner at Jubbulpore; transferred to Burma, July, 1876; 1st grade assistant commissioner, Aug., 1886; cantonment magistrate, Rangoon, May, 1888; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1892.

MACKENZIE, SIR ALEXANDER, B.A., K.C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educated at King Edward VI.'s Grammar School, Birmingham, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived, 11th Dec., 1862; assistant magistrate and collector, Shahabad, Sept., 1863; from Feb., 1866, to Dec., 1873, served as under secretary, junior secretary, and officiating secretary to government of Bengal; on famine duty, Dec., 1873; officiating secretary, board of revenue, Nov., 1875, and again in 1877; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Moorshedabad, July, 1876; also in charge of office of agent to governor-general; junior secretary, board of revenue, May, 1877; officiating secretary, government of Bengal, general and revenue departments, July, 1877; secretary, government of Bengal, financial department, Oct., 1877; lieutenant-governor's council, Jan., 1879; secretary, government of India, home department, April, 1882; C.S.I., May, 1886; chief commissioner of Central Provinces, March, 1887; chief commissioner of Burma, Dec., 1890; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1891; author of 'History of the Relations of the Government with the Hill Tribes of the N.E. Frontier of Bengal.'

MACKENZIE, GORDON THOMSON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at King William's College, Isle of Man, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 19th Nov., 1869, and served in various districts of Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; assistant commissioner, Nilgiris, Aug., 1881; acting collector and magistrate, Kistna, Sept., 1881; head assistant collector, Nilgiris, Feb., 1882; acting registrar, high court, July, 1883; acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, Oct., 1883, and acting secretary, Nov., 1884; president of the municipal commission, Jan., 1885; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Nov., 1886; acting district and sessions judge, Kistna, Nov., 1887; confirmed, Oct., 1888; compiled district manual of Kistna.
