MACKENZIE, KENNETH JAMES LOCH, C.I.E., Colonel, I.S.C., Berar Administration.—Joined Madras artillery from Addiscombe, 11th June, 1858; from April, 1862, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts, as assistant commissioner, assistant conservator of forests, and deputy commissioner; judicial commissioner, July, 1889; commissioner, March, 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1893.

MACKENZIE, THOMAS DUNCAN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in the Bombay presidency as assistant collector and magistrate; secretary, Baroda inquiry commission, Nov., 1873; from Nov., 1875, acted as under secretary to government in the revenue, financial, and general departments, and was confirmed in this appointment, Nov., 1877; member of civil and military examination committee, April–Oct., 1879; from Nov., 1879, served as acting collector and magistrate, and was placed on special duty at Baroda, March–Aug., 1880, in connection with the settlement of the affairs of Mir Zulfiqar Ali; acting junior collector of salt revenue, Bombay, Nov., 1882; junior collector and magistrate, Broach, May, 1884; collector, magistrate and political agent, Khandeish, Feb., 1886; acted as chief secretary to government of Bombay for various periods from May, 1886; commissioner, southern division, May, 1889; chief secretary to government, revenue, financial and general departments, May, 1891; acting commissioner of customs, &c., Bombay, March, 1893; additional member of governor's council, April, 1893.

MACKENZIE, SIR WILLIAM, Inspector-General of Hospitals, M.D., K.C.B., C.S.I., late Madras Medical Service.—A.M. of King's College, Aberdeen, 1830; M.R.C.S., London, 1832; M.D., University of Aberdeen, 1856; assistant surgeon in the Madras medical service, 1835; served with the 8th light cavalry, Madras, 1837–39, and with the army of His Highness the Nizam of Hyderabad, 1839–62; was superintendent of the Bolarum Medical School, 1842–44; served with the Ellickpoor field force against the insurgent Rohillas in 1841, and again in Nov., 1851, with the cavalry field force, under Brigadier W. Mayne; staff-surgeon with the Central India field force, 1857–58, and with the Berar field force, Nov., 1858, to March, 1859, and was present at numerous engagements; deputy inspector-general of hospitals, Mysore division, March, 1863; inspector-general, Aug.,

1866; inspector-general and head of the medical department, March, 1867; retired, Aug., 1871; appointed honorary physician to the Queen, July, 1867; author of a treatise on the medical topography of Ellickpoor, and papers on the Indian Materia Medica, and has translated into Hindustani 'Gregory's Practice of Physic,' and 'Thomson's Spectus of the British Pharmacopoeia.'

MACKIE, ANDREW WALLACE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Universities of Edinburgh and St. Andrew's; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 20th Oct., 1873; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; on famine duty, March, 1874, and again, in Nellore, March, 1877; assistant to director-general of statistics, Feb., 1875; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1884; employed in the Punjab in 1889 as divisional judge; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1890.

MACKINTOSH, HENRY DONALD, Store Department, India Office.—Writer, 27th June, 1874; clerk specially attached, 25th Sept., 1892.

MACKINTOSH, JAMES SIMON, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at the Royal Academy, Inverness, Edinburgh University, and Oriel College, Oxford; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant secretary, and officiating under secretary to government; private secretary to the lieutenant-governor, Feb.–April, 1874; officiating secretary to government, N.W. Provinces, Aug.–Nov., 1875; junior secretary to government, July, 1876; secretary, board of revenue, Aug., 1877; magistrate and collector, Jan., 1886; officiating commissioner, Lucknow, April, 1894.

MACLAGAN, EDWARD DOUGLAS, M.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Winchester and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1883; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; officiated as under secretary to government of Punjab in 1888, 1889, and 1890; superintendent of census operations in the Punjab, April, 1890; officiating under secretary to government of India, revenue and agricultural department, Aug., 1892; confirmed, April, 1893.

MACLARAN, FRANCIS BLANNEY, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1872, as local fund engineer, Thana; assistant engineer, April, 1875; executive engineer, Oct., 1880.

MACLEAN, CHARLES SMITH, C.B., C.I.E., Colonel, I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, 14th Dec., 1853; served in the Indian mutiny, 1857, China war, 1860, Afghan war, 1878–80, and Mashud-Waziri expedition, 1881; consul-general at Meshed, March, 1889; agent to governor-general, and consul-general for Khorassan and Seistan, Nov., 1889; returned to Europe, Dec., 1891.

MACLEAN, FREDERICK GURR, M.I.E.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Appointed to the department as assistant superintendent, 1st Sept., 1868; studied submarine

telegraphy in England for four months; arrived in India, Feb., 1869; held charge of various sub-divisions, and of construction of line on Kandahar state railway, 1879; held charge of various divisions, and was graded class IV., grade 3, Oct., 1887, and grade 2, April, 1890; personal assistant to director of telegraphs, April, 1890; in charge, Sind and Baluchistan division, Feb., 1892; in charge, check office, March, 1893.

MACLEAN, LONDON FRANCIS, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 12th Aug., 1871, and posted to state railways; employed in Bengal on famine relief works, 1873-74; and subsequently on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, Nov., 1880; C.I.E., May, 1892.

MACLEANE, CHARLES DONALD, Mus. Doc., M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Bath Grammar School, Shrewsbury Grammar School, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived, 19th Dec., 1865; acting inspector of schools, Oct., 1866; assistant collector, Dec., 1867; acting cantonment magistrate, Sept., 1868; acted for various periods as under secretary to government of Madras, and sub-secretary to the board of revenue; on special duty in preparing annual administration report, Nov., 1876, and again, Sept., 1878; on special duty revising standing orders of the board of revenue, Oct., 1877, and again in June, 1886; under secretary to government in chief secretary's department, July, 1880; fellow of the university of Madras, March, 1881; Canarese translator to government, April, 1881; member of board of examiners, Jan., 1882; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1883; on special duty at Madras in connection with preparation of 'Manual of Administration,' Nov., 1883, and again, Aug., 1884, and 1886; sub-secretary, board of revenue, Jan., 1884; acting collector of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants, Feb., 1884; on special duty as president of the committee on Muhammadan educational endowments, Aug., 1885; Tamil translator to government, Sept., 1885; collector and magistrate, Anantapur, Feb., 1886; on special duty in the general record department, Feb., 1886, and also to collect information for the finance committee, June, 1886; collector and magistrate, Nellore, March, 1888.

MACLEOD, BANNATYNE, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Edinburgh Collegiate School, Edinburgh University, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector, and forest settlement officer; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Jan., 1889.

MACLEOD, DONALD GRANT, M.A., LL.D., B.L., Judicial Department, Burma.—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge, and Inner Temple; judge of the court of small causes, Rangoon, Sept., 1869; judge of Moulmein, March, 1876.

MACLEOD, RODERICK, M.D., Bengal Medical Service.—House physician, medical college hospital, Calcutta, 24th June, 1865; served as

medical officer at several stations, and was appointed health officer of the port of Calcutta, Oct., 1889; in addition officiated as protector of emigrants and superintendent of emigration, Jan., 1891; and was appointed to that post, March, 1892.

MACLEOD, RODERICK HENRY, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at University College, London, and London University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating cantonment magistrate, and district and sessions judge; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Dec., 1886, and 1st grade, Feb., 1890; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, July, 1889; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, April, 1891; district and sessions judge, May, 1891; legal remembrancer, March, 1894; also member and secretary of legislative council from Jan., 1894.

MACMILLAN, MICHAEL, B.A., Bombay Educational Department.—Educated at Brasenose College, Oxford; joined the service, 12th June, 1878, as professor of logic and moral philosophy, Elphinstone college; was also lecturer in these subjects at Deccan college, June-Dec., 1882; professor of English literature, Elphinstone college, April, 1892.

MACMULLEN, GEORGE READE, Captain, I.S.C., Police Department, Burma.—First commission, 11th Sept., 1876; served in the Burmese war, 1887-88 (medal and clasp), and Miranzai expedition, 1891; appointed deputy inspector-general of military police, Burma, March, 1892.

MACNABB, SIR DONALD C., K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury in 1853; served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Peshawur, 1865; commissioner, Hissar division, 1872, and subsequently in the Lahore, Rawal Pindi, and Peshawur divisions; retired, 1881; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887.

MACNABB, JAMES WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 2nd Jan., 1852, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and was promoted to deputy commissioner, Aug., 1861; commissioner and superintendent, Umballa, Nov., 1875, and afterwards in the Hissar and Delhi divisions; retired, March, 1886.

MACNAGHTEN, CHESTER, M.A., Bombay Educational Department.—From July, 1866, to Dec., 1870, held the appointment of tutor to the minor Maharajah of Durbhunga under the court of wards, Bengal; joined the Bombay educational service, 19th Dec., 1870, and served from that date as principal of the Rajkumar College, Kathiawar.

MACONACHIE, GEORGE ARCHIBALD, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.D., C.M., Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical department, 1st April, 1867; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1867; was employed under the military department till Jan., 1872, including service in the Abyssinian campaign (medal), and subsequently as ophthalmic surgeon and professor of ophthalmic medicine and surgery and comparative anatomy at the Grant medical college, Bombay; also presidency surgeon from June, 1880; acted as principal, Grant medical

college, in 1891 and 1892; appointed principal and professor of ophthalmic medicine and surgery, &c., April, 1893.

MACONACHIE, JAMES ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School and London University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 9th Jan., 1872, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, settlement officer, and officiating judicial assistant; officiating settlement secretary to financial commissioner, March, 1880; officiating postmaster-general, Punjab, July, 1884; on special duty under the government of India, foreign department, Sept.-Oct., 1885; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1887; officiated as commissioner, Lahore, July-Nov., 1893; author of 'Agricultural Proverbs of the Punjab,' and a report on the settlement of the Delhi district.

MACONOCHE, ALEXANDER FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Westminster and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1883; acting joint administrator, Rajpipla, Oct., 1886, and again in 1890; survey and settlement superintendent, Baroda, Dec., 1890.

MACONOCHE, EVAN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Sherborne School, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1889, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; acted as administrator, Sachin state, Dec., 1890, and again in May, 1891.

MACPHERSON, ANDREW KENNEDY, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Punjab Administration.—First commission, 19th Dec., 1865; arrived, 9th March, 1866; from April, 1871, served as cantonment magistrate at various stations in the Punjab, and was promoted to the 1st class, Dec., 1879; officiating in the judge advocate-general's department, May, 1886, to March, 1888; employed under government of India, home department, in 1891 and 1892; cantonment magistrate, Meen Meer, March, 1893.

MACPHERSON, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE, K.C.I.E., late Judicial and Public Department, India Office.—Educated at the University and King's College, Aberdeen, and University of Edinburgh; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1852; 1st judge of Calcutta small cause court, 1859; secretary to the government of Bengal, legislative department, 1862; officiating deputy secretary to the government of India in the home department (legislative), 1863, and appointed assistant secretary, Oct., 1864; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1865; acted as chief justice, 13th Feb. to 25th June, 1875; retired, 1877; legal adviser to the secretary of state, Nov., 1879; secretary in the judicial and public department, India office, Feb., 1882, to Sept., 1893; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889.

MACPHERSON, CHARLES GORDON WELAND, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 30th Dec., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and superintendent of police; superintending under secretary to government of Bombay in the public works department for relief purposes, Nov., 1876; on famine relief

duty in Madras, as secretary to personal assistant to the governor, Sept., 1877, to Jan., 1879; from April, 1879, acted as under secretary to government of Bombay, political and judicial departments, and secretary to the governor's council for making laws and regulations, and was confirmed in this appointment, Feb., 1881; acting private secretary to governor of Bombay, Dec., 1880, to March, 1881; acted as secretary to government, political, secret, judicial, and educational departments, in 1884 and 1885; acting special judge under the Deccan agriculturists relief act, April-July, 1885, and again in 1886; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, Shikarpur, Nov., 1885; on special duty in the political department of the secretariat, Dec., 1885; 2nd grade judge, May, 1887; 1st grade, May, 1893.

MACPHERSON, DUNCAN JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at the High School and University, Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 19th Nov., 1878; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Sept., 1883; magistrate and collector, April, 1893.

MACPHERSON, GEORGE MORISON, M.A., LL.D., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at University of Aberdeen; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1860, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, small cause court judge, and judge and sessions judge in various districts; 1st grade judge, Oct., 1880; judicial commissioner in Sind, Jan., 1885; also judge of the sadar court, Oct., 1887; acted as commissioner in 1886.

MACPHERSON, JAMES, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen, and University of Aberdeen; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; on special duty under board of revenue, July, 1875; joint magistrate, May, 1884; superintendent of Dehra Dun, Aug., 1886; deputy commissioner, July, 1890; magistrate and collector, April, 1891.

MACPHERSON, JOHN MOLESWORTH, B.L., Legislative Department, Government of India.—Educated at Westminster School; advocate of the high court, Calcutta; served from Dec., 1877, as deputy secretary to the government of India in the legislative department; officiated as secretary from Oct., 1887, to Jan., 1888, and again in 1889 and 1892; editor of 7th edition of 'Macpherson's Law of Mortgage in British India,' and compiler of 'Lists of British Enactments in force in Native States.'

MACPHERSON, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1856; served in the revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating civil and sessions judge; magistrate and collector, Nov., 1866; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1874; judge of the high court of judicature, Calcutta, 10th Jan., 1885.

MACPHERSON, WILLIAM CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1877; assistant magistrate and collector, Patna, Dec., 1877; transferred to Assam, July, 1878, and served as assistant commissioner, assistant secretary and officiating secretary to chief commissioner, and officiating inspector of schools; transferred to Bengal, Oct., 1884; under secretary to government of Bengal, Nov., 1885; officiating under secretary to government of India, revenue and agricultural department, Jan., 1888, and home department, April, 1888; joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1888; magistrate and collector, July, 1892; officiating director of land records and agriculture, Bengal, May, 1892; confirmed, Jan., 1894; author of 'An Aid to Revenue and Magisterial Duties in Bengal.'

MACRAE, JOHN KENNETH, late Burma Commission.—Joined the commission as officiating assistant commissioner, 20th July, 1860; registrar to the court of the recorder, and judge of the court of small causes, Moulmein, May, 1864; deputy commissioner, July, 1869; town magistrate, Akyab, Nov., 1874; boundary officer, Nov., 1880; 1st grade, deputy commissioner, Sept., 1883; president, Akyab municipal committee, Jan., 1885; acted as commissioner in 1885, 1887, and 1893, and as president, Rangoon municipality, in 1892; retired, April, 1894.

MACRURY, COLIN WILLIAM, Brigade Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bombay Sanitary Department.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1869; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1869, and served on general duty in Sind; deputy sanitary commissioner, Feb., 1878; deputy superintendent of census in Sind, 1881; sanitary commissioner to the government of Bombay, May, 1888.

MADDEN, JOHN MELLISH, Colonel, Bombay Infantry.—Ensign, Bombay army, Sept., 1859; employed on military duty, including service in Abyssinia in 1868, up to 1876; served subsequently as assistant cantonment magistrate, Poona; appointed cantonment magistrate, Ahmadabad, January, 1880; retired, May, 1892.

MADDOX, STUART LOCKWOOD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Fettes College, Edinburgh, and Worcester College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant settlement officer.

MADGAVKAR, GOVIND DINANATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at St. Xavier's and Elphinstone Colleges, Bombay, Bombay University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1892, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

MAGRATH, HENRY MARSH SEPTIMUS, Colonel, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 5th April, 1858; officiated as magistrate and president of the municipal commission, Bangalore, May, 1884, to March, 1885; district magistrate and president municipal commission, Bangalore, Feb., 1887; acted as civil and sessions judge in 1887.

MAGUIRE, HUGH FITZJOHN TYRREL, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Clifton College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examina-

tion of 1876; arrived, 25th Nov., 1878; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and deputy collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Jan., 1889; officiated as political agent and assistant secretary to the government of India, foreign department, Feb., 1889; on special duty in connection with census work in Calcutta, Nov., 1890; magistrate and collector, April, 1893.

MAHADEV GOVIND RANADE, Rao Bahadur, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Judicial Department, Bombay.—Served from 28th May, 1866, in the educational department, and was appointed assistant professor of English literature at the Elphinstone College, Bombay, Oct., 1868; also served in the judicial department as assistant reporter to the high court, Bombay, acting magistrate of police, acting small cause court judge, and subordinate judge (June, 1872); acting presidency magistrate, Bombay, Jan.—March, 1881; appointed under the Deccan agriculturists' relief act to inspect and supervise the lower courts in districts of Poona and Satara, Aug., 1881; judge of the court of small causes, Poona, Feb., 1884, and was in charge of duties of law lecturer, Deccan College, Jan.—April, 1885; member of a committee on public expenditure, April, 1886; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; confirmed as special judge under Deccan agriculturists' relief act, May, 1887; judge, high court, Nov., 1893.

MAHANY, HORACE GEORGE HAROLD.—Appointed clerk, 2nd class, India office, and detached for duty with the assistant military secretary for Indian affairs, horse guards, 3rd April, 1894.

MAHESH CHANDRA NYAYARATNA, MAHAMAHOPADHYAYA, C.I.E., Bengal Education Department.—Served in the education department from 10th January, 1864, as assistant professor, and professor in the Sanskrit College, Calcutta, and was appointed principal, May, 1885; author of 'Tulsi Mimansa,' 'Loopta Sambatsara,' &c.

MAITLAND, JOHN, Surgeon-Major, M.D., M.C. (Edinburgh), Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1876; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1876, and served on civil, military, and famine duty; 2nd surgeon, general hospital, Madras, Feb., 1882; professor of materia medica and pharmacy, medical college, April, 1882; surgeon-major, March, 1888; surgeon to the governor of Madras, March, 1889; civil surgeon, Coconada, Jan., 1891; acting senior medical officer, Ootacamund, April, 1891; officiating surgeon, Madras general hospital, and professor of surgery at the medical college, April, 1893; surgeon, 4th district, Madras, and professor of medical jurisprudence, medical college, Aug., 1893.

MAITLAND, PELHAM JAMES, Colonel, I.S.C., Military Secretariat, Government of India.—First commission, 2nd May, 1866; served in the Afghan war of 1878-79 (medal); deputy-assistant quartermaster-general, intelligence branch, Bengal, 1880-85; assistant quartermaster-general in the same department, 1885-87; employed with the Afghan boundary commission, 1884-86; assistant secretary, military department, government of India, Jan., 1891; deputy secretary, April, 1892.

MAITLAND, WILLIAM JAMES, C.I.E., India Office.—Educated at the Edinburgh Academy

and abroad; temporary clerk, 3rd Nov., 1866; junior clerk, July, 1867; assistant private secretary and précis writer to the secretary of state, 1878; private secretary to the secretary of state (Viscount Cross), August, 1886; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; deputy government director of Indian guaranteed railways, June, 1892.

MALLET, FREDERICK RICHARD, F.G.S., F.C.S., late Geological Survey of India.—Appointed from England as assistant in the geological survey, and joined, 12th Feb., 1859; was employed in various districts of Bengal, Central India, the Central Provinces, Assam, Sikkim, and Bhutan; keeper of the mineral collections in the museum, Oct., 1875; 1st grade officer, Jan., 1883; deputy superintendent, April, 1883; designation changed to superintendent of survey; retired, 1889; author of contributions to the survey publications.

MALLET, ROBERT TREFUSIS, late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Joined the Bombay public works department, Jan., 1868; executive engineer on the Punjab Northern state railway, Nov., 1868; superintendent of works, Ravee section, April, 1873; temporary superintending engineer, Indus Valley railway, Jan., 1877; engineer-in-chief, Indus Valley railway, April, 1879; director of stores, railway branch, Oct., 1880; engineer-in-chief, Rewari Ferozepur railway, Nov., 1881; superintending engineer, 1st class, Jan., 1883; engineer-in-chief, Ferozepur bridge, in 1885; transferred to North-Western railway as chief engineer, Jan., 1886; chief engineer, 3rd class, Dec., 1885; engineer-in-chief, Chenab bridge project, May, 1887; temporarily held office of director-general of railways, Aug., 1887; officiating consulting engineer, Sept., 1887; chief engineer, 2nd class, March, 1889; retired, March, 1892.

MALLOCK, HENRY ARCHIBALD, Colonel, I.S.C., late Indian Telegraph Department.—Lieutenant, Bengal artillery, Dec., 1853; joined the telegraph department, Jan., 1861; superintendent, 1st grade, and storekeeper, Dec., 1866; director of construction, April, 1868; deputy director-general, June, 1883; officiating director-general, Nov., 1886, to Feb., 1887, and March, 1888; reverted to military department, 1889.

MALTBV, EDWARD PENNYCUICK, Lieut.-Col., late Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, Madras infantry, Jan., 1861; arrived in India, 7th Feb., 1861; served in civil employ in the Mysore commission from Jan., 1864; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1877; small cause court judge, and town and cantonment magistrate, Bangalore, March, 1879; district magistrate and collector, and president, municipal commission, in the civil and military station of Bangalore, from March, 1881; retired, Jan., 1887.

MAN, EDWARD HORACE, C.I.E., Port Blair Administration.—Joined the service as assistant superintendent, 3rd Nov., 1869; was in charge of Nicobar Islands in 1871 and 1874, and from 1883 to 1888; officiated for various periods as deputy superintendent, and was appointed 1st assistant superintendent, Jan., 1888; deputy superintendent, April, 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; joint author of a work on the South Andaman language; has also published a monograph on 'The Aboriginal Inhabitants of the Andaman Islands,' and a 'Grammar and Dictionary of the Central Nicobarese Language.'

MANCE, SIR HENRY CHRISTOPHER, Kt., C.I.E., late Indo-European Telegraph Department.—Appointed to the Indo-European telegraph department, July, 1863; superintendent, Kurrachee, Persian Gulf section, Sept., 1866; engineer and electrician, Sept., 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1883; Kt., 1885; retired, 1886.

MANCHERJI PESTANJI KHAREGHAT, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Xavier's College, Calcutta, University College, London, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived, 8th Dec., 1884, and served as assistant collector, acting assistant judge, and acting judge and sessions judge.

MANDER, ALFRED THOMAS, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1854; 2nd lieutenant, Dec., 1855; arrived in India, 21st Dec., 1857; served in the Bombay public works department, from March, 1858, as assistant and executive engineer; superintending engineer, Nov., 1886; retired, Sept., 1889.

MANDERSON, THOMAS CLARIDGE, Colonel, R.E., late Military Works Department, Government of India.—Lieutenant, Bengal engineers, Aug., 1858; joined the public works department, July, 1860, as assistant engineer, N.W. Provinces; executive engineer, Punjab, April, 1866; transferred to military works, 1872; served in the Rawal Pindi, Meerut, and Lahore commands, and was promoted to superintending engineer, 2nd class, March, 1886; officiating inspector general, military works, April-Nov., 1886; superintending engineer, 1st class, March, 1889; chief engineer, June, 1893; retired, April, 1894.

MANISTY, GEORGE ELDON, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Ruabon Grammar School, Marlborough College, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1873, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1884; magistrate and collector, July, 1890; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, March, 1894.

MANN, GUSTAV, late Forest Department, Assam.—Assistant in government cinchona plantations, Darjeeling, Nov., 1863; assistant conservator of forests, Bengal, Dec., 1864; from Nov., 1868, served in the Assam forest department, and was promoted to conservator, 3rd grade, Sept., 1882; 2nd grade, Jan., 1888; retired, May, 1891.

MANN, JOHN, M.A., Education Department, Bengal.—Educated at Glasgow University; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1878, as professor, Presidency college; professor, Hooghly college, Jan., 1879; principal, Krishnagur college, Jan., 1883; professor, Presidency college, March, 1888; professor, Hooghly college, June, 1889; again professor, Presidency college, Nov., 1892.

MANSER, ROBERT, Surgeon-Major, Bombay Medical Service.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1877; served in the military department, and on civil and jail duty; from May, 1882, served in the European general hospital as assistant surgeon, and professor of materia medica in the Grant medical college; professor of pathology and curator of museum of that college, April, 1887; second physician of Jamsetji Jijibhow hospital and professor of medicine and therapeutics, Nov., 1888; first physician, Nov., 1889.

MANSON, ALEXANDER, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Highgate Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1864; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating district and sessions judge, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Aug., 1885; also served as second inspector of registration offices, Aug., 1876; officiating commissioner, Chittagong division, Nov., 1885, and July, 1886; retired, July, 1889.

MARDON, EVELYN JOHN, B.A., LL.B., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Clifton College, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner and assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Nov., 1893.

MARETT, JAMES RICHARD, Colonel, I.S.C., Oudh Commission.—First commission, ensign, 2nd Grenadiers, 4th Feb., 1858; served during the Jynteah and Kassia rebellion in 1862-63, and captured the Kassia rebel chief; also during the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (medal and clasp); from July, 1867, served under foreign department of government of India; assistant commissioner, Oudh, July, 1867; sub-judge, 2nd grade, Aug., 1879; and 1st grade, Dec., 1881; officiated for various periods as district judge, deputy commissioner, and judge of the small cause court, and was confirmed as district judge, March, 1887.

MARINDIN, CHARLES RANDAL, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Shrewsbury Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; on famine relief duty, Tirhoot, Feb., 1874; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1884; magistrate and collector, April, 1889.

MARJORIBANKS, NORMAN EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Bishop Cotton's School and College, Bangalore, Belfast Royal Academical Institute, Royal University of Ireland, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

MARKEY, SIR W., K.C.I.E., late Judge of the High Court, Calcutta.—Educated at Merton College, Oxford; doctor of civil law, fellow of All Souls College, fellow of Balliol College; recorder of Buckingham, 1864-66; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1866-78; vice-chancellor of the university of Calcutta, 1877-78; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889; justice of the peace for county of Oxford; author of 'Elements of Law.'

MARKHAM, ALEXANDER MACAULAY, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Down and Connor Diocesan School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1862; from Jan., 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating postmaster-general, and collector, officiating magistrate, settlement officer, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Allahabad,

Nov., 1880; member of committee for considering revised rules for Patwaris and Kanungos, Nov., 1880; member of grass supply committee under military department of government of India, Oct., 1881; officiating commissioner, Rae Bareli division, March to July, 1882; and of Jhansi division, May to Sept., 1887; district and sessions judge, April, 1889.

MARKHAM, CLEMENTS ROBERT, C.B., F.R.S.—Educated at Westminster School; appointed a naval cadet, 28th July, 1844; midshipman, July, 1846, and passed for lieutenant, R.N., Dec., 1851; served in the Arctic expedition in search of Sir John Franklin, 1850-51; explored Peru and the forests of the Eastern Andes, 1852-54; from July, 1854, served as clerk under the India board of control, and on the transfer of the government of India to the crown, in 1858, was appointed a clerk in the correspondence department of the India office; private secretary to the parliamentary under-secretary of state (Mr. T. G. Baring), 1862 to 1864; assistant secretary in the public works department, 1867-71, and in the judicial, public, and revenue department from 1871 to 1877, when he retired from the service; appointed C.B. (civil), May, 1871; F.R.S., 1873; was employed in introducing chinchona cultivation into India, 1859-66, and visited India and Ceylon; geographer to the Abyssinian expedition, 1866-67, and was present at the storming of Magdala; secretary to the Hakluyt Society, 1858 to 1886, and president from that date; secretary to the Royal Geographical Society, 1863 to 1888, and president, 1893-94; in 1874 was created by the King of Portugal a commendador of the order of Christ; and by the Emperor of Brazil a chevalier of the order of the Rose; grand prix of the Paris International Exhibition, 1867; gold medal of the Royal Geographical Society, 1888; gold medal presented by the National Congress of Peru, 1893; author of 'Franklin's Footsteps,' 1853; 'Cuzco and Lima,' 1855; 'Travels in Peru and India,' 1862; 'Quichua Grammar and Dictionary,' 1863; 'Threshold of the Unknown Region,' 1874; 'History of the Abyssinian Expedition,' 1869; 'Life of Lord Fairfax,' 'Life of Admiral Fairfax,' 'Lives of Sir Francis and Sir Horace Vere,' 'A Naval Career in the Old War,' 'History of Persia,' 1873; 'History of the War between Peru and Chili,' 'Peru,' 1880; 'The Sea Fathers,' 'Irrigation in Eastern Spain,' 1867; 'Memoir on the Indian Surveys,' 1871; 'Life of the Countess of Chinchon,' 'Peruvian Bark,' and 'Missions to Thibet,' 1877; 'Life of John Davis,' 'Life of Columbus,' 'History of Peru,' 1893; has also translated and edited various works, including 'Embassy of Clavijo to the Court of Timour,' 'Royal Commentaries of Garcilasso de la Vega,' 'Chronicle of Cieza de Leon,' 'Memoir of Alonzo Henriquez de Guzman,' and 'Report of Pascual de Andagoya,' also editor of 'Early Voyages of the East India Company,' 'Voyages of Baffin,' 'Tractatus de Globis,' &c., &c.; wrote the reports on the 'Moral and Material Progress of India' for 1871-72 and 1872-73, and edited the 'Geographical Magazine,' 1872 to 1878.

MARRIOTT, ALFRED BARROW WILSON, Police Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1866, as officiating assistant district superintendent of police; confirmed, April, 1867; promoted to district superintendent, Aug., 1870.

MARRIOTT, CHARLES RICHARDSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Wellington College, and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived, 29th Nov., 1875; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1887; officiating collector of customs, Calcutta, May, 1891; magistrate and collector, Dec., 1891; junior secretary to revenue board, March, 1892; confirmed as collector of customs, Calcutta, July, 1892.

MARRYAT, ERNEST LINDSAY, Lt.-Col., R.E., late Railway Department, Government of India.—First commission, June, 1859; probationary assistant engineer, Mhow, Bombay, Nov., 1862; assistant engineer, Central Provinces, Oct., 1864; executive engineer, Bombay public works department, Jan., 1866; assistant consulting engineer for railways, Bombay, June, 1869; consulting engineer for reclamation, Bombay, April, 1870; acted for various periods as deputy consulting engineer, Bombay; acting under secretary to government of Bombay, public works department, April, 1874; acting manager, Dhond and Manmad state railway, Dec., 1878; confirmed as deputy consulting engineer, Bombay, March, 1879; executive engineer, 1st grade, June, 1880; manager, Punjab Northern state railway, in 1st class of the state railway revenue establishment, Nov., 1880; retired, June, 1886.

MARSDEN, FREDERICK JOHN, B.L.—Clerk of the crown, 14th May, 1873; officiating magistrate, Calcutta police, and coroner, Oct., 1874; presidency magistrate and coroner, Dec., 1877; chief magistrate, Calcutta, March, 1878; also judge of the court for the trial of pilots, and of the court of survey, port of Calcutta; resigned, April, 1894.

MARSH, HENRY, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, May, 1883.

MARSHALL, CHARLES HENRY TILSON, Colonel, I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1859; from July, 1865, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, officiating cantonment magistrate, and judicial assistant; officiating private secretary to lieutenant-governor, Oct., 1878; superintendent, Chamba state, Oct., 1879; divisional judge, 3rd grade, Sept., 1886; from Dec., 1886, served under foreign department, government of India; returned to the Punjab, Nov., 1888, and was appointed divisional judge, May, 1889; author of a 'Monograph of the Capitonidæ' (with G. F. L. Marshall), 'The Game Birds of India' (with A. O. Hume), 'The Ornithology of the Hills about Murree,' and 'The Ornithology of Chamba.'

MARSHALL, GEORGE FREDERICK LEYCESTER, C.I.E., Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—First commission, June, 1860; joined the public works department, 28th March, 1864, and served in the irrigation department, N.W. Provinces; executive engineer, Sept., 1868; special assistant, irrigation branch, N.W. Provinces secretariat, 1876; officiating assistant secre-

tary to government of India, civil works branch, Oct., 1876, to May, 1877, and again in 1878; confirmed in this appointment, Sept., 1879; officiating under secretary, government of India, public works department, railway branch, Nov., 1881, to Feb., 1882; under secretary, government of India, civil works branch, June, 1882; member of committee on reorganisation of the public works department, Sept., 1883; superintending engineer, Dec., 1884; officiating superintending engineer and secretary to agent to governor-general, Rajputana, April, 1887; superintending engineer and public works secretary, Rajputana and Central India, 1888; chief engineer and secretary to government of Punjab, public works department, June, 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; author of 'A Monograph of the Capitonidæ' (with C. H. T. Marshall), 'Bird-nesting in India,' and 'The Butterflies of India, Burma, and Ceylon.'

MARSHALL, LOUIS, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Rugby, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 15th Dec., 1891, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

MARTELLI, NORTON CHARLES, Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, 15th foot, 11th Oct., 1864; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, Oct., 1870; served in civil employ from April, 1872, as assistant cantonment magistrate, Morar, and political assistant in Rajputana; guardian and tutor to Maharaj of Alwar, Aug., 1876; served in Baluchistan, 1880-81, as political officer, Jacobabad, Quetta, and the Bolan; assistant at Banswara and Pertabgurh, and superintendent of operations for control of Moghias, Oct., 1881; officiating political agent, Baghencund, 1882, and in 1886; political agent, Bhopawur, 1885; political agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, June, 1888; officiating resident, Meywar, April-July, 1893; and at Gwalior, July-Oct., 1893.

MARTIN, CAULFIELD AYLMER, B.A., LL.D., Education Department, Bengal.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed, 22nd Nov., 1864; served as professor at the Dacca college, and at the Krishnaghur college; inspector of schools, March, 1874; 2nd class, educational service, August, 1877; 1st class, Dec., 1892; acting director of public instruction, Bengal, Dec., 1892, to Dec., 1893.

MARTIN, CHARLES WILLIAM WALL, B.A., LL.D., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments from March, 1865, in various districts as assistant collector and magistrate, acting small cause court judge, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; collector and magistrate, South Canara, Nov., 1882; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1883, in Cuddapah, and afterwards in Salem; retired, April, 1889.

MARTIN, EDWARD JAMES, M.I.C.E., and F.R.I.B.A., late Public Works Department, Bengal.—Appointed by the secretary of state, May, 1859; arrived in India, 6th Sept., 1859; served in the Punjab in the buildings and roads branch as assistant engineer and executive engineer till 1869-70, when he was transferred to the N.W. Provinces, and posted to the Rajputana state

railway; officiated as engineer-in-chief, Tirhoot state railway, April, 1877; engineer-in-chief, Rangoon and Irrawaddy Valley state railway, Aug., 1877; also ex-officio manager of the line, April, 1878; transferred to Bengal, railway branch, and appointed engineer-in-chief of the Central Bengal system of state railways, Nov., 1878; superintending engineer, 3rd class, Jan., 1880; transferred to Bengal provincial branch, Jan., 1880; on special duty under the chief engineer, Bengal, as government architect, April, 1881; superintending engineer, 2nd class, Feb., 1882; deputed to Indore on special duty, Jan., 1884; superintending engineer, 1st class, Dec., 1885; officiating chief engineer, and secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, June, 1888; 2nd chief engineer, May, 1889; chief engineer and joint secretary to government of Bengal, Jan., 1890; retired, 1891.

MARTIN, GERALD WARD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Assay Master, Bombay Mint.—Ensign, 88th Foot, 22nd June, 1867; joined Roorkee College, Nov., 1869, and obtained certificate as civil engineer; admitted to the staff corps, March, 1871; from March, 1871, to July, 1878, served as assistant superintendent of survey in Assam, in the Deccan topographical survey, and in the Soane irrigation survey; transferred to the financial department for employment in the assay department, July, 1878, and acted as deputy assay master in the Calcutta and Bombay mints; appointed assay master, Bombay mint, May, 1884; also served with the Afghan expeditionary force as assistant superintendent of survey, 1879-80 (medal and clasp for Cabul), and with the Waziri expeditionary field force.

MARTIN, HENRY, M.B., Surgeon-Major, Staff of Governor of Bombay.—Surgeon, 6th March, 1880; served with the Zohb Valley expedition of 1884; appointed medical officer to Lord Harris, governor of Bombay, April, 1890.

MARTIN, JAMES WENTWORTH, Madras Public Works Department.—Arrived, 8th March, 1868, and served in the Madras presidency as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Sept., 1876; superintending engineer, April, 1893.

MARTIN, WILLIAM THOMAS, B.L., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Regent's Park College, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1865; from July, 1867, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant settlement officer; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Aug., 1881; judge, 3rd grade, April, 1885; 2nd grade, June, 1889; retired, March, 1893.

MARTINDALE, ARTHUR HENRY TEMPLE, Indian C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; served in the foreign department of the government of India from Oct., 1877, to Oct., 1879, and from June, 1881, to May, 1882, as attaché, officiating political agent, 3rd class, and assistant secretary to government, and was placed on special duty, Dec., 1881; officiating under secretary to government of India, home department, May, Aug., 1882; from Aug., 1882, served again under the foreign department of the government

of India as political agent at Kotah, and assistant secretary to government; junior under secretary, Oct., 1884; officiating assistant to resident in Mysore and secretary to the chief commissioner of Coorg, Feb., 1885; officiating resident and chief commissioner, May-June, 1885; political agent, 3rd class, April, 1886; first assistant and secretary for Berar to the resident, Hyderabad, Nov., 1886; political agent, Quetta and Pishin, April, 1887; 1st assistant to governor-general's agent, Central India, Oct., 1888; political agent, Bhopal, July, 1890, and Jallawar, Jan., 1892; officiating commissioner, Ajmere, April, 1892; confirmed, May, 1894.

MARTINDALE, CECIL SYDNEY DE BUTTS, Captain, I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—In military employ from 12th Jan., 1881; joined the Punjab commission as assistant commissioner, 3rd Dec., 1883; district judge, Feb., 1893.

MARTINEAU, ALFRED EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at University College School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1889, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and officiating district judge; officiating registrar, chief court, March, 1894.

MARTYN, GEORGE VALENTINE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 10th Feb., 1873, and posted to Oudh; transferred to state railways, Dec. 1877; executive engineer, Oct., 1882; assistant director, North Western railway, Aug., 1887; deputy consulting engineer to government of India for railways, Lucknow, Jan., 1889; Calcutta, Oct., 1893.

MASSY, CHARLES FRANCIS, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 11th June, 1868; from June, 1870, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating cantonment magistrate, officiating judicial assistant, and officiating deputy commissioner, and was placed on special duty to settle boundary disputes in Feb. and Oct., 1874; on a deputation to Kashmir, Aug.-Dec., 1876; political officer with the Punjab native chief contingent, Nov., 1878; on special duty in connection with viceregal durbar, at Lahore, Nov., 1880; on special duty to inspect the Punjab-Rajputana border, Nov., 1883; officiating divisional judge, Jullundur, April, 1885; superintendent, Kapurthala state, Aug., 1886; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1890; has published a new edition of Sir Lepel Griffin's 'Punjab Chiefs,' and 'Chiefs and Families of Note in the Punjab.'

MASSY, GEORGE, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Punjab.—Arrived in India, 11th April, 1866; from April, 1867, served in the Punjab as civil surgeon, superintendent of jail, and superintendent of vaccination; deputy sanitary commissioner (sub. pro tem.), Nov., 1880; civil surgeon, 1st class, Oct., 1881.

MASTER, CHARLES GILBERT, C.S.I., Barrister-at-Law, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1854; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate from Sept., 1855; deputy secretary to government in revenue department, Dec., 1859; commissioner of income tax, 1860 to 1865; secretary to the board of examiners, April, 1861, to Jan., 1866; acting sub-secretary to the board of revenue for various periods between 1862 and

1866; acting civil and sessions judge, Calicut, May, 1869; on special duty in Godaveri and Kistna on irrigation works, May to Nov., 1869; from Nov., 1869, to May, 1877, served as collector and magistrate of Nellore, and other districts; secretary to government, revenue department, June, 1877; chief secretary to government, June, 1882; additional member of the legislative council, June, 1882; ordinary member of council, Madras, Jan., 1884; president of the committee in connection with the draft tenancy bill, July, 1885; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; retired, Jan., 1889.

MASTERS, EDWARD STEWART, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Educated at Magdalene College, Cambridge; first commission, 9th Aug., 1873; served under the military department (including service in the Afghan war, 1878-79), until Sept., 1891, when he was appointed cantonment magistrate, Secunderabad, and judge, small cause court.

MASTERS, JOHN, Bengal Police Department.—Joined the service, 21st July, 1861; assistant superintendent of police, June, 1863; district superintendent, April, 1870; 1st grade, Feb., 1890.

MATHEW, GEORGE FELTON, C.I.E., Railway Branch, Government of India.—Joined the public works department, June, 1876, as assistant to the manager, Rajputana state railway; officiating traffic superintendent, Indus Valley state railway, June, 1879; 2nd class, traffic department, Jan., 1880; Kandahar railway, March, 1880; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; officiating manager, Nizam's railway, March, 1882; Rajputana-Malwa railway, Dec., 1884; North-Western railway, April, 1888; attached to office of secretary to government of India, public works department, Oct., 1888; deputy manager, North-Western railway, May, 1890; officiated as manager, July-Oct., 1890, and as traffic superintendent in same year; manager, Burma state railway, May, 1892.

MATHEWS, HENRY MONTAGU, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Burma.—Appointed 4th grade executive engineer, 8th Oct., 1868; arrived in India, Dec., 1868; served in British Burma, and was promoted to 1st grade executive engineer, Oct., 1877; employed in examining, repairing, and reporting on lighthouses, British Burma, Jan.-March, 1878; transferred to railway branch as engineer-in-chief and manager of the Rangoon and Irrawaddy Valley state railway, Nov., 1878; officiated as chief engineer and secretary to the chief commissioner, public works department, Oct., 1880, and again in 1886; 1st class, 1st grade, state railway revenue establishment, Jan., 1882; also chairman, Rangoon port trust; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retired, 1887.

MATHIAS, HENRY VINCENT, Major-General, I.S.C., late Police Department, Central Provinces.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, Dec., 1849; in command, Nagode divisional military police, Oct., 1862; from 1st July, 1865, served in the police department, Central Provinces, and was promoted to district superintendent of police, 1st class, April, 1875; reverted to military duty, 1885.

MATTHEWS, HAROLD FRANCIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Tonbridge School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived, 10th Nov., 1871; served in the revenue and judicial

departments in Bengal and Assam; officiating superintendent of emigration and embarkation agent, Sept., 1878; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1884; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1889.

MAUDE, HENRY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Rugby; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1877, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; officiating senior secretary to financial commissioner, Nov., 1884; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab, Jan., 1885; confirmed, April, 1885; officiated as junior secretary to the government of the Punjab in 1888, 1889, 1890, and 1891; confirmed, April, 1892.

MAUDE, WALTER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Highgate School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1883; assistant magistrate and collector, Nov., 1883; acting under secretary to government of Bengal, general, revenue and statistical departments, April, 1889, and March, 1890; confirmed, Aug., 1890; officiating secretary in same departments, July, 1892; acted as collector of crops, Calcutta, in 1890; settlement officer, Orissa, Nov., 1892; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1893.

MAW, WILLIAM NEWTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Wesley College, Sheffield, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 19th Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

MAXWELL, FREDERICK DOVETON, Captain, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—First commission, July, 1882; employed in Burma as super-numerary assistant commissioner, July, 1885; assistant commissioner, July, 1887; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1891.

MAXWELL, HENRY ST. PATRICK, C.S.I., Major, I.S.C.—Educated at Wellington College and Sandhurst Military College; arrived in India, 26th March, 1869; joined as ensign, 106th Foot, April, 1869; from May, 1873, served in civil employ in the Assam commission; served in the Daffa expedition, 1874-75; acted as political officer, Naga Hills, Feb. to July, 1878; political officer with the Naga Hills expedition, Oct., 1879, to April, 1880 (medal); inspector of immigrants, Jan., 1881; from May, 1881, acted as deputy commissioner in the Garo Hills; political officer with the Aka field force, Dec., 1883, to Feb., 1884; deputy commissioner, Sept., 1885; acted as inspector-general of police in 1887; political officer, Manipur, April, 1891; C.S.I., May, 1892.

MAXWELL, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Belfast Methodist College, Royal University of Ireland, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1891, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

MAYES, HERBERT FREDERICK, B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Dover College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1888; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1890, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

MAYHEW, ALFRED HERCULES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Sind Commission. — First commission, Madras army, June, 1861; arrived in India, 4th Jan., 1862; from July, 1865, served in Sind as deputy collector and magistrate, acting political superintendent of Thar and Parkar, head assistant collector, and acting collector and magistrate; on special duty in the military department, March, 1882; in charge of office of district superintendent of police, Jacobabad, May-Aug., 1883, and March-May, 1884; deputy commissioner, Upper Sind frontier, Dec., 1884; collector and magistrate and district registrar, Shikarpur, and political agent, Khairpur state, Dec., 1885.

MAYNARD, HERBERT JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab). — Educated at Merchant Taylors' School and St. John's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1883; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating district judge; under secretary to government of Punjab, revenue department, Nov., 1891, to May, 1894.

MAYNE, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces). — Educated at Christ's Hospital and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1888; arrived in India, 27th Oct., 1890, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner; also registrar, judicial commissioner's court, March, 1892; assistant accountant-general, Bengal, May, 1893, to April, 1894.

MAYO, JOHN HORSLEY, Military Department, India Office. — Educated at Brighton College; East India house writer, old establishment, 28th Feb., 1855; junior clerk, marine department, 1860; statistical department, 1862; senior clerk, Jan., 1872; military department, 1875; assistant secretary in the military department, Dec., 1882.

M'BLAINE, FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Assam). — Educated at private schools and at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1886, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; employed in Burma as assistant commissioner, Jan., 1890, to Sept., 1892; transferred to Assam in same capacity, May, 1894.

M'CARTHUR, ALEXANDER DONALD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Department. — Appointed assistant engineer, March, 1869; and employed on irrigation works in Bengal; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; officiating under-secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, April, 1885, and again in 1887; in charge of office of chief engineer and joint secretary, Nov.-Dec., 1885; superintending engineer, July, 1891.

MCCABE, ROBERT BLAIR, Indian C.S. (Assam). — Educated at Victoria College, Jersey; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1876, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1884; on special duty as political officer, North Lushai, in 1890 and 1891-92; author of an outline grammar of Angami Naga language, 1887.

MCCALLUM, EDWIN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay). — Educated at the Wesleyan Connexional School, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil

service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting junior collector and magistrate; second assistant collector, May, 1884; first assistant collector, April, 1887; retired, March, 1893.

MCCARTHY, SAMUEL TRANT, late Madras C.S. — Educated at St. Patrick's College, Carlow; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed sub-collector and joint magistrate, Chingleput, Oct., 1877; acted as collector and magistrate, Cuddapah, Sept., 1879, to March, 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1882, in the district of Vizagapatam, and afterwards in Kistna, Madura, and Chingleput; retired, Jan., 1891.

MCCLEVERTY, WILLIAM ANSON, General. — Entered the army from the Royal Military College, Sandhurst, and obtained a commission in the 48th regiment, March, 1824; brigade major to the western auxiliary column, Coorg field force, March-April, 1834; temporarily attached to the engineer department at Gibraltar, 1840-41; deputy quartermaster-general in the Australian Colonies, July, 1846, to April, 1857; commanded the troops in New Zealand during the native disturbances in 1847 (medal); assistant adjutant-general in Scotland, Jan., 1859, to Feb., 1860; in command of the presidency division of the Madras army, April, 1860, to April, 1865; in command of the south-eastern district of England at Shorncliffe and Dover, 1866-67; commander-in-chief of the Madras army, with local rank of lieutenant-general, and member of the governor's council, July, 1867, to April, 1871.

MCCOLL, HUGH ERNEST, Indian C.S. (Burma). — Educated at Clifton College, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

MCCOLLUM, FLORENCE AUGUSTUS, Registry and Record Department, India Office. — Clerk, 2nd class, 31st Dec., 1894.

MCCONAGHEY, JOHN, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.D., Medical Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh. — Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; entered civil employ as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, April, 1876; appointed in same capacity to Allahabad, Oct., 1892.

MCCONAGHY, WILLIAM, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, M.D., Medical Department, Bombay. — Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; superintendent of Matheran, Sept., 1877, and Mahabaleshwar, Oct., 1878; civil surgeon, Karwar, Nov., 1882; employed under the military department, 1883-85; civil surgeon, Dharwar, Feb., 1885; also superintendent of jail, July, 1887; civil surgeon, Poona, Oct., 1892.

MCCONNELL, JAMES FREDERICK PARRY, M.D., Surg.-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service. — Joined the service, 1st April, 1870; house-surgeon, Calcutta medical college hospital, Oct., 1871; professor of pathology in the medical college, and resident physician to the college hospital, March, 1872; also officiated as professor of the comparative anatomy section of the college museum, in July, 1873; officiating civil surgeon, 24-Pergunnahs, and medical inspector

of emigrants, Sept.-Nov., 1878; surgeon-major, April, 1882; superintendent of vaccination, Feb., 1883; officiating professor of materia medica and clinical medicine, medical college, and medical inspector of emigrants, April, 1883, and again, July, 1885; civil surgeon, 24-Pergunnahs, medical officer, Alipore jail, and ex-officio deputy sanitary commissioner, 1885; appointed professor of materia medica and clinical medicine, medical college, Oct., 1891; also medical inspector of emigrants, April, 1892.

MCCORKELL, GILMOUR, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Foyle College, Londonderry, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1872, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting educational inspector, acting district superintendent of police, assistant judge and sessions judge, and acting district judge; 1st grade assistant judge, Feb., 1886; senior assistant judge, April, 1890; judge and sessions judge, June, 1894.

MCCUDDEN, EDMUND GERALD JAMES, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, Jan., 1870, and employed on irrigation and military works in Rajputana; transferred to state railways, July, 1875; executive engineer, Nov., 1880; engineer-in-chief, Cuttack-Midnapur-Calcutta railway survey, March, 1894.

MCCULLAGH, J. R., Colonel, R.E., India Survey Department.—First commission, 18th Dec., 1861; arrived in India, 20th Oct., 1865, and served with the Bengal sappers and miners; appointed to the public works department, Nov., 1868, and to the great trigonometrical survey of India, March, 1869; assistant superintendent, Jan., 1872; deputy superintendent, July, 1882; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892.

MCDERMOTT, BENJAMIN KENICOTT SELBY, Burma Commission.—Was appointed preventive officer on the Mandalay line, 20th Dec., 1872; superintendent of wharves, Rangoon port trust, April, 1874, and chief inspector of police, July, 1875; assistant superintendent of police, Oct., 1880, and district superintendent, Oct., 1882; appointed to the commission as assistant commissioner, Jan., 1887; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1891.

MCDOUGALL, H. R. L., Deputy Surgeon-General, M.D., L.R.C.S., L.M. (Ed.), late Bombay Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1858; civil surgeon, Rajkot, Oct., 1878; acted as superintendent of vaccination, Oct.-Nov., 1882; brigade-surgeon, May, 1883; superintendent of Matheran, March, 1884; retired, May, 1886.

MCDOWELL, GEORGE RONALD, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 26th Sept., 1864, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; transferred to N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Oct., 1865; executive engineer, March, 1874; in charge, Lucknow division, provincial works, May, 1894.

MCGANN, TERENCE JOSEPH, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Service.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; senior surgeon, Bangalore, and sanitary commissioner, Mysore, Sept., 1870.

MCINTOSH, HENRY JAMES, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy, the

University of Edinburgh, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1884; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1886, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating under secretary to government, financial and municipal departments, April, 1892; confirmed, Oct., 1892; acting in the general, &c., departments, May, 1893; employed temporarily in finance department, government of India, July, 1893; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Jan., 1894.

MCIVER, CHARLES, Bombay Police Department.—Joined the service as assistant superintendent of police, Karachi, 10th March, 1867; superintendent, March, 1873; district superintendent, Aug., 1875; 1st grade superintendent, July, 1888; district superintendent, Hyderabad, March, 1889.

MCLAUGHLIN, FREDERICK HUBERT, LL.M., B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Shrewsbury Grammar School, and Magdalen College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, April, 1877; from April, 1876, served in the judicial department, and was appointed district and sessions judge, 1st grade, March, 1884; retired, March, 1890.

MCLEAN, JOHN JAMES, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the International College, Ealing, and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1875, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, officiating cantonment magistrate, and officiating district judge; joint magistrate, 1st grade, July, 1880; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, March, 1890, and again, Dec., 1890; district and sessions judge, March, 1891.

MCLEOD, GEORGE EDMONSTONE, late Assam Commission.—Appointed extra assistant commissioner in Oudh, 14th Feb., 1870, and assistant commissioner in Assam, March, 1875; inspector of labourers, Feb., 1882; deputy commissioner, May, 1888; retired, 1892.

MCLEOD, HARRY, Lieut.-General, R.A., Madras Political Department.—First commission, 1st Nov., 1853; commissary of ordnance, May, 1879; deputy inspector-general of ordnance, Madras, Sept., 1880; superintendent, gun carriage factory, Sept., 1881; inspector-general of ordnance, Sept., 1886; consular agent, Pondicherry and Karikal, Aug., 1891.

MCLEOD, KENNETH, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., M.D., late Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, March, 1865; civil surgeon, Jessore, 20th Feb., 1866; on special duty as member, cattle plague commission, Dec., 1869; secretary to inspector-general of hospitals, Jan., 1871; officiating professor of surgical and descriptive anatomy, Calcutta Medical College, and ex-officio 2nd surgeon, College Hospital, April, 1874; also officiating medical inspector of emigrants, Calcutta, and officiating marine surgeon, Jan., 1875; rank of surgeon-major, March, 1877; professor of surgery, Medical College, and ex-officio 1st surgeon at the hospital, Jan., 1880; also health officer for town of Calcutta, Feb., 1880; brigade-

surgeon, June, 1888; retired, April, 1892; appointed member of medical board, India office, July, 1893; author of several publications on cattle diseases, including the report of the cattle plague commission, 1871, and on medico-legal subjects; also of 'History of the Medical Schools of the Bengal Presidency,' 'The Sanitary Past, Present, and Future of Calcutta,' 'Operative Surgery in the Calcutta Medical College.'

MCMAHON, ARTHUR HENRY, C.I.E., Captain, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 10th March, 1883; entered civil employ as assistant commissioner, Punjab, June, 1887; employed in Zohb from Oct., 1890; assistant political agent there, April, 1891; deputy commissioner, Thal, and political agent, Loralai, April, 1892; political agent in Zohb, April, 1893; accompanied mission to Kabul, Aug., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; acting revenue commissioner, Baluchistan, Feb., 1894; British joint commissioner, Afghan-Baluch boundary demarcation commission, April, 1894.

MCMILLAN, ALEXANDER, M.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Aldenham Grammar School, and Brasenose College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1868, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant settlement officer; officiating assistant secretary to government, Aug., 1874; under secretary to government, March, 1876; deputy superintendent, family domains of Maharaja of Benares, March, 1877; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, Oct., 1883; from April, 1884, served as officiating district and sessions judge and small cause court judge; district and sessions judge, April, 1887; retired, May, 1894.

MCMINN, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 21st Jan., 1863; from July, 1864, to Feb., 1879, served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, settlement officer, assistant commissioner, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was placed on special duty for completion of Oudh gazetteer, July, 1872, also at Simla, July, 1873; from March, 1879, served in the Central Provinces and was appointed deputy commissioner, 1st class, Jubbulpore, Nov., 1884; officiated as commissioner in 1887; deputy commissioner, Damoh, Feb., 1888; Chanda, Jan., 1890; Hoshangabad, May, 1891; retired, Aug., 1891; compiled Gazetteer of Oudh, 1877.

MCNAIR, ARTHUR WYNDHAM, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 8th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

MCNEILE, CHARLES, Colonel, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 11th Dec., 1858; from Dec., 1861, served in the Punjab police department as assistant superintendent and officiating district superintendent; from May, 1865, served in the commission as assistant commissioner, officiating small cause court judge, and judicial assistant; judicial

assistant, 1st grade, Sept., 1881; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, March, 1886; retired, March, 1888.

MCNEILE, JOHN MAGEE, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Bengal.—Joined at Addiscombe, Aug., 1854; 1st commission, Bengal Engineers, June, 1856; arrived in India, 10th April, 1858; from May, 1858, served in the public works department as assistant engineer, and executive engineer in Bengal; also as assistant superintendent, Alguada Reef Lighthouse works, 1860-65; officiating superintendent of works, Ajmere and Mairwara, 1874 to 1876; re-transferred to Bengal, Nov., 1876; superintending engineer, 3rd grade, Jan., 1879; transferred temporarily to Punjab, Oct., 1879; officiating superintending engineer and secretary to chief commissioner of Assam, public works department, Jan., 1880; confirmed in this appointment, Jan., 1882; chief engineer, 3rd class, special, and joint secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, Jan., 1884; officiating chief engineer and secretary to government of Bengal, July, 1885, to Oct., 1886; 2nd chief engineer, Nov., 1887; secretary to the government of Bengal, Jan., 1890, and 1st chief engineer, Feb., 1890; reverted to military duty, March, 1892; retired, Dec., 1892.

MCNEILL, DUNCAN, Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Punjab.—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1855; arrived in India, 29th Dec., 1855; served in the suppression of the mutiny at Sitabaldi, 1857, and with the Kamptee flying column in 1858-59; also with the expedition to the Pachmarhi Hills, and at the capture of Harrakote, Nov., 1859; assistant district superintendent of police, Punjab, July, 1862; district superintendent, 5th class, April, 1867; 1st class, May, 1890; reverted to military duty, Jan., 1893.

MCNEILL, JAMES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at University College, Blackrock, Royal University, Ireland, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1890; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1891.

MCIPHERSON, HUGH, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Paisley Grammar School, Glasgow University, London University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 28th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

MCQUEEN, SIR JOHN W., Major-General, K.C.B., A.D.C., I.S.C.—1st commission, Bengal Infantry, April, 1854; arrived in India, 15th May, 1854; employed in military department up to 31st Dec., 1882; served throughout Indian mutiny campaign of 1857-58, including siege and capture of Delhi and relief of Lucknow; commanded 4th Punjab infantry in Oudh and Rohilkund campaign of 1858; served against the Kabul Kheyl Waziris in 1860 (medal and clasp), and in the Jowaki Afreede expedition, 1877-78 (clasp); served in Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and two clasps, C.B.); served in Mahsud Waziri expedition, May, 1881; military secretary to government, Punjab, Jan., 1883; brigadier-general, commanding Hyderabad contingent, Sept., 1885; commanding Punjab frontier force, Oct., 1886; commanded Hazara expedition, 1888 (medal); K.C.B., April, 1889; aide-de-camp to the Queen.

McRAE, JOHN GRAHAM, Colonel, I.S.C., late Forest Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 10th July, 1858, and served in the Bombay presidency on military duty till March, 1867, and subsequently in the revenue survey department as assistant superintendent; on military duty, Nov., 1867, to July, 1868 (field service in Abyssinia), and again Dec., 1870, to Nov., 1871; from Nov., 1871, served in the Bombay forest department as deputy conservator; conservator of forests in Sind, Aug., 1878; 2nd grade conservator, Dec., 1886; retired, May, 1891.

MEAD, HENRY ROBERT, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—First commission as lieutenant, Dec., 1855; arrived in India, 26th Dec., 1857, and served in the Madras public works department from Jan., 1858, as assistant engineer, and executive engineer (4th grade, Jan., 1866), and was placed on special duty on the Neringipeth anicut project, March, 1871; superintending engineer, Oct., 1878; on special duty in Travancore, Feb., 1882; 1st class superintending engineer, April, 1886; chief engineer, 2nd class, and joint secretary to government, public works department, irrigation branch, March, 1887; acting chief engineer and secretary to government, Oct., 1888; retired, Sept., 1889.

MEADE, MALCOLM JOHN, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 9th Aug., 1873; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1873; served under the military and foreign departments, and was appointed officiating political assistant, Rajputana, March, 1878; assistant to resident, Hyderabad, and assistant general superintendent in thuggee and dacoity department, Aug., 1878; from 1879, was assistant to governor general's agent in Central India for various periods; boundary settlement officer, Bhopal, June, 1875; superintendent of operations for control of Monghyas, July, 1885; additional political agent, 1st class, and political agent, Baghelkhand, and superintendent of Rewah state, May, 1887; political agent in Bhopawar, Dec., 1889; officiating political agent, Baghelkhand, and superintendent of Rewah state, March, 1891; political agent, Bhopal, Dec., 1891.

MEADOWS, CHARLES JOHN WALFORD, Surgeon-Lieutenant-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1st Oct., 1869; from 26th April, 1872, served as civil assistant surgeon and civil surgeon at various stations in Bengal; also officiated as superintendent of medical school, Dacca, 1888-90.

MEARES, WILLIAM EDWARD, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 1st April, 1874, and posted to N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Nov., 1881; served temporarily on state railways, 1874-76 and 1884-88, and was transferred permanently to that branch, Dec., 1889.

MEDLICOTT, ARTHUR LOUIS, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Clifton College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1889, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner; personal assistant to chief commissioner, Nov., 1890, to Feb., 1891.

MEDLICOTT, HENRY BENEDICT, M.A., F.R.S., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India.

—Engaged for service in India from the geological survey of Great Britain; joined appointment in India, March, 1854; professor of geology, Roorkee College, Aug., 1854, to Oct., 1862, when he rejoined the geological survey as deputy superintendent for Bengal; received the Indian mutiny medal for special services in Rohilkhand; was variously employed in Bengal, Assam, Central India, Rajputana, Central Provinces, &c.; superintendent of the geological survey (subsequently designated director of the geological survey), April, 1876; fellow of Calcutta University, and member of the faculty of arts and engineering, April, 1876; retired, April, 1887; author of numerous contributions to the survey publications, and (in part) of a manual of the geology of India.
