MEHERJIBHAI KUVARJI TARAPURVALA, Khan Saheb, Bombay Political Department.—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1875, and served in the Bombay judicial department; chief judge, Kolhapur, Feb., 1882; divan and president of council, Kolhapur, from 1885.

MEIKLEJOHN, DAVID OGILVY, B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Rugby; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1870; assistant magistrate and collector, Hoogly, Dec., 1870; from Oct., 1871, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, acting district superintendent of police, and acting deputy commissioner, and was placed on special famine duty in Bengal, Feb., 1874; registrar, judicial commissioner's court, Dec., 1882; judge, small cause court, Jubbulpore, April, 1883; deputy commissioner, Sept., 1889.

MEIKLEJOHN, GEORGE FORBES, B.L., Coorg Administration.—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1867, as probationary assistant superintendent; confirmed, June, 1868; special deputy commissioner, March, 1881; deputy commissioner, Bangalore, and president of Mysore municipality, Nov., 1889; commissioner of Coorg, Jan., 1891.

MELHUISH, WILLIAM FRANCIS, M.I.E.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as inspector, 29th May, 1865; arrived in India, 9th Sept., 1865, and served in Bengal and Bombay; accompanied Persian Gulf cable expedition, Oct., 1869; superintendent, April, 1880; personal assistant to director-general, Dec., 1881; held charge of various divisions, including Bengal, Madras, Bombay; electrician, May, 1889; attached to office of director-general of telegraphs, Feb., 1891; director, traffic branch, Feb., 1891; chief superintendent, Sept., 1892; in charge, Calcutta telegraph office, Dec., 1892; director, March, 1894.

MELITUS, PAUL GREGORY, I.C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at St. Marylebone, All Saints' Grammar School, Marlborough College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1880; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; assistant commissioner in Assam, Nov., 1886; acted as assistant secretary to chief commissioner in 1889; and as postmaster-general, Calcutta, in 1890; under secretary to government of India, home department, Jan., 1891;

officiating deputy secretary, Jan., 1892; confirmed, March, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, Assam, Feb., 1894; confirmed, Oct., 1894.

MELLOR, CHARLES WARREN, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Nov., 1879; 1st grade, Feb., 1882; retired, Aug., 1887.

MELVILL, SIR PETER MELVILL, Major-General, K.C.B.—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School; ensign, Bombay army, 1819; arrived in India, 26th Dec., 1819; was employed with the revenue survey of Guzerat, 1822 to 1827; fort adjutant of garrison of Bombay, and A.D.C. to the governor, 1828; on special duty in Calcutta as assistant to the Bombay military member of the financial commission, 1829; 1st assistant to resident in Cutch and Sind, 1836; political agent in Cutch, 1838; secretary to government of Bombay in the military and marine departments, Dec., 1840, to April, 1859; appointed K.C.B., 1860; retired with honorary rank of major-general, Dec., 1861; was a member of Lord Hotham's committee on the amalgamation of the Indian with the British army in 1860, and of the royal commission appointed to report on the memorials of the Indian officers in 1863.

MELVILL, PHILIP JAMES, Captain, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 1st May, 1878; from Oct., 1886, held officiating appointments at Hyderabad as assistant resident, assistant secretary for Berar, and assistant general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti; appointed assistant agent, Indore, Dec., 1887; assistant to resident, Bushire, May, 1888; assistant commissioner, Merwara, Jan., 1890; assistant commissioner, Ajmere, Nov., 1892.

MELVILL, PHILIP SANDYS, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Rugby, and Haileybury College, from which he was appointed to the Bengal civil service in 1846; arrived in India, 4th Oct., 1846; attached to the Punjab, and served as assistant to the resident at Lahore, and assistant commissioner; settlement officer in the Hoshiarpur and Amballa districts, 1850-54; deputy commissioner, Amballa, Jan., 1854; commissioner, Hissar division, 1860 to 1862, and subsequently of the Delhi and Rawul Pindi divisions; officiated as financial commissioner of the Punjab in 1869, and again in 1873, and as judicial commissioner of the Central Provinces in 1870-71; commissioner, Jullundur division, 1872 to 1875, and officiated as judge of the chief court of the Punjab in 1873 and 1874; member of commission for investigating the charges brought against the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1875; agent to the governor-general and resident, 1st class, at Baroda, Oct., 1875; retired, Jan., 1882.

MENZIES, HUGH CHRISTIAN, Major-General, I.S.C., late Berar Commission.—Ensign, Dec., 1849; arrived in India, 7th June, 1850, and served under the military department in Madras, and took part in the operations against the insurgents in the Parla Kimedi District, 1857; served in Burma from August, 1859, as assistant

commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner; from Oct., 1860, served as superintendent of police in Madras, and was engaged in the organisation of the police in the South Malabar districts, and in the Hyderabad assigned districts from Oct., 1862; from Dec., 1864, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as assistant commissioner, and small cause court judge at Amraoti; inspector-general of registration, Aug., 1871; deputy commissioner, April, 1878; reverted to military duty, 1887.

MENZIES, OSWALD, Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Punjab.—First commission, 26th Aug., 1854; arrived in India, 19th Dec., 1854; from March, 1861, served in the police department, Punjab, as district superintendent, and was promoted to deputy inspector-general, July, 1880; inspector-general of police in the Punjab, and under secretary to government, March, 1885; reverted to military department, Aug., 1892.

MEREDITH, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Dulwich College; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner and district judge; officiating under secretary, revenue department, Dec., 1886; officiated as deputy commissioner and as district judge for various periods in 1887-90; district judge, and officiating divisional judge, April, 1891; deputy commissioner, March, 1894.

MERES, WILLIAM FITZPATRICK, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Oakham Grammar School, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived, 29th Nov., 1864; from Sept., 1865, served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and in Assam (March-Dec., 1881), and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, May, 1878, and district and sessions judge, 2nd grade, March, 1883, and 1st grade, Aug., 1888; acting judicial commissioner, Burma, Feb., 1885; president of the educational syndicate, Burma, Feb., 1885; retired, 1890.

MERK, WILLIAM RUDOLPH HENRY, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Kornthal, Wurtemberg, the Grammar School, Old Aberdeen, and the University of Aberdeen; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1875, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating cantonment magistrate, and officiating judicial assistant; on special duty, Jhelum, March, 1877, and in the Khyber Pass, 1879, and again as assistant political agent, Sept., 1879; assistant resident, Kandahar, Nov., 1880; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab, Sept., 1882, and again, Feb., 1884; on special duty with the Afghan boundary commission, Aug., 1884; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; deputy commissioner, Peshawar, Aug., 1890, and again from Dec., 1891; district judge, Peshawar, 1890-91; on special duty in the Kurram valley, Sept., 1892.

MERRIMAN, CHARLES JAMES, C.S.I., General, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—2nd lieutenant, Bombay engineers, June, 1850; arrived in India, 20th Oct., 1852; assistant engineer, Sept., 1853; field engineer, Persia, Nov., 1856; executive

engineer, Hyderabad, Sept., 1857, and subsequently at Kurrachee and at Aden, 1866; superintending engineer in Sind, May, 1868; in N.W. Provinces to examine irrigation works, Sept.—Nov., 1869; acting chief engineer for irrigation and under secretary to government of Bombay, 1871, and again in 1873 and 1877—79; chief engineer and secretary to government of Bombay, general and buildings and roads branches, March, 1880; retired from the public works department in 1885.

MERRIMAN, JOHN HENRY, Salt Department, Madras.—Served in the Madras revenue survey department from June, 1868, to Oct., 1880; on special duty in Ganjam for settling boundary disputes, March, 1877; from Nov., 1880, served in the salt department as assistant commissioner, and was on special duty in the office of the commissioner of salt and abkari revenue, Jan.—April, 1885; deputy commissioner, salt revenue, Dec., 1885.

MERRIMAN, WILLIAM, C.I.E., Colonel, R.E., late Military Works Department.—Joined the service, 12th Dec., 1856, and arrived in India, Feb., 1859; appointed special assistant engineer, harbour defences, Bombay, Nov., 1862; executive engineer, Jan., 1864; acted as under secretary to government in 1864; served under the home government, May, 1866, to March, 1882; returned to India as executive engineer, 1st grade, March, 1882; served as superintending engineer of West of India coast defences in 1888; chief engineer, Sept., 1890; Bombay command, July, 1891; retired, May, 1893.

MESTON, JAMES SCORGIE, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Grammar School and University of Aberdeen, and at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1885, and served in N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant commissioner, June, 1890; joint magistrate, May, 1890; settlement officer, April, 1892.

METCALFE, CHARLES THEOPHILUS, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1857; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments; deputy commissioner and civil officer, Bhutan field force, Oct., 1864; senior superintendent of survey, May, 1869; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, June, 1871; additional commissioner, Patna division, March, 1874; officiating inspector-general of police, Oct., 1876; officiating commissioner of police, Calcutta, Nov., 1876; officiating opium agent, Behar, July, 1884; officiated as commissioner in various divisions between 1876 and 1884, and was appointed commissioner of the Orissa division, and superintendent of tributary Mehals, Cuttack, Sept., 1884; retired, Oct., 1889.

METCALFE, EDWARD PARR, M.A., Madras Educational Department.—Appointed, 19th Nov., 1872, and served as head-master of Rajahmundry school, and acting inspector of schools; fellow of Madras university, March, 1880; 3rd class, educational department, May, 1884; principal of Rajahmundry college, Oct., 1887.

MEYER, CHARLES HARDWICK LOUW, M.D., Surgeon-Captain, Bombay Educational Department.—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1887; professor of physiology and histology and of hygiene, Grant medical college, Jan., 1889.

MEYER, WILLIAM STEVENSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Blackheath Missionary School, University College School, and University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 25th Dec., 1881, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; acting assistant secretary to government, judicial and legislative departments, Oct., 1886; confirmed, Nov., 1888; acting under secretary to government, revenue department, Nov., 1889, to Jan., 1890; acting secretary to the commissioner of salt, &c., in 1890, 1891 and 1892; deputy commissioner of salt, &c., revenue, Sept., 1891; acting secretary to commissioners of land revenue in 1891, and from July, 1892; confirmed, Nov., 1893; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Feb., 1894; principal assistant, Vizagapatam, March, 1894.

MICHAEL, JAMES, Lieut.-General, C.S.I., I.S.C.—Educated at the Temple School, Brighton; also studied on the Continent, and at the Military Academy, Edinburgh; ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1844; in 1848 was appointed to organise a scheme of forest conservancy, which he superintended till the establishment of a regular forest department in 1856; assistant chief engineer, Hyderabad, March, 1857; executive engineer, Secunderabad, Sept., 1862; superintendent of family payments and pensions, and curator of government books, Madras, 1865 to 1876; commissioner for Madras at the universal exhibition, Paris, 1867; acted as deputy secretary to government of Madras, military department, 1869; commissioner for India to the Vienna exhibition, 1873 (appointed commander of the order of Francis Joseph of Austria); fellow of the university of Madras, 1874; attached to the staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales during his visit to Madras, 1875; secretary to the government of Madras, military department, Feb., 1876, to 1883; C.S.I., March, 1876; commissioner for India to the Edinburgh Forestry Exhibition, 1884; royal commissioner for the Colonial and Indian Exhibition, 1886.

MICHAEL, WALTER HENRY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Newark Grammar School, and at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived, 5th December, 1887, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

MICHELL, RICHARD BROOKE, M.A., B.L., Madras Judicial Department.—Called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), April, 1872; admitted an advocate, Madras high court, Feb., 1874; officiated as law reporter, crown and public prosecutor, chief presidency magistrate, and law professor, presidency college; appointed to the latter post, July, 1881; acting administrator-general, May, 1893; chief judge, Madras small causes court, July, 1894.

MICHELL, The Ven. FRANCIS RODON, late Ecclesiastical Department, Bengal.—Appointed junior chaplain, 12th Aug., 1867; senior chaplain, Aug., 1877; officiating archdeacon of Calcutta, Feb., 1887; in charge of the diocese, March, June, 1888; appointed archdeacon, Feb., 1889; retired, Aug., 1892.

MICHELL, THOMAS BERNARD, Colonel, I.S.C., late Assam Commission.—First commission as ensign, Feb., 1858; arrived in India, 29th Jan., 1860; from March, 1866, served in the Assam commission, and was promoted to

deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Jan., 1885; also served temporarily in Bengal as cantonment magistrate, Dum-Dum, July, 1877, to Nov., 1878; retired, March, 1886.

MIDDLETON, JAMES RUSSELL, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at St. Andrew's, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1865, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; junior collector and magistrate, Kaladgi, Aug., 1883; senior collector and magistrate, Nov., 1886; acted as commissioner, southern division, March to June, 1889; collector and magistrate and district registrar, Nasik, June, 1889; also political agent of the Surgana state; retired, 1890.

MILES, SAMUEL BARRETT, Colonel, I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—Ensign, Bombay infantry, Aug., 1857; from Nov., 1867, served as cantonment magistrate and assistant resident, Aden; assistant political agent, Mekran Coast, 1871; from Oct., 1872, served as political agent and consul, Muscat; officiating political agent, Turkish Arabia, and consul-general, Baghdad, 1879-80; political agent, Zanzibar, 1881; returned to agency of Muscat, 1883; graded as political agent, 1st class; acting political resident, Persian Gulf, April, 1886; political resident, Meywar, Dec., 1890; retired, Dec., 1893; author of several papers on the history and geography of southern and eastern Arabia.

MILES, THOMAS WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 8th Oct., 1868, and posted to Rajputana; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; services lent to Jeypore state, 1873-74, and 1875-78; and to Kotah and Jhallowar states, July, 1878.

MILLAR, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Liverpool College, King's College, Cambridge, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

MILLER, SIR ALEXANDER EDWARD, Kt., B.A., Q.C., LL.D., Government of India.—Educated at Rugby and Trinity College, Dublin, where he obtained scholarship, 1st gold medal in mathematics and physics, and 2nd gold medal in classics, besides the Berkeley medal in Greek; hon. LL.D. in 1875; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1854; Q.C. in 1872; examiner in law, London University, 1874-79, and for council of legal education, 1877-78; legal member of railway commission, 1877-88; master in lunacy, 1880; appointed legal member of the government of India, Dec., 1890; took up his post, 8th April, 1891.

MILLER, ARTHUR, Library, India Office.—Writer, 11th Oct., 1867; clerk, 2nd class, March, 1880.

MILLER, JOHN FRANCIS JAMES, Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1868, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Sept., 1874; served through the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp), and Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); superintending engineer, Nov., 1890.

MILLER, JOHN ONTARIO, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at King's Col-

lege, Aberdeen; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, as assistant commissioner, 3rd grade, and assistant magistrate and collector; assistant secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Jan., 1883; under secretary to government, May, 1884; private secretary to the lieutenant-governor of the N.W. Provinces, Oct., 1886; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, Nov., 1887; settlement officer, 3rd grade, Oct., 1888; 2nd grade, Oct., 1890; director of land records and agriculture, March, 1892.

MILLER, LESLIE CREERY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Charterhouse, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1883; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1884; head assistant collector, June, 1890; special assistant collector, Dec., 1890; manager, Pittapur estate, Jan., 1891, to June, 1892.

MILLETT, H., Barrister-at-Law, late Bengal Judicial Service.—Professor of English law, Presidency College, Calcutta, April, 1870; officiating 2nd judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Oct., 1871; receiver, high court, April to Dec., 1873; officiating assistant secretary, government of Bengal, legislative department, Dec., 1873, to April, 1875; first judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1876; retired, 1889.

MILLETT, MONTAGUE, Colonel, Bengal Infantry, late Police Department, Punjab.—First commission, 1857; arrived in India, 16th Oct., 1857; from Sept., 1864, served in the police department, Punjab, as assistant superintendent, and district superintendent, and was promoted to 1st grade superintendent, June, 1884; reverted to military department, Oct., 1887.

MILLS, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1870, and posted to Bengal; executive engineer, May, 1883; inspector of works, Behar division, Nov., 1892.

MILLS, JAMES, Veterinary Captain, Bombay Medical Department.—Joined the department, 23rd June, 1875; appointed principal and professor, Bombay veterinary college, Aug., 1891.

MILNE, ALEXANDER, Surgeon-Major, M.B., C.M., F.C.S., Calcutta Mint.—First commission, 2nd April, 1881; after serving in military employ, and officiating as chemical analyser, Bombay, and civil surgeon at various stations, was appointed deputy assay master, Calcutta mint, Nov., 1892.

MILSOM, BOSWELL PARKINSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 1st March, 1871, and posted to state railways in the Central Provinces; in 1873-76 was employed in the provincial roads and buildings, and irrigation branches; executive engineer, Sept., 1883; employed on Kashmir railway survey, Nov., 1890; officiating deputy manager, Eastern Bengal line, March, 1891; services lent to Assam-Bengal railway company, Jan., 1893.

MINNS, WALTER HARDWICK CHRISTOPHER, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Marlborough, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1891, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

MIR SHUJAAT ALI KHAN, Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Served in the Madras revenue and judicial departments from Aug., 1876, in various subordinate posts, and was appointed by the government of India probationer for the civil service from 15th Dec., 1881; confirmed, Aug., 1884; assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1884; fellow of Madras University, March, 1886; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Nov., 1889.

MIRZA ABBAS ALI BAIG, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1882; employed in Janjira state, 1886-90; probationer under the native civil service rules, and assistant collector, 1st April, 1890; officiated as 4th presidency magistrate, April-June, 1893; appointed oriental translator to government, June, 1893.

MITCHELL, HORACE, Registry Department, India Office.—Educated at St. Paul's School; clerk, 2nd class, India office, 27th March, 1885.

MITCHELL, THOMAS CANDY, M.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1875, and served as assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal; from May, 1876, served in Burma as assistant commissioner and boundary and settlement officer; on special forest demarcation duty, Nov., 1880; on special duty in connection with waste land grants in Arakan division, March, 1882; deputy commissioner, 4th grade, Aug., 1886; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, April, 1888.

MITTER, GOPAL LAL, Bengal Administration.—Appointed vice-chairman of the commissioners of Calcutta, 3rd Sept., 1884.

MOBERLY, CHARLES MORRIS, Colonel, I.S.C., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Ensign, 2nd European regiment, Madras, May, 1855; joined the public works department, May, 1867, as examiner of railway accounts, Madras; controller of accounts, Calcutta, 1873; officiating deputy accountant-general, March, 1876; examiner, guaranteed railway accounts, Calcutta, March, 1878; Rajputana railway accounts, 1879; acted as manager, Rajputana railway, May-Aug., 1881; on special duty as secretary to railway conference, Jan.-March, 1884; examiner, 1st class, in the accounts establishment, Jan., 1886; examiner, guaranteed railway accounts, Bombay, March, 1887; reverted to military department, July, 1892.

MOBERLY, HENRY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Bradfield College, and King's College School, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and forest settlement officer; special assistant to collector and magistrate, Nov., 1888.

MOCKLER, EDWARD, Colonel, Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, Bombay infantry, Aug., 1859; served as assistant resident, Aden, Dec., 1866, to 1873; on civil employ, Mekran coast, from 1873; assistant to the governor-general's agent, Baluchistan, Oct., 1879; assistant agent and consul, Basra, Turkish Arabia, Nov., 1881;

acted as political agent, Muscat, 1883, and again April, 1886, and April, 1887; graded as political agent, 2nd class; assistant political agent, Basra, Oct., 1883; political agent, Muscat, April, 1886; Basra, Jan., 1887; resumed his post at Muscat, April, 1887; officiating political resident, Turkish Arabia, Nov., 1891; confirmed, May, 1892; author of an Arabic grammar, a Baluchi grammar, &c.

MOHENDRO NATH BOSE, late Judicial Department, Bengal.—From 26th Feb., 1859, served in the subordinate judicial service, Bengal, and was appointed subordinate judge, 4th grade, Sept., 1868; small cause court judge, Midnapore, Feb., 1872; 1st grade subordinate judge, and small cause judge, Hooghly, Serampore, and Howrah, May, 1877; officiating judge, high court, Jan., 1882; judge of the principal courts of small causes in Nuddea and Jessore, and of the courts of small causes at Krishnagur, Ranaghat, and Bongong, Oct., 1884; retired, Oct., 1890.

MOHSIN BADRUDIN TYABJI, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Mary's Institute and St. Xavier's College, Bombay, and at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 28th Oct., 1887; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1888.

MOIR, WILLIAM, M.A., M.B., Brigade Surgeon, late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 20th Jan., 1860; arrived in India, 27th April, 1860, and was employed in various military medical charges; from June, 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as civil assistant surgeon and superintendent of jail at Mainpuri, and afterwards at Meerut, and was placed in medical charge of the lock hospital at the latter place; held medical charge of camp of lieutenant-governor, 1863-64; superintendent, meteorological observatory, Meerut, June, 1868; rank of surgeon, Jan., 1872; surgeon-major, Jan., 1875; officiating chemical examiner, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April, 1878, to March, 1879, and again in April, 1888; brigade surgeon, April, 1888; retired, April, 1891.

MOIR, WILLIAM ROBERT GRAY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Charterhouse and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

MOLESWORTH, SIR GUILDFORD LINDSAY, K.C.I.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Educated at the College of Civil Engineers, Putney; in 1852 was chief assistant engineer on the London, Brighton, and South Coast Railway, which he left in order to superintend the construction of buildings and machinery in the Royal Arsenal at Woolwich, during the Crimean war; afterwards practised as a consulting engineer in London for some years; was awarded the 'Watt' medal and the 'Manby' premium by the Institution of Civil Engineers in 1858, for a paper read before the institution on the 'Conversion of Wood by Machinery'; engineer on the Ceylon railway, 1859; chief engineer, Ceylon government railway, 1862; director-general of the railway, 1865; director of public works in 1867; consulting engineer for state railways to the government of India, April, 1871; acted as

director-general of railways and deputy secretary to government of India, March-June, 1880; K.C.I.E., June, 1888; retired, 1889; fellow of the University of Calcutta; a British delegate to the international monetary conference, Brussels, 1892; is the author of many publications on engineering, monetary, and other subjects, including a 'Pocket-book of Engineering Formulae,' which has passed through twenty-one editions; of 'Proposals for the Establishment of Decimal Coinage' in Ceylon, 1865, and in India, 1871; of Reports on the Public Works of Ceylon, 1869, on Light Railways in Ceylon, 1870, on the Festiniog Railway, 1871, on State Railways in India, 1872, and on the Madras Harbour, 1884; 'Iron Manufacture in India,' 1884; 'Suggestions for the Establishment of an Engineer Volunteer Corps for India,' 1865; 'Imperialism for India,' 1885; 'Text-book of Bimetallism,' 1886, &c., &c.

MOLLOY, ROBERT ALFRED, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, May, 1883.

MOLONY, EDMUND ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Marlborough and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector and assistant commissioner; assistant settlement officer, Nov., 1893.

MONAHAN, FRANCIS JOHN, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at the Oratory School, Birmingham, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1885, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; officiated in 1889 as under secretary to the government of Bengal in the financial and municipal departments, and subsequently in the general, revenue, and statistical departments; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1891; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, March, 1892; deputy commissioner, May, 1894.

MONDY, EDMUND FELIX, A.R.S.M., F.C.S., Bengal Education Department.—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1880, as professor, Sibpur civil engineering college; professor, Rajshahi college, Oct., 1887; and at Dacca college, Nov., 1887; acted as principal in 1890-91.

MONEY, ALONZO, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1843; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Backergunge, 1846; superintendent of survey, Monghyr, 1850; magistrate and collector, Behar, 1857, and afterwards of Shahabad and Tirhoot; magistrate, collector, and salt agent, Pooree, 1859; controller of government salt-chowkies, 1860; commissioner of revenue and circuit, Bhagulpore, 1863; commissioner of Patna, and member of council of the lieutenant-governor of Bengal, 1869; member of revenue board, Lower Provinces, 1869; district and sessions judge, Bareilly, and member of revenue board, 1875; retired, 1878.

MONEY, ROBERT COTTON, Major-General, I.S.C.—First commission in army, 20th March, 1855; from 1st March, 1861, served in the non-regulation commission, Bengal, and was ap-

pointed deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, April, 1877; manager of the Durbhunga Raj estate, April, 1876, to Jan., 1878, and again, Jan., 1880; retired, March, 1886.

MONEY, WILLIAM JAMES, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1853; served in the revenue and survey departments in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant superintendent of survey, and was employed as civil officer with the troops in the Sonthal insurrection, 1855-56; private secretary to Sir James Outram, chief commissioner of Oudh, Aug., 1857; during the mutiny was present at several engagements, and was a member of the garrison of Lucknow (mutiny medal); served subsequently in Bengal and N.W. Provinces, and was appointed superintendent of revenue survey, 1860, in Jessore, and afterwards in other districts; magistrate and collector, and salt agent, Cuttack, 1861; officiated as district and sessions judge, Mymensingh, from 1869, and was confirmed in that appointment, Sept., 1873; retired, 1879.

MONK, HUGH LEWIN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Apprentice engineer, 1st Sept., 1865; assistant engineer, April, 1866; transferred to state railways, Dec., 1869; executive engineer, Sept., 1873; engineer-in-chief, Mu valley railway, April, 1891; superintending engineer, Jan., 1892.

MONRO, ALEXANDER, M.A., B.C.L., B.L., Education Department, Central Provinces.—Arrived in India, 2nd June, 1879; inspector of schools, 4th class, graded educational department, June, 1879; fellow of Madras University, March, 1885; member of the conference on the school fee question, Aug., 1886; inspector-general of education, Central Provinces, Jan., 1889; officiating director of public instruction, Madras, Oct., 1893; also member of legislative council, Nov., 1893, to Oct., 1894.

MONRO, JAMES, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Arrived in India, and appointed a junior civil servant, 27th Jan., 1858; served as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating under secretary to the government of Bengal, and in April, 1864, was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector; from Sept., 1864, served for various periods as officiating magistrate and collector, deputy collector of customs, Calcutta, and secretary to the board of revenue; appointed magistrate and collector, Feb., 1872, and district and sessions judge, April, 1874; inspector-general of police, Bengal, Jan., 1878; on special duty to report on the system of village police, Nov., 1882; commissioner of the presidency division, March, 1883; retired from the Bengal civil service, 1884, and was appointed director of criminal investigations in Scotland Yard; in Nov., 1888, was appointed chief commissioner of metropolitan police, but resigned that office, June, 1890.

MONTEATH, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Royal Academical Institution, and Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 5th Nov., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; on special duty in Bombay in connexion with the Kanara land assessment case, Nov., 1873; also acted for various periods as under secretary to government of Bombay in the

revenue, financial, and general departments, under secretary to government in the political, judicial, and educational departments, and secretary to the Bombay legislative council, and as assistant judge and sessions judge, and assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan; appointed under secretary to government, revenue, financial, and general departments, May, 1884, and acted as secretary, Sept.-Dec., 1884, and March, 1889; private secretary to the governor, Oct., 1886; acting collector of land revenue, &c., Bombay, April, 1890; junior collector, and collector and magistrate, Kanara, Aug., 1891; senior ditto, Bijapur, May, 1894.

MONTGOMERY, JAMES ALEXANDER LAWRENCE, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 26th Feb., 1868; from Nov., 1870, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, settlement officer, and officiating deputy commissioner; settlement officer, 1st grade, Sept., 1880; director of settlement and revenue records in the Punjab, Dec., 1885; deputy commissioner, May, 1887; officiating commissioner, Rawalpindi, April, 1894; author of a report on the settlement of the Hoshiarpur district.

MOORE, EDWARD JAMES, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868; transferred to state railways, Nov., 1869; executive engineer, April, 1874; employed on various lines, including the Rajputana-Malwa and Dhond-Manmad railways; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway company, Nov., 1887; to Gwalior durbar for Goonabina railway, Dec., 1890; and to Assam-Bengal railway company, Oct., 1892; rank of superintending engineer, July, 1892.

MOORE, ERNEST ANDREW, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Bradfield College and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in Burma, 25th Nov., 1889, and served as assistant commissioner and officiating deputy commissioner.

MOORE, GEORGE MONTGOMERY JOHN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., R.A.—Military secretary on personal staff of the governor of Madras (Rt. Hon. Sir M. E. Grant Duff, G.C.S.I., C.I.E.), 1st April, 1884, to 1886; president of Madras municipal commission, Dec., 1886; C.I.E., Jan., 1894.

MOORE, JOHN GEORGE, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, 1856; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1856, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector, magistrate, and political agent; served in Sind from Dec., 1862, to Nov., 1870, as assistant commissioner, educational inspector, branch registrar-general, and acting commissioner; junior collector and magistrate, Sattara, Oct., 1876; senior collector and magistrate, Sept., 1877; on special duty as president of the jail commission, July-Sept., 1878; was subsequently collector and magistrate of Poona, and acted on several occasions as commissioner of the southern and central divisions, Bombay, and as commissioner of customs, salt and opium, Feb., 1885; acting commissioner of customs, salt, opium, and abbkari, and reporter-general of external commerce, May, 1887; 2nd grade commissioner, and commissioner, central division, July, 1888; 1st grade, Sept., 1888; temporary member of governor's council, March, 1890; retired, Nov., 1891.

MOORE, LEWIS, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Royal School, Armagh, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1869, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting cantonment magistrate, sub-collector and joint magistrate (Dec., 1884), acting district and sessions judge, and acting collector and magistrate; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1888; compiled 'Trichinopoly District Manual.'

MOORE, LEWIS GRENVILLE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Cheltenham College, and Worcester College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 12th Dec., 1892, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

MOORE, ROSS ARTHUR LESLIE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Dulwich, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1883; 2nd assistant, May, 1891; 1st assistant, Nov., 1893.

MOORE, SIR WILLIAM JAMES, Surgeon-General, K.C.I.E., late Medical Department, Bombay.—First commission, Indian medical department, 1852; was employed on regimental and other military duty till April, 1862, when he was appointed residency surgeon at Jodhpur, and afterwards at Marwar; served from Aug., 1867, in Rajputana as agency surgeon and superintendent-general of dispensaries and vaccination; acting deputy surgeon-general, Bombay, 1877; deputy surgeon-general, Oct., 1877; surgeon-general with the government of Bombay, 1st April, 1885; hon. surgeon to the vice-roy; retired, Feb., 1888; K.C.I.E., May, 1888.

MORELAND, WILLIAM HARRISON, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, and acting under secretary to government; assistant settlement officer, Nov., 1893; assistant commissioner, April, 1894.

MORGAN, CHARLES, Registry Department, India Office.—East India house writer, old establishment, 22nd April, 1857; assistant to registrar, Oct., 1874; despatch clerk, Oct., 1892.

MORGAN, JOHN WILLIAM AKERMAN, Veterinary Captain, India Civil Veterinary Department.—Joined the (home) service, 12th June, 1878; served in the Zulu and Afghan wars; appointed superintendent, civil veterinary department, Bombay, May, 1892.

MORGAN, SIR WALTER, late Madras High Court.—Called to the bar, Middle Temple, 18th Nov., 1844; clerk of legislative council of India, 1854-59; master in equity of the supreme court, Calcutta, 1859-62; puisne judge of Calcutta high court, 1862-66; chief justice of the high court of the N.W. Provinces, 1866-71; and of the high court of Madras from 1871, until his retirement in 1879.

MORIARTY, ARTHUR STEPHEN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1880, and served as

assistant collector and magistrate, and assistant judge and sessions judge.

MORISON, WILLIAM THOMSON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Fettes College, Edinburgh, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1881, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer; transferred to Burma as assistant-commissioner, 1887 (India medal with Burma clasp); deputy commissioner, May, 1889; re-transferred as 2nd assistant, Nov., 1891; 1st assistant, Jan., 1893; officiating administrator, Porbandar state, Oct., 1893; confirmed, March, 1894.

MORRIS, JOHN GEORGE, Major, I.S.C., late Berar Administration.—Joined the service, 28th February, 1874, and served under the military department to Nov., 1880; acted as boundary settlement officer, Bhopal, to Nov., 1882; officiating cantonment magistrate, Saugor, Dec., 1882; confirmed, March, 1883; assistant commissioner, April, 1887; special assistant commissioner, July, 1890; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1893; retired, May, 1894.

MORRIS, SIR JOHN H., K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1847; arrived in India, 4th Jan., 1848; served in the Punjab as assistant resident, Lahore, assistant commissioner and assistant settlement officer in the Trans-Sutlej division; settlement officer, Mooltan, 1854; magistrate and collector, Allahabad, May, 1862; settlement commissioner, Central Provinces, Sept., 1863; judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, June, 1868; chief commissioner, Central Provinces, May, 1870; retired, 1883.

MORRIS, MATTHEW, late Police Department, Punjab.—Appointed to the police department, 12th Feb., 1861, as assistant district superintendent; served in the various grades of the department, and was promoted to be district superintendent, 1st grade, March, 1888; retired, Aug., 1892.

MORRIS, ROBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at High School, Glasgow, University of Glasgow, and University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 12th Jan., 1885; assistant collector and magistrate, March, 1885; head assistant, Jan., 1891; acting deputy accountant general, Madras, April, 1891; confirmed, May, 1891.

MORRIS, THOMAS WALTER, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at the Manchester Grammar School and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 17th Nov., 1892, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

MORRISON, FRANK, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the telegraph department, Sept., 1863; resigned, Feb., 1864; rejoined government service, and was employed in the accounts establishment (public works) in Bombay, Sind, the Punjab, and Calcutta; deputy controller, Nov., 1874; examiner of guaranteed railway accounts, Lahore, Dec., 1875; examiner, Indus Valley railway, June, 1878; examiner of public works accounts, Hyderabad, Dec., 1879; Northern Bengal railway accounts, May, 1882; examiner, 3rd class, May, 1886; acted as examiner of guaranteed railway accounts, Bombay, 1886-87; examiner,

Southern Mahratta railway company, March, 1887; examiner, guaranteed railway accounts, Madras, March, 1888; government examiner of accounts, East Indian railway, Nov., 1891.

MORSE, FRANK HAMAN WALFORD, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, April, 1870, and posted to military works branch; employed in Madras on famine relief works, 1877-79; transferred to state railways, Sept., 1881; executive engineer, May, 1883; in 1887-89 employed in Burma on provincial works; appointed to East Coast railway survey, Dec., 1889; and to duty on open section of same line, March, 1894.

MORSHEAD, LEONARD FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1889, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

MORTON, ANDREW, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, July, 1869; transferred to military works department, July, 1872; and to state railways, locomotive department, June, 1878; employed on W. Rajputana, N. Bengal, Tirhoot, and Nizam's state railways; district locomotive superintendent, North Western railway, April, 1889; officiating deputy superintendent, Dec., 1890; locomotive superintendent, East Coast railway, Aug., 1893.

MORTON, BOYCE WILLIAM DUNLOP, Lieut.-General, I.S.C., late Non-Regulation Commission, Bengal.—First commission, 6th June, 1848; served in Assam from June, 1854, as junior assistant and principal assistant to the commissioner; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, Goa-para, April, 1861; superintendent of Darjeeling, Dec., 1866; on special duty for conducting the prosecution of Captain Playfair, Aug., 1876; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Nov., 1879; retired, Aug., 1885.

MOSCARDI, EDWARD HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at the Somersetshire College, Bath, and Worcester College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived, 7th Nov., 1876, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; on special duty compiling the general administration report of the Bombay Presidency, Aug., 1881; from Dec., 1881, served in the judicial department, and was appointed 2nd grade assistant judge, Oct., 1883, and 1st grade, April, 1890; judge and sessions judge, May, 1894.

MOSLEY, HERBERT, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Bishop's Stortford Collegiate School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1862; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1863; from April, 1865, served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, May, 1884; and 1st grade, March, 1888; retired, Jan., 1891.

MOULE, HORACE FREDERICK D'OYLY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1863; from July, 1864, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector, and was placed on special duty in 1872 to conduct the prosecution

of the sub-judge of Mirzapur; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Sept., 1879; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, June, 1884; 1st grade, Dec., 1886; officiated as district and sessions judge, July and Oct., 1887, and May-Aug., 1888; secretary, board of revenue, Jan.-May, 1886; officiating commissioner, Lucknow division, March, 1890; confirmed, Nov., 1891; transferred to Rohilkhand division, March, 1893.

MOUNG HLA OUNG, Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the department, 1st April, 1874, and served in the accountant-general's office at Madras and Calcutta; assistant accountant general, Rangoon, Jan., 1880, and Madras, May, 1881; assistant comptroller general, Calcutta, March, 1882; assistant accountant-general and examiner of local fund accounts, Calcutta, March, 1892; deputy accountant-general, Allahabad, June, 1893; assistant comptroller general, Calcutta, Oct., 1893; assistant comptroller, Burma, March, 1894; revised Gordon's 'Burma Manual.'

MOUNSEY, CHARLES HERBERT, B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at King's College School; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant sessions judge, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting district and sessions judge; head assistant, Anantapur, May, 1886; sub-collector and joint magistrate, June, 1890; acting district and sessions judge in 1890 and 1891; acting collector and magistrate, Feb., 1892; compiled and edited the 'Reports of the Meriah Agents (Ganjam),' and wrote a 'History of the Ganjam Mahalias.'

MOUNTFORD, LEWIS JAMES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Dulwich College, and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 20th Nov., 1892, and served in Sind as assistant collector and magistrate.

MOWAT, M., M.A., late Education Department, Bengal.—Professor, Presidency College, June, 1876; Dacca College, Jan., 1878; officiating inspector of schools, Nov., 1879; professor, Patna College, Feb., 1880; officiating principal, Patna College, May, 1883; 3rd class of the education department, July, 1883; officiating principal, Hooghly College, April, 1885; retired, 1891.

MUHAMMAD ABBAS, MIRZA, late Statutory C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Probationer under native civil service rules, 16th Feb., 1880; confirmed as covenantant civil servant, March, 1882, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner; joint magistrate, Oct., 1888; resigned, Feb., 1894.

MUHAMMAD AFZAL KHAN, C.S.I., Nawab, Khan Bahadur, Hon. Lieut.-Colonel, late 11th Bengal Lancers.—Appointed British agent at the court of H.H. the Amir of Kabul, March, 1882; retired, 1885; native A.D.C. to governor-general, June, 1885.

MUHAMMAD AFZAL KHAN, Nawab, Khan Bahadur, Statutory C.S. (Punjab).—Probationer under native civil service rules, 3rd March, 1881; assistant commissioner, Oct., 1885; services placed at disposal of foreign department, government of India, April, 1891; British agent at Kabul, June, 1891, to March, 1894.

MUHAMMAD ALI, SAIYID, B.A. Statutory

C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Appointed a probationary assistant magistrate and collector under the statutory rules, 4th April, 1887; confirmed in the civil service, 17th April, 1889, and served as assistant magistrate and collector and joint magistrate.

MUHAMMAD ASLAM KHAN, Kazi, C.M.G., Punjab Commission.—Officiating extra assistant commissioner, 10th Sept., 1881; on special duty in civil secretariat, Dec., 1881; probationer under native civil service rules, Nov., 1882; assistant commissioner, 3rd grade, Nov., 1884; on special duty with Afghan boundary commission, Aug., 1884, to Nov., 1886; appointed Mir Munshi to Punjab government, May, 1887; native attaché to foreign office, April, 1888; assistant commissioner, March, 1889; officiated as district judge in 1890, and deputy commissioner in the same year, and from March, 1891.

MUHAMMAD HADI, SAIYID, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Appointed under the native civil service rules probationary assistant magistrate and collector, 5th April, 1890; assistant director of land records and agriculture, Dec., 1891; also superintendent of inland emigration, Sept., 1893.

MUHAMMAD HYAT KHAN, Khan, C.S.I., Punjab Commission.—Joined the service, 16th April, 1858; extra assistant commissioner, Punjab, March, 1862; promoted to assistant commissioner, Jan., 1872; C.S.I., May, 1872; on special duty with the camp of exercise at Hassan Abdal, 1873; officiating political assistant with the Kuram field force, 1878; political officer with the staff of Sir F. Roberts, Kabul field force, 1879-80; officiating judicial assistant, March, 1876; officiating district judge, at various periods from Nov., 1884; confirmed, Dec., 1886; divisional judge, March, 1890; member of council, Kashmir, Nov., 1892.

MUHAMMAD ISHAK KHAN, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Appointed a probationer under native civil service rules, Jan., 1884; confirmed in the civil service, Aug., 1886; assistant magistrate and collector, April, 1888; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, Nov., 1889; joint magistrate, May, 1890.

MUHAMMAD RAZA KHAN, Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Served from 14th Jan., 1874, in subordinate posts in the Madras revenue and educational departments, and in Hyderabad as attaché to the resident; appointed by the government of India to be a member of the civil service, 3rd Nov., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1883; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Feb., 1890.

MUHAMMAD SAMI-ULLA KHAN, Maulvi, C.M.G., late Judicial Department, N.W. Provinces.—Joined the service as sub-judge, 1st May, 1873; district judge (sub. pro tem.), April, 1885 and April, 1886; officiated as district judge in 1886 and 1887, and was confirmed in the appointment, May, 1887; retired, Nov., 1892.

MUIR, ARCHIBALD MUNGO, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 4th hussars, 30th Sept., 1868; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80, including the action of Takhti-i-Pul, and siege of Kandahar (medal and clasp) in Sept., 1873; appointed private secretary and A.D.C. to lieut.-governor of N.W. Provinces, from Aug., 1878; served in the foreign department in 1878, as assistant agent to the governor-general

in Rajputana, and afterwards as political officer at Kandahar, and as assistant general superintendent for the suppression of thuggee and dacoity; assistant boundary settlement officer, Central India, Nov., 1883, and in Rajputana, Nov., 1885; cantonment magistrate at Nasirabad, April, 1886, and afterwards at Hyderabad; 2nd assistant and assistant secretary for Berar to the resident, Jan., 1889; political agent, S.E. Baluchistan, July, 1890; political agent, Bhopawar, April, 1892; political officer in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul, March, 1893.

MUIR, JOHN FAIRLIE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Edinburgh Academy, and Rugby School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1867, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from Sept., 1869, to April, 1874, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, acting assistant secretary and personal assistant to the chief commissioner, and acting deputy commissioner; from April, 1874, served in the Bombay Presidency as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; 1st assistant collector, May, 1880; junior collector and magistrate, April, 1887; senior collector, June, 1890; collector and magistrate and political agent, Savanur, Sept., 1893.

MUIR, JULIUS WOOD, M.A., Barrister-at-Law, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Dumfries Academy, and Edinburgh University; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, officiating magistrate and collector, and officiating district and sessions judge; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, July, 1883; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, in Oudh, Sept., 1885; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1889.

MUIR, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., D.C.L., LL.D., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Kilmarnock Grammar School, and Edinburgh and Glasgow Universities; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury, 1837; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1837; settlement officer in Cawnpore and Bundelkhund, 1840-43; officiating collector, Futtehore, 1844-45; secretary, Sudder board of revenue, 1847; secretary to government of N.W. Provinces, 1852; member, board of revenue, 1856; in charge of intelligence department at Agra during mutiny, 1857; secretary with Lord Canning at Allahabad for N.W. Provinces, 1858; reverted to board of revenue, 1859; member of legislative council of governor-general, 1864; foreign secretary, government of India, 1865; lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces, 1868-74; financial member of governor-general's council, 1874-76; member of council of secretary of state, 1876-85; principal, Edinburgh University, 1885; honorary D.C.L. of Oxford, and LL.D. of Glasgow and Edinburgh; author of 'Life of Mahomet and History of Islam to the Era of the Hegira,' 1858; 'Annals of the Early Caliphate,' 1883, and other works on the same subject; pamphlet on indigenous education in Futtehore, 1845; papers on revenue settlement in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, &c.

MUIR, WILLIAM JAMES WEMYSS, Colonel,

I.S.C., late Foreign Department, Government of India.—Lieutenant, Bengal artillery, Dec., 1858; assistant agent to governor-general, Rajputana, Sept., 1863; assistant general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti, June, 1864; political superintendent, Sirohi, Sept., 1865; officiated for some time as agent of Haraoti and Tonk; political agent, Moorshedabad, 1876; cantonment magistrate, Neemuch, 1879; Haraoti and Tonk, 1880; graded as political agent, 1st class; cantonment magistrate, Ajmere-Merwara, May, 1889; officiating political agent, Ulwar, Aug., 1889; retired, June, 1892.

MUIR-MACKENZIE, JOHN WILLIAM PITT, M.R.A.C., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Eton; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived, 28th Nov., 1876, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant political agent in charge of Jath state, and forest settlement officer; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; on duty at Royal Agricultural College, Cirencester, Oct., 1886, to Oct., 1887; 2nd assistant collector and magistrate, Nov., 1887; acting director of land records and agriculture, Feb., 1888; under secretary to government of India in revenue and agricultural department, March, 1889, to April, 1893; officiated as secretary in 1889, 1890, 1891, and 1892; on special duty to Réunion, April, 1893; 1st assistant collector and magistrate, Aug., 1893.

MUKERJEE, SATIS CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated in India and at University College, London; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 16th Dec., 1892, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

MULLALY, CHARLES MYLNE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Southampton Grammar School, Hartley Institution, Southampton, and London University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 3rd Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; on special duty preparing Madras administration report, Sept., 1883; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Nov., 1887; acted as secretary to the commissioners of salt, &c., revenue, in Aug., 1891; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1892.

MULLALY, JOSEPH JOHN, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, Oct., 1882.

MULLEN, DOUGLAS FRENCH, Surgeon-Major, M.D., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1877; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1877, and served on general and military duty; from April, 1878, served under the foreign department in medical charge of the Meywar Bhill corps, and officiating residency surgeon, Meywar; was confirmed in the latter appointment, Nov., 1886; civil surgeon, Ajmere, and medical officer of Merwara battalion, March, 1893.

MULLINS, JOHN, Lieut.-General, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—2nd lieutenant, Madras engineers, 8th Dec., 1848; arrived in India, 28th Oct., 1850; assistant civil engineer, Feb., 1852; served as 1st class district engineer in Nellore and Tanjore, 1858-64; consulting engineer for the irrigation

and canal company, Madras, May, 1864, to Aug., 1869; under secretary to government, public works department, Feb., 1869; chief engineer for irrigation and joint secretary to government of Madras, public works department, Nov., 1870; acted as chief engineer and secretary to government in 1875 and 1877; president, irrigation committee, famine commission, Oct., 1878, to Jan., 1879; on special duty for drawing up the Bangalore water supply scheme, Oct., 1883; services replaced at disposal of military department, in 1884; retired, 1886; author of 'Madras Irrigation Manual.'

MULLOCK, FRANCIS BERRY, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Portora Royal School, Enniskillen, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector; on special duty as assistant private secretary to lieutenant-governor on occasion of visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Dec., 1875, to March, 1876; on special duty for arrangements connected with Imperial assemblage at Delhi, Nov., 1876; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, April, 1886; joint magistrate, 1st grade, April, 1887; magistrate and collector, Feb., 1890.

MULLOCK, HENRY PILKINGTON, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Kingstown School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1865; from Feb., 1866, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred temporarily to Bengal for employment on Behar famine relief works, Dec., 1873, to Oct., 1874; promoted to joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1878; district and sessions judge, 3rd grade, Aug., 1886; 2nd grade, Dec., 1890.

MULLOCK, WILLIAM BURY, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived 28th Nov., 1862, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant registrar of the high court, and assistant collector and magistrate; from April, 1873, to Nov., 1876, served as assistant commissioner and branch inspector-general of assurances in Sind, also as inspector of education, May–Nov., 1873, and was on various occasions in charge of the office of the commissioner; junior collector and magistrate, and political agent, Panch Mahals, and political agent, Rewa Kantha, Jan., 1880; acting collector of Bombay, and superintendent of stamps and stationery, June, 1884; senior collector and magistrate, Nasik, March, 1885; acting commissioner, northern division, Sept., 1888; retired, Nov., 1889.

MUNRO, JAMES HUME, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Newcastle-on-Tyne Grammar School, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived, 21st Nov., 1886, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; head assistant collector, Tinnevelly, March, 1893.

MUNRO, WELLESLEY HERBERT, Accounts Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk,

India office, 31st Jan., 1861; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

MURDOCH, DAVID DOIG, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at University of Aberdeen, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate in Kistna and Chingleput, Sept., 1884; head assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1890; deputy commissioner of salt, &c., revenue (S. division), and secretary to the commissioner, March, 1894.

MURPHY, MATTHEW JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at University College, Blackrock, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 21st Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

MURRAY, ALAN, Major-General, I.S.C., late Oudh Commission.—Arrived in India, 4th Feb., 1857, as ensign, Bengal Fusiliers; during the mutiny served at the siege of Delhi, and was present at the relief of Lucknow, Nov., 1857; served subsequently with Sir James Outram's force, Nov., 1857, to Feb., 1858, and took part in the pursuit of the rebel Tantia Topee, Jan., 1859 (medal and two clasps); served in the Oudh police department as assistant superintendent from June, 1866, to April, 1867, and subsequently in the Oudh commission as assistant commissioner, and assistant settlement officer; junior secretary to chief commissioner, 1874 to 1878, and acted as director of public instruction, Jan.–March, 1874, and as personal assistant to chief commissioner, April–Aug., 1876; attaché in foreign office, Nov., 1876, to Jan., 1877; in charge of Oudh Gazetteer, Aug., 1877, to Feb., 1878; officiated as cantonment magistrate and wasika officer, Lucknow, in 1879, and in 1881–82; district judge, April, 1886; deputy commissioner, Kheri, April, 1886; retired, March, 1887.

MURRAY, FREDERICK CHARLES, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engineering College; appointed, 24th May, 1872, and employed in the Punjab in military works branch; transferred to that province, Nov., 1879; executive engineer, Oct., 1881.

MURRAY, JOHN, Inspector-General of Hospitals, M.D., late Bengal Medical Service.—Educated at Peterhead, and Marischal College, and University of Aberdeen; A.M., 1828; M.D., University of Edinburgh, 1831; M.R.C.S., London, 1831; assistant surgeon, Indian medical service, 1832; field surgeon at the battle of Aliwal, 1846; surgeon, 1848; deputy inspector-general of hospitals, 1858; inspector-general of hospitals, 1865; retired, Jan., 1871; has written various papers on cholera and its treatment.

MUTHUSWAMI AIYAR, SIR TIRUVARUR, B.L., K.C.I.E., Judicial Department, Madras.—Served from 1st May, 1854, in various subordinate posts in the Madras educational, revenue, and judicial departments; deputy collector, July, 1859; magistrate of police, Nov., 1868; judge of the court of small causes, Madras, May, 1871; fellow of University of Madras, April, 1872; on duty at the Imperial assemblage, Delhi, Jan., 1877; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; judge, high court, Madras, Aug., 1883; acted as chief justice in 1891; K.C.I.E., May, 1892.

MYLNE, RIGHT REV. LOUIS GEORGE, M.A.,

D.D., Ecclesiastical Department, Bombay.—Arrived, 4th July, 1876; installed as Bishop of Bombay, 6th July, 1876.

NAFAR CHANDRA BHATTA, B.L., Bengal Judicial Service.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1865, as munsif; subordinate judge, Oct., 1877; small cause court judge, July, 1887; subordinate judge, 1st grade, Oct., 1893; has edited the Indian evidence act.

NAIRNE, CHARLES EDWARD, Lieut.-General, C.B., R.A., Commander-in-Chief, Bombay.—First commission, 7th Dec., 1855; served in the Indian Mutiny, 1857 (medal), Eusufzai expedition, 1863, Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal), and Egyptian expedition, 1882 (medal and star, C.B., and 3rd class Medjidie); commandant and superintendent, school of gunnery, Shoeburyness, 1885-87; inspector general of artillery in India, 1887-92; major-general, Bengal, 1892-93; commander-in-chief, Bombay, 4th Sept., 1893; took his seat as second in council, 16th Sept., 1893.

NANDA KRISHNA BOSE, M.A., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Employed in the education department from Aug., 1878; appointed under the native civil service rules, and served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, from Jan., 1880, as assistant magistrate and collector on probation; confirmed in his appointment in the civil service, Sept., 1881; joint magistrate and deputy collector, June, 1890; acting junior secretary, board of revenue, March, 1894.

NANNEY, LEWIS CHARLES, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, L.F.P.S., L.S.A., late Madras Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, 31st March, 1886; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1886; served under the military department, also acting as civil surgeon at various stations, and as superintendent of jail; acting presiding surgeon, Madras, 1872-73; superintendent, lunatic asylum, Madras, Oct., 1875; also professor of materia medica, medical college, July, 1876; surgeon-major, March, 1878; medical officer, Coonoor and Kotagiri, 1879-81, and at Ootacamund, 1881-83; special sanitary officer, Ootacamund, Aug., 1881, to July, 1883; civil surgeon, Cannanore, July, 1883; civil surgeon, Trichinopoly, June, 1884, and Kurnool, May, 1890; retired, May, 1892.

NAPIER AND ETTTRICK (BARON), RIGHT HON. FRANCIS NAPIER, K.T.—Attaché to the embassy at Vienna in 1840, and afterwards at Constantinople; served as secretary of legation at Naples and St. Petersburg, and was appointed secretary of embassy at Constantinople in 1854; British minister at Washington, 1857, whence he was transferred to the Hague, 1858, to St. Petersburg in 1860, and to Berlin in 1864; governor of Madras, March, 1866, till Feb., 1872; on the assassination of Lord Mayo, acted temporarily as viceroy; was president of the Social Science Association at Plymouth in 1872; and presided over the education section of the Association at Glasgow, in Oct., 1874; a royal commissioner on the Highland Crofters' question, March, 1883.

NAPIER, ALAN BERTRAM, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Rossall school, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 24th Sept., 1889, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner; registrar of the judicial commissioner's court, 1890-91; under secretary to chief commissioner, April to July, 1892.

NAPIER, HON. GEORGE CAMPBELL, C.I.E., Lieutenant-Colonel, Bengal Infantry, late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1861; from July, 1867, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and officiating small cause court judge; on special duty, March, 1874, in Persia, and again, Feb., 1881, at Kandahar; private secretary and aide-de-camp to lieutenant-governor of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April, 1882, to Sept., 1883; judicial assistant, Feb., 1883; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, Nov., 1884; superintendent, Kapurthala state, April, 1885; retired, April, 1887.

NARAIN DAS, Rai Bahadur, late Oudh Judicial Department.—Served in the postal department from June, 1855; temporary extra assistant commissioner, March, 1867; sub-judge, 3rd grade, Oct., 1881; judge of the small cause court, June, 1882; officiated as district judge, June, 1885, and April, 1887; retired, Sept., 1892.

NARENDRA NATH, Diwan, M.A., Punjab Commission.—Appointed probationer under native civil service rules, 3rd Feb., 1888; assistant commissioner, Feb., 1890; has translated Mill's 'Liberty' into Urdu.

NARSINGRAO BHOLANATH DIVATIA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Appointed probationer under the native civil service rules, Jan., 1844, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; confirmed in the Bombay civil service, 24th Feb., 1886; 2nd assistant, March, 1893.

NARUL HUDA, SYED, B.A., LL.B., B.L., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Joined the service, 4th Aug., 1886, as officiating munsif; assistant magistrate and collector, Jan., 1887; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1893; has acted as district and sessions judge.

NASH, ALFRED MOSES, M.A., Education Department, Bengal.—Appointed, 11th Jan., 1875; arrived, 31st March, 1875; served as assistant professor, Presidency college, and officiating inspector of European schools; 2nd class of the education department, April, 1883; professor, Presidency college, Nov., 1890; also registrar, Calcutta university, May, 1891.

NATHAN, ROBERT, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived, 20th Dec., 1888, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; acting assistant accountant-general, March-Oct., 1891; employed on settlement work in Orissa, Nov., 1891.

NAYLOR, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, Police Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the service, 9th July, 1867, as officiating assistant district superintendent of police; confirmed, April, 1874; promoted to district superintendent, Feb., 1875.

NAYLOR, JAMES RICHARD, C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at King's College School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, and acting judge and sessions judge; senior assistant judge and sessions judge, Kaladgi, June, 1875; remembrancer of legal affairs, July, 1876; acting chief secretary to government, judicial, educational, political, secret, and separate departments, Oct., 1879, to March, 1880,

and again in May, 1887; additional member of council, Bombay, Aug., 1884; on special duty in connection with the Bombay municipal bill, June, 1885; temporary member of council, Bombay, Aug. to Oct., 1887, and again from May to Nov., 1888; C.S.I., May, 1890; retired, June, 1892; legal adviser to the Gaekwar of Baroda, Oct., 1892.

NEALE, WALTER ERSKINE, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School, and St. John's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1862; from June, 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and from Nov., 1867, to April, 1880, as settlement officer; district and sessions judge, 3rd grade, Nov., 1881; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Dec., 1881; 1st grade, April, 1885; officiated as commissioner in 1888-89, and was appointed commissioner in Oudh, Feb., 1890; acting commissioner, Agra, March, 1890; confirmed, Nov., 1893.

NEATHAM, ALBERT HENRY JOHN, Auditor's Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Feb., 1879.

NEDHAM, WILLIAM ALFRED, Central Provinces Commission.—Joined the service as assistant commissioner, 12th July, 1864; served as assistant district superintendent and district superintendent of police, superintendent of school of industry, and in 1874 in Bengal on famine duty; in 1882 was deputy superintendent of census; judge of small cause court, Jubbulpore, Sept., 1889; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1889.

NEEL, EDMUND, C.I.E., Political Department, India Office.—Educated at Victoria College, Jersey (obtained Queen's gold medal for modern languages); India office writer, old establishment, 3rd March, 1859; junior clerk, military department, 1860; assistant précis writer to the secretary of state, 1868 to 1871; military department, 1871; senior clerk, Jan., 1872; funds department, 1874; assistant secretary in the military department, Aug., 1881; assistant secretary in the political and secret department, 1885; officiated as secretary, Jan. to May, 1887, Sept., 1889, to May, 1890, and June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; C.I.E., May, 1890; received the thanks of Lord Rosebery and Lord Kimberley for his services in connection with the Burma-China frontier and trade convention of 1st March, 1894; also received the thanks of Lord Kimberley and Mr. Fowler for services in connection with the delimitation of the British and Italian spheres of influence in Somaliland, May, 1894.

NEILL, JOHN WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at King's College, London, and Edinburgh University; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1864; assistant commissioner, Wardha, Jan., 1866; registrar, judicial commissioner's court, Central Provinces, July, 1866; assistant secretary to chief commissioner, May, 1870, and acted as secretary to the chief commissioner from June, 1870, to March, 1877, when he was confirmed in the appointment; also acted as inspector-general of registration, superintendent of stamps and commissioner of excise in 1872, and as inspector-general of education in 1873; commissioner, Nagpur division, June,

1880; acting judicial commissioner, June, 1881, to March, 1882, and again in 1885; fellow of the University of Calcutta, June, 1883; commissioner, Jubbulpore division, Oct., 1883; officiated as judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, for various periods, and as chief commissioner, Feb., 1887, and Nov., 1890; confirmed as judicial commissioner, Jan., 1891; president of the commission on the working of the Deccan agriculturists relief act, Dec., 1891; retired, April, 1894.

NEILL, LINDSAY, late Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at foreign schools, King's College, London, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1867; served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, and acted as inspector-general of education in 1874, and as assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, in 1872-74, and in 1875-76; under secretary to government of India, home department, April, 1877; inspector-general of registration, superintendent of stamps, and commissioner of excise, Central Provinces, Dec., 1877; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, June, 1880; acted as secretary to the chief commissioner, 1877 to 1882, also acting as inspector-general of police and prisons, June-Aug., 1881; acting commissioner, Nagpur division, April, 1882; additional commissioner, Jan., 1883; secretary to the chief commissioner, March, 1883; acted as commissioner in the Jubbulpur, Nagpur, and Nerbudda divisions, and was appointed commissioner, Jubbulpur, April, 1890; Nagpur, March, 1894; retired, Oct., 1894.

NELSON, JAMES HENRY, M.A., Barrister-at-law, late Madras C.S.—Educated at King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; assistant collector and magistrate, March, 1863; acted as small cause court judge of Madura, 1865-67; acting registrar of the high court, Madras, April, 1867; small cause court judge, Kumbakonum, Dec., 1867, to June, 1872; prepared the Madura district manual; from June, 1872, served as civil and sessions judge in Tanjore; district and sessions judge, Cuddapah, July, 1876, to March, 1880, and afterwards in South Arcot and Chingleput; retired, Nov., 1887.

NELSON, REGINALD ARBOUIN, B.A., LL.B., B.L., Madras Educational Department.—Educated at Clare College, Cambridge; called to the bar (Inner Temple), July, 1886; joined the service, 2nd May, 1891, as principal, law college, Madras; also acting law reporter, April, 1894.

NELSON, ROBERT EDWARD, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 2nd Oct., 1869, and posted to Central India; executive engineer, Jan., 1881; temporarily in state railways branch, 1883-84; transferred to Assam, Nov., 1885; and to Burma, March, 1890; employed in public works secretariat as officiating assistant to chief engineer and under secretary, March, 1890; after acting again in 1891, was appointed to that post, Dec., 1892; in charge, 3rd circle, Jan., 1893.

NESFIELD, JOHN COLLINSON, M.A., Education Department, North-Western Provinces.—Educated at Merton College, Oxford; from 22nd Jan., 1867, to May, 1872, served in Bengal as professor, Krishnagarh College, and at the Presidency College; from May, 1872, served in

Lower Burma as director of public instruction, and inspector of schools; transferred to Oudh, March, 1874, and served in the 2nd grade of education officers as director of public instruction; principal, Benares College, May, 1878; inspector of schools, Nov., 1879; fellow of the Allahabad University, Sept., 1887, and member of the syndicate, Feb., 1888; officiating director of public instruction, March, 1892; confirmed, Aug., 1892; author of 'An Essay on the Kanjar Tribe,' 1882, 'On the Results of Primary Education in N.W.
