RENNICK, ALBERT DE CLANEY, B.L., Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—First commission, 6th July, 1864; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1866; from Oct., 1870, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating cantonment and camp magistrate; cantonment magistrate, 1st class, Dec., 1880; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, June, 1888; deputy commissioner, May, 1889.

RENNIE, JOHN GEORGE MACKWORTH, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Winchester, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination

of 1879; arrived in India, 19th Dec., 1881, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; district judge, Dec., 1892.

RENNY, ROBERT HOME, Bengal Non-Regulation Commission.—Served in the executive department from 12th Nov., 1864, as deputy magistrate and deputy collector and extra assistant commissioner; joined the commission, March, 1877; deputy commissioner, April, 1890.

RENOUF, WINTER CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Victoria College, Jersey, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1889, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

REYNOLDS, CHARLES HENRY, M.I.E.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as assistant superintendent, 7th Jan., 1868; arrived in India, 13th May, 1868; personal assistant to director-general, April, 1870, and 1872; in charge of famine division, April, 1874; attached to director-general's office for various periods, and in April, 1886, appointed superintendent; delegate for India at the international telegraph conference of Berlin, Aug.—Sept., 1885; has held charge of various divisions; director, class 3, in charge of construction branch, July, 1889; charge of traffic branch, Jan., 1890; director, traffic branch, Dec., 1892; officiating deputy director general, April—Nov., 1893; and again from March, 1894.

REYNOLDS, EDWARD SWATMAN, Colonel, I.S.C., late Foreign Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, Oct., 1859; served with the Scinde horse, 1863—77; assistant superintendent and magistrate, Upper Scinde Frontier, 1871—77; assistant to agent to governor-general, Baluchistan, 1877; took part in Afghan campaign, 1879—80; political agent in Pishin, 1883; political agent at Jacobabad, April, 1883; on special duty to Mekran as member of a court of enquiry to investigate Rind and Gwadur disputes, Feb.—April, 1885; deputed to Gwadur in connection with that matter, Feb., 1886; political agent, S. E. Baluchistan, July, 1889; acting political agent, Kotah, July, 1890; acting resident, Baroda, June, 1891; on special duty to Europe with the Gaekwar in 1892; commissioner of Ajmere, Jan., 1893; acting resident, Gwalior, Jan., 1893; reverted to military department, Feb., 1894.

REYNOLDS, HERBERT JOHN, C.S.I., B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at King's College, Cambridge; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1855; arrived in India, 24th Sept., 1856; from March, 1857, to July, 1874, served as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, superintendent of survey, and magistrate and collector (1st grade, June, 1872); between 1874 and 1880 served as secretary to the government of Bengal, general and revenue departments, and as secretary to the board of revenue; additional member, governor-general's council, Jan., 1880, and again in 1882 and 1884; officiating member, board of revenue, June, 1880; officiating president, board of examiners, Dec., 1880; lieutenant-governor's council, Jan., 1881; and again Jan., 1883, and Jan., 1885; board of revenue, July, 1884; member of lieutenant governor's council, Jan., 1885, and Jan., 1887; chairman of port commissioners, Calcutta, Oct., 1887; retired, April, 1889.

REYNOLDS, HERBERT WILLIAM WARD, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Cheltenham; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 22nd Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; second assistant secretary to government, July, 1880; junior secretary, board of revenue, Oct., 1883; officiating deputy superintendent, family domains of the Maharaja of Benares, May—Oct., 1884; assistant commissioner, Aug., 1887; joint magistrate, Aug., 1890; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1893.

REYNOLDS, JOHN MICHEL POCOCK, Police Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Served as a volunteer during the Mutiny; joined the department, 26th Jan., 1861; was promoted to assistant district superintendent, Oct., 1866; and to district superintendent, Sept., 1872; 1st grade, Dec., 1893.

REYNOLDS, PLAYFORD, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, and was employed in Khandesh and Gujarat on irrigation works; executive engineer, May, 1879; services lent to Baroda state in 1883—85.

REYNOLDS, WILLIAM HENRY, Survey of India Department.—Joined the service, 14th March, 1861, and was engaged on survey duty on the N.W. Frontier until 1871, including service with the Umbeyla expedition, 1863; from Jan., 1871, served in the forest department as assistant conservator, deputy conservator, and deputy superintendent of forest surveys; rejoined the survey department as deputy superintendent, Oct., 1890; superintendent of forest surveys, March, 1891.

RHIND, ROBERT HUNTER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Department.—Appointed probationer by the secretary of state, June, 1862; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1862; served as assistant engineer, executive engineer, and officiating superintendent of works on the Upper Godavery works till Aug., 1872, when he was transferred to Bengal, and served in the Orissa, Sone, and South-Western circles; executive engineer, 1st grade, March, 1873; superintending engineer, 2nd class, Nov., 1886; 1st class, June, 1880; retired, May, 1893.

RHODES, FRANCIS WILLIAM, D.S.O., Colonel, 1st Royal Dragoons.—1st commission, lieutenant, 23rd April, 1875; major, Sept., 1885; colonel, Sept., 1889; served in the Soudan expedition in 1884 under Sir Gerald Graham as aide-de-camp to Sir Herbert Stewart, and was present at engagements of El Teb and Tamai (medal with clasp and Khedive's star); served in Nile expedition of 1884—85, and in the operations near Suakin, Dec., 1888 (3rd class of the Medjidie); military secretary to Lord Harris, governor of Bombay, 1890 to July, 1893.

RIBBENTROP, BERTHOLD, C.I.E., Forest Department, Government of India.—Appointed by the secretary of state for India as an assistant conservator of forests, Dec., 1866; conservator of forests, Burma, Oct., 1876; transferred to Punjab, 1883; inspector-general of forests, Jan., 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; author of 'Hints on Arboriculture.'

RICE, PERCIVAL STANLEY PITCAIRN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Winchester, and New College, Oxford; appointed after open com-

petitive examination of 1888; arrived, 8th Dec., 1890, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

RICE, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 16th Dec., 1892, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

RICE, WILLIAM GEORGE LEWIS, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Scott College, and Jesus College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1882, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; transferred to Burma, Feb., 1886, as assistant commissioner; resigned the service, Oct., 1890.

RICE, WILLIAM ROCHE, Surgeon-General, M.D., C.S.I.—Joined the Bengal medical service, Nov., 1856; arrived in India, 3rd Jan., 1857; was employed under the military department, including service during the Mutiny, at Indore (medal); civil assistant surgeon, Central Provinces, June, 1859; civil surgeon, Jubbulpore, May, 1871; acting assistant to sanitary commissioner, Central Provinces, July, 1872; superintendent, central jail, Jubbulpore, April, 1873; surgeon-major, Nov., 1876; civil surgeon and in medical charge of central jail, school of industry, and lunatic asylum, and superintendent, meteorological observatory, Jubbulpore, July, 1879; brigade-surgeon, April, 1884; officiating inspector-general of civil hospitals, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, May, 1887; and confirmed in the appointment, Oct., 1887; deputy surgeon-general, Oct., 1887; sanitary commissioner and surgeon-general to government of India, March, 1890.

RICHARD, HENRY JOSEPH, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, July, 1868, and arrived in India, Oct., 1868; assistant engineer, Rangoon, 28th Oct., 1868; served in various divisions in Burma, and was appointed executive engineer, April, 1875; had charge of Rangoon division, May-Dec., 1879; served with the Burma expeditionary force, Mandalay, 1885-86; superintendent of works, Upper Burma, Jan., 1886; superintending engineer, Sept., 1886; officiating chief engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, public works department, Sept., 1892, and again, April, 1893, to April, 1894.

RICHARDS, JAMES DAY, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Appointed supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

RICHARDSON, EDWARD, Judicial Department, Burma.—After service in various subordinate posts, was appointed extra assistant commissioner, 2nd Feb., 1865; first judge, civil court, Mandalay, Sept., 1887.

RICHARDSON, JOHN, Surgeon-Colonel, M.B., M.A., late Medical Department, N.W. Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 27th July, 1859; arrived in India, 23rd Feb., 1860, and was employed under the military department up to 1866, including service with the Bhootan field force (medal and clasp); from April, 1866, served under the government of the N.W. Provinces as civil assistant surgeon, and officiated as superintendent of the central and district jails at Agra, Farakhabad, and Allahabad; superintendent of

vaccination, March, 1867; rank of surgeon, July, 1871; surgeon-major, July, 1874; deputy sanitary commissioner and superintendent of vaccination, 3rd circle, Jan., 1879; sanitary commissioner, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Aug., 1886; deputy surgeon-general, Central Provinces, May, 1889; inspector-general of civil hospitals, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, March, 1890; in 1892 acted as surgeon-general and sanitary commissioner with the government of India; retired, May, 1894.

RICHARDSON, THOMAS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Brighton College, Cheltenham College, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating under-secretary, general, revenue, and statistical departments, Aug., 1890, and from April, 1892; confirmed, March, 1893; officiating registrar, high court (appellate side), July, 1894.

RICHARDSON, WILLIAM PELHAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 8th Oct., 1868, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Sept., 1872.

RICHEY, Sir JAMES BELLETT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1856; arrived, 7th Dec., 1856, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, educational inspector, assistant revenue commissioner, officiating under secretary to government, revenue, financial, and general departments, talukdari settlement officer, acting collector and magistrate, and assistant to the agent and special commissioner at Baroda; on special duty in connection with the disturbances in the Ahmednagar and Poona districts, July, 1875; junior collector and magistrate, Ahmednagar, Jan., 1879; senior collector, May, 1880; collector and magistrate, Ahmedabad, May, 1881; on special duty at Kharaghoda, Dec., 1883, and Feb., 1884, and at Baroda, Jan., 1884; acting 2nd grade commissioner of customs, March, 1884; acting member of council, Bombay, March, 1885, and again, Oct., 1886; chief secretary to government, political, secret, judicial, educational, and separate departments, May, 1885; confirmed as member of council, Bombay, Aug., 1887; retired, March, 1890; K.C.I.E., May, 1890.

RICKETTS, GEORGE H. M., C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Winchester, and Haileybury; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury, 1847; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and as assistant to the governor-general's agent, S.W. frontier; from 1853, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner, and from 1858 in the N.W. Provinces as magistrate and collector, and civil and sessions judge; C.B. for service in the Punjab in 1857, and medal for service with the troops; commissioner, Allahabad division, 1873; junior member, board of revenue, 1878; retired, 1879.

RICKETTS, MONTAGUE POYNTZ, Colonel, I.S.C., late Central Provinces Commission.—Arrived in India, 22nd March, 1855; served in the Central Provinces from Sept., 1859, as assistant commissioner; small cause court judge, Jubbulpore, Aug., 1862; deputy commissioner, May, 1867; acting inspector-general of police, Nag-

pur, in 1876-77; deputy commissioner, 1st class, March, 1880; acting commissioner of the Jubbulpore and Nagpur divisions successively, Dec., 1882, to Nov., 1883; additional commissioner, Central Provinces, Nov., 1883; acting commissioner for various divisions from 1884 to 1888; retired, April, 1890.

RIDDELL, ROBERT VANSITTART, Colonel, R.E., Mint Master, Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1853; lieutenant, royal engineers, 10th June, 1859; arrived in India, March, 1861; from Aug., 1862, served in the topographical survey of India as military assistant, and was employed in Gwalior and Central India, and in the Khasi and Garo Hills; deputy superintendent, Oct., 1867; assistant surveyor-general with charge of the mathematical instrument department and workshops, and of the lithographic branch, Calcutta, Oct., 1876; acted as mint master at Calcutta, July-Oct., 1881, and at Bombay, Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; appointed mint master, Calcutta, Jan., 1884; and mint master and mint engineer, Bombay, Aug., 1889.

RIDGEWAY, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOSEPH WEST, Colonel, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., late Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, Bengal infantry, Dec., 1860; in civil employ from May, 1868, in the Central India and Rajputana agencies; attaché, foreign department, government of India, 1873; assistant agent to governor-general in Rajputana, 1875; political agent, Eastern States, Rajputana, Aug., 1876; political agent, 3rd class, Aug., 1876; on special political duty with the field force in Afghanistan, under General Sir F. Roberts, 1880, and was present at the action of Saidebad; accompanied General Roberts' force in the march from Kabul to Kandahar, and was present at the battle of Kandahar, Sept., 1880 (medal with clasp, bronze star, brevet lieutenant-colonel); from Sept., 1880, served in the secretariat of the foreign department, and was appointed assistant secretary, Nov., 1880, junior under-secretary, Dec., 1881, and officiated as under-secretary in 1880, 1882, and 1883; from Aug., 1884, served as assistant with the commission for the delimitation of the Afghan frontier, and was appointed Her Majesty's commissioner for this purpose, Sept., 1885; subsequently was Her Majesty's commissioner for this purpose at St. Petersburg, and was promoted to the rank of colonel for his services; returned to Europe, 1887, and was appointed under-secretary for Ireland; retired from the army, 27th Aug., 1889; K.C.B., 1st Jan., 1891; envoy to Morocco, Jan., 1893; lieutenant-governor of the Isle of Man, Nov., 1893.

RIEU, JEAN LOUIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at University College School, London, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

RIGG, HENRY, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 13th June, 1862, and arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1862; assistant engineer, Feb., 1864; served in various divisions in Madras, and was appointed executive engineer, March, 1868; personal assistant to manager and engineer-in-chief, Rangoon and Sittang valley state railway, Burma, Aug., 1881; on special duty, Upper

Burma, Dec., 1885, to Jan., 1886; manager and engineer-in-chief, Burma state railway, June, 1889; consulting engineer to the government of India for railways, central division, May, 1892; chief engineer, Aug., 1892; officiating consulting engineer for railways, Lucknow, July, 1894.

RIGHY, VANS, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Bengal.—Appointed probationary assistant engineer, Feb., 1856, and served in the Central Provinces, Punjab, and Bengal; Nizam's state railway surveys, Nov., 1869; executive engineer, March, 1871; on special duty, Nalhati state railway, Oct., 1874; from Jan., 1875, was employed on Tirhoot state railway, and was appointed manager and superintendent of way and works, Tirhoot and Patna Gya state railways, March, 1880; executive engineer, 1st grade, Feb., 1881; transferred to superior revenue establishment, 1st class, 3rd grade, Oct., 1882; superintendent of way and works, North Western railway, 1886; retired, Dec., 1891.

RILEY, HENRY JAMES, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd April, 1882.

RIPON (MARQUESS OF), THE RIGHT HON. GEORGE FREDERICK SAMUEL ROBINSON, K.G., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., P.C., D.C.L.—Born 24th Oct., 1827; attaché to a special mission to Brussels, 1849; sat in the House of Commons (as Viscount Goderich) as member for Hull, 1852 to 1853, for Huddersfield, 1853 to 1857, and for the West Riding of Yorkshire, 1857 to 1859, in which year he succeeded to the Upper House; under-secretary for war, June, 1859; president of the royal geographical society, 1860; under-secretary for India, Jan.-July, 1861; sworn a privy councillor, April, 1863; lord high steward of Hull, Oct., 1863; secretary for war, April, 1863, to Feb., 1866; secretary of state for India, Feb.-July, 1866; lord president of the council, Dec., 1868, to Aug., 1873; K.G., 1869; chairman of the joint high commission which arranged the treaty of Washington, 1871; viceroy and governor-general of India, 8th June, 1880, in which office he was succeeded by the Earl of Dufferin, in Dec., 1884; honorary D.C.L. of Oxford, 1870; created Marquess of Ripon, June, 1871; grand master of the Freemasons of England, 1870 to 1874; president of the Yorkshire College, Leeds, 1882; is a magistrate and deputy-lieutenant for the north and west ridings of Yorkshire, and for the county of Lincoln; was a royal commissioner for the London Exhibition of 1886; first lord of the admiralty, Feb.-Aug., 1886; secretary of state for the colonies, Aug., 1892.

RISLEY, HERBERT HOPE, B.A., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester, and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1873; assistant magistrate and collector, Nov., 1873; assistant to director-general of statistics, Feb., 1875; on special duty in the Bengal secretariat, Nov., 1876; assistant secretary, government of Bengal, Oct., 1877; under-secretary, revenue and general departments, Jan., 1878; officiating under-secretary, government of India, home department, Feb., 1879; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Jan., 1880; served temporarily under railway branch, public works department, on special duty, Dec., 1882; superintendent of survey of Ghatwali and other service tenures in Manbhoom, Feb., 1884; officiating secretary,

government of Bengal, revenue and general departments, Nov., 1884; employed on the special duty of compiling statistics relating to the castes and occupations of the people of Bengal, Jan., 1885; deputy commissioner, April, 1886; secretary, police commission, 1890; magistrate and collector, May, 1891; officiating secretary to government of Bengal in the financial and municipal departments, April, 1891; confirmed, July, 1892; acted as secretary, general, &c., departments, March-Aug., 1893; member of lieut.-governor's council, 1892-93; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; author of 'Sikkim and Tibet,' 'Tribes and Castes of Bengal,' an 'Ethnographic Glossary,' and other works on ethnography.

RITCHIE, JOHN GERALD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 28th Oct., 1875; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1887; deputy commissioner, May, 1891; magistrate and collector, Dec., 1891; has also officiated as inspector-general of registration, and chairman of Calcutta commissioners.

RITCHIE, RICHMOND THACKERAY WILLOUGHBY, Political Department, India Office.—Educated at Eton, and Trinity College, Cambridge; junior clerk, financial department, India office, 18th May, 1877; revenue, statistics and commerce department, 1881; private secretary to parliamentary under-secretary of state, 1883-92; senior clerk, Oct., 1892; private secretary to permanent under-secretary, Oct., 1892, to Feb., 1894.

RIVAZ, CHARLES MONTGOMERY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Blackheath Proprietary School; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating small cause court judge, and officiating deputy commissioner; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab, March, 1870; officiating secretary to government, Punjab, May, 1874; secretary to under-secretary to government, Sept., 1874; superintendent, Kapurthala state, April, 1877; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, April, 1885; appointed secretary to Punjab government, May, 1887, but to officiate as commissioner and superintendent, Lahore division; confirmed as commissioner and superintendent, Nov., 1888; officiated as 2nd financial commissioner, Nov., 1890, to Dec., 1891, and again, Oct., 1892; confirmed, Feb., 1893.

RIVAZ, GEORGE WILMOT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Blackheath Proprietary School; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1870, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating cantonment magistrate; registrar, chief court, Punjab, 1877 to 1884; officiating inspector-general of registration and superintendent of stamps, Oct., 1884; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; district judge, Aug., 1886; divisional judge, March, 1891; edited the 2nd edition of the circular orders of the chief court.

RIVAZ, HENRY TULLIE, Barrister-at-Law, Punjab Judicial Department.—After officiating

as government advocate, Punjab, May-Nov., 1883, and March-April, 1888, and as judge of the Punjab chief court from 23rd April, 1889, was appointed additional judge of the court, 1st Nov., 1889; 4th judge, April, 1894; author of 'Acts and Regulations applicable to the Punjab,' commentaries on the Limitation and Registration Acts of 1877, 'The Punjab Income Tax Manual,' &c.

RIVETT-CARNAC, CHARLES JAMES, Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the department, 21st May, 1872; served in the N.W. Provinces till 1876, as assistant to the accountant-general, and officiated as personal assistant to the financial member of the governor-general's council, June, 1872; assistant to accountant-general, Punjab, Jan., 1876; officiating deputy accountant-general, Punjab, Aug., 1876; served again in the N.W. Provinces from March, 1878; assistant accountant-general, Bombay, Oct., 1882; assistant comptroller-general (in charge of forest branch), Oct., 1884; assistant accountant-general, and examiner of local accounts, Bengal, March, 1885; officiating comptroller, Central Provinces, March, 1889; confirmed, Nov., 1891; in 1893 acted as accountant-general, Punjab, and as deputy auditor-general.

RIVETT-CARNAC, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 12th Nov., 1858; assistant magistrate and collector, May, 1860; officiating secretary to income tax committee, June, 1861; officiating under secretary, government of India, home department, Sept., 1861; assistant secretary to the chief commissioner, Central Provinces, June, 1862; settlement officer, Chanda and Wardha, Aug., 1864; cotton commissioner, Central Provinces, Sept., 1866, and Berars, April, 1867; commissioner of cotton and commerce with the government of India, Dec., 1871; member and honorary secretary of central committee of international exhibitions; on duty in England, July, 1872, to Jan., 1873; special commissioner of transport, Bengal famine; opium agent, Benares, March, 1875; retired, Feb., 1894; author of reports on Indian cotton cultivation and trade, and on the interchange of traffic on Indian railways, &c.

ROBARTS, HARRY OCTAVIUS WARWICK, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 15th Dec., 1890, and served as assistant commissioner, and assistant magistrate and collector.

ROBB, JOHN, Surgeon-Major, M.D., C.M. (Aber.), late Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Indian medical service, 1st Oct., 1868; from Nov., 1873, to July, 1882, served as civil surgeon, Zanzibar, and acted as assistant political agent, in 1874-75 and again in 1881; from July, 1882, served under the government of Bombay, as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail at Aden, and at Surat; acting deputy sanitary commissioner, Sept.-Oct., 1883; civil surgeon, superintendent of lunatic asylum, and of the medical school, Ahmedabad, April, 1884; retired, Nov., 1889.

ROBERTS, ALLAN SCOTT, Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Infantry, Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 2nd April, 1862; from June, 1866, served

in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1884.

ROBERTS, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Colonel, Political Department, Government of India.—Cornet, Bengal cavalry, Feb., 1859; assistant to governor-general's agent, Rajputana, 1864; acted as political superintendent of Sirohi in 1868-69, and subsequently as political agent in various states of Rajputana; cantonment magistrate, Morar, Feb., 1875; assistant commissioner, Merwara, April, 1881; political assistant, Sujangarh, Aug., 1881, in charge of Bikanir and the Upper Rajputana thagi and dakaiti agency; in 1881-82 was employed in operations against the rebellious Bikanir thakurs and received the surrender of the fort of Nahargarh; assistant political agent, Banswara and Partabgarh, Aug., 1883; political agent and superintendent, Charkhari, Sept., 1884; cantonment magistrate, Nasirabad, Dec., 1885; political agent, Jacobabad, Dec., 1887; Kelat, March, 1888; Ulwar, May, 1888; assistant agent to the governor-general, Mount Abu, Nov., 1888; political agent, Kotah, April, 1889; cantonment magistrate, Nasirabad, Sept., 1889.

ROBERTS, CHARLES HAMILTON, Kumaun Commission.—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1874, and served as extra assistant commissioner, and cantonment magistrate; deputy collector, Jan., 1887; on special duty in Garhwal, April, 1890; superintendent, Tarai, Sept., 1890; deputy commissioner, Naini Tal, Oct., 1891.

ROBERTS, DAVID THOMAS, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Queen's College, Liverpool, Liverpool Institute, and London University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, April, 1879; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Dec., 1886; magistrate and collector, June, 1887; commissioner, April, 1894; author of 'Ballia Gazetteer' (in part).

ROBERTS (BARON), RIGHT HON. FREDERICK SLEIGH, General, V.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., R.A.—First commission, Bengal artillery, 1851; during the mutiny served at the siege and capture of Delhi, the relief of Lucknow, at Cawnpore, and the capture of Lucknow, and was present at numerous engagements (medal and three clasps, brevet of major, V.C.); deputy assistant quartermaster-general, Bengal, April, 1858; served in the N.W. frontier expedition, 1863 (medal and clasp); assistant quartermaster-general, Bengal, Feb., 1863; served with the Abyssinian expedition, 1868 (medal, brevet of lieut.-colonel), and with the Looshai expedition, 1871-72; deputy quartermaster-general, Bengal, Jan., 1872; quartermaster-general, Bengal, Jan., 1875; during the operations in Afghanistan in 1878-9-80, commanded the Kuram Valley field force, and subsequently the Cabul field force at the occupation of Cabul and battle of Charasiab, and in the march to the relief of Kandahar; received the thanks of both houses of parliament, and was created a baronet (medal with four clasps, bronze star, K.C.B., G.C.B.); commander-in-chief, Madras, and member of governor's council, Nov., 1881; commander-in-chief in India, and

member of the governor-general's council, 28th Nov., 1885, to 8th April, 1893; created a peer, Jan., 1892; G.C.S.I., June, 1893.

ROBERTS, SYDNEY GORDON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Haileybury College, and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

ROBERTS, THOMAS, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating small cause court judge, and officiating deputy commissioner; judicial assistant, 1st grade, April, 1877; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, Sept., 1883; additional commissioner, Jullundur, Aug., 1884; divisional judge, Nov., 1884 (2nd grade, Sept., 1886, and 1st grade, May, 1889); retired, April, 1891.

ROBERTS, WILLIAM HOWLAND, Surgeon-Colonel, M.D., late Madras Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, 23rd July, 1858; arrived, 24th Sept., 1858, and served in the Central Provinces till Sept., 1864, and subsequently in Madras as civil surgeon and superintendent of jail at Salem; acted as zillah surgeon, Coimbatore, July, 1866, to Jan., 1868; was also in charge of meteorological observatories at Coimbatore and Salem; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, and superintendent of fish-liver oil factory, Calicut, Aug., 1869; surgeon-major, July, 1870; superintendent of lunatic asylum, Calicut, May, 1872; surgeon, 2nd district, Madras, and health officer, and superintendent of lock hospital, April, 1881; reverted to military duty, Sept., 1885; retired, July, 1893.

ROBERTSON, BENJAMIN, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at the Institute, Elgin, the Grammar School and University of Aberdeen, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1885; assistant collector, June, 1886; transferred to Central Provinces as provincial superintendent of census operations, April, 1890; assistant commissioner, Oct., 1892.

ROBERTSON, CHARLES, Deputy Surgeon-General, M.D., late Madras Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, 4th Aug., 1856; arrived in India, 17th Dec., 1856; served under the military department from Dec., 1856, up to May, 1874, and also as zillah surgeon, Masulipatam, Jan., 1862, to March, 1865; from May, 1874, served as zillah surgeon, and superintendent of jail, Cuddalore; retired, Dec., 1885.

ROBERTSON, DONALD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—Ensign, Royal Scots Fusiliers, Aug., 1865; arrived in Madras, Oct., 1865; subaltern, 4th light cavalry, Madras, March, 1868; served from Nov., 1869, as assistant commissioner in the Central Provinces; transferred to Rajputana, June, 1872; cantonment magistrate, Nusseerabad, May, 1877; officiating assistant commissioner, Merwara and Ajmere, 1877-78; officiating superintendent, Jhallowar, 1879; 1st assistant agent to governor-general, Indore, 1881; political agent, Bhopawur, Feb., 1885; political agent, Bundelkhund, May, 1885; assis-

tant to the resident in Mysore, and secretary to the chief commissioner of Coorg, Dec., 1886; political agent in Baghclkhand, and superintendent of the Rewah state, Dec., 1888; resident, Gwalior, Feb., 1894.

ROBERTSON, ELPHINSTONE POURTALES, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 27th March, 1851, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, deputy commissioner of customs, salt, and opium, sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; on special duty in Bombay to report on abkari lands, March, 1868; senior collector and magistrate, Dharwar, Jan., 1873; revenue and police commissioner, Nov., 1876; commissioner of the central division, Jan., 1878; commissioner, 1st grade, July, 1881; retired, 1886.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at King's College, London; appointed after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; from Feb., 1882, served in the settlement department as assistant settlement officer; promoted to settlement officer, 3rd grade, April, 1884; settlement officer, 2nd grade, April, 1885; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, Feb., 1887; special duty, Maler Kotla settlement operations, Nov., 1888; officiating director of land records and agriculture, March, 1889, and was confirmed in that appointment, Dec., 1889; author of 'Customary Law of Rawalpindi District.'

ROBERTSON, GEORGE SCOTT, Surgeon-Major, C.S.I., Indian Medical Service.—Educated at Westminster Hospital; joined the service, 30th March, 1878; served through the Afghan war, 1879-80; civil surgeon, N.W. Provinces, 1880-88; on deputation to Gilgit, June, 1888; agency surgeon, Gilgit, July, 1889; on special duty in Kafiristan, 1890-91; chief political officer, Hunza-Nagar expedition, 1891; C.S.I., May, 1892; officiating agent, Gilgit, May-Oct., 1892; mission to Gor and capture of Chilas, 1892; in charge, Chitral mission, 1893; officiating agent, Gilgit, June, 1893; confirmed, Jan., 1894.

ROBERTSON, JAMES CASAMAIJOR, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1854; arrived in India, 31st Jan., 1855; from Jan., 1856, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant superintendent, Dehra Dun, small cause court judge, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, Allahabad, Oct., 1871; officiating inspector-general of registration, and commissioner of excise and stamps, July, 1876; civil and sessions judge, April, 1879; commissioner, Agra division, Jan., 1882, and subsequently of the Rohilkhund division, Feb., 1883; officiated as member of the board of revenue on various occasions, 1885-89; retired, Jan., 1890.

ROBERTSON, JOHN HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Sir Roger Cholmeley's School, Highgate, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in Madras, 29th Nov., 1889, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

ROBERTSON, LAURENCE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Glasgow Academy, Glasgow University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM, Funds Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India audit office, 27th Feb., 1886; transferred to funds department, India office, 3rd Dec., 1894.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM ROWNTREE, M.R.A.C., late Madras Education Department.—Arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1868; superintendent, government farms, Nov., 1868; on special duty to Mysore and Bangalore, Jan., 1876; superintendent of the school of agriculture, Saidapet, Oct., 1876; fellow of Madras University, May, 1882; superintendent, government farms and school of agriculture, and agricultural reporter to government of Madras, Nov., 1882; principal, agricultural college, Saidapet, April, 1884; member of committee in connection with scheme of technical education, Sept., 1885; retired, April, 1889.

ROBINSON, ALEXANDER, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Royal School, Armagh, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1865; from Jan., 1867, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; promoted to joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1879; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Fyzabad, Nov., 1884; magistrate and collector, April, 1887; retired, Sept., 1892; author of 'Memoirs of the Ballia and Jaunpur Districts.'

ROBINSON, WILLIAM, B.A., Military Department, India Office.—Educated at Carmichael School, Queen's College, Cork, and the Royal University of Ireland; appointed junior clerk, India office, 11th June, 1894; resident clerk, Dec., 1894.

ROBSON, SAMUEL, M.A., Bengal Education Department.—Educated at Edinburgh University; joined the department, 20th Dec., 1878, as professor, Presidency college; transferred to Patna college, June, 1880; and to Hooghly college, Jan., 1883; again professor, Patna college, Aug., 1888.

ROE, CHARLES ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating small cause court judge, officiating deputy commissioner, and settlement officer; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1878; additional commissioner, Lahore and Rawal Pindi divisions, May, 1883; divisional judge, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; officiating judge, chief court, March, 1885, April, 1887, and Nov., 1887; divisional judge, 1st grade, Feb., 1887; additional judge, chief court, Punjab, Nov., 1888; judge, chief court, Punjab, April, 1889; author of 'Customary Law of the Mooltan District.'

ROE, FRANCIS REGINALD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Winchester and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 8th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

ROE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER CRAWFORD, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; in military employ till 22nd March, 1878, when he was appointed to the department as officiating civil surgeon, Jullundur; in 1879 served with Kurram valley field force; medical adviser to Maharaja of Kapurthala, April to Oct., 1885; civil surgeon, 1st class, June, 1887; officiating sanitary commissioner, Punjab, 1888-89, 1893, and from March, 1894.

ROGERS, ALEXANDER, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, 1845; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1845; served in the Bombay revenue and judicial department, and was appointed 1st assistant collector and magistrate, Broach, Oct., 1851, and afterwards at Ahmedabad, Oct., 1852; settlement officer, Guzerat, Nov., 1852; collector and magistrate, Ahmedabad, March, 1860, and afterwards at Surat; revenue and police commissioner, northern division, April, 1865; member of council, Bombay, June, 1872; retired in 1879; author of a translation of three modern Persian plays, a 'History of the Land Revenue Settlement of Bombay,' and a translation of 'Yusuf and Zuleika'; has also edited the 'Bostan' of Sa'adi.

ROGERS, ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Bedford Grammar School and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Dec., 1890.

ROGERS, HENRY THOMAS, Major-General, R.E., late Revenue Survey Department, Madras.—2nd lieutenant, Dec., 1849; arrived in India, 28th Aug., 1852; served in the public works department, Bengal, from 1853 to 1859, and subsequently in Madras as assistant engineer and executive engineer; acted as comptroller of public works accounts, 1864, and as principal of the Civil Engineering College, Feb. to Aug., 1865; acting mint master, July, 1865; officiating superintending engineer, Sept.-Oct., 1870; fellow of the University of Madras, March, 1872; principal, Civil Engineering College, April, 1871, to Jan., 1880; member of the committee for the management of the school of arts, March, 1877; from Nov., 1879, served as superintendent of revenue survey, Madras; retired, July, 1886.

ROGERS, MALCOLM WILLIAM, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Survey of India.—Lieutenant, royal engineers, Dec., 1861; arrived in India, Oct., 1865; assistant surveyor, great trigonometrical survey of India, July, 1866; served with the Quetta field force as survey officer, 1878-79 (medal, brevet of major); deputy superintendent of survey, July, 1882; assistant surveyor-general in charge of mathematical instrument office, and correspondence office, Calcutta, May, 1884; 3rd grade, deputy superintendent, Aug., 1884; and 2nd grade, June, 1889; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892; assistant surveyor-general in charge of surveyor-general's office and mathematical instrument office, from May, 1889.

ROGERS-HARRISON, AUGUSTUS NAPOLEON, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1879, and was employed under the military department

until Dec., 1880, when he was appointed civil surgeon and superintendent of jail and lunatic asylum, Vizagapatam; civil surgeon, Salem, June, 1887.

ROLLAND, ALEXANDER TULLOCH, Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, April, 1856; arrived in India, 11th May, 1856; served in the Madras police department from Dec., 1862, as assistant superintendent, and superintendent; deputy inspector-general of police, Feb., 1885; acted as commissioner of police, Madras city, in 1889; reverted to military department, Oct., 1893.

ROLLO, JAMES THEODORE, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 7th Oct., 1870, and posted to Rajputana; transferred to Assam, June, 1880; executive engineer, May, 1881; transferred to Burma, June, 1892.

ROMESH CHUNDER DUTT, C.I.E., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Colootola Branch School, Calcutta, Presidency College, Calcutta University, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 28th Sept., 1871; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, June, 1882; magistrate and collector, March, 1888; acting commissioner, Burdwan, April, 1894; has published the following works: 'Three Years in Europe,' 1872; 'Peasantry of Bengal,' 1876; 'Literature of Bengal,' 1877; 'Satabarsa' (Historical Tales), and a 'Short History of India' in Bengali, 1878; a Bengali translation of the Rig Veda Sanhita; a popular account of the gods of the Rig Veda; a 'History of Civilisation in Ancient India,' 1888-89; a 'Brief History of Ancient and Modern India,' 1891; and 'Lays of Ancient India,' 1894.

ROMESH CHUNDER MITTER, Sir, B.L.—Puisne judge, high court of judicature, Calcutta, 30th March, 1874; acted as chief justice in 1882; retired, 1890; additional member of the council of the governor-general for making laws and regulations to Aug., 1891.

RONSON, WILLIAM TOMLINSON, Revenue and Statistics Department, India Office.—Writer, 1st Sept., 1873; clerk, 2nd class, Feb., 1879; junior clerk, Feb., 1894; compiler of the reports on sanitary measures in India annually presented to Parliament for the years 1878-79 to 1892-93 inclusive.

ROSE, BENJAMIN JAMES, Revenue and Statistics Department, India Office.—Educated at Williams' Grammar School, Clapham, and the Birkbeck Institution; entered the India office, 23rd Feb., 1871; technical assistant in the revenue and statistics department, June, 1882; technical assistant and clerk to the special assistant, 1891; awarded gold medal at the Amsterdam exhibition of 1883, for statistical diagrams shewing the condition and progress of British India; prepared abstract catalogue of the Indian section at the same exhibition; also a report on wheat, 1889; and on Madras tobacco, 1890.

ROSE, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Folkingham Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1868, and served in

the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, officiating magistrate and collector, and officiating district and sessions judge; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Oudh, Oct., 1883; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Nov., 1883; magistrate and collector, Aug., 1887; officiating commissioner, Gorakhpur, April, 1894.

ROSE, FRANCIS ERNEST, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 1st Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Provinces; transferred to the Punjab, Nov., 1866; executive engineer, Jan., 1877.

ROSE, HORACE ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at St. Paul's School and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 4th Oct., 1888, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating district judge.

ROSS, ALLAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Beaumont College, Windsor, and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 7th Jan., 1892, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

ROSS, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, Kt., C.S.I., Colonel, I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—Ensign, 3rd European regiment, Bombay, May, 1855; served in Central India during the mutiny, and was present at the siege and capture of Jhansi (medal and clasp); assistant to political agent, Muscat, 1863-69; assistant political agent, Gwadur, 1869; political agent and consul, Muscat, April, 1871; local lieutenant-colonel in Persian Gulf, May, 1872; political resident, Persian Gulf, from Oct., 1872; graded as resident, 2nd class; H.M.'s consul-general for the province of Fars, and the coasts and islands of the Persian Gulf, Nov., 1878; officiating resident, Hyderabad, April, 1886; political resident, 2nd class, Persian Gulf, Oct., 1886; reverted to military department, 1891; Kt., Jan., 1892; author of several reports and memoranda on subjects connected with the Persian Gulf.

ROSS, GEORGE CUMBERLAND, Surgeon-Colonel, Medical Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866; served from Nov., 1867, as civil surgeon in various stations in the Punjab; civil surgeon, 1st class, Oct., 1881; administrative medical officer and sanitary commissioner, Central Provinces, Jan., 1894.

ROSS, HENRY THOMAS, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Perth Academy, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting cantonment magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, forest settlement officer, and acting district and sessions judge; special assistant to the collector and magistrate, Malabar, Sept., 1884; acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, Aug., 1885; registrar, high court, Feb., 1886; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Sept., 1888; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1889.

ROSS, HERCULES GREY, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College,

1856; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1856; from April, 1858, served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, city magistrate at Lucknow, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; superintendent, Dehra Dun, Dec., 1869; magistrate and collector, Benares, Oct., 1882; officiating commissioner, Benares division, and agent to governor-general, March, 1883; on special duty, Dehra Dun, March, 1883; settlement officer, 2nd grade, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1883; commissioner, Kumaon, April, 1885; retired, Nov., 1888.

ROSSITER, CHARLES ARTHUR, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Appointed supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

ROUSE, ROBERT, Deputy Surgeon-General, late Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the Indian medical department in 1854; arrived in India, 31st Jan., 1855; from May, 1869, served as civil surgeon and officiating superintendent of jail in the Punjab; brigade surgeon, Nov., 1879; retired, Dec., 1885.

ROWE, FREDERICK JAMES, M.A., Education Department, Bengal.—Joined the service, 15th June, 1870, and served as professor at Krishnaghur, Dacca, and Hooghly colleges, also as inspector of schools and officiating principal, Krishnaghur college; professor, Presidency college, July, 1880; officiating principal of the Madrassa college, in addition to his own duties, 1892-94; joint author of various educational works, and has edited selections from Tennyson, &c.

ROWE, JOSIAH EBENEZER, Punjab Commission.—Joined the service, 20th March, 1858, and served in the Punjab in various capacities; extra assistant commissioner, Oct., 1865; small cause court judge in 1874, 1875, and from 1881 to 1884; extra judicial assistant, Nov., 1884.

ROWLANDS, PERCY JONES, Registry Department, India Office.—East India house writer, old establishment, 1st Sept., 1858; assistant to registrar, June, 1877.

ROY, MADHUB CHUNDER, Rai Bahadur, B.A., B.C.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engineering College; appointed, 16th Sept., 1862, and posted to Bengal; executive engineer, March, 1872.

ROYLE, JOSEPH R. E. J., C.I.E.—Educated at King's College School, London; entered Indian army from Addiscombe, 7th June, 1861; lieutenant, H.M.'s 107th foot, 1862; retired, Jan., 1867; on the extra staff of the India museum, 1st Jan., 1867; secretary, Indian commission for Vienna exhibition, 1873; superintendent, India Museum, 1874-79; clerk to the special assistant in the statistics and commerce department, India office, Oct., 1879, to 1891; executive commissioner for government of India, Amsterdam exhibition, 1883; assistant secretary for India to royal commission for the Colonial and Indian exhibition, 1886, and official agent for the government of India; C.I.E., 1886; curator of Indian section, Imperial Institute, 1891; author of the official reports on the Indian section of the Amsterdam exhibition of 1883, and on the Indian section of the Colonial and Indian exhibition, 1886; also of a lecture before the Society of Arts, June, 1882, on the 'Production of Tea, Cinchona, Wild Silks, and Rhea in India.'

RUNDALL, FRANCIS HORNBLow, General, C.S.I., R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Madras engineers, 1841; assistant civil engineer, 1844; civil engineer, public works department, Ganjam, 1854; district engineer, Rajahmundry, May, 1855; consulting engineer, Madras irrigation and canal company, Oct., 1859; chief engineer of irrigation and joint secretary to government of Bengal, July, 1867; chief engineer, 1st class, Aug., 1869; officiating inspector-general and deputy secretary to government of India, irrigation branch, Jan., 1871; inspector-general of irrigation and deputy secretary to government of India, April, 1872, to April, 1874.

RUNDALL, JAMES WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—Joined Madras public works department as super-numerary engineer, 2nd Aug., 1855, and served in the Godaveri, Tinnevelly, and Bellary districts; executive engineer, July, 1863; on special duty under chief engineer, May, 1877; superintendent of works, Kistna, Aug., 1880; superintending engineer, Oct., 1881; member of committee for drawing up navigation rules, Nov., 1882; 2nd class superintending engineer, April, 1884; retired, 1889.

RUSSELL, CHARLES LENNOX SOMERVILLE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Rugby, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

RUSSELL, EDGAR GEER, M.B., B.Sc., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Department.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and served on general and military duty, and as officiating inspector of emigrants, superintendent of Campbell medical school and hospital, professor of medical jurisprudence, Calcutta medical college, and police surgeon, Calcutta; officiating civil surgeon and superintendent of central jail, Rajshahye, Nov., 1888; civil surgeon, Patna, and superintendent of Temple medical school, March, 1890; civil surgeon, Darjeeling, April, 1892; Dacca, March, 1894; author of a work on 'Malaria and Injuries of Spleen.'

RUSSELL, SIR EDWARD LECHMERE, General, K.C.S.I.—Ensign, 12th N.I., Bombay, Jan., 1838; served in the Afghan war of 1842, and the Sind campaign of 1842-43, and was adjutant of the Sind irregular horse in the battles of Meanee and Hyderabad, and the surrender of Omerekote; fort adjutant, Bombay, Jan., 1855; remount agent, May, 1857; commanding brigade, Deesa, March, 1863, and subsequently at Bombay; political resident and commanding brigade, at Aden, Dec., 1867; commanding northern division, Bombay, Dec., 1870, to 1876; placed on unemployed list, July, 1881.

RUSSELL, GEORGE WILLIAM ERSKINE, M.P.—Son of Lord Charles James Fox Russell, grandson of the 6th Duke of Bedford, and nephew of the 1st Earl Russell; educated at Harrow, and University College, Oxford; M.P., Aylesbury, 1880-85; parliamentary secretary to local government board, 1883-85; alderman of the county of London, Feb., 1889; M.P. for North Bedfordshire, 1892; under-secretary of state for India, August, 1892; under-secretary of state, home department, March, 1894; author of the 'Life of Mr. Gladstone' in the 'Queen's Prime Ministers' series.

RUSSELL, ROBERT VANE, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Winchester, and

Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived, 5th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

RUSSELL, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Santry School and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, forest settlement officer, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate; special assistant to collector, magistrate, and agent, Ganjam, Feb., 1885; sub-collector and joint magistrate, April, 1890; district and sessions judge, Chingleput, Feb., 1893.

RUTHERFORD, DAVID, Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, July, 1867, as probationary assistant engineer; executive engineer, Feb., 1873.

RUTHERFORD, JAMES, Colonel, late Mysore Revenue Survey.—First commission, ensign, 4th Aug., 1859; joined the Mysore revenue survey department, Jan., 1865; deputy superintendent, April, 1881; reverted to military department, Nov., 1892.

RUTHERFORD, JOHN MURIC, late Railway Department, Government of India.—Was specially trained in Europe for the telegraph department, and was appointed assistant superintendent, 4th grade, Oct., 1869; arrived in India, 8th Jan., 1870, and served in the Punjab, and in Rajputana; acted as assistant manager, Rajputana state railway, in addition to his own duties, June-Nov., 1879; transferred to traffic department of state railways, March, 1880, and served as district traffic superintendent on the Rajputana state railway; Indus Valley state railway, March, 1882; class 1 of the state railway revenue establishment, Jan., 1886; deputy traffic superintendent, Lahore, North-Western railway, July, 1886; traffic superintendent, North-Western railway, Oct., 1888; retired, 1891.

RYLAND, WILLIAM HENRY, late Judicial Department, Bengal.—From 10th Dec., 1852, served in the subordinate executive service, Bengal, and was appointed deputy magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, March, 1866; officiating collector of stamps, and superintendent of abkhari revenue, March, 1868; officiating assistant secretary, government of Bengal, May, 1869; from Dec., 1874, served in the judicial department, and was appointed subordinate judge, 1st grade, and judge of the small cause court at Sealdah, April, 1875; officiating superintendent of stamps and stationery, April, 1880; retired, April, 1887.

RYVES, ROBERT HUGH, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner.

RYVES, THOMAS JAMES, Local Capt., Police Department, N.W. Provinces.—Served as a volunteer in the Bengal yeomanry cavalry from July, 1857, to Jan., 1859, and received the mutiny medal and the local rank of lieutenant for distinguished services in the field; adjutant of military police, Bijnor, 1859; assistant inspector-general, Rohilkund, April, 1861; from Aug., 1861, served as superintendent of police in various districts, and was promoted to the 1st

grade, June, 1885; assistant inspector-general, railway police, June, 1888; deputy inspector-general, June, 1892.

SABONADIERE, ALFRED, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Charterhouse and St. John's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 19th Jan., 1888, and served as assistant commissioner, and assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, March, 1893.

ST. QUINTIN, WILLIAM ASTELL, Auditor's Department, India Office.—Educated at Cheltenham College and Blackheath Proprietary School; temporary clerk, 31st Dec., 1866; junior examiner, Dec., 1868; senior examiner, Nov., 1878; designation changed to senior clerk, Oct., 1892.

SAIYID MAHMUD, B.L., late High Court, North-Western Provinces.—Educated at the Delhi Government College, Queen's College, Benares, and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service as district judge, 3rd grade, Oudh, 1st Aug., 1879; served temporarily under foreign department of government of India, June, 1881, to May, 1882; acting puisne judge, high court, N.W. Provinces, May, 1882, and again in 1884 and 1886; served under education committee, Nov., 1882, to March, 1883; district judge, Rae Bareli, Nov., 1883; judge of high court, N.W. Provinces, 9th May, 1887; retired, Dec., 1893; author of 'The Law of Evidence in British India' (in Hindustani).

SALAMAN, SELIM MYER, Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., M.A., M.D., M.Ch., M.R.C.S., Bombay Jail Department.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Bijapur, Nov., 1870; from Feb., 1872, served under the military department until June, 1874, when he was appointed officiating civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Shikarpur; acting superintendent, Yerauda central jail, June, 1876; confirmed, May, 1878; in addition superintendent, Poona city jail, Dec., 1878; in 1892 acted as inspector-general of registration, &c.

SALIG RAM, Rao Bahadur, late Postal Department, N.W. Provinces.—Served in the postal department, N.W. Provinces, from March, 1847, as clerk, inspecting postmaster, and head assistant in the office of the postmaster-general; inspector, post offices, Agra division, July, 1876; superintendent, Agra division, April, 1881; postmaster-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, June, 1884; retired, 1887.

SALISBURY (MARQUESS OF), RIGHT HON. ROBERT ARTHUR TALBOT GASCOIGNE CECIL, K.G., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D., M.A.—Born in 1830; educated at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated, and was elected a fellow of All Souls' College in 1853; represented Stamford in Parliament from 1853 to April, 1868, when he succeeded to the Upper House; secretary of state for India, July, 1866, to March, 1867; chancellor of the university of Oxford, Nov., 1869; in 1871-72 he acted with Lord Cairns in conducting the investigation into the affairs of the London, Chatham, and Dover Railway Company; secretary of state for India, second time, Feb., 1874; was sent as special ambassador to the Sublime Porte in 1876, and acted with Sir Henry Elliot as joint plenipotentiaries of Great Britain at the conference of Constantinople, Dec., 1876; he was appointed secretary of state for foreign affairs on the resignation of Lord

Derby, April, 1878, and afterwards accompanied the Earl of Beaconsfield to Berlin, as representative of Great Britain at the congress held there; invested with the Order of the Garter, July, 1878; prime minister and again secretary of state for foreign affairs, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; prime minister and first lord of the treasury, Aug., 1886; prime minister and again secretary of state for foreign affairs, Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; has served on the royal commissions on historical manuscripts, 1882, on the housing of the working classes, 1884, and for the Indian and Colonial exhibition, 1886; is honorary colonel, Bedfordshire regiment, and an elder brother of the Trinity House.

SALMON, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 11th May, 1859, and served on military duty till March, 1867, and subsequently in the political department as acting assistant resident, Baroda, acting political agent, Rewa Kantha, and acting assistant political agent in Kathiawar on special duty; personal assistant to political agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1871; acting assistant superintendent for thuggee and dacoity, Baroda, Feb.-March, 1873; served as political assistant in Kathiawar up to Jan., 1879, and was in charge of the Gondal state and of Hala Prant, July-Aug., 1877; from Feb., 1879, served as assistant political agent, Kolaba, in charge Janjira state; acting political agent, Mahi Kantha, Aug.-Nov., 1882; assistant political agent in charge of Southern Maratha country Jaghirs, Aug., 1883; joint administrator of Rajpipla, July, 1884; joint administrator of Sangli, Feb., 1885; assistant to the governor-general's agent in charge of the Okhamandal district, and commandant Wagheer corps, Nov., 1887; political agent, Cutch, and Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; political superintendent, Palanpur, July, 1893; acting political agent, Kolhapur, June, 1894.

SAMMAN, HERBERT FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at King Edward VI.'s School, Stratford-on-Avon, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 4th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, April, 1894.

SAMPSON, ARTHUR BISKS, B.A., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Assistant secretary, public works department, May, 1859; under secretary to government of India, public works department, general branch, Sept., 1869; retired, May, 1887.

SAMUELLS, CHARLES ARCHIBALD, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King's College School, Blackheath Proprietary School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1869; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector, and was placed on famine duty at Chumparun, March, 1874; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1881; officiating junior secretary, board of revenue, March, 1885; officiating port commissioner, Calcutta, April, 1886; magistrate and collector, Oct., 1886; officiating commissioner of excise, Bengal, April to Aug., 1892.

SAMUELLS, WILLIAM LEYCESTER, Colonel, I.S.C. — First commission, 20th Nov., 1857; served from 18th Dec., 1864, in the non-regulation commission, Bengal, as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner; special boundary commissioner (Rewah and Chota Nagpur), 1868-71; officiating cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, Barrackpore, April, 1873; officiating political agent, Hill Tipperah, April, 1875; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, Aug., 1885; 2nd grade, Aug., 1887; retired, April, 1890.

SANDELL, JOSEPH WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Postal Department. — Joined the service, 1st March, 1862, and held various subordinate posts until Dec., 1878, when he was appointed chief assistant to the deputy accountant general, Assam; rank of chief superintendent, finance department, Jan., 1881; transferred to office of accountant general, Bombay, Sept., 1881; comptroller, Calcutta post office, Jan., 1890.

SANDEMAN, JOHN EDWARD, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Survey of India Department. — Joined at Addiscombe, 1860; ensign, Bengal infantry, 7th June, 1861; arrived in India, Oct., 1861; studied at Roorkee College, and obtained certificate qualifying for engineer, public works department, Sept., 1864; appointed to the survey department, Oct., 1864, and served as assistant superintendent in the Central Provinces, Oudh, and the Punjab; served with the Hazara expedition, 1868 (medal); in charge of Midnapur survey, Bengal, 1873; Goalpara survey, Assam, Dec., 1873; Behar irrigation survey, Oct., 1874; in charge of British Burma cadastral survey, Dec., 1878; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, survey of India, Jan., 1879; in charge of Mirzapur and Gorakpur district cadastral survey, from Nov., 1883; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892; director of Bengal surveys, April, 1892.

SANDERS, EDWIN, Surgeon-Major, late Bengal Medical Department. — Served under the government of Bengal from 1st Nov., 1860, to May, 1871; officiating civil surgeon, Chittagong, March, 1881; surgeon-major, April, 1881; civil surgeon, Moorsheebad, June, 1889; retired, Jan., 1891.

SANDERS, JAMES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh). — Educated at the Collegiate School and University of Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector and assistant commissioner; officiating city magistrate and wasika officer, Lucknow, May-Aug., 1886, and again in 1888 and 1889; joint magistrate, March, 1888; deputy commissioner, April, 1894.

SANDERS, RICHARD CARELESS, M.D., F.R.C.S., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service. — First commission, 1869; in military employ till July, 1874, from which date he served as surgeon and civil surgeon at various stations in the N.W. Provinces; transferred to Bengal as officiating ophthalmic surgeon and professor of ophthalmic medicine and surgery, medical college, Calcutta, April, 1884; confirmed, Aug., 1886.

SANDHURST (BARON), RIGHT HON. WILLIAM MANSFIELD. — Born, 1855; educated at Rugby; sub-lieutenant, Coldstream guards, 1875; succeeded his father (who had been chief

of the staff during the Indian Mutiny, commander-in-chief, Bombay, 1860-65, commander-in-chief in India, 1865-70, and commander-in-chief, Ireland, 1870-75) in the title in 1876; resigned his commission, 1879; acted as a lord in waiting to H.M. the Queen from Sept., 1880, to June, 1885; under secretary of state for war, Feb.-Aug., 1886, and again from Aug., 1892; has been nominated to succeed Lord Harris as governor of Bombay.

SANDIFORD, CHARLES THOMAS, M.I.C.E., Railway Branch, Government of India. — Joined the department from Scinde, Punjab, and Delhi railway as locomotive superintendent, North-Western state railway, Jan., 1886; class 1, state railway revenue establishment, July, 1886; class 1, grade 1, superior revenue establishment, permanent, April, 1887.

SANFORD, GEORGE EDWARD LANGHAM SOMERSET, C.B., C.S.I., Major-General, R.E., late Military Works Department, Government of India. — Lieutenant, R.E., Oct., 1856; served in the China war, 1858 (medal and clasp); served in the public works department (military works), April, 1872, to Jan., 1878, as assistant and executive engineer, and in the Jowaki campaign, 1878 (medal and clasp); assistant quartermaster-general, Bengal, Feb., 1878, to March, 1880; during this period served in the Afghan campaign, and was present at the capture of Ali Musjid (medal and clasp, brevet of lieutenant-colonel); deputy quartermaster-general, Bengal, March, 1880, to Nov., 1885; commanded Royal Engineers in Burmese expedition, 1885-86; C.B., Nov., 1886; inspector-general of military works, March, 1886; chief engineer, 2nd class, March, 1887; 1st class, March, 1889; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; director-general, military works, Sept., 1890; chief engineer, military works, Bombay, April, 1893; commanding at Meerut, Sept., 1893.

SANKEY, SIR RICHARD HIERAM, Lieutenant-General, K.C.B., late R.E. — Ensign, 11th Dec., 1846; arrived in India as supernumerary second lieutenant, Madras engineers, Nov., 1848; officiating superintending engineer, Nagpore subsidiary force, Feb., 1850, to June, 1853; superintendent of the east coast canal, 1856-57; additional under secretary, government of India, public works department, March, 1857; on outbreak of mutiny proceeded with special appointment to Allahabad, acting for a time as superintending engineer and consulting engineer for railways, N.W. Provinces; acted as field engineer at Cawnpore, and was subsequently senior engineer officer with the Gurkha force under Jung Bahadur; executive engineer and superintendent of convicts, Moulmein, Jan.-Nov., 1859, and subsequently garrison engineer, Fort William; assistant to chief engineer, Mysore, June, 1861; chief engineer, Mysore, 1866 to 1877; services placed by Lord Mayo at disposal of government of Victoria for specially reporting on the waterworks of the colony, March-Oct., 1871; deputy secretary to government of India, Sept., 1877; commanding engineer of the South Afghanistan expeditionary force under lieutenant-general Stewart, C.B., Oct., 1878, to Feb., 1879; chief engineer, 1st class, and secretary to government of Madras, public works department, April, 1879; fellow of Madras University, March, 1881; resigned the public works department in 1883; retired from the army, Jan., 1884; chairman, board of works, Dublin.

SAPTE, BRAND, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Winchester; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury, 1843; served in the revenue and judicial departments in Bengal, the Punjab, and N.W. Provinces; magistrate and collector, Bulandshahr, 1857, and subsequently at Meerut; C.B. in 1859; from 1864 to 1865 served as judge of Ghazipur, and subsequently of Farakhabad; retired, 1871.

SARGEAUNT, CHARLES CHESTER, Colonel, I.S.C., late Revenue Survey Department, Madras.—Ensign, 31st May, 1856; arrived in India, 25th Sept., 1856, and served under the military department till Feb., 1867, and subsequently in the revenue survey department, Madras, as assistant superintendent; was on special duty in Madras, 1873, and 1875-76, and in England, 1883; deputy-superintendent of revenue survey, Dec., 1881; on special duty at Coonoor to revise and republish the survey manual, April, 1886; colonel in the army, May, 1886; superintendent of revenue survey, June, 1886; retired, July, 1887.

SARGEAUNT, RICHARD ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Railway Department, Government of India.—1st commission, Dec., 1861; joined the Bombay public works department, May, 1864; assistant consulting engineer for railways, Nov., 1864; served in the Abyssinian expedition of 1868 (medal); examiner of railway accounts, Bombay, April, 1872; acting examiner, guaranteed railway accounts, Calcutta, Dec., 1875; deputy consulting engineer for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, May, 1877; acted as consulting engineer for railways in 1878; secretary to railway conference, Jan., 1879; executive engineer, 1st grade, Jan., 1880; revenue assistant, railway branch, public works secretariat, government of India, 1880; manager, Northern Bengal state railway, in class 1 of the state railway revenue establishment, July, 1882; officiating under secretary to government of India, railway branch, Feb., 1883; officiating manager, Punjab Northern state railway, June, 1884; officiating manager of East Bengal railway, June, 1887; officiating consulting engineer for railways, and officiating joint secretary, railway department, Bombay, May, 1888; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889; officiating director-general of railways, and deputy secretary to government of India, public works department, Aug., 1890; confirmed, May, 1892.

SARGENT, SIR CHARLES, Knt., M.A., B.L., Judicial Department, Bombay.—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), Nov., 1848; appointed judge of the high court, Bombay, 29th April, 1866; chief justice, Aug., 1882.

SATYENDRANATH TAGORE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Presidency College, and Calcutta University; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived, 12th Dec., 1864, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, acting judge of the small cause court, Ahmednagar, and judge and sessions judge; 1st grade judge, Sholapur-Bijapur, Aug., 1883.

SAUNDERS, ARTHUR LESLIE, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at the Oratory School, Edgbaston, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open

competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1882, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to the Central Provinces, Oct., 1885; acted as under secretary to chief commissioner, at various periods from 1887 to 1890; assistant commissioner, Oct., 1888; acting commissioner of excise, March, 1891; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commissioner of excise, April, 1892; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1893; also second secretary to chief commissioner, Feb., 1894.

SAUNDERS, LESLIE HARRY, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Dulwich College and Wadham College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1887; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1889, and served as assistant commissioner, and officiating under secretary; officiating superintendent, northern Shan states, Sept., 1893, to May, 1894; officiating deputy commissioner, June, 1894.

SAVAGE, HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Liverpool Institute; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 23rd Oct., 1874; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Sept., 1885; magistrate and collector, Jan., 1891.

SCANNELL, HENRY, Police Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, 28th May, 1868, as acting assistant superintendent of police; deputy conservator of forests, Feb., 1871; assistant ditto, May, 1871; assistant superintendent of police, Feb., 1872; superintendent, Jan., 1874; superintendent of police, Great Indian Peninsula railway, Dec., 1890; officiating inspector-general, April-Dec., 1893.

SCHARLIEB, WILLIAM MASON, Barrister-at-Law, late Judicial Department, Madras.—Served from 25th March, 1847, in various subordinate posts, and was appointed first uncovenanted assistant to the board of revenue, June, 1862; resigned the service to practise at the bar, Oct., 1867; acting administrator-general, Madras, in 1867 and 1874; acting government pleader, May, 1874; assistant secretary to government, judicial and legislative departments, Dec., 1874; from April to Oct., 1879, acted as first judge of the court of small causes, Madras, and again in 1885 and 1888, and served from 1885 as presidency magistrate for the town of Madras; board of examiners, Jan., 1882; has acted as chief presidency magistrate for various periods since 1884; retired, 1890.

SCHLICH, W., Ph.D., C.I.E., late Forest Department, Government of India.—Appointed by the secretary of state for India as an assistant conservator of forests, Dec., 1866; conservator of forests, Bengal, Dec., 1872; transferred to the Punjab, 1880; inspector-general of forests, April, 1883; retired, 1888, and was appointed principal professor of forestry at the Royal Indian Engineering College, Cooper's Hill; author of 'A Manual of Forestry,' &c.

SCHNEIDER, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, General, K.C.B.—Educated at Christ's Hospital, and afterwards at the military college at Addiscombe; ensign, 2nd native infantry, Bombay, 20th Feb., 1841; served in the Southern Maharratta campaign, 1845, and was present at the

capture of the Panalla and Powanghur forts; second in command and commandant of the Kolhapur infantry, and also assistant political superintendent, 1852-59; in 1857-58, took part in the suppression of the mutiny at Kolhapur, and the recapture of the city (mutiny medal); commissioner under Act XIV. of 1857 for the summary trial and punishment of offences against the State; president of the civil and military committee at Bombay for the examination of officers in native languages, 1859-61; commanded 2nd regiment, Bombay infantry, 1859-66; served in the Abyssinian expedition of 1867-68, and commanded a brigade at action of Arogee, and assault and capture of Magdala; medal, and C.B., Aug., 1868; judge advocate-general, Bombay army, 1868-72; political resident at Aden, 1872-77; also commanded Aden brigade; commanded northern division, Bombay army, 1877-81; K.C.B., May, 1889.

SCOBLE, SIR ANDREW RICHARD, K.C.S.I., Q.C., M.P.—Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1856; held the offices of clerk of the crown and commissioner for taking accounts and for local investigations, under the high court, Bombay, 1863-66; was an advocate of the high court, 1866-76, and successively held the offices of sheriff, 1866-67, legal remembrancer, 1869-72, and advocate-general, 1872-77; conducted the prosecution of the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1875; was also additional member of the council of the governor of Bombay, 1870-76; Q.C., 1876; member of the governor-general's council, 1886 to 1891; C.S.I., Jan., 1889; K.C.S.I., May, 1890; M.P. for Central Hackney, 1892.

SCONCE, GIDEON COLQUHOUN, B.L., late Judicial Department, Bengal.—Officiating magistrate, Calcutta, 12th Nov., 1866; clerk of the Calcutta court of small causes, Dec., 1870; judge of the Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1874; promoted to third judge, Oct., 1884; chief judge, Oct., 1889; retired, Oct., 1893.
