SCONCE, JAMES, Colonel, I.S.C., late Survey Department.—Joined at Addiscombe, Feb., 1852; lieutenant, Bengal artillery, Dec., 1853; arrived in India, April, 1854; during the mutiny served at the siege and capture of Delhi, and in the operations leading to the relief of Lucknow, also during the capture of Lucknow, March, 1858 (medal and clasps); served from Oct., 1859, as assistant revenue surveyor in the Bundelcund and Central Provinces surveys; revenue surveyor, Jan., 1863; served in Bengal from March, 1866, in the office of superintendent of revenue surveys, Calcutta, and at Hazaribagh, Midnapur, and Arrah; deputy superintendent of survey, 2nd grade, Nov., 1868; deputy surveyor-general (revenue survey branch), Jan., 1879; has also officiated as surveyor-general of India; reverted to military duty, April, 1891.

SCORGIE, JAMES, F.C.S., late Educational Department, Bombay.—Joined the service as head master of the Akola High School, Berar, 12th Nov., 1866; in charge of the office of director of public instruction, Berar, April-June, 1872; principal, Jeypore school of arts, Oct., 1872; from Feb., 1875, served as professor of mechanism and applied science, Civil Engineering College, Poona; 3rd grade of the education department, March, 1883; retired, Aug., 1890.

SCOTLAND, SIR COLLEY H., Knight.—Educated at the Royal School, Raphoe; entered as a student at the Middle Temple in 1840, and was

called to the bar in 1843; practised on the common law side of Westminster Hall, and on the Oxford circuit; appointed chief justice of the supreme court, Madras, 1861, and of the high court of judicature on its establishment in 1862; resigned, 1871; vice-chancellor of the Madras University, 1861 to 1871; contributed to the Law Journal reports of cases in the court of Queen's bench, 1848 to 1857, and is joint author of a work 'On the Crown Office Practice of the Court of Queen's Bench.'

SCOTT, ARTHUR GUILLEM, Deputy Accountant-General, India Office.—Educated at the Royal Agricultural College, Cirencester, and at private schools; East India house writer, old establishment, 7th Nov., 1857; junior clerk, accountant-general's department, India office, Aug., 1860; senior clerk, Jan., 1872; assessor of income-tax, 1892; deputy accountant-general, June, 1893.

SCOTT, BUCHANAN, C.I.E., Major, R.E., India Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1871; from Oct., 1874, served as assistant engineer in various divisions of the Punjab; on field service in the Afghan war, 1878-79; executive engineer, May, 1883; superintendent of works and engineer-in-chief, Zoh valley survey, Aug., 1888; acted as mint master, Calcutta, 1891, as deputy consulting engineer for railways, 1892, and as mint master, Bombay, in 1891, and from April, 1893.

SCOTT, EDWIN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at St. Paul's School and University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 15th Jan., 1887, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; head assistant, Vizagapatam, July, 1893.

SCOTT, JAMES GEORGE, C.I.E., Political Department, Burma.—Appointed assistant commissioner, 9th April, 1886; on duty in the Shan states, Dec., 1886; assistant superintendent, Shan states, Dec., 1888; on duty with Anglo-Siamese boundary commission, 1889-90; officiating superintendent, northern Shan states, May, 1891; confirmed, July, 1892; chargé d'affaires at Bangkok, Nov., 1893; author of 'The Burman, His Life and Notions,' 'France and Tong-king,' 'Burma as it was, as it is, and as it will be,' 'Burma, the Eastern Country, and the Race of the Bramahs,' and 'The Hill Slopes of Tong-king.'

SCOTT, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G., M.A., Barrister-at-Law, late Judicial Department, Bombay.—Judge of the high court, Bombay, 18th Nov., 1882; services lent to the Egyptian government from April, 1890; resigned, April, 1891; K.C.M.G., Feb., 1894.

SCOTT, MATHEW HENRY, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived, 16th Dec., 1863, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate and assistant judge and sessions judge; senior assistant judge and sessions judge, Kaladgi, July, 1876; 2nd grade judge, Khandesh, Feb., 1881; 1st grade judge and sessions judge, Ahmednagar, May, 1882; acting district judge, Khandesh, April, 1888; and Satara, Jan., 1889; confirmed, April, 1892; retired, May, 1893.

SCOTT, ROSS, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Omagh Academy, the Royal Academical Institution and Seminary, Belfast, and Queen's College,

Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector and officiating additional and district and sessions judge; joint magistrate, Nov., 1886; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1890.

SCOTT, THOMAS AUGUSTUS, Colonel, I.S.C., late Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, June, 1857; arrived in India, 29th June, 1857; from Jan., 1862, served in the police department, Central Provinces, and was appointed district superintendent, March, 1864; from June, 1865, served as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1882; officiated as commissioner, Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1891; officiating additional sessions judge, June to Oct., 1892; reverted to military department, Aug., 1894.

SCOTT-MONCRIEFF, SIR COLIN CAMPBELL, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., Colonel, R.E., retired.—Joined the service as 2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, 13th June, 1856; arrived in India, 10th June, 1858; served with the Bengal sappers and miners until Feb., 1859, through the campaign in Oudh, and was present at several actions (medal); joined public works department, Feb., 1859, and served in the N.W. Provinces in the irrigation branch; principal, Thomason Civil Engineering College, Roorkee, Sept., 1861; assistant to chief engineer, irrigation branch, April, 1869; from Nov., 1869, served on the Ganges canal as executive and afterwards superintending engineer; major, April, 1874; in 1877 was sent on deputation to Mysore to serve as chief engineer and famine commissioner; C.S.I., May, 1878; member of an irrigation committee under the famine commission, Aug., 1878, to Feb., 1879, when he again joined the irrigation department of the N.W. Provinces; officiated as chief engineer and secretary to the chief commissioner of British Burma, May, 1881; promoted to superintending engineer, 1st class, Feb., 1882; retired, Jan., 1883; K.C.M.G., 1887; under secretary, public works ministry, Egypt, to 1892, when he was appointed under secretary for Scotland.

SCULLY, JOHN, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Medical Department.—Joined the department, 1872; served under the military department, and also as medical officer, Kashgar political agency, 1874-76; residency surgeon, Nepal, 1876-78; medical officer, Gilgit, 1878-80; acting deputy assay master, Calcutta mint, Oct., 1882, to Jan., 1883; from Nov., 1883, served as officiating assay master, and acting deputy assay master of the Bombay mint; confirmed in the latter appointment, May, 1884; assay master of the Calcutta mint, May, 1886.

SEABROOKE, JAMES HERBERT, B.A., Military Department, India Office.—Educated at King's College, London (Jelf medallist and associate, 1871); open classical scholarship, Queen's College, Oxford, 1871-74; B.A., Dec., 1874; junior clerk, financial department, India office, 13th Jan., 1876; military department, 1877; senior clerk, June, 1887.

SEALY, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Bombay Political Department.—First commission, 17th July, 1866; cantonment magistrate, Aden, May, 1875; 2nd assistant to resident, Oct., 1885; assistant political agent, Southern Mahratta country, May, 1889; acting

political superintendent, Savantvadi, July, 1889; 2nd assistant to political agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1889; acting political agent, Mahi Kantha, July-Dec., 1892; 1st assistant to resident, Aden, and political agent for the Somali coast, Aug., 1893; acting political resident, Aden, May-July, 1894; joint compiler of an account of the Arab tribes in the vicinity of Aden, and has translated Van Den Berg's work on Hadthramuth, and part of Carsten Niebuhr's description of Arabia.

SEATON, WILLIAM JOHN, Colonel, I.S.C., late Forest Department, Burma.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Sept., 1855; assistant superintendent in the forest department, Dec., 1860; deputy conservator, 1st class, April, 1865; conservator, Nov., 1867; 1st grade conservator, Sept., 1882; reverted to military duty, 1892.

SECCOMBE, HENRY LAWRENCE, Financial Department, India Office.—East India house writer, old establishment, 14th May, 1856; junior clerk, financial department, India office, Dec., 1866; senior clerk, Dec., 1882.

SECCOMBE, SIR THOMAS LAWRENCE, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., C.B.—Entered the home service of the Honorable East India Company in Jan., 1829, as a clerk in the department of auditor of India accounts (subsequently merged in the secretary's department); on the transfer of the government of India to the Crown in 1858 was appointed assistant financial secretary; financial secretary, 1859; director of military funds, and chairman of the consulting committee, 1866; in Jan., 1869, was elected chairman of a joint India office and war office committee for adjusting the charges against Indian revenues for services of H.M.'s regiments on the India establishment, and was nominated a member of a similar committee in 1874, and again in Feb., 1879; C.B., Dec., 1869; accountant-general, in addition to his other duties, Feb., 1872; assistant under secretary of state for India, July, 1872; K.C.S.I., June, 1877; appointed one of three commissioners to represent H.M.'s Government at the International Monetary Conference at Paris, 1878; continued assistant under secretary of state until 1881, but resigned his other offices on 31st Jan., 1879; member of a joint India office and admiralty committee on financial questions connected with the India troop service, 1879 to 1892, when he finally retired from the service; G.C.I.E., Aug., 1892.

SEDDON, CHARLES NORMAN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Liverpool College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1891, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

SEDGEFIELD, ARTHUR ROBERT WYATT, M.B., Surgeon-Major, Bengal Opium Department.—In military employ from 14th April, 1881; appointed superintendent, Chenawan jail, Sept., 1887; civil surgeon, Murree, March, 1891; superintendent, Benares opium agency, Oct., 1891.

SELBY, FRANCIS GUY, M.A., Educational Department, Bombay.—Educated at Wadham College, Oxford; arrived in India, 7th Feb., 1877, and served from that date as professor of logic and moral philosophy in the Deccan college, Poona; also held acting appointments for various periods as principal of the college, and as principal of the Rajkumar college,

Kathiawar; appointed principal, Deccan college, Dec., 1890.

SELBY, HENRY OLIPHANT, Major, R.E., India Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1860; arrived, 26th Dec., 1871; on special duty in 1874 during the Bengal famine; served in Afghanistan (medal), 1879 to 1881, the latter part of the time as superintendent, signalling lines of communications; from May, 1881, acted as deputy consulting engineer for railways, Bombay; confirmed, Dec., 1883; has acted at various periods as under secretary to government of Bombay in the public works department (railway branch), and was appointed to that post, March, 1892; deputy consulting engineer, Nov., 1892; also under secretary (railway), Jan., 1894; officiating secretary and consulting engineer, April, 1894.

SELLS, ARTHUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Muzaffarnagar, Aug., 1878, and civil and sessions judge, 3rd grade, Cawnpore, April, 1882; district and sessions judge, Meerut, Oct., 1886; retired, May, 1890.

SEN, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Dacca College, Presidency College, and the University, Calcutta, and University College, London; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

SETON-KARR, WALTER SCOTT, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Rugby; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, where he gained honours, in 1842; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1842; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate of Jessore, 1847; under secretary, government of Bengal, 1847-53; member of council of education, 1853; collector of Tirhoot, 1857; civil and sessions judge, Jessore, 1858-60; president of indigo commission, 1860; secretary to government of Bengal, 1860-61; legislative council of India, 1861; commissioner of revenue and circuit, Chittagong, and member of council of lieutenant-governor of Bengal, 1862; puisne judge, high court of judicature, Calcutta, July, 1862, to April, 1868; foreign secretary, government of India, April, 1868; retired, 1870; vice-chancellor of the university of Calcutta in 1868 and 1869; is a member of the council of Marlborough College.

SEWELL, EBENEZER JAMES, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1872, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting principal assistant collector and additional sessions judge, and acting collector and magistrate; on famine duty, June, 1877, to Sept., 1878; senior assistant collector and magistrate, Ganjam, June, 1882; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1889; collector

and magistrate, Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, North Arcot, Jan., 1891.

SEWELL, HENRY, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Radley, and University College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; served in Madras as assistant magistrate and collector, acting small cause court judge, acting under secretary to government in the revenue department, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; acting district and sessions judge, North Arcot, June, 1874, to April, 1875; from May, 1875, served as collector and magistrate, Trichinopoly, and political agent, Pudukotta; district and sessions judge, North Arcot, Nov., 1886; collector and magistrate, South Arcot, Nov., 1887; retired, Jan., 1891.

SEWELL, ROBERT, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at St. Peter's College, Radley; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-secretary to the board of revenue, acting collector and magistrate, and acting district and sessions judge; on famine duty, Oct., 1877, and on special duty in connection with the archaeological survey of the Madras presidency, Jan., 1881, and again, Jan., 1883; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Tinnevelly, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras University, March, 1887; acted as collector and magistrate, and in Sept., 1888, was appointed district and sessions judge, Kistna; collector and magistrate, Bellary, Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, March, 1890; collector and magistrate, Jan., 1891; author of the following works, published by order of government:—'A Report on the Amaravati Tope, and Excavations on its Site,' 1880; 'Chronological Tables for Southern India from the 6th Century, A.D.,' 1881; 'A Sketch of the Dynasties of Southern India,' 1883; volumes I. and II. of the 'Archaeological Survey Series of Southern India,' 1882 and 1884; 'Early Buddhist Symbolism,' 1886; &c.

SHADBOLT, ERNEST IFILL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, May, 1883; services lent to Bhavnagar and Gondal states, 1887-91; engineer-in-chief, Bezvada-Madras railway survey, Feb., 1894.

SHAKESPEAR, ALEXANDER MURISON WAKE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Harrow; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Aug., 1887; private secretary to lieutenant-governor, March, 1888; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Jan., 1890; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Nov., 1890; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1894.

SHAMS-UD-DIN ALI KHAN, SAIYAD, Khan Bahadur, Berar Administration.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1869; assistant commissioner, Jan., 1882; special assistant commissioner, Jan., 1893.

SHANKER MADHO CHITNAVIS, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Central Provinces).—Appointed a probationer for the civil service under the statutory rules, July, 1885; admitted member of

the statutory service, and appointed assistant commissioner, 5th Oct., 1837.

SHARP, F. W., Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Milton College, Ullesthorpe; clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, March, 1835.

SHARP, HENRY GRANVILLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Horncastle Grammar School, Marlborough College, and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 27th Jan., 1868; served in the revenue and judicial departments in Bengal; on special duty at Madras on famine relief work, Feb., 1877; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1878; magistrate and collector, Oct., 1884; retired, Jan., 1894.

SHARPE, CHARLES FERGUSON, Major-General, I.S.C., late Police Department, North-Western Provinces.—Arrived in India as ensign, 1st Feb., 1851, and served under the military department till Sept., 1858, when he was appointed to the Oudh military police; medal for mutiny services; from Feb., 1859, served as district superintendent in the police department in Oudh, and was promoted to 1st grade, April, 1876; reverted to military duty, March, 1891.

SHARPE, FREDERIC ROBERT, Registry Department, India Office.—Educated at Christ's Hospital; writer, India office, 7th Aug., 1877; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878.

SHARPE, HENRY GRATTAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Dublin High School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 12th Nov., 1890, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

SHARPIN, VEN. FREDERICK LLOYD, M.A., late Ecclesiastical Department, Bombay.—Educated at Exeter College, Oxford; joined the service, Aug., 1865; arrived in India, 13th Oct., 1865; served as chaplain at various stations in Bombay and Sind; senior chaplain, Oct., 1875; senior presidency chaplain, June, 1880; archdeacon and commissary, June, 1886, also chaplain of Satara; retired, Oct., 1888.

SHAW, ARTHUR TREVOR AMBROSE, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Cheltenham College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1883, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Burma as assistant commissioner, Feb., 1886; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1890.

SHAW, GEORGE WATSON, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Hertford Grammar School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 20th Dec., 1879, and served in British Burma as assistant commissioner, and officiating deputy commissioner; deputy commissioner, May, 1888.

SHAW-MACKENZIE, CHARLES FORBES HUDSON, Barrister-at-Law, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to Bombay civil service from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 21st Aug., 1850, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate up to Feb., 1864, and subsequently as judge and sessions judge in various districts; retired, Sept., 1885.

SHAW-STEWART, JOHN HERON MAXWELL, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department,

Madras.—First commission as lieutenant, Madras Engineers, June, 1851; arrived in India, 31st Dec., 1853; served in the Madras public works department from Jan., 1854, as assistant engineer and executive engineer; served in the China campaign, 1860 (medal and two clasps); deputy secretary to government, public works department, Dec., 1863; under secretary to government, Oct., 1866; controller, public works accounts, Madras, May, 1867; consulting engineer for railways, Madras, Oct., 1867; chief engineer, Oct., 1870; joint secretary to government, railway branch, Feb., 1877; chief engineer, 1st class, and secretary to government, public works department, Sept., 1883; additional member of council, Madras, for making laws and regulations, Sept., 1883; retired, Sept., 1886.

SHEPHARD, HORATIO HALE, M.A., B.L., Madras Judicial Department.—Professor of law at Presidency College, Madras, 1st Oct., 1873; acted as government pleader from time to time from Dec., 1874; fellow of Madras University, March, 1881; appointed government pleader, June, 1881; additional member of Madras legislative council, March, 1885; advocate-general of Madras and officiating judge of high court, Jan., 1888; confirmed as judge of high court, July, 1889.

SHEPHERD, CHARLES EDWARD, Colonel, I.S.C., India Public Works Department.—Appointed, 26th Sept., 1864, and posted to Central Provinces; executive engineer, Sept., 1868; transferred to military works, Dec., 1872; and to state railways, Nov., 1874; officiating deputy consulting engineer for railways, Lucknow, March, 1889; confirmed, May, 1891; acted as consulting engineer, Lucknow, in 1890 and 1892.

SHEPHERD, GEORGE EDWARD, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th September, 1893.

SHEPPARD, GEORGE FREDERICK, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1854, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; acting collector of Bombay and superintendent of stamps and stationery, Jan., 1870; on special duty in Bombay, March, 1870; collector of salt revenue, Bombay, Dec., 1871; collector and magistrate, Kaira, Feb., 1873; senior collector and magistrate and political agent, Kaira, Nov., 1875; on special duty at Poona, Oct., 1878; from Nov., 1880, acted as commissioner, and was confirmed as 2nd grade commissioner, Oct., 1882; 1st grade commissioner, March, 1886; retired, May, 1889.

SHEPPARD, WILLIAM DIDSBURY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Hereford Cathedral School and New College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1886; assistant collector and magistrate, Oct., 1887; 2nd assistant and forest settlement officer, Nov., 1893.

SHERER, JOHN WALTER, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1846; served in the N.W. Provinces in the grades of assistant, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; assistant secretary to government of N.W. Provinces, 1852; on special duty under board of revenue to report on re-eflorescence, Western Jumna Canal, 1856-57; magistrate and collector of Cawnpore, 1857; civil officer with General Havelock's column in 1857, in the advance on

Cawnpore; special commissioner to try rebels; commissioner to enquire into claims against the former king of Oudh, 1864; member of jail committee, Calcutta, 1864; judge of Allahabad, 1871, and afterwards of Azimgurh and Mirzapore; retired, 1877.

SHERIDAN, CHARLES JAMES, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 1st April, 1872, and posted to N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Feb., 1876; employed on state railways, 1876-78.

SHERIDAN, PHILIP, Post Office Department, Government of India.—Served in the postal department, Punjab, from March, 1861, as postmaster, assistant superintendent of military horse van dāk, and inspector; chief superintendent, travelling post office, Allahabad, Dec., 1873; chief inspector of post offices, Assam, Dec., 1873; assistant director-general of the post office of India, July, 1880; inspector of railway and mail service, Nov., 1885; deputy director-general of the post-office of India, April, 1888; officiated as postmaster-general, Calcutta, in 1889 and 1890; postmaster-general, Lahore, July, 1891.

SHERMAN, WILLIAM HANBURY PETTINGALL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 31st July, 1866, and employed in the Central Provinces; transferred to state railways, April, 1874; executive engineer, Jan., 1877; services lent to South Indian railway company, Feb., 1889, and to Gwalior durbar for Bhopal-Ujjain line, Feb., 1891; superintending engineer, Nov., 1892.

SERRING, CHARLES ATMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Westminster and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

SHEWAN, ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at the Gymnasium, Aberdeen, and University of Aberdeen; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant political agent in charge of Jath state, and acting forest settlement officer; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; on special forest duty in connection with the dahlī cultivation settlement in Ahmednagar, March, 1881, and Feb., 1882; from Jan., 1885, served as assistant judge and sessions judge; acting under secretary to government, Bombay, political, judicial, and educational departments, and secretary to the legislative council, in 1886 and 1892; administrator of the Rajpila state, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, July, 1894.

SHIPLEY, REGINALD HEBER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Winchester and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 20th Dec., 1883, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; special assistant, July, 1890.

SHIRRES, LESLIE PITTENDRIGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Aberdeen and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1882; served in the revenue and judicial department of Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector;

officiated as under secretary to government of Bengal, Feb., 1887, and was confirmed, Jan., 1888; officiated as under secretary to the government of India, department of finance and commerce, in Nov., 1887, and again from April, 1888; officiated as junior secretary to board of revenue, in 1889 and 1893; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Aug., 1890.

SHORTREDE, ROBERT, Audit Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, 1st Feb., 1864; junior assistant, Sept., 1866; senior examiner, July, 1873; designation changed to senior clerk, Oct., 1892.

SHRIRAM BHIKAJI JATAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., C.I.E., Hyderabad Administration.—Served in the Bombay education department from Jan., 1859, in the Marathi translator's office, and as assistant master in the high schools at Bombay and Belgaum, and head master of the Carwar English school; from July, 1867, served in Berar as deputy educational inspector, and acting head master, high school, Amraoti; educational inspector, Aug., 1876; director of public instruction, Hyderabad assigned districts, Aug., 1884; C.I.E., May, 1894.

SHURLOCK, HENRY JAMES, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 5th Aug., 1886.

SHUTTLEWORTH, ALLEN THORNTON, Forest Department, Bombay.—Appointed to the Indian marine, Nov., 1855; arrived in India, 21st April, 1856; served in the Persian war, including the bombardment of Bushire and Mohumerah (medal), and in the operations against the Wagheers in Okhamandal; from Nov., 1863, served in the Bombay forest department as assistant conservator, and deputy conservator, and was appointed conservator of forests and superintendent of botanical gardens, Sept., 1869; 1st grade conservator, Nov., 1881; while stationed at Alibag as deputy conservator of forests was instrumental in saving upwards of a hundred lives from different wrecks; these services were acknowledged in different ways by the government of Bombay, the Bombay harbour board, and the merchants of Bombay; Albert medal of the 1st class, medals of the Royal Humane Society, the Shipwrecked Mariners' Company, and Lloyds, &c.

SIBOLD, ERNEST ALFRED, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 23rd Dec., 1864, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; executive engineer, Sept., 1872; in 1874 served in Bengal on famine relief works.

SIBTHORPE, CHARLES, Surgeon - Major - General, L.R.C.S., F.K. & Q.C.P. (Dublin), Madras Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, 1st April, 1870; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1870; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Banda, July, 1874; from May, 1875, to Nov., 1880, served as resident surgeon, general hospital, Madras, and professor of pathology and materia medica in the medical college, and acted for various periods as surgeon, general hospital, and professor of surgery, and as ophthalmic surgeon, and professor of ophthalmic surgery and physiology; also served in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); and in the Burma expedition, 1885-86; from Oct., 1882, served as fort surgeon, inspector of seamen at Madras, and professor of anatomy at the medical college, also acting as secretary and statistical officer to the surgeon-general,

Her Majesty's forces, April-Aug., 1833, and as principal medical store-keeper, Madras, Sept., 1833; fellow of Madras University, March, 1834; surgeon, general hospital, and professor of surgery, &c., medical college, Jan., 1838; reverted to military duty, May, 1890; surgeon-general, Madras, May, 1894; author of a 'Clinical Manual.'

SILCOCK, HENRY FOSTER, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Drogheda Grammar School and Kingstown School, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1874, and served as assistant magistrate and collector, acting district superintendent of police, acting cantonment magistrate, and forest settlement officer; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; 2nd assistant collector, April, 1887; on special duty, Nov., 1889; 1st assistant collector, April, 1890; junior collector, collector and magistrate, and political agent, Aug., 1893.

SILCOCK, JAMES GREER, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Armagh Royal School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived, 16th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating judicial assistant; on special duty at Peshawur, Feb., 1881; officiated as district judge, Oct., 1885, to Jan., 1886, and as divisional judge, April, 1886, and again April, 1888; district judge, 2nd grade, June-Nov., 1888, and March, 1889; deputy commissioner, April, 1889.

SIM, HENRY ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1876; arrived in India, 19th Nov., 1878, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate and acting collector and magistrate; from Jan., 1883, served as deputy conservator of forests, Kurnool, and forest settlement officer; special assistant to collector and magistrate, Malabar, Feb., 1886; acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, March, 1886; employed under government of India as officiating deputy accountant-general, Aug.-Nov., 1887; deputy accountant-general, Madras, Jan., 1888; acting secretary to the commissioners of land revenue, 1888-90, and again, 1890-92; sub-collector and joint magistrate, July, 1890.

SIME, JOHN, M.A., LL.D., Education Department, Punjab.—Professor, government college, Agra, April, 1869; from Sept., 1870, served in the Punjab as principal, Delhi college, till 1877, and subsequently as professor, and officiating principal, Lahore college; inspector of schools, Aug., 1887; on special duty at Naini Tal, educational commission, April-May, 1885; tutor to H.H. the Maharaja of Patiala, May, 1885; director of public instruction, Punjab, and under secretary to government in the educational department, Oct., 1890; author of 'Man and his Duty,' and of works on arithmetic and mensuration.

SIMEON, LIONEL BARRINGTON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Oct., 1886; personal assistant to chief engineer and

under secretary to government, March, 1892; in charge, Lucknow division, Oct., 1893; on special duty in secretariat, Jan., 1894.

SIMPSON, SIR BENJAMIN, Surgeon-General, K.C.I.E., M.D., late Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the Indian medical department, 1853; served in civil employ from 1860, as assistant surgeon in Bengal, and was in medical charge of the Bhootan mission in 1863; on special duty on north-eastern frontier, 1867-68; civil surgeon, Darjeeling, April, 1868; from Jan., 1871, served as civil surgeon of Patna with charge of the Meeteapore jail; on special duty to the cinchona commission, Feb., 1871; surgeon-major, Oct., 1873; guardian to the Maharajah of Cooch Behar during his visit to Europe, March, 1878; deputy surgeon-general, southern Afghanistan field force, 1881; principal medical officer, Quetta, 1881; deputy surgeon-general, March, 1882; sanitary commissioner, Central Provinces, 1882; surgeon-general in the Punjab, 1883-84; surgeon-general, Bengal, 1884-85; sanitary commissioner, and surgeon-general with the government of India, March, 1885; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retired, March, 1890.

SIMPSON, JAMES THOMAS, B.C.E., M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed, 4th May, 1869, and posted to Bengal; executive engineer, March, 1880; transferred to Burma, June, 1887.

SIMPSON, JOHN HOPE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at foreign schools and at St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

SIMSON, D., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at the Grammar School and Marischal College, Aberdeen; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, in 1845; arrived in India, 5th April, 1846; served in the N.W. Provinces and in the Punjab in the grades of assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and deputy commissioner; commissioner in Oudh, June, 1859; extra judge, court of Sudder Dewanny and Nizamut Adawlut, N.W. Provinces, April, 1864; judge, chief court, Punjab, April, 1868; retired, 1872.

SINCLAIR, DAVID, Brig.-Surg.-Lieut.-Col., M.B., C.M., Jail Department, Burma.—Joined the Indian medical department, 1st Oct., 1869; arrived in Madras, 14th May, 1870; from 1871, served in British Burma, and was employed in the analysis of potable waters in cantonments, April, 1871, to Dec., 1872; served subsequently as superintendent of the central jail at Moulmein, with occasional charge of the central jail at Rangoon; also officiated as civil surgeon and superintendent of lock hospital at Moulmein in 1874, and was in medical charge of the general hospital in that year; officiating inspector-general of prisons, British Burma, May-Oct., 1875, and again July, 1882; appointed superintendent of the central jail at Rangoon, Feb., 1883; inspector-general of jails, sanitary commissioner, superintendent of vaccination, and chief of civil medical department, Burma, Jan., 1884.

SINCLAIR, DUNCAN EVANS, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Westminster School, King's College School, and Merton College, Oxford; writer in post office, British

museum, and admiralty, 1873-80; supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depot, 20th Aug., 1880.

SINCLAIR, WILLIAM FREDERICK, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Armagh Royal School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1868, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting railway magistrate, and was on special duty for demarcation of forests in Khandesh, April, 1873, and in Alibag, Jan., 1874; on special duty, Poona, Nov., 1875; forest demarcating officer, Thana, Jan., 1876; on famine duty, April-Aug., 1877; first assistant collector and forest settlement officer, Feb., 1883; junior collector and magistrate, and political agent, Cambay, March, 1890; senior collector and magistrate, and political agent, Thana, March, 1892; retired, June, 1892; author of 'Notes on Arboriculture in the Bombay Presidency.'

SKINNER, JOHN ALLAN CLEVELAND, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Wellington College, and Keble College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 28th Oct., 1885; supernumerary assistant commissioner, Khandwa, Nov., 1885; registrar, judicial commissioner's court, April, 1888; assistant commissioner, Jan., 1890; acted as under secretary to chief commissioner in 1889, and as cantonment magistrate in 1892.

SKINNER, RUSSELL MORLAND, Col., I.S.C., late Police Department, Bengal.—First commission, 7th Dec., 1855; served through the Indian Mutiny; joined the Bengal police department as assistant superintendent, 1st grade, April, 1862; district superintendent, Aug., 1863; on special duty, June, 1873; 1st grade district superintendent, Aug., 1876; retired, April, 1888.

SKIPWITH, GRAY TOWNSEND, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 11th June, 1858; joined public works department, Oct., 1860; 1st assistant engineer, Nov., 1863; executive engineer, April, 1865; assistant secretary to chief commissioner, Central Provinces, and assistant to chief engineer, Feb., 1870; assistant secretary to government of India, public works department, Feb., 1872; 3rd superintending engineer, Feb., 1880; 2nd superintending engineer, March, 1887; officiated as chief engineer and joint secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, public works department, irrigation, Feb. to May, 1889; and again in Nov., 1889; 1st superintending engineer, Dec., 1889; charge of 1st circle irrigation works, April, 1890; retired, Jan., 1893.

SKRINE, FRANCIS HENRY BENNET, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Blackheath Proprietary School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1870; assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal, Dec., 1870; on famine relief duty, May, 1874, and Oct., 1877, to April, 1878 (in Madras); superintendent of Salem central jail, Jan., 1878; joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1884; magistrate and collector, Aug., 1887; officiating commissioner, Bhagalpur, Dec., 1893, to Jan., 1894.

SLACK, FRANCIS ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Taunton College School University College, Oxford, and St.

John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1874; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1876; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; settlement officer of wards' and attached estates in Chota Nagpur; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Jan., 1883; officiated as commissioner, Chittagong, Sept., 1890, and as district and sessions judge, July, 1891; magistrate and collector, May, 1892; acted as secretary to board of revenue, Aug.-Dec., 1893.

SLADEN, JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 19th Nov., 1888, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; assistant commissioner in Sind, Oct., 1891.

SLATER, ALFRED WILLIAM, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 6th Nov., 1872, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh; on famine relief duty in Madras, 1877-78; transferred to buildings and roads branch, Feb., 1881; executive engineer, Nov., 1881.

SLATER, JOHN SAMUEL, Educational Service, Bengal.—From Sept., 1870, to Nov., 1875, served in the public works department, Punjab, as engineer apprentice and assistant engineer, on the Bari Doab and Indus canals; from Nov., 1875, served in the Bengal education department as professor, civil engineering department, Presidency college, and, from April, 1880, as professor of the Howrah engineering college; officiated as principal in 1881; principal, Sibpur engineering college, Dec., 1891.

SLIGHT, ANDREW MURE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Royal College, Mauritius, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived, 12th Nov., 1889, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

SLOCOCK, FRANCIS SAMUEL ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Marlborough and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

SLOCOMBE, CHARLES, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd July, 1892.

SLY, FRANK GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1887, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner and settlement officer.

SMART, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Madras Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 15th July, 1868; from March, 1871, served in Madras as assistant engineer and executive engineer; assistant chief engineer for irrigation, and under secretary to government, irrigation branch, April, 1885, to April, 1886; acting principal, college of engineering, Feb., 1891, to Jan., 1892, and from April, 1894.

SMEATON, DONALD MACKENZIE, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at the Abbey Park Institution, St. Andrew's, and St. Andrew's University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 22nd Nov., 1867, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant

magistrate and collector; from May, 1870, served in the settlement department, and was appointed settlement officer, 3rd grade, Feb., 1877; transferred to British Burma as settlement secretary to the chief commissioner, April, 1879; on special duty in India, April, 1882; secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, in the land revenue and agricultural department, and director of agriculture, May, 1882; officiating director of agriculture and commerce, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Aug., 1884, and was confirmed in this appointment, May, 1886; officiating chief secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, April, 1887; commissioner, central division, Upper Burma, May, 1888; financial commissioner, March, 1891; in May, 1892, officiated for a short time as chief commissioner of Burma; C.S.I., Jan., 1895; author of an annotated edition of the N.W. Provinces Revenue Act of 1873, 'The Currency of India,' and 'The Karens of Burma.'

SMEATON, ROBERT, M.A., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at the Abbey Park Institution, St. Andrew's, and St. Andrew's University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1868, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating magistrate and collector, and assistant settlement officer; first assistant secretary to government, July, 1876; under secretary, May, 1877; junior secretary, Aug., 1877; officiated for various periods as secretary to government in the Oudh revenue department, and also in the judicial department; secretary to government, financial department, May, 1884; member of lieut.-governor's council, Dec., 1891; retired, April, 1894.

SMITH, ALBERT CHARLES, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., late Madras Public Works Department.—Arrived in India, 5th Jan., 1864; joined the public works department, Sept., 1864, and was appointed assistant engineer, Oct., 1865, and executive engineer, March, 1870; proceeded to England on home duty, Feb., 1871, and returned to Madras, Nov., 1873; held charge of 4th and 6th circles, and was appointed superintending engineer, Sept., 1889; officiating joint secretary in the public works department, with charge of general buildings and road branch, April-July, 1891; retired, Jan., 1894.

SMITH, ALEXANDER, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Marischal College, Aberdeen; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1856; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1856; from Dec., 1857, served as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and magistrate and collector, and was appointed senior superintendent of survey, Aug., 1872, magistrate and collector, 1st grade, March, 1875, and commissioner of the Orissa division, and superintendent of tributary mehals, Cuttack, Oct., 1879; commissioner, Presidency division, July, 1884; retired, Dec., 1891.

SMITH, ARNOLD CREMER, Store Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, India office, 23rd June, 1865; junior clerk, store department, Nov., 1866; senior clerk, Nov., 1876.

SMITH, CHARLES JOHN, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—1st commission, Madras engineers, Dec., 1855; arrived in India, 26th Dec., 1857; assistant engineer

in the Madras public works department, Jan., 1858; executive engineer, Nov., 1865; appointed to carry out certain investigations in connection with the flood waters of the Cauvery, Dec., 1869; executive engineer, 1st grade, and deputy consulting engineer for railways, Oct., 1870, to 1883; under secretary to government, public works department, May, 1872; superintending engineer, 1st class, Sept., 1883; consulting engineer and joint secretary to government, railway branch, 1883; chief engineer, 3rd class, Dec., 1885; 2nd class, May, 1888; 1st class, Dec., 1889; re-appointed consulting engineer, July, 1889; reverted to military duty, July, 1891; retired from the army, May, 1892.

SMITH, FRANCIS WHITMORE, Military Department, India Office.—Educated at Bedford School and Cheltenham College; temporary clerk, India office, 23rd Jan., 1864; junior clerk, military department, July, 1866; senior clerk, Jan., 1880.

SMITH, GILBERT LESLIE, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Twyford School and St. Andrew's University; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 9th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, settlement officer, and officiating judicial assistant; on famine duty in Bengal, Feb., 1874; employed under the public works department in taking up land for the Punjab Northern state railway, Aug., 1877, to Feb., 1878; officiating divisional judge, April, 1886, May, 1887, and June, 1888; district judge, 2nd grade, Jan., 1887; divisional judge, Nov., 1889.

SMITH, HENRY ANDREW FELTON, Police Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the service, 7th Aug., 1861; inspector of police, April, 1866; assistant district superintendent, July, 1867; district superintendent, March, 1876; 1st grade, Jan., 1892.

SMITH, HENRY BABINGTON, M.A., Private Secretary to H.E. the Viceroy.—Educated at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; examiner in the education department (England), Dec., 1887; private secretary to the chancellor of the exchequer (Mr. Goschen), May, 1891; clerk in the treasury, Aug., 1892; secretary to the British delegates at the Brussels monetary conference, Nov., 1892; private secretary to the viceroy, Feb., 1894.

SMITH, HENRY SCOTT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Trinity College, Stratford-on-Avon, and Keble College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and acting district judge.

SMITH, JOHN MACDONALD, Colonel, I.S.C., late Jail Department, Madras.—First commission, Madras infantry, 20th April, 1855; served in the China expedition with the 12th regiment, native infantry; staff corps, Nov., 1861; assistant to the agent in Vizagapatam, Dec., 1861, to 1868; junior assistant collector, Ganjam, 1870 to 1878; wrote a hand-book in the Khond language; superintendent of central and district jails, Rajahmundry, April, 1880, to April, 1882, and was on special duty in 1881 as secretary to the special commissioner on his tour to the northern districts; from June, 1882, to Dec., 1885, served as paymaster of pensioners,

Bangalore, and also acted as district magistrate and president of the municipality of that place, 1883-84, and in 1885; chief presidency magistrate, Madras, Dec., 1885; acting inspector-general of jails, Feb., 1890; confirmed, March, 1891; reverted to military department, Aug., 1892; author of a hand-book in the Khond language.

SMITH, JOHN MANNERS, C.I.E., V.C., Captain, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, 10th March, 1883, and served under the military department until Nov., 1887, when he was appointed attaché in the foreign department; on special duty to Sikkim, in connection with the negotiations with the Chinese Amban, Nov., 1888; assistant to the officer on special duty at Gilgit, June, 1889; assistant to agent, Rajputana, Sept., 1892; on deputation to Kotah State, Oct., 1892; on duty under the orders of the British agent at Gilgit, April, 1893; on deputation with Kabul mission, Aug.—Dec., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894.

SMITH, JOSEPH, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 25th Sept., 1861; from Sept., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Etah, Aug., 1881, and afterwards at Etawah and at Banda; officiating district and sessions judge, July, 1886; retired, April, 1887.

SMITH, SIR ROBERT MURDOCH, K.C.M.G., Major-Gen., R.E., late Indo-European Telegraph Department.—Appointed to the Indo-European telegraph department, Aug., 1863; director of the Persian section from June, 1865; director in chief, Indo-European telegraph department, March, 1887; retired, Feb., 1888.

SMITH, SAMUEL GEORGE, Accounts Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, 6th Jan., 1833.

SMITH, SIDNEY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Department.—First commission, 13th Jan., 1869; served in Egyptian expedition, 1882 (medal, clasp and star); joined the department, 27th July, 1883, as executive engineer and officiating deputy consulting engineer, Madras; acting under secretary to government, May, 1888; appointed deputy consulting engineer and under secretary to government, Feb., 1890; deputy consulting engineer for railways, Assam, Feb., 1893.

SMITH, THOMAS, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, and deputy commissioner; appointed district and sessions judge, Tipperah, Dec., 1877, and served subsequently in the judicial department; 1st grade district and sessions judge, Nov., 1881; retired, Jan., 1888.

SMITH, THOMAS WILLIAM, Store Department, India Office.—Appointé 1st and 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 23rd May, 1888.

SMITH, TURTON, Police Department, Punjab.—From 26th March, 1863, served in the police department as assistant superintendent and district superintendent, and was promoted to 1st grade superintendent, May, 1881; between Dec.,

1871, and Sept., 1878, also served in the postal department as officiating assistant director-general of post offices, and as officiating postmaster-general at Bombay, Aug., 1876, and at Madras, May, 1878; deputy inspector-general of police, Aug., 1892; acted as inspector-general, Nov., 1893, to Feb., 1894.

SMITH, VINCENT ARTHUR, B.A., M.R.A.S., M.A.S.B., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; from Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1880, served in the settlement department in Hamirpur, and was appointed assistant settlement officer, 1st grade, Jan., 1878; on special duty in the Allahabad district, Nov., 1880; served subsequently as assistant magistrate, joint magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector, and was also settlement officer of Basti, 1883-84; joint magistrate, 1st grade, July, 1885; magistrate and collector, June, 1889; author of 'The Settlement Officer's Manual,' 'Coinage of the Early Gupta Dynasty,' and 'Græco-Roman Influence on the Civilization of Ancient India,' and editor of 'Sleeman's Rambles and Recollections.'

SMYTH, GEORGE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, officiating registrar of the chief court, and judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, March, 1880; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Delhi, Oct., 1883; divisional judge, 2nd grade, April, 1889; commissioner and superintendent, Nov., 1889; retired, March, 1894.

SMYTH, JOHN WATT, Barrister-at-Law, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1857; arrived in India, 29th Dec., 1857, and was appointed assistant magistrate and collector, Agra, Nov., 1858; transferred to Punjab, May, 1859, and served as assistant commissioner, and as personal assistant to the financial commissioner, and to the judicial commissioner; registrar of the chief court, Feb., 1866; deputy commissioner, Oct., 1869; officiated as commissioner and superintendent of the Lahore division in 1869, 1872, and 1879, and as additional commissioner of Amritsar, 1874-77; promoted to deputy commissioner, 1st class, April, 1877; judge, chief court, March, 1880; retired, 1885.

SMYTH, RALPH GRAHAM, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Burma.—Lieutenant, Bengal engineers, 7th Dec., 1855; from 6th Nov., 1857, served in the public works department in the Central and N.W. Provinces as assistant engineer; served in the field force at Lucknow, and at the final capture of Lucknow, 1858 (medal and clasp); officiating garrison engineer, Lucknow, June, 1859; on military duty, May, 1861; re-appointed to public works department, April, 1862, and served in Bengal as assistant engineer and executive engineer (Jan., 1864); on special duty in investigations connected with river Selye, and with reservoirs in the Purulia and Palamow districts, Nov., 1867, to May, 1869; served in the Ramghur and Burbee divisions

and on the grand trunk road, and was promoted to executive engineer, 1st grade, Sept., 1870; superintending engineer, July, 1876; transferred to Hyderabad, Dec., 1881, as secretary to resident in the public works department; 1st class superintending engineer, Sept., 1882; officiating chief engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, Aug. to Dec., 1885; retired, Dec., 1889.

SMYTH, THOMAS WATT, M.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1862, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and small cause court judge; registrar of the chief court, 1867-70; deputy commissioner, April, 1877; on special duty in the civil secretariat, Feb. and Oct., 1882; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Delhi, Aug., 1882; additional commissioner, Jullundur, Umballa, and Amritsar divisions, April, 1883; divisional judge, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; officiating judge, chief court, March, 1886; confirmed as judge of the chief court, Jan., 1887; retired, April, 1889; author of 'Acts and Regulations applicable to the Punjab.'

SMYTH, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Bath College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 1st Jan., 1894, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

SNOW, PHILIP CHICHELEY HYDE, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Haileybury and Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1876; assistant commissioner, Delhi, Nov., 1876; from March, 1877, served in British Burma as assistant commissioner, assistant cantonment magistrate, and small cause court judge, and personal assistant to the chief commissioner; from Nov., 1881, served in the Bombay presidency as assistant collector and magistrate and district registrar, and was permanently transferred to the Bombay civil service, June, 1885; forest settlement officer, Dec., 1885; acting under secretary to government, political, judicial, and educational departments, Sept., 1886; 2nd assistant collector, Nov., 1887; 1st assistant collector, Dec., 1890; junior collector and magistrate, June, 1894.

SORABJI PADAMJI PESTANJI, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Probationer under the native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1880; confirmed as member of the Bombay civil service, March, 1882; served as assistant collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer; 2nd assistant collector and magistrate, May, 1889; 1st assistant, March, 1893.

SPANKIE, GEORGE THEOPHILUS, B.L., Judicial Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 6th June, 1871; appointed additional judicial commissioner in Oudh (sub. pro tem.), 11th Nov., 1892.

SPANKIE, JOHN PATRICK WALTER, Captain, I.S.C., Judicial Department, N.W. Provinces.—First commission, 9th Aug., 1873; served during the Afghan war, 1877-78 (medal); officiating cantonment magistrate, Oct., 1881; confirmed, Sept., 1882; 1st class, Oct., 1892.

SPEARMAN, HORACE RALPH, Colonel, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—Ensign, 91st Highlanders, 18th Dec., 1857; probationary assistant engineer, public works department, 1862; from Dec., 1862, served in the Burma commission as assistant commissioner and assistant secretary to the chief commissioner (Feb., 1868); also officiated as secretary to the chief commissioner, and as director of public instruction; officiating assistant political agent, Bhamo, March, 1871; appointed a deputy commissioner, Jan., 1877, and was promoted to 1st grade, Aug., 1886; on special duty in Rangoon, Feb., 1879, and Feb., 1883; president, Rangoon municipal committee, May, 1883, and again, Jan., 1886, and of the Akyab municipal committee, Jan., 1884; member of the education syndicate, Feb., 1886; commissioner, April, 1890; author of 'British Burma Gazetteer.'

SPEDDING, ROBERT DECY, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1861; served from Aug., 1862, as assistant magistrate and collector and joint magistrate and deputy collector; deputed to conduct demarcation of boundary between districts of Banda and Hamirpur and the native states of Bundelkhand, Oct., 1870; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, Jhansi, April, 1879; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Gorakpur, Dec., 1879, and subsequently in Moradabad; officiated as commissioner in the Agra division in 1884, and in the Benares division in 1886; commissioner, Rae Bareli division, Oct., 1889; Agra division, April, 1891; retired, July, 1891.

SPENCE, JAMES KNOX, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Cheltenham College and Royal Military Academy, Woolwich; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1865, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; from May, 1878, to Oct., 1880, served in the postal department, as acting postmaster-general, Bombay, also in the Punjab and Bengal; junior collector and magistrate, and political agent, Kolaba, June, 1885; senior collector and collector of land revenue, customs, &c., Bombay, superintendent of stamps, &c., Nov., 1888; acting commissioner of customs, &c., Dec., 1889; also British delegate for the Portuguese treaty; acting commissioner, northern division, March, 1891; confirmed, Nov., 1891; central division, Nov., 1892.

SPENCER, CHARLES GORDON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Marlborough and Keble College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

SPENCER, HUGH, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Bristol Grammar School, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 17th Dec., 1888, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Nov., 1893.

SPENCER, LIONEL DIXON, Surgeon-Colonel, M.D., Indian Medical Department.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, March, 1865; served under the civil department in the Central Provinces, and with the Central

India horse; from Oct., 1871, served as agency surgeon, Eastern States, Rajputana; on special famine duty, Oct., 1877, and acted as political agent, Karauli, in 1877, and in Bhartpur, 1878; in 1879 was in medical charge of the Marwar and Jaisalmer agency; from March, 1881, served as agency surgeon, Rajputana, and superintendent-general of dispensaries and vaccination; reverted to military department, July, 1892.

SPILSBURY, EDGAR CHARLES, Major, R.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed to Bombay public works department as assistant engineer, 17th Aug., 1876; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); employed on Bombay defences, March, 1883; executive engineer, March, 1886; on special duty at Simla with defence committee in 1886 and 1887; transferred to Burma, Dec., 1893.

SPRING, FRANCIS JOSEPH EDWARD, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 4th Aug., 1870, and employed on irrigation works in the Punjab; transferred to state railways, May, 1873; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; officiating under secretary to government of Bengal, railway branch, April, 1884; confirmed, Sept., 1884; employed on North-Western line, March, 1888; engineer-in-chief, East Coast railway, Nov., 1890; officiating consulting engineer for railways, Bombay, and secretary to government, Aug., 1893; employed on Bezvada-Madras railway survey, Nov., 1893; consulting engineer to government of India for railways, Assam, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894.

SPRING-BRANSON, JAMES HENRY, B.L., Madras Judicial Department.—Crown prosecutor and public prosecutor for town of Madras, 4th July, 1885; acting advocate general, March, 1887; member of Madras legislative council, March, 1887, Feb., 1892, and Feb., 1894; advocate general, Madras, Sept., 1889.

SPRY, ARTHUR HUME, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, 1857; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1857, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate, special officer of income tax, acting collector and magistrate, assistant political agent, and acting talukdari settlement officer; junior collector and magistrate, Dharwar, Dec., 1878; senior collector, Kaira, Oct., 1882; on special duty in the Cambay state, Feb., 1883; retired, 1886.

SRI LAL, Pandit, B.L., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Appointed by the government of India a probationer under the statutory civil service rules; assistant magistrate and collector, 30th March, 1889; confirmed, 21st May, 1891; assistant settlement officer, Jan.-May, 1894.

SRI NAVASA RAGHAVA AIYANGAR, SESHAYANGAR, B.A., C.I.E., Dewan Bahadur, Madras Registration Department.—Joined the service as vernacular clerk in collector's office, Tanjore, Nov., 1870; head accountant, revenue board's office, March, 1878; 2nd assistant, Nov., 1879; and 1st assistant, Aug., 1880; inspector-general of registration, Madras, Sept., 1880; C.I.E., June, 1893; member of legislative council, Nov., 1893.

SRI NAVASA RAO, PATEKI, Dewan Bahadur, Judicial Department, Madras.—Served in the judicial department in various capacities from 16th Dec., 1844; appointed police magis-

trate, Madras, May, 1871; fellow of Madras University, Nov., 1875; small cause court judge, Madras, April, 1880; judge, Madras city civil court, Dec., 1892.

STACE, EDWARD VINCENT, C.B., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Political Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1862; from June, 1871, served in the Bombay revenue survey department as assistant superintendent, and as boundary settlement commissioner (July, 1872); between May, 1873, and March, 1875, acted as assistant political resident, Aden, and also as cantonment magistrate; from April, 1875, acted as assistant political agent, Kathiawar, and was on special duty with the son of H.H. the Jam of Navanagar, May, 1877; also acted as assistant political agent of Kolhapur and Southern Mahrattra country, and as superintendent of surveys under the Rajasthanik court, Kathiawar; second assistant resident at Aden, Sept., 1880; assistant political agent, Kathiawar, Dec., 1881; joint administrator, Rajpipla, Feb., 1885; lieutenant-colonel, July, 1887; first assistant political resident at Aden, and political agent for the Somali coast, Feb., 1889; acted as political resident at various periods; political agent and collector of stamp revenue, Cutch, Aug., 1893; political agent, Mahi Kantha, but officiating in Cutch, Jan., 1894.

STACK, GEORGE AUGUSTINE, Bengal Education Department.—Served from 1st Jan., 1868, as professor at the Agra, Bareilly, and Dacca colleges, and acted at different times as principal of the Bareilly college, inspector of schools and principal of the Krishnaghur college; professor, Patna college, Jan., 1883; professor in the Presidency college, Feb., 1888.

STACK, OLIVER STOKES, late Police Department, Bengal.—From 8th June, 1859, to May, 1862, served as deputy magistrate and deputy collector; from May, 1862, served in the police department, and was appointed district superintendent, 1st grade, Oct., 1878; deputy inspector-general, July, 1885; officiating inspector-general, June, 1886; retired, Oct., 1890.

STALEY, ALFRED EVELYN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Paul's School, Concord, U.S.A., and St. Peter's College, Radley; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived, 20th Oct., 1875, and was attached to N.W. Provinces; served in revenue and judicial department as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating joint magistrate and deputy collector; transferred to Bengal, Jan., 1880; officiating under secretary, government of Bengal, March, 1884, and again in 1885; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Aug., 1886; acting junior secretary to revenue board, March, 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891.

STANDEN, BERTRAM PRIOR, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Uppingham, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service (Burma) after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner; transferred in same capacity to Central Provinces, Jan., 1891; appointed also registrar, judicial commissioner's court, Nagpur, March, 1891; acting commissioner of excise, May to Aug., 1892; acting small cause court judge, Feb.-April, 1893.

STANSFELD, RIGHT HON. JAMES, M.P.—Born in 1820; educated at University College, London, where he took the degree of LL.B.; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1849; was returned to parliament for Halifax, April, 1859; appointed a lord of the admiralty in April, 1863; resigned in April, 1864; under secretary of state for India, Feb. to June, 1866; third lord of the treasury, Dec., 1868; joint secretary to the treasury, Oct., 1869; president of the poor law board, March, 1871; president of the new local government board, Aug., 1871, to Jan., 1874, and again March–Aug., 1886.

STANTIALL, WILLIAM, Public Works Department, India Office.—Educated at the City of London School; clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1882.

STAVELEY, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM DUNBAR, General, G.C.B.—Educated at the Scottish Naval and Military Academy, Edinburgh; 2nd lieutenant, 87th foot, March, 1835; assistant military secretary to Col. W. Staveley, in Mauritius, Feb.–Nov., 1842, and at Hong Kong, June, 1847, to Dec., 1850; A.D.C. to lieutenant-general Earl Cathcart, in Canada, March, 1845, to May, 1847; served in the campaign of 1854–55, including the battles of Alma and Balaklava, and the siege of Sebastopol (medal, C.B., Sardinian and Turkish medals, and 5th class of the Medjidie); brigadier-general in North China, 1860–62 (medal); from Feb., 1862, to June, 1863, was in command of the troops during the operations against the Taepings, in the vicinity of Shanghai, resulting in the capture of the entrenched camp of Onkatz, and the towns of Kadin, Tsinpoo Cholin, Najow, and Tserpoo; K.C.B., March, 1865; from March, 1865, to Sept., 1867, served as brigadier-general in the Bombay presidency, and was in command of the northern division and the Poona brigade successively; was second in command in the Abyssinian expedition, 1867–68, and was present at the action of Arrogee, and capture of Magdala; received the thanks of parliament, and was promoted to a major-general, 1868 (medal); major-general on the staff, western division, Devonport, Jan.–March, 1869; commander-in-chief, Bombay, and member of the governor's council, Oct., 1874, to Oct., 1878; G.C.B., May, 1884.

STEAD, VEN. SAMUEL, M.A. (Oxon.), late Ecclesiastical Department, Bombay.—Joined the service, March, 1863; arrived in India, 24th April, 1863, and served as chaplain at Karachi, Sholapur, cantonment of Belgaum, and Poona; senior chaplain, April, 1873; archdeacon and commissary, Nov., 1878; also held charge of the diocese of Bombay, Oct., 1877, to Nov., 1878, and Oct., 1883, to June, 1884; retired, June, 1886.

STEANE, WILLIAM COPLE, Registry Department, India Office.—Temporary clerk, 9th Nov., 1860; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

STEAVERSON, JOSEPH, B.A., Judicial Department, Madras.—Served from 25th Feb., 1878, as acting registrar of the high court (original side), Madras, and was confirmed in this appointment, Feb., 1880.

STEDMAN, EDWARD, C.B., Major-General, I.S.C.—First commission, 19th Dec., 1860; served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp); in the Afghan war of 1878–80, was with Sir Frederick Roberts in the march to Candahar, and was present at the battle of Candahar (medal with clasp and bronze decora-

tion); served in the Burma expedition of 1886–87, and commanded the operations in the Shan states (two clasps; C.B.); inspector-general of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; and of Burma, Oct., 1888 appointed quarter-master-general in India, March, 1892.

STEEDMAN, EDWARD BRITTAIN, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Shrewsbury; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and settlement officer; officiating director of settlements and revenue records, March, 1887; confirmed, May, 1887; designation changed to director of land records and agriculture; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1889; in 1892 officiated as director of land records and agriculture, and revenue secretary to government; author of 'Report on the Settlement of the Jhang District.'

STEEL, CHARLES DENTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Wesleyan Connexional School and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Oct., 1886; assistant superintendent and judge, small cause court, Dehra Dun, in 1888–89; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, April, 1889; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, April, 1891; on special duty, May, 1891; deputy commissioner, April, 1892; magistrate and collector, March, 1894; author of 'Allahabad Gazetteer.'

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Caius College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 10th Nov., 1862, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner; services placed temporarily at disposal of public works department in connection with Punjab Northern state railway, July, 1874; judicial assistant, 1st grade, Dec., 1878; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Nov., 1884; 1st grade, Jan., 1887; retired, May, 1889.

STEEL, JOHN PHILIP, Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Lieutenant, Bengal engineers, Dec., 1855; joined the public works department, March, 1860, as assistant engineer, Bengal; executive engineer, April, 1868; officiating assistant to chief engineer, and assistant secretary to government of Bengal, July, 1871; officiating under secretary, government of India, public works department, March, 1873, to 1875, and again Feb., 1877; superintending engineer and secretary to governor-general's agent, Rajputana, May, 1879; superintending engineer, 1st class, May, 1886; officiating chief engineer and secretary to government, Punjab, March, 1887; officiating secretary to chief commissioner, Central Provinces, Oct., 1887; chief engineer and joint secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, 1888; 3rd chief engineer, April, 1888; officiated as secretary to government, Feb.–May, 1889; and again Nov., 1889; confirmed, Dec., 1889; 2nd chief engineer, Dec., 1890; retired, March, 1892.

STEELE, CHARLES EDWARD STAFFORD, Sind Commission.—Educated at Victoria College,

Jersey; joined the service, 11th June, 1869, and served in Sind as supernumerary assistant settlement officer; supernumerary deputy collector, Dec., 1871; employed under the Transvaal government as finance and revenue commissioner, Oct., 1879, to Aug., 1881; served subsequently as assistant collector and magistrate, manager of encumbered estates, acting city magistrate, Karachi, and acting conservator of forests; head assistant collector, Feb., 1892; deputy commissioner, upper Sind frontier, Feb., 1894.

STEINBELT, JEAN MARI CONRAD, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant commissioner, and officiating deputy commissioner; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Mirzapur, Sept., 1878; from April, 1881, served as civil and sessions judge in Banda, and afterwards at Azamgarh; retired, April, 1887.

STEINBERG, ALFRED FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Dulwich College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1881, and served in Assam as assistant commissioner; transferred to Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, Jan., 1892; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1892; has also officiated as district and sessions judge.

STENT, WILLIAM KITSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 14th April, 1871, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, April, 1880; engineer-in-chief, Bellary-Kistna railway, in 1885 and 1886; services lent to South Indian railway company, Jan., 1891; employed on Bengal-Assam line, Nov., 1891; engineer-in-chief, Delhi-Minchinabad railway survey, Nov., 1892.

STEPHEN, ARTHUR, M.A., M.B., Brigade-Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Punjab.—Joined the service as probationer at Netley, 30th Sept., 1867; served under the military department in Abyssinia and the Punjab as assistant surgeon and surgeon; acted at various periods as civil surgeon, jail superintendent, and chemical examiner to the Punjab government; superintendent of jail, Lahore, Sept., 1875; statistical officer to government of India, sanitary and medical departments, Nov., 1880; officiated as sanitary commissioner, Punjab, in 1885 and 1886, and was confirmed, Nov., 1886; acting principal medical officer and sanitary commissioner, Assam, March, 1894.

STEPHEN, CARR, Barrister-at-Law.—Served in the Punjab from 11th June, 1856, as extra assistant commissioner and small cause court judge; extra judicial assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Oct., 1875; officiating divisional judge, Nov., 1885, to Jan., 1886; retired, 1890; has published 'Indian Registration Act,' 4th edition, 'Handbook for Delhi,' and 'Archæology of Delhi.'

STEPHEN, KENT HUME, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 24th Sept., 1878, and employed on irrigation works in

Bengal; executive engineer, March, 1889; under secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, March, 1892.

STERNDALE, R. A., late Financial Department, Government of India.—Served during the Mutiny as a volunteer, 1857, and took part in the operations in Central India (medal); lieutenant, Behar station guards, Jan., 1858; from Aug., 1859, served in the Central Provinces as deputy collector and assistant settlement officer; from June, 1864, served in the financial department in Nagpore, Calcutta, and the Punjab, as assistant commissioner of paper currency, acting assistant accountant-general, acting deputy accountant-general, and acting assistant comptroller-general; 3rd class of financial department (new scheme), Aug., 1880; assistant accountant-general, Bombay, Jan., 1884, and Madras, Nov., 1887; retired, 1890; is a fellow of the Royal Geographical and Zoological Societies; author of 'Mammalia of British India and Ceylon,' 'Denizens of the Jungles,' 'Seoni; or Camp Life on the Satpura Range,' a 'Topographical and Historical Account of the District of Seoni,' 'Turkey and India; or Our Indian Moslems,' 'The Afghan Knife,' and other works; joint editor of the 'Journal of the Bombay Natural History Society.'

STEVENS, CHARLES CECIL, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S.
