(Bengal).—Educated at University of Melbourne; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 2nd Feb., 1862; from Jan., 1863, served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Oct., 1873; officiating secretary, board of revenue, April, 1882; commissioner, Aug., 1885; officiating chief secretary to government of Bengal, May-Nov., 1890; member of revenue board, Dec., 1891; member of legislative council of governor-general, Jan., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1895.

STEVENS, JOHN FOSTER, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Birmingham Grammar School, at Versailles, and at King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1864; arrived, 16th Oct., 1865; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, May, 1876; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1884; officiating judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, Dec., 1891, to Dec., 1893, and was appointed to that post, April, 1894.

STEVENSON, FRANCIS, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Appointed supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

STEVENSON, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King's College, Aberdeen, and Glasgow University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1872; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Assam, Jan., 1880, and served as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Sylhet, Jan., 1885; returned to Bengal, May, 1889; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, June, 1889; magistrate and collector, July, 1889.

STEVENSON - MOORE, CHARLES JAMES, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Felsted

School, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1837, and served as assistant magistrate and collector.

STEWART, ARTHUR BENNET, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Rugby and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 5th Nov., 1870, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant political agent, acting junior collector and magistrate, and acting forest settlement officer; second assistant collector, Dec., 1882; acting postmaster-general, Bombay, Aug., 1884, to May, 1885; acting assistant judge, Poona, and assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan, Oct., 1885; judicial assistant to political agent, Kathiawar, Nov., 1887; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, May, 1891; 2nd grade, Nov., 1893.

STEWART, ALFRED JOSEPH, Political Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 27th Sept., 1892.

STEWART, SIR DONALD MARTIN, Bart., Field-Marshal, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Ensign, Bengal army, 12th Oct., 1840; adjutant, 9th Bengal native infantry, 1845; interpreter and quartermaster, 9th N.I., 1852; served against the hill tribes on the Peshawur frontier in 1854-55, and against the Bussee Kheyl tribes in 1855 (medal and clasp); commanded volunteers in Allyghur district, May and June, 1857; deputy assistant adjutant-general, Delhi field force, June, 1857; assistant adjutant-general of army, Dec., 1857; was present at siege and capture of Lucknow, and the operations in Rohilcund (medal with two clasps); deputy adjutant-general in India, Feb., 1863; brigadier-general, Abyssinian expeditionary force, 1868 (medal and C.B.); in command of Peshawur district, July, 1869; chief commissioner of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, 1871; in command of Lahore Division, April, 1876; commanding Kandahar and Southern Afghanistan force, 1878-80; commanding at Cabul and in Northern Afghanistan, 1880, till withdrawal from that country (received the thanks of both houses of parliament; K.C.B., G.C.B., baronet, and medal with clasp); military member of governor-general's council, 1880; commander-in-chief in India, and extraordinary member of the governor-general's council, 1881; member of council of India, 16th Dec., 1885; field-marshal, May, 1894.

STEWART, ROBERT BARTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Dulwich and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1886; assistant collector and magistrate, May, 1887; 2nd assistant, Feb., 1893.

STEWART, WILLIAM HALL MACKINTOSH, Captain, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 25th May, 1878; from June, 1889, acted as attaché, foreign department, and assistant secretary to government; confirmed in former post, Oct., 1890; assistant agent, Indore, Jan., 1891; assistant resident, Kashmir, July, 1891; assistant to the political agent, Gilgit, April, 1893.

STEWART-WILSON, CHARLES, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Edu-

cated at Edinburgh High School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1886; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Dec., 1890; officiating under-secretary to government, Oct., 1889; confirmed, Aug., 1891; officiating post-master-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April, 1892, Bengal, Nov., 1893, and Bombay, June, 1894.

STIFFE, ARTHUR W., Captain, late R.I.M.—From 24th Feb., 1849, served in the Indian marine as midshipman, lieutenant, and senior lieutenant, and assistant surveyor; served in Persian war, 1856-57; on special survey for telegraph cable in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1862; on special duty in Turkish Arabia, Feb., 1863; engineer, Persian Gulf telegraphs, and commanding 'Amberwitch,' Oct., 1863; officiating director, Persian Gulf telegraphs, April-Aug., 1866, and Sept., 1869, to Jan., 1871; also electrician, Aug., 1871; on special duty in England, May, 1875, and May, 1878; in command of cable steamer 'Patrick Stewart,' May-July, 1879; port officer, Calcutta, Aug., 1879; officiating superintendent of marine, July, 1880; ex officio shipping master of Calcutta, Feb., 1882; was also president of board of examiners in seamanship, registrar of shipping and wrecks, and presidency magistrate; retired, 1888; has brought out new charts and pilots of the Persian Gulf and Mekran coast; also a new chart of Maskat, and a description of the Hooghly.

STIMPSON, EDWARD JOHN, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 21st May, 1873; resigned, 24th June, 1879; reappointed, 10th July, 1880.

STOGDON, ABRAHAM WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Uppingham Grammar School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating cantonment magistrate; judicial assistant, Sept., 1877; services placed temporarily at disposal of government of India, military department, Feb., 1881; deputy commissioner, Aug., 1883; divisional judge, Nov., 1884; additional judge of the chief court, Nov., 1893.

STOKER, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Rathmines School and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; deputy superintendent of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, March, 1878; 1st assistant secretary to government, April, 1884; under secretary to government, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, May, 1884; officiated as secretary to government in the financial department in 1885 and 1886; settlement officer, Bulandshahr, Sept., 1886; inspector-general of registration, and commissioner of excise and stamps, Nov., 1891; author of 'A Gold Standard for India.'

STOKES, GABRIEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Kilkenny College, Royal School, Armagh, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in

India, 28th Oct., 1871, and served in Bombay till Dec., 1872, and afterwards in Madras as assistant magistrate and collector, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting principal assistant, and acting collector and magistrate; acting additional under secretary to government of Madras in the revenue (famine) department, May, 1877, to April, 1879; transferred permanently to the Madras civil service, Sept., 1878; on special duty preparing Madras administration report, Sept., 1879; acting Canarese translator to government, Feb., 1880; acting assistant secretary to government, judicial and legislative departments, July, 1880; deputy superintendent of census operations, Sept., 1880; sub-collector and joint magistrate, South Arcot, May, 1886; collector and magistrate, Aug., 1880.

STOKES, SIR HENRY EDWARD, K.C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Minehead Diocesan School, Kilkenny College, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived, 4th Jan., 1864; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1866; under secretary to government of Madras in the departments under the chief secretary, Aug., 1869; acting collector of sea customs and protector of emigrants, Madras, Jan., 1871; fellow of the university of Madras, Feb., 1875; sub-secretary, board of revenue, July, 1875; on special duty under government of India, with the Madras salt commission, Jan.-Sept., 1876; secretary, board of revenue, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878, and additional secretary on famine duties, Feb., 1877; district and sessions judge, South Malabar, Oct., 1879; collector and magistrate, Salem, June, 1881; member of forest draft bill committee, April, 1882; member of local self-government committee, June, 1882; secretary to government of Madras in the revenue department, Jan., 1884; acting chief secretary to government, Oct., 1886; additional member of council, Madras, for making laws and regulations, Oct., 1886; chief secretary to government, Oct., 1887; temporary member of governor's council, July, 1888; appointed ordinary member and assumed office, 9th Nov., 1888; C.S.I., May, 1889; K.C.S.I., May, 1892; retired, Nov., 1893.

STOKES, HENRY JOHN, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at St. Columba College, Rathfarnham, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; assistant collector and magistrate, Oct., 1863; served in Bombay from Oct., 1864, to Jan., 1870, and subsequently in Madras, and was appointed principal assistant, July, 1871, district and sessions judge, Kistna, June, 1877, and collector and magistrate, Madura, June, 1879; on special famine duty, Madura, Aug., 1877; district and sessions judge, Madura, Jan., 1884; collector and magistrate, North Arcot, Oct., 1884; member of draft tenancy bill committee, July, 1885; district and sessions judge, Coimbatore, May, 1886; on deputation as a member of the public service commission, Nov., 1886; on special duty in connection with rent recovery bill commission; retired, July, 1889.

STOKES, WHITLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., Hon. D.C.L. (Oxon.), LL.D. (Dublin), Barrister-at-Law (Inner Temple), late Legislative Department, Government of India.—Reporter to the high court and acting administrator-

general, Madras, 1863-64; served subsequently as assistant secretary to government of India, legislative branch of home department, secretary to the governor-general's legislative council, and secretary to the government of India in the legislative department; law member of the council of the governor-general, May, 1877, to May, 1882; president of Indian law commission, 1879; in 1868, framed the scheme for collecting, copying, and cataloguing the Sanskrit MSS. preserved in India, in recognition of which he was made honorary member of the Deutsche Morgenländische Gesellschaft; correspondent of the Institut de France (Académie des Inscriptions et Belles Lettres), fellow of Calcutta University, and honorary fellow of Jesus College, Oxford; author of 'A Treatise on the liens of legal Practitioners;' 'On Powers of Attorney' ('Bythewood and Jarman's Conveyancing,' vol. VIII., part 1); 'Hindu Law Books,' Madras, 1865; the 'Indian Succession Act, with a Commentary,' Calcutta, 1865; the 'Indian Companies Act, 1866, with Notes'; the 'Older Statutes in force in India, with Notes,' 1874; the 'Unrepealed General Acts of the Governor-General of India, with Chronological Tables,' &c., 3 vols., Calcutta, 1875 and 1876; the 'Anglo-Indian Codes,' vol. I., 1887, and vol. II., 1888 (Clarendon Press, Oxford); the 'Tripartite Life of Patrick, with other documents relating to that Saint,' 1887; and various philological works published in Germany, France, and England.

STONE, SAMUEL JAMES, late Police Department, Punjab.—Appointed assistant district superintendent of police, 10th March, 1861; district superintendent, Nov., 1872; served in Assam from Dec., 1884, to March, 1885, when for a short time he was on special duty at Rawalpindi; resumed his post in the Punjab, April, 1885; promoted to 1st grade, Sept., 1888; retired, Feb., 1894.

STOREY, HENRY FRANCIS, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, Nov., 1868, as executive engineer on the Punjab Northern state railway; transferred to the Neemuch-Nasirabad railway surveys, 1878, and subsequently to the Indus Valley railway; engineer-in-chief, Kandahar state railway March, 1880; engineer-in-chief, Indus Valley and Kandahar railways, Oct., 1881; superintendent of way and works, Sukkur, 1883; officiating manager, Indus Valley railway, Nov., 1885; superintending engineer, North-Western railway, Jan., 1886; superintending engineer, 3rd class, May, 1886; 2nd class, Feb., 1888; officiating secretary to resident, Hyderabad, June, 1888; confirmed in that appointment, Dec., 1889; 1st superintending engineer, Dec., 1889; retired, April, 1893.

STOW, ALAN CHARLES, Accounts Department, India Office.—Appointed 2nd class clerk, 7th Oct., 1893.

STRACHEY, SIR JOHN, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1842; served in the N.W. Provinces in the grades of assistant commissioner and assistant magistrate and collector; senior assistant, Kumaon, 1848; magistrate and collector, Moradabad, 1854; officiating commissioner of Kumaon, 1861; president of commission to inquire into cholera epidemic of

1861; judicial commissioner, Central Provinces, 1862; president of the sanitary commission with government of India, 1864; chief commissioner, Oudh, 1866; member of governor-general's council, 7th March, 1868; acting viceroy and governor-general on death of Earl of Mayo, 9th to 23rd Feb., 1872; lieutenant-governor, N.W. Provinces, 7th April, 1874; retired from the civil service, Dec., 1876; financial member of the governor-general's council, 23rd Dec., 1876, to 1880; left India, Dec., 1880; member of council of India, 14th July, 1885; joint author with Lieutenant-General R. Strachey of 'The Finances and Public Works of India,' 1882; author of 'India,' 1888 (2nd ed., 1894), and of 'Hastings and the Rohilla War,' 1892.

STRACHEY, RICHARD, Lieutenant-General, R.E., C.S.I., F.R.S.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1836; was employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Provinces from 1840, and appointed executive engineer, Ganges canal, July, 1843; under secretary to government of India, public works department, 1857; secretary to government, Central Provinces, 1857; consulting engineer, railway department, Sept., 1858; secretary to government of India, public works department, 1862; inspector-general of irrigation, and chief engineer, 1st class, Dec., 1866; additional member of governor-general's council, 1869; inspecting engineer of machinery and stores for state railways, India office, 1871; member of council of India, 5th Jan., 1875, to 12th July, 1878; on special duty in India, 1878; officiating financial member of council of governor-general, 1879; officiating member of council of governor-general, 1879; president of the famine commission, 1880; reappointed member of council of India, 11th March, 1879; president, royal geographical society, 1887; retired from the council of India, on his appointment as chairman of the East Indian railway company, 2nd Oct., 1889; appointed a representative of India at the international monetary conference, Brussels, Aug., 1892; joint author with Sir John Strachey of 'The Finances and Public Works of India,' 1882.

STRAFFORD (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. GEORGE HENRY CHARLES BYNG.—Born in 1830; educated at Eton and Oxford; sat in the House of Commons for Tavistock, 1852 to 1857, and for Middlesex, 1857 to 1874, when he succeeded to the upper house, as Viscount Enfield; attached to Earl Russell's special mission to Vienna, 1855; parliamentary secretary to the poor law board, 1865 till July, 1866; under secretary for foreign affairs, Dec., 1870; under secretary of state for India, Sept., 1880, to Dec., 1882; succeeded to the earldom of Stafford in 1886.

STRAHAN, CHARLES, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., Survey of India Department.—First commission, Bengal engineers, June, 1860; arrived in India, Oct., 1862; joined the survey department as military 2nd assistant, Sept., 1863; served with the Bhutan field force in charge of reconnoitring party, Oct., 1865, to July, 1866 (medal and clasp); surveyor, Dec., 1867; deputy superintendent, Sept., 1869; served with the Peshawur field force in Afghanistan, 1879 (medal, brevet of major); assistant surveyor-general in charge of cartographic and engraving branches, May, 1884; officiating deputy surveyor-general (revenue branch), April, 1889; confirmed, July, 1892.

STRAHAN, GEORGE, Colonel, R.E., late Survey of India Department.—Obtained commission from Addiscombe, Dec., 1858; arrived in India, Nov., 1860; served under the military department, and also in the irrigation branch, public works department, till July, 1862, when he was appointed to the survey department; served as assistant in the Gwalior and Central Indias survey, and afterwards held charges in the Rajputana, Mysore, and Khandesh surveys; deputy superintendent, Nov., 1867; officiating assistant surveyor-general, in charge of engraving and cartographic branches, Calcutta, April-July, 1883; deputy superintendent, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; on duty in Europe, April-Aug., 1885; deputy surveyor-general (trigonometrical branch), Oct., 1889; officiated as surveyor-general in 1892; reverted to military department, May, 1894.

STRAIGHT, SIR DOUGLAS, Kt., B.L., late Judicial Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple, Nov., 1865; counsel to London banks association from 1868; junior counsel, treasury prosecution, from 1877; sat in House of Commons as member for Shrewsbury, 1870 to 1874; appointed one of the royal commissioners to inquire into corrupt election practices at Boston, 1875; puisne judge, high court, N.W. Provinces, June, 1879; fellow of Calcutta University, June, 1883, and of the Allahabad University, Nov., 1887; officiated as chief justice of the N.W. Provinces high court in 1883, 1884, and 1886; retired, April, 1892; Kt., Aug., 1892.

STRATTON, JOHN PROUDFOOT, Brigade-Surgeon, M.D., late Bombay Medical Establishment.—Assistant surgeon, Bombay medical service, Oct., 1852; between 1854 and 1859 was employed as residency surgeon, Baroda, also serving as assistant resident, 1857-59; from Sept., 1859, served as assistant resident in Bundelcund; political agent, Bundelcund, June, 1864; surgeon-major, Oct., 1872; political agent, Meywar, 1881; resident, Western states, Rajputana, July, 1881, and in the Eastern Rajputana states, 1882, till Dec., 1885, when his services were replaced at disposal of military department; retired, Dec., 1886.

STRATTON, WALLACE CHRISTOPHER RAMSAY, Captain, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 26th July, 1881; from May, 1885, acted as assistant agent, Rajputana, and assistant-general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti; confirmed as assistant agent, Mount Abu, March, 1888; political agent and consul, Muscat, April, 1889; 1st assistant resident, Nov., 1889; transferred to Bushire, Dec., 1889; assistant political agent and consul, Basra, April, 1890; 1st assistant agent, Baluchistan, April, 1891; author of a narrative of Muscat affairs, 1885-88.

STREATFEILD, CLAUDE ARTHUR CECIL, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Marlborough and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 21st Nov., 1891, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

STREATFIELD, HENRY CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Marlborough and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived, 29th Dec., 1887, and

served as assistant magistrate and collector; officiating under-secretary to the government of Bengal, financial and municipal departments, June, 1890; confirmed, Nov., 1890; officiating in the judicial, &c., departments, April, 1892; confirmed, Oct., 1892; employed as secretary to the jury commission, Feb.-March, 1893.

STREET, BROOKS HENRY GEORGE, Store Department, India Office.—Writer, 30th June, 1874; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878; supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depôt, Oct., 1883.

STREET, HENRY ALFRED, Captain, R.I.M.—Commander of 'Tenasserim,' Dec., 1876, to June, 1880; assistant secretary to government of India, military (marine) department, June, 1880, to March, 1882; on duty in Bombay, 1882-84; assistant director, Indian marine, April, 1884; 1st grade commander, Dec., 1886; port officer, Madras, Dec., 1887; presidency port officer, Madras, Aug., 1888, to Jan., 1893; officiating in the same post from June, 1893, to May, 1894.

STRICKLAND, WILLIAM ALEXANDER WICKEDE, Captain, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—1st commission, Oct., 1880; employed in Burma as assistant commissioner, Aug., 1886.

STROVER, GEORGE AUGUSTUS, Colonel, I.S.C., Burma Commission.—Joined the service as ensign, 20th Jan., 1857; arrived at Madras, Feb., 1857; served in the Burma commission from 14th April, 1862, as assistant commissioner and cantonment magistrate; officiating magistrate and small cause court judge of Rangoon, 1866; superintendent of hill tribes, North Arakan, Sept., 1868; from Aug., 1869, to Sept., 1875, served in the political department as assistant political agent at Bhamo, and political agent at Mandalay (Jan., 1872); from Nov., 1877, served as deputy commissioner of Toungheo, and acted as commissioner of Arakan, Aug. to Nov., 1883; 1st grade deputy commissioner, July, 1881; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1885; commissioner, May, 1889; acted as financial commissioner in 1892.

STUART, ANDREW EDMUND CASTLE-STUART, F.G.S., F.L.S., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Wellington College and Kingstown School, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, and forest settlement officer; special assistant to the collectors of Nilgiris and Malabar for settlement purposes, Aug., 1886; head assistant to collector and magistrate, July, 1888; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Feb., 1893; compiled a 'Manual of the Wynad.'

STUART, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, late Postal Department, Bombay.—Served from 23rd Nov., 1855, as postmaster in Mysore, Madras, and Hyderabad; presidency postmaster, Madras, Jan., 1864; from March, 1865, served in Bombay as presidency postmaster, and also officiated for various periods as postmaster-general, Bombay, Punjab, and Madras; retired, Feb., 1892.

STUART, CHARLES JOSEPH, Colonel, I.S.C., late Madras Revenue Department.—Arrived in India, 21st Aug., 1852; served in the Madras revenue settlement department from April, 1861, as assistant director and deputy director; special assistant to collector of Kurnool for survey and settlement purposes, April, 1871,

to Nov., 1873, and again in Chingleput, Dec., 1873, and in Madura, April, 1882; 1st grade of deputy directors, June, 1883; retired, 1889.

STUART, GEORGE HENRY, M.A., Madras Educational Department.—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Aug., 1882, as principal, Kumbakonum college; has acted at various periods as principal and professor, Presidency college.

STUART, HAROLD ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at High School, Bishop's Stortford, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1879; arrived, 25th Nov., 1881, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting assistant secretary to government in the judicial and legislative departments (Dec., 1884); acting under secretary to government, revenue department, Jan., 1886; sub. pro tem. under secretary in the chief secretary's department, Oct., 1886; under secretary to government, revenue department, Sept., 1888, and chief secretary's department, Nov., 1888; acting private secretary to governor of Madras, Nov.-Dec., 1889; provincial superintendent of census operations, April, 1890; on special duty to revise district manuals, March, 1893; sub-collector and joint magistrate, August, 1893.

STUART, HARRY, India Public Works Department.—Joined the service as deputy examiner of railway accounts, 29th Jan., 1878; examiner, Jan., 1882; acted as manager, Tirhoot state railway, in 1889 and 1890; government examiner, Indian Midland railway, Nov., 1890; examiner of accounts, E. Bengal state railway, July, 1892.

STUART, LOUIS, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at the Charterhouse, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 17th Dec., 1891, and served as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner.

STUART, SIR ROBERT, Knt., Q.C., late High Court, N.W. Provinces.—Educated at Perth, and afterwards at the University of Edinburgh; called to the Scotch bar, July, 1840; and to the English bar at Lincoln's Inn, June, 1856; practised at the chancery bar as an equity draughtsman and conveyancer, and as an advising counsel; practised also in the House of Lords and privy council, and before parliamentary committees of both houses until 1868, when he was appointed Q.C., and soon after elected a bencher of his inn; appointed chief justice of the North-West Provinces, India, and knighted, August, 1871; retired, March, 1884; is a member of the general council of the University of Edinburgh, and has been a frequent contributor to periodical literature, especially on legal subjects; author of many legal pamphlets.

STURDY, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Audit Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 27th Dec., 1894.

STURROCK, JOHN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the High School, Dundee; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1864; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1866; acting secretary to government in the revenue department, Nov., 1873;

boundary commissioner between Canara and Mysore, May, 1873, and again in 1880-81; Canarese translator to government, Jan., 1874; under secretary to government in the chief secretary's department, July, 1875; additional sub-secretary, board of revenue, for special duty with famine commission, Nov., 1878, also acting as secretary, Jan., 1879; sub-secretary, board of revenue, Oct., 1879; collector and magistrate, Oct., 1883; acting fourth member of board of revenue and commissioner of salt revenue, &c., in 1888, 1889, and from Jan., 1894; acting collector of sea customs, &c., May-Aug., 1893; member of legislative council, March, 1894.

STURT, WILLIAM NEVILLE, Revenue and Statistics Department, India Office.—Educated at Repton; temporary clerk, 1st May, 1862; junior clerk, store department, 1862; accountant-general's department, 1863; military department, 1863; private secretary to parliamentary under-secretary of state, 1863-64, and 1868-77; senior clerk, revenue department, April, 1877; served temporarily in political and secret department, 1890-94.

SULLIVAN, HENRY EDWARD, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1850; served in revenue and judicial departments, Madras, and was appointed small cause court judge, Cuddalore, Sept., 1866; civil and sessions judge, Cuddapah, Dec., 1866; collector and magistrate, Bellary, March, 1868, and subsequently of Trichinopoly; officiating director of revenue settlement, Feb., 1874; acting member, board of revenue, and president of the committee for examination of assistants, April, 1876; in charge of the inam department, May, 1876; acting resident of Travancore and Cochin, March, 1877, to Feb., 1878; board of revenue, April, 1878; famine commission, Jan., 1879; 1st member, board of revenue, and commissioner, uncovenanted civil service examinations, Jan., 1879; on duty studying records of famine commission, April, 1879; on special duty in Godaveri district, investigating causes of the disturbances in Rump country, July, 1879; on duty to England as member of famine commission, Oct., 1879, to July, 1880; additional member of council for making laws and regulations, Jan., 1881; in charge of settlement department, Feb., 1881; ordinary member of council, June, 1882; president of local self-government committee, June, 1882; retired, Dec., 1886.

SULLIVAN, WILLIAM JAMES AUGUSTUS, Madras Police Department.—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1869; assistant superintendent of police, April, 1873; superintendent, June, 1882; deputy inspector-general, Oct., 1893.

SUMBRAMANIYAM, NARANAIIYAR, B.A., B.L., Madras Judicial Department.—Acted as administrator-general, Madras, May, 1891, to Feb., 1892; appointed judge, Madras small cause court, April, 1892.

SURYA KUMAR AGASTI, M.A., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Appointed under the native civil service rules assistant magistrate and collector, Purneah, on probation, March, 1885; confirmed in the statutory civil service, April, 1887; joint magistrate and deputy collector, June, 1892.

SUTTON, ERNEST WILLIAM, Political Department, India Office.—Second division clerk, census office, 7th April, 1891; 2nd class clerk, India office, 1st Sept., 1891.

SWAIN, WILLIAM HENRY, B.Sc., Military Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th June, 1881; B.Sc. (honours), London University, 1882.

SWAMINATHA AIYAR, TIRUVARAR MUTUSWAMI AIYAR, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Madras University; appointed probationer for the civil service, Nov., 1883; confirmed as a member of the civil service, Jan., 1886; assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1887; head assistant, Feb., 1893.

SWETENHAM, EDMUND, Colonel, I.S.C., late Public Works Department, North-Western Provinces.—Joined public works department from Roorkee college, Nov., 1853, and served in the Punjab; resigned on obtaining commission in the army, March, 1857; served in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1858 (medal); reappointed to public works department, Feb., 1859, and served in N.W. Provinces, buildings and roads branch; executive engineer, 1st grade, Sept., 1869; transferred to British Burma, May, 1872; transferred to Central India, and served from Sept., 1874, to Aug., 1880, as executive engineer, Mhow division, and acted for various periods as chief engineer, and secretary to governor-general's agent; transferred to N.W. Provinces, buildings and roads branch, Aug., 1880, and promoted to superintending engineer, 3rd class, Sept., 1880; 2nd class, July, 1884; 1st class, permanent, April, 1888; retired, Feb., 1890.

SWIFTE, LATHAM CODDINGTON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Eton and New College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 6th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

SYAD ALAY MAHOMED, Statutory C.S. (Central Provinces).—Acting extra assistant commissioner, 4th class, 26th Jan., 1881; probationer for the native civil service, Dec., 1882; appointed member of the civil service, and supernumerary assistant commissioner, 12th Dec., 1883; judge, small cause court, April, 1892; deputy commissioner, April, 1894.

SYAD NUR-UD-DIN KHAN, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Served in Hyderabad from May, 1878, as attaché to resident, judge of small causes, &c.; assistant commissioner, Jan., 1883; under secretary to Hyderabad state, Jan., 1885; appointed a probationer under native civil service rules, March, 1886; assistant collector and magistrate, Jan., 1888; 2nd assistant, May, 1893.

SYKES, ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Giggleswick Grammar School and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1888, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and officiating under secretary to government; junior secretary to financial commissioners, Dec., 1893.

SYMES, EDWARD SPENCE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at University College School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1875, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Burma, Aug., 1876; served as assistant commissioner, and was placed on special duty in the secretariat, July and Nov., 1878; on

special forest demarcation duty, Feb., 1879; personal assistant to chief commissioner, May, 1879; junior secretary to chief commissioner, June, 1881; also officiated as secretary to the chief commissioner for various periods, and as settlement secretary, March-May, 1882; secretary to chief commissioner and officer in charge of Delhi state prisoners, April, 1886; C.I.E., May, 1886; chief secretary to chief commissioner, April, 1887; commissioner, Minbu, July, 1890; resumed post as chief secretary, Dec., 1890.

SYMNS, ROBERT CORSER MONTFORT, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Westminster, King's College School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner; acting under secretary, March, 1894; officiating deputy commissioner, June, 1894.

SYMONDS, WILLIAM POWELL, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Hereford Cathedral School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1876, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; member of the central committee for departmental examinations, April, 1884; acting under secretary to government, revenue, financial, and general departments, 1884; forest settlement officer, March, 1887; and assistant collector, and acting director of land records and agriculture, Aug., 1887; acting first assistant collector and magistrate, Poona, Nov., 1887; on special duty, Kathiawar, March, 1889; postmaster-general, Bombay, May, 1889; revised the manual of revenue accounts in Bombay.

SZCZEPANSKI, HENRY CHARLES ANTHONY, Colonel, I.S.C., Berar Commission.—First commission, Bengal infantry, June, 1857; arrived in India, 9th Sept., 1857; was employed under the military department up to 1860, including service during the Mutiny (medal); assistant commissioner, Punjab, July, 1860; on special famine duty, Nov., 1861; transferred to Hyderabad assigned districts, Nov., 1864, and served as assistant commissioner, cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, and deputy commissioner (1st class, Feb., 1885); has officiated as judicial commissioner at various periods, and as commissioner in 1888 and 1889.

TABOR, FRANCIS SAMUEL, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at St. Paul's School and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; transferred in the latter capacity to the Central Provinces, May, 1893.

TAHEIRUDDIN AHMED, B.L., Bengal Provincial Service.—Appointed probationer under the native civil service rules, 9th March, 1890; assistant settlement officer in Orissa, March, 1891; appointed to provincial service as deputy magistrate and deputy collector, 6th grade, Nov., 1893.

TALBOT, ADELBERT CECIL, C.I.E., Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, royal artillery, Jan., 1867; joined the Bengal staff corps, 1869, and served with 4th cavalry, Hyderabad contingent; on special duty with the Afghan commissioner, Seistan arbitration, 1872-73; officiating assistant

agent, Rajputana, 1873; attaché, secretariat, foreign department, 1874; officiating assistant secretary to the government of India, legislative department, and officiating secretary to board of examiners, Calcutta, 1875-76; acting assistant resident, and assistant general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti department, Hyderabad, 1878; 1st assistant agent, Rajputana, Feb., 1878; fellow of Calcutta University, March, 1879; additional political agent, Eastern Rajputana states, 1881; political agent, Bhurtpore and Kerowlee, 1882; political agent, Bikanir, 1883; graded as additional political agent, 1st class; officiated as resident in Turkish Arabia, June, 1888; political agent in Bikanir, May, 1889; officiating resident, Persian Gulf, March, 1891; confirmed, Sept., 1891; deputy secretary to government of India, foreign department, May, 1894; edited a revised edition of 'Aitchison's Treaties,' 1876.

TALBOT, GERALD HENRY, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at St. Peter's College, Radley; supervisor, 1st grade, India store depot, 10th June, 1877.

TALBOT, WALTER, Store Department, India Office.—Appointed supplemental clerk, India store depot, 1st Jan., 1893.

TALBOT, WALTER STANLEY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Wellington College and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 31st Oct., 1890, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; officiating under secretary to government, May to Oct., 1892.

TANNER, HENRY CHARLES BASKERVILLE, Colonel, I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commission, Bombay artillery, June, 1854; accompanied the Persian expeditionary force of 1856 (medal and clasp); joined the survey department as assistant in the Sind revenue survey, Oct., 1862; was afterwards employed in the Central Provinces revenue survey, and was in charge of the Bombay city survey, Dec., 1869; deputy superintendent, 3rd grade, April, 1870; was subsequently employed in the Bhawalpur revenue survey, and the Deccan survey, and accompanied the Lushai expedition in 1871-72, and the Khyber column in the Afghan campaign, 1878-79; was afterwards in charge of the Darjeeling and Nepal boundary parties, Nov., 1882; deputy superintendent, 2nd grade, Oct., 1884; employed with the Tibet mission from May, 1886; reverted to military duty, 1890.

TAPLIN, HERBERT, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Appointed supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

TARGET, CHARLES ALBERT BAGENAL, India Public Works Department.—Appointed to the Madras public works department as assistant engineer, 8th Oct., 1868; transferred to state railways, April, 1870; and to Mysore, irrigation branch, March, 1872; executive engineer, Sept., 1874; employed in Calcutta mint, Feb., 1880; served in Rajputana, 1880-83, and in Madras, 1883-88; transferred to Burma, March, 1888; to Madras, June, 1888; and to Rajputana, May, 1890.

TATE, ALEXANDER CHAMBERS, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector

and magistrate; head assistant, Anantapur, Sept., 1888; principal assistant, Vizagapatam, Jan., 1892; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Godavari, March, 1894.

TATE, JOHN, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Feb., 1873.

TATE, WILLIAM JACOB, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Repton; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1874, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; head assistant collector, Salem, Jan., 1883; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1889; district and sessions judge, March, 1890.

TAUNTON, ALFRED GEORGE, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at the Jesuits' College, Tournay; writer, India store dépôt, 29th July, 1868; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

TAWNEY, CHARLES HENRY, C.I.E., M.A., Librarian, India Office.—Educated at Rugby and Trinity College, Cambridge, of which he was elected a fellow in 1860; joined the Bengal educational department, Nov., 1864, and served as professor, Presidency college, up to Nov., 1876, when he was appointed principal of the college, and promoted to 1st class of the educational service; officiating director of public instruction, Aug., 1879, and again in 1885, 1887, 1889, and 1892; registrar of Calcutta university, 1877, 1884, and 1889; librarian, India office, 1st Oct., 1893; has translated from the Sanskrit the Kathā Sarit Sāgara, the Uttara Rāma Charita, the Mālavikāgni-mitra, and two centuries of Bhartrihari.

TAWNEY, JAMES WALTER, late Bengal C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Repton School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 23rd Dec., 1863; served as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner, Bhutan, till Oct., 1865, and subsequently in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner; also acted as small cause court judge, and cantonment magistrate, Jubbulpore, in 1873, and as small cause court judge of Nagpur in 1876; deputy commissioner, 4th class, Oct., 1881; retired, April, 1889.

TAYLER, SKIPWITH HENRY CHURCHILL, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived, 2nd March, 1853; between July, 1854, and Sept., 1872, served as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and magistrate and collector, 1st grade; from Sept., 1872, served in the judicial department, and was appointed district and sessions judge, 1st grade, March, 1875; retired, April, 1887.

TAYLER, WILLIAM VANSITTART GRAHAM, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived, 6th Nov., 1855; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Pubna, Oct., 1866; retired, 1890.

TAYLOR, SIR ALEXANDER, G.C.B., General, R.E.—Educated at Hofuzl, Switzerland, and Addiscombe military college; 1st commission, 9th June, 1843; major, Jan., 1858; colonel,

March, 1864; lieutenant-general, Oct., 1877; and general (honorary rank), Dec., 1878; entered the Indian public works department, 26th Dec., 1846, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Jan., 1848; superintendent of the Lahore and Peshawur road, Dec., 1849; superintending engineer, Nov., 1856; officiating secretary to the government of the Punjab, public works department, Jan., 1865, to Jan., 1867, and Feb., 1871, to Feb., 1872; chief engineer, April, 1872; deputy inspector-general of military works, 1870-78; president of the defence committee of India, 1876, 1877, 1878; president of the royal Indian engineering college, Cooper's Hill, from Nov., 1880; served throughout the Sutlej campaign (medal with clasps for Ferozeshah and Sobraon), and throughout the Punjab campaign, 1848-49, being present at the action of Sooruj Khoond, the capture of Mooltan, and the battle of Goojerat (medal with 2 clasps); served throughout the Indian mutiny of 1857-58; present at the operations before, and at the siege and capture of Delhi; commanded the Bengal engineers at siege and capture of Lucknow (medal with 2 clasps); served during the Umbeyla campaign, 1863; C.B. in 1858, K.C.B. in 1877, and G.C.B. in 1889.

TAYLOR, FRANKLIN EDEN, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1884; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1886; and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Aug., 1891; acted as under-secretary to government in 1891 and 1893, and was confirmed in that post, June, 1893.

TAYLOR, FREDERICK BEATSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Christ's Hospital, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived, 30th Nov., 1874; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1886; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891.

TAYLOR, GEORGE NOBLE, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, in 1840; served in the grades of assistant and head assistant collector and magistrate, and was appointed secretary to the college and university boards, and assistant collector of customs at Madras in special charge of the salt department, 1849; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Rajamundry, 1851; acted as collector and magistrate of Nellore in 1857, and of South Arcot in 1858; inam commissioner, 1858; special commissioner for railways, Calcutta, to report upon the affairs of the East Indian railway, May, 1863, and subsequently held a similar post at Bombay; provisional member of governor-general's council, 1864; ordinary member of council, March, 1865; retired, March, 1870.

TAYLOR, HERBERT DOUGLAS, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Winchester, and St. Peter's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1885; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1886; manager of Jeypore estate, Feb., 1890.

TAYLOR, JAMES, late Financial Department, Government of India.—Joined the financial

department, 1st Sept., 1865, and served in Bengal as assistant to the accountant-general, and deputy accountant-general; assistant comptroller-general, June, 1879; retired, March, 1893.

TAYLOR, SAM ARTHUR, Store Department, India Office.—Educated at Epsom College; junior clerk, India office, 31st Jan., 1867; senior clerk, Jan., 1890.

TEAGUE, GEORGE FLETCHER, Funds Department, India Office.—Senior book-keeper, funds department, India office, 28th Sept., 1866; head book-keeper, March, 1873; designation changed to senior clerk, Oct., 1892.

TEMPLE, HENRY MARTINDALE, Major, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—Sub-lieutenant, 41st regiment, June, 1872; arrived in India, Sept., 1872; extra aide-de-camp to lieutenant-governor, Bengal, Dec., 1875; junior attaché, foreign department, government of India, March, 1876; senior attaché, June, 1877; cantonment magistrate, Nasirabad, Feb., 1878; assistant to resident, Hyderabad, and assistant general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti, Oct., 1880; officiating judicial superintendent of railways, Nizam's dominions, June, 1881; assistant to governor-general's agent, Baroda, Nov., 1882; boundary settlement officer, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1884; political agent, Kalat, May, 1885; political assistant, 1st class, March, 1886; political agent, South Eastern Baluchistan, May, 1887; and at Kalat, April, 1888.

TEMPLE, JAMES HERBERT, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Burnley Grammar School, Repton School, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed member of the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 20th Dec., 1889, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; acted as collector of customs, Calcutta, Oct.-Dec., 1893; officiating under secretary in the general, &c., departments, April, 1894, and in the financial and municipal departments from May, 1894.

TEMPLE, JOHN ALEXANDER, Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, Dec., 1860; arrived in India, 8th Aug., 1861; from Feb., 1864, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner, and acted at various times as cantonment magistrate, Saugor, Jubbulpore, and Kamptee; small cause court judge, Jubbulpore, May, 1872, to 1874, and again, 1877; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1884; officiating sessions judge, Jubbulpore, Nov., 1893.

TEMPLE, SIR RICHARD, Bart., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., D.C.L. (Oxon.), LL.D. (Cantab.), M.P., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1846; served in the N.W. Provinces and Punjab in the grades of assistant commissioner and assistant magistrate and collector; settlement officer, trans-outlej division, 1852; secretary to chief commissioner, Punjab, 1854; commissioner, Lahore, 1859; chief commissioner of currency and chief assistant to financial member of council, 1860; chief commissioner, Central Provinces, 1865; resident, Hyderabad, 1867; financial member of governor-general's council, April, 1868; president of statistical committee, 1868; lieutenant governor of Bengal, April, 1874; governor of Bombay, April, 1877, to March, 1880; retired from the civil service in 1877; chairman of the board of visitors, royal Indian engineering

College, Cooper's Hill, 1880; in Nov., 1885, and again in July, 1886, was returned as M.P. for Evesham, and in 1892 for Kingston; member of the London school board from 1884 to 1894, and vice-chairman, 1885-88; author of 'India in 1880,' 'Men and Events of my Time in India,' 1882, 'Oriental Experience,' 1883, 'Cosmopolitan Essays,' 1886, 'James Thomason,' 1893, 'Life in Parliament,' 1893, &c.

TEMPLE, RICHARD CARNAC, C.I.E., Major, I.S.C.—First commission, 4th Jan., 1871; from 17th Aug., 1879, served in the Punjab as assistant cantonment magistrate, and cantonment magistrate, and was promoted to 1st class magistrate, Oct., 1882; transferred to Burma as cantonment magistrate, Mandalay, March, 1887; assistant commissioner, April, 1887; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1888; provisional superintendent of census, 1890; president of the Rangoon municipality, Feb., 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; officiating chief commissioner, Andamans and Nicobars, July, 1894; author of 'Notes on the Transliteration of the Burmese Alphabet,' 'A Dissertation on the Proper Names of Punjabis,' 'Wide awake Stories' (Punjabi and Cashmiri Folk Tales), and other works; also founded and conducted 'Indian Notes and Queries,' and is an editor of the 'Indian Antiquary.'

TENNANT, JOHN FRANCIS, Lieutenant-General, C.I.E., R.E.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1847; arrived in India, 7th March, 1849; assistant engineer, Ganges canal, 1849 to 1851; assistant in the great trigonometrical survey of India, 1851 to 1859; was garrison engineer to the Delhi field force, and served at the siege and assault of Delhi, and at the siege and capture of Lucknow (medal with two clasps, and brevet of major); government astronomer at Madras, Oct., 1859, to Oct., 1860; commander, sappers and miners, Oct., 1860, to Sept., 1863; served in the public works department, Sept., 1863, to July, 1871, as executive engineer in Burma, and in the Punjab and Bengal; while on leave was appointed to observe eclipse of the sun, 1867-68, and was deputed for a similar duty in 1871; served as mint master, Calcutta, from July, 1871, to Jan., 1884; on special duty in England, Feb.-April, 1872; superintended the observations of the transit of Venus at Roorkee and Lahore, 1876; C.I.E., Jan., 1879; retired from military service and civil duties on 10th Jan., 1884; is a fellow of the Royal Society, and of the Royal Astronomical Society.

TEUNON, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at University of Aberdeen, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 24th Dec., 1883; assistant magistrate and collector, Jan., 1884; transferred to Assam as assistant commissioner, Jan., 1888; deputy commissioner, July, 1892.

THIBAUT, GEORGE FREDERICK WILLIAM, Ph. D., Education Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the department, 17th March, 1875, as professor, Benares college; inspector of schools, Jan., 1878; principal, Benares college, Nov., 1879; professor of English literature, Muir central college, Oct., 1888; also superintendent of Sanskrit studies in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Jan., 1893; has translated the Jatapatala, the Vedanta Entras, with San-karacharya's commentary, &c., &c.

THOM, WILLIAM, Surgeon-General, F.R.C.S., late Bombay Medical Service.—Educated at Marischal College, and University of Aberdeen, and at University College, London, and in Paris; F.R.C.S., England; fellow of University of Bombay; assistant surgeon on the Bombay establishment, 1841; served under the military department, first with the artillery, and then with the 3rd regiment native infantry, the 2nd Baluch battalion, the Bombay fusiliers, and the 17th native infantry at Baroda; surgeon, 1854; served subsequently at Belgaum and Poona; in medical charge, marine battalion, Bombay, May, 1860; garrison surgeon, and surgeon to the European general hospital, 1861; served on the medical board of the India office, 1866-67; deputy inspector-general of hospitals, March, 1867; surgeon-general, Feb., 1874; retired, Sept., 1877.

THOMAS, GEORGE EBENEZER, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Burma.—Appointed from the East Indian railway as assistant engineer, 28th Aug., 1867, and posted to the Central Provinces; transferred to the Punjab Northern state railway, March, 1868; locomotive superintendent, Punjab Northern state railway, April, 1875; executive engineer, April, 1876; transferred to Indus Valley state railway, Nov., 1878; 2nd class, revenue establishment, July, 1879; transferred to British Burma, Nov., 1881; class 1, grade 3, revenue scale, Jan., 1887; retired, 1889.

THOMAS, HENRY SULLIVAN, F.L.S., F.Z.S., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1855; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, sub-collector, and joint magistrate, acting collector of sea customs, member of the board of examiners, and acting civil and sessions judge; from May, 1867, to Sept., 1874, served as collector and magistrate of South Canara, and subsequently at Tanjore, and as political agent for French settlement at Karikal; from April, 1878, served on the board of revenue, Madras, and acted as secretary to government in the revenue department in 1878-79; in charge of the inam department, Jan.-April, 1879; president, board of examiners, Oct., 1881; on special duty as a member of the local self-government committee, Sept., 1882; on duty to Calcutta as an additional member of the governor-general's council for laws and regulations, Dec., 1882, and again, Dec., 1883; on special duty in connection with the pearl fisheries at Tuticorin, and deputed to study system of management in force in Ceylon, March-May, 1884; 1st member, board of revenue, July, 1884; on deputation to Tanjore district to report on necessity for granting remissions, April, 1885; fellow of Madras University, March, 1886; on special duty to Madura, March to Sept., 1886, and to Delhi, to attend conference on agricultural and veterinary questions, March, 1888; on special duty drawing fisheries bill, April, 1888; retired, Jan., 1889; has published 'A Report on Pisciculture in South Canara,' 1870; 'The Rod in India,' 1881; and 'A Report on Pearl Fisheries and Chank Fisheries,' 1884.

THOMAS, ROBERT MOSELY BRYCE, Colonel, I.S.C., Central Provinces Commission.—First commission as ensign, 6th Jan., 1859; arrived in India, 26th Feb., 1859; from Oct., 1864, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, acting deputy commissioner, and acting

small cause court judge, and was appointed cantonment magistrate, Jubbulpore, Nov., 1876, and subsequently at Kamptee; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Dec., 1885; officiating inspector-general of police and jails, April, 1892; acting commissioner, Chhattisgarh, July, 1893; inspector-general of police and jails, April, 1894.

THOMAS, WILLIAM CHARSLEY, Lieut.-Colonel, late Police Department, North-Western Provinces.—Joined the service as officiating adjutant of military police, Mainpuri, Dec., 1858, having previously served as a volunteer in General Havelock's cavalry, 1857-58; adjutant of military police, Oct., 1859; inspector of district police, Jan., 1861; district superintendent of police, May, 1861; officiated as assistant inspector-general of the government railway police, July-Oct., 1868; on special duty with the camp of the lieutenant-governor, Dec., 1876, and again in March, 1878, with the governor-general's camp; 1st grade superintendent, Lucknow, April, 1885; junior deputy inspector-general of police, May, 1886; senior, Jan., 1890; retired, March, 1893.

THOMASON, CHARLES SIMEON, Major-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Department, India.—Lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1852; joined the public works department, July, 1855, as deputy superintendent, Ganges canal; during the Mutiny served at the battle of Budlekeserai and the siege of Delhi (medal and clasp); executive engineer, Oct., 1858; superintendent, Rohilkund canals, Dec., 1864; officiating superintending engineer and deputy secretary, public works department, N.W. Provinces, Sept., 1873; on special duty to Madras, in connection with harbour work, 1877-79; superintending engineer and secretary to governor-general's agent, Central India, Aug., 1880; superintending engineer, 1st class, March, 1882; reverted to military department, May, 1888.

THOMPSON, ARTHUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at St. Cuthbert's, Ushaw, and University of London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived, 20th Nov., 1876, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting principal assistant, assistant sessions judge, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, and district and sessions judge; head assistant collector, Cuddapah, Nov., 1884; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, N. Malabar, June, 1891.

THOMPSON, CHARLES THOMAS, Registry Department, India Office.—Educated at the City of London School; clerk, 2nd class, India office, 19th March, 1883.

THOMPSON, DANIEL ROBERT, Surgeon-Major, C.I.E., M.D., M.R.C.S., and L.M., late Madras Medical Service.—Acting civil surgeon, Tellicherry, April, 1865, to Sept., 1866; joined the Indian medical service as assistant surgeon, 1st April, 1867; arrived in India, 6th Nov., 1867; and served under the military department, also acting as zillah surgeon and superintendent of jail at Chingleput, April, 1868; and at Tinnevelly, April, 1869, to Sept., 1870; in medical charge of garrison, lock hospital, and meteorological observatories, Bellary, Dec., 1873; resident surgeon, general hospital, Madras, and professor of pathology and materia medica in

medical college, March, 1875; from May, 1875, served as surgeon, 1st district, Madras, and medical inspector of emigrants; also superintendent of auxiliary provincial medical school, and native infirmary, Royapooram; C.I.E. (for special famine services in 1876-78), Jan., 1879; surgeon major, April, 1879; retired, May, 1888.

THOMPSON, DAVID WILLIAM, late Education Department, Punjab.—Served in the education department from March, 1862, as master in the zillah schools at Lahore and Amritsar; inspector of schools, May, 1870; 3rd grade of the department, Nov., 1883; retired, Dec., 1892.

THOMPSON, FREDERICK C., Public Works Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, accounts branch, 9th March, 1872; public works department, Aug., 1875; editor of India Office List, 1885-87; senior clerk, 1887.

THOMPSON, GEORGE, B.A., Educational Department, Central Provinces.—Head master, Saugor high school, Sept., 1869, to Oct., 1875; served from Oct., 1875, as inspector of schools, Central Provinces; was deputed to attend educational conferences at Naini Tal in 1885 and 1886; officiating inspector-general of education, May to Nov., 1889, and again from Oct., 1893.

THOMPSON, HENRY EDWARD, M.I.E.E., late Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as Morse assistant, 12th May, 1857; arrived in India, 12th Sept., 1857; superintendent, Dec., 1866; held charge of various divisions, including Malabar, Bangalore, Arakan, Nagpur, and Rajputana; retired, Dec., 1892.

THOMPSON, HENRY MUNGO, B.A., M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, July, 1866, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, April, 1871; acted as superintending engineer in Sind in 1884 and 1885, and from Nov., 1890; confirmed, June, 1892.

THOMPSON, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at St. Peter's School, York, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

THOMPSON, RICHARD HORATIO ELY, Forest Department, Central Provinces.—Joined the service as overseer of the government tea plantation, Gurhwal, Oct., 1855; assistant superintendent, Kumaon forests, Jan., 1860; assistant conservator of forests, April, 1864; transferred to Central Provinces, Aug., 1871; deputy conservator, Oct., 1871; on special duty in 1880 and 1885; services lent to government of Ceylon, June, 1887; conservator, 3rd grade, April, 1892; 2nd grade, April, 1894.

THOMPSON, ROSS, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—First commission, June, 1858; arrived in India, 14th Jan., 1861; joined the public works department, Aug., 1863; executive engineer, April, 1867; district engineer, Presidency, April, 1875; consulting architect to government, May, 1875; commanded Queen's Own sappers and miners throughout the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); rejoined public works department, Nov., 1880; acted as deputy chief engineer and under secretary to government, Oct., 1881; posted to West Coast division, Oct., 1884; Presidency division, April, 1886; Nilgiri division, July, 1890; Trichinopoly

division, Nov., 1890; reverted to military department, Aug., 1894.

THOMPSON, THOMAS MOORE LANE, India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 1st Aug., 1872, and posted to Bengal; executive engineer, Sept., 1886.

THOMSON, ALEXANDER, late Education Department, N.W. Provinces.—Served in Oudh, Aug., 1866, to April, 1877, as inspector of schools, and acted as director of public instruction in 1869 and 1868; from April, 1877, served as professor of English literature in the Agra college, and acted for various periods as principal; confirmed, July, 1883; retired, April, 1892.

THOMSON, CAMPBELL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 15th July, 1869, and posted to Bengal; transferred to state railways, Sept., 1872; executive engineer, Jan., 1880; services lent to Meywar durbar, Jan., 1885.

THOMSON, GEORGE, Surgeon-Colonel, M.B., late Medical Department, Central Provinces.—Arrived in India, 7th April, 1866; served in military department to July, 1879; services placed at the disposal of the Punjab government, and appointed officiating civil surgeon, 2nd class, Aug., 1879; confirmed in this appointment, Sept., 1880; medical adviser to H.H. the Maharaja of Patiala, in 1882, 1888 and 1890; administrative medical officer and sanitary commissioner, Central Provinces, April, 1893; reverted to military department, June, 1893.

THOMSON, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Aberdeen Grammar School, and University of Aberdeen; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1871, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; sub-collector, joint magistrate and principal assistant to collector, magistrate and agent, Godaveri, June, 1885; collector and magistrate, Nov., 1889.

THOMSON, JOHN BROWN, M.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at the Academy, and University, Edinburgh; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating magistrate and collector, officiating small cause court judge, and officiating cantonment magistrate; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, Feb., 1882; deputy superintendent of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, Nov., 1882; registrar of the high court, N.W. Provinces, May, 1884; magistrate and collector, April, 1889; officiated as secretary to government in 1889 and 1890; inspector-general of police, May, 1894.

THOMSON, ROBERT GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Islington Proprietary School, and University of London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1872; arrived in India, 13th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner and settlement officer; junior secretary to financial commissioner, Dec., 1882; officiating under secretary (revenue department) to government, Punjab, June, 1883; officiating junior secretary to government, Jan., 1884, and again in 1885 and 1886; senior secretary to

financial commissioner, March, 1886; on special duty revising rules under the Punjab land revenue bill; postmaster-general, Punjab, Feb., 1888; junior secretary to government of Punjab, and officiating revenue secretary, April, 1890; confirmed, April, 1892.

THOMSON, SAMUEL JOHN, Surgeon-Major, Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Joined the service as surgeon, 1st Oct., 1877; arrived in India, 27th April, 1878, and went through Afghan campaign, 1880 (medal); served on military, general, and jail duty, and as officiating deputy sanitary commissioner, and was confirmed in that office, May, 1885; officiated as sanitary commissioner in 1887, 1888, and 1892; author of 'Sanitary Principles.'

THOMSON, WILLIAM BOYD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at the Belfast Methodist College, Trinity College, Dublin, and the Royal University of Ireland; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 16th Dec., 1892, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

THORBURN, SEPTIMUS SMET, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, officiating divisional judge, and settlement officer, and officiating commissioner and superintendent; judicial assistant, 1st grade, Jan., 1881; deputy commissioner, April, 1883; officiating commissioner and superintendent, April, 1889; confirmed, Jan., 1892; author of 'Bannu, or Our Afghan Frontier,' 'David Leslie,' 'Asiatic Neighbours,' and a report on the settlement of the Bannu district.

THORBURN, WALTER MILLER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Dalhousie College, and University, Halifax, University of Edinburgh, and King's College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, and acting district and sessions judge; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890.

THORNHILL, EDMUND BENSLEY, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1854; arrived in India, 16th Aug., 1854; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating small cause court judge; from Feb. to Sept., 1865, served in Oudh as officiating deputy commissioner, Fyzabad; magistrate and collector, Banda, March, 1872; civil and sessions judge, Banda, Nov., 1877; retired in 1885.

THORNHILL, GEORGE, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Rugby and Haileybury, and appointed to the Madras civil service in 1842; arrived in Madras, 7th April, 1843; served as assistant to collector and magistrate in various districts, and was employed as civil officer in connection with the construction of the Godaveri and Kistna anicuts; principal assistant to collector and magistrate, Ganjam, 1856; collector and magistrate, Kistna, 1861; member of board of revenue, 1870-78, during which period he was frequently employed by

government on special duties of various kinds; was also appointed director of bank of Madras, and commissioner for uncovenanted civil service examinations, 1875; retired, 1878.

THORNHILL, HENRY BEAUFROY, Major, North-Western Provinces Administration.—1st commission, 23rd April, 1873; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1873; on field service, Afghanistan, Oct., 1878, to April, 1879 (medal); cantonment magistrate, Feb., 1887; appointed deputy superintendent, Port Blair, May, 1888; cantonment magistrate, N.W. Provinces, June, 1891.

THORNHILL, JOSEPH HELEN, M.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrigation works in N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Sept., 1879.

THORNTON, ARTHUR PARRY, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Ensign, 14th foot, 14th Sept., 1867; arrived in India, Jan., 1868; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, Sept., 1871; served under government of Bengal in Purneah and Burdwan as relief circle officer during famine, March-Dec., 1874; served under the foreign department of the government of India from Jan., 1875, and acted as assistant agent to governor-general, Rajputana, and as magistrate, Mount Abu, and assistant general superintendent for suppression of thuggee and dacoity; in charge of office of political agent, Marwar, Jan.-March, 1877; attaché to foreign office during Delhi durbar, 1876-77; on military duty, 1878-79; from March, 1879, served under foreign department as cantonment magistrate at Morar, and at Nasirabad; assistant to governor-general's agent, Rajputana, Jan., 1882; cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, Secunderabad, Feb., 1884; political agent, Bhopawur, May, 1886; Harowtee and Tonk, March, 1889.

THORNTON, EDWARD LABOUCHERE RUTHVEN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Marlborough, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 5th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; acted as manager, Jeypore estate, Aug.-Nov., 1893.

THORNTON, JAMES HOWARD, Surgeon-Colonel, C.B., B.A. and M.D., late Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 1856; from 9th Nov., 1861, served as civil assistant surgeon and civil surgeon, besides service under the military department; rank of surgeon-major, Jan., 1868, and brigade surgeon, March, 1882; principal medical officer with the Indian forces, Egypt, 1885; C.B., Aug., 1885; deputy surgeon-general, Punjab frontier force, June, 1886; retired, Aug., 1891.

THORNTON, LESLIE MELVILLE, M.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Marlborough, and University College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1878; arrived in India, 1st Jan., 1881, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Oct., 1883; under secretary to government, Oct., 1886; officiated as financial secretary, Feb., 1891, and district and sessions judge, April, 1891; joint magistrate, Aug., 1891; officiating legal remembrancer,

and member of, and secretary to, legislative council, Dec., 1892, to March, 1893; officiating deputy secretary to government of India, home department, Sept., 1893; confirmed, Oct., 1894.

THORNTON, THOMAS HENRY, C.S.I., D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Merchant Taylors School, and St. John's College, Oxford (1st class, moderations; Pusey and Ellerton Hebrew scholar; 2nd class, final schools); appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1855; assistant commissioner, Punjab, 1857-59; personal assistant to financial commissioner, Punjab, 1859-60; personal assistant to judicial commissioner, 1860-62; small cause court judge, Lahore, 1862-63; deputy commissioner, Delhi, 1863-64; secretary to the government of the Punjab, 1864-78, and was on two occasions temporarily in charge of the administration of the province; acted as foreign secretary to the government of India, 1876-77; additional member of governor-general's council, 1877-79; judge, chief court, Punjab, 1878-81; retired, 1881; fellow of University of Calcutta, 1875; vice-president, University College, Lahore, 1879.

THUILLIER, SIR HENRY EDWARD LANDOR, General, Kt., C.S.I., R.A., F.R.S., late Survey of India.—Educated at Addiscombe, 1831-32; first commission, Bengal artillery, Dec., 1832; arrived in India, 19th Oct., 1833; served in the survey department, from Dec., 1836, and conducted the surveys of Jynteah, the Pooree division of Cuttack, and the district of Cachar, and part of Sylhet; while on furlough to England, 1844-46, was orderly officer of Warley barracks for a year; deputy surveyor-general of India, Jan., 1847, to March, 1861, when he was appointed surveyor-general of India; retired, Dec., 1877; lieutenant-colonel, 1861; C.S.I., May, 1870; knighted in 1879; major-general in 1870; lieutenant-general, July, 1879; general, July, 1881; colonel commandant, R.A., Jan., 1883; joint author of the 'Manual of Survey for India.'

THUILLIER, SIR HENRY RAVENSHAW, K.C.I.E., Colonel, R.E., Survey of India.—Entered Addiscombe, Aug., 1855; lieutenant, Bengal engineers, June, 1857; arrived in India, 29th April, 1859; appointed to the survey department as assistant in the great trigonometrical survey, May, 1859; deputy superintendent, Sept., 1869; officiating superintendent, great trigonometrical survey, April-Oct., 1876; 1st grade deputy superintendent, Oct., 1878; deputy surveyor-general in charge of the revenue survey branch, Oct., 1884; acting surveyor-general, Feb., 1886; confirmed, Feb., 1887; C.I.E., June, 1893; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

THURBURN, JAMES WHITE, Major, R.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 7th May, 1872, and employed in the military works branch in the Punjab; served in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); reverted to home establishment, Nov., 1880; re-appointed, Jan., 1884, and posted to the Punjab; executive engineer, Feb., 1884.

TIDY, WILLIAM MEYMOTT, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1860; served in the N.W. Provinces from Aug., 1862, as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint

magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, Sept., 1878; retired, 1886.

TINNEY, GEORGE FREDERICK, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th July, 1882.

TODD-NAYLOR, HENRY PAUL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Shrewsbury, and University College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1882; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1882; transferred to Burma as assistant commissioner, March, 1886; officiating cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, April, 1886; deputy commissioner, May, 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1890.

TODHUNTER, CHARLES GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Aldenham Grammar School and King's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM PERCIVAL, C.I.E., Major-General, R.E., Military Works Department, Government of India.—First commission, June, 1860; joined the public works department, June, 1863, as assistant engineer, Assam; transferred to British Burma, Jan., 1865; acted as assistant to chief engineer and assistant secretary to chief commissioner, Jan.-Aug., 1865; executive engineer, Oct., 1866; transferred to military works branch, Nov., 1873; assistant secretary to government of India, civil and military works branch, April, 1880; superintending engineer and secretary to agent to governor-general, Baluchistan, 1882; superintending engineer, Aug., 1884; took part in Zohob valley expedition, Sept., 1884; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; chief engineer, Sept., 1890; director-general of military works, April, 1893.

TOMLINSON, THOMAS ARMSTRONG, Madras Revenue Survey Department.—Appointed 10th Aug., 1859, and served as assistant superintendent; deputy superintendent, June, 1886; on special duty, Pudukota boundary, 1887, and in 1888.

TOOGOOD, JOHN HENRY, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engineering College; appointed, 11th Oct., 1871, and employed on irrigation works in Bengal; on famine relief duty in Madras, 1877-78; executive engineer, Nov., 1881; employed in Madras, 1888-89; officiating under secretary to government of Bengal, public works department, April, 1894.

TOTTENHAM, LOFTUS RICHARD, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed from Haileybury College; arrived, 11th July, 1856; served as assistant, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and magistrate and collector; also as superintendent of stamps and stationery, Jan., 1866, and registrar of the high court, Dec., 1866; from Jan., 1874, served in the judicial department as district and sessions judge, and officiated as superintendent and remembrancer of legal affairs, Feb., 1878; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 16th July, 1879; retired, March, 1893.

TOWERS, ROBERT MASON, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863; assistant magistrate and collector, Aug., 1863; from July, 1867, served in the judicial

department as small cause court judge and district and sessions judge; judge of the principal courts of small causes in Nuddea and Jessore, Feb., 1879; from Jan., 1878, to Feb., 1880, served as officiating judicial commissioner, Chota Nagpur; served subsequently as district and sessions judge, and was promoted to 1st grade, May, 1885; retired, 1888.

TOWLE, ARTHUR HENRY, Store Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 12th Dec., 1892.

TOWNSEND, STEPHEN CHAPMAN, Surgeon-General, C.B., late Bengal Medical Service.—Educated at the Grammar School, Ottery St. Mary, Devon, and the London Hospital Medical School; assistant-surgeon in the Indian medical service, June, 1852; arrived in India, 26th Sept., 1852; employed on regimental and other military duty to Feb., 1862, and served in the Burma campaign, 1852-53; civil surgeon, Nagpur, Jan., 1865, to March, 1868; sanitary commissioner, Central Provinces, March, 1868, to Oct., 1878, and officiated as sanitary commissioner with the government of India, Feb., 1877, to March, 1878; rank of deputy surgeon-general, Dec., 1878; principal medical officer with the Kuram field force under General Sir F. Roberts, April, 1879; and was wounded in action, Sept., 1879; surgeon-general with the Punjab government, April, 1880; C.B., Feb., 1881; retired, Dec., 1883.

TOYNBEE, GEORGE, Indian C.S.
