(Bengal).—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1864; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1865; assistant magistrate and collector, Aug., 1866; superintendent of canal revenue, Oct., 1871; on famine relief duty, Nov., 1873; officiating secretary, board of revenue, lower provinces, Dec., 1874; second inspector of registration offices, April, 1877; on special duty connected with compulsory irrigation rate, May, 1877; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1877; special duty at the Bengal secretariat, July, 1878; magistrate and collector, Feb., 1884; commissioner, Cuttack division, May, 1891; author of 'Sketch of the History of Orissa from 1803 to 1828,' 'Sketch of the Administration of the Hugli District from 1795 to 1845,' and 'A Chowkidari Manual.'

TOZER, HENRY JOHN, M.A., Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 19th Jan., 1882; B.A. (honours), London university, 1889; M.A. (phil. & pol. econ.), 1892.

TRACY, THOMAS BURROWES, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1857; arrived in India, 29th Dec., 1857; served in the N.W. Provinces from Nov., 1858, as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Muttra, April, 1877; from Feb., 1883, served as civil and sessions judge, and acted as judicial commissioner in Oudh, 1884-85; retired, Sept., 1887.

TRAFFORD, RICHARD WYLME, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Rugby; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating cantonment magistrate; judicial assistant, April, 1879; district judge, 1st

grade, Nov., 1884; divisional judge, 3rd grade, Dec., 1886, and 2nd grade, Nov., 1889; retired, Oct., 1891.

TREASURE, WILLIAM HOUTON, Legal Adviser's Department, India Office.—Clerk to the legal adviser to the secretary of state, June, 1877; solicitor of the supreme court, 1887; assistant to the solicitor, Aug., 1892.

TREMENHEERE, CHARLES WILLIAM, Lieut.-General, C.B., R.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Educated at Adiscombe; 2nd lieutenant, Bombay engineers, 11th Dec., 1829; served as civil engineer in Dharwar, 1833-40, and as executive engineer, Surat and Broach, and acting superintending engineer, Guzerat, 1843-46; assistant executive engineer, Azen, Jan., 1846, to Feb., 1847; from Dec., 1848, to March, 1850, served in Scinde as executive engineer, Kurrachee, and acting superintending engineer; dockyard, Bombay, March, 1850, to May, 1853; acting secretary, military board, May, 1853, to Jan., 1854; on special duty, Bombay, designing reclamations, docks, and fortifications, Jan., 1854, to Jan., 1855; major, Sept., 1855; deputy consulting engineer, railway department, Jan., 1855, to Aug., 1856; garrison engineer, Bombay, and acted as superintending engineer, Poonah circle, 1856-57; was commanding engineer to the forces under Sir Hugh Rose, and General Roberts, 1857-58, and was present at the sieges of Awah and Kotah, and the engagements of Sanganeer and Kotarea; Indian mutiny medal and clasp for Central India; military C.B., March, 1861; superintending engineer, southern provinces, Feb., 1860; chief engineer, Scinde, May, 1862; chief engineer, presidency, Nov., 1866; major-general, March, 1867; political resident, Aден, with command of the brigade, Dec., 1870; retired with honorary rank of lieutenant-general, July, 1874.

TREMENHEERE, JAMES HENRY APPERLEY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Lancing and Cheltenham Colleges; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting assistant commissioner (Nilgiris); from May to Aug., 1883, served in Mysore as acting civil and sessions judge; served subsequently in Madras as head assistant collector, and acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and was appointed acting assistant secretary to government, judicial and legislative departments, June, 1884; officiating private secretary to the governor of Madras, May to Aug., 1885; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Aug., 1888; collector and magistrate, Nilgiris, July, 1890; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1890; collector and magistrate, Kistna, Jan., 1894.

TREMLETT, JAMES DYER, M.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1860, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and deputy commissioner; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Hissar, Aug., 1878; additional commissioner, Umballa and Jullundur, Nov., 1880; promoted to deputy commissioner, 1st class, Sept., 1881; commissioner and superintendent, Delhi, April, 1883; additional judge, chief court, April, 1884;

divisional judge, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; judge, chief court, Punjab, Sept., 1886; retired, March, 1889.

TRETHEWY, ANTHONY WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Winchester, and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885; served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, 2nd grade, March, 1890; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, March, 1893; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Nov., 1893.

TREVELYAN, ERNEST JOHN, B.L., Calcutta High Court.—Advocate, high court, Calcutta, 1875-85; officiating secretary, board of examiners for pleaders, &c., 1875-82; officiating magistrate, Calcutta, N. division, 1876; judge, high court of judicature, Calcutta, 12th Jan., 1885; also president, board of examiners for pleaders and mootkhars, 1886.

TREVOR, ARTHUR CHARLES, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Lincoln College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived 28th Oct., 1861, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting collector of stamp revenue and political agent, acting collector of Bombay and superintendent of stamps and stationery, acting municipal commissioner, and deputy commissioner of customs and opium (June, 1877); collector of customs, Bombay, April, 1879; acting commissioner of customs, and collector of salt revenue, May, 1884, and July, 1888; senior collector, Dec., 1884; collector of salt revenue, Oct., 1885; 2nd grade commissioner and commissioner of customs, salt, opium, and abkari, and reporter-general of external commerce, Sept., 1888; also British delegate for the Portuguese treaty; 1st grade commissioner, and acting commissioner in Sind, May, 1889; confirmed in the latter post, Nov., 1890; member of Bombay council, Nov., 1892; C.S.I., May, 1894.

TREVOR, CHARLES BINNY, late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service in 1830; arrived in India, 12th June, 1832; served in Bengal, in the grades of assistant commissioner, assistant collector and magistrate, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate of Backergunge, June, 1840; superintendent and remembrancer of legal affairs, 1849; judge of sudder dewanny and nizamat adawlut, Presidency, 1859; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1863; retired in 1867.

TREVOR, EDWARD WALTER, Colonel, I.S.C., late Sind Commission.—1st commission, April, 1855; arrived in India, 8th May, 1855; appointed acting superintendent of bazaars and cantonment magistrate, Neemuch, March, 1864; from Nov., 1864, served in Sind, as deputy collector and magistrate, acting district superintendent of police, and acting collector and magistrate; on military duty, Oct., 1879, to Jan., 1881, as general transport officer, Khyber line, and director, Kuram transport; deputy commissioner, Upper Sind frontier, Jan., 1881; on special duty to Calcutta, in connection with the prosecution of commissariat frauds, Nov., 1881; collector and magistrate, Hyderabad, Dec., 1884; at various periods acted as commissioner in Sind; reverted to military department, April, 1893.

TREVOR, FREDERICK GEORGE BRUNTON, Director of Funds, India Office.—Educated at St. Peter's School, York, and Marlborough College; East India house writer, old establishment, 10th March, 1858; junior clerk, public, educational, and ecclesiastical departments, Aug., 1860; senior clerk, July, 1875; judicial and public department, 1877; assistant secretary in that department, June, 1887; director of funds, Feb., 1894.

TREVOR, GEORGE HERBERT, C.S.I., Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—Joined the Madras artillery, 5th April, 1858; from June, 1862, served in the Central Provinces as inspector of schools, and was secretary to the Jabalpur exhibition committee in 1866; subsequently acted as cantonment magistrate, assistant secretary to chief commissioner, and assistant commissioner; from Oct., 1867, acted as assistant resident, Hyderabad; secretary for Berar, April, 1877; deputed to accompany Sir Salar Jung on his tour to Europe in 1867 as political officer; political agent, 1st class, July, 1881; officiated as resident at Hyderabad, May-June, 1882; officiating resident (2nd class) and commissioner, Ajmere-Merwara, Nov., 1885, and confirmed in those appointments, Nov., 1887; agent to governor-general, Rajputana, and chief commissioner of Ajmere-Merwara, March, 1890; author of 'Rhymes of Rajputana.'

TREVOR, JOHN SALUSBURY, C.S.I., Major-General, late R.E.—Passed out of Addiscombe, June, 1847; 2nd lieutenant, Bombay engineers, April, 1848; assistant superintendent of roads and tanks, 1849-55; transferred to railway department in Aug., 1855, as deputy consulting engineer; municipal commissioner, Bombay, 1861; resigned appointment in railway department, 1864; rejoined the public works department in Nov., 1866, as executive engineer, 1st grade, Tapti irrigation; consulting engineer and under secretary to government of Bombay, railway branch, Nov., 1868; chief engineer, 3rd class, Sept., 1870; acting deputy secretary to government of India in railway branch, 1871-72; resigned appointment in Bombay railway department, 1874; in England, 1874-78; officiating deputy secretary to government of India, railway branch, June, 1878; resigned the army, 1879, continuing in the public works department; director-general of railways, and deputy secretary, railway branch, 1880; retired in 1881; chairman of the Bombay, Baroda, and Central India, and the Rohilkund and Kumaon railway companies, and a director in several other Indian railway companies.

TREVOR, SALUSBURY THOMAS, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Bengal.—1st commission, Dec., 1852; arrived in India, 2nd Nov., 1854; from Jan., 1855, to Oct., 1863, served in British Burma as assistant engineer, executive engineer, assistant to chief engineer, and assistant secretary to chief commissioner in the public works department; transferred to Bengal, Oct., 1863; served as garrison engineer, Fort William, and superintendent engineer, 3rd class, and was appointed secretary to chief commissioner, Assam, public works department, July, 1869; chief engineer, and joint secretary public works department, Bengal, March, 1881; on deputation in connection with International Exhibition buildings at Calcutta, July, 1883; chief engineer and secre-

tary to government of Bengal, public works department, railways and roads and buildings, Jan., 1884; chief engineer, 1st class, July, 1885; retired, March, 1889.

TREVOR, WILLIAM SPOTTISWOODE, Major-General, V.C., R.E., late Public Works Department, India.—2nd lieutenant, Bengal engineers, Dec., 1849; arrived in India, 5th Feb., 1852; served in the Burmese war, 1852-53 (medal with clasps); employed on the Pegu survey, 1854; executive engineer, Bassein, Nov., 1855; was subsequently posted to Darjeeling and Dinagepur; garrison engineer, Fort William, 1861; controller and examiner of accounts, Bengal, May, 1863; served in the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (medal and V.C.); superintending engineer, Calcutta, Jan., 1867; consulting engineer to government of Bengal, irrigation, Jan., 1868; superintending engineer, military works, Calcutta, 1872; additional chief engineer for famine relief works, Bengal, 1874; officiating chief engineer and secretary to governor-general's agent, Indore, April-Dec., 1875; officiating chief engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, Feb., 1876; chief engineer and secretary, British Burma, Dec., 1878; director-general of railways and deputy secretary to government of India, March, 1881; secretary to government of India in the public works department, Feb., 1882; retired, Feb., 1887.

TROWARD, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Victoria College, Jersey; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 9th Oct., 1869, and served in the Punjab as assistant cantonment magistrate, assistant commissioner, officiating judicial assistant, and officiating deputy commissioner; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1887; divisional judge, May, 1889.

TRUEMAN, THOMAS, Colonel, I.S.C., late Cantonment Magistrate, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1856, and served on military duty up to June, 1872, including the Mutiny campaign under Sir Hugh Rose (medal and clasp), and subsequently as assistant cantonment magistrate at Poona and Kirkee, and cantonment magistrate, and city magistrate, Karachi; appointed cantonment magistrate, Ahmedabad, April, 1877, and subsequently at Belgaum; on famine duty, Sholapur, Sept., 1877; retired, Nov., 1888.

TUCKER, ALEXANDER LAUZUN PENDOCK, Indian C.S.—Educated at Winchester, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1880; arrived in India, 28th Dec., 1882; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1883; forest settlement office, Bijapur, Feb., 1886; services placed at disposal of government of India, foreign department, April, 1888; assistant to resident at Hyderabad, and assistant secretary for Berar; on deputation to Khettri state, 1888-89; assistant to the governor-general's agent, Central India, April, 1890; in 1891 acted as under secretary to government of India, foreign department; 1st assistant to resident, Hyderabad, and secretary for Berar, Oct., 1893.

TUCKER, HENRY ST. GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at University College, London; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1865, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating

deputy commissioner, and settlement officer; judicial assistant, 1st grade, Dec., 1883; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, Nov., 1884; appointed commissioner in Burma, May, 1886; retired, Sept., 1890; author of reports on the settlement of the Dera Ismail Khan and Kohat districts.

TUCKER, HENRY PENDOCK ST. GEORGE, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, in 1842; arrived in India, 14th April, 1843; served in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, and assistant judge and sessions judge in various districts in the Bombay presidency, and acted as deputy registrar of the Sudder court in Bombay, in 1845-46, and as judge and sessions judge, in 1853, and in 1856-57; acting first assistant accountant-general, Bombay, July, 1858; acting civil auditor, Bombay, Dec., 1858, to March, 1859; collector and magistrate, North Canara, July, 1862; judge and sessions judge, Konkan, Aug., 1862; appointed to report on the measures necessary for a redistribution of judicial jurisdiction and modification of judicial establishments, Feb., 1863; officiating judge of the high court, Bombay, June, 1863; member of council, Bombay, 7th April, 1869; retired in 1876.

TUCKER, LOUIS HENRY EMILLE, C.I.E., Colonel, Bengal Infantry, Police Department, Punjab.—First commission, 1860; arrived in India, 11th April, 1861; from Sept., 1864, served in the police department, Punjab, as assistant superintendent and district superintendent; on special duty with Major Cavagnari in connection with Kabul war, Nov., 1878, and again with the staff of Major-General Bright, Sept., 1879; 1st grade, district superintendent, Sept., 1880; officiating deputy inspector-general, 2nd grade, March, 1885; confirmed in appointment, March, 1888; served under the government of India, home department, from Sept., 1888, as chief commissioner, Port Blair; reappointed deputy inspector-general of police, Punjab, May, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; officiating inspector-general, Jan., 1894.

TUDBALL, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Bedford Modern School and Christ Church, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 12th Nov., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, March, 1892.

TUDOR-OWEN, EDWARD, B.A., Accountant-General's Department, India Office.—Educated at Christ Church, Oxford; appointed junior clerk 18th June, 1894.

TUPP, ALFRED COTTERELL, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at University College School, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1862; from Oct., 1863, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and was placed on special duty for the conduct of the case of the munsif of Mahoba, Dec., 1867; and again, July, 1869, for the preparation of a statistical memoir of Futehpur; assistant superintendent of Dehra Dun, April, 1871; also subordinate judge from March, 1873; held charge of provincial gazetteer of N.W. Provinces, March, 1876, to Feb., 1877; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Azimgarh, Aug., 1877; transferred to the financial depart-

ment, government of India, Nov., 1878, and served in Madras, till 1881, as deputy accountant-general, deputy controller-general, and accountant-general, and commissioner of state paper currency; officiated as accountant-general in Bombay, Dec., 1881, to Dec., 1882, and in Bengal from the latter date; officiating comptroller-general, Sept.-Oct., 1883; from Nov., 1883, served as accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, and deputy commissioner of paper currency; accountant-general, class I., Nov., 1887; accountant-general of Bombay and commissioner of the department of state paper currency, Bombay, Dec., 1887; accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Nov., 1888; retired, Oct., 1889; author of 'The Indian Civil Service and the Competition System,' 1875; 'The Statistics of the North-West Provinces of India,' 1876; The Imperial Gazetteer of India—N.W.P. portion, 1877; 'The Indian Civil Service List, and Record of Services,' 1880; 'The Paris International Monetary Conference of 1881, and Bimetallism,' 1881; 'The Competitive Civil Service of India, from 1856 to 1882,' 1882.

TUPPER, CHARLES LEWIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Harrow, and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, assistant settlement officer, and officiating under secretary to government, and was placed on special duty in office of financial commissioner, Sept., 1874, and with H.H. the Maharaja of Patiala, Nov., 1875; appointed under secretary to government of Punjab, April, 1877; officiating under secretary to government of India, department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, Sept., 1878; officiating secretary to government, Punjab, Jan., 1880; from Feb., 1880, served under the government of India as acting under secretary and acting secretary in the revenue and agricultural department, and was placed on special duty, Sept., 1881 (in connection with the Bengal rent bill), and again, Feb., 1882; junior secretary to government, Punjab, March, 1882; on special duty in connection with scheme of local self-government, June-Oct., 1883; acted as secretary to government, April, 1884, to Feb., 1886, and was on special duty in connection with the frontier crime commission, Feb.-March, 1886; officiating secretary to government, Punjab, Dec., 1887; confirmed, Nov., 1888; chief secretary to government, March, 1890; author of 'Customary Law of the Punjab,' vols. I-III, and of 'Our Indian Protectorate.'

TURNBULL, PETER STEPHENSON, Surgeon-Major-General, M.D., Bombay Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1860, and was employed under the military department until April, 1864, when he was appointed superintendent of vaccination, central circle; served in the Abyssinian campaign, 1867-68 (medal); examiner of medical accounts, Feb., 1876; acted at various periods as superintendent-general of vaccination, deputy sanitary commissioner, and inspector-general of prisons; secretary to surgeon-general, Bombay, Aug., 1882; deputy surgeon-general, Sind, Feb., 1888; surgeon-general with the government of Bombay, Feb., 1893.

TURNER, SIR CHARLES ARTHUR, K.C.I.E., Council of India.—Educated at Exeter College, Oxford (fellow, 1855, B.A., 1856, M.A., 1859); called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1858, and went the western circuit; appointed judge of the high court of judicature, N.W. Provinces, and arrived in India, May, 1866; acted as chief justice in 1870, 1875, and 1878; chief justice of the high court of judicature, Madras, Jan., 1879; fellow of the University of Madras, Jan., 1879; member of the Indian law commission, Bengal, Feb.-Sept., 1879; vice-chancellor of Madras University, April, 1880, and May, 1882; retired, 1st July, 1885; appointed member of the public service commission, 1885-86; member of the council of India, 2nd Feb., 1888.

TURNER, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Shrewsbury, and University College, London; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived, 20th Nov., 1864; served in various districts of Madras from May, 1866, as assistant collector and magistrate, and was employed on state famine relief operations, Jan., 1877, to March, 1878; sub-collector and joint magistrate, April, 1882; collector and magistrate, Cuddapah, Jan., 1885; district and sessions judge, Cuddapah, Feb., 1885; collector and magistrate, Madura, Jan., 1886; acting collector of sea customs, &c., Madras, May, 1894.

TURNER, HENRY GRIBBLE, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; assistant collector and magistrate, April, 1866; from Aug., 1866, to Dec., 1867, served in the police department as assistant superintendent and district superintendent; on special duty, Madras, inquiring into condition of poorer classes, Jan., 1868; acting under secretary to government of Madras, revenue department, March, 1875; postmaster-general, Madras, March, 1875, to June, 1881; from June, 1881, acted as collector, magistrate, and political agent, Vizagapatam; appointed district and sessions judge, North Arcot, June, 1884, but continued to act as collector, &c., at Vizagapatam, and was confirmed in this post, Nov., 1885; retired, July, 1890.

TURNER, LAWRENCE HERBERT, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at City of London School, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner, and assistant magistrate and collector; officiating under secretary to government, May, 1894.

TURNER, SAMUEL COMPTON, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Military Works Department, Government of India.—1st commission, 17th April, 1866; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1868, and joined the public works department as assistant engineer in Bombay, Jan., 1869; executive engineer, Feb., 1873; from Dec., 1873, served under the government of India; took part in Mahsud-Waziri expedition, 1881; officiated as assistant secretary, military department, in 1886 and 1887; superintending engineer, March, 1890.

TUSON, FRANCIS EDWARD, Port Blair Administration.—Joined the service, 25th Dec., 1868, as extra assistant superintendent, Port

Blair; assistant superintendent, Dec., 1884; 1st assistant superintendent, April, 1891.

TUTE, ARTHUR CLIFFORD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at St. Peter's School, York, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 28th Oct., 1871; assistant magistrate and collector, in Bengal, April, 1872; on famine relief duty, Salem, March, 1877, and at Trichinopoly, Sept., 1877; joint magistrate and deputy collector, July, 1884; magistrate and collector, March, 1888.

TWEEDIE, JOHN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived, 13th Dec., 1860; assistant magistrate and collector, July, 1861; officiating superintendent, Chittagong hill tracts, June, 1863; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1864; officiating postmaster-general, Bengal, Aug., 1870, to March, 1873; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Oct., 1867; from June, 1874, served in the judicial department, and was appointed judge of the small cause courts, Kishnaghur, Ranaghat, and Meherpore, Oct., 1876; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1877.

TWEEDIE, MAURICE, Colonel, I.S.C., Police Department, N.W. Provinces.—Arrived in India as ensign, 23rd Oct., 1856; during the Mutiny served with the field force in Central India, and subsequently with the mounted police in Oudh, and on the Nepal frontier (medal and clasp); from June, 1860, served as district superintendent, and was promoted to 1st grade, May, 1886; officiating deputy inspector-general of police, April, 1888; junior deputy inspector-general, Jan., 1890; senior deputy, March, 1893; author of 'A Chapter on the Fauna of the Province of Oudh,' for incorporation in the Oudh Gazetteer.

TWEEDIE, WILLIAM, C.S.I., Colonel, I.S.C.—First commission, Bengal infantry, Jan., 1857; during the Mutiny served in Oudh, and was present at the siege and capture of Lucknow, and afterwards took part in the pursuit of Tantia Topee in Central India (medal and clasps); adjutant, 1st cavalry, Hyderabad Contingent, March, 1859; acted as assistant to resident, Hyderabad, from 1863; served in the Abyssinian expedition, 1868 (medal and brevet of major); 1st assistant resident, Hyderabad, and general superintendent, thagi and dakaiti department, Oct., 1868; agent to governor-general, Moorshedabad, 1876-77; political agent, Gwalior, 1878-79; during the Afghan war was senior political officer with Major-General Bright, during advance on Kabul, and was in charge of Jalalabad division, March, 1880 (medal, C.S.I.); officiating political agent, Western Rajputana states, 1880-81; acted subsequently as resident in Western Rajputana, in Gwalior, and Turkish Arabia; officiating commissioner, Ajmere, March-June, 1885; officiating resident in Turkish Arabia, July, 1885; confirmed, March, 1886; reverted to military duty, Oct., 1891; author of 'The Arabian Horse,' 1894.

TWEEDY, GEORGE ALFRED, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at King's College School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1879, and served in

the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and cantonment magistrate; joint magistrate, Nov., 1887; assistant commissioner, March, 1890; superintendent, Dehra Dun, April, 1891; deputy commissioner, April, 1894; has also officiated as magistrate and collector, and district and sessions judge.

TWEMLOW, EDWARD D'OYLEY, Colonel, R.E., Military Works Department, Government of India.—Joined Indian establishment, 9th Dec., 1859; appointed assistant engineer, July, 1862; executive engineer, Dec., 1865; served in Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); transferred to military works department, Sept., 1887; superintendent engineer, west of India coast defences, Feb., 1888; transferred to Lahore and Sirhind command, April, 1892; and to Rawal Pindi, Nov., 1892; re-transferred to Lahore and Sirhind, Feb., 1893.

TWIGG, JOHN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Repton, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate and forest settlement officer; special assistant to collector and magistrate, Dec., 1887; head assistant collector, Dec., 1890; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Nov., 1891.

TWIGG, JOHN HILL, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Royal School, Dungannon, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1863; from May, 1864, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and was deputed on special duty under the commissioner of cotton and commerce for the organisation of carriage and supply of fodder for Bengal, Feb. to Sept., 1874; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, May, 1880; magistrate and collector, 2nd grade, Hamirpur, April, 1882; 1st grade, Aug., 1885; additional commissioner, Benares division, April, 1889; retired, July, 1890.

TWISADAY, CHARLES EDWARD JAMES, Indo-European Telegraph Department, India Office.—Appointed to the London office of the Indo-European telegraph department on the 20th July, 1869; accompanied the director-in-chief to Constantinople in 1880, as personal assistant, and to the Persian Gulf in 1885 with the third Persian Gulf cable expedition, and afterwards to India; transferred to India office, 1st Jan., 1889.

TWOMEY, DANIEL HAROLD RYAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at St. Stanislaus College, Tullamore, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 22nd Dec., 1884; assistant commissioner, British Burma, Dec., 1884; under secretary to chief commissioner, Jan., 1889; acted as secretary, July-Dec., 1890, and as junior secretary, Dec., 1890, to Feb., 1891; deputy commissioner, April, 1891; officiating junior secretary, March-July, 1892; officiating secretary, March-Sept., 1893.

TYLER, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, Kt., C.I.E., M.D., F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., L.M., L.S.A., Jail Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the service as officiating civil surgeon, Etah, 6th May, 1863; appointed

also civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Manipuri, March, 1866; superintendent, central and district jails, Meerut, April, 1873; superintendent, central and district jails, Agra, April, 1875; on special duty at the Calcutta exhibition, Nov., 1883, to Jan., 1884; officiated as inspector-general of prisons in 1884; deputed for duty in connection with the Colonial and Indian exhibition, March, 1886; C.I.E., June, 1886; on special duty in Dholepur state, Dec., 1886; deputed to accompany H.H. the Maharaja of Bhartpur to England for Her Majesty's Jubilee; Ke., 1888; inspector-general of prisons, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Sept., 1890.

TYRRELL, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1858; arrived in India, 12th May, 1859; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating under secretary to government, officiating small cause court judge, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; registrar, high court, N.W. Provinces, Aug., 1870; magistrate and collector, Jan., 1878; civil and sessions judge, 2nd grade, Jan., 1882; puisne judge, high court, N.W. Provinces, Nov., 1882; retired, Nov., 1894.

UDNY, RICHARD, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at University of Aberdeen, and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1869, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, Feb., 1885; on special duty, March to April, 1885; and again from Aug. to Dec., 1888; officiating divisional judge, Dec., 1890; officiating commissioner and superintendent, Peshawar, Feb., 1891; C.S.I., Jan., 1894.

UMESH CHUNDER BATABYAL, M.A., Bengal Provincial Service.—Master in the Sanskrit College, Calcutta, Aug., 1873; deputy magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1880; appointed member of the statutory civil service, May, 1883; assistant magistrate and collector, June, 1883; deputy magistrate and deputy collector, in provincial service, May, 1893.

UNDERWOOD, ORMSBY CHARLES HENRY, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Blairlodge School, Trinity College, Oxford, and University College, London; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

UNDERWOOD, WALTER GREGORY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Rossall School, and Chester College; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived, 17th Sept., 1870, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; on special duty to the Laccadives, Nov., 1881; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Tanjore, Dec., 1885; acting district and sessions judge, June, 1886; collector and magistrate, Jan., 1889.

UNWIN, ARTHUR HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at King's School, Sherborne, and Tonbridge Grammar School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served as assistant collector and

magistrate; from Feb., 1873, served in British Burma as assistant commissioner, inspector of schools, acting judge of the small cause court, and acting director of public instruction; from Jan., 1880, served in the Bombay Presidency as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge, and acting judge and sessions judge; 1st grade assistant judge, Oct., 1883; assistant judge and joint sessions judge, for Kaira and Panch Mahals, May, 1885; 3rd grade judge and sessions judge, May, 1887; 2nd grade, May, 1893.

UPCOTT, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service, Dec., 1868, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, Nov., 1874; specially thanked for services in connection with the erection of the Jhelum bridge on Sindh-Sagar state railway, June, 1887; superintendent of way and works, Punjab section, North Western railway, Dec., 1887; superintending engineer, Dec., 1890; engineer-in-chief, Bengal-Assam railway, northern section, Nov., 1891; consulting engineer for railways, and joint secretary to government, railway branch, Madras, Feb., 1893; rank of chief engineer, April, 1894.

UPTON, ROBERT LEYCESTER.—Government solicitor, high court of judicature, Calcutta, from 4th March, 1881.

UPTON, SAMUEL RICHARD, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 3rd Feb., 1876; clerk specially attached, Sept., 1892.

VAN GEYZEL, JOHN LAWRENCE, Surgeon-Major, M.B., Madras Medical Department.—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1880, and served under the military department until Jan., 1888, when he was appointed civil surgeon, Negapatam; chemical examiner and professor of chemistry, medical college, Feb., 1890.

VAN SOMEREN, GODLIEB JAMES, Colonel, late Forest Department, Government of India.—First commission, Madras infantry, Sept., 1859; appointed to the forest department, May, 1864; conservator of forests, Mysore and Coorg, May, 1869; transferred to Punjab, 1881; assistant comptroller-general of forest accounts under the financial department, Feb., 1883; conservator of forests, Berar, Jan., 1885; also director of the forest school from Aug., 1889; retired, Feb., 1892.

VARADA RAO, TUMALAPALLI, B.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Madras University; probationer for the civil service, July, 1885; assistant to collector and magistrate, Dec., 1886; confirmed in civil service, 17th Aug., 1887.

VAUGHAN, ERNEST LLEWELLYN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Blackheath School, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived, 10th Jan., 1888, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; acting assistant accountant-general, Madras, May, 1892, to July, 1893.

VAUX, ALFRED ROBERT BRICKWOOD, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 29th June, 1874; clerk specially attached, Sept., 1892.

VENGAL VENUGOPAL CHETTI, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Madras Christian College, Madras University, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of

1888; arrived, 21st Nov., 1890, and served as assistant collector and magistrate.

VENKATRAO RUKMANGAD INAMDAR. B.A., LL.B., Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Served in the subordinate judicial department from 27th Feb., 1880; appointed assistant collector in Bombay presidency under native civil service rules, 9th April, 1889; acting assistant judge and sessions judge, from same date.

VENNING, FREDERICK, late Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at University College, London; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1863; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1864; served from Aug., 1865, to Nov., 1868, in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; from Nov., 1868, served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner, and acted as small cause court judge of Jubbulpore in 1874, and as secretary to the chief commissioner in 1878; inspector-general of police and prisons, Central Provinces, June, 1880; commissioner of the Jubbulpur division, Aug., 1881, and of the Nagpur division, Nov., 1883; acting judicial commissioner, June, 1889, Nov., 1890, and Sept., 1891; reverted to Nagpur appointment, Nov., 1891; transferred to Jubbulpur, Feb., 1893; retired, April, 1894.

VENTRIS, HENRY, Revenue and Statistics Department, India Office. —Educated at Highgate School, and Brighton College; supernumerary writer, 2nd Feb., 1860; temporary clerk, Dec., 1860; clerk in registry department, Feb., 1864; junior clerk, revenue department, Jan., 1873; employed in records branch, 1879 to 1885; senior clerk, June, 1888.

VERNER, WILLIAM HENRY, late Bengal C.S. —Educated at Cheltenham College, and Victoria College, Jersey; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1861; arrived 2nd Dec., 1863; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed officiating assistant secretary, board of revenue, Feb., 1867, joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Nov., 1877, and district and sessions judge, Nov., 1879; also served as inspector of schools, April, 1866, and officiated as under secretary, government of India, home department, Feb., 1871, and as postmaster-general, Bengal, in 1871, 1872, and 1875; district and sessions judge, 1st grade, Oct., 1885; retired, Dec., 1888.

VERTUE, WILLIAM, Colonel, I.S.C., late Central Provinces Commission.—First commission, June, 1857; from 2nd Dec., 1862, to May, 1865, served in the police department of the Central Provinces as acting district superintendent, and personal assistant to the inspector-general; from May, 1865, served as assistant commissioner, and acted as inspector of schools, and superintendent of the school of industry at Jubbulpur; cantonment magistrate, Saugor, Jan., 1877; small cause court judge, Nagpur, Jan., 1882; deputy commissioner, Sept., 1883; reverted to military department, Nov., 1894.

VIBART, HENRY MEREDITH, Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—First commission as lieutenant, Dec., 1857; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1859; assistant engineer in the Madras public works department, Sept., 1861; executive engineer, Dec., 1866; on special duty in Bangalore, Nov., 1877, to Feb., 1878; in special charge of the restoration of Tiroomal Naick's palace, Madura, Feb., 1878; acting

deputy chief engineer for irrigation, and under secretary to government, public works department, March, 1879; superintendent of works, Presidency, April, 1880; superintending engineer, April, 1884; 1st class, June, 1888; retired, Dec., 1891; author of 'The Military History of the Madras Engineers,' and 'The History of the East India Company's Military College at Addiscombe.'

VIBERT, ARTHUR LIONEL, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Victoria College, Jersey, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 8th Nov., 1893, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

VIDAL, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Eton, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 26th Oct., 1867, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, forest settlement officer, and acting collector and magistrate; on special duty connected with the Jath state, March, 1873; first assistant collector and magistrate, Dec., 1878; on special duty as a British member of the mixed commission at Goa, March-May, 1880, and Jan., 1885; president of the Bombay forest commission, Dec., 1885; junior collector and magistrate, Panch Mahals, and political agent, Rewa Kantha, Sept., 1886; collector of salt revenue, Sept., 1888; senior collector, March, 1890; acting chief secretary to government, Dec., 1890, to April, 1891, and again from March, 1893.

VINCENT, CLAUDE, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to Madras; officiating deputy chief engineer and under secretary to government, irrigation branch, Nov., 1879; assistant chief engineer and under secretary, March-Oct., 1880; executive engineer, May, 1883; acting private secretary to governor of Madras, March, 1889; under secretary to government of India, civil works branch, Dec., 1889; transferred to Central Provinces, 1892; acted as deputy secretary to government of India, public works department, in 1892 and 1893; services lent to government of Straits Settlements for Krian irrigation scheme, Jan., 1894; returned to Central Provinces, July, 1894.

VINCENT, HENRY ALEXANDER, Colonel, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, 9th Nov., 1866; president, council of regency, Rampur, Nov., 1893; political agent, Baghalkhand, and superintendent, Rewa state, April, 1894.

VINCENT, ROBERT WILLIAM EDWARD HAMPE, C.I.E., Bombay Police Department.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1869, and served as inspector, officiating deputy commissioner and acting superintendent of police; assistant superintendent, May, 1875; superintendent, March, 1876; on special duty in 1875 and 1886; 1st grade, May, 1892; acting commissioner of police, Bombay, April, 1893; confirmed, Sept., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1894.

VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY HOARE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Christ College, Brecon, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector in Bengal.

VOWELL, CHRISTOPHER HENRY, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; served in the revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1877; magistrate and collector, July, 1884; retired, March, 1893.

WACE, ALFRED AUGUSTUS, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Eton, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1868; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, Feb., 1880; also served as officiating inspector-general of registration, March, 1879; magistrate and collector, Nov., 1884; in 1891-92 served in Assam as acting judge and commissioner, Assam Valley districts; commissioner, Burdwan, April, 1894.

WADDINGTON, GEORGE, late Bombay C.S.—Appointed to the Bombay civil service from Haileybury College, 1857; arrived, 21st Dec., 1857, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; junior collector and magistrate, Kaladgi, Dec., 1878; senior collector, Aug., 1881; collector and magistrate, district registrar, Ahmednagar, Nov., 1886; retired, Nov., 1891.

WADE, EDWARD JOHN, M.A., India Office.—Educated at Rugby, and Trinity College, Dublin (scholar and exhibitor, first senior classical moderator and gold medalist); assistant librarian, India office, 23rd May, 1881; was for some time editor of 'Allen's Indian Mail.'

WAHAB, ROBERT ALEXANDER, Major, R.E., Survey of India Department.—First commission, 29th April, 1873; assistant superintendent, survey department, Jan., 1883; deputy superintendent, July, 1888; acting assistant surveyor-general, April-July, 1893; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80, in Mahsud Waziri expedition, 1881, Zhow Valley expedition, 1884, and with Hazara field force and Miranzai field force in 1888 and 1891.

WAKE, HERWALD CRAUFURD, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College in 1851; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate of Shahabad, 1858; C.B., May, 1860; magistrate and collector, Patna, 1861; superintendent, Darjeeling, 1862; retired on invalid pension, 1868.

WALCH, GEORGE TURNER, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 31st March, 1866; executive engineer, April, 1873; acted as deputy chief engineer, and under secretary to government, Feb., 1877; on special duty, Madras Irrigation Company's canal, July, 1882; had charge of Kurnool canal, and other works; superintendent engineer, March, 1887; acted as chief engineer for irrigation, and as joint secretary and afterwards as secretary to government in 1888, 1889 and 1890; chief engineer for irrigation, Sept., 1880; joint secretary, public works department, with charge of irrigation branch, July, 1890; officiated as secretary, April-July, 1891; retired, April, 1893; author of 'Notes on some of the Navigable Rivers and Canals of the United States and Canada,' 1876.

WALKER, ALEXANDER COULL, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Dulwich College, and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 30th Oct., 1893; and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector.

WALKER, FRANCIS BLENNERHASSETT, late Railway Branch, Government of India.—Appointed to the Bombay public works department, Sept., 1863; executive engineer, July, 1868; transferred to Punjab, Jan., 1870, and was employed on Punjab Northern state railway; served subsequently on the Neemuch and Western Rajputana state railways; transferred to Bombay railway branch, March, 1881, and subsequently to Bengal; engineer-in-chief, Northern Bengal and Tirhoot railway extensions, March, 1883; superintending engineer, 3rd class, Dec., 1885; 2nd class, May, 1889; engineer-in-chief, East coast railway survey, Nov., 1889; 1st class superintending engineer, Feb., 1890; chief engineer, 3rd class, Jan., 1892; consulting engineer for railways, Madras, Oct., 1892; retired, Feb., 1893.

WALKER, GEORGE CASSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Winchester College, and New College, Oxford; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner; served under the foreign department of the government of India, Feb.-Sept., 1880; on special duty, Lahore, in connection with viceregal durbar, Oct., 1880; from April, 1883, served in the financial department of the government of India, and was appointed assistant accountant-general, Punjab; transferred to office of accountant-general, Bengal, Dec., 1883; from Feb., 1884, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating district judge; served under the government of India from Sept. to Nov., 1886, as under-secretary, finance and commerce department; settlement officer, Lahore, Feb., 1888; also officiating director of land records and agriculture, May to July, 1892; officiating commissioner of excise, &c., Feb., 1894.

WALKER, GEORGE HERBERT DACRES, B.A., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed assistant secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, 1st May, 1874; served subsequently in accounts branch; officiating under secretary to government of India, public works department, Nov., 1882, and Nov., 1886, and was appointed permanently, May, 1887.

WALKER, GEORGE WARREN, General, R.E., late Public Works Department, Madras.—Second lieutenant, Madras engineers, 9th Dec., 1842; assistant civil engineer, 1847; district engineer, Canara, Sept., 1859; superintending engineer, Coimbatore, May, 1863; chief engineer, and secretary to government of Madras, public works department, May, 1872; retired from the public works department in 1876.

WALKER, HARRY BRUCE, Store Department, India Office.—Matriculated at London University, 1875; clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, 6th July, 1877; 2nd grade supervisor, July, 1888.

WALKER, JAMES, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at the University of Aber-

deen, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1883; arrived, 1st Dec., 1886, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; transferred to Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, Dec., 1890.

WALKER, JAMES THOMAS, General, R.E., C.B., LL.D., late Survey of India Department.—Appointed to the Bombay Engineers from Addiscombe, 9th Dec., 1844; assistant chief engineer, Bombay, May–Sept., 1845; executive engineer, Upper Sind, May, 1847, to Oct., 1848; served throughout the Punjab campaign, 1848–49, and was present at siege of Mooltan, and battle of Goojerat (medal and clasps); from 1849 to 1853 served as military surveyor of the Trans-Indus frontier from Peshawur to Dera Ismail Khan, and was present at numerous frontier engagements; from Dec., 1853, to March, 1861, served as assistant in the great trigonometrical survey; during the Mutiny of 1857 served with the Punjab movable column, and subsequently as field engineer at Delhi (brevet of major, medal and clasp); in 1860 accompanied the Mahsud Waziri expedition (medal and clasps for frontier service); from March, 1861, to Feb., 1883, served as superintendent of the great trigonometrical survey, and from Jan., 1878, to Feb., 1883, also held the appointments of surveyor-general of India, and superintendent of topographical surveys, and carried out the amalgamation of the three survey departments; fellow of the Royal Society, 1865; C.B., 1877; hon. LL.D. of Cambridge, 1885; superintended the preparation and publication of the first nine volumes of the account of operations of the great trigonometrical survey; author of occasional papers for the Royal Geographical Society and United Service Institution, presidential address to the geographical section, British Association, in 1885, and contributions to the Encyclopædia Britannica.

WALKER, JAMES WILLIAM, LL.B., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at King's College School, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1868, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, and acting judge and sessions judge; on special duty in connection with the Baroda commission, Feb.–March, 1875; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, Jan.–March, 1880; 2nd grade judge and sessions judge, Feb., 1885; 1st grade, Nov., 1887; retired, Nov., 1893.

WALKER, THOMAS GORDON, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at the Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen, and University of Aberdeen; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 4th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, and officiating deputy commissioner, and was settlement officer of Ludhiana from May, 1878, to 1884; attached to civil secretariat on special duty in April, 1883; officiating under secretary to government, revenue department, Punjab, April, 1883; officiating senior secretary to financial commissioner, Sept. to Dec., 1883; officiating registrar of the chief court, Punjab, Nov., 1884, and April, 1887; officiating junior secretary to government, Aug., 1886, to Feb., 1887; on special duty, Punjab civil

secretariat, Feb.–April, 1887; senior secretary to financial commissioner, Feb., 1888; commissioner of excise, and inspector-general of registration, &c., Nov., 1888.

WALKER, THORNTON, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Edinburgh University, and University College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived, 8th Dec., 1885, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, 2nd assistant, and officiating district and sessions judge.

WALKATE, HAROLD THORNTON, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India audit office, 31st Oct., 1892; transferred to record and registry department, India office, 9th Aug., 1894.

WALL, RICHARD, M.A., LL.D., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Queen's College, Cork; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1856; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1856; served in N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, Benares, Feb., 1877; officiating civil and sessions judge, May, 1877, and in 1858; commissioner of excise and stamps, and inspector-general of registration, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, Feb., 1882; retired, Oct., 1891; author of stamp, excise, and registration manuals.

WALLACE, ALFRED CHARLES, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872.

WALLACE, SIR DONALD MACKENZIE, M.A., LL.D., K.C.I.E.—Educated at the Universities of Edinburgh, Heidelberg, and Berlin, and the Ecole de droit, Paris; was 'Times' correspondent in Russia, Austria, and the East, 1878–84; private secretary to the Marquess of Dufferin and Ava, when governor-general of India, 1884–88; was member of mission to Cabul, 1888; appointed to accompany the Czarewitch during his visit to India, 1890–91; author of 'Russia,' 'Egypt and the Egyptian Question,' &c.

WALLACE, WILLIAM ARTHUR JAMES, C.I.E., Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, Punjab.—1st commission, Dec., 1860; arrived in India, April, 1864; assistant engineer, public works department, April, 1864; officiating deputy consulting engineer, railway branch, Bengal, Jan., 1869; assistant consulting engineer, Feb., 1870; executive engineer, 3rd grade, Sept., 1870; deputy consulting engineer for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, Oct., 1871; acted as consulting engineer, Lucknow, and at Calcutta; on special duty in connection with imperial assembly at Delhi, acting as under secretary to government of India, Oct., 1876, to Feb., 1877; on deputation to England in connection with Paris exhibition, 1878; secretary to railway conference at Calcutta, Oct., 1878; acting under secretary to government of India, railway branch, 1878–79; served in the Afghan war, 1879 (medal); engineer-in-chief, and manager, Northern Bengal state railway, Nov., 1879; 1st class state railway revenue establishment, Nov., 1879; served in the Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal and clasp, and bronze star, 4th class Osmanieh); officiating consulting engineer for guaranteed railways, Lahore, Oct., 1884; class 1, grade 1, revenue establishment, Oct.,

1885; director, North-Western railway, May, 1887; deputed to Switzerland to examine the Abt system of railways, July-Oct., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; reverted to military department, Oct., 1892.

WALLER, RICHARD MAUNSELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Marlborough College, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; assistant magistrate and collector, Jan., 1868; officiating deputy collector of customs, Calcutta, Feb., 1877; joint magistrate and deputy collector, April, 1878; deputy commissioner, April, 1879; magistrate and collector, Aug., 1885.

WALLIS, BERESFORD GAHAN, L.C.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 17th July, 1871, and posted to the Punjab; executive engineer, Oct., 1881.

WALPOLE, SIR HORACE GEORGE, K.C.B., Assistant Under Secretary of State, India Office.—Educated at Eton; temporary clerk, India office, 12th Dec., 1862; junior clerk, June, 1863; store department, 1863; accountant-general's department, 1863-64; public works department, 1864; resident clerk, 1870-77; senior clerk, April, 1877; private secretary to the permanent under secretary of state, 1866-74 (Mr. Herman Merivale, C.B.), and to the secretary of state, 1874-80 (the Marquis of Salisbury, 1874-78, and subsequently, Viscount Cranbrook); C.B., 1880; also served as clerk of the council from 1877, and was appointed assistant to the under secretaries of state in addition to his other duties, Sept., 1881; assistant under secretary of state, Nov., 1883, continuing to be clerk of the council; royal commissioner of the Colonial and Indian exhibition, 1886; secretary to the royal commission on civil establishments, 1886 to 1890; K.C.B., Aug., 1892.

WALSH, ERNEST HERBERT COOPER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Trent College and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1882; arrived in India, 11th Dec., 1884; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1884; joint magistrate and deputy collector, Nov., 1891.

WALTON, FREDERICK THOMAS GRANVILLE, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Railway Department, Government of India.—Appointed to department as engineer-in-chief, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, 1st Jan., 1889.

WAPSHARE, ARTHUR, Major, I.S.C., Bombay Judicial Department.—First commission, 28th Oct., 1871; served in the Soudan expedition, 1834-35 (medal, clasp and star); officiating cantonment magistrate, Karachi, June, 1887; appointed in similar capacity to Deesa, Sept., 1888.

WARBURTON, HARRIS GRANT, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Dover College and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competition of 1885; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1887, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner; from Oct., 1891, to Aug., 1893, was employed under the government of India as assistant accountant-general, Bengal, and as assistant comptroller general (paper currency).

WARBURTON, JOHN PAUL, Police Department, Punjab.—Joined the service, 28th May, 1864, as assistant district superintendent of

police; district superintendent, April, 1873; assistant inspector-general, railway police, June, 1894.

WARBURTON, ROBERT, C.S.I., Lieutenant-Colonel, I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 24th Oct., 1862; from Sept., 1869, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating cantonment magistrate, and officiating deputy commissioner; on special political duty in Khyber, July, 1879; employed with Cabul force, Oct., 1879; officiating judicial assistant commissioner, Peshawur, Nov., 1881; political officer, Khyber, Feb., 1882; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, but to continue political officer, Khyber, Nov., 1884; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; author of an account of the Boner and Amazai tribes, and a report on the district of Sughman.

WARBURTON, WILLIAM PLEACE, M.D., Surgeon-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the Indian medical department, 31st March, 1866; arrived in India, 11th Dec., 1866; civil surgeon, Jalandhar, April, 1868; acted as superintendent of central jail, Lahore, 1872-73; medical officer to H.H. the Maharaja of Kapurthala, June, 1874; from Oct., 1893, acted as principal medical officer and sanitary commissioner, Assam; and was confirmed in that post, Jan., 1894; officiating inspector-general of civil hospitals, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, March, 1894.

WARD, ALFRED WILLIAM, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, accountant-general's department, 2nd Aug., 1892; transferred to registry and record department, Dec., 1894.

WARD, GEORGE ERNEST, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Wadham College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from Sept., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, assistant superintendent of Dehra Dun, and joint magistrate and deputy collector; magistrate and collector, Jaunpur, July, 1879; on special duty on the education commission under the government of India, 1882; commissioner, Jhansi division, July, 1885; retired, July, 1889.

WARD, HENRY CONSTANTINE EVELYN, C.I.E., Colonel, I.S.C., late Central Provinces Commission.—Arrived in India, as ensign, April, 1855; was employed under the military department up to 1864, including service at the siege and assault of Delhi, 1857 (medal and clasp), and at the battle of Narnoul, Nov., 1857; served also with Eusufzai expedition, 1858, and in the campaign against the Mahsood Waziris, 1859-60 (frontier medal); assistant commissioner, Dec., 1864; deputy commissioner, May, 1870; acting inspector-general of registration, superintendent of stamps, and commissioner of excise, Central Provinces, Nov., 1873; additional commissioner for the administration of affairs in Kalahandi, June, 1882; acting commissioner, Chhattisgarh division, March-Nov., 1883, and again in 1884; on special duty as special commissioner for the repression of dacoity in Nerbudda division, Feb., 1886; from June, 1886, served under the government of India, as minister of the Bhopal state; commissioner, Nerbudda division, Aug., 1889; reverted to military department, 1892.

WARD, THEODORE METHUEN, Colonel, late Survey Department, Sind.—Joined the

service, 9th Dec., 1859; lieutenant, Bombay artillery, April, 1860; admitted to Bombay staff corps, Nov., 1864, and from that date served in the revenue survey department; lieutenant-colonel, Dec., 1885; superintendent, Sind revenue survey, June, 1889; reverted to military duty, 1890; retired, Dec., 1891.

WARD, WILLIAM ERSKINE, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1861, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; officiating under secretary to government of India, home department, Nov., 1865, to April, 1866, and again in 1867, and in department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, March, 1873; officiating magistrate of police, Calcutta, April, 1873; from Oct., 1873, served in judicial department as acting district and sessions judge, and was transferred to Assam, March, 1876, and served as judge and commissioner of Assam Valley districts; officiated temporarily as judicial commissioner of Central Provinces, Jan., 1883; fellow of University of Calcutta; officiating chief commissioner of Assam, July-Oct., 1883, and from Feb., 1885, to 1887; officiating judicial commissioner, British Burma, April, 1884; president of provincial committee for collecting exhibits of Assam for Calcutta exhibition, 1883; judicial commissioner, Burma, May, 1886, continuing to act as chief commissioner of Assam; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; took charge of office in Burma, March, 1889; chief commissioner of Assam, May, 1891.

WARD-SMITH, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed, 3rd Dec., 1872, and employed on irrigation works in N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, Dec., 1881.

WARDEN, AUGUSTUS BROOKE, late Bombay C.S.—Educated at the Kensington Grammar School, and Haileybury College; appointed to the Bombay civil service, 1842; served in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, and assistant judge and sessions judge, and was appointed judge and sessions judge of Ahmedabad, March, 1856; judge and sessions judge, and agent for governor, Surat, 1861; acting judge of the high court, May, 1863; judge and sessions judge of Poona, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, Sept., 1863; judge of the high court of judicature, Bombay, 1865; was also president of the departmental examinations committee for junior civilians for five years; retired, 1871.

WARDEN, CHARLES JAMES HISLOP, M.D., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, late Bengal Medical Service.—First commission, 31st March, 1874; from 18th March, 1876, acted as civil surgeon of Bhagulpore, with medical charge of the central jail; on special duty in the excise department, board of revenue, Dec., 1876; officiating principal assistant to the opium agent, Behar, April-Oct., 1878; officiating superintendent, central jail, Bhagulpore, Nov.-Dec., 1879; professor of chemistry, medical college, and chemical and opium examiner to government, Dec., 1879; member of opium committee, Oct., 1880; services placed at disposal of military department, April, 1892.

WARDEN, FREDERICK, Commander, late Royal Indian Marine.—Joined the Indian navy, 2nd March, 1852; from Dec., 1865, served as port-master of Canning, and, in addition (from Jan., 1867), as assistant collector of customs and ex-officio superintendent of customs; from Dec., 1866, acted as assistant master attendant, and agent for government consignments, Calcutta, and from March, 1870, as agent for transports; also government prosecutor for trial of pilots, May, 1873, to March, 1876; officiating deputy master attendant, Aug., 1875; officiated as port officer, March, 1876, to Aug., 1879, and performed duties of superintendent of dockyard from March, 1876, to Nov., 1877; assistant port officer, Aug., 1879; examiner in navigation, Nov., 1879; transferred to Rangoon as port officer, March, 1882; also superintendent of lighthouses, superintendent of mercantile marine, and transport agent; retired, Sept., 1890.

WARDEN, JOHN LODWICK, B.A. (Oxon.), Barrister-at-Law, late Judicial Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 8th March, 1852, and served in the Bombay covenanted civil service as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant judge and sessions judge, and deputy magistrate in charge of the Adalat, Hyderabad; resigned the covenanted civil service, May, 1858; appointed extra assistant secretary to government of Bombay, in the judicial department, Dec., 1866; assistant judge and sessions judge, April, 1867, to June, 1868; acting magistrate of police, July-Oct., 1868; from Jan., 1869, served as small cause court judge, and was appointed third judge, Bombay, Dec., 1874, and second judge, May, 1888; retired, 1889.

WARRE, SIR HENRY JAMES, General, K.C.B.—Obtained commission from Sandhurst, 1836; ensign, 54th foot, 3rd Feb., 1837; from 1839 served as aide-de-camp to commander of the forces in British North America (Sir R. Downes Jackson, K.C.B.); during 1845 and 1846 was employed by colonial office in exploring a route for transport of troops from Canada to the Red River settlement, by Ottawa, and in surveying and reporting on the various settlements in the Oregon territory, and on various islands on the coast of the Pacific; served in the Crimea from March, 1855, and succeeded to the command of the 57th regiment; present at the siege and fall of Sebastopol, the assaults on the Redan, and the surrender of Kinbourn (medal with clasp, C.B., 5th class of the Medjidie and Turkish medal); in 1858 was in command of a mixed force on the Taptsee River in co-operation with the Central India field force, and was subsequently appointed brigadier at Mhow; military secretary to the commander-in-chief in India (Lieutenant-General Sir Hugh Rose, K.C.B.), June, 1860; served in the Maori war in New Zealand, 1861, and acted for a short time as governor of the province of Auckland; was subsequently in command of the troops on the west coast, and in the province of Taranaki (under martial law), 1863-66 (medal); from 1870-74 was stationed in Ireland as brigadier-general; commander-in-chief, and member of governor's council, Bombay, Nov., 1878, to 1881.

WARRY, WILLIAM, M.A., Political Department, Burma.—Educated at Exeter College, Oxford; in 1878 passed the competitive examination for student interpreterships in China; was acting assistant Chinese secretary at Peking.

from Oct., 1831, to July, 1832; employed on special service under the government of India from Dec., 1835; received the Burma war medal for active service, 1887-88; appointed political officer on special duty in Burma, 10th Feb., 1887; adviser on Chinese affairs, May, 1892; author of a translation of the Peking Gazette, 1881-82.

WATERFIELD, SIR HENRY, K.C.S.I., C.B., Financial Department, India Office.—Educated at Westminster; junior clerk, 1st Sept., 1853; India board, financial and railway department, 1853; political and secret department, 1856; private secretary to president of the India board, 1858; India office, financial department, 1858; assistant private secretary and précis writer to the secretary of state, 1860; financial department, 1863; senior clerk, April, 1863; assistant secretary in the military department, 1867; public works department, 1871; secretary in the statistics and commerce department, 1874; secretary, financial department, 1879; C.B., Dec., 1885; secretary of the Indian currency committee, 1892-93; K.C.S.I., July, 1893; author of the first report on the moral and material progress of India, 1866, and of a memorandum on the census of British India of 1871-72, both of which were presented to Parliament.

WATERFIELD, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Westminster; appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury, 1852; arrived in India, 19th Nov., 1852; served in Bengal in the revenue and survey departments till 1859, when he was transferred to the financial department as 1st assistant to the accountant-general for India; acted as sub-treasurer, Madras, and as civil auditor and superintendent of stamps, Madras, Calcutta, and Bombay; from 1864 to 1877 served successively as civil pay-master, deputy accountant-general, and accountant-general, N.W. Provinces and Oudh; comptroller-general, 1877-80; retired, 1881.

WATERFIELD, WILLIAM GARROW, C.S.I., B.L., Colonel, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1852; from Sept., 1857, served in the Punjab as personal assistant to commissioner of Delhi, and assistant commissioner; small cause court judge, Sept., 1863, to Nov., 1865; assistant settlement officer, Gujarat, Nov., 1865; deputy commissioner, Dec., 1867; on special duty for reporting on the Gujarat settlement, July, 1868; commissioner and sessions judge, Peshawur, Feb., 1878; commissioner and superintendent, Sept., 1878; on special duty, Nov., 1878 (with the Kurum field force); served under the foreign department of the government of India, 1881-82; reverted to military department, Nov., 1888; author of a report on the settlement of the Gujarat district.

WATERHOUSE, JAMES, Colonel, I.S.C., Survey of India.—Lieutenant, Bengal artillery, June, 1859; arrived in India, 11th Oct., 1859; assistant revenue surveyor, Lower Provinces, June, 1866; assistant surveyor with superintendence of the photozincographic operations in the office of the surveyor-general of India, July, 1866; assistant superintendent of survey, 1st grade, June, 1870; on special duty with the Indian eclipse expedition, Nov., 1871, to Jan., 1872, and for observing the transit of Venus, Oct., 1874, to Jan., 1875; in charge of Indian eclipse expedition to Nicobar Islands, March, 1875; assistant surveyor-general, April, 1874;

deputy superintendent, and assistant surveyor-general, July, 1882; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892; in charge of photo-lithographic office, Calcutta, from Dec., 1886; author of a pamphlet on 'The Application of Photography to the Reproduction of Maps and Plans,' and other reports and handbooks on professional subjects.

WATERS, GEORGE, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Ed.), Medical Department, Bombay.—Joined the Bombay medical service, 1st Oct., 1869; arrived in India, 27th May, 1870, and served in the military department, and as acting ophthalmic surgeon, Bombay, and civil surgeon at Bushire (1871-75); on special duty under the government of India, department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, March-April, 1877; surgeon to governor of Bombay, May, 1877; from Oct., 1881, served as third physician and registrar, Jamsetji Jijibhoy hospital, curator of the pathological museum, and professor of pathology, Grant medical college; also acted as first physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoy hospital, and professor of medicine, clinical medicine, and hygiene; confirmed as first physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoy hospital, and professor of pathology, morbid anatomy, and hygiene, Grant medical college, Nov., 1888; presidency surgeon, 3rd district, Nov., 1889.

WATKINS, LAWRENCE AUGUSTUS, Judicial Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1863; served in the Bombay judicial department from Jan., 1865, as deputy registrar of the high court, clerk to the high court judges, and commissioner for taking affidavits; also acted as assistant commissioner for taking accounts, and assistant taxing master, Aug.-Oct., 1878.

WATSON, HUBERT DIGBY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Harrow, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1892; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner.

WATSON, SIR JOHN, General, K.C.B., V.C., I.S.C., late Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, Bombay infantry, 1848; served in Punjab campaign, 1848-49, Bozdar expedition, 1857, Mutiny, 1857-58, and Umbeyla expedition, 1863; from Sept., 1871, served as commandant, Central India horse, and political agent; resident, 2nd class, and agent to the governor-general at Gwalior. April, 1877, also acting as agent for Central India; brigadier-general commanding cavalry brigade, Malta expeditionary force, 1878; commanding Punjab chiefs' contingent in Afghanistan, 1878-79; in political charge of the district of Kuram, Nov., 1879, to Nov., 1880; resident, 1st class, and agent to the governor-general, Baroda, Jan., 1882; retired from the political department, March, 1886.

WATT, GEORGE, M.B., C.I.E., Revenue and Agricultural Department, Government of India.—Appointed, 11th July, 1873; arrived, 4th Nov., 1873; served in the 3rd class of the Bengal education department as professor, Presidency, Hooghly, and Patna colleges, and as officiating principal, Krishnagur college, and was placed on special duty in connection with the Burma-Manipur boundary expedition, Oct., 1881; services placed at disposal of executive committee of the Calcutta international exhibition, Feb., 1883; served under government of India, revenue

and agricultural department, from May, 1884; appointed to charge of imperial Indian economic court of Colonial and Indian exhibition; C.I.E., June, 1886; reporter on economic products, April, 1887; author of several scientific papers, and editor of the 'Dictionary of Economic Products.'

WATTS, GEORGE KEMPTHORNE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Hyderabad; officiated as assistant to superintending engineer and assistant secretary to resident in 1881, and was appointed to that post, Sept., 1886; executive engineer, Nov., 1881; temporarily transferred to Assam, Oct., 1891.

WAY, ROBERT ATWELL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; executive engineer, Oct., 1880; member of re-organization committee, Sept., 1883; services lent to Bengal and North-Western railway company, July, 1884; to Bengal-Nagpur company, Aug., 1887; and to Delhi-Umballa-Kalka company, Jan., 1889; employed on Chittagong-Akyab-Minhlra railway survey, Oct., 1893.

WAY, THEODORE ALBAN HENRY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Clifton College and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant commissioner.

WEBB, WILLIAM TREGO, M.A., late Educational Service, Bengal.—Joined the service, 6th April, 1875; professor, Dacca College, May, 1875; officiated as inspector of schools, Feb., 1877; professor, Presidency College, from Jan., 1878; officiating principal, Calcutta Madrasa, in addition to other duties, 1880, and again in 1887 and 1890; 3rd class, education department, Dec., 1880; retired, March, 1892; has published 'Hints on the Study of English,' 'Indian Lyrics,' and translations.

WEBSTER, EDMUND FORSTER, C.I.E., B.L., late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1857; served in the revenue and judicial departments, Madras, and was appointed small cause court judge, Madura, May, 1867, and afterwards at Cuddalore; district and sessions judge, Cuddapah, June, 1873, and at Trichinopoly, Aug., 1873; collector and magistrate and agent, Tanjore, April, 1878; on special duty in connection with the famine commission, Oct., 1878, and again, Aug., 1881, as secretary to the famine code committee; member of the forest draft bill committee, March, 1882; secretary to government, revenue department, June, 1882; member of the local self-government committee, June, 1882; additional member of council for making laws and regulations, July, 1883, and again, July, 1885, and March, 1886; chief secretary to government of Madras, Jan., 1884; fellow of Madras University, March, 1884; member of the committee in connection with the draft tenancy bill, July, 1885; retired, Oct., 1887; C.I.E., May, 1888.

WEBSTER, HENRY BINNY, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury College, 1854; arrived in India, 17th June, 1854; served in

the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and assistant settlement officer, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Saharanpur, Nov., 1867, and subsequently at Gorakhpur and Meerut; commissioner, Jhansi division, Jan., 1878; inspector-general of police, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April, 1880; retired, 1886.

WEBSTER, JOHN EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at the Charterhouse and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 26th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

WEDDERBURN, HARRY GEORGE, B.L., Judicial Department, Madras.—Reporter for the Indian law reports (Madras series), June, 1881; law reporter, March, 1888; administrator-general, temporary, Aug., 1888; confirmed, March, 1889; acting advocate-general, and additional member of legislative council of Fort St. George, 1891-92.
