WEDDERBURN, SIR WILLIAM, Bart., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; arrived, 25th Nov., 1860, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, acting judicial assistant to the commissioner in Sind, and small cause court judge; acting under secretary to government in the judicial and political departments, and secretary to the council for making laws and regulations, June, 1866; acting registrar-general of assurances, Oct., 1866; acting secretary to government in the judicial, political, educational, secret, and Persian departments, Dec., 1866, and again in 1870 and 1874; under secretary to government, judicial and political departments, May, 1867; acting registrar, high court, Jan., 1869; acting judicial commissioner in Sind and judge of the sadar court, May, 1874, to Feb., 1876; 2nd grade judge and sessions judge, June, 1875; on special duty in Bombay, Nov., 1876; 1st grade judge and sessions judge, Poona, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, May, 1882; on special duty under government of India, April-May, 1884; acting judge, high court, Bombay, Jan., 1885; acting chief secretary to government, Oct., 1886; retired, May, 1887.

WEEKES, ARTHUR, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Aug., 1885; on famine relief duty at Madras, Feb., 1877; retired, March, 1888.

WEIDEMANN, GEORGE LEVER, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Bedford Grammar School and Oriel College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1868; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1870, and served in the Burma commission as assistant commissioner and deputy commissioner; on special duty in Rangoon, July, 1879; officiated as additional sessions judge, 1890, and 1892, and as commissioner from March, 1893.

WEIR, CHARLES JAMES, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Royal Academical Institution, Belfast, and Queen's College, Belfast; appointed

to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, and assistant sessions judge; from May, 1884, served in Coorg as officiating first assistant commissioner and district magistrate; acting under secretary to government of Madras, revenue department, Oct., 1886; officiating under secretary to government of India, finance department, May, 1887, to April, 1888; officiating deputy accountant-general, Bengal, April, 1888; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Aug., 1888; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Jan., 1891; acting collector and magistrate in 1891 and 1892, and from March, 1893.

WEIR, JOHN WILLIAM ALLEN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Godolphin School, Hammersmith, and University College, London; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived, 12th Nov., 1884, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, administrator, Sachin state, and acting judge and sessions judge.

WEIR, PATRICK ALEXANDER, Surgeon-Major, M.B., Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the service in 1875; officiating and resident surgeon, presidency general hospital, Aug., 1876; on special duty with lieutenant-governor on tour in cyclone districts, Nov., 1876; superintendent of vaccination, and ex-officio deputy sanitary commissioner, March, 1882; principal assistant to opium agent, Benares, May, 1882; officiating residency surgeon, Nepal, May, 1891; officiating agency surgeon and administrative medical officer, Baluchistan, June, 1894.

WEIR, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Queen's College, Belfast; appointed to Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived, 8th Dec., 1867, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; deputy registrar, high court, Jan., 1870, to June, 1872; acted as registrar, high court, Feb., 1874, to Feb., 1880; Tamil translator to government, Jan., 1879; head assistant to the collector and magistrate, Godaveri, March, 1880; board of examiners, Jan., 1882; confirmed as registrar, high court, April, 1882; published the law of offences and criminal procedure, and the civil digest of rules and standing orders of the high court; from July, 1883, acted as district and sessions judge in the districts of Coimbatore, North Tanjore, and Madura, and was placed on special duty in connection with the reorganization of the subordinate judicial service, Nov., 1883; sub-secretary, board of revenue, Feb., 1886; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1887; officiating judge of high court, March, 1890, to Jan., 1891; author of 'Law of Offences and Criminal Procedure, as expounded by the High Court of Madras,' and of 'Civil Digests of Rules and Standing Orders of the Madras High Court.'

WEIR, THOMAS STEPHENSON, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, Bombay.—First commission, Indian medical department, 1st April, 1870; health officer, Bombay, from 1874.

WELD, MATTHEW RICHARD, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Dublin University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in various districts of Madras as assistant magistrate and collector,

acting cantonment magistrate, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; acted as district and sessions judge, Kistna, Jan.-March, 1885, and as collector and magistrate in the same district from April, 1885; appointed district and sessions judge, Ganjam, Sept., 1888; Kurnool, May, 1889; retired, Aug., 1893.

WELDON, THOMAS, C.I.E., Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Madras.—Joined Addiscombe, Jan., 1852; ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1853; arrived in India, 12th April, 1854; from July, 1860, served in the Madras police department as assistant superintendent and superintendent; senior magistrate of police for town of Madras, March, 1867; on special duty, in connection with the note forgery case, July, 1872, to Jan., 1873; chief magistrate of police, Madras, April, 1877; on special duty in Burma in connection with the commissariat case, Nov., 1879; chief presidency magistrate, Madras, Dec., 1880; board of examiners, Jan., 1882; commissioner of police for town of Madras, Dec., 1885; C.I.E., May, 1888; reverted to military department, Dec., 1891.

WELLS, HENRY LAKE, Lieut.-Colonel, R.E., M.I.E.E., Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Appointed to the public works department as assistant engineer, Feb., 1876; transferred to state railways, Dec., 1876; lent to military department, Dec., 1878, and served on special duty in Baluchistan, in opening up Thal Chotiali route, 1879; lent to telegraph department for surveys in connection with a telegraph line from Srinagar to Gilgit, Aug., 1879; again lent to military department for field service, 1880 (medal); transferred to Indo-European telegraph department as assistant director, Persian section, 1880; officiating director, May, 1885; confirmed, Jan., 1891; author of several reports on tours in Persia, &c.

WELLS, WALTER FOX WILLIAMSON, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Cheltenham; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 16th Nov., 1875, and served in Oudh as assistant commissioner, and officiating deputy commissioner; city magistrate, Lucknow, Oct., 1885; joint magistrate, July, 1889; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1891; district and sessions judge, May, 1894.

WELSH, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Haileybury; appointed after open competitive examination of 1874; arrived, 20th Nov., 1876, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting sub-collector, assistant sessions judge, &c.; head assistant to collector and magistrate, Jan., 1885; senior assistant to collector and magistrate, Dec., 1887; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Aug., 1889; collector and magistrate, Nov., 1891; district and sessions judge, Cuddapah, Jan., 1894.

WENLOCK (BARON), RIGHT HON. BEILBY LAWLEY, G.C.I.E.—Educated at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; M.P. for Chester. April to July, 1880; was vice-chairman of county council, East Riding; appointed governor of Madras, 20th Dec., 1890, and assumed charge of office, 23rd Jan., 1891.

WEST, SIR RAYMOND, K.C.I.E., M.A., B.L., LL.D., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Queen's College, Galway; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive

examination of 1855; arrived in India, 18th Sept., 1856, and served in the Bombay revenue and judicial departments as assistant collector and magistrate, assistant judge and sessions judge, officiating under secretary to government in the judicial and political departments, and acting judge and sessions judge; registrar, high court, Bombay, June, 1863; judicial commissioner and judge of the sadar court in Sind, June, 1872; judge of the high court, Bombay, Sept., 1873; on special duty as member of the law commission, Calcutta, April–Nov., 1879; vice-chancellor of the university of Bombay, Oct., 1886; member of council of governor of Bombay, 12th Nov., 1887; K.C.I.E., June, 1888; retired, April, 1892.

WESTERN, JAMES HALLIFAX, C.M.G., Lieutenant-Colonel, R.E., late Public Works Department, India.—First commission, Bengal engineers, 1860; arrived in India, Oct., 1862; assistant engineer, public works department, N.W. Provinces, Dec., 1863; Eastern Jumna canal, Jan., 1864; executive engineer, Ganges canal, June, 1868; superintendent of works, Betwa canal, Nov., 1881; transferred to Punjab as temporary superintending engineer, Sirhind canal, May, 1882; superintending engineer, 3rd class, Sept., 1882; officiating chief engineer and joint secretary for irrigation, Punjab, Nov., 1884; services lent to Egyptian government; retired, Jan., 1887.

WESTLAND, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I., Government of India.—Educated at Marischal College, Aberdeen, and Wimbledon School; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived in India, 10th Oct., 1862; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, March, 1867; officiating magistrate and collector, Jessore, Aug., 1868; officiating junior secretary, government of Bengal, July, 1869; officiating superintendent and remembrancer of legal affairs, June, 1870; under secretary, government of India, financial department, Dec., 1870; officiating accountant-general, Bengal, April, 1873; accountant-general, Central Provinces, and deputy commissioner, paper currency, Nagpore circle, Dec., 1873; on special duty in the home department, Nov., 1875; inspector of local offices of accounts, Nov., 1877; accountant-general, Bengal, Oct., 1878; acting comptroller-general and head commissioner, department of issue of state paper currency, April, 1880, and was confirmed in this appointment, Sept., 1881; officiating secretary, government of India, department of finance and commerce, Nov., 1883, to Jan., 1884, Nov., 1886, and again Nov., 1887; in England on special duty, April–Sept., 1884; member of finance committee, March, 1886; officiating member, governor-general's council, Aug., 1887; temporary member, Nov., 1887; C.S.I., June, 1888; secretary to government of India, department of finance and commerce, Nov., 1888; chief commissioner of Assam, July, 1889; retired, Oct., 1889; financial member of governor-general's council, 27th Nov., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895.

WESTMACCOTT, EDWARD VESEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Rugby, and Magdalen College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1861; arrived, 8th Jan., 1863; served in

the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was on famine relief duty, Jan., 1874; officiating commissioner, Dacca division, Aug., 1882; on special duty for the purpose of reporting on the existing chowkidary system, Nov., 1882; services placed temporarily at disposal of financial department, Oct., 1883; officiating inspector-general of jails, March, 1884; officiating commissioner, Burdwan division, in 1887, and again in 1888; commissioner of excise, Bengal, April, 1889; commissioner, Presidency division, March, 1893; acting member of revenue board, April, 1894.

WESTROPP, ALEXANDER SLIGO ANDERSON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Dover College, and Magdalene College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1891; arrived in India, 5th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

WESTROPP, GEORGE RALPH COLLIER, Major-General, I.S.C., late Political Department, Bombay.—First commission, Bombay infantry, Dec., 1850; arrived in India, 27th Jan., 1851; was employed under the military department, including service in Khandeish against the rebel leader Tantia Topee, 1858; adjutant, Savantvadi local corps, Feb., 1859; acting assistant magistrate in Ratnagiri, May, 1860; assistant to political superintendent, Savantvadi, Nov., 1862; assistant political agent, Kolhapur, 1865 to 1869; in charge Southern Mahratta jaghir states, 1869 to 1877; political superintendent, Savantvadi, and commandant of the local corps, Aug., 1877; deputed on special duty in the Savanur state in 1880; retired from civil employment, 1888.

WHALLEY, PAUL, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 31st Oct., 1861; from Nov., 1862, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, officiating junior secretary, and under secretary to government; officiating under secretary to government of India, department of revenue, agriculture, and commerce, Dec., 1871, to Feb., 1872; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, May, 1877; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, in Oudh, Dec., 1877; magistrate and collector, Pilibhit, Nov., 1879; 1st grade magistrate and collector, Oct., 1883; retired, Feb., 1890; author of 'A Collection of the Laws and Rules of the Non-Regulation Provinces.'

WHEELER, FRANK, Store Department, India Office.—Appointed supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

WHEELER, HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1889; arrived, 14th Dec., 1891, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector.

WHEELER, PERCY COTTERILL, B.L., late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 25th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; assistant settlement officer, Jaunpur, Nov., 1882; assistant commissioner, 2nd grade, April, 1887; joint magistrate, Nov., 1888; officiating magistrate and collector, July, 1890.

officiating district and sessions judge, March, 1891; retired, 1892.

WHISH, CHARLES WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Tonbridge School, and Blackheath Proprietary School; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate, and officiating magistrate and collector; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, Oct., 1886; joint magistrate, 1st grade, April, 1887; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, Feb., 1890; magistrate and collector, July, 1890; author of 'The District Officer in Northern India.'

WHISHAW, JOHN CHARLES, M.D., Surgeon-Major, late Medical Department, North-Western Provinces.—Assistant surgeon, Bengal medical establishment, 23rd July, 1858; arrived, 3rd Sept., 1858, and held various military medical charges up to 1860; civil surgeon and superintendent of jail, Fyzabad, April, 1860; superintendent, central prison and district jail, Lucknow, Nov., 1867; also civil surgeon, Feb., 1868; officiating sanitary commissioner, Oudh, Oct., 1870; also ex-officio inspector-general of prisons, March, 1871; civil surgeon, and superintendent of lunatic asylum, Lucknow, Oct., 1873; also in charge of meteorological observatory, Lucknow, Dec., 1877; retired, Feb., 1886.

WHITE, EDMUND, late Bengal C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1865; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector and officiating magistrate and collector; officiating cantonment magistrate of Bareilly, Feb., 1880; deputy superintendent of census operations, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April, 1880; also under secretary to government in the census department; on special duty in connection with census, Sept., 1882; joint magistrate, 1st grade, Nov., 1882; director of public instruction, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, June, 1885; member of lieutenant-governor's legislative council, Feb., 1880; director of public instruction, Oct., 1890; retired, Aug., 1892; author of 'Report on the Census, N.W. Provinces and Oudh,' 1881.

WHITE, SIR GEORGE STUART, G.C.I.E., K.C.B., V.C., General, Commander-in-Chief of the Forces in the East Indies.—First commission, 4th Nov., 1853; served in the Indian Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal), and Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and star, V.C., and C.B.); military secretary to the viceroy, 1880-81; took part in the Soudan expedition, 1884-85 (medal and star), and was assistant-adjutant and quarter-master-general in Egypt, Feb. to July, 1885; brigadier-general, Madras, Sept., 1885, and commanded the brigade detailed for service in Burma; from 1886 to 1889, commanded the Upper Burma field force, and was thanked for his services by the government of India, and promoted to K.C.B.; commanded Zhob field force, 1890; commander-in-chief in India, 8th April, 1893; extraordinary member of viceroy's council, 12th April, 1893.

WHITE, HENRY FREDERICK, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—

Trained at Cooper's Hill College; joined the service, 19th Dec., 1859; assistant engineer, Dec., 1860; 1st civil divisional engineer, April, 1865; executive engineer, Oct., 1866; charge of Rohilkund roads division; in charge of Mhow military works division, 1872, 1878, and 1883; officiating chief engineer and secretary to chief commissioner, Burma, July, 1885; officiating superintending engineer and secretary to resident, Hyderabad, Sept., 1887; superintending engineer, May, 1888; thanked for services in connection with construction of Savage Island and Oyster Island lighthouses in 1890 and 1892; superintending engineer and secretary to agents to the governor-general for Rajputana and Central India, public works department, June, 1892.

WHITE, HENRY RICHARD, B.A., Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th Dec., 1872; B.A., London university, 1882.

WHITE, HERBERT THIRKELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Dulwich College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 2nd Jan., 1878, and served in the Burma commission as assistant commissioner, and personal assistant to the chief commissioner; on special duty, Rangoon, April, 1880; officiating deputy commissioner, Thayetmyo, April, 1882; cantonment magistrate and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, April-Sept., 1882, and again in 1883; officiated for various periods between 1880 and 1886 as assistant secretary, junior secretary, and secretary to the chief commissioner; also as director of agriculture, Aug.-Nov., 1884; secretary to the chief commissioner for Upper Burma, March, 1886; secretary to the chief commissioner, April, 1887; officiating commissioner, Northern division, Feb.-June, 1888; officiating chief secretary to chief commissioner, in 1888 and 1889; confirmed, July, 1890; commissioner, Dec., 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; officiating judicial commissioner, Upper Burma, May, 1894.

WHITE, JOHN CLAUDE, India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; joined the service, 17th Nov., 1876, as assistant engineer, and was posted to Bengal; temporarily employed on Behar-Assam railway, 1884; executive engineer, Aug., 1887; assistant political officer, Sikkim, June, 1889; executive engineer, Sikkim, in addition to other duties, April, 1890.

WHITE, JOHN HUBBARD, Major-General, R.E., late Mint-Master, Bombay.—Second lieutenant, Bombay engineers, Dec., 1853; arrived in India, Nov., 1855; from June, 1856, served in the Bombay public works department as assistant engineer and executive engineer (for irrigation); from May, 1865, to Feb., 1874, served as deputy consulting engineer and acting consulting engineer for railways, Bombay; acting superintending engineer, Feb., 1874; acting chief engineer, and under secretary to government of Bombay, railway branch, June-Dec., 1874; transferred to financial department of government of India as acting mint-master, Bombay, June, 1876; rejoined the railway department, Feb., 1878; from March, 1879, served as mint-master and mint-engineer, Bombay; senior mint-master from Jan., 1884; retired, March, 1890.

WHITE, PHILIP JAMES, Oudh Commission.—Signaller in telegraph department, July, 1854; was employed in various capacities in several

government offices till the outbreak of the Mutiny, when he served with a body of volunteer horse and was present at various engagements (medal); deputy collector and deputy magistrate, Gorakhpur, Sept., 1858; assistant settlement officer, Nov., 1859; from March, 1863, served in the Jhansi commission as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, Dec., 1870; settlement officer, 1st grade, June, 1885, to March, 1888; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, Sept., 1892.

WHITE, WALTER HANBURY, Major, R.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, Jan., 1874, and posted to state railways; assistant director, north-eastern system, and assistant secretary to government of Bengal, Oct., 1878; served in Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); executive engineer, Jan., 1880; services lent to South-Indian railway company, Jan., 1891; employed on East Coast line, Sept., 1891; officiating consulting engineer to government of India for railways, central division, April, 1893; and at Calcutta, April, 1894.

WHITE, WILLIAM HENRY, Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 23rd June, 1873, as local fund engineer, Surat; assistant engineer, April, 1875; executive engineer, March, 1882.

WHITEHEAD, JOHN EDWARDS, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Appointed supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

WHITESIDE, WILLIAM SOUTHNEY, late Madras C.S.—Appointed to the Madras civil service from Haileybury College, 1855; served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting deputy secretary to government, sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting civil and sessions judge, small cause court judge of Cuddalore, 1866-67, and acting collector and magistrate; collector and magistrate of North Arcot, Feb., 1873, and subsequently at Tanjore; on special duty as member of the forest draft bill committee in Ootacamund, May, 1882; served on the board of revenue from Aug., 1882, in charge of the inam department; acting president of the board of examiners, Nov., 1882; acted as director of revenue settlement and agriculture, and inam commissioner, April-July, 1884; on deputation in the southern districts for reporting on working of licence tax act, Dec., 1884; additional member of the governor-general's council for making laws and regulations, Nov., 1886; acting 2nd member, board of revenue, March, 1888; appointed 3rd member, Jan., 1889, and 2nd member, Oct., 1889; retired, Dec., 1890.

WHITEWAY, RICHARD STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at University of London, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 10th Dec., 1868, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, acting assistant secretary to government, assistant settlement officer, and officiating magistrate and collector; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, Oct., 1879; settlement officer, 3rd grade, on special duty at Aligarh, Nov., 1881; settlement officer, Ajmere and Merwara, Jan., 1883; deputy commissioner, 1st grade, March, 1888; magistrate and collector, April, 1888; retired, Aug., 1893.

WHITLOCK, CHARLES JAMES TOLLER, Colonel, I.S.C., Madras Police Department.—

First commission, 20th Feb., 1858; and served in the suppression of the Mutiny (medal); joined the department, April, 1867, as probationary assistant superintendent; confirmed, Nov., 1868; resumed military duty, August, 1871; rejoined the department, April, 1874; promoted to superintendent, Nov., 1881.

WHITMORE, JOHN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Merchant Taylors' School, and Merton College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1866; arrived in India, 31st Dec., 1868; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal, and was appointed joint magistrate and deputy collector, Dec., 1878; officiating judicial commissioner, Chota Nagpur, Dec., 1881; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1885.

WHITTEN, RICHARD JOHN, Northern India Salt Revenue Department.—Joined the service in Feb., 1862, as assistant patrol; deputy inspector, internal branch, Jan., 1867; on deputation to inquire into illicit manufacture of salt in Behar, 1869; personal assistant to commissioner of inland customs, Oct., 1869; collector of customs, Sept., 1871; on deputation to report on salt taxation in Sind, July-Aug., 1872; 1st grade collector, July, 1876; on deputation in Rajputana to report on salt sources in connection with proposed abolition of inland customs line, July-Oct., 1877; employed temporarily under government of Bombay in salt inquiries in Kutch and Kathiawar; deputy commissioner of inland customs, Dec., 1879; at various periods has officiated as commissioner of Northern India salt revenue.

WHITTLE, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1890, and served as assistant magistrate and collector and assistant commissioner.

WHITWORTH, GEORGE CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Sandicraft School, Northwich, and Queen's College, Liverpool; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 5th Dec., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, acting assistant judge and sessions judge, and assistant agent for sardars in the Deccan; acting under secretary to government of Bombay in the political, judicial, and educational departments, and secretary to the governor's council, Jan., 1876; pro tem. under secretary to government in the public works department for relief purposes, Sept., 1877, to March, 1878; second assistant collector, Feb., 1881; judicial assistant to the political agent, Kathiawar, Nov., 1886; judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1887; acting secretary, political, &c., departments, April, 1893, to April, 1894; author of 'The Theory of Relevancy for the purpose of Judicial Evidence,' an 'Anglo-Indian Dictionary,' 1885, and the 'Rajkumar Law Lectures,' 1888.

WICKES, THOMAS HAINES, M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed as special assistant engineer, April, 1861; engineer to the suburban municipality, Calcutta, Jan., 1865; re-appointed as executive engineer, public works department, Bengal, Nov., 1865; on special duty in the famine districts, May, 1874, and in connection with

the Eastern Bengal railway, April, 1875; also with the secretary to the government of Bengal, March, 1878; assistant secretary to the government of Bengal, April, 1880; executive engineer, 1st grade, June, 1880; superintending engineer, 2nd class, March, 1886; 1st superintending engineer, temporary, April, 1888, and permanent, May, 1889; officiating chief engineer and joint secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, June, 1888, and again from May, 1890; confirmed, July, 1891; chief engineer, July, 1891; secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, buildings and roads branch, Dec., 1892.

WICKHAM, WILLIAM ALLEN, Store Department, India Office.—Appointed 2nd grade supervisor, India store depot, 23rd Sept., 1889.

WIGHT, JOHN KNOX, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Queen's Collegiate School, Trinidad, and Clare College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1872, and served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector; on famine duty, April, 1874; transferred to Assam, Dec., 1874, and served as assistant commissioner and assistant inspector of immigrants; deputy commissioner, March, 1884; returned to duty in Bengal, May, 1890; district and sessions judge, June, 1890.

WIGNER, JOHN MURCH, B.A., B.Sc., Store Department, India Office.—Educated at King's Lynn Grammar School, and London University; temporary clerk, India office, 17th July, 1862; junior clerk, Jan., 1867; senior clerk, July, 1885.

WILCOX, FREDERICK, Bengal Police Department.—Joined the service, 25th May, 1862, as assistant superintendent of police; district superintendent, June, 1873; 1st grade, July, 1891; has officiated as deputy inspector-general.

WILD, ALBERT EDWARD, India Forest Department.—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1869, as assistant conservator, and served in Burma and the Punjab; deputy conservator, Oct., 1876; conservator, Jan., 1892; in charge, central circle, N.W. Provinces, April, 1892; officiating conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1894.

WILD, CHARLES EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at City of London School and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1887; arrived in India, 17th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant commissioner.

WILKINS, CECIL ANSELL, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at King's College School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived, 26th Nov., 1868; served in revenue and judicial departments, Bengal; officiating registrar, high court, April, 1880, and again from 1881 to 1884; on special duty in the high court, July, 1880; joint magistrate and deputy collector, March, 1884; registrar, high court, March, 1885; district and sessions judge, April, 1886; member of Bengal legislative council, March, 1894; compiled revised editions of the High Court's Rules and Orders, in 1881 and 1883.

WILKINS, WILLIAM HENRY, Colonel, I.S.C., Survey of India.—Joined the Bengal artillery,

Dec., 1857; arrived in India, Jan., 1858; served with the Cawnpur field force, Feb.—March, 1858; studied at Thomason College, Roorkee, March, 1861, to Aug., 1862; served in the revenue survey from April, 1863, as assistant surveyor, and was employed in the Central Provinces, Punjab, Midnapur, Dearah, and British Burma surveys; deputy superintendent, Oct., 1878; in charge of map record and issue offices, May, 1891; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892; assistant surveyor-general, Sept., 1892.

WILKINSON, ALFRED REGINALD, Lieut.-Colonel, Bengal Infantry, late Bengal Police.—First commission, 1860; from 10th July, 1863, served in the Bengal police department as assistant superintendent and district superintendent; attached to the special police levy for service in Bhutan, Oct., 1864; on special duty at the presidency, June, 1866; personal assistant to the inspector-general of police, 1869 to 1881; promoted to 1st grade district superintendent, June, 1886; also acted as deputy commissioner of police, Calcutta, and as deputy inspector-general; retired, March, 1889.

WILKINSON, FRANCIS HENRY, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Milton Abbas School, Blackheath Proprietary School, and Pembroke College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competition of 1862; arrived, 10th Nov., 1863; served in Madras from Feb., 1865, as assistant collector and magistrate, and acted as under secretary to government in the revenue department, Aug.—Nov., 1867; on special duty as arbitrator of boundary disputes, June, 1870; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Salem, April, 1878; district and sessions judge, South Malabar, June, 1882; judge of the high court, Madras, Nov., 1887; retired, Feb., 1893.

WILKINSON, THOMAS ORMEROD, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Chudleigh Grammar School, and King's College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 3rd Dec., 1865, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating small cause court judge; officiated as registrar of the chief court in 1878, 1879, and 1880; judicial assistant, 2nd grade, Dec., 1883; district judge, 1st grade, Nov., 1884; divisional judge, 3rd grade, Jan., 1887, and 2nd grade, March, 1890; retired, Sept., 1890.

WILLCOCKS, ALEXANDER JOHN, M.D., Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, Medical Department, N.W. Provinces and Oudh.—Joined the service as assistant surgeon, 30th Sept., 1873; arrived in India, 5th May, 1874; served on military, civil, and jail duty, and was appointed civil surgeon, 2nd class, April, 1877; surgeon-major, Sept., 1885; civil surgeon, 1st class, Jan., 1888; officiating professor, Calcutta medical college, March to Nov., 1892.

WILLIAMS, ALBERT, LL.M., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Uppingham, Clifton, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1884; arrived in India, 8th Jan., 1886, and was appointed assistant commissioner, Punjab; under secretary to government of Punjab, April, 1890; officiating under-secretary to government of India, foreign department, Aug., 1892; confirmed, May, 1894.

WILLIAMS SIR EDWARD CHARLES SPARSHOTT, K.C.I.E., Lieut.-General, R.E., India Office.—Educated at the Royal Naval School, New Cross, and the Military Seminary, Addiscombe; first commission, 1848; adjutant of engineers, Fort William, and studying machinery in Calcutta mint, 1850; extra assistant secretary, military board, 1851; superintendent, iron bridge yard, Calcutta, 1851-52; served with the Pegu expedition, 1852-53, as adjutant of engineers and in charge of engineer park, and was present at the capture of Martaban, Rangoon (wounded and mentioned in despatches) and Prome (medal and clasp); superintendent, Pegu topographical survey, 1854-56; principal, Civil Engineering College, Calcutta, 1856-60; member of senate, Calcutta University; acted as under secretary to government of India, public works department, for one year, during the Mutiny; principal, Thomason College, Roorkee, 1861-62; under secretary, government of India, public works department, 1863-69; member of Bengal sanitary commission; in charge of office of inspector-general of forests for one year; acting mint-master and commissioner of paper currency, Bombay, 1867; deputy secretary, government of India, public works department (railway branch), 1869-74, and again, 1877-78; director of state railways, 1874-77; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; deputy government director of Indian railway companies, India office, March, 1880; government director, March, 1892; K.C.I.E., June, 1893.

WILLIAMS, GEORGE ROBERT CARLISLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1864; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1865; from March, 1866, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and officiating magistrate and collector; promoted to joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, April, 1881; deputy commissioner, 2nd class, Jhansi, March, 1883; magistrate and collector, Dec., 1886; commissioner, Allahabad, Feb., 1894; author of 'An Historical and Statistical Memoir of Dehra Dun,' and 'Gazetteer of Dehra Dun' (part).

WILLIAMS, HENRY CLISSOLD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Haileybury College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived in India, 25th Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Provinces as assistant commissioner, officiating district superintendent of police, and officiating deputy commissioner; on famine duty in Bengal, Feb., 1874; from Nov., 1878, served in Assam as assistant commissioner; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 2nd grade, Jan., 1883; inspector-general of police and jails, superintendent of stamps and commissioner of excise, Assam, Sept., 1885; returned to Bengal as magistrate and collector, Nov., 1891.

WILLIAMS, RALPH KING, Public Works Department, Government of India.—From Nov., 1869, to Feb., 1873, served in the office of the consulting engineer for railways, Bombay, as accountant and deputy controller, and was appointed assistant examiner of guaranteed railway accounts in 1872; served in Madras from Feb., 1873, to March, 1878, and was appointed deputy examiner, May, 1876; acted as examiner of guaranteed railway accounts in Calcutta, March to May, 1878, and subsequently in Madras, and

at Lahore, March, 1880; examiner, 4th class, Indus Valley state railway accounts, Aug., 1880; transferred to Nagpur, Central Provinces, March, 1885; examiner, guaranteed railway accounts, Lahore, April, 1885; examiner, North-Western railway accounts, Lahore, Jan., 1886; 2nd class examiner, Dec., 1886; 1st class, Jan., 1892.

WILLIAMS, WILLIAM, M.I.E.E., late Telegraph Department, Government of India.—Joined the department as assistant superintendent, 16th Jan., 1868; arrived in India, 29th Feb., 1868; served in various divisions, and in 1874 was on special duty at Bombay in connection with introduction of duplex system; superintendent, April, 1880; attached to director-general's office for various periods, and was also personal assistant to director-general; held charge of various divisions, including Indore, Bombay, and Nagpur; retired, 1890; author of 'Manual of Telegraphy.'

WILLIAMS-WYNN, ARTHUR W.—Educated at Westminster School and Christ Church, Oxford; graduated B.A. in 1878; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1882; appointed assistant private secretary (unpaid) to the secretary of state (Viscount Cross), Aug., 1886; assistant private secretary, Dec., 1889; private secretary, June to Aug., 1892.

WILLIS, JOHN, Funds Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 3rd Nov., 1862; junior book-keeper, Nov., 1868; assistant book-keeper, Dec., 1881; clerk, Oct., 1892.

WILLOCK, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Trinity College, Dublin, and Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 12th March, 1870, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate; on special duty, Jan., 1876, as secretary to the salt commission; from March, 1879, served in the salt department as deputy commissioner and acting commissioner, and was also employed in suppressing the disturbances in the Gudum Hills, May, 1879; collector and magistrate, Dec., 1888; collector and agent, Vizagapatam, July, 1890.

WILLOUGHBY, MICHAEL WEEKES, C.S.I., Lieut.-General, I.S.C., late Bombay Secretary. — First commission, Bombay army, June, 1849; acted as secretary to government of Bombay in the military, marine, and ecclesiastical departments, from May, 1880, and was confirmed in this appointment, Feb., 1881; returned to Europe, 1888.

WILLSON, JAMES, M.A., Bengal Educational Service (Assam).—Educated at Ballenasloe College, Ranelagh Institute, and Trinity College, Dublin; professor, cathedral mission college, Calcutta, Jan., 1870; assistant, geological survey of India, Sept., 1871, to Feb., 1873; professor, Hooghly college, March, 1873; Dacca college, Jan., 1874; Patna college, May, 1875; from Jan., 1879, served in Assam as inspector of schools, and was appointed director of public instruction, Assam, Jan., 1889; has edited Willson's 'Dynamics.'

WILMER, JOHN RANDAL, Colonel, I.S.C., Survey of India.—Joined the service as lieutenant, R.A., 22nd June, 1859; arrived in India, 27th Oct., 1860; served in the military department, and in Aug., 1869, was appointed to the staff corps and assistant superintendent topographical survey; deputy superintendent, July, 1882; in

charge of Baluchistan survey party, Oct., 1885, to Oct., 1886; deputy superintendent, Feb., 1887; superintendent (new scale), Aug., 1892.

WILSON, ARTHUR, Legal Adviser, India Office.—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 11th June, 1862; puisne judge, high court of judicature, Calcutta, 1878; vice-chancellor, Calcutta university, 1880-83; president of the Crawford enquiry commission at Poona, 1888-89; legal adviser, India office, 27th April, 1892.

WILSON, DAVID, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Hutcheson's Grammar School, Glasgow, University of Glasgow, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 4th Feb., 1886, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner; officiating deputy commissioner, Dec., 1890.

WILSON, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, Colonel, I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—First commission, as lieutenant, Bengal artillery, 8th June, 1860; arrived in India, Oct., 1860; admitted to the staff corps, April, 1869; extra A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1869; from Nov., 1870, served under the foreign department of the government of India as guardian and tutor to the Nawab of Jaora, and from 1874, as additional political assistant and political assistant in Jaora, in Central India, and at Tehri; tutor to the Maharaja of Mysore, June, 1876; 1st assistant resident, Hyderabad, Oct., 1879; political agent, 3rd class, Oct., 1881; agent to the governor-general with the king of Oudh, and superintendent of political pensions, March, 1882; boundary settlement officer and assistant to political agent, Bundelkhand, Jan., 1883; political agent, 2nd class, Jan., 1884; officiated as resident, 2nd class, in Nepal, and political agent, Bhartpur and Karauli; political agent, Bundelkhand, Oct., 1886; commissioner, Ajmere, Feb., 1894; resident, Persian Gulf, May, 1894; served in the Bhutan campaign of 1864-65 (medal and clasp), the Hazara campaign of 1868, the Black Mountain (clasp).

WILSON, GEORGE, Commander, R.I.M., Marine Department, Burma.—Commander, Indian marine, Aug., 1878; port officer, Bassein, April, 1882; acting assistant director of Indian marine, May, 1889; acted as port officer, Rangoon, in 1881 and 1889, and was confirmed in that post, Sept., 1890.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, Major, R.E., India Public Works Department.—Appointed assistant engineer, 20th May, 1875, and posted to state railways; assistant director, north-eastern system, June, 1877, and again in 1879; assistant secretary, railway branch of public works department, Bengal, 1878; executive engineer, May, 1883; served in Soudan expedition (medal), and was employed on Soudan railway, 1884-85; assistant secretary to government of India, public works department, April, 1888; officiating deputy manager, North-Western railway, March, 1890; confirmed, April, 1891.

WILSON, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Perth Academy, and University of Edinburgh; appointed to Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1873; arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1875, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, settlement officer, and officiating deputy commissioner; on

special duty, Sirsa, April, 1879, and again, Oct.-Nov., 1880, in the civil secretariat; officiating under secretary to government, July, 1881, and again in 1884; on special duty at Simla for the purpose of writing the final report on the Sirsa settlement, Aug., 1883; officiating senior secretary to financial commissioner in 1885 and 1886; deputy commissioner, Sept., 1890; author of works on tribal customs in the Gurgaon and Sirsa districts, and of the Sirsa settlement report.

WILSON, JOHN HATTON, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Bengal.—Probationary assistant engineer, 5th March, 1856; served in the public works department in the Central Provinces, and temporarily in Oudh, 1865-67, as assistant engineer and executive engineer; temporarily transferred to military works branch, July to Sept., 1879; in charge of the Nagpore and Chattisgarh railway reconnaissance survey division, Nov., 1881; superintending engineer, and transferred to Bengal, Dec., 1881; superintending engineer, 2nd class, Dec., 1885; 1st superintending engineer, temporary, March, 1886; retired, 1890.

WILSON, ROBERT HENRY, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862; served in the revenue and judicial departments as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, and magistrate and collector; officiating registrar-general of assurances, Sept., 1870; also officiating marriage registrar, Calcutta; officiating under secretary to the government of Bengal, Dec., 1870; inspector of registration offices, July, 1872; officiating inspector-general of registration, Sept., 1872; officiating secretary, board of revenue, July, 1877, and again in 1879; magistrate and collector, 1st grade, Aug., 1884; officiating commissioner, Burdwan division, Aug., 1884; employed under the public works department on the commission in connection with the Orissa canals, Nov., 1884; officiating secretary, government of Bengal, financial department, Feb., 1886; on special duty with the finance committee, 1886; retired, Sept., 1887.

WILSON, THOMAS CORBY, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Clifton College, and Trinity College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1883, and served as assistant commissioner, Central Provinces; acting district superintendent of police, Hoshangabad, Sept.-Nov., 1884; assistant settlement officer, June, 1886; settlement officer, May, 1888; transferred to Burma as deputy commissioner and officiating director of land records and agriculture, Feb., 1891; confirmed, Aug., 1892.

WILSON, WILLIAM, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educated at Marischal College, Aberdeen; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1859; served in the revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed district and sessions judge, Kistna, Feb., 1874, and collector and magistrate, June, 1877; on special duty for compiling irrigation accounts, Nov., 1875, and again, Oct., 1879, and Aug., 1881; acting director of revenue settlement, April, 1878, to Sept., 1879; collector and magistrate, Coimbatore, Oct., 1879, to Aug., 1881;

collector, magistrate and agent, Vizagapatam, July, 1882; acted as member of the board of revenue in 1882, 1883, 1884; director of revenue settlement and agriculture, Nov., 1882; in charge of the inam department, March, 1883; also an additional member, board of revenue, April, 1883; permitted to visit on duty agricultural institutions in N.W. Provinces, and Bombay, Nov., 1883; fellow of Madras university, March, 1885; acting secretary to government of Madras in the revenue department, April, 1885; additional member of council for making laws and regulations, Sept., 1885; acting chief secretary to government of Madras, Oct., 1885; 3rd member of board of revenue, April, 1887; acted from time to time as 2nd and 1st member of board; appointed 1st member and commissioner of land revenue settlement, &c., Jan., 1889; retired, Oct., 1889.

WILSON, WILLIAM HENRY, Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, Bombay.—Arrived in India, 22nd Jan., 1857, and was employed on military duties till April, 1864, when he was appointed acting assistant political agent, Sadra; from Nov., 1864, served as assistant superintendent of police, subordinate magistrate, superintendent of railway police on Bombay, Baroda, and Central India railway, and district superintendent; promoted to 1st grade superintendent of police, June, 1883; acted as commissioner of police for city of Bombay, Oct., 1885, to June, 1886; colonel, Dec., 1886; appointed commissioner of police, Bombay, July, 1888; reverted to military duty, Sept., 1894.

WILSON, WILLIAM HERBERT, Ph.D. (Heidelberg), F.I.C., F.C.S., F.G.S., Education Department, Madras.—Served from 4th Feb., 1875, as professor of physical science, presidency college; fellow of Madras university, March, 1880; 3rd class, education department, June, 1882; in addition, registrar of Madras university, March, 1886; 2nd class, education department, Dec., 1888; professor of chemistry, presidency college, Nov., 1892.

WILSON, WILLIAM JOHN, M.I.C.E., F.C.H., India Public Works Department.—Trained at Cooper's Hill College; appointed assistant engineer, 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrigation works in N.W. Provinces and Oudh; executive engineer, May, 1883; personal assistant to chief engineer, and under secretary to government, July, 1888, and again, Sept., 1891; services lent to Egyptian government from Feb., 1892.

WILTSHIRE, CHARLES PALMER BLUETT, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at the Proprietary School, Islington, City of London School, King's College, London University, and Middle Temple; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 8th Nov., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting port officer at Karwar, and superintendent, timber depot, Canara; boundary commissioner between Canara and Mysore, May, 1873; on famine duty, Dharwar, in 1876, 1877, and 1878; in charge of Huzur treasury in 1880 and 1881; 1st assistant collector, Sept., 1886; also forest settlement officer, Poona, June, 1891; retired, Aug., 1894; translated the preliminary chapter of Mill's 'Political Economy' into Marathi.

WIMBERLEY, REGINALD JUSTUS, Colonel, late I.S.C.—Ensign, 3rd Bengal European regiment, 1859; served in the Punjab police

force, 1861 to 1867; commissariat department, March-Aug., 1867; from Aug., 1867, to 1872, served in Bengal as district superintendent of police; in command of police force to quell insurrection in the tributary mehals, May, 1868; 1st assistant superintendent, Port Blair and Nicobars, April, 1872; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1877; deputy superintendent, Dec., 1884; retired, Dec., 1887.

WINCHESTER, CHARLES BLAKE, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Blundell's School, Tiverton; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting forest settlement officer, and acting junior collector and political agent; on famine duty, Sept., 1877; 2nd assistant collector, Nov., 1886; 1st assistant collector, March, 1890; junior collector and magistrate, May, 1893.

WINCHESTER, ERNEST CHARLES, Registry Department, India Office.—Writer, India office, 16th Dec., 1872; clerk, 2nd class, Nov., 1878.

WINDSOR, JOHN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educated at Old Trafford School, Manchester, and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1884; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1887, and served as assistant magistrate and collector; in 1891 officiated as under secretary, financial and municipal departments.

WINGATE, ANDREW, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Elizabeth College, Guernsey, Kensington Grammar School, University of London, and University of Heidelberg; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, and acting collector and magistrate; compiled Bombay administration report for 1874-75 and for 1875-76; in political charge of the Jath state, Feb.-Sept., 1876; additional secretary in the famine department under chief commissioner of Mysore, Aug., 1877; assistant secretary to famine commission, Aug., 1878; settlement officer in Oodeypore state, Aug., 1879; officiating resident, Meywar, and settlement officer, Kashmir state, Feb., 1887; junior collector and magistrate, Kanara, July, 1890; acting collector of salt revenue, Bombay, Oct., 1890; also acting commissioner of customs, &c., April, 1891; settlement officer, Oodeypore state, May, 1891; senior collector, &c., Broach, Sept., 1893.

WINGATE, JOHN BRUCE, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at Old Aberdeen Gymnasium, Aberdeen University, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner.

WINMILL, GEORGE, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Joined the service as second sub-engineer, Indus Valley railway, 17th Nov., 1875; transferred to North-Western railway, May, 1888; and to Oudh and Rohilkhand line, Oct., 1888; locomotive superintendent, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889.

WINNY, THOMAS HERBERT, India Office.—Appointed assistant to the surveyor and clerk of the works, India office, 17th Aug., 1885.

WINTER, EDGAR FRANCIS LATIMER, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated

at Harrow, Trinity Hall, Cambridge, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1881; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1884; attached to the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; under secretary to government, Nov., 1889; deputy superintendent, family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1893; superintendent, April, 1894.

WINTER, HENRY EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Marlborough College, and King's School, Canterbury; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1866; arrived in India, 21st Nov., 1868, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant political agent, and acting collector and magistrate; first assistant collector and magistrate, and forest settlement and demarcation officer, Poona districts, Jan., 1883; 2nd grade judge, Jan., 1885; junior collector and magistrate, and political agent, Kolaba, Nov., 1888; senior collector, Dec., 1890; retired, May, 1894.

WINTERBOTHAM, HENRY MARTIN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Portland Grammar School, and London University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1867; arrived in India, 19th Nov., 1869, and served in Madras in the grades of assistant collector and magistrate, principal assistant, and sub-collector and joint magistrate; acting Malayalam translator to government, March-Dec., 1877; acting sub-secretary, board of revenue, June-July, 1883; acted for various periods as collector and magistrate, and as district and sessions judge in various districts; was appointed collector and magistrate, Malabar, Aug., 1888; Tanjore, Nov., 1893.

WODEHOUSE, THE HON. ARMINE.—Assistant private secretary (unpaid) to secretary of state for colonies (Earl of Kimberley), 1880; private secretary (unpaid) to secretary of state for India (Earl of Kimberley), Dec., 1882, to 1885; private secretary, Feb. to Aug., 1886; and again, from Aug., 1892; private secretary to secretary of state for foreign affairs (Earl of Kimberley), March, 1894.

WOLFE-MURRAY, FRANCIS D'ARCY OSBORNE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Harrow, and St. Andrew's University; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived in India, 19th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting assistant secretary to government in the judicial and legislative departments, acting principal assistant collector, assistant sessions judge, forest settlement officer, sub-collector and joint magistrate, collector and magistrate, and district and sessions judge; appointed sub-collector and joint magistrate, March, 1890; collector and magistrate, S. Canara, March, 1893.

WOLLASTON, ARTHUR NAYLOR, C.I.E., Revenue and Statistics Department, India Office.—Educated at Stockwell Grammar School; entered the India office by open competition as writer, old establishment, 3rd March, 1859; junior clerk, political and secret department, 1860; senior clerk, Jan., 1873; assistant secretary, revenue, statistics, and commerce department, Jan., 1884; in charge of the revenue and statistics department, May to Oct., 1886, and April, 1887, to March, 1888; in 1877 published

a translation of the 'Anvár-i-Suhaili, or Fables of Bidpai'; in 1879 edited Sir Lewis Pelly's miracle play of 'Hasan and Husain'; appointed to succeed Sir F. J. Goldsmid as examiner in Persian at the Royal Staff College, Sandhurst, 1880; selected in 1881 by the science and art department to superintend the translation of the oriental inscriptions in the South Kensington Museum; in 1882 published an English-Persian dictionary, and in 1889 a larger work of the same kind; C.I.E., Jan., 1886.

WOMACK, ARTHUR SHAW, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1882; assistant magistrate and collector, Dec., 1882; transferred to the Central Provinces, July, 1885, and served as supernumerary assistant commissioner, and registrar of the judicial commissioner's court; assistant commissioner, Nov., 1888; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commissioner of excise, Feb., 1893; deputy commissioner, April, 1893; also political agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, May-Nov., 1893.

WOOD, ARTHUR LIDBETTER MARTIN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at St. Paul's School, and Christ's College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 13th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as assistant collector and magistrate.

WOOD, EDWARD SEPTIMUS, Captain, late Forest Department, Oudh.—Arrived in India as lieutenant, 93rd Sutherland highlanders, 20th Sept., 1857; from Dec., 1859, served in the public works department on irrigation works, N.W. Provinces; officiated as conservator of forests, 1864-66; reverted to his regiment, Feb., 1866; appointed executive engineer, 4th grade, March, 1867; from April, 1867, served in the forest department, Oudh, and was appointed conservator, Oct., 1868; promoted to 2nd grade, Sept., 1882; 1st grade, Dec., 1888; retired, May, 1891.

WOOD, GEORGE PINCKNEY, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Educated at Marlborough College, and Addiscombe; writer, India office, 25th Jan., 1869; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

WOOD, HENRY MACKENZIE MACDONALD, Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 29th May, 1863; from April, 1867, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating deputy commissioner, and judicial assistant; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1884; and divisional judge, Nov., 1889.

WOOD, JULIUS JOHN, M.B., Surgeon-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Medical Department.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1868, and served on general and jail duty, and as superintendent of vaccination; on famine relief duty in April, 1874; deputy sanitary commissioner, Ranchi circle, Sept., 1888; retired, Feb., 1892.

WOODBURN, ARCHIBALD FRANCIS, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Ayr Academy, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1870; arrived in India, 11th Nov., 1872, and served as assistant collector and magistrate; also acted as forest settlement officer, and as junior collector and magistrate; second assistant collector, June, 1885; on special duty in judicial department in connection with Deccan agriculturists relief act, July, 1888; 1st

assistant collector and magistrate, Sept., 1888; collector and magistrate, Feb., 1893.

WOODBURN, JOHN, M.A., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Ayr Academy, Glasgow University, and Edinburgh University; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1863; from July, 1864, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector; transferred to Oudh, Feb., 1865, and served as assistant commissioner, assistant settlement officer, officiating personal assistant, and officiating secretary to chief commissioner; deputy commissioner, 3rd grade, Fyzabad, Aug., 1879; secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, in the Oudh revenue department and forest branch, Jan., 1882; also in the Oudh political department, March, 1882; secretary to government of N.W. Provinces and Oudh, judicial department, May, 1884; acted as chief secretary in 1884-86, and in 1887, and was confirmed in appointment, Nov., 1888; member of the lieutenant-governor's council, Dec., 1886; member of viceroy's legislative council, Feb., 1891; resumed his post as chief secretary, April, 1891; again member of viceroy's legislative council, Nov., 1891, to April, 1892, and again, Feb.-Nov., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1892; officiating chief commissioner of the Central Provinces, May, 1893; confirmed, Dec., 1893.

WOODCOCK, ELBOROUGH MARTIN, Colonel, I.S.C., late Police Department, North-Western Provinces.—Arrived in India as ensign, 9th March, 1856; served with the Persian expeditionary force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp); appointed to Oudh military police, 27th Nov., 1858; from March, 1860, served in the police department as district superintendent, and was promoted to 1st grade, Oct., 1880; reverted to military department, Jan., 1892.

WOODS, ALBERT EDWARD, Captain, I.S.C., Assam Commission.—Educated at the Royal Naval School, New Cross, and the Royal Military College, Sandhurst; entered the army, 22nd Oct., 1881; joined the Assam commission, 12th Aug., 1889, as assistant commissioner; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1893.

WOODTHORPE, ROBERT GOSSETT, C.B., Colonel, R.E., Survey of India Department.—Joined the service, 25th June, 1865; entered the department as assistant superintendent, July, 1871; deputy superintendent, Nov., 1884; on special duty with Gilgit mission, May, 1885; on special duty in Europe, Feb., 1888; deputy quartermaster-general for intelligence, Simla, Feb., 1889; superintendent, April, 1894; served in the Lushai expedition, the Afghan war, the Burma expedition, &c.; has written various papers on military and geographical subjects, and an account of the Lushai expedition.

WOODWARD, HILLERSDEN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educated at Oakham Grammar School, and Birmingham Grammar School; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1869; arrived, 28th Oct., 1871, and served as assistant collector and magistrate and acting railway magistrate; on special duty in connection with the Deccan agriculturists relief act, Dec., 1882, and again, Aug., 1883; forest settlement officer, Sholapur, Nov., 1883; acting junior collector and magistrate, Belgaum, Sept., 1884; second assistant

collector, May, 1885; 1st assistant collector and magistrate, July, 1888; junior collector and magistrate, Nov., 1892.

WOODWARD, WILLOUGHBY, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educated at Oakham Grammar School, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1863, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting magistrate of police at Bombay, and acting collector and magistrate and political agent; junior collector and magistrate, and district registrar, Panch Mahals, and political agent, Rewa Kantha, Aug., 1881; senior collector and magistrate and political agent, Satara, March, 1886; retired, March, 1890.

WOOTTON, JOHN JOSEPH, Accounts Department, India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 20th Feb., 1872.

WORDSWORTH, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Educational Department, Bombay.—Joined the educational service, May, 1861; arrived in India, 10th July, 1861, and served as head-master of the Elphinstone Institution, Bombay, acting professor of history in the Elphinstone College, and assistant inspector of schools; from May, 1862, served as principal of the Deccan College, Poona, and also acted as professor of English literature; from June, 1872, acted as principal of the Elphinstone College, Bombay, and was also appointed to select the government records at the secretariat; confirmed as principal of Elphinstone College, and professor of logic and moral philosophy, Nov., 1874; principal, and professor of history and political economy, from June, 1878; also sheriff of Bombay, Dec., 1885; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retired, 1890.

WORGAN, JOHN BERNEY, late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Cheltenham College; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1856; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1856; served in Bengal as assistant magistrate and collector, joint magistrate and deputy collector, magistrate and collector, and officiating district and sessions judge; from May, 1876, served in the judicial department, and was appointed district and sessions judge, 1st grade, July, 1879; retired, Dec., 1891.

WRIGHT, FRANCIS NELSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Eton, and Exeter College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 15th Jan., 1864; from Dec., 1864, served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and from May, 1870, to April, 1878, in the settlement department, and was appointed settlement officer, 3rd grade, Cawnpore, April, 1876; officiating director of agriculture and commerce, N.W. Provinces and Oudh, April, 1878, and again, 1880-81; joint magistrate and deputy collector, 1st grade, Nov., 1879; deputy commissioner, 2nd grade, March, 1885; magistrate and collector, April, 1885; officiating commissioner, Rae Bareli, Aug.-Oct., 1890; and Benares, April, 1891; commissioner, Allahabad, Nov., 1891; opium agent, Benares, Feb., 1894; author of a memorandum on the 'Agriculture of Cawnpore,' 'Cawnpore Gazetteer,' 'Cawnpore Settlement Report,' and 'Report on Wheat.'

WRIGHT, FREDERICK, Police Department, Hyderabad Administration.—Arrived in India, 20th Aug., 1859; served as inspector in the

Madras police from Jan., 1860; transferred to Hyderabad assigned districts, Aug., 1862, and served as assistant superintendent and district superintendent of police (1st class, April, 1877); officiated for various periods as inspector-general of police, jails, and registration; author of 'Margpradurshuk' (a treatise on the Indian Penal Code), 1878, a police manual of laws in 1883, and a schedule to all the penal acts of the government of India, 1841 to 1883.

WRIGHT, FREDERICK CHARLES BUCKLEY, Store Department, India Office.—Junior clerk, 4th July, 1867; senior clerk, June, 1891.

WRIGHT, HENRY NELSON, Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at Eton and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; appointed after open competitive examination of 1888; arrived in India, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector.

WRIGHT, JAMES McCALLUM, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Fordyce School, University of Aberdeen, and University College, London; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1883; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Provinces and Oudh as assistant magistrate and collector; joint magistrate, Nov., 1890; assistant commissioner, 1st grade, April, 1894.

WRIGHT, JAMES WALKER, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Department, Government of India.—Appointed to the department in July, 1859; served in the Punjab as assistant and executive engineer; in charge of Madhopur workshops, March, 1868; executive engineer, military works, Chukrata road, and afterwards in the Jullundur division, 1872; transferred to the Punjab provincial list, Nov., 1879; superintending engineer, 2nd class, July, 1886; 1st class, May, 1889; officiating chief engineer, and secretary to chief commissioner of Burma, April, 1890; officiating chief engineer, buildings and roads branch, and joint secretary to government of Punjab, Sept., 1890; officiating secretary to chief commissioner of Assam, March, 1891; confirmed, with permanent rank of chief engineer, 3rd class, June, 1891; 2nd class, June, 1892; retired, Jan., 1893.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM CHARLES, Public Works Department, Government of India.—Trained at Thomason College; appointed probationary assistant engineer, Oudh, 23rd Sept., 1864; confirmed, 1st April, 1865; executive engineer, March, 1871; lent to Cawnpur municipality, Sept., 1884; in charge of provincial works, Agra division, and subsequently Lucknow division, 1885; services lent to Rampore state, Jan., 1890; superintending engineer, May, 1891.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., Educational Department, North-Western Provinces.—Educated at Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; appointed, 7th Aug., 1867, and served from 7th Dec., 1867, as professor, Benares College; professor of English literature, Muir Central College, Allahabad, July, 1872; 3rd grade of educational officers, July, 1872; principal, Bareilly College, Dec., 1873; officiating inspector of schools, June, 1875, to March, 1876; from Jan., 1877, served as professor at the Muir Central College, and officiated as principal of the Benares College, in 1878; 2nd grade of educational officers, Aug., 1888; principal of

Benares College, Oct., 1888; has published a handbook of the education department, an account of the Muir Central College, and various educational works.

WYBROW, GEORGE DIGBY, A.M.I.C.E., Public Works Department, Madras.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served in the Madras Presidency as assistant engineer and executive engineer; superintendent engineer, Jan., 1892.

WYER, FREDERICK, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1860; arrived, 31st Dec., 1861; served in the Bengal revenue and judicial departments, and was appointed magistrate and collector, 1st grade, May, 1884; officiated as commissioner of the Burdwan division in 1883; and of the Dacca division, in 1885; retired, May, 1887.

WYER, THOMAS ROWLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (North-Western Provinces).—Educated at University of Glasgow, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived in India, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and assistant settlement officer, and in acting appointments as joint magistrate, magistrate and collector, small cause court judge, and district and sessions judge; assistant commissioner, Nov., 1884; joint magistrate, March, 1888; settlement officer in addition, March, 1890; deputy commissioner, Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891.

WYLIE, HENRY, C.S.I., Lieut.-Colonel, Foreign Department, Government of India.—Ensign, Oct., 1861; arrived in India, 15th Nov., 1861; served with various regiments, and took part in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1863, the 2nd Yusufzai expedition (medal and clasp), the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (clasp), the Abyssinian expedition, 1868 (medal), and the Hazara campaign, 1868 (clasp); on special duty in Baluchistan, with Major R. Sandeman, Nov., 1875, to Feb., 1877; assistant to governor-general's agent, Baluchistan, 1877; served in the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); C.S.I., Feb., 1881; assistant to the resident in Mysore, and secretary to chief commissioner, Coorg, 1882; political agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; political agent, Jhalawur, April, 1885; political agent, Bhopal, March, 1887; officiating resident, Nepal, March, 1891; confirmed, Oct., 1893.

WYLLIE, WILLIAM HUTT CURZON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Department, Government of India.—Ensign, 106th Foot, Oct., 1866; arrived in India, Feb., 1867; served in the Oudh commission, 1870-79, as assistant commissioner and officiating cantonment and city magistrate; staff corps, 1868; acted as inspector-general of registration in 1875, and as junior secretary to government, Oudh, 1876-77; from Jan., 1879, served under the foreign department, government of India, as cantonment magistrate, Nasirabad, assistant commissioner in Merwara and Ajmere, and as assistant to governor-general's agent, Baluchistan; served in Madras, 1880-81, as military secretary to the governor, and acted as private secretary; C.I.E., May, 1881; political agent, 3rd class, in political charge of ex-Gaekwar of Baroda, Mulhar Rao, 1881; assistant resident, Hyderabad, 1882; assistant commissioner, Ajmere, Nov., 1883; 1st assistant

agent to governor-general, Rajputana, 1884; additional political agent, 1st class, Kotah, April, 1885; boundary settlement officer, Meywar-Marwar border, Nov., 1886; political agent, Kotah, Jan., 1889; officiating commissioner, Ajmere, July, 1891; officiating political agent, Jhallowar, in 1891 and 1892; officiating resident, western states of Rajputana, May, 1893; resident, Meywar, March, 1894.

WYNCH, LIONEL MALING, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Bedford Grammar School, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1882; arrived in India, 7th Dec., 1885; assistant collector and magistrate, Feb., 1886; acted as deputy director of land records and agriculture, Nov., 1891; also acting secretary to commissioner of salt, &c., revenue, May-June, 1893; secretary to land revenue commissioners, Feb., 1894.

WYNDHAM, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces and Oudh).—Educated at Giggleswick Grammar School and Queen's College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competition of 1886; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant commissioner; joint magistrate, Aug., 1893; deputy superintendent of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1894.

WYNNE, SYDENHAM HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at the Royal Naval School, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed to the Madras civil service after open competitive examination of 1871; arrived, 21st Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate, acting cantonment magistrate, acting sub-collector and joint magistrate, acting collector and magistrate, and acting district and sessions judge; head assistant to the collector and magistrate, South Arcot, Oct., 1882; sub-collector and joint magistrate, Dec., 1887; collector and magistrate, Jan., 1890.

YATE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.M.G., Lieut.-Colonel, I.S.C., Political Department, Government of India.—First commission, ensign, 49th regiment, 9th Nov., 1867; arrived in India, 27th Feb., 1868; admitted to Bombay staff corps, March, 1871; from August, 1871, served under foreign department of government of India at Serohi, and with the Erinpuna irregular force, and afterwards with the Bhil corps; on special duty, Sind border, Sept., 1872, and Nov., 1873; boundary settlement officer, Meywar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Meywar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; officiated for various periods as political assistant, assistant political agent, magistrate, and assistant to superintendent for suppression of thuggee and dacoity; superintendent of operations for control of Moghias in Rajputana and Central India, Sept., 1879; served under the military department in Afghanistan, Feb. to May, 1880, when he was appointed political officer at Kalat-i-Ghilzai; political officer with Kabul-Kandahar field force under Sir F. Roberts on march to Kandahar, Aug., 1880 (bronze star); political officer with cavalry brigade at Kokeran, Sept., 1880; and in Argandab Valley, Oct., 1880; political officer in charge of city of Kandahar, and with southern Afghanistan field force, Nov., 1880, to April, 1881 (medal and clasp for Afghanistan); subsequently served in

the political department in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commission, July, 1884; political assistant, 1st class, July, 1885; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, July, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commission; political agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; officiating revenue commissioner, Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; political agent, Zhor, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting governor-general's agent and consul-general, Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; author of Gazetteers of Meywar, Banswara and Partabgarh, &c.

YELD, HORACE PARR, Surgeon-Lieut.-Colonel, late Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the Indian medical department, 1874; was employed in the military department up to Aug., 1884, when he was appointed probationer in the assay department, Bombay mint; transferred to Calcutta mint, Sept., 1884; surgeon-major, March, 1886; deputy assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1886, and Calcutta mint, Jan., 1888; deputy assay master, Bombay mint, May, 1892; has several times officiated as assay master at Calcutta or Bombay; retired, Nov., 1894.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, Registry and Record Department, India Office.—Appointed 2nd class clerk, 22nd May, 1888.

YOUNG, GEORGE GORDON, Colonel, I.S.C., late Punjab Commission.—Arrived in India, 23rd Oct., 1856; from June, 1858, served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, officiating small cause court judge, and assistant settlement officer; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1873; commissioner and superintendent, Jan., 1881; officiating 2nd financial commissioner, Feb.—Nov., 1888, and again April and May, 1890; reverted to military department, 1892.

YOUNG, JAMES, Bombay Public Works Department.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as assistant engineer; executive engineer, July, 1879.

YOUNG, MATTHEW, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educated at Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as assistant collector and magistrate.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, B.A., B.L., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Educated at Heidelberg University and University College, London; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1856; arrived in India, 16th Dec., 1856; served in the N.W. Provinces as assistant magistrate and collector, and joint magistrate and deputy collector, and was appointed magistrate and collector, Basti, March, 1875; was subsequently posted to Mainpuri, Moradabad, and Muzaffarnagar; civil and sessions judge, 2nd grade, Bareilly, Oct., 1880; and afterwards at Allahabad; judicial commissioner, Oudh, Aug., 1886; acting judge of high court, April-Dec., 1890; C.S.I., Jan., 1891; retired, Dec., 1891.

YOUNG, WILLIAM MACKWORTH, M.A., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Eton, and King's College, Cambridge; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1862; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as assistant com-
