missioner, assistant settlement officer, officiating secretary to financial commissioner, and officiating deputy commissioner; attached on special duty to office of financial commissioner, Oct., 1872; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab, Oct., 1873; settlement secretary to financial commissioner, March, 1875; deputy commissioner, 3rd class, Jan., 1878; officiating superintendent, Kapurthala state, Feb., 1878; secretary to government, Punjab, Jan., 1880; promoted to commissioner and superintendent, May, 1887, but officiated as secretary to government until Dec., 1887; officiating secretary to government of India, Jan.-March, 1888; commissioner, Jullundur division, April, 1888; 2nd financial commissioner of the Punjab, April, 1889; 1st commissioner, Feb., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; member of viceroy's legislative council, June-Nov., 1893; president of the Indian hemp drugs commission, Oct., 1893.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ARTHUR DELAVAL, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Educated at Rugby, and Clifton College; appointed to the Bombay civil service after open competitive examination of 1875; arrived, 9th Dec., 1877, and served as assistant collector and magistrate, acting junior collector and magistrate, talukdari settlement officer, and assistant political agent; acted as private secretary to H.E. the governor, July-Oct., 1890; and as administrator, Rajpipla state, in 1888, and again in 1890-91; transferred to Central Provinces as assistant commissioner,

Nov., 1891; deputy commissioner, Jan., 1892; also political agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Nov., 1893.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ROMER EDWARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educated at Rugby, Clifton College, and Balliol College, Oxford; appointed to the Bengal civil service after open competitive examination of 1877; arrived in India, 14th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as assistant commissioner, settlement officer, officiating judicial assistant, officiating deputy commissioner, and officiating district judge; officiating under secretary to government, Punjab, March, 1885; acted as junior secretary and as senior secretary to financial commissioners; confirmed as junior secretary to financial commissioners, March, 1886; under secretary to government of Punjab, Jan., 1888, to April, 1890; officiating junior secretary, May, 1893.

YUSUF, MOHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educated at the Patna Collegiate School, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; appointed after open competitive examination of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as assistant commissioner and personal assistant to chief commissioner.

ZORAB, JOHN MANOOK, M.B., Surgeon-Lieutenant-Colonel, Bengal Medical Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the military department, and as civil surgeon, Balasore, March, 1887, and Cuttack, March, 1891.

LIST OF CASUALTIESAMONG OFFICERS INCLUDED IN THE PREVIOUS LIST.

ANDREW, DAVID CLAPHAM, Lieut.-Colonel, Survey of India (ret.).—Died, 24th Nov., 1893.

BALFOUR, SIR GEORGE, K.C.E., General.—Died, 12th March, 1894.

BRADSHAW, JOHN, Madras Education Department.—Died, 5th Jan., 1894.

BRUCE, HENRY ALEXANDER, Inspector-General.—Died, 8th May, 1894.

CHURCHILL, RIGHT HON. LORD RANDOLPH H. S., LL.D., M.P.—Died, 24th Jan., 1895.

CRAWFORD, CHARLES EDWARD GORDON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Died, 4th May, 1894.

DRUMMOND, The Hon. SIR EDMUND, K.C.I.E., Bengal C.S. (ret.).—Died, 10th Jan., 1895.

GANPATRAO BHASKAR, Judicial Department, Bombay.—Died, 29th Dec., 1893.

GROVES, FREDERICK WILLIAM, India Office (ret.).—Died, 14th Nov., 1894.

HAWKINS, CÆSAR RICHARD, Bengal C.S. (ret.).—Died, 20th Oct., 1894.

HUDLESTON, WILLIAM, C.S.I., Madras C.S. (ret.).—Died, 25th Oct., 1894.

HUTCHINSON, HENRY STEPHEN, Colonel, Survey of India.—Died, 27th April, 1894.

INGLIS, JOHN FORBES DAVID, C.S.I., Bengal C.S. (ret.).—Died, 13th March, 1894.

JENKINS, THOMAS LOWTEN LAYTON, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Died, 20th July, 1894.

JEWELL, ALFRED, India Office.—Died, 10th Jan., 1895.

LAKSHMIKANTHA RAO PANTULU, JIDDU, Madras Revenue Department.—Died, —, 1893.

LAL BEHARI DE, Rev., Bengal Education Department (ret.).—Died, 28th Oct., 1894.

LAMBERT, PARRY, Colonel, R.E., India Public Works Department (ret.).—Died, 10th Nov., 1892.

LONDON, CHARLES PALMER, Indian Telegraph Department.—Died, 2nd Oct., 1894.

LEVY, FRANK, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Died, 20th Oct., 1894.

McDONELL, WILLIAM FRASER, V.C., Bengal C.S. (ret.).—Died, 31st July, 1894.

MEADE, SIR RICHARD JOHN, General, K.C.I.E., C.S.I.—Died, 20th March, 1894.

NETHERSOLE, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Central Provinces).—Died, 12th Oct., 1888.

NUND KISHORE DAS, Bengal Provincial Service.—Died, 7th April, 1894.

ORR, JOHN HENRY, Inspector-General, Madras Medical Service (ret.).—Died, 4th Aug., 1894.

PARESH NATH BANERJI, Judicial Department, Bengal.—Died, 11th Nov., 1893.

PEACOCK, FREDERICK BARNES, C.S.I., Bengal C.S. (ret.).—Died, 14th April, 1894.

PENNEY, JOHNSTON, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Died, 25th July, 1894.

PHILIPPS, HERBERT REES, India Office.—Died, 4th Feb., 1894.

ROSS, ALEXANDER, Bengal C.S. (ret.).—Died, 1st March, 1894.

RULE, EDWARD, India Finance and Commerce Department (ret.).—Died, 22nd May, 1894.

SHANKAR PANDURANG PANDIT, Bombay Political Department.—Died, 18th March, 1894.

SHAW, PATRICK DUNLOP, Madras Judicial Department.—Died, 8th May, 1894.

STEPHEN, SIR JAMES FITZJAMES, K.C.S.I.—Died, 12th March, 1894.

VENKATA JAGA RAO, CHENDIKA, Madras C.S. (ret.).—Died, 13th Oct., 1893.

WALLERSTEIN, PHILIP HERMAN, Major.—Died, 23rd Sept., 1894.

WATSON, HERBERT EDWARD, Sind Commission.—Died, 27th Feb., 1894.

WHITE, JAMES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Provinces).—Died, 17th March, 1894.

WHITTALL, RICHARD HENRY CECIL, India Forest Department.—Died, 23rd Feb., 1894.

WILLIAMS, FLEETWOOD, C.S.I., Bengal C.S. (ret.).—Died, 4th Dec., 1894.
